name: Aberrant Paradox
cheats: If a Wizard joins the duel late, Aberrant Paradox will cast a single-target version of Power Nova for that Wizard's maximum Health, ignoring all Resist and all Wards. This occurs at the beginning of the Round.

At the beginning of the first Round, the Divine Paradox in the background will cast a random School Global Spell. These Global Spells boost the Damage of their School and weaken the Damage of the opposite School. The only exception is the Balance School, which weakens the damage of all other Schools. The first Global Spell lasts two Rounds. All others last three Rounds.

When this Global Spell is cast, all Wizards will receive a card related to the School of the Global Spell in their hand that cannot be discarded. Reshuffle does not remove them. These cards either help the enemy team or hurt the Wizard's team.
Changing the Global Spell will make the Abberant Paradox recast the previous Global Spell that was in effect.
If the Global spell is changed by an enemy spell, such as Gaze of Fate, the Global will not be recast.
Fire Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Smoke Screen][Anti-Steal Charm][Anti-Scimitars]
Ice Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Anti-Tower Shield][Cursed Taunt][Anti-Climaclysm]
Storm Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Anti-Cleanse Charm][Anti-Disarm][Anti-Iron Sultan]
Myth Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Cleanse Ward][Cursed Stun][Anti-Snake Charmer]
Life Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Armor][Cursed Pigsie][Anti-Lamassu]
Death Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Infection][Cursed Juju][Qismah's Curse (2 Pip)]
Balance Phase - Wizards will receive one of the following Spells.
[Cursed Balanceblade][Cursed Mass Hex][Anti-Sand Wurm]

If all Aberrations are not defeated before the Abberant Paradox, it will respawn with 5,775 Health. This also applies if the Wizard defeats the last Aberration on the turn the Abberant Paradox is defeated.
Defeating any of the initial Minion Aberrations in the specified slot will summon the following Minions in the same slot.
[Key] Aberration (Myth Elite) [Myth]
[Gem] Aberration (Death Elite) [Death]
[Spiral] Aberration (Life Elite) [Life]
Defeating any of the second Minion Aberrations in the specified slot will summon the following Minions in the same slot. No more will spawn after this.
[Key] Aberration (Shadow Elite) [Shadow]
[Gem] Aberration (Balance Elite) [Balance]
[Spiral] Aberration (Balance Elite) [Balance]
spellNotes: Casts Feint (Shadow) that applies a +70% Trap to all enemies and does not apply a Trap on Self.
Casts an All Enemies version of Poison (Shadow).
Casts a Tatzlewurm Terror (Shadow) that has a chance to Confuse Wizards for three Rounds.
strategy: Enter together and on time. Anyone who joins late eats a single-target Power Nova for their maximum health that ignores resist and wards - an instant defeat.

Bring multiple hitters, not one. The fight runs three continuous waves with no reset, so the guide favors several hitters over the usual one-hitter setup; a healer helps but is optional if you clear fast.

Kill order is the fight: every Aberration must die before the boss. Each slot spawns three waves (Fire/Ice/Storm, then Myth/Death/Life, then Shadow/Balance/Balance). If the boss dies before the last Aberration, or on the same turn as it, he respawns with 5,775 health.

Pack AoE hits that land around 5,000-6,000 damage to wipe each minion wave quickly; the minions carry large spell arsenals and are the real threat. Hitters want 20-30% pierce because the boss resists 70% to every school except Balance (5% boost) - Balance wizards actually boost on him.

Do not cast the offensive cursed cards the Divine Paradox forces into your hands each global phase - they burn shadow pips and help the boss. Instead exploit the harmless ones: play Cursed Stun while beguiled/confused so it hits enemies, pair Cursed Infection with Cursed Pigsie to strip debuffs, and dump Anti-Tower Shield on a jade player for near-zero cost. The cards cannot be discarded and survive Reshuffle.

Time hits around the rotating school globals: the round-1 global lasts two rounds, later ones last three. Hit while your school is boosted, hold when weakened. Do not overwrite the global (he recasts the previous one) - only Gaze of Fate changes it without a recast.
Keep the hitters bladed through the waves and save the finishing Balance/high-pierce hit until the ninth Aberration is confirmed dead, then kill the boss on a later turn.
cheatNotes: Joining the duel late means a Power Nova for your full health bar that ignores all resist and wards. Never join mid-fight; enter as a group from the start.

A random school global goes up at round 1 and rotates. It boosts one school's damage and weakens the opposite school (a Balance global weakens everyone). First global lasts two rounds, the rest last three - plan your hit for a phase that favors you.
Each global phase shoves a cursed card into your hand that cannot be discarded and survives Reshuffle. These cards help the boss or hurt your team - just leave them in hand.
If you replace the global with your own, the boss immediately recasts the previous one, wasting your pips. Do not bother casting globals.
Globals changed by a spell like Gaze of Fate do not get recast, so a Gaze of Fate hit is the one safe way to clear a phase that hurts you.
During the Fire global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
During the Ice global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
During the Storm global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
During the Myth global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
During the Life global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
During the Death global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
During the Balance global you receive one of the listed cursed/anti cards. Do not cast it.
If any Aberration is still alive when the boss dies - including one killed on the same turn - he comes back with 5,775 health. Confirm all minions are dead, wait a turn, then kill the boss.
Killing a first-wave Aberration spawns a second-wave minion in the same slot (Myth, Death, or Life depending on the slot). Expect the fight to refill as you clear.
Killing a second-wave Aberration spawns the third and final wave in that slot (Shadow or Balance). After these die, no more spawn - that is your window to finish the boss.

[Heal] If all Aberrations are not defeated before the Aberrant Paradox, it will respawn with 5,775 Health. This also applies if the Wizard defeats the last Aberration on the turn the Aberrant Paradox is defeated.
[Minion][Afterlife] Defeating any of the initial Minion Aberrations in the specified slot will summon the following Minions in the same slot.
[Key] Aberration (Myth, Challenge) [Myth]
[Gem] Aberration (Death, Challenge) [Death]
[Spiral] Aberration (Life, Challenge) [Life]
[Minion][Afterlife] Defeating any of the second Minion Aberrations in the specified slot will summon the following Minions in the same slot. No more will spawn after this.

spellNotes: Casts Feint (Shadow) that applies a +70% Trap to all enemies and does not apply a Trap on Self.
Casts a Tatzlewurm Terror (Shadow) that has a chance to Confuse Wizards for two or three Rounds.
strategy: Bring your whole team in before Round 1. Joining late triggers a single-target Power Nova for your max Health that ignores all Resist and Wards, an instant near-kill.

Respect the 45% blanket resist and 137% boost. Nobody survives trading blows, so this is a stack-and-burst race: supports feed one hitter with blades and the Shadow Feint the Paradox already sets up on itself hits everyone, so use your own Feints too.
Read the active Global each phase. It boosts one school and weakens the opposite, so time your big hit for a Global that boosts your hitter's school and never swing into a weakened one.

The uncleanable phase cards are the trap: some help the Paradox, some hurt you. Discard-immune, reshuffle-immune. Play only the ones that help you and hold the harmful ones rather than dumping them onto your team.
Clear all Aberrations first. Killing minions spawns replacements in the same slot per the Afterlife chart, so grind the second wave down completely before touching the boss.

Do not leave any Aberration alive and do not kill the last one on the same turn the Paradox dies, or the Paradox respawns at 5,775 Health. Kill every minion a round early, then AoE the boss down clean.
cheatNotes: If anyone ports in after the fight starts, that wizard eats a solo Power Nova equal to their full Health with no resist or shields applying. Everyone must be present at the start.
A background Global buffs one school's damage and weakens the opposite school (Balance weakens all others). First one lasts two Rounds, the rest three. Attack under a Global that favors your hitter.
Each Global also hands every wizard a fixed card you cannot discard or reshuffle away. Some cards help the boss, some hurt you, so play only the useful ones.
If you swap out the current Global, the Paradox re-casts the previous one, so changing it is usually wasted effort.
The one exception: a spell like Gaze of Fate that changes the Global stops the recast. That is the only clean way to replace it.
If any Aberration is still alive when the Paradox dies, or you kill the last one on the same turn, the Paradox comes back at 5,775 Health. Clear all minions a round before the finisher.
Killing an initial Aberration in a slot summons a specific replacement in that same slot, so expect a second wave.
Killing a second-wave Aberration summons one final replacement per slot; nothing spawns after that wave.

spellNotes: Casts Feint (Shadow) that applies a +70% Trap to all enemies and does not apply a Trap on Self.
Casts a Tatzlewurm Terror (Shadow) that has a chance to Confuse Wizards for two or three Rounds.
strategy: Have all four wizards enter before Round 1. A late join takes a single-target Power Nova for its full max Health that ignores resist and every ward.

Each cycle the background casts a random school Global that boosts one school and weakens its opposite; the first lasts two Rounds, the rest three. Plan your hit for a window that favors your hitter's school.
You cannot discard the cursed cards it forces into your hand, and Reshuffle will not remove them, so play around them rather than trying to clear them.
Supports blade the hitter and stack Feints. The Paradox resists every school 26% with no weakness, so bring pierce or a prism and pile on charms and traps.
Clear all three Aberrations before or on the same Round as the Paradox; if one lives when the boss falls it respawns with 685 Health. Remember each slot respawns a new-school Aberration up to twice.
Fire the AoE finisher inside a boosted Global window so the Paradox and every Aberration die together.
cheatNotes: If you join after Round 1, the Paradox hits you for your full max Health, ignoring resist and all wards, so enter before combat starts.
The background sets a random school Global each cycle that boosts one school and weakens its opposite; the first lasts two Rounds, the rest three.
Each Global also drops an undiscardable card in your hand that helps the boss or hurts your team; Reshuffle will not clear it.
If you change the Global with your own, the Paradox simply recasts the one it wanted back.
The one way to strip a Global for good is an enemy-style swap like Gaze of Fate, which it will not recast.
If any Aberration is still alive when the Paradox dies, it respawns with 685 Health, so clear the minions first or on the same Round.
Killing an initial Aberration in a slot summons a new-school Aberration in that same slot.
Killing a second-wave Aberration summons one more in that slot; after this no further Aberrations spawn.

name: Admiral Bontau
cheats: "Midnight, cloud their vision!" - During Round 1 (and every 3rd or 4th Round thereafter), Admiral Bontau's Pet will cast a -50% Death version of Black Mantle on a random Wizard.
"Midnight, bring them to their knees!" - Sometimes, if a Wizard casts a single-target attack at Admiral Bontau, then he will cast Virulent Plague (-45%).
"Midnight, weaken that one!" - Alternating with the first cheat, Admiral Bontau's Pet casts a Death version of Weakness on a random Wizard.
strategy: Bring Fire or Life attacks. He takes +15% from both and resists Ice and Death 30%, so never hit into his own school.

Expect his pet to open with a -50% Death Black Mantle on round 1 and again every 3-4 rounds, plus a Death Weakness on the off rounds. Pack accuracy enchants or a Cleanse Charm so your setup and final hit do not fizzle.
Keep single-target attacks to your final blow. Any single-target hit makes him counter with a -45% Virulent Plague on you.
He has no minions and only 9800 health, so stack blades and a Feint for 2-3 rounds, then land one blade-stacked Fire or Life hit.
cheatNotes: His pet blinds a random wizard with -50% accuracy on round 1 and again every few rounds. Carry accuracy enchants or a Cleanse Charm so your setup and kill shot do not fizzle.
Hitting him with any single-target attack makes him drop a -45% Virulent Plague weakness on you, gutting your next hit. Do your damage in one blade-stacked shot rather than chipping.
On the rounds it is not casting Black Mantle, his pet throws a Death Weakness on a random wizard instead. Same fix: keep a Cleanse Charm ready or blade through it.

name: Admiral Dynt
locations: Empyrea: Nimbus Citadel - Control Room (Nimbus Citadel)
cheats: "Yar! What in the Seventy Skies Do You Scallywags Think You're Doing?!" - Boss will cast Grand Phoenix on Wizards who are late to join the duel.
"Good heads!" - At the end of each Round, Boss will say this and he casts one of the following Spells on the Wizard(s):
Bladestorm, Legion Shield (-30%), Precision, Rebirth, Spirit Armor (1,000), Sprite Swarm, Stunblock, Unicorn.
"Ye Barnacle-Brained Buffoons, Look at What Ye've Done!" At the end of the fifth Round, he will cast Wild Bolt on a random Wizard.
After casting Wild Bolt, Admiral Dynt uses Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps placed upon him.
After casting his Natural Attack, Admiral Dynt will receive a Blade based on which Natural Attack he used:
Admiral Dynt receives a +40% version of Fireblade after using his Fire Natural Attack.
Admiral Dynt receives a +30% version of Shadowblade after using his Shadow Natural Attack.
spellNotes: Casts an all-enemies version of Hex
Deals 120 Fire Damage per Pip for a maximum of 4 Pips
Deals 105 Shadow Damage per Pip for a maximum of 4 Pips
strategy: Join the duel on time — latecomers eat a Grand Phoenix before the fight even starts.
Hit with Ice, Life, or Balance only. He resists everything else at 70% and takes +5% from these three.

Let his end-of-round 'Good heads!' cheat work for you: every round he hands your team a free buff or heal (Bladestorm, Rebirth, Legion Shield, Stunblock, and more). You can afford a slower setup because of this.

Plan around round 5: at the end of it he casts Wild Bolt on a random wizard, then Cleanse Wards ALL traps off himself. Either finish before the end of round 5, or hold your Feints and traps until round 6.
He blades himself (+40% Fire or +30% Shadow) after each natural attack, so his damage climbs. Don't let the fight drag — stack Ice blades, place traps after his round-5 cleanse, and hit.
cheatNotes: Don't join late. Anyone who enters after the fight starts gets hit with Grand Phoenix immediately.
Free support every round: at the end of each round he casts one of a list of helpful spells (blades, shields, heals, stunblocks) on your team. This is pure upside — no counterplay needed.
At the end of round 5 a random wizard takes a Wild Bolt. Keep health topped up going into that round.
Right after the round-5 Wild Bolt he strips every trap off himself. Don't place Feints or traps before round 6 unless you're hitting before the end of round 5.
Every Fire natural attack he uses gives him a +40% Fireblade, so his next Fire hit gets much bigger.
Every Shadow natural attack gives him a +30% Shadowblade. His damage snowballs the longer the fight runs — kill before he stacks several.

name: Adrok
locations: Empyrea: Aeriel Shores - Beastman Fight Club
cheats: Adrok
[Late] "I'm gonna set you straight for bein' late" - If a Wizard enters the battle late, Adrok will cheat-cast Rocklops that Stuns for two Rounds, and places a -100% Accuracy Charm on the Wizard.
[Accuracy] "Check your head!" - If Adrok or an ally fizzles, he will cast Lightning Strike on the fizzler.
[Curse] "Leave Adrok alone!" - If a harmful Charm is cast on Yowck, Adrok will cast Cleanse Charm (Star) to remove it.
[Damage] After casting his Natural Attack, Adrok will cheat-cast Healing Current (325) on a random ally.
Adrok's Interrupt Cycle
[Minion] "Feel the Beet!" - At the end of every Round that the Boss does not have a Minion, Adrok will use a Moon Spell to summon a Beets Minion.

[Round] "This gonna hurt!" - If a Wizard has not been defeated two Rounds after the Beets Minion is summoned, and no Trap has been placed on Adrok in the last two Rounds, Adrok casts a Spell to instantly defeat the Beets Minion and a random Wizard.
"Eh, the beets are mmm.... stopped" - This cycle can be reset by casting a Trap on Adrok.
Prisms will not reset this cycle.
Beets
strategy: Enter on Round 1. Arriving late gives you Rocklops: a two-Round Stun plus a -100% Accuracy Charm that guarantees your next spell fizzles.
Attack Myth. Adrok takes +10% from Myth and resists Storm 75% and everything else 25%, so never hit Storm; prism if you are a Storm wizard.
Keep a fresh Trap on Adrok every two Rounds. Traps reset his beet-kill cycle and double as your damage setup, so make Feint the trap you keep replacing.
Keep accuracy high. Any fizzle on either side triggers Lightning Strike on the fizzler, so bring accuracy gear or sniper enchants.
Do not put weaknesses on Yowck. Adrok cleanses harmful charms off the ally, so it is wasted.
Stack blades and traps on your hitter, then AoE Myth to clear Adrok and the Beets minion together.
cheatNotes: Being late gets you Rocklops: a two-Round Stun and a -100% Accuracy Charm that fizzles your next cast. Come in on Round 1.
Any fizzle from Adrok's side or yours triggers Lightning Strike on the fizzler. Run accuracy so you never fizzle.
Casting a harmful charm on Yowck makes Adrok cleanse it off. Save your weaknesses for Adrok.
After his natural attack Adrok heals a random ally for 325. Minor, but factor it into your overkill.
Any Round Adrok has no minion, he summons a Beets minion at the end of the Round.
Two Rounds after Beets is summoned, if no one has died and no Trap has hit Adrok in the last two Rounds, he instantly kills Beets and one random Wizard. Keep a fresh Trap on him.
Casting any Trap on Adrok resets the beet-kill cycle, so your Feint setup doubles as the counter.
Prisms do not count as a Trap and will not reset the cycle. Use an actual Trap.

name: Aethyr Elemental
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Northeast Aero Plains Stone Key Room
cheats: "You're Late" - Casts Lord of Winter (Shadow) on any Wizard who joins after the second round of the duel. Each Boss that is present will cast this cheat.
Casting a Feint Spell will trigger casting of a double Cleanse Ward, which removes the last two Traps placed on the Boss.
Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Casting a Heal Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Snow Drift.
Casting a Damage Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Mass Triage.
Phase 1

Casting a Charm or Global Spell triggers casting of a Mass version of Enfeeble (Moon). This is also triggered by casting a negative Charm on the Boss. This cheat only applies for the Phase 1 Boss. Charms are allowed in Phase 2 and Phase 3.
Aegis can protect one Blade Spell.
Phase 2
After being defeated, this Boss splits into two Aethyr Elemental (16,000) Bosses.
Phase 3
The Boss will split again into two Aethyr Elemental (8,000) Bosses per each Aethyr Elemental (16,000) that is defeated.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Tower Shield (-55%) and Icy Shadowblade.
strategy: Run this as a 4-wizard speed team: Support 1, Support 2, a Storm secondary hitter, and a Fire primary hitter, entering in that order so the Fire hitter acts last. Flee and re-enter until your team goes first to dodge chain stuns, and give everyone one stun block.
Only Feint with the Balance version or protect it with Indemnity. A normal Feint triggers a double Cleanse Ward that strips your last two traps, so Support 1 opens with an Indemnity-protected Feint.
Never cast Heal-over-Time or Damage-over-Time spells: HoTs trigger Snow Drift and DoTs trigger Mass Triage. Keep healing to flat heals and skip poisons/DoT hits entirely.
In Phase 1 avoid casting Charms or Globals directly on the boss, since they trigger Mass Enfeeble (Moon). Set up traps and Aegis-protected blades instead; charms are safe again in Phases 2 and 3.
Phase 1 (R1-R4): stack Elemental Trap plus Fire Trap and Backdraft on the Fire hitter with Aegis on his blade, then finish the 32,000 boss with Scion of Fire.
Phase 2: the boss splits into two 16,000 copies. Blade the Storm hitter (elemental/storm/balance blades) and wipe both with Glowbug Squall or Storm Lord in one AoE.
Phase 3: each defeated 16,000 boss splits into two 8,000 copies. Re-blade and clear them with an AoE like Raging Bull or Meteor Strike so every copy dies the same round.
cheatNotes: If a wizard shows up after round 2, every boss present hits that latecomer with Lord of Winter. Enter on round 1 and stay through the fight.
A regular Feint makes the boss cleanse off the last two traps you placed, undoing your setup. Use the Balance Feint or protect it with Indemnity.
Putting Indemnity on your Feint stops the boss from cleansing it, so the trap sticks.
Mass Feint counts as a Feint here and still triggers the trap-removal cheat, so avoid it unprotected.
The Balance-school Feint is the exception and does not make the boss cleanse your traps, so it is the safe Feint to cast.
Casting any Heal-over-Time makes the boss cast Snow Drift. Stick to instant heals like Satyr or Pixie.
Casting any Damage-over-Time makes the boss cast Mass Triage, which wipes DoTs off itself. Do not rely on poison-style hits.
In Phase 1 only, casting a Charm, a Global, or a negative charm on the boss triggers Mass Enfeeble (Moon). Hold your bubbles and blades until Phase 2 or protect blades with Aegis.
Aegis on one blade lets that blade survive the Phase 1 Enfeeble cheat, so shield your hitter's key blade.
Killing the Phase 2 boss splits it into two 16,000-health copies, so line up an AoE to clear both at once.
Each 16,000 copy you defeat splits again into two 8,000 copies, so save an AoE finisher for Phase 3 rather than single-target hits.

name: Aethyr Elemental (16,000)
cheats: [Late] "You're Late" - Casts Lord of Winter (Shadow) on any Wizard who is late in joining the duel. Each Boss that is present will cast this cheat.
[Feint] Casting a Feint will trigger casting of a double Cleanse Ward.
Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
[Heal over Time] Casting a Heal Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Snow Drift.
strategy: Join before the duel starts. Any late wizard eats Lord of Winter, and every boss present casts it, so this stacks hard in a team.
Hit Fire or Life. He resists everything else heavily (70% Ice, 50% Shadow, 25% Storm/Myth/Death/Balance) but takes +5% from Fire and Life and does not resist them.
Set up with Feints, but cover each one with Indemnity. A raw Feint triggers a double Cleanse Ward that strips it right back off him.
Do not use Heal-over-Time spells. A HoT makes him cast Snow Drift. Use flat heals if you must heal.
He is not stunable and heals at 50% incoming, so anti-heal is less urgent than just landing one big protected Fire or Life hit. Stack blades, Indemnity-lock a Feint, then swing.
cheatNotes: Any wizard who joins after the fight begins gets hit with Lord of Winter (Shadow). Each boss on the field casts it separately, so a late joiner in a group can be hit multiple times. Enter from the start.
Placing a Feint on him triggers a double Cleanse Ward that removes the Feint you just cast. Your setup trap vanishes unless protected.
Indemnity on the Feint stops the double Cleanse Ward from removing it, so the trap survives for your hit.
Mass Feint also sets off the double Cleanse Ward, so it does not get around the cheat.
Casting any Heal-over-Time makes him cheat-cast Snow Drift. Heal with instant spells instead.

name: Aethyr Elemental (8,000)
cheats: "You're Late" - Casts Lord of Winter (Shadow) on any Wizard who is late in joining the duel. Each Boss that is present will cast this cheat.
Casting a Feint will trigger casting of a double Cleanse Ward.
Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
Casting a Heal Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Snow Drift.
Casting a Damage Over Time Spell will trigger the Boss to cast Mass Triage.
strategy: Enter the duel together and on time. Anyone who joins late eats a Lord of Winter from every boss present.
Build your deck around blades, not DoTs or heal-over-times. His cheats punish Feints, HoTs, and DoTs specifically; plain blades are safe.

If you want a Feint, protect it with Indemnity. An unprotected Feint triggers a double Cleanse Ward and gets wiped; the record confirms Indemnity keeps it on. Mass Feint triggers the cheat too, so it also needs protection.
Skip Rejuvenate-style heal-over-times. Each one triggers Snow Drift on you. Use flat heals like Satyr instead.
Skip damage-over-time attacks. Each one triggers Mass Triage, which just removes your DoT. Pick single-burst hits.
He is not stunable, so do not waste rounds on stuns. Stack blades, land one Indemnity-protected Feint, and end it with a single big hit.
cheatNotes: Join the fight from round 1. Every boss in the circle hits latecomers with Lord of Winter, so a late friend can get hit multiple times at once.
If you cast a normal Feint, he immediately casts Cleanse Ward twice and your Feint is gone. Unprotected traps are wasted pips here.
Put Indemnity on your Feint and it survives the double Cleanse Ward. This is the only safe way to trap him.
Mass Feint counts too. Casting it triggers the same double Cleanse Ward, so protect it or skip it.
Any heal-over-time (Rejuvenate-type spell) makes him cast Snow Drift on you. Stick to instant heals.
Any damage-over-time spell makes him cast Mass Triage, which strips the DoT off him. Use single-hit attacks instead.

name: Aethyr Titan
cheats: The Divine Paradox is in a Star School Polymorph throughout this duel, with the given Spells:
Fire Cat (0 Pip)
Headless Horseman (0 Pip)
Ice Wyvern (0 Pip)
Loremaster (0 Pip)
Minotaur (0 Pip)
Sacred Charge (0 Pip)
Stormzilla (99,999)
At the beginning of the duel, the Paradox Wizard casts a Star Spell which places an Aura that gives the Wizard masteries of all 7 schools (Balance, Fire, Ice, Storm, Death, Myth, and Life).
The Paradox Wizard and the Boss gain seven Power Pips every Round.
Henchmen summoned in this duel will also gain seven Power Pips every Round.
Global Spells will be removed if they are cast by Henchmen.
If the Wizard starts with or has low hit points, they will cast Dryad that restores them back to full Health.

When the Boss goes down to about 15% of his current Health, he restores his Health back to 100%, and invisible polymorphs the Paradox Wizard that lasts 99 Rounds, allowing to use either of the following Spells to finish the Boss:
Scion of Elements
Scion of Spirits
spellNotes: All Aethyr Titan's spells deal 9,999 Damage to target and cost 14 Pips.
These Shadow spells deal damage based on their School and are not Shadow Damage.
Frost Giant and Medusa only can be cast on the first turn.
strategy: Bring a full team. The Titan has 500,000 health and every spell he casts deals 9,999 damage for 14 pips.
Do not stress over survival. If anyone drops low, the fight script casts Dryad and restores them to full health.
Supports blade the hitter every round while the Paradox ally chips in with its free 0-pip spells. The opening aura grants mastery of all seven schools, so everyone's power pips count for any school.
Do not have henchmen cast globals; the script removes them. Henchmen do get seven power pips per round though, so they hit hard here.

Grind the Titan down toward 15% of his health. He and the Paradox wizard gain seven power pips every round, so expect a 9,999-damage spell from him almost every round and keep your own damage constant.
At about 15% he heals back to 100% and you are invisibly polymorphed. That is the win signal, not a setback: cast the granted Scion of Elements or Scion of Spirits to finish him.
cheatNotes: You fight alongside the Divine Paradox, who is locked in a Star polymorph with a fixed set of spells for the whole duel.
The opening aura gives the Paradox wizard mastery of all seven schools, so power pips work for any school's spells all fight.
The boss and the Paradox wizard each gain seven power pips every round. He can afford his 14-pip, 9,999-damage spells nearly every round.
Henchmen you summon also gain seven power pips per round, which makes them unusually strong in this duel.
Globals cast by henchmen are removed. Do not count on henchmen for bubbles.
If you start low or drop low, the script casts Dryad and restores you to full health. Deaths are largely prevented, so do not panic-heal.
At about 15% health the Titan heals back to 100% and invisibly polymorphs you for 99 rounds. This is the finisher phase: cast Scion of Elements or Scion of Spirits to end the fight.

name: Akbari
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Kur will place a protected Myth Trap (+35%) on the Wizard.
strategy: Enter on time. The only cheat here punishes late joiners with a protected +35% Myth Trap, and with his 70% damage boost that trap makes his Myth hits hurt.
Hit with Storm - he takes +15% from it. Avoid Myth attacks into his 30% resist.
He is stunable and beguilable, so stuns are a legitimate way to buy setup rounds.
Stack blades and a Feint over 2-4 rounds, then finish with one big Storm hit. Nothing in the fight interferes with standard setup.
cheatNotes: Only triggers on wizards who join after the fight starts: they get a protected +35% Myth Trap placed on them, boosting his next Myth hit on that wizard. Join from the start and this cheat never happens.

name: Akheilos the Contriver (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea (Challenge) - Hall of the Contriver (Challenge)
cheats: [Curse] "Your weakness is wasted!"- If a harmful Charm is cast on Akheilos the Contriver, he will cast his Natural Attack on the caster.
strategy: Pack Storm damage. He takes boosted Storm and resists Myth 80%, so never hit into his own school.
Do not cast weaknesses or any harmful Charm. Each one makes him hit the caster with his Natural Attack.
Build damage with blades and traps instead of curses; these do not trigger the retaliation.
Set up over 2-3 rounds, then finish with one big Storm AoE so the Storm Shark minion dies with him.
He is not stunable, so plan around his turns rather than trying to lock him down.
cheatNotes: Any weakness or harmful Charm you place on Akheilos makes him retaliate with his Natural Attack on you. Set up with blades and traps instead of curses.

name: Akhtang WormCrawl
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Upper Halls
cheats: "I rip it from you!" - If a Wizard casts a Charm or Global Spell, Akhtang WormCrawl will cast Steal Charm on the recipient of the Charm or the caster of the Global Spell.
Akhtang will steal only the last Blade of Elemental Blade (Fire) or Spirit Blade (Life).
Akhtang will steal only the caster's Blade from a Bladestorm.
strategy: Bring an Ice or Storm hitter. Akhtang takes +30% from both and resists Fire 80%, so never hit him with Fire.
He steals your buffs: every Charm or Global triggers Steal Charm on the recipient or caster. Aegis your hitter's key blade, or set up and hit in the same round so there is nothing to steal.

Exploit his blade-steal limits: he only takes the last Elemental Blade or the caster's blade from Bladestorm. Stack a plain Stormblade or Frostblade under an Elemental Blade so he grabs the throwaway and your real blade survives.
Cast Bladestorm from a support, not the hitter, since only the caster loses a copy.
Supports Feint the boss (traps are safe) and pass pips; the hitter unloads one big Ice or Storm AoE to clear the Draconian Eidolon and Akhtang together.
cheatNotes: Every blade, charm, or global you cast lets him steal it back immediately. Aegis the hitter's key blade, or set up and hit the same round.
He only grabs the last Elemental Blade or Spirit Blade, so stack a plain Stormblade or Frostblade on top and he steals the throwaway while your real blade stays.
Bladestorm only loses the caster's own copy, so cast it from a support to blade the hitter safely.

name: Ali Baboon
locations: Mirage: Thieves' Den - Ali Baboon's Private Lounge
cheats: "You are late. How embarrassing" - If a Wizard is late to combat, he will interrupt-cast a Cloaked version of a negative Charm on all Wizards.
"Now, you made Ali Baboon angry" - At the beginning of Round 1, he will cast a Sun Spell that summons one of the following Gobblorean Guard Minions:
After defeating his first minion, he will cast a +45% in / +35% out version of Berserk. Berserk is only cast the first time a minion is defeated.
At the start of every Round that Ali doesn't have a minion he will resummon another one of a random type.
"Pick on Ali Baboon, not his men!" - If a Gobblorean Guard is attacked, he will cast an Aegis-protected Mander Armor and Healing Current on the Minion.
Hitting Ali with Spells that have an all enemies secondary effect, such as Winged Sorrow, will still activate this cheat.
"Here, consider this a gift" - After casting his Natural Attack, he will interrupt-cast a Cloaked version of a negative Charm on all Wizards.
Spell used to be a -55% version of Smoke Screen, but now the spell may be bugged.
"Such tricks won't work on me" - If a Feint is placed on Ali Baboon, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps from himself .
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent removal.
strategy: Pack Mass Feint (+75%) — it is the centerpiece here. A normal Feint makes him Cleanse Ward all traps off himself; Mass Feint does not trigger the cheat at all. Alternatively, Indemnity-protect single Feints.
Be on time. Late wizards get a Cloaked negative charm cast on the whole team.

Leave his Gobblorean Guards alone. Attacking one (even with a secondary all-enemies effect like Winged Sorrow) makes him Aegis a Mander Armor and Healing Current onto it, and he resummons a new guard every round anyway. Ignore them and build.

Killing his first minion triggers a one-time Berserk (+45% incoming / +35% outgoing) on him — he takes 45% more damage afterward. Time this for your kill turn: hit with a big AoE so the minion dies and the boosted hit lands together, or accept it as a bonus on the final AoE.
Hit with Ice — he takes +15% from it and resists Fire 30%.
Expect a Cloaked negative charm on your team after each of his natural attacks; keep a Cleanse Charm handy for your hitter's turn.
cheatNotes: Anyone who joins the fight late causes a hidden (Cloaked) negative charm to land on every wizard.
On round 1 he summons one Gobblorean Guard (Balance, Death, or Storm). You cannot prevent this.
The first time one of his minions dies, he gains a Berserk that makes him take +45% damage but deal +35% more. Net win for you — your kill hit gets a big boost. It only happens once.
He never runs out of guards — any round he starts without a minion, he summons a new random one. Don't waste turns clearing them.
If you attack a Guard, he gives it an Aegis-protected Mander Armor plus Healing Current. Attacking minions directly is a losing trade.
Even AoE side-effects count as attacking the Guard — spells like Winged Sorrow with an all-enemies secondary effect still trigger the protection cheat.
After each of his natural attacks he puts a Cloaked negative charm on all wizards. Assume you have a hidden weakness before big hits.
A standard Feint on him triggers Cleanse Ward, wiping ALL traps off him — including ones you placed earlier.
Pet-cast Feints also set off the trap wipe, so consider benching Feint pets.
Mass Feint (+75%) does NOT trigger the cleanse. This is the safe way to Feint him — build your whole trap plan around it.
Indemnity on a regular Feint also stops the removal if you don't have Mass Feint.

name: Alpha Wolf

stats.resist: 38% to all schools68% to all schools

cheats: [Aura] "Don't even try to wear me down!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Alpha Wolf will cast a Domination Aura on himself for 99 Rounds that reduces all incoming Damage to 1.
To remove the Aura, Wizards must pass through three phases by placing the following hanging effects on Alpha Wolf:
Phase 1: Four Damage over Time effects
Phase 2: Four harmful Wards
Phase 3: Four harmful Charms

To move to the next phase, all four hanging effects must be active at the same time. If any of the necessary hanging effects are removed before all four are placed (ie.: the first Damage over Time expiring before a fourth can be placed), the phase will not change.
Attempting to remove this Aura without passing through the phases will cause Alpha Wolf to cast Exit Combat on the caster.
[Heal over Time] Phase 1:
"Ha! I supplement my Health!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Alpha Wolf will restore 11 Health to himself, and apply three Heal over Time effects that each restore 111 Health over 50 Rounds.
"You know, these little bites are annoying." - Whenever a Damage over Time effect is applied on Alpha Wolf during this phase, he will say this once.

"No... not my supplements!" - Once four or more Damage over Time effects are on Alpha Wolf at the same time, he will remove all hanging effects on himself and deal 25% of his max Health (4,051 Damage) to himself.
Removing the Heal over Time effects other than by placing four or more Damage over Times on Alpha Wolf will cause him to recast his Heal over Times.
[Shield] Phase 2:

"You won't trick me again! I'm too smart for any trap!" - After Alpha Wolf removes the three Heal over Time effects from himself, he will restore 11 Health to himself and apply three -50% Damage Wards on himself.
"Gah! Who put that there?" - Whenever a harmful Ward is applied on Alpha Wolf during this phase, he will say this once.

"Enough games! I'll just dominate you" - Once four or more harmful Wards are on Alpha Wolf at the same time, he will remove all hanging effects on himself and deal 25% of his max Health (4,051 Damage) to himself.
Removing the -50% Damage Wards other than by placing four or more harmful Wards on Alpha Wolf will cause him to recast his -50% Damage Wards.
[Blade] Phase 3:
"Weakling." - After Alpha Wolf removes the three -50% Damage Wards from himself, he will restore 11 Health to himself and apply three +50% Damage Charms on himself.
Despite their appearance, these Charms will not be used to increase the Damage of Alpha Wolf's attacks.
"Whoa! Where'd my muscles go?" - Whenever a harmful Charm is placed on Alpha Wolf during this phase, he will say this once.

"No! You found my only weakness! That's cheating!" - Once four or more harmful Charms are on Alpha Wolf at the same time, he will remove all hanging effects on himself and deal 25% of his max Health (4,051 Damage) to himself.
After the third phase is completed, Alpha Wolf will cast a Dominated Aura on himself which will allow Wizards to deal more than 1 Damage.
strategy: Pack multi-target Damage-over-Time spells, harmful Wards, and harmful Charms. Do not try to strip his Domination Aura directly or he casts Exit Combat and boots you.
Phase 1: land four Damage-over-Time effects and keep all four active at the same time. He then wipes his effects and self-deals 4,051.
Phase 2: stack four harmful Wards on him simultaneously. He wipes and self-deals another 4,051.
Phase 3: stack four harmful Charms on him simultaneously. He wipes, self-deals another 4,051, and drops the Domination Aura.
With the aura gone, damage finally lands normally. Blade up and burst him down; he takes +30% from Life and resists Death 68%.
Team the phases: assign who lands each set of four so all four are up before any expire, then the hitter finishes once he is dominatable.
cheatNotes: At round 1 he auras himself so all incoming damage is capped at 1. You cannot hurt him until it is removed the intended way.
The only way to drop the aura is to clear three phases: four DoTs, then four harmful Wards, then four harmful Charms, in order.
Phase 1 gate: you need four Damage-over-Time effects on him at once.
Phase 2 gate: you need four harmful Wards on him at once.
Phase 3 gate: you need four harmful Charms on him at once.
All four of a phase must be active simultaneously. If one expires before the fourth lands, the phase does not advance.
Trying to remove the aura without doing the phases makes him Exit Combat the caster, removing them from the fight.
He heals himself 11 and applies three Heal-over-Times at round 1; the phase-1 goal is to overwrite these with your four DoTs.
Once four or more DoTs are on him he removes all his effects and deals 4,051 (25% max) to himself, advancing to phase 2.
If you strip his HoTs any other way, he simply recasts them, so beat them with DoTs rather than cleansing.
Entering phase 2 he heals 11 and shields himself with three -50% Damage Wards.
Once four or more harmful Wards are on him he wipes everything and self-deals 4,051, advancing to phase 3.
Removing his -50% Wards any other way makes him recast them; place your four harmful Wards instead.
Entering phase 3 he heals 11 and gives himself three +50% Damage Charms.
Those charms are decorative; he never uses them to boost his attacks, so ignore them.
Once four or more harmful Charms are on him he wipes everything and self-deals a final 4,051.
After phase 3 he auras himself so wizards can finally deal more than 1 damage; now go for the kill.

name: Alyssa Crowly
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor (Location) - Castle Darkmoor Dungeon
cheats: [Dispel] "Behold! The Mighty Magic of Beyond!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Alyssa Crowly will cast a Dispel Charm of the Wizard's School on each Wizard.
It has the visual appearance of a +100% Damage Charm of the Wizard's School.
"Why... isn't this working?" - Alyssa Crowly will say this if one of her Dispels are triggered.
strategy: Round 1 she casts a Dispel of your school on each wizard, disguised to look like a +100% Damage Charm. Do not treat it as a blade - your next spell of that school will fizzle.
Burn the Dispel first with a cheap same-school cast (a wand hit or 0-1 pip spell). She says "Why... isn't this working?" when it triggers harmlessly.
Only after the Dispel is gone should you start stacking blades and traps as normal.
Hit with Storm - she resists Myth and takes 30% more from Storm. Prism if you are off-school.
Feint plus blades on one hitter, then a single big hit to close it out.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she puts a Dispel of YOUR school on each wizard; your next spell of that school fizzles. Clear it with a cheap same-school cast before your real hit.
The Dispel is disguised as a +100% Damage Charm of your school. It is not a blade - do not build around it.

name: Ammit the Devourer
locations: Krokotopia: Upper Zigazag - House of Scales
cheats: "Why don't we redirect all that potential?" - At the end of every Round, Ammit the Devourer will cast a Spell that deals 300 Death Damage to herself or one of her Pit Vipers, then Heal herself for 100% of the Damage dealt.
Negative Healing Charms do not affect this healing.
If all of the Pit Vipers have been defeated, this cheat will not be triggered.
"Ammit is the Judge of Souls now!" - At the beginning of every second Round, she will cast a 0-Pip Judgement on a random Wizard, which deals 1,000 Balance Damage.

"The Condemned Serve the Mighty Ammit! / Behold the Mighty Ranks of the Army of the Condemned!" -When a Pit Viper has low Health, it may cheat-cast Brilliant Light, followed by Ammit cheat-casting Sap Health on the Minion, which deals 2,735 Balance Damage.

"Ammit's Rebellion Will Never Fail! / Soldiers of the Condemned, Protect Your Queen!" -If a Pit Viper has low Health, it may cheat-cast Donate Power, followed by Ammit cheat-casting Take Power on the Minion, which deals 2,375 Fire Damage.
If a Wizard places a negative Charm on Ammit, she will cheat-cast Sacrifice on herself.
Unbalance does not trigger this cheat.
Target All Enemy Spells such as Plague and Virulent Plague do not trigger this cheat.
This cheat can also occur when a Spirit Shield is cast on Ammit. It's unclear what exact conditions are required. Death Prism and / or Feint seem to be related.
strategy: Pack Ice or Life hits — she takes +35% from both — and skip Feint entirely: any negative charm on Ammit triggers Sacrifice, healing her.

Kill the Pit Vipers first, and kill them fast. While any viper lives she heals 300 every round off her end-of-round drain, and a viper left at low health can feed her a 2,735 or 2,375 damage cheat combo.
Burst each viper from healthy to dead in one hit so it never sits at low health.
With the vipers gone the round-end healing stops. Now blade up (blades on yourself are safe), use ordinary traps if you like but no Feint, no weaknesses.
Budget healing for the 0-pip Judgement she throws at a random wizard every second round for 1,000 Balance damage — keep everyone above that line.
Hit with a big Ice or Life attack. Her 80% Death resist makes Death hits pointless without a prism.
cheatNotes: Every round she chips 300 Death damage off herself or a Pit Viper and heals herself for all of it. While vipers are up this is a free 300 heal per round for her.
Infection-style negative healing charms do nothing against this cheat — you cannot shut it down that way.
Kill all the Pit Vipers and the round-end drain-heal stops completely. That is the reason to clear minions first.
Every second round a random wizard takes a free 1,000 Balance damage Judgement. Keep health topped so nobody drops to it.
A viper at low health can cast Brilliant Light and feed Ammit a 2,735 Balance damage Sap Health. Never leave a viper barely alive — finish it in one hit.
Same idea with Donate Power: a low-health viper can feed her a 2,375 Fire damage Take Power. Burst vipers, don't chip them.
If you put a weakness, Feint, or any negative charm on Ammit, she casts Sacrifice on herself and heals. Blade yourself instead of trapping her with charms.
Unbalance is the one negative charm that slips through without triggering the Sacrifice.
AoE debuffs like Plague and Virulent Plague are also safe — they do not trigger the Sacrifice.
Spirit Shields (and possibly Death Prism or Feint interactions) have also set off the Sacrifice. Safest play: put nothing on Ammit except plain traps you are about to use.

name: Angrus Hollowsoul
locations: Wizard City: Colossus Boulevard - Briskbreeze Tower
cheats: At the start of Round 2, Angrus will interrupt to use Weakness on a random Wizard.
Although this Weakness is not cast, it can be dispelled.
At the end of every fourth Round, an Exploding Ember will join the duel and become active two Rounds later.
If Wizards flee and return, then the Exploding Ember(s) will remain.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Fire Damage per Pip.
strategy: Bring Ice hits. He takes +50% from Ice and resists Fire at 80%, so Fire wizards should pack an Ice off-school hit or a prism.
Pack a Cleanse Charm or a Fire dispel answer. At the start of Round 2 he puts a Weakness on a random wizard; clear it before your big hit.
Work fast. At the end of every fourth round an Exploding Ember joins the fight and goes active two rounds later. Every extra cycle adds another one.
He is stunable, so a stun can buy a safe setup round if the Embers are stacking up.
Aim to blade up in rounds 1-3 and kill with an Ice AoE by round 4-5, so the boss and any pending Ember die before the adds snowball. Fleeing does not reset the Embers, so do not bank on a restart.
cheatNotes: At the start of Round 2 he interrupts and slaps a Weakness on one random wizard. If it lands on your hitter, cleanse it before hitting.
Even though the Weakness is not formally cast, it can still be dispelled or removed like a normal one.
Every fourth round an Exploding Ember minion joins, then starts acting two rounds later. The longer the fight runs, the more Embers pile up.
Fleeing and re-entering does not clear the Embers. You cannot reset the fight by leaving, so just kill him fast.

name: Annoushka

stats.resist: 40% to all schools70% to all schools

cheats: [Damage] "Little doll fights you all. In the end you all will fall!" - At the end of the first Round, Annoushka will use a Moon school version of Poison that deals 50% max Health Damage to all Wizards and does not apply a Damage over Time.
[Round] "I have something for you. Try some!" - At the end of the second Round, and every Round after, Annoushka will cast a version of Poison that does regular Damage to all Wizards.

[Damage] [Polymorph] "Don't leave. Be like me. It's not so fun... is it?" - If two or more Wizards are present in combat and a Wizard is defeated in battle, Annoushka will revive, beguile, and polymorph that Wizard into a Doll for 99 Rounds.
If one Wizard is present in battle and has an active Minion when they are defeated, this cheat will trigger.
Minions cannot be Polymorphed by this cheat.

[Round] "All I've ever wanted was to destroy you!" - If a Wizard is currently polymorphed, Annoushka will activate this cheat. It is unclear what this cheat does and further testing will be necessary.
strategy: Attack with Life. She takes +30% Life and resists Death 70% and every other school 40%, so Life is your only efficient damage.
Top off before the end of Round 1. She hits the whole team with a Moon Poison worth 50% of each wizard's max Health with no lingering DOT, so do not enter Round 2 low.
From Round 2 on she poisons the team every round for normal damage. Bring a healer or heal-over-time to soak the steady drip.
Keep everyone alive at all costs. If any wizard dies with two or more present, she revives and turns that wizard into a Doll for 99 Rounds, effectively removing them from the fight.
Blade and Feint onto one hitter, then close with a big Life hit. She is not stunable, so you cannot skip her poison turns.
Treat any polymorph as a loss condition, not something to play through, since her follow-up cheat on a Dolled wizard is unconfirmed.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1 everyone takes a Moon-based Poison worth 50% of their max Health with no damage-over-time. Heal up first and do not enter Round 2 low.
From Round 2 onward she poisons the whole team every round for normal damage, so bring sustained healing or a heal-over-time.
With two or more wizards present, if one dies she revives and polymorphs that wizard into a Doll for 99 Rounds, taking them out of the fight. Keep everyone alive.
Soloing does not save you: dying with an active minion out triggers the same Doll polymorph on you.
Your own minions are immune to the Doll polymorph.
Once a wizard is a Doll she runs an additional, untested cheat, so reset rather than play a polymorph out.

name: Anubis
cheats: "Offer your Soul unto Death." - At the beginning of the first Round, if a Wizard has less than 100% Max Health, Anubis will cast a version of Blessing, restoring the Wizard to 100% max Health.
"Clench tight your fist with MAGIC!" - Anubis will say this at the end of the second Round.
"I shall feast on your final breath." - Each time a Wizard attacks Anubis after the second Round, he will give all Wizards a "Hunger of Anubis".
[Hunger of Anubis]
Power Cycle: Anubis will gain an Aura that decreases incoming Damage and decreases in power as Wizard's Health is lowered.
"Death demands tribute!" - If Anubis' Aura is removed, he will cast a version of Anubis on the Wizard that heals them for 4,800 Health, then recast the Aura.
"Let your spirit fade and feed my power." - If a Wizard is above 70% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -100%" -100% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
"Yes, embrace death." - If a Wizard is between 50% and 70% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -70%" -70% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
"Good. Your spirit will be delicious." - If a Wizard is between 20% and 50% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -50%" -50% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.

"Yes, I see your spirit is true.": After this cheat has been triggered, Anubis can now be defeated. If he is defeated before this, he will respawn with 114 Health. Only one Wizard must be below this threshold to be able to defeat Anubis.
If a Wizard is between 10% and 20% max Health, Anubis will cast a "Power: -20%" -20% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
If a Wizard is below 10% max Health, Anubis will cast a +100% incoming Damage Aura for 99 Rounds.
strategy: Hit with Life — he takes +30% from it and resists every other school at 43% or more (73% to Death).

His whole gimmick is your health bar. His Power aura blocks 100% of your damage while you are above 70% health, 70% at 50-70%, 50% at 20-50%, and 20% at 10-20%. Below 10% max health it flips to +100% incoming damage on him.
He heals you to full on round 1, then let his hits drop you. Do not heal, and do not remove his aura — removal triggers a 4,800 heal on you plus a fresh aura, undoing your progress downward.
Every attack after round 2 hands all wizards a Hunger of Anubis card, so keep your attack count low: stack blades, then throw one big Life hit.

Only deliver the kill once a wizard is below 10% max health — one wizard below the threshold qualifies the whole team. If you kill him too early he respawns, but with just 114 health, so a wand hit finishes the job.
cheatNotes: If you enter below full health, he restores you to 100% on round 1. You cannot pre-lower your health before the fight.
Voice line at the end of round 2. It marks the point where attacking him starts triggering Hunger of Anubis.
From round 3 on, every attack against him gives all wizards a Hunger of Anubis card. Fewer, bigger hits mean fewer cards.
His aura reduces the damage he takes, and it weakens as your health drops. Managing your own health bar is the fight.
Do not strip his aura. He answers by healing the wizard for 4,800 — pushing you back up the health brackets — and recasting the aura.
Above 70% max health his aura blocks 100% of incoming damage. You literally cannot hurt him while healthy.
Between 50% and 70% health he still shrugs off 70% of your damage.
Between 20% and 50% health the aura drops to -50%. Better, but keep going lower.
Until this triggers he cannot truly die — killed early, he respawns, though with only 114 health. Get one wizard below the low-health threshold before the kill hit; one wizard qualifies everyone.
Between 10% and 20% health the aura only blocks 20% of your damage.
Below 10% max health the aura flips to +100% incoming damage on Anubis. This is your kill window — stay low and hit hard.

name: Apep the Snaky One
locations: Krokotopia: Upper Zigazag - House of Scales
cheats: "Lets make this interesting." - At the beginning of the first Round, Apep will interrupt cast a Balance of Power.
"You'll have to do better than that!" - If a Wizard casts a Global Spell that gives a Damage bonus, such as Balefrost, he will immediately replace it back with Power Play.
Doom and Gloom, and Sanctuary will trigger this Cheat.
Power Play will not trigger this Cheat.
Damage Spells that cast a Global effect, such as White Rat Magician, will not trigger this Cheat.
Global Spells that give Critical and Armor Piercing effects, such as Katabatic Wind, will not trigger this Cheat.
Apep's Pit Viper Minions have 1,870 Health, instead of their normal 2,190 Health.They Boost to Fire, Ice, and Storm Damage, rather than their original Life, Myth, and Death Damage.

If a Pit Viper is defeated (or is missing at the start of the battle due to being defeated in an earlier attempt), Apep will summon a Minion with 1,150 Health. This Minion will attack for two Rounds, before Apep sacrifices it with Draw Health, Sap Power, Siphon Health, or Take Power.
When Apep uses one of his Minion Sacrifice Spells, he casts the Spell from first to last once. He then hidden casts the Spells two more times on each Minion.
When Dispelled once, it skips over the first Minion, but after others are sacrificed, the Minion he skipped over will still be sacrificed.
When with all his Minions, it takes 7 Dispels to save one, 8 to save 2, and 9 to save all 3.
When one Minion is in combat with two other Minions, it takes 3 Dispels to save him.
When two Minions are in combat with one other Minion, it takes 6 Dispels to save them.

The healing Spells will heal Apep even if the Minion he is trying to sacrifice is already dead (so if all three Pit Vipers have been defeated then he will be healed nine times). This may be a bug. The Pip-gaining Spells will only give Apep Pips once per Minion.
After one more Round, Apep will summon one of the following Minions (to be replaced in order):
strategy: Skip damage globals entirely. Anything like Balefrost gets replaced with Power Play immediately, and even Doom and Gloom or Sanctuary trigger the swap. If you want a global, Katabatic Wind-style crit/pierce globals are safe.

Do not kill the Pit Vipers early. Every dead viper starts a summon-and-sacrifice loop that feeds Apep heals and pips, and his healing sacrifices land even on dead minions - with all three vipers gone he can heal nine times per cycle.
Ignore the dispel counterplay. Saving all three minions from one sacrifice cycle costs 9 dispels; it is never worth the pips.
He has no resist and no boost, so any school works. Blade for 2-3 rounds while wand-hitting or shielding.

Finish with one AoE that kills Apep and both vipers in the same round, so the sacrifice loop never starts. The vipers only have 1,870 health but boost to Fire, Ice, and Storm - those schools clear them easily.
cheatNotes: He opens the fight with a free interrupt Balance of Power on round 1. Expect the hit before you act; nothing to counter, just budget for the damage.
Cast a damage-boosting global like Balefrost and he instantly replaces it with Power Play. Your global is wasted pips - do not bring damage globals.
Doom and Gloom and Sanctuary also get swapped for Power Play, so healing globals are wasted too.
Casting Power Play yourself does not trigger anything - it is the one global he tolerates.
Attack spells that happen to set a global, like White Rat Magician, are safe and will not trigger the swap.
Crit and pierce globals like Katabatic Wind are safe. If you want a global up, use one of these.
His Pit Vipers have 1,870 health here (down from 2,190) and boost to Fire, Ice, and Storm instead of Life, Myth, and Death. An elemental AoE shreds them.

Each dead viper gets replaced by a weaker 1,150-health minion that attacks for two rounds before Apep sacrifices it with Draw Health, Sap Power, Siphon Health, or Take Power - free healing and pips for him. This is why you save minion kills for your final AoE.
When he sacrifices, he casts the spell once openly, then hidden-casts it two more times on each minion - the real value he gains is triple what you see.
One dispel makes him skip the first minion, but that minion still gets sacrificed after the others. Dispels delay, they do not prevent.
With all three minions up it takes 7 dispels to save one, 8 to save two, and 9 to save all three. Not worth the pips - just kill him faster.
With one minion left it takes 3 dispels to save it.
With two minions left it takes 6 dispels to save them.

His sacrifice heals go through even when the target minion is already dead - all three vipers dead means nine free heals per cycle. This is the strongest reason to kill the vipers and Apep in the same hit.
A round after a sacrifice cycle he summons a replacement minion, so the loop repeats until he is dead.

name: Apollo Bright One (Mount Olympus, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Mount Olympus (Challenge) - Sun Chamber (Challenge)
cheats: [Jinx] "I am too bright for that" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward on Apollo, Apollo will cast Lifeblade on himself.
[Damage over Time] "Interesting Idea, But I can do better" - Whenever a Wizard casts a DoT on Apollo, Apollo will cast Link on the Caster.
spellNotes: Casts a Spell version of Cleansing Blade.
Casts a version of Natural Attack (Apollo) that doesn't apply an accuracy debuff.
strategy: Attack with Storm or Death; Apollo takes +13% from both and resists Fire and Myth 27%. He is not stunable, so plan to burst.
Do not cast traps on him. A harmful Ward triggers a free Lifeblade on himself, so set up with blades on your hitter instead of Feint.
Skip damage-over-time spells. A DoT makes him Link the caster, which chips you and heals him.
He has only 2,170 health and brings one Elite Cyclops Guard, so stack two or three blades and end it with a single Storm or Death AoE that clears the minion too.
cheatNotes: Casting a harmful Ward on Apollo (a trap like Feint) makes him Lifeblade himself. Skip trap setup here and blade your hitter instead.
Casting a damage-over-time on Apollo makes him Link the caster, a DoT that also heals him. Use direct damage, not DoTs.

name: Apollo Bright One (Zeus Exalted Challenge)
cheats: "Wither under the Sun's rage!" / "The Seas boil beneath the angry Sun!" At the beginning of the first Round, Apollo will cast his Natural Attack that deals Damage to all Wizards, then gives DoT to all Wizards, and healing over time to all Bosses.
"The light shines upon those who share it." - If any type of AoE Healing Spell is used, Apollo will give the Wizard who cast the spell a Guiding Light (+40%).
Pet heals will also trigger this cheat.

"You try to smite an Immortal?” - If a Boss is either defeated or reduced to very low health, Apollo will cheat-cast Eternal Leviathan (777) on the Wizard that cast the attack, reviving any defeated Bosses.
spellNotes: Deals 30 then 780 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds to all Wizards and 525 Heal to all Bosses over 3 Rounds.
strategy: This is a multi-boss challenge (19,200 health, healing over time on all bosses), so run it as a team: one hitter, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer to manage the round-1 damage-over-time.
Attack with Fire or Death (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Life resist without a prism.
Round 1 he lands a Fire DoT (30 then 780 over 3 rounds) on the whole team and heals all bosses 525 over 3 rounds, so pack Triage and open with setup, not a rushed hit.
Do not pick bosses off one at a time: at very low health or a single kill he revives all downed bosses and blasts the killer with Eternal Leviathan (777).
Stack blades and Feints across rounds, then burst every boss dead in the SAME round with a shared AoE so the revive cheat never fires.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he hits the whole team with a Fire DoT (30 then 780 over 3 rounds) and heals all bosses 525 over 3 rounds. Bring Triage and expect chip damage before you can set up.
Any AoE heal hands the caster a Guiding Light (+40% to their next heal) — this is harmless, even helpful, so don't avoid group heals out of fear.
Pet may-cast heals also grant that Guiding Light, so the same harmless buff applies.
If any boss is defeated or dropped to very low health, Apollo revives all downed bosses and hits the killer with Eternal Leviathan (777). Kill every boss in one shared round instead of one at a time.

name: Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm)
locations: Wallaru: Outback - Outback Ridge,Wobbegong Territory - Bunyip Warrens
cheats: "Sweet dreams, wizard." - Arcane Toad casts a Spell that Stuns a Wizard for 2 Rounds, and then places a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on them.

"One, two, here's a nightmare for you!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has the Nightmare Prone mark, Arcane Toad casts Winged Sorrow on the marked Wizard, and then casts Cleanse Ward on them.
strategy: Pack stun blocks. His signature cheat stuns a wizard for 2 rounds and marks them 'Nightmare Prone' — a blocked stun is two saved turns.
Once someone is marked, that wizard eats a Winged Sorrow at the end of every round, followed by a Cleanse Ward that strips their wards. Don't waste shields on the marked player; heal them instead.
He shifts into Bunferatu and Grey Wolf forms, and brings an Air Rider and a Death Punk Wallaby. Blade up fast and end it with one AoE so the minions die with him.
Keep the fight short — every extra round is another stun window and another Winged Sorrow on the marked wizard.
cheatNotes: He stuns one wizard for 2 full rounds and tags them with a 'Nightmare Prone' mark. Stun block prevents the lockout; the mark is what feeds his other cheat.

Any wizard carrying the Nightmare Prone mark takes a Winged Sorrow at the end of each round, then gets Cleanse Warded — so shields placed on them are wasted. Prioritize heals over wards for the marked player.

name: Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm, Standard)
locations: Wallaru: Outback - Outback Ridge,Wobbegong Territory - Bunyip Warrens
cheats: "Sweet dreams, wizard." - At the beginning of the first Round, and at the end of every fourth Round, Arcane Toad casts Smoke Screen (Shadow), and then places a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on a random Wizard.
If a Wizard joins the battle late, Arcane Toad will Stun all Wizards.
Cleanse Ward can be used to remove the mark.
"One, two, here's a nightmare for you!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has the 'Nightmare Prone' mark, Arcane Toad casts one of the following Spells on the marked Wizard:
Call of Khrulhu
Monster Mash (Tier 5a)
Shift Grey Wolf
Winged Sorrow

"This better not be love conquering all!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Arcane Toad will cast a version of Cleanse Ward, removing all negative Wards (including the 'Nightmare Prone' mark) from the caster.
strategy: Hit with Myth — he takes +30% from it. Storm is the wrong school here (54% resist); everything else eats 24%.
Enter with your full team on round 1. Late joiners get the whole team stunned.

Round 1 opens with Smoke Screen plus a 'Nightmare Prone' mark on a random wizard, and the mark repeats at the end of every fourth round. A marked wizard eats a big cheat spell (Call of Khrulhu class) at the end of every round.

Exploit the heal cheat to clear the mark: if you are marked, cast any healing spell — his 'love conquering all' counter is a Cleanse Ward that strips all negative wards from the caster, including the Nightmare Prone mark. A cheap heal removes the mark for you.
Otherwise pack Cleanse Ward to pull the mark off a teammate. Do not leave anyone marked into the end of a round.
Clear the Air Rider and Punk Wallaby with your Myth AoE while blading — the accuracy debuff from Smoke Screen means an accuracy enchant on your hit is worth a slot.
Blade up between mark windows and end it with one big Myth hit. No stuns on him, so just burst.
cheatNotes: On round 1 and after every fourth round he casts Smoke Screen and marks a random wizard with a 'Nightmare Prone' stun shield. Expect fizzles unless you enchant for accuracy.
If anyone joins the fight late, the whole team gets stunned. Enter together.
Cleanse Ward removes the Nightmare Prone mark — carry it to save a marked teammate.
A marked wizard gets hit by one of his nightmare spells at the end of every round until the mark is gone. Remove it before round end.

His counter to healing is a Cleanse Ward that removes all negative wards from the caster — including the Nightmare Prone mark. If you are marked, deliberately cast a cheap heal and the mark comes off.

name: Arcane Toad (Star-Storm)
cheats: "Sweet dreams, wizard." - Arcane Toad casts a Moon version of Stun that Stuns the Wizard for 2 Rounds and then places a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on them.

"One, two, here's a nightmare for you!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has the Nightmare Prone mark, Arcane Toad casts Winged Sorrow on the marked Wizard, and then casts Cleanse Ward on them.
strategy: Pack stun blocks and a heal. His whole kit is a Moon Stun that locks you down for 2 rounds, then a follow-up attack while you are marked.

Expect the 'Nightmare Prone' mark after the stun. While it is on you, he casts Winged Sorrow on you at the end of the round, then Cleanse Ward to clear the mark. Budget health to eat one Winged Sorrow, or shield ahead of time if you see the stun coming.
Do not plan around stunning him back. He is not stunable.
Otherwise play the standard loop: blade for 2-3 rounds while healing through the stun cycle, then finish with one big hit. Nothing in his cheats punishes blades or traps.
cheatNotes: He casts a Moon version of Stun that locks a wizard for 2 full rounds and leaves a 'Nightmare Prone' stun shield mark on them. Stun blocks are your counter; without them you lose two turns.

While the Nightmare Prone mark is on you, he casts Winged Sorrow on you at the end of the round, then Cleanse Ward removes the mark. In practice you eat one Winged Sorrow per stun cycle, so keep your health topped up.

name: Arcane Toad (Star-Storm, Standard)
locations: Wallaru: Wobbegong Territory - Bunyip Warrens,The Dreaming - Freddie's Domain (Standard)
cheats: Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm):

"Sweet dreams, wizard." - At the beginning of the first Round, and at the end of every fourth Round, Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) casts Smoke Screen (Shadow), and then places a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on a random Wizard.
If a Wizard joins the battle late, Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) will Stun all Wizards.
Cleanse Ward can be used to remove the mark.
"One, two, here's a nightmare for you!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has the 'Nightmare Prone' mark, Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) casts one of the following Spells on the marked Wizard:
Call of Khrulhu
Monster Mash (Tier 5a)
Shift Grey Wolf
Winged Sorrow

"This better not be love conquering all!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell (including positive healing charms), Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) will cast a version of Cleanse Ward, removing all negative Wards (including the 'Nightmare Prone' mark) from the target of the heal.
Heals that affect All Allies, such as Pigsie, will remove negative Wards from the caster.
Pet maycasts that target all players will remove negative Wards from all players.
Arcane Toad (Star-Storm):
"One, two, Freddie's not done with you!" - If Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) is targeted, Arcane Toad (Star-Storm) will give all Wizards 1 Living Nightmare card:
[Living Nightmare]
strategy: Hit with Myth (he takes +30% from it); never attack into Storm (54% resist), and everything else is 24% resist.
Don't arrive late — every late wizard gets stunned.

Round 1 and end of every 4th round he Smoke Screens and slaps a 'Nightmare Prone' stun shield on a random wizard; while a wizard carries that mark he gets hit every round by Call of Khrulhu, Monster Mash, or similar.

Clear the Nightmare Prone mark fast: Cleanse Ward removes it, and any heal on the marked wizard makes the toad Cleanse Ward off all their negative wards — heal the marked wizard to strip it, or use an all-ally heal like Pigsie to clear it team-wide.
Build blades and traps over 2-3 rounds, keep the mark cleared, then land one big Myth hit.
cheatNotes: Round 1 and end of every 4th round he casts Smoke Screen and puts a Nightmare Prone stun shield on a random wizard — expect the accuracy debuff and plan to clear the mark.
Don't join late; every late wizard gets stunned.
Cleanse Ward removes the Nightmare Prone mark — keep one ready.
While a wizard carries the mark he hits them every round with one of the following spells, so clear it quickly.
Any heal (even a positive healing charm) makes him Cleanse Ward the heal's target, stripping all negative wards including the mark — heal the marked wizard to clear it.
All-ally heals like Pigsie strip negative wards from the caster instead, so heal-to-clear works across the team.
Pet may-casts that hit all players clear the mark from everyone.
If the Moon-Storm toad is targeted, the Star-Storm toad hands every wizard a Living Nightmare card.

name: Archmagus Lorcan
locations: Khrysalis: The Hive - Chamber of the Orb Weavers
cheats: "Serve your Archmagus!" - At the end of Round 1 (or any Round where he casts Shadow Sentinel), Archmagus Lorcan will interrupt to summon two Elder Magus Minions. They become active in Round 2.
This cheat cannot be dispelled.
He will summon three Elder Magus Minions if more than two Wizards are present in the duel.
Once he receives a Shadow Pip, he will begin to cast Shadow Sentinel every three Rounds.

"Your sacrifice feeds the Shadow Web!" - Three Rounds after being summoned (or after Lorcan's Shadow Sentinel Spell has ended), the Elder Magus Minions will cheat-cast a 0-Pip Fire version of Sacrifice, that deals 6,000 Fire Damage to themselves and restores 1,500 Health for Archmagus Lorcan.
Defeating Lorcan will stop this cheat.
spellNotes: Uses a version of Shadow Sentinel with +15 Backlash.
strategy: Focus everything on Lorcan and ignore the Elder Magus minions. They kill themselves anyway - three rounds after being summoned they cast a 0-pip Sacrifice for 6,000 Fire damage to themselves, healing Lorcan 1,500 each. Killing Lorcan stops the whole loop.
This is a race against his healing. Every sacrifice cycle refunds up to 3,000-4,500 health, so supports blade the hitter fast rather than spending rounds on Feint stacking.
Keep the group at two wizards if you can - more than two players means he summons three Elder Magus instead of two, which means more sacrifice healing.
Hit with Myth, Life, or Death - he takes +30% from all three. Never hit Balance into his 80% resist.

Watch for Shadow Sentinel once he has a shadow pip (every three rounds after). Each Sentinel round comes with a fresh pair of minion summons, so the longer the fight runs the more healing he gets. Aim to burst him down within the first sacrifice cycle.
cheatNotes: At the end of round 1 (and any round he casts Shadow Sentinel) he summons two Elder Magus minions that wake up the following round. Do not waste hits on them - they are healing batteries, not threats worth killing.
With three or four wizards in the fight he summons three minions instead of two. Smaller groups literally reduce his healing.
Once he earns a shadow pip he casts Shadow Sentinel every three rounds, and each cast brings fresh minion summons. His Sentinel also carries +15 backlash, so it costs him health to use.

Three rounds after appearing, each Elder Magus sacrifices itself for 6,000 Fire self-damage and heals Lorcan 1,500. You cannot profitably stop this by killing minions - they replace themselves - so outpace it.
The sacrifice healing ends the moment Lorcan dies. Kill the boss first; leftover minions are irrelevant.

name: Archmagus Lorcan (Gallery)
cheats: Archmagus Lorcan
"Serve your Archmagus!" - At the end of the first Round or any Round that he casts Shadow Sentinel, Archmagus Lorcan will summon two Elder Magi Minions which becomes active two Rounds later.
Once Archmagus Lorcan receives a Shadow Pip, he will begin to cast Shadow Sentinel every 3 Rounds.

"Your sacrifice feeds the Shadow Web!" - After Archmagus Lorcan's Shadow Sentinel expires, the Elder Magi will interrupt-cast a Fire version of Sacrifice that deals 10,000 Fire Damage to themselves and heals Archmagus Lorcan for 1,500 Health.
Defeating Archmagus Lorcan before the Elder Magi will stop this cheat.
Lord Ombra
"Your power belongs to me!" - Every 4 Rounds, Lord Ombra will interrupt-cast a Balance version of Steal Pips on the Wizard with the most pips, which steals 4 Pips and gives him 3 regular Pips.
spellNotes: This Boss uses a version of Shadow Sentinel MOB with +15 Backlash.
strategy: Two-boss fight: Archmagus Lorcan (Balance, resists Balance 80%, takes +30% from Myth, Life, and Death) plus Lord Ombra. Run a team with a hitter using Myth, Life, or Death, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer.
Expect two Elder Magi to spawn at the end of Round 1 and again after any Shadow Sentinel; they stay inactive for two rounds, so use that window to set up.
Once he gains his Shadow Pip he casts Shadow Sentinel every 3 rounds. Time your hitter's big AoE for a round the Sentinel is down, or clear the Sentinel first.
When his Shadow Sentinel expires the Elder Magi self-Sacrifice for 10,000 Fire and heal him 1,500. Let it happen to clear the Magi rather than killing them by hand.
Clear the Elder Magi before Lorcan so nothing keeps reviving the Sacrifice loop, then burst Lorcan down.
Lord Ombra steals 4 pips from your highest-pip wizard every 4 rounds. Spend pips before his steal window so he grabs an empty hand.
cheatNotes: Two Elder Magi appear at the end of Round 1 and after any Shadow Sentinel cast, but stay inactive for two rounds. Use that window to blade up before they can attack.
Once he has a Shadow Pip he throws Shadow Sentinel every third round; the Sentinel intercepts hits, so time your big attack for a round it is not up or kill it first.
When his Sentinel expires the Magi self-Sacrifice for 10,000 Fire and heal him 1,500. It removes the Magi for you, so it is fine to let it happen rather than killing them manually.
Killing Lorcan before the Elder Magi keeps this Sacrifice-heal from repeating, but the cleaner route is to clear the Magi first so nothing revives the loop.
Every 4 rounds Ombra steals 4 pips from whoever holds the most. Spend pips before his steal window so he grabs an empty hand.

name: Ares Savage Spear (Mount Olympus)
locations: Wizard City: Mount Olympus - Hall of Battle
cheats: At the beginning of Round 1 (and every fourth Round thereafter), Ares will interrupt-cast Amplify on himself.
"You Dare Strike Me Directly?" - If a Wizard attacks Ares with a single target damage spell, he will cheat-cast a 0-pip version of his Natural Attack.
DoT spells (such as Fire Elf) will not trigger this cheat constantly each round, and will only trigger it during the initial hit.
Frostbite triggers his cheat. (Needs further research.)
Steal spells will not trigger this cheat.
Pet spells will not trigger this cheat.
Drain spells will not trigger this cheat.
Wand hits followed by wand may casts will cause Ares Savage Spear to use his natural attack twice.
strategy: Deck Ice AoE hits (Blizzard-style, all-enemies). He takes +50% from Ice, resists Fire at 80%, and his counter cheat only punishes SINGLE-target damage — AoE spells dodge it entirely.
Never single-target him. Any single-target damage spell triggers a free 0-pip natural attack back at you. Steal, drain, and pet spells are safe; a DoT only triggers it once, on the initial hit.
Skip wand hits — a wand hit plus a wand may-cast can make him counter twice.
He Amplifies himself on round 1 and every fourth round after, so his hits ramp. He is stunable — a stun buys a safe blading round.
Blade up with Ice for 2-3 rounds, then kill with one boosted Ice AoE. At 3,600 health he folds fast to +50% boost.
