cheatNotes: He starts round 1 with Amplify on himself and refreshes it every 4 rounds, so his outgoing damage is always buffed. Expect harder hits than his rank suggests.
Any single-target damage spell on him triggers an instant free natural attack on you. Use all-enemy (AoE) spells only and this cheat never fires.
A DoT like Fire Elf only triggers the counter once, when it first lands — the tick rounds are safe.
Frostbite has been observed to trigger the counter, so treat it like any other single-target hit.
Steal spells are safe — no counter.
Pet spells are safe — no counter.
Drain spells are safe — no counter.
A wand hit whose may-cast also fires can make him use his natural attack twice in one round. Avoid wand-poking him.

name: Ares Savage Spear (Mount Olympus, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Mount Olympus (Challenge) - Hall of Battle (Challenge)
cheats: [Round] At the beginning of the first Round, and any Round that he does not have an Aura, Ares will cast Amplify on himself.
[Jinx] "Your underhanded tactics are unbefitting" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward on Ares, Ares will cast Immolate on the caster.
[Ward] "Breach their wards!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Ares will cast Fire Elf on the recipient.
[Damage] "You Dare Strike Me Directly?" - If a Wizard attacks Ares with a single-target Damage Spell, he will cast a 0-Pip version of his Natural Attack.
strategy: Attack into Storm or Life. He takes +14% from both, resists Fire, Death, Balance, and Shadow only 6%, and resists Ice and Myth 26%, so pick Storm or Life.
Expect a hard opener. He casts Amplify on himself round 1 and any round he has no aura, boosting his next attack, so shield or keep resist up early.
Skip traps entirely. Any harmful ward on Ares triggers Immolate on you. Skip shields too, since a helpful ward gets the recipient hit with Fire Elf.
Stack blades instead. Charms do not trigger his cheats, so build damage over two or three rounds safely.
Finish with an AoE, not a single-target hit. A single-target damage spell makes him fire a free 0-pip natural attack back, and an AoE also clears the Ophidian Archer.
cheatNotes: Round 1 and any round he has no aura, Ares casts Amplify on himself to boost his next attack. Keep resist or a shield up.
Any trap or weakness you place on him triggers Immolate, a Fire DoT, on you. Skip traps and blade instead.
Any shield or helpful ward you cast gets the recipient hit with Fire Elf. Do not shield.
Hitting him with a single-target damage spell makes him retaliate with a free 0-pip natural attack. Use an AoE to avoid the counter.

name: Ares Savage Spear (Zeus Exalted Challenge)
cheats: At the beginning of the first Round after Hades or Apollo used their Natural Attacks, Ares will use his AoE Natural Attack, then Detonate all DoT.
"Your underhanded tactics are unbefitting!" - If a Trap is placed on an enemy, Ares will cheat-cast an Efreet (1,000) that applies a -95% Damage Charm on the Wizard who cast the Spell.
If Apollo and Zeus have been defeated, this Cheat can be prevented by using Quench.
"Such prowess! But I think you can do better." - If a Damage Spell is used, Ares will give the Wizard a Blade by casting Dark Pact (+30%).
"We battle on! For Zeus!" - If Zeus has been defeated, Ares will use his Natural Attack at the start of each second Round.
spellNotes: Casts a Spell similar to Wrath of Ares, dealing 705 Fire Damage to all opponents, and Detonates all Tics, which acts like a Natural Attack.
strategy: Team fight in the Zeus Exalted Challenge. Bring Ice or Storm hitters — Ares takes +40% from both and resists Fire at 80%.
No traps, ever. Any trap on an enemy triggers a 1,000-damage Efreet with a -95% weakness on the caster. Build damage with blades only.

Exploit his Dark Pact cheat: every damage spell you cast earns that wizard a +30% Dark Pact blade. Supports can throw cheap one-pip attacks each round to farm free +30% blades onto themselves, then pass value to the hitter with team blades.
Watch the DoT detonate: the round after Hades or Apollo uses a natural attack, Ares fires his AoE and detonates all DoTs. Keep Triage handy and do not stack DoTs on yourselves before that window.
Once Zeus is down, Ares natural-attacks every second round — time shields and heals to those rounds. If Apollo and Zeus are dead, Quench shuts off the Efreet counter if someone slips a trap.
Blade the hitter, stack the farmed Dark Pacts, and kill through 18,800 with one big Ice or Storm hit.
cheatNotes: The round after Hades or Apollo natural-attacks, Ares uses his own AoE natural attack and detonates every DoT on the field. Do not carry DoT ticks into that round; Triage them off.
Any trap placed on an enemy gets the caster a cheat Efreet for 1,000 damage plus a -95% damage weakness. Skip traps completely — the weakness ruins your hit.
Once Apollo and Zeus are dead, casting Quench prevents the Efreet counter. Only then are traps even worth considering.
Every damage spell you cast is rewarded with a +30% Dark Pact blade. Deliberately spam cheap attacks to farm free blades for the kill.
After Zeus dies, Ares uses his natural attack at the start of every second round. Shield and heal on that rhythm.

name: Arion Spiny Stallion (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea (Challenge) - House of Fury (Challenge)
cheats: [Jinx] "You cannot diminish me!"- If a harmful Ward is cast on Arion Spiny Stallion, he will cast Devotion (6 Rounds).
Spells that place harmful Wards on all enemies will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Pack Death attacks. Arion is Life and takes boosted Death damage, so a Death hitter or Death prisms carry this fight; avoid Storm, which he resists.
Never place a harmful Ward on him. Weakness, Infection, or any AoE that lands a curse-ward triggers Devotion (6 Rounds) and his 24% outgoing heal makes that a real problem.
Skip the shields-on-enemy tricks entirely. Buff yourself instead: stack blades and traps, then hit.
Set up over two or three rounds with damage charms and Feint (a Trap, not a Ward, so it is safe here), then swing one large Death hit.
Clear Scaled Eques or hit both with an AoE so nothing lingers, then finish Arion in one burst before he can out-heal you.
cheatNotes: Landing any harmful Ward on Arion makes him cast Devotion for 6 Rounds, a heal-over-time that, with his 24% outgoing healing, keeps refilling him. Use blades and Traps instead of Weakness-style wards.
AoE spells that drop a harmful Ward on all enemies also set off the Devotion cheat, so watch your area curses too.

name: Armored Ned Collie
locations: Wallaru: Wobbegong Territory - Ned Collie's Den
cheats: "There ain't no spell piercing this armor!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Armored Ned Collie casts a -300% "Invunerability" Resist Brace Aura.
"Wizard, that smoothie kicking in yet?" - After the first Round, if Armored Ned Collie is attacked, the Wizard who attacked will receive a +300% Armor Piercing Infallible Aura.

"We'll never break that armor without hitting!" - Starting on the second Round, at the end of every Round, Armored Ned Collie will say this at the end of each Round, if a Wizard does not have the Armor Piercing Aura.
"I'll take that!":
If a Wizard casts a Damage Blade, Armored Ned Collie will cast Steal Charm on the caster.
For any Spell that applies multiple Blades, only the first Blade is removed.
Aegis can be used to protect against this cheat.
If a Wizard casts a positive Ward, Armored Ned Collie will cast Steal Ward on the caster.
If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time, Armored Ned Collie will cast Snow Drift on the caster.
strategy: This is an exploit fight: his round-1 aura gives him -300% resist (immunity), but attacking him after round 1 gives YOU a +300% Armor Piercing Infallible aura that punches straight through it. Poke him with a cheap attack early to claim the aura.
Open round 1 with setup only, since the Invulnerability aura is up and hits are wasted. From round 2 on, throw a small, low-pip attack to trigger the +300% pierce aura on yourself.
Keep the pierce aura fresh on your hitter. If nobody has it, he reminds you every round that you will never break the armor. Re-attack to reapply before your kill turn.
Blade carefully: any Damage Blade you cast gets stolen with Steal Charm unless it is Aegis-protected. Use Aegis blades, or lean on traps that are not positive wards.
Do not cast positive wards (shields, absorbs) or heal-over-times. Wards get stolen and HoTs trigger Snow Drift on you.
Kill plan: cheap poke for the pierce aura, Aegis-protected blades, then one big hit while the +300% pierce aura is active. He has 16,810 health and cannot be stunned, so make the hit count.
cheatNotes: At the start of round 1 he gives himself a -300% resist brace aura, making him effectively immune. Do not waste a real hit into it.
This is the fight's gift: after round 1, whoever attacks him gets a +300% Armor Piercing Infallible aura. Deliberately hit him with any cheap attack to gain it, then your real hits ignore his armor.
From round 2 on, he calls this out at the end of any round where no wizard holds the Armor Piercing aura. Treat it as a reminder to poke him again.
If you cast a Damage Blade, he immediately casts Steal Charm on you and takes it. Unprotected blades feed him.
Spells that apply several blades at once only lose the first blade to the steal. Multi-blade spells like tri-blades are relatively efficient here.
Aegis-protected blades survive the Steal Charm. Put Aegis on the blades your hit actually depends on.
Casting a positive ward (shield, absorb) gets it stolen with Steal Ward. Skip shields; rely on the fast kill instead.
Casting a heal-over-time gets you hit with Snow Drift. Use flat heals only.

name: Assistant Warden Porfiry
locations: Polaris: The Basstille - Cell Block A
cheats: If a Wizard casts an Aura, Assistant Warden Porfiry will use his turn during the next Round to cast Supernova if he has sufficient Pips.
strategy: Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+30% incoming); never attack into his 80% Balance resist.
Do not cast Auras. Any aura makes him Supernova the whole team the next round.
Set up with blades and Feint as normal, keep shields up, and stack toward one burst.
He is not stunable, so plan around his turns rather than skipping them.
AoE your final hit to catch the Walrusk Mercenary alongside him.
cheatNotes: If you cast an aura like Amplify or Berserk, he answers with Supernova next round. Skip auras entirely, or only use one on the round you kill him.

name: Astraeus
locations: Celestia: Trial of the Spheres - Sanctum of the Stars
cheats: "The constellations form again!" - At the end of the first Round, Astraeus will summon three Ptolemos (Rank 6 Minion) Minions, which become active two Rounds later.
During the first Round, Astraeus will use his turn to cast a -90% Tower Shield on himself.
He will interrupt-cast the Tower Shield (-90%) every time he is damaged for as long as his Minions are alive.
Sometimes Damage over Time (DoT) Spells will not trigger this cheat.
If all his Minions are defeated, he will no longer cast the Tower Shield (-90%), until he re-summons them two to three Rounds later.
On occasion, he will not re-summon the Minions.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Pierce or Steal Ward can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield (-90%).
strategy: Hit with Ice, he takes +35% from it, and never attack into his 35% Storm/Life resist.
His -90% Tower Shield is the wall. Pack Pierce, Steal Ward, or Melt to strip it safely without provoking a re-cast.
Kill the three Ptolemos minions first. While any of them lives he interrupt-recasts the shield every time he is damaged.
Once all minions are dead the shield stops until he re-summons two to three rounds later, that gap is your hit window.
A stacked DoT sometimes slips past the shield re-cast, so damage-over-time can chip him while minions are up.
Build Ice blades under a Steal Ward or Melt, then land one big Ice hit in the clean window after the minions fall.
cheatNotes: He summons three Ptolemos minions at the end of Round 1 that activate two rounds later. Clear them fast because they keep his shield up.
He shields himself for -90% on Round 1, so a raw hit does almost nothing. Strip it before you attack.
While any minion lives he re-shields every time you damage him. Kill the minions or the -90% shield never stays gone.
Damage-over-time sometimes slips past the shield re-cast, so a stacked DoT can still tick him down.
With every minion dead he stops re-shielding until he re-summons two to three rounds later, that is your burst window.
Occasionally he skips the re-summon, giving you an even longer clean window to finish him.
Melt dispels the -90% shield outright, pack it to open him up.
Pierce or Steal Ward removes the -90% shield without provoking a re-cast, steal it to keep him exposed.

name: Atavus Monquistan
cheats: Aeon of Atavus

""Devour them all!"" - At the beginning of every Round a Wizard joins, including the first Round, Atavus Monquistan will trigger Aeon of Atavus to devour all Wizards, removing all hanging effects, and then cast a Shadow Spell on the next Round to spit them out into Polymorph forms.
If Atavus Monquistan is defeated, the cheat will be given to the Boss who has the team's first turn.
""Become, yourself, pure life"" - Aeon of Atavus will Polymorph all Wizards for 20 Rounds into one of the following forms:
Atavus Dog
Atavus Monquistan
Atavus Pingouin
Atavus Unicorn
All Wizards will become the same form
"Rooaarr!!" - Aeon of Atavus will cast a Laser spell that deals 750 Shadow Damage on all Wizards.
The Damage and Pierce stats for the Laser cheat are affected by the Boss who conducts this cheat.
"Your mastery of magic won't save you" - After one of the enemies is defeated, all Wizards will be unpolymorphed.
After all bosses have been defeated, a cutscene will trigger where Aeon of Atavus defeats itself.
strategy: Novus finale for a full team of four. On entry everyone is forced to pass, then devoured and polymorphed into one of four forms (Dog/Balance, Unicorn/Storm-Life, Monquistan/Fire-Myth, Pingouin/Ice-Death), each 30,000 health, 170% damage, 70% resist. You only revert by killing a boss, so do not over-invest before the devour fires.

Follow the guide's kill order: attack the boss your form boosts against. Dog hits Dog, Unicorn hits Monquistan, Monquistan hits Pingouin, Pingouin hits Unicorn. The whole team funnels onto that single target.

Build the polymorph deck around tri-school conditional hits: each needs your two primary hanging effects plus one secondary or tertiary effect. Keep four copies of the 1-pip Damage Aura (25% boost) and the matching ramp spells; discard single-school hits and off-school ramps.

Rounds 1-3: discard aggressively to find your ramp spells and conditional hit, and stack self-hanging effects (blades, shields, heals) first since they land fastest. Prioritize school pips for your primary schools.
Round 4 onward: once all three required hanging effects are up, cast the Damage Aura, then unleash the tri-school conditional for roughly 12,000 damage into your boosted boss.
Killing that one boss instantly unpolymorphs the whole team. Return to normal stats and AoE the remaining three down together.

For fast farming, coordinate pet willcasts (Frozen Kraken Trained, Earth Walker, Link Trained, or Sword and Shield Trained) to pre-apply hanging effects. Don't waste deck slots on traps, DoTs, or weaknesses; let teammates handle enemy-side effects.
cheatNotes: This fires at the start and every time a wizard joins, stripping every blade, trap, and ward from the team and polymorphing you. Have all four wizards join on Round 1 so it only triggers once.
Defeating the boss does not end it cleanly; the devour cheat jumps to whichever enemy takes the first turn, so expect it to keep happening.
You are locked into an Atavus polymorph form for 20 rounds unless you break out early by defeating an enemy.
Expect 750 Shadow damage to the whole team whenever this triggers; keep everyone healed above that.
Defeating any one enemy instantly unpolymorphs the whole team, so funnel damage onto a single target to free yourselves fast.

name: Atlach-Leng
cheats: "Fear the Children of Leng!" - After two rounds, he will begin summoning one Crypt Spider every other Round.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Ice Damage.
strategy: Hit with Fire or Myth — he takes +35% from both and resists Ice at 80%.
You are on a timer: after round two he summons a Crypt Spider every other round. Each extra setup round adds a body to the field.
Build fast — blades and a Feint in the first 3-4 rounds — and finish with one big Fire or Myth AoE so the boss and every accumulated spider die together.
If the fight drags, keep using AoE hits; single-target kills on spiders just get replaced two rounds later.
He is not stunable, so don't pack stuns — spend those slots on blades and heals for the 18,920 health grind.
cheatNotes: Starting after round two, a fresh Crypt Spider joins the fight every other round. There is no cap mentioned — the only real counter is ending the fight quickly with AoE damage.

name: Austri
cheats: "Wrong brother! I'm it!" - Casting Spells at a Boss without the Stun Shield will trigger Austri casting Colossus, dealing 460-540 Ice Damage.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Ice Damage.
strategy: This is a 'wrong brother' fight: only attack the boss that has the Stun Shield. Casting spells at a boss without it makes Austri counter with Colossus for 460-540 Ice damage.
Check wards before every cast. If your target has no Stun Shield, pick again or pass.
Hit with Fire or Myth — Austri takes +35% from both. Never use Ice into his 80% resist.
He only has 2,110 health. One blade and a modest Fire or Myth hit on the correct target ends it — no long setup needed.
cheatNotes: Attacking a boss that lacks the Stun Shield triggers a 460-540 damage Colossus counter from Austri. Target only the Stun-Shielded boss and you never see this cheat.

name: B'rel
cheats: "A little help here Wartle."
Storm Wartle randomly casts a Myth Shield.
If Minion is still alive, Attack on All does not seem to trigger.
When Boss is alone, it has been observed that any Myth attack triggers the cheat.
strategy: Pack Myth. He takes +15% from Myth and resists Storm by 30%, so Myth is your damage school.
He is not stunable, so plan around clean setup rounds rather than stun-locking him.
Keep the Storm Wartle minion alive while you stack blades; his Attack on All punish stays dormant while the minion stands.
Watch for the Wartle's random Myth Shield and shatter or pierce it before you commit a big Myth hit.
Once you are set, drop your full Myth burst so it kills B'rel outright. Hitting him while he is alone otherwise triggers the Attack on All retaliation.
cheatNotes: The Storm Wartle minion may throw up a Myth Shield that blunts your boosted Myth hits. Break or pierce it before your big attack lands.
The boss's Attack on All counter stays dormant while the Wartle minion is alive, so leaving the minion up keeps the AoE punish from firing.
Once B'rel is alone, any Myth attack triggers his Attack on All punish. Time your Myth hits to kill him rather than leaving him standing to retaliate.

name: Baane
cheats: "[???]" - If a Wizard joins the duel late, Baane will do a Natural Attack, which Stuns the Wizard and increases Baane's Damage by 10%.
"I will [???] down before I strike!" - At the end of every third Round, he will switch the turn order. This continues if he is defeated before his Minion.
"Ah! Well played." - He will say this when attacked.
"Shadow magic! I was born in it!" - Baane will say this when he casts Dark Nova.
spellNotes: Frost Hound Pet will cast Blizzard (290-330, 100%).
strategy: Pack Fire damage; he only takes +5% from Fire but resists Ice and Shadow 50%, so keep those schools out of your deck.
Join before the duel starts. A late wizard eats a stun and hands Baane +10% damage.
He is not stunable and hits hard at 177% outgoing, plus his Frost Hound's Blizzard, so keep shields and a heal ready.
He flips the turn order at the end of every third Round; count Rounds so your setup-and-hit turn lands when you act first.
Stack Fire blades and a Feint over two or three Rounds, then end with one large Fire hit.
cheatNotes: Joining after the fight begins gets you stunned and buffs his damage by 10%; enter with your team, do not wander in late.
Every third Round he swaps who goes first; time your blades-and-hit turn for when you have the advantage and plan heals around the flip.

name: Baba Yaga
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard enters the duel late, Baba Yaga's Egg will interrupt the duel casting a Myth Spell that polymorphs the tardy Wizard into a Polymorph Egg.
The Wizard will have only one Spell present, Hatch, which will unpolymorph the Wizard for 3 Pips.
This cheat will still be triggered if a Wizard joins during the planning phase of the first Round.
[Ward] "Learn that at Ravenwood? How quaint!" - If a Wizard casts a positive Ward, such as a Shield, she may cheat-cast Shatter at the Wizard.
[Round] At the start of the 3rd Round

[Sun][Minion] "Let's take the bull by the horns!"' - Baba Yaga will cast a Sun version of Frost Minotaur on a random Wizard, which deals 50 + 445 Death Damage. The Minotaur Statue Minion is summoned to the first spot (Dagger).
[Round]At the start of the 6th Round

[Sun][Minion] "Fall before my gorgon's gaze!" - Baba Yaga will cast a Sun version of Medusa resembling Gorgon Statue on a random Wizard, which deals 770 Storm Damage and Stuns for 2 Rounds. The Gorgon Statue Minion is summoned to the second spot (Key).
[Round] At the start of the 10th Round

[Sun][Minion] "My wyrm will cool things down!" - Baba Yaga will cast a Sun version of Catalan resembling Wyrm Statue which deals 700 Ice Damage to and -25% Accuracy to all Wizards. The Wyrm Statue Minion is summoned to the third spot (Gem).
spellNotes: Casts an all-enemies version of Curse (95%).
Casts Earthquake (638) which removes only three Charms.
The Minion Only Spells in her Deck can be cast on herself.
The Pet, Baba Yaga's Egg, can randomly cast Blinding Light (85%) during the duel.
strategy: This is a four-wizard Feint-stack race to kill Baba Yaga (22,800 health) in two rounds, before her round-3 summon cheats start. Enter the duel as a single unit with the hitter joining last so nobody gets polymorphed.

Assign three trappers and one hitter. Trappers carry Feints from every source: hard cards (Feint x2, plus Potent Trap), amulet Feint, pet Feint, and TC Feint. Keep Shatter and Cleanse Charm in side decks for contingencies.
The hitter packs a lean deck: one main-school blade, one Sharpened Blade, one 5-6 pip hit, one Shadow hit, and two Colossal enchants. Do not pack extra cards; a bloated deck just prolongs the fight.
Round 1: the three trappers each cast a Feint on Baba Yaga while the hitter casts Sharpened Blade on themselves.

Round 2 (ideal): the trappers lay their remaining Feints and the hitter fires the enchanted main-school attack for the kill. Use a strong single-target nuke per your school (Storm: Triton/Rusalka's Wrath; Fire: Helephant/Fire From Above; Ice: Ice Colossus/Abominable Weaver; Myth: Ninja Pigs/Witch's Housecall; Death: Skeletal Pirate/Winged Sorrow; Life: Centaur/Hungry Caterpillar; Balance: Judgement/Nested Fury).

Adapt round 2 if she interferes: if she Shatters/shields, one trapper casts Shatter instead of a Feint; if she lands Virulent Plague, one trapper casts Cleanse Charm instead. Never cast your own shields, since a positive Ward can trigger her Shatter cheat.
If the setup falls apart and you slip past round 3, just flee and re-enter rather than trying to survive her Minotaur, Medusa, and Wyrm summons.
cheatNotes: Anyone who enters late gets polymorphed into an Egg by Baba Yaga's pet. Enter as a group so no one is turned into a helpless egg.
A polymorphed wizard has only the Hatch spell and must spend 3 pips to change back, costing them a full turn. This is why late entry wrecks the two-round plan.
Even joining during the planning phase of round 1 counts as late and triggers the polymorph, so everyone must already be in before the timer.
Casting a positive Ward such as a Shield can make Baba Yaga cheat-cast Shatter on you, wiping your shields. Do not shield; rely on the two-round kill instead.

At the start of round 3 she summons a Frost Minotaur on a random wizard for 50 + 445 Death damage and drops the Minotaur Statue minion in the first slot. Kill her before round 3 to skip this entirely.
At the start of round 6 she casts a Medusa-style hit for 770 Storm damage that also Stuns for 2 rounds, summoning the Gorgon Statue in the second slot. Another reason not to let the fight drag.

At the start of round 10 she casts a Wyrm-style hit for 700 Ice damage and -25% Accuracy to all wizards, summoning the Wyrm Statue in the third slot. A long fight becomes very hard to land hits in, so end it fast.

name: Babbling Meints
cheats: Interrupt Cycle This duel revolves around an interrupt cycle that is seven Rounds long, and repeats until Babbling Meints is defeated.
"Thanks, little buddy!" - At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle, Babbling Meints' Pet GobblerBall will give him a Balanceblade (+30%).
"Best pet ever!" - At the beginning of the third Round in this cycle, his GobblerBall Pet will give him an Elemental Blade.
"Just what I need!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, his GobblerBall Pet will cast a Donate Power (+3 Pips).
spellNotes: Applies a Fire, Ice, or Storm DoT effect to the target, dealing 300 Damage over three Rounds.
strategy: This is a race against his pet's 7-round buff cycle. Kill him before round 5-7 when the Balanceblade, Elemental Blade, and donated pips stack into a big swing.
He resists Balance 30% and takes only +5% from Myth, Life, or Death, so attack with your own strong school; prisms barely help here.
Set up over rounds 1-3 while the pet is still feeding him: stack your blades and a Feint.
Pack Triage or a Cleanse for the Fire, Ice, or Storm DoT (300 over three rounds) from his natural attacks.
Land one big hit by round 4-5, before he has the Elemental Blade plus extra pips to hit back hard.
cheatNotes: The whole fight runs on a 7-round loop of pet buffs. Count rounds and burst him down before round 5-7 when everything stacks.
Round 1 his pet hands him a +30% Balanceblade, so expect his balance hits to jump.
Round 3 the pet adds an Elemental Blade on top, another reason to end things before round 4.
Round 5 the pet donates +3 pips, giving him a full-power nuke. You want him dead before this.

name: Balanced Flamefists
locations: Zafaria: Stone Town - Hall of Fury
cheats: "Use this special deck of cards to aid our experiments" - At the beginning of the first Round, Cornelia Hornswoggle will cast a Storm Spell that Polymorphs each Wizard in battle. This Polymorph does not change your Wizard's appearance, but grants a unique deck and stats.
Hall of Fury (Balance)
Hall of Fury (Death)
Hall of Fury (Fire)
Hall of Fury (Ice)
Hall of Fury (Life)
Hall of Fury (Myth)
"Do your worst, Wizard, but the final blow is mine" - At the beginning of the second Round, Cornelia Hornswoggle will say this line.

"I told you, Wizard, the final strike must be mine" - If the last enemy is not defeated by Cornelia Hornswoggle, then she will speak this line and the battle will end normally. However, all enemies will respawn afterwards and the battle must be restarted to progress the quest.
Cornelia Hornswoggle only needs to defeat the last enemy standing. She does not need to defeat every opponent.
This cheat will still occur if Cornelia Hornswoggle is defeated, but the cheat phrase will not be spoken.
strategy: This is a scripted fight: on round 1 Cornelia Hornswoggle polymorphs your whole team, giving each wizard a fixed Hall of Fury deck and stats. Your normal deck barely matters — don't over-prepare.
The one rule that matters: Cornelia must land the killing blow on the LAST enemy standing. If you kill the last enemy yourself, the battle ends but everything respawns and you must redo it.
Use your polymorph deck to grind Balanced Flamefists down — Storm damage is best, he takes +30% from it.
Once the last enemy is low, stop attacking it. Pass, shield, or heal and let Cornelia finish. She only needs the final kill, not every kill.
This works even if Cornelia has been defeated — just don't deliver the last blow yourself.
cheatNotes: Round 1, Cornelia polymorphs every wizard. You keep your appearance but fight with a preset deck and stats for the whole battle.
Round 2 she announces the final blow must be hers. Treat it as the fight's rulebook: stop your damage before the last enemy dies.
If anyone but Cornelia defeats the last enemy, the fight ends normally but all enemies respawn and the quest doesn't progress — full restart. Bring the last enemy low, then stand down.
She only needs to finish the final enemy. Kill everything else yourself freely.
The respawn rule still applies even if Cornelia herself goes down, so never take the last kill.

name: Balaur (Balance, Standard)
cheats: [Late] "Fool! You dare to sneak in late?" - If a wizard joins the battle late, Balaur (Ice-Storm) will cheat-cast a version of Mana Burn that removes all of the wizard's pips.
[Round] "The world turns to my will!" - At the start of combat, Balaur (Balance) designates himself as "active" and all other Balaurs as "inactive".
All spells can only target the "active" Balaur, including AOE spells.
"Inactive" Balaurs will always pass until they are able to use a 7 pip or stronger spell.
Once the "active" Balaur is defeated, the next Balaur in sequence will become "active". The order of Balaurs is as follows:
[Balance] Balaur (Balance, Standard)
[Life] [Myth] Balaur (Life-Myth, Standard)
[Death] [Fire] Balaur (Death-Fire, Standard)
[Ice] [Storm] Balaur (Ice-Storm, Standard)

[Afterlife] "Even death cannot fell me!" "The wheel of fortune turns without you" - When the "active" Balaur dies, the next Balaur in sequence will become "active" and begin its cheats. All cheats from the previous defeated Balaur will be removed.
[Feint] At the start of the round, if Balaur has a feint on it, it will remove the feint. Indemnity does not stop this cheat. Other traps are not affected.
[Global] "I am the emperor of this realm. I decide its state." - If a wizard casts a Global spell, the "active" Balaur will replace it with its own Global.
"Let Spring rejuvenate our strength" - Casts Balance of Power upon becoming "active".
If the Order Idol has been placed, this head will also cast Quicken on all Balaur heads, which will be removed once this head is defeated.
"The star itself will replenish my pips!" - At the end of the first Round and every 4 Rounds thereafter while it is "active", Balaur (Balance) gains seven Power Pips.
"Let Summer sear with the sun" - Casts Circle of Thorns upon becoming "active".
At the end of each Round while it is "active", Balaur (Life-Myth) places a -20% Damage Ward on itself. This shield increases by 20% potency after each cast. Once it reaches -100%, this cheat stops.
"Let Autumn balance the scales of justice" - Removes all helpful Charms and helpful Wards from all Wizards and casts Deadzone upon becoming "active".
"Let Winter's temperance wither you all" - Casts Mana Burn on all Wizards and casts Balefrost upon becoming "active".
"My power is towering, now for the destruction" - At the end of each Round while it is "active", Balaur (Ice-Storm) gains two Power Pips and a Pip.
spellNotes: Casts a Natural Attack for 7 Pips that deals 780 Balance Damage to all Wizards
strategy: Enter before the fight begins; a late wizard is Mana Burned to zero pips by the Ice-Storm head.
You can only damage the current 'active' head, even with AoE, so treat this as four bosses in the fixed order: Balance, Life-Myth, Death-Fire, Ice-Storm.
The active head removes any Feint on it at the start of the Round and Indemnity will not save it. Set up with non-Feint traps, or Feint and hit in the same Round.
Kill each head fast. The Balance head refills to seven Power Pips every four Rounds, the Life-Myth head stacks growing damage wards up to -100%, and the Ice-Storm head gains pips every Round.

Funnel blades to one hitter and burst each active head down before its ramp gets out of hand, expecting charm and ward wipes from the Death-Fire head and Mana Burns from the Ice-Storm head between heads.
Balaur resists Balance and takes more from Myth, Life, and Death, so bring one of those schools for your hits.
cheatNotes: Join before combat: a late wizard gets Mana Burned by the Ice-Storm head and loses all pips.
At the start, the Balance head marks itself 'active' and the others 'inactive'.
Every spell, even AoE, only hits the active head, so you cannot cleave the heads down together.
Inactive heads pass until they can throw a 7-pip-or-stronger spell, then unload.
Kill the active head and the next in the fixed order takes over.
When the active head dies the next becomes active and all the dead head's cheats are removed.
It strips any Feint off itself at the start of the Round and Indemnity does not stop this; other traps stay, so lead with non-Feint traps or hit the same Round you Feint.
Cast a Global and the active head replaces it with its own.
The Balance head casts Balance of Power when it becomes active.
If the Order Idol was placed, this head also Quickens all heads until it dies.
At the end of Round 1 and every 4 Rounds after, the Balance head gains seven Power Pips.
The head casts Circle of Thorns when it becomes active.
Each Round the Life-Myth head stacks a damage ward on itself, growing 20% per cast up to -100%, so burst it down before it turtles.
The Death-Fire head strips all your helpful charms and wards and casts Deadzone when it becomes active.
The Ice-Storm head Mana Burns everyone and casts Balefrost when it becomes active.
Each Round the Ice-Storm head gains two Power Pips and a Pip, so it ramps quickly.

name: Balor the Broken Fang
cheats: "Prepare for Battle!" - Every second (or third) Round, he will hidden cast a Tower Shield (Sun -70%) on himself or a Minion.
Melt cannot dispel this cheat.
Pierce can be safely used to remove the Shield.
At the start of every third Round, he will cast his Damage over Time (DoT) Natural Attack (over 3 Rounds) at the most "intimidating" Wizard (most-cast Blades and/or Traps).
spellNotes: Uses a version of Shadow Shrike with +15 or +30 Backlash.
strategy: Team fight, 15,545 health plus a Behemoth minion. Bring Myth or Life damage — he takes +35% from both and resists Storm at 80%.

Know the intimidation rule: every third round his 3-round DoT natural attack targets whoever has the most blades and traps cast. Decide up front who eats it — either the tankiest player deliberately carries visible buffs, or the hitter accepts it and the team packs Triage.
Pack Pierce. Every second or third round he hides a -70% Sun Tower Shield on himself or the Behemoth. Melt does not work on it; a plain Pierce strips it safely.
Supports blade the hitter and Feint Balor; healer keeps Triage ready for the DoT target and heals through the Shadow Shrike chip.
Strip the Tower Shield with Pierce the round before the hit, then kill boss and Behemoth together with a bladed Myth or Life AoE.
cheatNotes: Every second or third round he sneaks a -70% Sun Tower Shield onto himself or his Behemoth. Assume a shield is up before any big hit and strip it first.
Melt will not remove this Tower Shield — do not waste the card.
Pierce removes the shield cleanly with no counter. Keep one in the deck at all times.
Every third round his 3-round DoT natural attack goes at the wizard with the most blades and traps cast. Whoever stacks buffs is volunteering — have Triage ready for them.

name: Balor the Broken Fang (Gallery)
cheats: "Thralls of Shadow, obey me!" - At the end of Round 1, Balor the Broken Fang will summon one Blackback Thrall and one Ashen Man Eater.
At the end of every second and fourth Round, the Ashen Man Eater will cast a +35% version of Stormblade on Balor.
At the end of every third Round, the Blackback Thrall will cast a Dragonblade on Balor.
"Prepare for battle!" - He will cast a Tower Shield (Sun -70%) on himself or an ally.
If Balor's Shadow Shrike is active during this cheat, the Shield will increase his Backlash.

Every 3 Rounds, he will cast his Damage over Time (DoT) Natural Attack (Balor) at the most "intimidating" Wizard (most Blades or casted Traps), dealing 200 initial Storm Damage + 600 Storm Damage over 3 Rounds.
spellNotes: This Boss uses a version of Shadow Shrike MOB with +15 or +30 Backlash.
strategy: Shadow Palace boss with 19,770 health and self-buffing summons, so run it as a team with one hitter, blading supports, and a healer.
Attack with Myth or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Storm resist without a prism.

End of round 1 he summons a Blackback Thrall and an Ashen Man Eater whose only job is to blade him (Stormblade rounds 2 and 4, Dragonblade every 3rd round) — AoE them down early or race him before his stack piles up.
His DoT natural attack (200 + 600 Storm over 3 rounds) every 3rd round targets the wizard holding the most blades/traps, so keep those on the hitter and have them stack late.
Bring Pierce or Shatter for his Tower Shield (Sun -70%), then funnel blades to the hitter and land one big Myth or Life hit.
cheatNotes: End of round 1 he summons a Blackback Thrall and an Ashen Man Eater that exist only to buff him. Clear them early or plan to out-race their blades.
The Ashen Man Eater feeds him a +35% Stormblade on rounds 2 and 4, so his hits grow each cycle — take him down before the stack builds.
The Blackback Thrall adds a Dragonblade every 3rd round, another stacking buff on Balor. Same answer: kill the minion or burst fast.
He throws up a Tower Shield (Sun -70%) on himself or a minion. Bring Pierce or Shatter, or overhit through it.
If his Shadow Shrike is active when he shields, his own backlash grows, so his attacks recoil harder on himself — minor, but it can help chip him.
Every 3rd round he DoTs the wizard holding the most blades/traps for 200 + 600 Storm over 3 rounds. Keep that load on the hitter and pack Triage.

name: Banrigh Sluagh (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet - Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: "Late to Doomsday, yer still doomed!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Banrigh Sluagh will cast a Curse +100% on all Wizards.

"The Dead are arming the Wizard! What a twist!" - At the start of the second Round, and every four Rounds thereafter, Unforgiven Dead (Balance) will cast a +30% version of Mass Hex on Banrigh Sluagh's team.
"The Banrigh Sluagh brings the curse of blight" - At the end of the second Round, and every three Rounds thereafter, Banrigh Sluagh will cast either Mass Infection or Plague.
"The Dead escape their bonds and bless the living" - At the start of the third Round, and every five Rounds thereafter, Unforgiven Dead (Star) will cast Magnify on all Wizards.
strategy: Hit with Life (+5% incoming); never attack into Death (73% resist).
Don't arrive late — late wizards eat a +100% Curse.

Exploit the minions: on round 2 and every 4 rounds the Balance minion drops a +30% Mass Hex on the boss side (free damage traps for you), and on round 3 and every 5 rounds the Star minion casts Magnify on all wizards — line up your hit right after round 3 to cash in both.
Watch the boss's Mass Infection/Plague (end of round 2 and every 3 rounds) cutting your healing — bring Cleanse Charm or don't lean on heals.
Blade up, wait for the Magnify and Hex traps, then land a big Life hit.
cheatNotes: Late wizards eat a +100% Curse — join on time.
Round 2 and every 4 rounds the Balance minion drops a +30% Mass Hex trap on the boss side — free damage traps, so line up a hit to use them.
End of round 2 and every 3 rounds he casts Mass Infection or Plague, weakening you and cutting healing — bring Cleanse Charm.
Round 3 and every 5 rounds the Star minion casts Magnify on all wizards, boosting your next spell — hit the same round to use it.

name: Banrigh Sluagh (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet - Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: "Late to Doomsday, yer still doomed!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Banrigh Sluagh will cast a Curse +100% on all Wizards.
"The Dead are arming the Wizard! What a twist!" - At the start of the second Round, Unforgiven Dead (Balance) will cast an AoE Hex +30% on the Boss' Team

strategy: Join on time. Arriving late hits the whole team with a +100% Curse.
She is Death, resists Death 73% and takes only +5% from Life. Pack Life hits or a prism and blade up.
On Round 2 her own Unforgiven Dead (Balance) drops a +30% Hex on the bosses for you. Build your own traps on top of that free trap and hold your hit.
Carry Cleanse Charm for her Mass Infection or Plague, which cut your outgoing damage before you can swing.
When the Star Dead casts Magnify on your team, hit that round or the next. The round after the Magnify she fires Mass Supernova and strips your charms.
Her Deathblade is Aegis-protected, so a single shatter will not remove it. Plan to hit through it.
cheatNotes: Being late hits everyone with a +100% Curse. Zone in promptly.
At the start of Round 2 her Unforgiven Dead lands a +30% Hex on the bosses, a free trap for you. Build on it instead of wasting a trap card.
Her Mass Infection or Plague cuts your outgoing damage. Cleanse Charm it off before your setup hit or the damage falls short.
The Star Dead's Magnify boosts your charms, but it is bait. Hit while it is up.
The round after that Magnify she casts Mass Supernova and strips your blades. Fire your attack before Supernova lands, not after.

name: Banrigh Sluagh (Tier 3)

strategy: Team gauntlet: assign a hitter, blading supports, and a healer. Everyone joins before the fight starts.
Set up with Feint and traps rather than blades. Round 7 Putrefaction rots all blades off your team, but traps and Feints survive.
Exploit the zombies: the Balance minion stacks a +30% Hex on the boss side (round 2) and the Star minion Magnifies your team (round 3). Time the hitter's attack right after the Magnify.
Healer carries the boss's Mass Infection/Plague. Infection cuts healing, Plague weakens damage. Cleanse or heal big.
From round 4 on he throws Mass Supernova at the whole team. Shield or heal through it.
Funnel to one hit; AoE the Unforgiven Dead minions if they overwhelm, otherwise pressure Banrigh.

Round 2 the Balance zombie stacks a +30% Hex on the boss's own side, meaning they take more damage. Land a hit while it is up.
He casts Mass Infection or Plague. Infection cuts your healing, Plague weakens your damage. Cleanse or heal accordingly.
Round 3 the Star zombie Magnifies your team, boosting outgoing damage. Line up your hitter's big attack the round after.
Putrefaction rots all blades off your team. Lean on Feints and traps instead, or blade and hit the same round.

name: Barbarous Van Der Borst (Portal of Peril, Tier 1)
locations: Portal of Peril: Spiral Cup (Portal of Peril) - Spiral Cup (Portal of Peril, Tier 1)
cheats: [Minion] "Time for physical education!" - At the end of the first Round, this Boss says and spawns his Minions.
[Charm] '"At the beginning of every round, Barbarous Van Der Borst wil cast Bladestorm.
[Afterlife] "Time for extra credit!" - If the Minions are defeated before the Boss, he will summon one Rude Goblin (Portal of Peril, Tier 2); at a time.
strategy: Hit Storm. He takes +50% from Storm and resists Myth 80%, so prism if you are Myth.
Focus the boss, not the minions. If the minions die first he keeps resummoning a Rude Goblin one at a time.
Do not slow-play. He gives his whole side a Bladestorm every Round, so their hits escalate fast.
Blade and trap the boss over two or three Rounds, then land one single-target Storm hit before the adds snowball.
cheatNotes: He spawns his minions (two Edacious Ogres and a Rude Goblin) at the end of Round 1.
Every Round he gives his whole side a Bladestorm, so their attacks grow each Round. Kill quickly.
Kill the boss before the minions. If the minions fall first he keeps summoning a fresh Rude Goblin one at a time.

name: Barbarous Van Der Borst (Portal of Peril, Tier 3)
locations: Portal of Peril: Spiral Cup (Portal of Peril) - Spiral Cup (Portal of Peril, Tier 3)
cheats: [Minion] "Time for physical education!" - At the end of the first Round, Barbarous Van Der Borst will summon the following Minions in a set order:
Edacious Ogre (Portal of Peril, Tier 3)
Rude Goblin (Portal of Peril, Tier 3)

If the Rude Goblin occupies the far right slot, the blade will be given to themselves.
spellNotes: Natural Attack deals an unknown amount of Myth Damage per Pip (3 Pips max).
strategy: Hit Storm - he takes boosted Storm damage and resists Fire, Ice, and Myth. He is not stunable, so plan around his cheats rather than locking him.
Expect two summons at the end of round 1 (Edacious Ogre, then Rude Goblin). Do not tunnel the minions - killing a minion before Barbarous just respawns a Rude Goblin at end of round.
Focus Barbarous himself. Because he self-blades every round and goblins keep feeding blades, the fight only shortens by killing the boss, not the adds.
Plan your kill as one big Storm AoE that takes the boss and both minions together, so nothing respawns after he dies.
Track his growing blades: he gains a 20-25% Bladestorm every round and goblins stack Balanceblades. Feint the boss and swing before his stacked charms turn a natural attack into a spike.
cheatNotes: At the end of round 1 he summons minions in a fixed order.
At the start of every round he self-casts a Bladestorm - either an unprotected 20% or a protected 25% - so his attack grows each round you delay.
If any minion dies before Barbarous does, a Rude Goblin is summoned at the end of the round to replace it. Kill the boss first or kill everything at once.
Only one Rude Goblin is replaced per round, so a same-turn multi-kill still thins their numbers.
Each round any Rude Goblin present casts Balanceblade on the enemy to its right, stacking blades across his side.
A Rude Goblin in the far-right slot blades itself instead.

name: Barbarous Van Der Borst (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 1)

strategy: He has only 840 health. If you can, burst him down before the second Round, before he spawns his minions.
Hit with Storm; he only lightly resists Myth (40%) and takes a small Storm boost.

While any minions are alive, each creature passes a Dragonblade left down the line and the last one casts Tempest for up to 320 Storm. Clearing the minions stops it.

locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 2)

Rude Goblin (Tier 2) will have 750 Health and Edacious Ogre (Tier 2) has 830 Health.

Every Round his minions are in battle, Boss will cast Balanceblade (+35%) on first Minion and each Creature casts this blade on the next Minion until it's the last Creature's turn in which it cheat-casts Tempest dealing 160, 240, 320, or 400 Storm Damage.
If Boss is not first, the first minion will start the sequence, but the Boss will not cast the blade. The rest of his minions will continue the cheat.
If a Minion dies, he will summon a Rude Goblin (Tier 2) in its place.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage.
strategy: Kill him round 1 or 2 if you can — his minions don't spawn until round 2, and at 1,640 health with 20% Storm boost one buffed Storm hit ends it before the cheats start.
If the fight goes past round 2, expect a cheat Tempest (160-400 Storm damage) at the end of the enemy turn every round the minions are up, fed by a Balanceblade chain down his line.
Don't pick minions off one at a time — every dead minion is replaced by a fresh Rude Goblin (750 health). Use one AoE that kills the boss and both minions together.
He resists Myth 50%, so Myth wizards should pack a prism or off-school hits.
cheatNotes: You get one free round: the Rude Goblin and Edacious Ogre only appear on round 2.
Minion health for planning your AoE: Rude Goblin 750, Edacious Ogre 830.

While minions are alive, the enemy team chains a +35% Balanceblade down the line each round and the last creature cheats a Tempest for 160-400 Storm damage. Expect this hit every round the minions stand — shield or outpace it.
If the boss doesn't act first, the blade chain starts with the first minion instead (the boss skips his blade), but the Tempest still comes.
Dead minions are replaced with a new Rude Goblin, so clearing them one by one gains nothing. Save damage for one AoE finish.

name: Barbarous Van Der Borst (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 3)
cheats: "Time for physical education!" - At the end of the first Round, this Boss says and spawns his Minions.
The Minions have higher Health than in the last (Level 2) battle: the two Edacious Ogre (Tier 3) Minions have 785 Health, and the Rude Goblin (Tier 3) has 750.

"Extra credit time!" - Boss will only say this at the start when he has full minions. On every Round after the second Round, each Creature will use a Dragonblade to the right Creature until the last Creature cheat-casts Tempest.
This cheat will occur even if the boss isn't in combat.
Barbarous Van Der Borst will not cast Dragonblade if he's not in the first duel slot.
If three Minions have their turn before the Boss, they'll cast in order second, third, and first.
Barbarous Van Der Borst doesn't cast Tempest when in the last duel slot.
"Time for extra credit!" - If the Minions are defeated before the Boss, he will summon one Rude Goblin (Tier 3) at at time.
strategy: Speed is the whole strategy. From every round after the second, his crew chains Dragonblades down the line and the last creature fires a cheat Tempest. You want the fight decided before that engine gets rolling.
He starts alone with 2,035 health; the two Ogres (785 each) and the Goblin (750) spawn at the end of round 1.
Hit with Storm — he takes +40% from it — and never Myth into his 70% resist.
Kill the boss first. Clearing minions first just makes him summon a fresh Rude Goblin each turn, and the Dragonblade-Tempest chain keeps running off whoever is left.
Ideal line: blade round 1, then a bladed Storm AoE on round 2-3 that deletes the boss and the freshly spawned minions before the Tempest chain fires more than once.
If a Tempest slips through, it comes off stacked Dragonblades — keep a Tower Shield or heal in hand for round 3 onward.
cheatNotes: He fights round 1 alone, then spawns his minions at the end of it. Use the free round to blade.
The Tier 3 minions are beefier: two Edacious Ogres at 785 health and a Rude Goblin at 750. A single decent AoE still clears them.
From round 3 on, each enemy passes a Dragonblade to the right and the last one fires a cheat Tempest. Every extra round adds blades to that Tempest — end the fight fast.
The blade chain keeps going even for creatures not actively in combat, so you cannot stall it out.
If the boss is not in the first duel slot he skips his own Dragonblade, slightly weakening the chain — nothing you control, just less incoming.
Turn-order quirk in how the minions sequence their blades; it does not change your plan.
If the boss sits in the last duel slot he will not be the one casting Tempest — a minion will.
Killing minions before the boss makes him summon Rude Goblins one at a time. That is why the boss dies first.

Stack blades and Feint, then finish with an AoE. He is Myth, so hit with Storm to clear the boss and minions together.
Do not leave the boss for last. If the minions die first he summons a Rude Goblin every round until all spaces refill, so kill the boss with the same AoE or take him down first.

name: Baron Hairkonnen
cheats: "H-H-Hruh. You're late!" - If a Wizard joins after the second Round has started, Baron Hairkonnen will cast a Balance version of Steal Pips at the Wizard, which steals 3 pips and deals Balance Damage.
"H-H-Hruh. Meow! Haha!" - At the beginning of Round 1, he will cast a permanent -200% version of Brace aura that makes him immune to all damage.
Aura says Invulnerability when used.
Wizards can only damage the Baron after defeating all of his Minions.
Armor piercing that deals more than +100% will bypass the aura's effects (e.g. armor pierce equipment, spear spells, globals, Infallible, Shadow Shrike, etc.)
"A sick Qhat doesn't deserve that!" - If a Wizard removes Baron Hairkonnen's Aura, he will recast it.
"Feel the impact of my illness!" - At the beginning of Round 1 (after he casts his immunity aura), he will cast a Myth spell that summons one of the following Minions:
The number of Minions summoned is equal to the number of Wizards engaged in the duel.
This cheat will also be triggered by Wizards who join after the battle has started.
"I embrace the madness!" - The turn after all Minions are defeated, he will cheat-cast a +45% (in) / +35% (out) version of Berserk.
strategy: His health is only 9, so any hit that actually connects kills him. The whole fight is getting one hit to land through his invulnerability aura.
Enter on time. Late joiners get 3 pips stolen, take Balance damage, and add an extra summoned Minion.
Expect a permanent immunity aura on Round 1 plus one Mirror Image minion (Death, Ice, or Storm) summoned per Wizard.
Do not waste turns removing the aura; he recasts it. Instead build a hit with over +100% armor pierce using pierce gear, a spear or pierce enchant, Infallible, and Shadow Shrike to bypass it.
Clear the summoned Minion(s) first; you cannot damage the Baron until they are all gone.
The turn after the last Minion dies he cheat-casts Berserk that adds +45% to his incoming damage. Use that window to land your pierced hit and end his 9 health instantly.
cheatNotes: If you join after Round 2 he steals 3 pips and hits you with Balance damage, so be in the fight on time.
From Round 1 he wears a permanent -200% Brace aura that makes him immune to all damage, so you cannot simply hit him down.
You can only deal damage to the Baron after every one of his summoned Minions is dead.
Armor pierce above +100% punches through the immunity aura. Stack pierce gear, a spear or pierce enchant, Infallible, and Shadow Shrike so your hit connects.
Stripping his aura is pointless; he immediately recasts it. Bypass it with pierce instead of trying to remove it.
On Round 1 he summons a Mirror Image minion (Death, Ice, or Storm) that you must clear.
He summons one Minion per Wizard in the fight, so solo you face a single Mirror Image.
Late-joining Wizards each add another summoned Minion, another reason to be on time.
The turn after you clear all Minions he casts a Berserk that raises his incoming damage by 45%, a perfect window to land your pierced killing hit.

name: Baron von Bracken (Tier 1)
locations: Wysteria: Tanglewood Way - Von Bracken's Grove
cheats: "Feel the sting of my nettles!" - If a Feint is cast on Baron von Bracken, he'll cast a Natural Attack dealing 210, 315, or 420 Death Damage on the Wizard.

"Nature Will Always Find a Way!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Steal Spell on Baron von Bracken that deals less than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (< 25,000), he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his max Health, and use a Shadow version of Triage on himself.
Casting a Damage over Time Spell without the initial hit, such as Scald, will trigger this cheat.
"Curses!" - This cheat will not trigger if the Damage Spell deals more than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (> 25,000).
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Feint (+70%/+30%).
strategy: Bring Life damage (he takes +30%) and heavy blades; skip Death spells into his 45% resist.
Do not open with Feint on him. A Feint makes him counter with a Death natural attack of up to 420.
Any hit or steal under 25,000 (half his health) fully heals him with Blessing and Triages your damage-over-time off, so never chip him.
Stack blades until a single hit clears 25,000, then fire; repeat once more to finish his 50,000 health.
Avoid pure damage-over-time spells like Scald; a DoT with no upfront hit counts as a sub-threshold hit and heals him.
cheatNotes: Casting Feint on him triggers a Death natural attack of 210, 315, or 420 on you; rely on blades instead of Feint here.
Any damage or steal under 25,000 (half his max health) makes him Blessing to full-heal and Triage off your DoTs; every hit must exceed 25,000.
A damage-over-time with no initial hit, like Scald, counts as a low hit and triggers the full heal; do not use bare DoTs.
Hits above 25,000 are safe and skip the heal, so build blades until one shot clears the threshold.

name: Baron von Bracken (Tier 2)
locations: Wysteria: Tanglewood Way - Von Bracken's Grove
cheats: [Death] "Feel the sting of my nettles!" - If a Feint is cast on Baron von Bracken, he'll cast a Natural Attack dealing 210, 315, or 430 Death Damage on the Wizard.

[Heal] "Nature Will Always Find a Way!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Steal Spell on Baron von Bracken that deals less than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (< 100,000), he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his max Health, and use a Shadow version of Triage on himself.
Casting a Damage over Time Spell without the initial hit, such as Scald, will trigger this cheat.
"Curses!" - This cheat will not trigger if the Damage Spell deals more than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (> 100,000).
strategy: Never Feint him. Any Feint triggers a Death Natural Attack for 210-430 on the caster. Set up with blades and non-Feint traps only.
Never chip him. Any damage or steal under 100,000 (50% of his max health) makes him full-heal and Triage himself. Every hit must clear 100,000.
Funnel all buffs onto one hitter (Elemental and Spirit blades, damage traps that are not Feint) and land one enormous hit over 100,000.
Avoid DoT-only spells like Scald that skip the initial hit; they count as sub-50% damage and trigger the full heal.
You need two 100,000+ hits to clear his 200,000 health. Hit off-school since he resists Death 55% and takes +25% from Life.
cheatNotes: Casting any Feint on him makes him fire a Death Natural Attack at the caster for 210, 315, or 430. Skip Feint entirely.
Any damage or steal spell that deals less than 100,000 makes him heal to full and Triage. You must one-shot for 100,000+.
Damage-over-Time spells cast without their initial hit (like Scald) read as under-50% damage and trigger the full heal.
A hit over 100,000 is safe and does not trigger the heal, so build big and swing once.

name: Baron von Bracken (Tier 3)
locations: Wysteria: Tanglewood Way - Von Bracken's Grove
cheats: [Death] "Feel the sting of my nettles!" - If a Feint is cast on Baron von Bracken, he'll cast a Natural Attack dealing 420 Death Damage on the Wizard.

[Heal] "Nature Will Always Find a Way!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Steal Spell on Baron von Bracken that deals less than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (< 500,000), he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his max Health, and use a Shadow version of Triage on himself.
Casting a Damage over Time Spell without the initial hit, such as Scald, will trigger this cheat.
"Curses!" - This cheat will not trigger if the Damage Spell deals more than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (> 500,000).
strategy: This is a one-shot puzzle: any hit under 500,000 damage (less than 50% of his health) makes him full-heal and Triage himself. You must land a single hit over 500,000 or reset.
Never chip him and never cast a damage-over-time or Scald-style DoT - a DoT with no upfront hit also triggers the full heal.
Stack everything on one hitter - every blade, Feint, and trap - over several rounds. He resists Death 65% and takes 15% more from Life, so hit with Life or prism off Death.
Feinting is fine but each Feint draws a 420 Death natural attack on the caster; heal through it, the +70% trap is worth it.
When your hitter can clear 500,000 in one cast, fire the single hit. Anything less full-heals him back to 1,000,000.
cheatNotes: Feinting him triggers a 420 Death natural attack on the caster. Minor - heal through it, the trap is worth it.
Any damage or steal spell under 500,000 (less than 50% of his health) makes him full-heal and Triage himself. Only a single hit over 500,000 works.
A DoT with no upfront hit (like Scald) counts as a sub-500k hit and triggers the full heal. Avoid all DoTs.
The heal does NOT trigger when your hit exceeds 500,000 - that one huge hit is the entire fight.

name: Baron von Bracken (Tier 4)
locations: Wysteria: Tanglewood Way - Von Bracken's Grove
cheats: "Feel the sting of my nettles!" - If a Feint is cast on Baron von Bracken, he'll cast a Natural Attack dealing 420 Death Damage on the Wizard.
The Balance version of Feint will not activate this cheat.

"Nature Will Always Find a Way!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Steal Spell on Baron von Bracken that deals less than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (< 1,000,000), he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his max Health, and use a Shadow version of Triage on himself.
Casting a Damage over Time Spell without the initial hit, such as Scald, will trigger this cheat.
"Curses!" - This cheat will not trigger if the Damage Spell deals more than 50% of the Boss's Max Health (> 1,000,000).
strategy: Kill the minions first. The Life minions cast endless Bladestorms and Pigsies, so three blades plus an AoE should clear them in round one before you build on the Baron.
This is a one-shot puzzle: any damage or steal spell under 1,000,000 (half his 2,000,000 health) heals him to full and Triages off all DoTs. Chipping is impossible — build one colossal hit.

Assign one hitter to carry the school-specific buffs (school blade, school spear, elemental/spirit blade and trap, Sharpened Blade, Potent Trap, Shadow Shrike for 50% pierce). Supports feed Balance/Death spells and treasure-card blades.
Do not cast Feint directly on the Baron — he retaliates with a Feint or a 420 Death natural attack. Mass Feint (and the Balance version of Feint) is exempt, so stack trap damage that way.
Hit into Life if you can (+5%) and never into Death — he resists it 73%. His own +40% Deathblade and +10% Deathspear are protected, so the healer keeps the team up through his 181% boosted damage.
Never throw a DoT or probing hit — even a Scald with no upfront damage counts as an under-threshold spell and resets him to full.
When the hitter can clear 1,000,000+ in one shot, go. A hit over half his health skips the heal entirely; two such hits, or one monster hit, finish him.
cheatNotes: Casting Feint on him gets the caster hit with a 420 Death natural attack. Annoying but survivable — still, prefer the exempt Balance Feint.
The Balance-school version of Feint does NOT trigger the counter. It's the trap of choice for this fight.
Any damage or steal spell dealing less than 1,000,000 makes him heal 100% of his health and Triage all overtime effects. Never attack until the team's single hit can break that number.
Even a DoT with no up-front damage (like Scald) counts as a failed hit and triggers the full heal. No probing attacks of any kind.
Hits above 1,000,000 (over half his max health) bypass the heal completely. That threshold is the entire fight plan.

name: Bartleby Quietus
cheats: Cheat Cycle

[Round] "I won't let you destroy me!" - Every turn, Bartleby Quietus will cast a Spell that applies hanging effects corresponding to one of the six non-Balance Schools of Magic. Whenever there are enough of any hanging effect to trigger a Scion Gambit, he will cheat-cast the corresponding Scion Spell.
This cheat can trigger multiple times in the same Round.
Hanging Effect Counters
[Charm] Helpful Charms
If the Wizard casts a helpful Charm, Bartleby Quietus will remove all helpful Charms from the Wizard and do one of the following:
Applies two +30% Death Damage Charms on himself
Applies two -20% Damage Charms on the Wizard
Applies two +35% Ice Damage Wards on the Wizard
Applies two -40% Damage Wards on himself
[Curse] Harmful Charms
If the Wizard casts a harmful Charm, Bartleby Quietus will remove all harmful Charms from himself and do one of the following:
Applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 291 Myth Damage after 3 Rounds
Applies two +35% Myth Damage Wards on the Wizard
[Jinx] Harmful Wards
If the Wizard casts a harmful Ward, including Prisms, Bartleby Quietus will remove all harmful Wards from himself and do one of the following:
Applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 312 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds
Applies two +70% Damage over Time Wards on the Wizard
Applies two +30% Life Damage Charms on the Wizard (bugged)
Applies a Heal over Time effect on the Wizard that restores 321 Health over 3 Rounds (bugged)
[Ward] Helpful Wards
If the Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Bartleby Quietus will do one of the following:
Removes all helpful Wards from himself and applies a Heal over Time effect on himself that restores 432 Health over 3 Rounds (bugged)
Removes all helpful Wards from the Wizard and applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 436 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds
Removes all helpful Wards from the Wizard and applies two +35% Myth Damage Wards on the Wizard
[Damage over Time] Damage Over Time
If the Wizard casts a Damage Over Time Spell, Bartleby Quietus will remove all Damage Over Time effects from the himself and do one of the following:
Applies a -25% Damage Charm on the Wizard.
Applies a +30% Storm Damage Charms on the Wizard (bugged)
Applies a Heal over Time effect on the Wizard that restores 432 Health over 3 Rounds (bugged)
Applies a Damage over Time effect on the Wizard that deals 374 Life Damage after 3 Rounds
[Aura] Auras
"Your future is my memory." - If the Wizard casts a helpful Aura on themself, it will immediately be removed.
Bartleby Quietus will also say this at the beginning of the first Round with no effect.
[Global] Global
"Your future is my memory." - If the Wizard casts a Global spell, it will immediately be removed.
Helpful Cheats
[Round] "He's all-seeing, Wiz-Kid. He knows what you're going to do." - At the end of every third Round, Miranda Briar warns the Wizard.
[Spell] "Here, take this, just something I dreamed up." - At the end of the sixth Round, Miranda's Dream is added to the Wizard's Hand.
[Miranda's Dream]
This Spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat Bartleby Quietus.
spellNotes: Any Spells that Bartleby Quietus casts do not consume any Pips.
Casts a Fire Spell that applies two Damage over Time effects that each deal 275 Fire Damage over 10 Rounds.
strategy: Understand the gimmick before you pack: he punishes almost every setup spell. Blades, weaknesses, shields, prisms, auras, globals, and damage-over-time all trigger a counter-cheat. Plan to win with raw pips and flat hits, not the usual blade stack.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (he takes +30% each); never Balance (65% resist) and avoid the elemental schools (35% resist).
Skip prisms, shields, and auras entirely. Prisms count as harmful wards, self-shields are helpful wards, and any helpful aura is stripped instantly, so all are wasted or punished.
Watch his per-turn Scion counter. Every turn he stacks a hanging effect of a non-Balance school and cheat-casts that school's Scion once enough pile up, so do not feed one school repeatedly.
Bring Triage and heals for his Fire DOTs. He casts a Fire spell applying two DOTs of 275 Fire each over 10 Rounds, plus his counter-cheats add more.
Pool pips and hit with enchanted single hits or shadow hits you can afford without blading. At 15,000 Health this is a team fight: rotate damage while a healer manages his DOTs.
cheatNotes: Every turn he stacks hanging effects of a non-Balance school and cheat-casts that school's Scion spell once enough of one school pile up. Do not feed one school repeatedly.
This can fire more than once in a single Round, so multiple Scions can land back to back.
Casting a helpful Charm strips all your charms and gives him a random buff or you a random debuff, so do not blade on yourself here.
Casting a harmful Charm on him (a weakness) clears his harmful charms and hits you with a random Myth punish.
Casting a harmful Ward on him, including Prisms, wipes his harmful wards and applies a random DOT or ward to you. Skip prisms.
Casting a helpful Ward triggers removal plus a random effect: sometimes he heals himself, sometimes you take Fire over time. Do not shield up.
Casting a damage-over-time spell clears all DOTs off him and hands you a random debuff or DOT, so do not rely on DOT damage here.
Any helpful Aura you cast on yourself is removed instantly, so auras are wasted pips.
He says the same line harmlessly on Round 1; ignore it then.
Any Global you cast is removed instantly, so do not bother with Globals.
Every third Round Miranda Briar warns you before a cheat; use it as a timing cue.
At the end of Round 6 she adds Miranda's Dream to your hand as a bonus.
You do not need to cast Miranda's Dream to win.

name: Basil the Brigand
locations: Novus: Puerto Nuovo - Saboteur Cave,La Ville Rose - Matheus' House
cheats: [Global][Blade][Trap] "The dance of battle is child's play to one such as me!" - Occasionally, when any Global, Blade, or Trap Spell is cast by either side, excluding Prisms, Basil the Brigand will cast one of the following Spells:
Plague
Plague (-25%)
Mass Weakness
Tower Shield
Protected Tower Shield (-55%)
Legion Shield
Protected Frozen Armor (185)
strategy: Basil resists 45% to every school except Life (+5%), so hit with Life or your highest-pierce hitter; he also carries 18% pierce and a 113% damage boost, so respect his return hits.

His cheat fires occasionally whenever either side casts a Global, Blade, or Trap (Prisms excluded), so minimize how many charm and trap casts you make. Multi-blades like Bladestorm and Prisms set up with fewer triggers.
Expect weaknesses (Plague, Mass Weakness) on your team and shields on himself, so pack Cleanse Charm and a way to break shields (Shatter or a wand hit) before your kill shot.
Protect your key blade or Feint with Aegis or Indemnity, then break any shield he stacked and land one big Life hit.
cheatNotes: Occasionally, when either side casts a Global, Blade, or Trap (Prisms excluded), Basil casts one of the spells below. Keep your blade and trap casts few to limit the triggers.
Plague: a -25% damage weakness on your whole team. Cleanse it before you hit.
A weaker Plague, another team-wide damage weakness. Cleanse or eat it before the kill shot.
Mass Weakness: a next-attack weakness on all wizards. Cleanse Charm clears it.
A -50% Tower Shield on himself. Pop it before your real hit.
A -55% Tower Shield behind an Aegis, so it survives one removal. Strip the Aegis first, then break the shield.
Legion Shield covers his whole side. Shatter or wand it down before committing.
An Aegis-protected Frozen Armor absorbing 185 damage. Clear the Aegis, then break the absorb before your nuke.

name: Bearded Dragon (Sun) (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Night Mire Gauntlet - Night Mire Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: [Late] "Just what we needed- time to bask under the sun!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.
[Round] "Moon and Stars keep Sun eternal!" - At the start of the battle, Bearded Dragon (Sun) says this.

[Afterlife] "You cannot defeat us at full strength!" - If Bearded Dragon (Sun) is defeated before or at the same time as Moon and Star Bearded Dragons, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Ra, dealing 75% of Wizard's max Health, and use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.

[Afterlife] "As long as two remain we shall survive!" - If Bearded Dragon (Sun) is defeated before or at the same time as one other Boss, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Power Nova, dealing 50% of Wizard's max Health, and use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.
Cheat Cycle:
Beginning of Round 2:
[Trap] "The Sun Rises" - Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast Sun Trap (+25%).
[Blade] "The Stars Twinkle" - Either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast Star Blade on a random ally.
[Shield] "The Moon Glows" - Either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast Moon Shield on a random ally.
[Spell] End of Round 2:
"The Radiant Sun Glows" - Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast one of the following Spells:
Blinding Light, Guardian Spirit, Smoke Screen
(Note: Guardian Spirit will only target either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon)
"Star Light Shine Bright" - Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast one of the following Spells:
Unicorn, Restoring Rain 2, Virulent Plague
"We Dance in the Moonlight" - Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast one of the following Spells:
Legion Shield, Grendel's Amends (Tier 3b), Mass Infection
strategy: Enter before round 1. Joining late triggers a Sun Blessing that fully heals every dragon.

Kill order is everything: defeat the Moon and Star dragons first and leave Sun, the boss, for last. Killing Sun before or with the others triggers a Sun Ra or Power Nova AoE plus a full heal for the survivors.
It has no boost weakness and 15% universal resist, so pick your strongest reliable school and stack blades.

Expect the round-2 cycle: a Sun Trap on the boss, a Star Blade and Moon Shield on the dragons, then buffs and heals like Unicorn, Restoring Rain, or Guardian Spirit. Bring infection or a heal-cut, or just out-pace their healing.
Burst the two minions down together, then finish Sun alone so no afterlife cheat fires.
cheatNotes: Joining after round 1 triggers a Sun Blessing that restores all three dragons to full health.
Kill Sun before or at the same time as both Moon and Star and he fires a Sun Ra for 75% of your max health, then fully heals the group.
Kill Sun before or with just one other dragon and he fires a Sun Power Nova for 50% max health plus a full group heal. Sun must die last and alone.
Round 2 he puts a +25% Sun Trap on himself.
Round 2 a Moon or Star dragon gives a random ally a Star Blade.
Round 2 a Moon or Star dragon gives a random ally a Moon Shield.
End of round 2 the Sun dragon casts one of Blinding Light, Guardian Spirit, or Smoke Screen.
End of round 2 a Moon or Star dragon casts one of Unicorn, Restoring Rain 2, or Virulent Plague.
End of round 2 a Moon or Star dragon casts one of Legion Shield, Grendel's Amends, or Mass Infection.

name: Bearded Dragon (Sun) (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Night Mire Gauntlet - Night Mire Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: "Just what we needed- time to bask under the sun!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.

"As long as two remain we shall survive!" - If Bearded Dragon (Sun) is defeated before or at the same time as one other Boss, Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast a Sun version of Power Nova, dealing 50% of Wizard's max Health, and use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health to all allies.
Cheat Cycle:
Beginning of Round 2:
"The Sun Rises" - Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast Sun Trap (+25%).
"The Stars Twinkle" - Either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast Star Blade on a random ally.
"The Moon Glows" - Either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon will cast Moon Shield on a random ally.
End of Round 2:
"The Radiant Sun Glows" - Bearded Dragon (Sun) will cast one of the following Spells:
Blinding Light, Guardian Spirit, Smoke Screen
(Note: Guardian Spirit will only target either Moon or Star Bearded Dragon)

strategy: Kill order is the fight: Moon and Star Bearded Dragons must both die before Sun. Kill Sun first and he answers with a Sun Ra for 75% of everyone's max health plus a full-team enemy heal; kill him alongside one survivor and it is a 50% Power Nova plus full heal.
That also bans the easy AoE finish. Never let an AoE drop Sun before or at the same time as the others — single-target the Moon and Star dragons down first.
Enter together on round 1. A late joiner triggers Sun Blessing, restoring 100% health to all three dragons and undoing your progress.

Expect the round 2 script: Sun Trap on a wizard, Star Blade and Moon Shield on their side, then support casts (heals, Virulent Plague, Legion Shield, Mass Infection) at round end. Watch for Guardian Spirit landing on Moon or Star — confirm the target is actually staying dead before switching.
No boost school exists (20% resist to everything), so pick your best school, blade normally, and burst Moon first, then Star, then turn everything on Sun.
Sun has 12,500 health with no kill-order penalty left once he is alone — set up a clean single-target or AoE kill.
cheatNotes: A wizard joining after round 1 triggers Sun Blessing: all three dragons heal to full. Full team from the start, no mid-fight ports.
If Sun dies before (or at the same time as) both Moon and Star, he cheats a Sun Ra for 75% of each wizard's max health and fully heals his allies. This is the fight's hard rule: Sun dies last.
Same trap one step earlier: if Sun dies while one other dragon still stands, it is a 50% max-health Power Nova plus a full ally heal. Never include Sun in your AoEs until he is the last one standing.
Start of round 2 he puts a +25% Sun Trap on someone — expect a boosted follow-up on that wizard.
Start of round 2 a Star Blade goes on a random enemy, buffing their next hit.
Start of round 2 a Moon Shield goes on a random enemy — check wards before committing a hit.
At the end of round 2 Sun casts Blinding Light, Guardian Spirit, or Smoke Screen. Stun blocks and accuracy enchants blunt two of the three.
Guardian Spirit only ever goes on Moon or Star — one of them may pop back up after you kill it. Re-kill before moving on.
The Moon/Star support casts include Unicorn and Virulent Plague. Cleanse the plague off your hitter before the big hit.
Legion Shield or Mass Infection can land here — shields on their side and infections on yours. Pack a shield-breaker and Cleanse Charm.

strategy: This is a 3-boss gauntlet and kill order is the whole fight: Bearded Dragon (Sun) must die LAST. If Sun falls before or with the others it Ra's 75% of your max health and full-heals the enemy team.
Focus the Moon and Star Dragons down first. The hitter funnels blades while supports blade and Feint the target dragon.
