They have no incoming boost and 40% universal resist, so over-stack blades and traps to punch through.
Kill Sun last in a single clean hit. If any wizard joins late, Sun full-heals the team, so no stragglers.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard makes Sun full-heal the whole enemy team. Everyone must be in from the start.
If Sun dies before or at the same time as both other dragons, he Ra's 75% of your max health and full-heals everyone. Kill Sun last.
Even killing Sun alongside just one other dragon triggers a 50% max-health Power Nova and a full heal. Sun always dies alone, last.
Round 2 he places a +25% Sun Trap. Treat it as a minor buff and keep to your plan.
Moon or Star blades one of their own each round 2, so expect their damage buffed and re-Feint as needed.
They also shield an ally on round 2. Over-stack blades so a shield does not stop your hit.
Random effects here can be Blinding Light (accuracy down) or Guardian Spirit on a dragon. Carry accuracy and expect a revive-heal buff on them.
They may heal with Unicorn or Restoring Rain, or drop Virulent Plague on you. Pack infection removal and burst faster than they heal.
They can Legion Shield the enemy team or Mass Infect you. Clear infections before you heal and re-blade through the shields.

name: Beast With A Million Eyes
locations: Darkmoor: Mortal Plain - House of a Million Eyes

stats.resist: 29% to all schools59% to all schools

cheats: Note: These Cheats do not start until the Second Round.
[Polymorph] Immediately after Beast with a Million Eyes casts a Polymorph Spell, one of the following Cheats will trigger, depending on the Spell that was cast:
[Ice] Shift Dread Paladin: "I see with my million eyes... Ice!" - Casts Sleet Storm.
[Life] Shift GreenOak: "I see with my million eyes... Life!" - Casts Devotion.
[Fire] Shift Grendel: "I see with my million eyes... Fire!" - Casts Furnace.
[Myth] Shift Ogre: "I see with my million eyes... Myth!" - Casts Reliquary.
[Storm] Shift Piscean: "I see with my million eyes... Storm!" - Casts Galvanic Field.
[Death] Shift Sugar Glider "I see with my million eyes... Death!" - Casts Virulence.

[Afterlife] "Wrong!" - If Beast With A Million Eyes is killed while the cheat is active, he will revive himself to 100% HP. Depending on the Aura that Beast with a Million Eyes has, and what School's damage is dealt to it, Beast with a Million Eyes will retaliate with various Spells:
While Sleet Storm is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Fire and Myth Spells, or else it will cast Lord of Winter.
While Devotion is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Death and Storm Spells, or else it will cast Spinysaur.
While Furnace is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Life and Storm Spells, or else it will cast Sun Serpent.
While Reliquary is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Fire and Life Spells, or else it will cast Celestial Calendar.
While Galvanic Field is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Death and Ice Spells, or else it will cast Storm Owl.
While Virulence is active, the Beast can be safely damaged with Ice and Myth Spells, or else it will cast Avenging Fossil.
For Spells that do multiple Schools of damage, such as Hydra, each school will be counted separately, and if any of them would cause this Cheat to trigger, it will trigger once.
[Round] "Hahahahahahahaha!" - At the end of any Round that Beast with a Million Eyes did not take damage during, it will cast one of the following at random:
Blizzard
Ratatoskr's Spin
Meteor Strike
Humongofrog
Tempest
Sandstorm
Ship of Fools
strategy: Use Round 1 freely; the cheats do not begin until Round 2. Set up blades and a trap early while it is safe.

Track the Beast's Polymorph. Each shift casts a specific aura (Ice/Sleet Storm, Life/Devotion, Fire/Furnace, Myth/Reliquary, Storm/Galvanic Field, Death/Virulence) that dictates which two schools may safely damage it that turn.
Match your hit to the safe schools for the active aura. Hitting with a wrong school triggers a big retaliation spell (Lord of Winter, Spinysaur, Sun Serpent, and so on).
Never damage it while its kill cheat is armed. Killed with the cheat active, it revives to 100%. Strip or wait out the aura, or land your finisher only with the currently safe school.
Do damage every round. At the end of any round it took no damage, it free-casts a random AoE (Blizzard, Tempest, Meteor Strike, etc.), so keep chipping even between big setups.
It is Moon with 59% Storm resist but takes 30% boosted Myth, so a Myth hitter fits many of the safe-school windows; watch multi-school spells since each school is checked separately.
cheatNotes: Its cheats are dormant on Round 1, giving you one free setup turn before you must play around the aura rules.
Right after it Polymorphs, a cheat fires based on the shift it took, applying the aura that sets that turn's safe damage schools.
Ice shift casts Sleet Storm.
Life shift casts Devotion.
Fire shift casts Furnace.
Myth shift casts Reliquary.
Storm shift casts Galvanic Field.
Death shift casts Virulence.
If you kill it while the retaliation cheat is active it revives to full Health, so clear or account for the aura before the finisher.
Under Sleet Storm, only Fire and Myth are safe; anything else triggers Lord of Winter.
Under Devotion, only Death and Storm are safe; anything else triggers Spinysaur.
Under Furnace, only Life and Storm are safe; anything else triggers Sun Serpent.
Under Reliquary, only Fire and Life are safe; anything else triggers Celestial Calendar.
Under Galvanic Field, only Death and Ice are safe; anything else triggers Storm Owl.
Under Virulence, only Ice and Myth are safe; anything else triggers Avenging Fossil.
Multi-school spells like Hydra check each school separately; if any one school is unsafe, the retaliation fires once.
End any round without dealing damage to it and it free-casts one random AoE from its list, so always land at least a chip hit.

name: Belloq
cheats: "I thought you came here to hurt me!" - If Belloq does not take Damage during a Round, he will interrupt-cast Ra at the beginning of the next Round, which deals 1,460 - 1,540 Balance Damage.
spellNotes: Despite what the Spell text indicates, this is a 3 Pip maximum Spell. It has been observed to deal:
strategy: Belloq punishes stalling: any round he takes zero damage, he interrupt-casts Ra for ~1,460-1,540 the next round. Deal at least some damage every round.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death; he takes +25% from those and resists Balance 80%.
Keep a wand hit or small spell to chip him on setup rounds so the Ra never triggers.
Blade up across a couple of rounds while chipping, then finish with one boosted hit.
If you would rather burst, just shield for Ra (~1,500) and hit hard next round.
cheatNotes: If he takes no damage in a round he hits back with Ra (~1,500). Throw a wand or small chip every round so this never fires, or shield up for it.

name: Belloq (Hidden Temple, Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Hidden Temple Gauntlet - Hidden Temple Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: [Global] "This is Belloq's game now!" - At the start of the battle, Belloq will cast Power Play.
[Trap] "Traps? My specialty! - If a Wizard uses a Trap, Belloq will cast Elemental Trap on the Wizard that cast the Trap.
[Blade] "Building up your attack? I did already." - If a Wizard uses a Blade. Belloq will use Balanceblade on himself.

spellNotes: Casts Hex (1 Pip) on all enemies.
strategy: Expect two phases. Bodleian starts Balance, then Polymorphs into a Storm version once the Balance form dies, so plan for a longer fight than the 3,250 Health suggests.

Do not lean on Traps or harmful Charms in phase one. Either triggers a Cleanse Ward that wipes your setup, and Traps additionally get a Hex applied to all enemies. Build damage with blades on yourself instead.
He is Balance and takes boosted Myth (30%) and Life (80%). A Life hitter with self-blades cuts through both forms fast; avoid dumping curses onto him.
In phase one, set up with blades and burst the Balance form down in one hit rather than feeding the Trap and Charm cleanse cheats round after round.
After the Polymorph he summons two random minions on his first Storm round and re-summons when they all die. Use AoE to clear minions and hit the boss together.
Watch the round-4 Siphon: he sacrifices a minion to heal 400. Kill minions on your terms and finish him before he stalls with heals.
cheatNotes: Cast a harmful Charm and he Cleanse-Wards off all harmful Charms on himself, so Weakness-style setup is wasted.
Cast a Trap and he Cleanse-Wards off all Traps on himself, so pure trap stacking does not stick in phase one.
When the Balance form dies he does not die; he Polymorphs into a Storm version and the fight continues.
On his first round after Polymorphing he summons two random minions from the Tier 1 list.
In the Storm phase, casting a Trap makes him Cleanse off his harmful Charms and then Hex all enemies, so traps stay risky after the shift too.
Every 4th round after summoning he Siphon-Healths a minion, killing it to heal himself 400, so don't let the fight drag.
When all his minions are dead he re-summons more the next round; clear them only when you can also close out the boss.

name: Bodleian Harrow (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Forbidden Library Gauntlet - Forbidden Library Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: [Balance] Bodleian Harrow
"I'm turning over a new page!" - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Bodleian Harrow will cast a Balance version of Cleanse Ward to remove all Traps on him.
"I read from the restricted section." - At the beginning of every third Round, Bodleian Harrow will cast one of the following:
Monk of Mourning (450-530)
Monster Mash (1,170)
Shield Wall
Spirited Minotaur (Life)
Wrath of Aquila
A 4-Pip version of Loremaster that deals 390-470 Balance Damage to and applies -20% Damage and -35% Accuracy Charms on target
A version of Efreet that deals 895 Fire Damage to and applies a -90% Damage Charm on target
[Polymorph] Polymorph: "At last I will read The- wait a minute!" - After Bodleian Harrow (Balance) is defeated, he will Polymorph himself into a Shadow version.
[Shadow] Bodleian Harrow (Tier 2)
"Knowledge is power!" - On the first Round after being Polymorphed, Bodleian Harrow will cast a Shadow Spell that summons two random Minions:
Flames of Doom (Tier 2)
History of Horrors (Tier 2)
Malefic Mythology (Tier 2)
Savage Stories (Tier 2)
Terrors of the Deep (Tier 2)
The Road to Ruin (Tier 2)
The Woeful Wastes (Tier 2)
"Literacy dies in darkness." - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Bodleian Harrow will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All), and then cast an all enemies version of Shadow Trap.

"Reading is a great restorative!" - At the end of every fourth Round after summoning Minions, Bodleian Harrow will cast Sacrifice on a random Minion, defeating it and healing himself for 2,000 Health.
"Time for a book recall!" - When all of Bodleian Harrow's Minions are defeated, he will resummon more Minions at the beginning of the next Round.
spellNotes: Casts Hex (1 Pip) on all enemies.
strategy: Hit with Myth, Life, or Death. He takes +30% from all three. Skip Balance (42% resist).
Do not use Traps at all. Both forms Cleanse Ward your Traps, and the Shadow form also lays a team Shadow Trap. Stack blades instead.
Expect a random spell every third Round. Keep a Cleanse Charm ready for the Efreet (-90% Damage) or Loremaster (-20% Damage / -35% Accuracy) rolls.
Burst the Balance form down fast. Every fourth Round after summoning he Sacrifices one of his own Minions to heal 2,000.
When the Balance form dies he Polymorphs into a Shadow form and summons two Minions. Reload blades and repeat the blade-and-burst plan for phase two.
cheatNotes: Casting any Trap makes him Cleanse Ward all Traps off himself, so traps are wasted here. Build with blades instead.
Every third Round he casts one random spell from the list below, ranging from a summon to a hard-hitting attack.
This roll is a 4-pip Loremaster that hits 390-470 Balance and applies -20% Damage and -35% Accuracy charms on you. Cleanse them before your hit.
This roll is an Efreet dealing 895 Fire plus a -90% Damage charm on you. Clear that charm or your next attack does almost nothing.
When his Balance form dies he immediately Polymorphs into a Shadow form, so the fight has a second phase. Save resources for it.
On his first Round as the Shadow form he summons two random Minions you will need to clear.
In Shadow form, casting a Trap makes him Cleanse all your traps and then lay a Shadow Trap on your whole team. Again, avoid traps.
Every fourth Round after summoning he Sacrifices one of his own Minions to heal 2,000, so kill his Minions before he can eat them for health.
Clear all his Minions and he just resummons more next Round. Race the boss down rather than chasing minions forever.

name: Bodleian Harrow (Tier 3)

Monk of Mourning (450-530)

Wrath of Aquila

A version of Efreet that deals 895 Fire Damage to and applies a -90% Damage Charm on target
[Polymorph] Polymorph: "At last I will read The- wait a minute!" - After Bodleian Harrow (Balance) is defeated, he will Polymorph himself into a Shadow version.
[Shadow] Bodleian Harrow (Tier 3)

Flames of Doom (Tier 3)

"Literacy dies in darkness." - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Bodleian Harrow will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All), and then cast an all enemies version of Shadow Trap.

"Reading is a great restorative!" - At the end of every fourth Round after summoning Minions, Bodleian Harrow will cast Sacrifice on a random Minion, defeating it and healing himself for 2,000 Health.
"Time for a book recall!" - When all of Bodleian Harrow's Minions are defeated, he will resummon more Minions at the beginning of the next Round.
Summon Cheats - Randomly, at the end of each Round, one of the summoned Minions will cheat:
"You will serve the Master of Words!" - Flames of Doom casts Mass Infection.
"No weapon can defeat the written word!" - History of Horrors casts Disarm on a random Wizard.
"Your knowledge is mine!" - Malefic Mythology casts Steal Pip from a random Wizard.
"Time for a chilling tale!" - Savage Stories casts Blizzard.
"Behold the power of dense prose!" - Terrors of the Deep casts Power Current on Bodleian Harrow or Confuses a random Wizard for 1 Round.
"Your destruction is written on every page!" - The Road to Ruin casts Leafstorm.
"In my story I'm the hero, you're the villain!" - The Woeful Wastes casts Power Link (75+375) on a random Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts Hex (1 Pip) on all enemies.
strategy: Attack with Myth, Life, or Death; each does +30%. Avoid Fire, Ice, Storm, and Balance.
Do not cast Traps. In both forms he answers a Trap with Cleanse Ward, and in Shadow form he adds an all-enemy Shadow Trap on you.
Set up with blades and auras instead of traps, and note he Hexes all enemies each Round.
Burst down the Balance form; when it dies he Polymorphs into a Shadow form that summons two random minions next Round.
In the Shadow phase use AoE to clear the summoned minions, which cheat-cast Infection, Disarm, Steal Pip, and more; he resummons when all are dead.
Kill minions in the same Round when possible so his every-fourth-round Sacrifice heal (2,000) has fewer targets to convert.
cheatNotes: Any Trap you place is wiped by his Balance Cleanse Ward, so set up with blades instead.
At the start of every third Round he casts a random spell from the list below, including a 4-pip Loremaster or an Efreet with a -90% damage charm; keep a heal ready.
When his Balance form dies it does not end; he Polymorphs into a Shadow form with new cheats, so save AoE for the second phase.
On his first Round as the Shadow form he summons two random minions that then cheat every Round; plan AoE to thin them fast.
In Shadow form a Trap gets cleansed and he drops a Shadow Trap on your whole team; keep avoiding traps.
Every fourth Round after summoning he sacrifices a minion to heal 2,000; kill minions yourself so he has fewer to convert.
Clearing every minion just makes him resummon next Round, so time your kill on him for the same Round you clear the board.
Flames of Doom can Mass Infect your team, cutting your healing; carry Cleanse Charm.
History of Horrors can Disarm a random wizard, losing your queued card.
Malefic Mythology can steal a pip from a random wizard, slowing your setup.
Savage Stories can hit the team with Blizzard.
Terrors of the Deep either buffs Bodleian with Power Current or Confuses a wizard for one Round.
The Road to Ruin can hit the team with Leafstorm.
The Woeful Wastes can Power Link a random wizard, healing itself off the drain.

name: Bort Shadowmane
cheats: "Poor Showmanship!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already started, Bort Shadowmane will interrupt cast Steal Pips (Moon) to steal one Pip from the Wizard.
"Check Out My Armor!" - If a Wizard casts an attack Spell at Bort, he will cast an Aegis-protected Bronze Armor on himself.
After casting his Natural Attack, Bort Shadowmane will interrupt-cast a Shadow version of Shatter on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Deals 110 Shadow Damage per Pip, despite the Spell card's 3-Pip max description.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+10%); avoid Myth (40% resist). He is Shadow with 12600 health and no minions.
Your first attack makes him Aegis-protect a Bronze Armor absorb on himself. Throw a cheap hit or wand first to pop the armor, then land your blade-stacked real hit next round.
Do not lean on Shields. After his Natural Attack he Shatters your positive Wards, so heal through his hit instead.
Late joiners lose a pip to Steal Pips, so arrive on time.
Stack blades and a Feint, then burst him once the armor is gone.
cheatNotes: Joining late costs you a pip to his Steal Pips. Be in before combat starts.
Your first attack triggers an Aegis-protected Bronze Armor absorb on him, so the shot lands into an absorb and the shield resists one removal. Pop it with a throwaway hit, then land your real damage.
After he attacks he Shatters your positive Wards, so do not count on shields to block his hit. Heal through it instead.

name: Briar Scourge
cheats: Briar Scourge's Pet casts a Tower Shield on the fourth Round, which stacks with his regular Tower Shield Spell.
strategy: He resists Ice and Death 70%. Attack with Fire or Life (+5% each) or bring a prism.
His only trick is stacked Tower Shields: his pet adds one on round 4 on top of his own. Time your hit before round 4, or pack Pierce or Shatter to blow through both.
Watch his +95% outgoing damage. Shield the incoming hits while you build blades.
Deal with the Vine Thrasher and Mutant Briar with an AoE finish, or pick them off if they threaten you.
Stack blades and a Feint over rounds 1-3, then hit on round 3 before the pet's shield, or bring Shatter for round 4 and later.
cheatNotes: On round 4 his pet stacks a Tower Shield with his own, doubling his defense. Either land your big hit by round 3 or carry Pierce or Shatter to strip both before you swing.

name: Broken Branch Queen

stats.resist: 28% to all schools58% to all schools

cheats: Broken Branch Queen:

[Aura] "The Queen protects her King!" - At the beginning of the first Round, and every sixth Round threafter, Broken Branch Queen casts a -100% incoming Damage Aura for 6 Rounds on Broken Branch King.

"Not while I still stand!" - If a Spell is cast to remove the Aura, such as Supernova, Broken Branch Queen will restore Broken Branch King to 100% Health, and recast the Aura. If the Aura is replaced with a harmful Aura, Broken Branch Queen will recast it without healing Broken Branch King.
This does not remove the harmful Aura.
The Broken Branch King can be defeated on the Round that the Aura expires, if the Bosses have the first turn in the battle.
Broken Branch King:
[Ward] "Are you the king?" - At the beginning of the first Round, Broken Branch King marks a random Wizard with a Stun Shield labeled "King."
[Trap] "Check!" - At the end of every third Round, Broken Branch King places a +100% Damage Ward on the marked Wizard.
[Trap] "Checkmate!" - After the marked Wizard reaches below 10% Health, Broken Branch King casts a +200% Damage Ward instead.
[Afterlife] "As goes the King, so the kingdom..." - When the marked Wizard is defeated, Broken Branch King will cast a Death version of Meteor Strike that deals 100% max Health Damage to all Wizards.
[Afterlife] "My strongest piece!" - When the Broken Branch Queen is defeated, Broken Branch King will remove his Aura.
"The King has fallen, so fall we all!" - When the Broken Branch King is defeated, the Broken Branch Queen will cast a Spell that defeats herself.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Storm Lord that doesn't Stun.
strategy: Kill order is the whole fight: the King is shielded by the Queen's -100% Aura, and killing the King instantly ends the fight because the Queen then defeats herself.

Do not try to Supernova the Aura off; she just heals the King to full and recasts it. Instead kill the Queen first to make the King drop his Aura, or time the King kill for the exact Round the Aura expires (every sixth Round) if the bosses move first.

Protect the wizard marked 'King' and never let them die: their death triggers a Death Meteor Strike for 100% max Health on everyone. Keep them topped off, especially once they hit the +200% ward below 10% Health.
Stack blades on one hitter while the Aura is up, then burst the King the moment he is exposed.
The Queen is Storm and resists Storm 58% and other schools 28%, taking 30% more from Myth, so hit with Myth where you can.
cheatNotes: At Round 1 and every sixth Round the Queen puts a -100% incoming damage Aura on the King for 6 Rounds, making him untouchable while it holds.
Strip the Aura with something like Supernova and she heals the King to full and recasts it; a harmful Aura only makes her recast without healing.
You can kill the King on the exact Round the Aura expires if the bosses take the first turn.
At Round 1 the King marks a random wizard with a Stun Shield labeled 'King'.
At the end of every third Round the King puts a +100% Damage Ward on the marked wizard.
Once the marked wizard drops below 10% Health the King upgrades it to a +200% Damage Ward.
If the marked wizard is defeated, the King casts a Death Meteor Strike for 100% of everyone's max Health, likely wiping the team.
When the Queen dies, the King drops his Aura.
When the King dies, the Queen immediately kills herself, ending the fight.

name: Broken Land Guardian
locations: Polaris: Forlorn Tayg - Cave of the Broken Land
cheats: Every fifth Round, Broken Land Guardian will cast the Spell Fireblade.
Every sixth Round, Broken Land Guardian will cast a Treasure Card Fireblade.
strategy: Attack with Ice or Storm (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Fire resist without a prism.
His only cheats are slow self-blades (a Fireblade on round 5, another on round 6), so there is nothing to play around early.
Stack your blades and traps over rounds 1-4 and add a Feint for the big multiplier.
Land one Ice or Storm hit by round 4-5 and his Fireblades never matter.
cheatNotes: He gives himself a Fireblade on round 5 — a minor self-buff. If your kill lands by round 4-5 it never matters; otherwise expect a slightly bigger Fire hit from him.
A second Fireblade (treasure card) on round 6. Same story — just finish before his buffs stack.

name: Broodmother (Throne Room)
locations: Khrysalis: Moon Cliffs - Throne Room (Moon Cliffs)
cheats: "Guard Me!" - At the end of the first Round, the Broodmother will summon a Royal Guard (Khrysalis) Minion.
"Guard Your Queen!" - At the end of each succeeding Round, the Royal Guard will summon a Yellow Jacket Drone.
"Give Me Your Health, Drone!" - At the end of a Round, the Broodmother may order a Yellow Jacket Drone to destroy itself by Sacrifice to give her 1,500 Health.
Whenever a Yellow Jacket Drone attacks, even if it fizzles, it is Sacrificed.
Once the Royal Guard Minion is defeated, the Yellow Jacket Drones will stop spawning.
spellNotes: Deals 170 Fire Damage for 1 Pip, 290 Fire Damage for 2 Pips, or 435 Fire Damage for 3 Pips + 630 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds.
strategy: Run this as a team with a dedicated hitter and supports. The Broodmother is a Rank 13 Fire boss (18,360 health) with 80% Fire resist, so hit with Ice or Storm (35% boost). Fire wizards must carry a Fire Prism to punch through her resistance.
Kill the Royal Guard minion first. Once it is defeated the Yellow Jacket Drones stop spawning, which cuts off her healing engine, so prioritize the Guard before committing to the boss.

Deny her heals: whenever a Yellow Jacket Drone exists she may Sacrifice it to heal herself 1,500. Drones also Sacrifice themselves whenever they attack (even on a fizzle). Clear drones or race the Guard down so she cannot cash them in.

Play around her Smoke Screens (-45% accuracy) and Weakness; keep Cleanse Charm in the side deck to strip Weakness and the Royal Guard's PowerNova debuffs, and bring accuracy or sniper enchants so your setup does not fizzle.

Supports stack blades and Feints on the hitter while the hitter clears the Guard, then reload blades for the boss. Handle her Glacial Shields with shield-removal (Ice/Storm) or conversions before the big hit.
Finish with one large bladed AoE once the Guard and drones are gone, so any remaining adds die alongside her and she gets no last-second Sacrifice heal.
cheatNotes: At the end of round 1 she summons a Royal Guard minion. This Guard is the real problem, so plan to focus it down first.
Each round after that the Royal Guard summons a Yellow Jacket Drone, steadily building a swarm of fodder she can heal from. Kill the Guard to stop the summons.

She can order a Yellow Jacket Drone to Sacrifice itself to heal her 1,500 at the end of a round, so leaving drones alive lets her claw back health. Clear drones or end the fight before she cashes them in.
A Yellow Jacket Drone is destroyed whenever it attacks, even if the attack fizzles, so their damage is a one-time thing and they mainly exist as heal fuel.
Once the Royal Guard is dead, no new Yellow Jacket Drones spawn. Killing the Guard is the single most important step to shutting off her minion and healing loop.

name: Brulee Sucre (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Grand Tourney Arena - Grand Tourney Arena (Tier 1)
cheats: "My wyrm sight will guide you!" - Whenever, Claudas uses a Blade, Brulee Sucre will cast Guidance.
strategy: Low-health tourney boss (1,090). A couple of blades and one hit end it.
She is Life, resists Life 40% and takes +10% from Death. Hit with Death or any non-Life school.
Her only trick is casting Guidance for accuracy when her partner Claudas blades. It does not threaten you, so ignore it and keep hitting.
cheatNotes: Whenever her ally Claudas blades, she gives Guidance for accuracy. It is harmless to you, so there is no need to play around it.

name: Brulee Sucre (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Grand Tourney Arena - Grand Tourney Arena (Tier 2)
cheats: "My Wyrm Sight will guide you" - Whenever, Claudas uses a Blade, Brulee Sucre will cast Guidance.
"Hiss! I encase myself in armor!" - Boss will cast Spirit Armor after taking damage.
strategy: Don't chip at her. Every instance of damage she takes triggers a Spirit Armor, so poking just wraps her in absorbs.
Set up clean: stack Death blades (she takes +20% Death and resists Life 50%) without hitting her at all.
Watch her partner — whenever Claudas uses a blade, Brulee casts Guidance, buffing enemy accuracy. Nothing to counter, just expect fewer fizzles from their side.
Kill her in one buffed Death hit. At 2,720 health, one blade-and-Feint hit blows through any armor she has before she can restack it.
cheatNotes: Whenever Claudas casts a blade, Brulee follows with Guidance. It just makes the enemy team more accurate — no direct counter needed.
Every time she takes damage she casts Spirit Armor on herself. Multi-hit chip damage is the worst thing you can do; one big single hit gives her no chance to armor up behind it.

name: Bunferatu
locations: Wizard City: Upper Halls - Bunferatu's Chamber
cheats: "Doom and gloom!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Bunferatu casts Doom and Gloom (-75%).
Attempting to replace the Global will have this cheat recast.
"I plague you!" - If a Wizard attacks Bunferatu, he will cast Infection on the Wizard.
Interrupt Cycle:
"Death sharpens my bite!" - At the end of every second Round in this cycle, Bunferatu will cast Dark Pact (+30%), Deathblade, and Balanceblade (+30%).
"Death swallow you!" - At the beginning of every third Round in this cycle, Bunferatu will cast Call of Khrulhu.
strategy: Expect a long grind: 23,600 health, 40% pierce, and heavy self-blading. Win through damage, not healing.
Round 1 he drops Doom and Gloom -75%, so your heals barely work. Do not waste turns replacing it; he just recasts it.
Hit with Ice or Life (+30% incoming); avoid Death (80% resist).
Every attack you land gives you an Infection that cuts healing further. Carry Cleanse Charm or plan to burst without relying on heals.
Every second round he self-casts Dark Pact, Deathblade, and Balanceblade. Strip the charms or out-pace the stack.
Every third round he casts Call of Khrulhu. Shield for the incoming Death hit, then commit to one big trap-and-Feint burst.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he casts Doom and Gloom -75%, so healing barely works. Win with damage instead.
Replacing his Doom and Gloom global just makes him recast it. Do not waste turns fighting it.
Every attack lands an Infection on you, cutting heals further. Carry Cleanse Charm or burst without healing.
Every second round he self-casts Dark Pact, Deathblade, and Balanceblade, ramping his damage. Remove the charms or pressure him down.
Every third round he casts Call of Khrulhu; shield for the Death hit.

name: Cake Mimic
cheats: [Round] "The Cake Mimic has marked a player." - At the end of every Round, the Cake Mimic places a Stun Block on a random Wizard, marking them.
Any existing marks are removed when a new one is placed.
The same Wizard can be marked multiple times in a row.
[Spell] "The Cake Mimic mimics the marked player." - After the marked Wizard casts a Spell, the Cake Mimic will cast the same Spell that the marked Wizard did on the marked Wizard.
If the marked Wizard passes or uses Reshuffle, at the end of the Round the Cake Mimic will cast the folowing Spell depending on which Wizard was marked:
Granny Ravenblood - Tick's Wrath
Grok Tuskstab - Grok Hit
N'longa Windwhisper - Womp
One-Thump Tlocc - Power Thump
Rodrick the Rat - Backstab (1 Pip)
Wesley Fancypants - Anguish A Capella
spellNotes: Casts a 0-Pip Myth spell that applies a +25 Damage Ward on all enemies.
Casts a 1-Pip version of Qismah's Curse that deals 50 Death Damage to all enemies.
Casts a 1-Pip version of Blizzard that deals 50 Ice Damage to all enemies.
Casts a 1-Pip version of Tatzelwurm Terror that deals 50 Myth Damage to all enemies.
Casts a 2-Pip version of Deer Knight that deals 75 Death Damage to and applies a -25 Damage Charm on all enemies.
Casts a 2-Pip version of Frost Giant that deals 50 Ice Damage to all enemies and applies a -100 Damage Ward on caster.
Casts a 2-Pip version of Orthrus that deals 75 Myth Damage to and applies a +25 Damage Ward on all enemies.
strategy: Low-Health Moon boss at 2,500. Hit Fire or Myth, since it takes +25 from both.
Every Round it marks a Wizard and copies that Wizard's next spell back onto them. While marked, cast blades and heals, which copy harmlessly.
Do not cast an attack you cannot survive while marked. A damage spell you cast gets mirrored onto the marked Wizard.
Do not pass or Reshuffle while marked. That triggers a set punishment spell instead.
Stack blades, then land one Fire or Myth hit. At 2,500 Health it drops in a single blow even if the mirror hits you back.
cheatNotes: Every Round it marks a random Wizard with a Stun Block. That marked Wizard is who it copies.
Only one mark exists at a time. A new mark cancels the old one.
The same Wizard can hold the mark several Rounds running, so do not assume it rotates.
Whatever spell the marked Wizard casts, the Mimic re-casts on that same Wizard. If you are marked, cast something you can survive, or a heal or blade.
If the marked Wizard passes or Reshuffles, the Mimic instead fires a set spell based on which Wizard was marked.

name: Caleban
cheats: "No fair! I'm too smart for that!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has already started, Caleban will cast Savage Paw on that Wizard.
"Beastmen! Caleban commands you to fight!'" - At the end of each Round, he will cast a Moon Spell that summons one of the following Minions:

The number of Minions summoned is equal to the number of filled spots on the Wizard's team. If the Minions are defeated before Caleban, he will resummon them, one per Round, until they again equal the number of Wizards present.
"'Beastmen! Protect me!" - Caleban will cast a Moon Spell that steals 400 Health from a Minion and gives him a Balanceblade.
Caleban will also receive a Chromatic Blast by interrupt, and the percentage depends on the amount of Pips he has.
Every few Rounds, Caleban will take two Pips from each of his allies and gain two Power Pips each time.
"You can't trick me, puny Wizard! Ha!'" - If a Feint is placed on Caleban, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast the Spell.
This can be avoided with Indemnity or Melt.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
"Caleban will crush you now, Wizard!" - If an ally or enemy is defeated, he will cast Berserk on himself.
strategy: He resists Balance 50% and takes only +5% from Myth/Life/Death, so use a strong off-Balance hit or a prism rather than Balance.
He summons a Beastman Guard at the end of every round equal to your team size and re-summons them, so do not waste turns clearing minions, race him with AoE.
Use Mass Feint (+75%) instead of a regular Feint. A normal Feint makes him Swap Ward the traps onto you, Mass Feint does not trigger it. Indemnity or Melt also protects a single-target Feint.
Any defeat, ally or enemy, gives him Berserk and more damage, so avoid picking off minions right before your hit, kill him in the same burst.
Stack Mass Feint plus blades, then land one large off-school hit, and watch him drain 2 pips from allies to power up.
cheatNotes: Join after the duel starts and he hits you with Savage Paw.
He summons a Beastman Guard at the end of every round via a Moon spell.
He keeps minions equal to your team size and re-summons one per round until they match, do not over-invest in clearing them.
He steals 400 health from one of his minions and gives himself a Balanceblade.
He also interrupt-casts Chromatic Blast on himself, scaled to his pips, buffing his damage.
Every few rounds he drains 2 pips from each minion and converts them to power pips, so he ramps quickly, end the fight before he snowballs.
Place a single-target Feint and he Swap-Wards it onto you. Use Mass Feint or protect it instead.
Indemnity or Melt stops the Feint swap, pack one to trap him safely.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger the swap, so it is the safe way to trap him.
Any defeat, ally or enemy, gives him Berserk and more damage, burst him down rather than trading kills.

name: Capricorn the Gruff Goat
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Troll Den
cheats: "Ambush me? Oh no you don't!" - If a Wizard joins after Round 1, Capricorn the Gruff Goat will cheat-cast Goat Monk on the late Wizard.
"*bleat*" - At the end of every third Round, Capricorn the Gruff Goat will cheat-cast a protected Ion Wind (Life).
spellNotes: Casts a protected Lifeblade (+40%) and Lifespear (+10%).
strategy: Everyone joins on Round 1 to dodge the Goat Monk ambush he throws at latecomers.
He is stunable and beguilable, so a stun or Beguile buys you safe setup rounds.
He self-buffs with a protected Lifeblade and Lifespear and throws a protected Ion Wind every third round that can strip your charms; set up and hit inside a Round 1-3 or 4-6 window.
Hit with Death (he takes only +5% but resists everything else as Life 70%); prism if you are off-school.
Stack blades and Feint and burst; his incoming healing is cut 75%, so he cannot out-heal you.
cheatNotes: Latecomers eat a Goat Monk, so have everyone join on Round 1.
Every third round he casts a protected Ion Wind that can strip your blades; land your hit before Round 3 or 6, or rebuild fast after it fires.

name: Captain Argento

stats.resist: 4% to all schools54% to all schools

cheats: "Shields up!" - When a Wizard attacks Captain Argento, he will cast a -75% Shield of the matching school of that Wizard.
If the Wizard is of the Balance School, Argento will cast a Tower Shield.
Damage from DoTs will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that hit multiple times, such as Minotaur, will only trigger this cheat once.
Randomly cheat casts the following Spells:
Abominable Weaver
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Hex.
strategy: Attack with Fire. His resist to it is only 4% versus 54% to every other school.
Every direct hit makes him shield the attacker's school at -75%, but DoT ticks never trigger it. Lean on Fire DoTs like Scald to bypass Shields Up.
If you use a single big Fire hit instead, expect a -75% Fire Shield to go up first. Break or pierce it, or bait it with a small hit before your real one.
Multi-hit Fire spells only trigger one shield, so a multi-hit attack is safer than several small ones.
Watch for random extra cheat casts (including Abominable Weaver) and keep your health buffered through them.
cheatNotes: Attacking him makes him drop a -75% Shield matching your attack's school, so your follow-up hit into that school lands soft.
Balance attackers instead get a Tower Shield, which blocks every school. Plan to break it.
Damage-over-time ticks do not trigger the shield, so Fire DoTs slip past his Shields Up entirely.
Multi-hit spells like Minotaur only make him shield once, not per hit.
He also randomly cheat-casts extra spells during the fight.

name: Captain Hockins
locations: Zafaria: Waterfront - Waterfront Stone Key Room

stats.resist: 85% to all schools100% to all schools150% to all schools

cheats: Captain Hockins
"I won't go down easy!" - When hit once, Hockins gives the Wizard who attacked a Dragonspear.
"Still got a few tricks up my sleeve!" - When hit a second time, Hockins gives the Wizard who attacked a +20% Dragonspear.
"I'll shake this curse yet!" - When hit a third time, Hockins gives the Wizard who attacked a +30% Dragonspear.
The speech recycles after Hockins is hit for a fourth time, but the Dragonspears continue to gain +10% Armor Piercing with each attack up to +100% maximum.
Damage over Time Spells will not trigger the Dragonspear cheats.
"Uh Oh! That's...not good" - Hockins shouts this when the Witch Doctor is defeated.
Witch Doctor
"Let's keep things under control" - At start of the Duel, the Witch Doctor interrupt casts a version of Saga of Heroes which lowers a Wizard's critical chance by -75.
"Uh uh uh! We'll have none of that!" - If a Wizard casts a Global spell, then the Witch Doctor will replace it with his Saga of Heroes.
"A curse on the un-punctual!" - If a Wizard joins late, then the Witch Doctor will cast Steal Pips (Moon) that steals 1 Pip.
spellNotes: Captain Hockins's Deathblade stacks with Witch Doctor's Deathblade.
The Burlap Boy Pet casts the Spell Poison.
strategy: Bring Life damage and pierce. Hockins is immune to every school but Life and resists even Life around 85%, so hit with Life (or a Life prism) and stack armor piercing.

Exploit the Dragonspear cheat. Every direct hit hands your attacker a universal pierce blade that grows +10% each hit up to +100%, which is exactly the pierce you need to beat his resist. Keep hitting to build it, and use direct attacks, not DoTs (damage-over-time won't grant the spears).
Don't rely on criticals; the Witch Doctor opens with a Saga-of-Heroes cheat that drops your critical chance -75. Kill the Witch Doctor early to remove the debuffer.
Skip Global/bubble spells; casting one makes the Witch Doctor overwrite it with his own Saga of Heroes.
Pick an approach the guide gives: blade a hitter and burst with Shadow Shrike, Infallible, and Extraordinary, or spam smaller attacks first and save your big spell for when the pierce is near +100%.
Join on time so the Witch Doctor doesn't steal a pip with Steal Pips (Moon). He is stunable and beguilable if you need safe setup Rounds.
cheatNotes: The first hit on Hockins gives your attacker a Dragonspear (universal pierce blade). That's good for you, since his 85% Life resist needs pierce, so start hitting to build it.
The second hit gives a +20% Dragonspear, stacking more pierce onto your hitter.
The third hit gives a +30% Dragonspear, and the pierce keeps climbing.
After the fourth hit the pierce blades keep coming, adding +10% armor piercing each hit up to +100% max, so repeated attacks steadily build enough pierce to shred his resist.
Damage-over-time spells like Poison don't grant the Dragonspears, so use direct hits to farm the pierce.
The Witch Doctor opens with a Saga-of-Heroes version that lowers your critical chance -75, so don't count on crits; kill it to remove the debuffer.
Casting any Global (bubble) makes the Witch Doctor replace it with his Saga of Heroes, so your bubbles won't stick. Skip them.
Joining late makes the Witch Doctor cast Steal Pips (Moon) to steal 1 pip. Enter on time.

name: Cerberus (Balance)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus - Courtyard of Tartarus
cheats: Cerberus (Balance) and Cerberus (Myth)
Every 5 rounds, the Balance and Myth heads will cast three -40% Tower Shields each around themselves.
Attacking any of the heads while they have Shields will trigger both of the other heads to retaliate, dealing ~750 Damage each.
Dispels can be used to prevent this cheat from activating.
Shadow Creature spells will trigger this cheat.
Multi-target spells and Beguile will activate this cheat twice.
Call of Khrulhu doesn't trigger this cheat.
The Balance and Myth heads will remove any Traps cast upon them.
This cheat can be prevented by using Vaporize.
Multi-Trap spells will result in only the final Trap (e.g. Storm from Elemental, Life from Spirit) being removed.
Pet spells will not activate this cheat.
Spells such as Windstorm will not activate this cheat.
Indemnity enchantment on any single or multi trap spells will prevent any traps from being removed.
Cerberus (Fire)
The Fire head does not cast any Tower Shields.
Sometimes a Cerberus with shields trades to one without tower shields.
Traps can be placed on this head without triggering any cheats.
Defeating the Fire head will prevent the Balance and Myth head from cheat-casting their Tower Shields.
strategy: Kill order is the strategy: focus the Fire head FIRST. It never shields, traps placed on it are cheat-free, and once it dies the Balance and Myth heads can no longer cheat-cast their Tower Shields at all.

While all three heads are up, never attack a shielded head. Every 5 rounds the Balance and Myth heads each get three -40% Tower Shields; hitting a shielded head makes BOTH other heads retaliate for ~750 each — and multi-target spells trigger it twice.
Supports: put all traps on the Fire head only. The Balance and Myth heads strip traps cast on them unless you enchant with Indemnity (or Vaporize their cleanse).
Hitter: build Myth, Life, or Death damage (+30% boost on the Balance head; never Balance into 80% resist). Blades funnel to the hitter as usual.
Once Fire is down, trap the remaining heads freely (Indemnity still helps), and clear Balance and Myth with boosted AoE hits. Both are stunable — stuns cover your setup rounds.
If you must hit through shields, dispel first — dispels stop the retaliation cheat.
cheatNotes: On a 5-round timer, the Balance and Myth heads each stack three -40% Tower Shields on themselves. Plan your burst for the rounds before the refresh.
Hitting a head that still has its cheat shields makes the other two heads counter for roughly 750 damage each. With three heads up that's ~1,500 per mistake.
Dispelling a head prevents its retaliation hit — useful if you have to swing into shields.
Shadow creature spells count as attacks and set off the retaliation, so time them for shield-free heads.
AoE spells and Beguile trigger the retaliation twice. Save your AoE for after the Fire head is dead or shields are gone.
Call of Khrulhu is the exception — it doesn't set off the retaliation.
The Balance and Myth heads remove any trap you place on them. Put traps on the Fire head instead.
Vaporize dispels their trap-removal cheat if you need traps on Balance or Myth.
With Elemental or Spirit Trap, only the last trap of the three gets removed — the first two stick.
Pet may-cast traps don't trigger the removal.
Windstorm-type multi-traps don't trigger the removal either.
Indemnity on any trap spell fully protects it from being removed. Enchant your Feints.
The Fire head never casts Tower Shields — it's always safe to attack.
Shields sometimes trade between heads, so re-check who is shielded each round before committing a hit.
Traps on the Fire head are completely cheat-free. Stack everything there first.
Killing the Fire head shuts off the Tower Shield cheat for the other two heads permanently. This is why Fire dies first.

name: Cerberus (Balance, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Courtyard of Tartarus (Challenge)
cheats: [Round] "*GROWL*" - At the beginning of the first Round and every fourth Round afterward, the Balance and Myth heads will remove all harmful Wards on themself, and each head will do the following:
The Balance head will cast Donate Power on itself.
The Fire head will cast Ha-ha! A 1-Up! on itself that restores 250 Health over 5 Rounds (307 Health with its outgoing Healing boost).
The Myth head will cast three -40% Damage Wards on itself.
[Shield] "*SNARL*" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, the Fire and Myth heads will cast their Natural Attacks on the recipient.
[Trap] "*HAPPY BARK!*" - If a harmful Ward is cast on the Fire head, it will cast a Spell to remove all Traps from itself.
strategy: Three heads fight together: the Balance boss plus Fire and Myth heads. Bring an AoE hitter to clear all three at once, plus support to blade and Feint.
Hit Myth, Life, or Death - all three heads take boosted damage from these. The Balance head resists 80% Balance, so never attack into Balance.

Round 1 and every 4th round the Balance and Myth heads strip their harmful wards and buff (Donate Power, a heal, and three -40% towers on the Myth head). Time your burst just after this window so your Feints and traps are not wiped.
Do not shield yourself. Casting a helpful ward makes the Fire and Myth heads natural-attack whoever received it.

Keep harmful wards off the Fire head as your win condition - if you trap it, it cleanses all traps off itself. Instead stack blades and Feints on the Myth or Death-boosted line and finish with one AoE that clears all three heads together.
cheatNotes: On round 1 and every 4th round after, the Balance and Myth heads remove all harmful wards from themselves and each head buffs. Burst right after this window, not before.
The Balance head gives itself Donate Power for extra pips.
The Fire head heals itself 250 over 5 rounds (307 with its healing boost).
The Myth head shields itself with three -40% damage wards.
If any wizard casts a helpful ward, the Fire and Myth heads both natural-attack the recipient. Do not shield up.
If you place a harmful ward on the Fire head, it cleanses every trap off itself, wasting your Feints.

name: Chester Droors
locations: Wysteria: Pigswick Academy - School of Earth
cheats: "Did you forget how to use traps?" - Starting at the beginning of the third round, if there are no traps placed on him, he will interrupt-cast a zero-pip Helping Hands on himself. If a trap is placed on him, the Helping Hands does not cast the following round (after healing once more). When the healing effects wear off, he will use this cheat again if no traps are on him.
strategy: Tiny 675-health Life boss. Hit with Death (+40%) and never Life (70% resist).
From round 3 on, if he has no trap on him he heals with Helping Hands. Keep at least one trap stuck on him and the healing stops entirely.
With his low health, stack a blade and a trap, then kill him before round 3 or right through the heal cheat.
cheatNotes: Starting round 3, an untrapped Chester heals himself with Helping Hands every time the heal wears off. Keep a single trap on him and the healing stops completely.

name: Chief Whip
cheats: "Chip up!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage Spell, there is a chance that the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Spirit Armor on the target of the attack.
If a multi-target Damage Spell triggers this cheat, then the Spirit Armor will be cast on whoever is in the first spot.
If a multi-target Damage Spell that hits all enemies at the same time triggers this cheat, such as Climaclysm, then the Spirit Armor will be cast on the Chief Whip.
"Hold the line!" - The first time that the Bulldog Sergeant Major is defeated, the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Rebirth to revive him.
If a multi-target Damage Spell triggers this cheat, then Rebirth is cast for each enemy hit.
"I'll catch you up!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, then the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Entangle on the Wizard.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
"Let me help you, Chief!" - Randomly, whenever the Bulldog Sergeant Major has enough Pips, he will say this and use his turn to cast Donate Power on Chief Whip.
strategy: He resists Life 70% and takes +40% from Death, so bring Death attacks or a prism.
He is stunable and beguilable. A stun or Beguile buys safe setup rounds.
Every damage spell can pop a Spirit Armor absorb onto your target, so chipping stalls. Stack blades and hit once, hard, to blow through it.
Do not heal casually: any Healing Spell, including pet heals, makes him Entangle you. Survive with shields and absorbs, then Cleanse or wait out the Entangle.
Kill the reviving Bulldog Sergeant Major last, or with your final AoE, since the first time it dies he Rebirths it.
Build blades and a Feint over two or three rounds, then finish through the Spirit Armor.
cheatNotes: Any damage spell can pop a Spirit Armor absorb onto your target, so a chip-heavy approach stalls. Stack blades and hit once, hard.
On a multi-hit spell the Spirit Armor lands on whoever is in the first spot.
An all-at-once AoE like Climaclysm makes him shield himself instead, so expect the boss to gain an absorb when you AoE.
The first time the Bulldog Sergeant Major dies he revives it with Rebirth. Save it for your finishing hit.
If your AoE triggers the revive, he Rebirths for every enemy it hit.
Casting any heal makes him Entangle you. Lean on shields and absorbs instead of healing.
Even pet heals set off that Entangle, so watch your pet's may-casts.
His minion can donate its pips to him, powering up his attacks, so pressure it if it starts feeding him.

name: Chief Whip (Challenge)
cheats: [Life][Jinx] - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, the Chief Whip will cast a Life Spell that restores 75 Health over 3 Rounds to himself.
[Life][Damage over Time] - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect, the Chief Whip will cast a Life Spell that restores 75 Health over 3 Rounds to himself.
[Pip] "Let me help you, chief!" - The Bulldog Sergeant Major will say this on any turn where it casts Donate Power on the Chief Whip.

[Minion][Afterlife] "Hold the line!" - The first time that the Bulldog Sergeant Major is defeated before the Chief Whip, the Chief Whip will cast a Spell that restores 15% max Health to all allies, reviving the Bulldog Sergeant Major.
strategy: Bring Death attacks. He is Life, resists his own school, and Death pressures his self-heals.
Build damage with Blades, not Traps or Damage-over-Time. Every harmful Ward or DoT you cast heals him 75 over 3 Rounds.
Focus the Chief Whip himself, not the Bulldog Sergeant Major. Killing the minion first revives it once and heals the whole enemy team 15%.
Expect him to reach his spells faster; the Bulldog Sergeant Major Donates Power to him.
Finish with one AoE so the Chief Whip and the minion die the same round, avoiding the revive.
cheatNotes: Casting a harmful Ward (a Trap) heals him 75 over 3 Rounds. Build with Blades instead.
Casting a Damage-over-Time heals him 75 over 3 Rounds. Skip DoTs here.
The Bulldog Sergeant Major Donates Power to the Chief Whip, speeding up his bigger spells.
If you kill the Bulldog Sergeant Major before the Chief Whip, he heals all allies 15% and revives it once. Kill him first or AoE both together.

name: Chilly
cheats: [Late] "Steal Pips!" - If a Wizard joins the duel late (third Round or later), all of the Germ Ghosts will remove one Pip from the Wizard, and gain one Pip.
[Polymorph] "Medicine mode!" - At the end of every five Rounds, the Germ Ghosts will cheat-cast a Polymorph for two Rounds, at which time they are vulnerable to Damage.
All Ghosts will pass while polymorphed.
This Polymorph changes all of the Germ Ghosts' Schools to Shadow for that Round.
A crafted Animus Summon Card of these Creatures will have their original School instead of Shadow.
At the end of the next turn, they will cheat-cast a reverse Polymorph into their original forms.
The Polymorph cheats will not fizzle, even with multiple negative Accuracy Charms (such as Smoke Screen).
[Round] "Next level!" - At the end of every six Rounds, they will remove all Traps from themselves and all Blades from each Wizard.
[Afterlife] If Chilly is defeated on the fifth Round, then the Blades/Traps will not be removed on the sixth Round, and the Germ Ghosts will stay Polymorphed for the full two turns.

The Germ Ghost in the next position will take over the Blade/Trap removal and Polymorph cheats when the previous one is defeated, the order being: Chilly > Achey > Sneezy > Cough. "May need further confirmation for later Rounds."
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Ice Damage for 3 Pips maximum.
strategy: Chilly's team resists 200% to every school normally, so raw attacks do nothing — the ONLY damage window is their polymorph, which hits every 5th round and lasts two rounds.
Spend rounds 1-4 stacking blades, traps, and a Feint; enter on time so they don't steal your pips.
On the round-5 polymorph they become Shadow school and vulnerable, so any school hits them — no prism needed for the burst.
Defeat Chilly during that round-5 window: doing so on round 5 skips the round-6 blade/trap strip and keeps the ghosts polymorphed and open the full two rounds.
If the fight drags, work down the takeover order (Chilly, then Achey, Sneezy, Cough), always hitting during a polymorph window.
cheatNotes: Join late (round 3+) and every Germ Ghost steals a pip from you. Enter at the start.
Every 5th round they polymorph for two rounds — this is the only window they take damage. Bank your blades and traps for it and unload.
They pass while polymorphed, so it is a completely safe window to set up and hit.
The polymorph turns them Shadow school, so any school damages them — no prism needed during the burst.
The polymorph reverts after two turns, so the damage window closes on the second round; land your kill inside it.
The polymorph cast never fizzles, even with Smoke Screen or other accuracy debuffs — don't waste a turn trying to stop it.
Every 6th round they strip all your blades and their own traps. Land your kill in the round-5 window before this hits.
If you defeat Chilly on round 5, the round-6 strip is skipped and the ghosts stay polymorphed and vulnerable the full two rounds — the cleanest line.
Kill order matters: when one falls, the next inherits the strip and polymorph cheats in the order Chilly, Achey, Sneezy, Cough. Work down the line.

name: Chronomaster Devlin
cheats: "Bad time to be late!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the first Round, Chronomaster Devlin will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
Time is on my side!" - At the beginning of Round 1 (and every 10th Round thereafter), he will cast a +45% version of Power Play.
If a Wizard casts any type of global spell while this is active, he will recast Power Play.

"Time is not on your side!" - At the end of Round 5 (and every 10th Round thereafter), he will replace the Power Play with a Balance global with -45% Power Pip gain that limits the maximum pip limit to 2 if all pips gained before are spent, similar to Dampen Magic.
Shadow pips can still be earned while the global is active.
Wizards who have more than 3 pips total are unaffected until they spend their pips.
If a Wizard casts any type of global spell while this is active, he will recast this spell.
"You can't trap the Cabal!" - If a trap is placed on Chronomaster Devlin or his Minion, he will place a Weakness (-30%) and a Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip) on the Wizard who cast the spell.
strategy: Don't cast global spells — he recasts Power Play (round 1+) or, from round 5, the pip-limiting Balance global in response.
Don't trap him or his minion; any trap slaps Weakness (-30%) and Black Mantle (-55%) on the caster. Build with blades instead.
His round-5 global caps you at 2 pips once you spend down, but wizards holding more than 3 pips are unaffected until they spend — stockpile pips and land your big hit before or right as round 5 hits.
Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+5%); he only resists Balance 30%.
Shadow pips still charge under the global, so keep a shadow finisher online.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get Mana Burned — join on time.
Round 1 and every 10th round he casts a +45% Power Play for faster pips.
Casting any global while Power Play is up makes him recast it — skip globals.
End of round 5 and every 10th he swaps to a Balance global that cuts power-pip gain and caps you at 2 pips once you spend down — burst before it locks you.
Shadow pips still charge under that global, so keep a shadow finisher ready.
If you hold more than 3 pips you're unaffected until you spend — stockpile before round 5.
Casting a global under this second global makes him recast it too — avoid globals entirely.
Trapping him or his minion hits you with Weakness (-30%) and Black Mantle (-55%) — skip traps and blade up instead.

name: Cipactli
locations: Azteca: Tierra de Brea - Cipactli's Cave
cheats: Interrupt Cycles

"Can you follow the steps of my war dance?" - At the beginning of the first Round in each Dance cycle, Cipactli will place a Stun Shield "mark" on each Wizard. If the mark is not removed in the first or second Round of each Dance cycle, then he will cheat-cast a Dr. Von's Monster dealing 820 Steal Damage, and places a -25% Healing Charm at the Wizard in the beginning of the third Round.

"Those who cannot keep up must perish!" - At the beginning of the third Round in each Dance cycle, he will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster at any Wizard who did not get rid of the Stun Shield mark in the first or second Round.
Melt can be used to Dispel him from placing his mark on Wizards.
Ward Shields may only be cast on the Wizard in the first position (Sun spot). Shields cast on other Wizards are removed.
If a Wizard casts Legion Shield, the Shield will only be removed from the caster.
The self-trap portion of Feint will be immediately removed after casting.
Global Spells, such as Sanctuary and Wyldfire, will trigger him to remove the mark.
The Earthquake cast by his Minion(s) will remove the mark.
Aura Spells, such as Galvanic Field and Sleet Storm, will not trigger him to remove the mark.
Pet Spells will not trigger him to remove the mark.
Gear/Equipment Item Cards will not trigger him to remove the mark.
strategy: This is a dance-cycle fight: at the start of each cycle he marks every wizard with a Stun Shield. Clear your mark in the first two rounds or eat Dr. Von's Monster (820) and a -25% healing charm at Round 3.
The simplest mark remover is damaging him; even a small hit clears it. A Global like Sanctuary or Wyldfire also makes him remove the mark, and Melt dispels it being placed at all.
He resists Death 80% and takes +35% from Ice and Life, and he is stunnable and beguilable. Bring Ice or Life hits and consider a stun to skip a turn.
Shields only stick on the wizard in the first (Sun) position; wards on other spots vanish. Legion Shield only covers its caster here.
Feint's self-trap is stripped instantly, so Feint gives you the full +70% trap on him with no -30% drawback. Use it freely.
Blade up across the safe rounds, clear each mark with a chip hit or Global, then land your Ice or Life nuke.
cheatNotes: Each cycle he marks you with a Stun Shield. Clear it by Round 2 of the cycle or he hits you for 820 and adds a -25% healing charm. Damaging him removes the mark.
If you still have the mark at Round 3 he fires Dr. Von's Monster at you. Chip him each cycle to stay clean.
Melt cast on him dispels the mark before it is even placed, handy if you cannot spare a hit that round.
Only the first-position (Sun spot) wizard can be warded; shields on anyone else vanish. Plan defense around the front slot.
Legion Shield only protects its caster here, not the team.
Feint's self-trap is removed immediately, so you get the full +70% trap on him with no -30% on yourself. Use Feint freely.
Casting a Global like Sanctuary or Wyldfire makes him remove your mark, a no-attack way to clear it.
His minion's Earthquake also clears your mark.
Auras like Galvanic Field or Sleet Storm do NOT clear the mark, so do not count on them for it.
Pet-cast spells do NOT clear the mark.
Item and gear cards do NOT clear the mark.

name: Cleric Elezar

stats.resist: 37% to all schools67% to all schools

cheats: [Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Cleric Elezar or her minions are hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on themself:
If they are not defeated by the attack, they will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If they are defeated by the attack, they will cast a version of Minor Blessing on themself that heals for 100% of their max health.

[Spell] "Is that the sun?!" - If either Cleric Elezar or her minions are hit by a spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal dispel on themself.
strategy: Do not attack with Drain spells. A non-killing Drain hands Elezar an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade, and a Drain that would kill just full-heals her instead.
Avoid sun-embedded hits (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) unless the hit is lethal; a non-killing sun hit lets her dispel herself.
Build with blades and traps (Feint is fine here) and finish with one normal, non-drain, non-sun hit.
She takes +30% from Life and resists Death 67%, so bring a boosted off-school hitter.
Team: supports blade the hitter and Feint the boss, and the hitter kills in a single round so none of her steal or heal triggers ever fire.
cheatNotes: Hitting Elezar or her minions with a Drain spell makes the target cheat-cast on itself; the two outcomes follow.
If the Drain does not kill, the target gives itself an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade.
If the Drain would kill, the target instead casts Minor Blessing and heals to full, surviving.
Hitting her or a minion with a spell containing a sun (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) that does not kill lets it dispel itself.

name: Cobraxas
cheats: "Prepare for a shock!" - Cobraxas' Pet Viper in a Jar will cast Tingling Poison on random Wizard.
"Feel your strength slip away!" - His Pet Viper in a Jar will cast Weakness on a random Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Myth (+15% incoming); he only 30% resists Storm, so a Storm wizard can prism or just push through.
Expect a Weakness on a random wizard from his pet. Cleanse it or stack an extra blade so it does not gut your hit.
Bring Triage for the pet's Tingling Poison DoT.
Only 10,640 health and no minions: stack blades and Feint, then one big hit.
cheatNotes: His pet drops a Tingling Poison DoT on a random wizard. Bring Triage or heal the tick.
His pet lands a Weakness on a random wizard, shrinking that wizard's next hit. Cleanse it or add an extra blade before attacking.

name: Colonel Ivanenko
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Colonel Ivanenko will cheat-cast Wild Bolt on the Wizard.
"You can't fool me!" - If a Feint is placed on Ivanenko, he will cheat-cast Tower Shield on himself.
Melt can be used to Dispel this cheat.
"In the Gulag, we share such things!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cheat-cast Healing Current on himself.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
"Prepare for a storm of pain!" - At the beginning of Round 4, he will cheat-cast Darkwind.
"It's cold in the Gulag!" - At the beginning of Round 8, he will cheat-cast Balefrost.
"You will not weather this battle!" - At the beginning of Round 12, he will cheat-cast Darkwind.
The Pet, Ivanenko's Tiger. may randomly cast Storm Cat, Ice Cat (75%) (removes one Pip/Power Pip), or Snow Serpent.
strategy: Hit with Fire or Myth, he takes +15% from each, and avoid Storm and Ice (50% resist each).
If you Feint him he throws up a Tower Shield, bring Melt to dispel it before you hit.
Do not heal. Any heal, including pet maycast heals, makes him self-cast Healing Current. Pre-shield and tank instead.
Plan your kill around his globals: Darkwind on Round 4, Balefrost on Round 8, Darkwind again on Round 12. Aim to burst before Round 4 or right after clearing a global.
Stack a Feint, clear his Tower Shield with Melt, add blades, and land one big Fire or Myth hit.
cheatNotes: Join late and he opens with Wild Bolt on you.
Feint him and he answers with a Tower Shield, strip it with Melt before you hit.
Melt dispels that Tower Shield, so pack one when you plan to Feint.
Any heal you cast makes him self-cast Healing Current, avoid healing and pre-shield instead.
Pet maycast heals also trigger his Healing Current, so watch your pet.
He casts Darkwind at the start of Round 4.
He casts Balefrost at the start of Round 8.
He casts Darkwind again at the start of Round 12.
His pet Tiger randomly casts Storm Cat, Ice Cat (may strip a pip), or Snow Serpent, minor chip damage.

name: Commander Ahk'To (Challenge)

stats.resist: 48% to all schools68% to all schools88% to all schools

cheats: [Blade][Trap][Aura][Global][Polymorph] If a wizard casts a damage-increasing blade, trap, aura, global, or shadow polymorph, Commander Ahk'to will cheat-cast one of the following spells in order. After 5 buffs, the cycle repeats itself:
Shadow Shepherd will not trigger this cheat.
"I can match any offense!" - Casts an all-allies version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 650 damage
"You do not learn! Shields!" - Casts Mass Tower Shield
"Learn this: No blade, no trap, nothing can harm me." - Casts an all-allies version of Brace that gives -40% incoming damage for 4 rounds
"Still, you fail to comprehend." Casts a global spell that decreases power pip chance by 25%. Needs confirmation on global effect
"So be it, let darkness consume!" Casts Shadow Sentinel
Interrupt Cycle (Resets every 2 [Round])
[Absorb] If the enemy creature is at full health, she will cast a version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 735 damage.
[Heal] If the enemy is below full health, she will cast Minor Blessing. This can revive enemies defeated on the same turn.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Coolest Centaur that only deals Damage.
strategy: Every damage-increasing blade, trap, aura, global, or shadow polymorph you cast makes him cheat-cast a defensive spell from a 5-spell cycle. Buff as little as possible.
Use Shadow Shepherd to boost - it is the one buff that does not trigger the cheat.
He resists 48% to Fire and Myth and far more to everything else, so hit with Fire or Myth and stack armor piercing.

The defensive counters cycle in order (Spirit Armor 650, Mass Tower Shield, Brace -40%, -25% pip global, Shadow Sentinel) and repeat after 5 buffs. Track where you are and trigger the cheaper ones if you must.

He also interrupts every 2 rounds to shield himself (735 absorb at full health) or Minor Blessing when hurt, which can revive an ally lost that same turn. Burst through it in one hit rather than chipping.
As a team, let supports feed one hitter with minimal buffs (ideally Shadow Shepherd), then land a single pierced Fire or Myth hit.
cheatNotes: Casting any damage-increasing blade, trap, aura, global, or shadow polymorph makes him cheat-cast a defensive spell from a 5-spell cycle. Minimize your buffs.
Shadow Shepherd is exempt - it boosts your damage without triggering the counter. Prefer it.
First counter in the cycle: an all-allies Spirit Armor absorbing 650 damage.
Second counter: Mass Tower Shield on his side.
Third counter: an all-allies Brace giving -40% incoming damage for 4 rounds.
Fourth counter: a global that drops power pip chance by 25%, slowing your pip gain.
Fifth counter: Shadow Sentinel. After this the cycle repeats from Spirit Armor.
Separate from the buff cycle, at full health he interrupts (every 2 rounds) with a 735 Spirit Armor absorb. Push past it in one hit.
When he is below full health he casts Minor Blessing instead, which can revive an ally defeated that same turn. Kill minions and boss together.

name: Commander Ahk'To (Standard)

stats.resist: 51% to all schools81% to all schools

cheats: [Blade][Trap][Aura][Global][Polymorph] Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage-increasing Blade, Trap, Aura, Global, or Shadow Polymorph, Commander Ahk'to will cast one of the following Spells in order. After 5 buffs, the cycle repeats itself.
Shadow Shepherd will not trigger this cheat.
Effects that increase Armor Piercing will not trigger this cheat.
"I can match any offense!" - Casts an all-allies version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 650 Damage.
"You do not learn! Shields!" - Casts Mass Tower Shield
"Learn this: No blade, no trap, nothing can harm me." - Casts an Aura on all allies that reduces incoming Damage by 40% for 4 Rounds.
"Still, you fail to comprehend." Casts a Global that decreases all incoming Damage by 25%.
"So be it, let darkness consume!" Casts Shadow Sentinel
Interrupt Cycle (Resets every 2 [Round])
[Absorb] If the enemy creature is at full health, she will cast a version of Spirit Armor that absorbs 735 Damage.
[Heal] If the enemy is below full health, she will cast a version of Minor Blessing that restores 500 Health (735 Health when including Ahk'To's outgoing Healing boost).
spellNotes: Casts a version of Coolest Centaur that only deals Damage.
strategy: Expect a defensive counter for every damage buff you cast. Blades, traps (including Feint), auras, globals, and shadow polymorphs each trigger one of five defenses in order, so buff sparingly.
Lean on armor piercing and Shadow Shepherd for damage. Neither triggers his counter, so pierce is your safest way to build up without feeding his shields.
Attack with Fire (he takes +30% Fire); never Ice (81% resist) and expect 51% resist on everything else.
Before your kill hit, Shatter and Cleanse his stacked shields and damage-reduction, then punch through with armor piercing.
Drop him below full Health early and apply Doom and Gloom or Infection. His every-other-round interrupt becomes a heal once he is damaged, and you want to weaken it.
He is not stunable and has 30,930 Health, so bring a team with one dedicated hitter and let supports feed pierce and Shadow Shepherd.
cheatNotes: Every damage buff you cast (blade, trap, Feint, aura, global, or shadow polymorph) makes him cast the next of five defenses, then repeats after five. Buff sparingly and expect shields.
Shadow Shepherd is the exception, so cast it freely since it never triggers his counter.
Pure armor-piercing effects also do not trigger him, so pierce is your safest damage source.
The first counter is a 650-damage Spirit Armor on his whole side.
The second is Mass Tower Shield across his side.
The third is a -40% incoming damage aura for 4 Rounds.
The fourth is a -25% incoming damage global.
The fifth is Shadow Sentinel, then the cycle restarts.
While he is at full Health his every-other-Round interrupt is a 735 absorb shield, so more armor to clear.
Once he is below full Health that interrupt becomes a ~735 heal instead, so apply Doom and Gloom or Infection to weaken it.

name: Copy Qhat (Ice-Myth)
locations: Novus: La Ville Rose - Cabal Base
cheats: Copy Qhat:
"A yarn copied is a yarn retained" - Copy Qhat will cast a Moon spell to summon one of the following Minions.
Elemental Copy Qhat
Spiritual Copy Qhat
"Feel the curse of Dasein" - After she casts one of the following Spells, she will follow up with another:
Snow Serpent (Tier 3b) - Troll (Tier 3a)
Blizzard - Humongofrog
Colossus (Tier 3b) - Stone Colossus (Tier 3b)
Frost Giant - Orthrus
Snow Angel - Medusa
Abominable Weaver - Mystic Colossus
Freeze Ray - Barbarian's Saga on all Wizards.
Quake Charmer:
"Back to the Basics!" - At the end of every third round Quake Charmer will cast Earthquake.
Vaporize can be used to dispel this cheat.
"This ain't natural" - After he casts one of the following Spells, he will follow up with another:
Troll (Tier 3a) - Fire Elf (Tier 3b)
Humongofrog - Meteor Strike
Stone Colossus (Tier 3b) - Helephant (Tier 3b)
Mystic Colossus - Fire from Above
Orthrus - Fire Dragon
Medusa - Efreet
strategy: This is a two-boss team fight: Copy Qhat (Ice) plus Quake Charmer. Copy Qhat summons an Elemental or Spiritual copy minion, so expect adds early.

Watch the Earthquake timer. Quake Charmer casts Earthquake at the end of every third round, wiping all blades, traps, and wards from the team. Set up and hit inside a three-round window, or Vaporize to cancel the cheat.
Hit Copy Qhat with Fire (she takes +21%, resists everything else 29%).
Both bosses chain a second spell after certain casts, so their turns hit twice; keep heals up and shield against the Fire and Myth follow-ups.
Blade the hitter, Feint the target, and burst each boss inside the Earthquake window.
cheatNotes: She summons an Elemental or Spiritual Copy Qhat minion, so expect an extra body on the board early.
After certain spells she immediately follows with a second, paired spell, so her turns hit twice; shield and heal for two hits.
Quake Charmer casts Earthquake every third round, stripping all blades, traps, and wards from the whole team; hit before it lands or you lose your setup.
Vaporize cancels the Earthquake cheat, letting your blades survive the third round.
Like Copy Qhat, Quake Charmer chains a follow-up spell after certain casts, so his damage comes in pairs too.

name: Copy Qhat (Star)
cheats: Copy Qhat [Star]
"More of you? That's my trick!" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Copy Qhat (Star) will interrupt-cast Mana Burn (Star) on them.
Responses towards Copy Qhat (Moon).
"No, Don't..." - Ally Copy Qhat (Moon), on the enemy side, will cast Bladestorm (+25%) on the Wizard's team.
"You fool!" - ??? (needs clarification)
"Stop that!" - Copy Qhat (Star) will say this after Copy Qhat (Moon) casts Major Snowburst.
Copy Qhat [Moon]
"I am ENDLESS!" - Copy Qhat (Moon) will summon a Copy Qhat (Moon) to any empty space on the enemy side.
"We gonna get you! Pow!" - Summons a Copy Qhat (Moon) on the Wizard's team in the first opening slot.
"There's more where she came from!" - If there isn't currently a Copy Qhat minion on the Wizard's team, a new one will be summoned if a slot is available.
Copy Qhat (Moon) will replace a Minion that was summoned by the Wizard, but won't if all duel slots are occupied.
Defeating the enemy Copy Qhat (Moon) will stop all these cheats.
spellNotes: These Spell notes apply to Copy Qhat (Moon) and not the Boss, Copy Qhat (Star).
Casts a Tower Shield that applies itself to all enemies.
Casts a Hex that applies itself to all allies.
Casts a Black Mantle that applies itself to all allies.
Casts a Balance Blade that applies itself to all enemies.
Casts a Weakness that applies itself to all allies.
strategy: Target the enemy Copy Qhat (Moon) first. Defeating it shuts off all the endless summoning cheats.
Avoid Myth (68% resist). The boss has almost no other weakness, so use your strongest non-Myth school (Storm gets a slim +5%).
He is stunable and beguilable. A stun or beguile buys safe setup rounds against the summon spam.
Expect copies to keep spawning on both sides until the Moon copy is dead, so use AoEs to clear the board while you set up.
With the summons cut off, blade up and burst the Star boss down.
cheatNotes: Joining after Round 2 gets you hit with a Star Mana Burn, so be in the fight from the start.
The ally Copy Qhat (Moon) on the enemy side casts Bladestorm (+25%) buffing the whole enemy team, another reason to remove it fast.
The Moon copy summons another Copy Qhat (Moon) into any open enemy slot, refilling their side.
It also drops a Copy Qhat (Moon) onto your team's first open slot.
If no Copy Qhat minion is on your team, it summons one whenever a slot opens.
The summoned Copy Qhat overwrites a minion you summoned unless every duel slot is already full.
Defeating the enemy Copy Qhat (Moon) shuts off every one of these summon cheats. Make it your first kill.

name: Corporal Tenni'syn
locations: Empyrea: Zanadu - Corporal Tenni'syn Stone Key Room
cheats: "Reinforcements? Ha! You're late!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Tenni'syn will interrupt-cast his natural attack on the Wizard.
At the end of every Round, Tenni'syn will gain a number of additional power pips that is equal to the number of Wizards that is present in the duel minus one.
"Into the jaws of Death with you!" - Tenni'syn will say this at the beginning of the Round that he gains his seventh Shadow pip to cast Lord of the Squall (Shadow).
Dispel Cycle:
"Ah, clever! Don't do that again" - When the first Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow).
"Oh, no! - I'm ready for you this time!" - When the second Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow).
"I'm warning you! No more!" - When the third Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow).
"You can't hurt me! - You've made me stronger!" - When the fourth Dispel is cast on Tenni'syn, he uses Cleanse Charm (Shadow) and Healing Current on himself.
"Volley and thunder! Shatter and Sunder!"' - After the cheat above triggers, he'll cast Cleanse Charm (Shadow), cast his Natural Attack then proceed with the cheat below.
"Lightning from the left of you! Lightning from the right!" - After casting his Natural Attack, Tenni'syn will repeatedly cast Wild Bolt on random Wizards up to seven times.
Feint Cycle:
"Ah, clever! Don't do that again" - Tenni'syn will say this when the first Feint per Round is cast on him.
"Oh, no! - I'm ready for you this time!" - For every second Feint per Round that is cast on Tenni'syn, he will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2) on himself removing 2 Traps.

"You can't hurt me! - You've made me stronger!" - When a Damage over Time is used after a Feint is cast on him on the same turn, then Tenni'syn will interrupt-cast a Windstorm, a Supercharge (+12%), a +12% version of Stormspear, and a version of Storm Hound (150) that attacks all Wizards, followed up by Shift on a random Wizard and Incindiate on all Wizards then use Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) on himself to remove all Traps.
"Time to finish this!" - Every four Rounds, Tenni'syn will use his Natural Attack dealing Storm Damage.
If Tenni'syn is damaged on the Round before this cheat occurs, he will use Wild Bolt for every trap that was consumed when he was damaged.
spellNotes: Casts both a protected and unprotected Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).
Casts both a protected and unprotected Windstorm (+25%).
Casts Lord of the Squall (Shadow) that removes all Charms, Wards, Pips, and Shadow Pips from all Wizards.
Casts a Shadow Cleanse Ward that removes all negative Wards.
