Deals an unknown quantity of Storm Damage.
strategy: A team fight against a 64,000-health boss whose cheats snowball the longer it runs. The classic answer is a coordinated Fire two-round burst: the team stacks Backdrafts and Feints onto one Fire hitter (bring Fire-mastery amulets for off-school wizards) so the boss dies before Lord of the Squall. (A solo clear is possible with the same lines, but the setup is built for a team.)

Do NOT use any Damage-over-Time. The guide is flagged outdated because DoTs are now reflected, and a DoT cast the same turn as a Feint triggers a brutal storm barrage (Windstorm, Supercharge, Storm Hound on all, Shift, Incindiate) plus a full trap wipe.
Only place ONE Feint per round. A second Feint in the same round makes him Cleanse Ward off your last two traps, so split your two Feints across the two rounds or protect one with Indemnity.
Round 1: Feint, Backdraft, Frenzy or Berserk aura on the hitter, then a Fire Trap.
Round 2: a DIFFERENT Backdraft (trained and potent Backdraft do not stack, so use one item/TC Backdraft), a different Feint, then Heckhound followed by Incindiate as the finish.
Advanced line: Round 1 potent Feint, Indemnity Backdraft, Frenzy, Indemnity Feint; Round 2 item Backdraft, item/TC Feint, Heckhound, Incindiate.

Enter on round 1 and hold your entry position: latecomers eat an interrupt natural attack, and he gains extra power pips per extra wizard, so a slow fight lets him reach seven Shadow pips and cast Lord of the Squall which strips all your charms, wards, and pips.
cheatNotes: Any wizard joining after round 2 gets interrupt-hit by his natural attack. Enter on round 1.
He gains bonus power pips each round equal to the number of wizards minus one, so more wizards means he powers up faster. Keep the fight to two rounds so he never banks enough.
When he reaches his seventh Shadow pip he casts Lord of the Squall, wiping every wizard's charms, wards, pips, and shadow pips. Kill him before he gets there.
The first Dispel each cycle just makes him Cleanse Charm it off himself. Dispels are largely wasted here.
The second Dispel again gets cleansed off with Cleanse Charm.
The third Dispel is cleansed off the same way, so stacking dispels does not lock him down.
The fourth Dispel makes him Cleanse Charm and heal himself with Healing Current, actively undoing your progress. Avoid dispel strategies entirely.
After that fourth-dispel heal he cleanses again, fires his natural attack, and rolls into the Wild Bolt spam below. Do not push the dispel cycle this far.
He then repeatedly casts Wild Bolt on random wizards up to seven times, which can one-shot squishy wizards. This is why the dispel cycle is not worth triggering.
The first Feint each round just gets his warning line and is safe. One Feint per round is fine.
Every second Feint in the same round makes him Cleanse Ward off two of your traps, so never double-Feint in one round.

Casting a Damage-over-Time on the same turn as a Feint triggers a devastating storm combo (Windstorm, Supercharge, +12% Stormspear, Storm Hound on all, Shift, Incindiate) and a total trap wipe. Keep DoTs out of your deck.
Every four rounds he fires his natural attack for Storm damage, another reason to finish inside two rounds.
If you damage him the round before that four-round natural attack, he adds a Wild Bolt for each trap consumed by your hit, so time your burst carefully.

name: Count Strigoi (Challenge)

stats.resist: 33% to all schools53% to all schools73% to all schools

cheats: [Minion] "They... ascended" - At the beginning of Round 1, Strigoi will summon 3 Ascended Guards.
[Manipulation] "I told you child... I protect you from the dangers beyond" - At the beginning of the round, Count Strigoi will cast Confusion on on a random wizard and heal himself for 6600 HP.

[Afterlife] "And now my hunger returns!" - When Ascended Guard dies, Strigoi will cast Sacrifice to revive them with 20% of their max HP at the cost of 2000 of his HP. If multiple Ascended Guards are defeated in 1 spell, he will cast Sacrifice multiple times.
[Damage] "We turn our pain into the master's boon!" - When Ascended Guard is hit with an attack, if they are not defeated, they will heal Count Strigoi for 5000 HP.
[Health] "Eh, I'm feeling a little overstuffed" - Count Strigoi will say this when he is at full HP at the start of a turn, allowing the Wizards to defeat him.

[Health] "The only thing I feel is hunger pangs!" - Count Strigoi will say this when he is not at full HP at the start of a turn. Defeating him in this state will result in him casting Scarecrow and reviving.
[Steal] "Are you alone? Let me fix that!" - If Count Strigoi is not defeated from full HP in a single attack, he will cast Scarecrow and revive himself.
[Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Count Strigoi or an Ascended Guard is hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on himself:
If he is not defeated by the attack, he will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If he is defeated by the attack, he will cast a version of Minor Blessing on himself that heals for 100% of his max health.

[Spell] "Is that the sun?!" - If either Count Strigoi or an Ascended Guard is hit by a spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal dispel on themself.
strategy: Run this as a team: a hitter who lands the kill, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer to manage Confusion and the round-1 guards. He resists Storm and Life 73%, so hit with Ice or Myth (only 33% resist).
Round 1 he summons three Ascended Guards, and every round he Confuses a random wizard and heals himself 6,600, so you must burst, not grind.
Do not chip the guards or the boss for partial damage. Hitting a guard without killing it heals Strigoi 5,000, and killing a guard makes him Sacrifice-revive it.

The win condition is his full-health state (he says he is overstuffed): defeat him from FULL HP in a single attack. If he is below full when he dies, or you fail to one-shot him, he Scarecrows and fully revives.

Never Drain him or an Ascended Guard and never use a spell with a sun in it: a drain that kills him full-heals him, a drain that fails Aegis-blades him, and sun spells give him a protected universal dispel.
Stack blades and Feints across several rounds, let him heal back to full, then unload one massive hit that kills him from full while also clearing the guards.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he summons three Ascended Guards. Expect to fight through them or clear them on your kill turn.
At the start of the round he Confuses a random wizard and heals himself 6,600. Burst him down before the heals add up.
When an Ascended Guard dies he Sacrifice-revives it at 20% HP for 2,000 of his own HP, casting once per guard killed in a single spell.
Hitting an Ascended Guard without killing it heals Strigoi 5,000. Never chip the guards.
When he is at full HP he says he is overstuffed. This is the state you want: kill him from here in one hit.
When he is below full HP he says he feels hunger pangs. Defeating him in this state makes him Scarecrow and revive.
If he is not defeated from full HP in a single attack, he Scarecrows and revives himself. Your kill must be a one-shot from full.
Hitting Strigoi or a guard with a Drain triggers one of the two responses below. Do not use Drain spells on this fight.
If the drain does not kill him, he Aegis-protects a +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If the drain does kill him, he casts a Minor Blessing that heals him for 100% of his max health. Draining him to death fully heals him.
Hitting Strigoi or a guard with a spell that has a sun in it (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) makes them protected-universal-dispel themselves. Avoid sun spells.

name: Count Strigoi (Standard)
cheats: "They... ascended" - At the beginning of Round 1, Strigoi will summon 3 Ascended Guards.
"I told you child... I protect you from the dangers beyond" - At the beginning of the round, Count Strigoi will cast Confusion on on a random wizard and heal himself for 6600 HP.

"And now my hunger returns!" - When Ascended Guard (Standard) dies, Strigoi will cast Sacrifice to revive them with 20% of their max HP at the cost of 2000 of his HP. If multiple Ascended Guards are defeated in 1 spell, he will cast Sacrifice multiple times.
"We turn our pain into the master's boon!" - When Ascended Guard (Standard) is hit with an attack, if they are not defeated, they will heal Count Strigoi for 5000 HP.
"Eh, I'm feeling a little overstuffed" - Count Strigoi will say this when he is at full HP at the start of a turn, allowing the Wizards to defeat him.
"The only thing I feel is hunger pangs!" - Count Strigoi will say this when he is not at full HP at the start of a turn. Defeating him in this state will result in him casting Scarecrow and reviving.
"Are you alone? Let me fix that!" - If Count Strigoi is not defeated from full HP in a single attack, he will cast Scarecrow and revive himself.
"You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Count Strigoi or an Ascended Guard is hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on himself:
If he is not defeated by the attack, he will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If he is defeated by the attack, he will cast a version of Minor Blessing on himself that heals for 100% of his max health.

"Is that the sun?!" - If either Count Strigoi or an Ascended Guard is hit by a spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal dispel on themself.
strategy: Team Darkmoor fight. The win condition is killing Strigoi from FULL health in a single attack. If he is below full when defeated, or survives the shot, he Scarecrows and revives.
He heals 6600 to full every round anyway, so do not chip him. Treat every attack on him as the finisher while supports pile blades on one hitter and Feint Strigoi.
Hit with Life (+30%); he resists everything else 37% and Death 67%.
Leave his Ascended Guards alone. Hitting a guard without killing it heals Strigoi 5000, and killing one makes him Sacrifice-revive it for 2000 of his own HP.
Expect Confusion on a random wizard each round, so the hitter should not gamble the kill shot on a Confused turn.
When blades are stacked and he says he is overstuffed (full HP), the hitter lands one huge Life hit big enough to kill him outright from full.
cheatNotes: He opens by summoning three Ascended Guards, so expect a full board immediately.
Every round he Confuses a random wizard and heals 6600 back toward full. Your hitter should not risk a Confused turn on the kill shot.
Killing an Ascended Guard makes him revive it with Sacrifice at the cost of 2000 of his HP. Do not clear guards unless you can end the whole fight that turn.
Hitting a guard without killing it heals Strigoi 5000. Never tickle the guards; hit only Strigoi, and only to kill.
When he says he is overstuffed he is at full HP. That is the state you must kill him in, so this is your green light.
If he says he feels hunger pangs he is below full HP; killing him now just makes him Scarecrow and revive. Wait for full.
He must be dropped from full HP in ONE attack. A hit that leaves him alive triggers Scarecrow and a revive, so build enough to one-shot roughly 20000.
Avoid Drain spells on him or the guards; the target counters as noted below.
If a Drain does not kill him, he Aegis-shields a +100% Balanceblade onto himself, making his next hit far worse. Skip Drain attacks.
If a Drain kills him he just Minor-Blessings back to full health. Drains cannot finish this fight; use a straight Life hit.
Hitting him with a Sun-infused spell (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) that does not kill makes him self-cast a protected universal dispel, wasting your setup. Use a non-Sun finisher.

name: Crash the Bandit Coot (Challenge)
cheats: "Crickey, too many power-ups!" - At the end of every Round, Crash the Bandit Coot casts a 0 Damage Shadow version of Empower that gives him a random School Pip.
"Crash Attack!" - At the beginning of every Round, if Crash the Bandit Coot has 2 or more of the same School Pip, he will cast the following Spells:
[Fire School Pip] 2 Fire Pips: Sun Serpent and a version of Backfire that clears all negative Wards from all allies.
[Ice School Pip] 2 Ice Pips: Lord of Winter and a version of Glacial Fortress that clears all negative Charms from all allies.
[Storm School Pip] 2 Storm Pips: Storm Owl and Disarm (Shadow, All Enemies), removing one Charm from all enemies.
[Myth School Pip] 2 Myth Pips: Celestial Calendar and Cleanse Ward (Shadow, All Allies), removing one Ward from all allies.
[Life School Pip] 2 Life Pips: Spinysaur and a version of Oni's Naturalism that clears all protected Wards from all allies.
[Death School Pip] 2 Death Pips: Avenging Fossil and a version of Putrefaction that clears all protected Charms from all enemies.
[Balance School Pip] 2 Balance Pips: Sabertooth and casts a 0 Damage Shadow version of Empower that gives him a random School Pip.
This cheat can repeat multiple times during the same Round.

"Crash Lives Again!" - After Crash the Bandit Coot is defeated once, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring himself to 100% max Health, then cast an Equalize Global, setting the Damage cap to 4,000
This Global will be recast if replaced.
strategy: Two health bars: the first time Crash dies he heals to full and drops an Equalize setting the damage cap to 4,000, so plan to grind him in 4,000-damage chunks after the revive.

He gains a random School Pip every round and, when he holds two of one school, opens the round with that school's big attack plus a strip that clears blades, wards, or traps depending on the school. Expect setup to get partially wiped, so hit fast and re-blade cheaply.
Hitter goes with blades funneled; supports re-apply blades each round since Crash keeps stripping charms and wards.
Because single hits cap at 4,000 after the revive, favor repeatable blade-plus-hit turns over one huge stacked setup that his strips can eat.
Healer manages the incoming Sun Serpent, Lord of Winter, Storm Owl and similar hits and keeps the hitter topped through both phases.
Do not bother replacing his Equalize Global; he recasts it immediately.
cheatNotes: He gains a free random School Pip at the end of every round, feeding his Crash Attack, so the fight has a built-in clock.
When he has two matching School Pips he opens the round with that school's attack and a cleanse or strip. Watch his pips to predict which buffs you are about to lose.
Two Fire pips brings Sun Serpent and clears negative wards off his side.
Two Ice pips brings Lord of Winter and strips your negative charms, so weaknesses come off.
Two Storm pips brings Storm Owl and Disarms a charm from every wizard.
Two Myth pips brings Celestial Calendar and clears one ward from each of his allies.
Two Life pips brings Spinysaur and removes your protected wards.
Two Death pips brings Avenging Fossil and strips protected charms off enemies, so even Aegis'd blades on the boss are not safe.
Two Balance pips brings Sabertooth and just gives him another random pip.
All of this can fire more than once in a single round if he has multiple pip pairs.
His first death is not the end: he heals to 100% and sets a 4,000 damage cap, so pace your hits around that ceiling.
Replacing his Equalize Global is pointless; he recasts it.

name: Crash the Bandit Coot (Standard)

"Crash Attack!" - At the beginning of every Round, if Crash the Bandit Coot has 2 or more of the same School Pip, he will cast the following Spells:
[Fire School Pip] 2 Fire Pips: Sun Serpent
[Ice School Pip] 2 Ice Pips: Lord of Winter
[Storm School Pip] 2 Storm Pips: Storm Owl
[Myth School Pip] 2 Myth Pips: Celestial Calendar
[Life School Pip] 2 Life Pips: Spinysaur
[Death School Pip] 2 Death Pips: Avenging Fossil
[Balance School Pip] 2 Balance Pips: Sabertooth
This cheat can repeat multiple times during the same Round.
strategy: The guide calls this a simple fight: he never blocks your buffs, so blade and trap freely, then kill.
It plays as a 4-hitter team. Each wizard blades and casts a 7-pip AoE like Frenzy; a coordinated team is often done in two rounds.
He has no school resist listed, so hit with your strongest school and good accuracy.
Keep it short so his cheats never matter: every 2nd round he Empowers a random school pip, and once he holds 2+ of one school he free-casts a matching big hit.
If you are slower, stun or beguile him to skip a Crash Attack turn, but the guide's plan is just to out-pace him and burst before he banks a matching pip pair.
cheatNotes: Every 2nd round he Empowers himself a random school pip, feeding his Crash Attack. Stun or beguile him to disrupt the buildup, or just kill him before it snowballs.
At the start of any round he holds 2+ pips of one school, he free-casts a matching big hit (see the school list). Keep the fight short so he never banks a matching pair.
With enough matching pips this can fire multiple times in one round, so never let him stockpile a school's pips.

name: Cronus (Tartarus)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus - Cave of Nyx
cheats: Every Round, Cronus will cast a Sun Spell that places a +40% Trap of the following Schools.
Balance, Death, Fire, Ice, Myth, Life, Storm.
Sometimes Cronus casts an invisible trap, probably due to being a Balance trap, that seems to act like a universal trap. This trap is not listed in effects.
Every four Rounds, he will interrupt cast Lord of Night on a random Wizard.
When a Wizard places a trap on him, he will place a Marked Stun Block on himself.
Cronus will only place up to one marked stun block on himself, regardless of how many traps are placed on him, until he is attacked with a non-DoT spell.
When attacked by a single-targeting spell, a Cleanse Ward will be cast on him removing all Traps, and the Marked Stun Block.
Vaporize can dispel this cheat.
Spells that only place a DoT on Cronus will not remove the mark.

"Attacks alone will never hurt me!" - If any Wizards attack without placing a trap on him, Cronus will interrupt cast his Natural Attack of the opposite School that a Wizard cast (Example: Storm damage spell = Myth Myth Natural Attack +40% to the next Myth damage ward spell).
Casting a Balance damage spell will result in Cronus using Natural Attack (Cronus Storm).
Multiple damage spells such as Minotaur will trigger him casting his natural twice.
All enemies damage spells will trigger this cheat.
DoT spells will trigger this cheat only on initial hit.
Any Astral spells trigger an unknown damage school.
Shadow spells trigger an unknown damage school.
strategy: Team fight (24k, three Lost Shades, Tartarus finale). Assign a hitter; supports blade the hitter and manage traps, healer covers the Lord of Night hits. Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+30%); never Balance (80% resist).

He counters two ways: attack him WITHOUT a trap on him and he fires his opposite-school natural attack; attack with a single-target spell and he Cleanse Wards off all traps plus his stun block. So always have a trap on him and set up plus hit the same round.
He lays a +40% trap every round — stack your own Feint the same round the hitter fires so the traps get cashed before he can cleanse.
Use DoTs to hold traps if needed — a DoT-only hit won't remove the mark or traps, but avoid single-target and AoE hits until you're committing the kill.
Watch round 4 and every 4th: Lord of Night on a random wizard — keep health topped.
Funnel blades to the hitter and burst him through the setup window.
cheatNotes: Every round he lays a +40% trap (rotating schools) — extra damage if you can hit into them.
He may add an invisible universal-acting trap too; treat his stacked traps as free damage.
Every 4 rounds he interrupt-casts Lord of Night on a random wizard — keep health up.
Trapping him makes him put a Marked Stun Block on himself.
He keeps only one stun block no matter how many traps, and holds it until hit by a non-DoT spell.
A single-target attack makes him Cleanse Ward off all traps and the stun block — set up and hit the same round.
Vaporize can dispel the cleanse cheat.
A DoT-only hit won't remove the mark or traps — useful for keeping traps up.
Attack without a trap on him and he fires his opposite-school natural attack at you — always trap him first.
Hitting with Balance makes him use his Storm natural attack in return.
Multi-hit spells like Minotaur make him fire his natural attack twice.
AoE attacks also trigger the natural-attack counter.
DoT spells trigger the counter only on the initial hit.
Astral spells trigger an unknown-school counter — risky.
Shadow spells trigger an unknown-school counter — risky.

name: Cronus (Tartarus, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Cave of Nyx (Challenge)

stats.resist: 17% to all schools37% to all schools57% to all schools

cheats: [Trap] At the beginning of every Round, Cronus will cast a Sun Spell that applies a +40% Trap of a random School on all Wizards.
[Shield] "For every shield, I have a trap!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Shield, Cronus will cast a Sun Spell on the Wizard that applies a +40% Trap of a random School.
[Jinx] If a harmful Ward is cast on Cronus, he will remove it.

If a Multi-Trap Spell such as Elemental Trap is cast on Cronus, he will only remove one Ward, and Cronus will place a Marked Stun Block on himself. After Cronus is attacked while he has a Marked effect, he will remove the Mark and all harmful Wards from himself.
All Enemies harmful Ward Spells such as Windstorm will cause the Ward to be removed from Cronus only.

Casting an attack on Cronus that places multiple Wards will trigger his below cheat (if not Marked), then cause him to remove one Ward and place a Mark. If he is Marked when hit with a spell that places multiple Wards, he will remove the Mark but not place another.

[Damage] "Attacks alone will never hurt me!" - If Cronus is attacked when he doesn't have a Marked effect, Cronus will cast a version of his Natural Attack based on the School of the Spell that hit him.
Fire Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Storm)
Ice Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Fire)
Storm Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Ice)
Life Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Death)
Myth Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Life)
Death Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Myth)
Balance or Shadow Spell -> Natural Attack (Cronus Balance)
Damage Spells that hit multiple times such as Minotaur will trigger this cheat multiple times.
Damage over Time Spells will only trigger this cheat on their initial hit.
strategy: Team fight in Tartarus with three Lost Shades. Assign a hitter and keep the rest on setup and cheat management.
Every round he traps all wizards with a +40% random-school trap, and any shield you cast adds another, so skip shields and keep heals ready for the extra damage.

His main cheat: attacked while not Marked, he counters with a natural attack of the opposing school. Mark him first by casting a multi-trap spell like Elemental Trap, which makes him place a Marked Stun Block on himself.
While he is Marked, attacking removes the Mark instead of triggering the counter, so land your hits during that window.
Hit with Storm. He resists Storm only 17% versus 37-57% for everything else, so avoid Fire and Myth (57%).
He is not stunable and clears harmful wards off himself, so do not rely on traps sticking to him. Blade the hitter and burst.
cheatNotes: Every round he drops a +40% random-school trap on all wizards, raising the damage you take.
Any shield you cast earns you another +40% random-school trap.
A harmful ward placed on him is simply removed.
A multi-trap spell like Elemental Trap makes him remove one ward and place a Marked Stun Block on himself; hitting him while Marked strips the Mark and his wards.
All-enemies ward spells like Windstorm only get removed from Cronus.
Attacking with a multi-ward spell triggers his counter if he is unmarked, then removes one ward and Marks him.
Attacked while not Marked, he counters with a natural attack based on the school that hit him.
A Fire hit makes him counter with Cronus Storm.
An Ice hit makes him counter with Cronus Fire.
A Storm hit makes him counter with Cronus Ice.
A Life hit makes him counter with Cronus Death.
A Myth hit makes him counter with Cronus Life.
A Death hit makes him counter with Cronus Myth.
A Balance or Shadow hit makes him counter with Cronus Balance.
Multi-hit spells like Minotaur trigger the counter once per hit.
DoT spells only trigger the counter on their first tick.

name: Cyphurr
cheats: [Pet][Charm] "I know just the trick" - At the start of Round 1 and every 4 rounds after, Cyphurr's Pet will cast an Item Card version of Precision.
[Blade]"I'll take that!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade spell, Cyphurr's Pet will cast Steal Charm.
[Pet][Stun] "Grimoire! Page 43!" - On the third or fourth turn Cyphurr's Pet will cast Stun on a random Wizard.
[Pet][Aura] "Grimoire! Page 87!" - On the second turn Cyphurr's Pet will cast Icewind.
strategy: Bring Fire. Cyphurr is Ice with 30% Ice resist and takes 15% boosted Fire, so a Fire hitter is the clean pick.
Delay blades. His pet counters any Blade spell with Steal Charm, so wait to blade until the turn you plan to hit, or use Aegis on the key blade so it survives the steal.
Plan around the turn-2 and turn-3/4 pet plays. Icewind lands as an aura on turn 2, and a Stun hits a random wizard on turn 3 or 4, so carry a stun block for your hitter.
Expect a Precision item card on his pet at Round 1 and every 4 rounds, boosting his accuracy; there is no fizzle to bait, so just out-pace him.
Set up quickly, blade and hit in the same window to dodge the Steal Charm, and burst him with one large Fire attack.
cheatNotes: At Round 1 and every 4th round after, his pet gives him an Item Card Precision, raising his accuracy for the coming attacks.
Cast any Blade and his pet immediately Steal Charms it. Blade the same turn you hit, or Aegis-protect the blade you need.
On turn 3 or 4 his pet Stuns a random wizard, so keep a stun block ready for your hitter.
On turn 2 his pet casts Icewind as an aura; account for the extra Ice output that turn.

name: Dark Cloud Leader
locations: Empyrea: Nimbus Citadel - Audience Chamber (Nimbus Citadel)
cheats: "Reinforcements? Ha! You're late! " - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, he will interrupt cast Queen Calypso (Shadow).
spellNotes: Casts a Shadow version of Triton.
strategy: He resists every school 70% except Life and Balance (+5%). Hit with Life or Balance, or prism into them.
He is stunnable and beguilable, so a stun or Beguile buys setup rounds against him and his Dark Cloud Agent and Blue Ox minions.
Join on time; arriving late triggers an interrupt Queen Calypso (Shadow) hit.
His Shadow Trap is Indemnity-protected and he casts a Shadow Triton, so expect a sizeable hit. Shield or heal through it.
Blade up, clear or ignore the minions, and land a Life or Balance AoE.
cheatNotes: This only punishes late arrivals with an interrupt Queen Calypso. Zone in on time and it never fires.

name: Dasein Duelist
cheats: [Afterlife] "Good thing I have extra lives!" - When Dasein Duelist is defeated the first time, he will revive himself with 50% of his total Health and cast Meteor Strike.
Spells like Doom and Gloom will not decrease the amount of healing that he receives.
[Afterlife] "It ain't game over yet!" - When Dasein Duelist is defeated the second time, he will revive himself with 25% of his total Health and cast Rain of Fire.
[Trap] "Hammer Time!" - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Dasein Duelist casts a Fire version of Hammer of Thor that clears all Blades and Traps from himself and deals 475 Fire Damage to the Wizard.
Indemnity will not protect Traps from this cheat.
strategy: Plan for three full health bars: he revives once at 50% and again at 25%, and healing debuffs like Doom and Gloom will not shrink those revives.
Do not trap him. Any Trap you cast triggers a Fire Hammer of Thor that strips all blades and traps from him and hits you for 475, and Indemnity will not save the traps.
Build damage with blades and Feints on yourself instead, since Feint sits on you, not on him. He resists everything 40% except Ice, which he takes 10% more of, so Ice hits are best.
Hit hard enough to punch through each revive, and expect a Meteor Strike after the first death and a Rain of Fire after the second, so keep your Health up between kills.
cheatNotes: The first time you defeat him he revives at 50% Health and casts Meteor Strike.
Doom and Gloom and other healing cuts will not reduce his revive healing.
The second time you defeat him he revives at 25% Health and casts Rain of Fire.
Cast a Trap and he answers with a Fire Hammer of Thor that wipes all blades and traps off himself and hits you for 475 Fire.
Indemnity will not protect your traps from that Hammer of Thor.

name: Death Card
locations: Darkmoor: Outsiders Camp - Zone of Fortune

stats.resist: 36% to all schools66% to all schools

cheats: [Health] "Embrace Your Truth, Use It." - At the beginning of the First Round, the Death Card will cast a Spell that deals 80% of each Wizard's maximum Health, then give each Wizard a copy of Healing Current (The Death Card).
[Healing Current (The Death Card)]
This spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat The Death Card.
strategy: Come in at full Health with no self-damage effects up. On Round 1 he hits everyone for 80% of their max Health.
Use the free Healing Current he hands each Wizard to top off on Round 2, then start setup.
Hit Life. He takes +30% from Life and resists Death 66%, so prism if you are Death.
Stack blades and traps over a few Rounds, then land one big Life hit. This is a team fight at 16,115 Health, so funnel blades to one hitter.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he hits everyone for 80% of their max Health, then gives each Wizard a free Healing Current. Survive the shot, then heal up.

name: Death Minotaur
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus - Garden of Statues
cheats: This Boss uses his first turn casting a Death Global Spell that increases Death Damage by +60%.
"With life comes death!" - Casting a Heal will trigger the Death Minotaur to cast a +75% version of Death Trap on the caster.
Pet 'may casts' do not trigger this Cheat.
strategy: Tartarus team fight: hitter, blading supports, and a healer. Hit with Ice or Life (+35% incoming); avoid Death (80% resist).
Minimize healing. Every heal plants a +75% Death Trap on the healer that his next Death hit exploits. Heal only when you can cleanse it, or use pet may-cast heals which do not trigger it.
Note his round-1 Death global boosts all Death damage by 60%, so keep shields up for his hits.
He is stunable; use a stun to skip a dangerous turn.
Funnel blades and Feint to the hitter, then one big hit through the 15,840 health. AoE to catch the Ill-Fated Serpentine, or tank it and focus the boss.
cheatNotes: His first turn plants a Death global that boosts all Death damage by 60%, so his hits land harder. Keep shields up.
Any Heal stamps a +75% Death Trap on the wizard who healed, so his next Death attack on them is brutal. Cleanse it or avoid healing.
Pet may-cast heals do not trigger the Death Trap, so a pet heal is a safe way to top off.

name: Death Minotaur (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Garden of Statues (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade][Aura] "Your charms can't pierce this!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm or helpful Aura, Death Minotaur will cast Headless Horseman (Tier 2b) on the recipient.
[Harmful Aura] "Is that your strategy? Seems weak to me" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Aura on Death Minotaur, it will cast Weakness on the caster.

[Healing] "With life comes death!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Death Minotaur will cast a +70% incoming Death Damage Aura for 4 Rounds on the recipient, or on the caster if it was an All Allies Spell.
strategy: Hit Life. He takes boosted Life damage and resists 80% Death, so never attack into Death without a prism.
Do not use helpful charms or auras. Each one makes him cast Headless Horseman on whoever received it.
Do not put harmful auras on him - that returns a Weakness on you.
Avoid healing spells. A heal puts a +70% incoming Death damage aura for 4 rounds on the recipient (or on the caster if it was a group heal), which sets up his Death natural attacks to spike.
Since charms, auras, and heals are all punished, keep setup lean: use Feints (traps are not punished here) and a Life hit to burst him and his lone Serpentine minion down fast.
cheatNotes: Casting a helpful charm or helpful aura makes him hit that recipient with Headless Horseman (Tier 2b). Skip blades and offensive auras.
Putting a harmful aura on him returns a Weakness onto you, the caster.
Casting any healing spell places a +70% incoming Death damage aura for 4 rounds on the recipient - or on the caster if it was an all-allies heal - amplifying his next Death hit.

name: Death Satyr (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - House of Faun (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade][Aura] "There is no strength, only weakness" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm or helpful Aura, Death Satyr will cast Weakness on the recipient.
[Harmful Aura] "Reduce me? I'm so sad!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Aura, Death Satyr will cast Monk of Mourning (Tier 2a) on the recipient.
[Healing] "With recovery comes reduction" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Death Satyr will cast Attenuate on the recipient, or on the caster if it was an All Allies Spell.
strategy: Hit with Life. He is Death, resists Death 80%, and takes boosted Life damage.
Build damage with Traps and Feint, not Blades or Auras. Every helpful Charm or Aura you cast earns a Weakness on that Wizard.
Avoid harmful Auras; he answers them with Monk of Mourning on the caster.
Heal only when you must; any Healing spell triggers Attenuate on the healed Wizard.
Stack traps over 2-3 rounds, then finish with one Life AoE to take the Satyr and its three minions together.
cheatNotes: Any helpful Charm or Aura you cast gets that Wizard a Weakness. Set up with Traps instead of Blades.
A harmful Aura makes him cast Monk of Mourning on the caster. Skip damage auras.
Healing triggers Attenuate on the healed Wizard (or on the caster for all-allies heals). Heal only when necessary.

name: Death Tendril
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Graveyard Stone Key Room
cheats: Aphrodite II:
The following duel circle spots are considered to be across from each other, in regards to the Boss cheats:
If a Blade Damage Charm or Damage-Increasing Global Spell is cast (e.g. Darkwind), the Tendril across from the Wizard will cheat-cast the following Spells:
"Terrify the vermin!" and "Find their weak points!" - The Death Tendril will cheat-cast Scarecrow and Feint (+75%) on a random Wizard.
"Tear down the Blades!" and "Seek out the cracks in their foundation!" - The Life Tendril will cheat-cast Forest Lord and Guidance.
"Shake the earth!" and "Stun the strongest!" - The Myth Tendril will cheat-cast Earthquake, then Stun a random Wizard.
When the Tendril across from the Wizard has been defeated, that Wizard may safely cast Blades without triggering this cheat.
If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time Spell, the Tendril across from the Wizard will cheat-cast the following Spell:
"Summon the deadly hunt!" - The Death Tendril will cheat-cast Deer Knight.
"Shake it off!" - The Life Tendril will cheat-cast Mass Triage, which remains active for 3 Rounds.
"Steal the strength!" - The Myth Tendril will cheat-cast Shift at the Wizard.
When a Wizard is defeated, the Tendril across from the Wizard will cheat-cast the following Spell:
"Find their weak points!" - The Death Tendril will cheat-cast Feint (+75%).
"Seek out the cracks in their foundation!" - The Life Tendril will cheat-cast Guidance.
"Stun the strongest!" - The Myth Tendril will cheat-cast Stun on a random Wizard.
Interrupt Cycle:

"I am untouched! My spores will spread over everyone!" - Any Round that a Tendril is not attacked, Aphrodite II will cast a unique Spell on all Wizards, which deals 333 Shadow Damage and 666 Shadow Damage over 3 Rounds, and applies a -30% Damage Charm, a -40% Accuracy Charm, and a -50% Healing Charm.
A Wizard fleeing will trigger this cheat.
A Wizard joining late will not trigger this cheat.
"No! That stings!" - Attacking any of the Tendrils will interrupt this cheat.
strategy: Team fight against Aphrodite II and her Death, Life, and Myth Tendrils. Hit with Ice or Life (+35%), never Death (80% resist).

Every round at least one Tendril must be attacked or she blasts all wizards for 333 Shadow plus 666 Shadow over 3 rounds and stacks -30% damage, -40% accuracy, -50% healing. Assign someone to hit a Tendril each round.
Blades and damage globals trigger the Tendril directly across from the caster. Kill the Tendril across from your hitter first so the hitter can blade freely.
DoTs also punish across (Death: Deer Knight, Life: Mass Triage, Myth: Shift), so hold DoTs until the opposing Tendril is dead.
Supports blade the hitter from safe spots and keep a Tendril attacked every round to suppress the spore blast, then funnel one big Ice or Life AoE to clear the Tendrils and Aphrodite together.
cheatNotes: Cheats punish the wizard directly across from the acting Tendril, position your hitter opposite the Tendril you kill first.
Casting a blade or damage global makes the Tendril across from you retaliate, do not blade until that Tendril is dead.
The Death Tendril answers a blade across from it with Scarecrow and a +75% Feint on a random wizard.
The Life Tendril answers with Forest Lord and Guidance.
The Myth Tendril answers with Earthquake, which wipes your blades and traps, then stuns a random wizard.
Once the Tendril across from you is dead you can blade freely from that spot.
Casting a DoT also triggers the Tendril across from you, hold DoTs until it is dead.
The Death Tendril answers a DoT with Deer Knight.
The Life Tendril answers a DoT with Mass Triage, which removes your DoTs for 3 rounds.
The Myth Tendril answers a DoT by Shifting it back onto you.
If a wizard is defeated, the Tendril across from them punishes the team.
The Death Tendril casts a +75% Feint after a wizard falls.
The Life Tendril casts Guidance after a wizard falls.
The Myth Tendril stuns a random wizard after a wizard falls.
Any round no Tendril is attacked, she blasts all wizards for 333 Shadow plus 666 over 3 rounds and stacks -30% damage, -40% accuracy, -50% healing. Always attack a Tendril each round.
A wizard fleeing counts as not attacking and sets off the spore blast.
A wizard joining late does not set off the spore blast.
Hitting any Tendril cancels the spore blast for that round, this is how you suppress it.

name: Despair

stats.resist: 42% to all schools72% to all schools

cheats: *SIGH*:
At the beginning of the first Round, Despair will cast a -100% version of Doom and Gloom and will recast this Spell when replaced.
If Despair casts a Spell on a Wizard, it will follow it up with a Deathblade (+45%).
If Despair takes Damage, it will cast a Virulent Plague on itself.
Multi-Hit Spells will trigger this cheat multiple times.
Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
If a Wizard casts one of the following Spell Effects, Despair will cast the Spells:
[Caster][Damage] - Self-Damage Spells, like Empower, will trigger a protected Balanceblade (+100%) on Despair.
[Aura] - Auras will trigger Supernova (Shadow) on the caster.
If a negative Aura is cast on Despair, it will cast the Supernova on itself.
[Charm] - Positive Charms will trigger Enfeeble (Shadow) on the caster.
Aegis can protect against this cheat.
[Ward] - Positive Wards will trigger a Shadow All Enemies version of Shatter on the caster.
Feint or Mass Feint, including Pet Item Cards, will trigger an Ice version of Swap Ward on the caster.
Indemnity or Melt can protect against this cheat.
strategy: Doom and Gloom (-100% healing) is up from Round 1 and recast when removed, so healing is off the table. Win the damage race and rely on shields and avoidance instead of heals.
Every positive charm you cast triggers Enfeeble on you; Aegis-protect your blades so they survive and dodge the punish.
Feint triggers an Ice Swap Ward that flips it back onto you; protect it with Indemnity or Melt.
Skip auras (they Supernova you) and positive wards (they Shatter you). Keep setup to Aegis blades plus an Indemnity Feint.
Hit with Life (he takes +30%, resists Death 72% and everything else 42%) and burst. Note each spell he lands buffs him with a +45% Deathblade and damaging him gives him Virulent Plague.
cheatNotes: He keeps a -100% Doom and Gloom global up all fight and recasts it if removed, so healing is impossible; race him down or shield instead.
Any spell he lands on a wizard is followed by a free +45% Deathblade on himself, so he ramps his own damage; kill before it stacks.
Damaging him makes him Virulent Plague himself, a minor self-weakness, so expect it.
Self-damage spells like Empower give him a protected +100% Balanceblade, so avoid them.
Any aura you cast rebounds as Supernova on you; leave auras at home.
Every positive charm you cast triggers Enfeeble on you; Aegis your blades so the charm survives.
Aegis on the charm blocks the Enfeeble punish.
Positive wards trigger an all-enemies Shatter on you, so do not rely on shields.
Feint or Mass Feint triggers an Ice Swap Ward that flips the trap onto you; protect it.
Indemnity or Melt stops the Swap Ward, keeping your Feint on him.

name: Desperation

stats.resist: 44% to all schools74% to all schools

cheats: "GAH!":
At the beginning of the first Round, Desperation will cast Katabatic Wind and will recast this spell when replaced.
If a Feint or Mass Feint is cast, Desperation will cast a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb version of Frozen Armor on itself.
Melt can be used to prevent this cheat.
Health Threshold Cheats: If Desperation's Health reaches the following percent health thresholds, the following cheats will occur.
Below 80% Health - If Desperation takes Damage, it casts an Ice version of Healing Current restoring 100, 400, or 1,000 Health to itself.
Below 50% Health - At the beginning of every Round, Desperation will cast a -80% Random School Shield or a Tower Shield on itself.
Below 25% Health:
Casts an Ice version of Life Feint that applies a +30% Damage Trap on caster and a +75% Ice Trap on target.
Casts Resuscitate.
spellNotes: Desperation's Brace has the appearance of Sleet Storm.
Casts an Ice version of Sabertooth.
strategy: Assign one hitter and have supports funnel blades. Attack with Fire; he takes +30% from it and resists Ice 74%, everything else 44%.
Do not Feint him. Feint triggers a protected 5,000 Frozen Armor absorb. Bring Melt to strip it, or set up with Fire traps and blades only.
Below 80% health he self-heals every time he is damaged, so avoid poke damage and build fully before hitting.
Below 50% he shields himself every Round, and below 25% he Feints himself and casts Resuscitate to heal back up.

Because of those heal and shield thresholds, stack blades and traps over several rounds, then have the hitter deliver one massive Fire hit that carries him from high health straight through the 25% mark to dead.
cheatNotes: He opens with Katabatic Wind on Round 1 and recasts it whenever it is replaced, so expect that Ice global to stay up.
Casting Feint or Mass Feint makes him shield himself with a protected 5,000-damage Frozen Armor absorb. Do not Feint him unless you can strip it.
Melt removes that Frozen Armor absorb, so bring Melt if you want to Feint safely.
Below 80% health he heals 100 to 1,000 each time he takes damage, so chipping him is counterproductive. Hit big or not at all.
Below 50% he shields himself every Round with a -80% or Tower Shield. Pierce or shatter to push through.
Below 25% he Feints himself, setting a +75% Ice Trap on your hitter and a +30% Damage Trap on himself, so be ready for a hard counter-hit.
Below 25% he also casts Resuscitate to heal back up, so you must burst through the last 25% in one turn.

name: Detolli the Destroyer
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Detolli will cheat-cast Fuel on the Wizard.
"Burn! Burn it all down!" - If a Wizard casts a blade, global, or an aura spell, then Detolli will cast one of the following on himself.
Firespear: +5%, +10% or +15%
Pet spells will trigger this cheat.
Steal Charm will not trigger this cheat.
"We'll reduce this tower to cinders if we must!"' - During Round 1 (and every 2 Rounds thereafter), Detolli will summon a Scorcher (Summon).
Scorcher (Summon) Cheat Cycle: - Detolli will order the Minion to cast Spells on each Round as follows.
Round 1: "How about a bit of fire and brimstone?" - Scorcher (Summon) will cast Brimstone Revenant.
(This cheat is bugged since Scorcher has no archmastery, so it will pass.)
Round 2: "You know nothing of fire!" - Scorcher (Summon) will cast Mass Fire Prism (Random), which converts Fire damage into a random School of damage, including Shadow damage.
Round 3: "Burn out, my friend, and give me a boost!" - Scorcher (Summon) will interrupt with a version of Take Power on Detolli, sacrificing itself to give Detolli the Destroyer two Power Pips.
spellNotes: Detolli's Dragon can cast Meteor Strike (355-385) or Fire Dragon (495+395).
strategy: Hit with Ice; he takes +15% from it and resists Fire 50%.

name: Divine Cabalist
cheats: "L337-HAX9" - Divine Cabalist will cast a Blade, Absorb, and Heal over Time effect on himself, and a Damage Charm, Healing Trap, and Balance Damage over Time on all Wizards. The effect is called "Super Cheat".
"All effects are Permanent" - Whenever any of the hanging effects from the cheat above are removed, he will recast those individual effects.
"Enter: 'Balance is all Things' Weird." -
spellNotes: Casts a version of Old One's Endgame that deals 940 Balance Damage for 5 Pips and 2 Shadow Pips.
Casts a version of Sandstorm that deals 375 Balance Damage.
Casts a version of Clockwork Spider.
strategy: Raid boss at 110,000 health. Bring a full team with a dedicated hitter, blade supports, and a healer. Hit with Myth, Life, or Death, never Balance.

He keeps a permanent Super Cheat package: a blade, an Absorb, and a heal-over-time on himself, plus a damage charm, healing trap, and Balance DoT on every wizard. Removing any piece just makes him recast it, do not waste turns cleansing.
Pop his Absorb with a small throwaway hit right before the hitter's big blow so the nuke is not soaked.
Watch for Old One's Endgame (940 Balance) and Sandstorm, keep the team shielded and topped through the Balance DoT.
Funnel all blades and traps to one hitter and burst in a single stacked Myth/Life/Death nuke while the healer manages the DoT and healing trap.
cheatNotes: He hangs a blade, Absorb, and heal-over-time on himself plus a damage charm, healing trap, and Balance DoT on all wizards. Play through it rather than fighting it.

name: Doctor Demented
locations: Empyrea: Southwest Aero Plains - Dark Cloud Base
cheats: "Time burns away all things, especially when you are late!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Doctor Demented will interrupt-cast Burning Rampage on the Wizard.

"Your fears protect me!" - At the beginning of Round 1, she will cheat-cast a permanent version of Fortify that resists -60%, and it will increase by 20% for each additional Wizard in the duel for up to a maximum of -120%.
The Aura cannot be removed.

"Fear still remains" - If a Wizard joins or flees the duel after it has already begun, she will update her aura accordingly. This also occurs if a Minion is Beguiled with 3 or less Wizards in the duel, treating the Minion as an additional Wizard.
The aura will reset back to its original percentage after the Beguile effect ends.
"You have overcome your fears!? No! Never!" - The turn after all Minions are defeated, she will cheat-cast Frenzy and immediately after cast Supernova on herself.
Universal Traps can amplify the damage of the Supernova.
"Aren't we all having a BLAST!?" - Boss will cheat-cast Incindiate.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Poison (45+450) that attacks all enemies.
Deals 420 Death Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Join by Round 2 (late joiners eat Burning Rampage) and keep the team small. Her Fortify aura is -60% incoming solo and grows +20% per extra wizard up to -120%, so more wizards means she takes far less.
The aura cannot be removed, so bring hard-hitting Ice or Life spells (she takes +5% from both, resists Fire and Death 70%) to punch through the reduction.
Before you clear the minions, load Feint and universal traps on her. The turn after the last minion dies she Frenzies and Supernovas herself, and universal traps amplify that self-damage.
Do not Beguile her minions with 3 or fewer wizards; it counts the minion as an extra wizard and boosts her aura.
Expect Incindiate and an AoE Poison; heal through them and land your amplified hit.
cheatNotes: Joining after Round 2 gets you hit with Burning Rampage, so join early.
From Round 1 she has a permanent Fortify cutting her incoming damage by 60%, rising 20% per extra wizard to 120%; the more wizards, the tankier she is.
That aura cannot be stripped, so plan on out-damaging it rather than removing it.
She updates the aura when wizards join or flee, and Beguiling a minion with 3 or fewer wizards counts it as an extra wizard, worsening the aura.
The turn after the last minion dies she Frenzies and Supernovas herself, so pre-load traps to make her take big self-damage then.
Universal traps like Feint amplify that self-Supernova, so stack them on her before clearing minions.
She also throws Incindiate; nothing to do but expect the extra fire damage.

name: Doctor Jackall
cheats: "I invoke the power of Ice!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Doctor Jackall will interrupt-cast Balefrost.
"I must defend myself!" - If Doctor Jackall is damaged by a Spell, then he will interrupt-cast a -35% version of Legion Shield.
If Doctor Jackall is damaged more than once by a single Spell, then he will use this cheat for every time that he is damaged.
Ticks from Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
This cheat will not trigger if Doctor Jackall is damaged during the Round that his Polymorph ends.
"I'll take that!" - If Doctor Jackall uses his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then he will interrupt to use Steal Ward on the Wizard that he attacked.

"See my true face!" - At the end of the fourth Round, and at the end of every fourth Round after, Doctor Jackall will interrupt-cast a Spell that Polymorphs him into Mister Hound and applies a +35% Fire Damage Global.
"You dare strike me?" - If Mister Hound is damaged by a Spell, then he will interrupt-cast Fireblade on himself, then use his Natural Attack on the Wizard that attacked him.
If Mister Hound is damaged more than once by a single Spell, then he will use this cheat for every time that he is damaged.
If this cheat triggers while Mister Hound has no Pips, then his Natural Attack will act as though he has one Pip.
Although his Natural Attack is not cast when used through this cheat, it can still be Dispelled.
This cheat will trigger if Doctor Jackall is damaged during the Round that his Polymorph ends.
"I'll take that, thank you!" - If Mister Hound uses his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then he will interrupt to use Steal Charm on the Wizard that he attacked.
"The weakness returns!" - At the beginning of the eighth Round, and at the end of every fourth Round after, Doctor Jackall will interrupt-cast Balefrost.
"My rage boils over!" - If all of the Lanyon Scratchers are defeated, then Doctor Jackall or Mister Hound will interrupt-cast Lava Giant.
If there are no Lanyon Scratchers present at the beginning of the duel, then this cheat will trigger whenever the Wizard team casts their first Spell.
May-Cast Spells will trigger this cheat.
If Doctor Jackall or Mister Hound is Beguiled, then this cheat will trigger the first time that a Lanyon Scratcher casts a Spell.
This trigger can only occur once during the duel.
strategy: Hit with Fire. He takes +50% from it and resists Ice 80%.
Every direct hit triggers a -35% Legion Shield, and multi-hit spells stack several. Use single-hit Fire attacks or Fire DoTs, since DoT ticks do not trigger the shield.
Try to kill him before the end of Round 4. At that point he Polymorphs into Mister Hound, adds a +35% Fire global, and starts counter-attacking whoever damages him.
Keep shields and blades in reserve or protected. His Natural Attacks Steal Ward, and Steal Charm as Mister Hound.
He is stunable and beguilable, so a stun can buy a clean setup or hit round.
Clear the Lanyon Scratcher(s) only when you are ready to end it. Wiping them triggers a one-time Lava Giant on you.
cheatNotes: He opens Round 1 with Balefrost, so expect an Ice global on the board from the start.
Any spell that damages him makes him interrupt-cast a -35% Legion Shield on himself, softening your next hit.
A multi-hit spell triggers that Legion Shield once per hit, stacking several shields. Avoid multi-hit attacks.
Damage-over-time ticks do not trigger the shield, so Fire DoTs get around it.
He will not shield if you damage him on the exact Round his Polymorph ends.
When his Natural Attack hits you, he interrupt-casts Steal Ward to take one of your shields.
At the end of Round 4 (and every 4th after) he Polymorphs into Mister Hound and lays a +35% Fire Damage global.
As Mister Hound, damaging him makes him Fireblade himself and then Natural-Attack whoever hit him.
Multi-hit spells trigger that counter once per hit while he is Mister Hound too.
If Mister Hound has no pips when this fires, his counter Natural Attack still hits as a 1-pip attack.
That counter Natural Attack can be Dispelled even though it is cheat-cast.
Unlike his shield cheat, this Mister Hound counter does trigger on the Round his Polymorph ends.
When Mister Hound's Natural Attack hits you, he interrupt-casts Steal Charm to take one of your blades.
At the start of Round 8 (and every 4th after) he interrupt-casts Balefrost again.
Defeating every Lanyon Scratcher makes him or Mister Hound interrupt-cast Lava Giant on you.
If no Scratchers are present at the start, that Lava Giant instead fires when your team casts its first spell.
May-cast pet or wand spells count as that first spell for the Lava Giant trigger.
If you Beguile him, the Lava Giant instead fires the first time a Scratcher casts a spell.
The Lava Giant punish can only happen once during the whole duel.

name: Don Manzana
cheats: "Those spells are too rich for your blood" - If a Wizard casts a Damage Spell that costs 4 or more Pips on Don Manzana, even if it defeats him, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing that restores 100% of his max Health.

X Rank Damage Spells and Damage over Time effects that cost 4 or more Pips will also result in Don Manzana casting a version Triage on himself, removing all overtime effects, including protected overtimes.
"Ack, I can't handle such low-grade tactics!" - Casting a Damage Spell that costs 3 or less Pips on Don Manzana won't result in any consequences.
School pips do not count toward this total.
strategy: Attack with Death; his resist list covers every school except Death, so Death hits for full while everything else is halved.
Keep your finisher at 3 pips or fewer. Any damage spell of 4+ pips fully heals him with Blessing, and 4+ pip X-spells or DoTs also Triage off your effects.
School pips do not count toward the pip cost, so a Death wizard can power a cheap spell with school pips and stay under the limit.
Stack Death blades and a Feint, then deliver one 3-pip-or-less Death hit that ends him; if it is not enough, chip only with sub-4-pip spells.
cheatNotes: A damage spell costing 4 or more pips fully heals him even if it would kill him; keep your attacks at 3 pips or fewer.
X-pip spells and DoTs of 4+ pips also make him Triage off all your damage-over-time, including protected ones; avoid big-pip DoTs.
Spells costing 3 pips or less do no such thing, so a small enchanted hit is safe.
Only regular pips count toward the 4-pip line; school pips are free, letting a Death wizard fuel a cheap hit under the limit.

Keep the fight short. Every third Round he removes another 5% max Health and casts Mass Infection, which stacks against you over time.
He has only 5,000 Health and resists every school just 15%, so blade once and hit with your strongest school.
Clear the two Gator minions with an AoE so they do not add pressure while you burst the boss.

Every third Round he removes another 5% max Health and casts Mass Infection, so burst him down before this stacks.

name: Dr. Von Katzenstein (Rank 11)
cheats: "Tis a good time for my monster to come out to play!" - At the end of every third Round, Dr. Von Katzenstein will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster (1,005) on a random Wizard, which acts as his Natural Attack.
"Don't worry, there's time for everyone to play!" - At the start of every fourth Round, he will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard.

"It's not safe to run with Blades!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, he will cheat-cast Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
"One must keep a sanitary work environment!" - If a Wizard casts a negative Damage Charm, he will cast an AoE version of Cleanse Charm.
strategy: Storm boss (4300). Hit with Myth (+50%); never Storm (80% resist).
He hits with Dr. Von's Monster (~1005) at the end of every 3rd round and Monster Mash (~1170) at the start of every 4th round, so plan heals or absorbs for those windows.
Careful with blades: casting one makes him Enfeeble the wizard who received it, stripping a charm. Stack blades in one round then hit, or Aegis your key blade.
Do not bother with Weakness-type damage charms; he answers with an AoE Cleanse Charm.
He is low health, so set up 2-3 rounds and one Myth hit ends it.
cheatNotes: At the end of every 3rd round he hits a random wizard for ~1005 as his natural attack. Keep health up going into rounds 3, 6, and 9.
At the start of every 4th round he Monster Mashes a random wizard for ~1170. Expect it and heal ahead.
Casting a blade makes him Enfeeble its recipient, removing a charm. Stack your blades the same round you hit, or Aegis the important one.
Throw a Weakness or similar and he AoE-Cleanses it right back off. Do not rely on damage-reduction charms.

name: Dr. Von Katzenstein (Rank 14)

Every blade you cast gets hit with Enfeeble on the receiver, stripping it. Aegis your key blade, or blade and hit in the same round.
Plan around his timers: Dr. Von's Monster (~1,005) hits a random wizard every 3rd round and Monster Mash (~1,170) every 4th round. Shield or heal on those rounds.
Traps and Feint are safe from the Enfeeble cheat, so lean on traps for setup instead of blades.
Build a Feint plus one Aegis'd blade, then land a big Myth or Life hit.

cheats: Drake Lockley

Secondary effects will trigger this cheat.
Quentin Quail
"Let me see. Oh, yes! That's the ticket!" - If a Wizard casts one of the following hanging effects, Quentin will cheat-cast one of the following Spells:

[Enemy][Jinx] Single Target Harmful Ward - Tower Shield (Balance)

[All Enemies (Squared)][Jinx] Harmful Ward on All Enemies - Legion Shield
strategy: Understand the two punishers. Drake zaps any damage/pierce Charm, Trap, Aura, or Global for 500 Storm, and his partner Quentin counter-casts based on the type of hanging effect you play.

Use single-target buffs on your hitter, not multi-target. Drake's Duck Savage lands on the receiver of a single-target buff but redirects to the caster on multi-target spells, so avoid Bladestorm-style setup.
Turn Quentin's counters into free setup: cast a single-target harmful Charm on Drake and Quentin answers with a Balanceblade, and a harmful Ward gets you a Tower Shield. Bait the ones that help you.
Avoid the punishing answers: helpful Charms on your team draw Weakness, helpful Wards draw Hex, and all-enemy curses draw Bladestorm or Legion Shield for the bosses.

He is Storm with heavy resist across the board (36% most schools, 56% Fire, 16% Ice) and no boost school, plus 194% damage, so this is a team burst: minimize triggers, stack on one hitter, and swing once.
Since almost every buff triggers something, keep setup lean, use protected/Aegis blades where you must, and end it fast before the 500 Storm zaps and forced shields add up.
cheatNotes: Any damage or pierce Charm, Trap, Aura, or Global you cast makes Drake zap the target for 500 Storm damage with a Storm Duck Savage.
Multi-target buffs redirect that Duck Savage onto you, the caster, so single-target your hitter's blades to keep the hit off yourself.

Attenuate also counts as the harmful-Ward trigger.

name: Dream Belloq
cheats: "One of me is not enough!" - Dream Belloq will cast a Shadow Spell to summon one of the following Minions per Wizard in the dueling circle, if there is an open slot.
Belloq Image (Fire), Belloq Image (Ice), Belloq Image (Myth), Belloq Image (Storm)
”A new arrival? The more the merrier!” - If a Wizard is late joining the duel, Dream Belloq will cast Ra on the Wizard, dealing 33% of the Wizard’s maximum Health, and summon a new Minion.
Dream Belloq will summon one Minion once every six to ten Rounds, if there is an open slot.

"Behold, the power of - Oh no!" If a Minion is defeated, Belloq will cast Ra on himself and take Damage for every Minion defeated. Damage varies depending on the number of Wizards in the dueling circle. Example: If there is one Wizard, Dream Belloq will take 300,000 Damage. If there are two Wizards, Dream Belloq will take 150,000 Damage per Minion. If there are more than two Wizards, Dream Belloq will take 99,000 Damage per Minion.
*POSSIBLE BUG* Dream Belloq will respawn and rejoin the duel if a Wizard joins the dueling circle after Dream Belloq has been defeated. "Needs verification."
strategy: Ignore his 300,000 health completely — you win through his minions, not by damaging him (he resists Balance and Shadow 70% with almost no boost school).
On round 1 he summons one Belloq Image minion per wizard; that minion is your real target.
When a minion dies, Belloq takes huge self-damage: 300,000 with one wizard present, 150,000 each with two, 99,000 each with three-plus — so fewer wizards is far stronger here.
Solo or duo, stack blades, traps, and a Feint on a single Belloq Image and kill it: one minion death is his entire health bar and ends the fight.
Enter on time — a late wizard eats a Ra for 33% of their max health plus an extra summon.
cheatNotes: He summons one Belloq Image minion per wizard at the start — these minions are your actual target, not Belloq.
Join late and he Ra's you for 33% of your max health and adds a minion. Enter at the start.
He tops up minions every 6-10 rounds if a slot is open, so there is always a target to kill.

The core exploit: when a minion dies, Belloq takes massive self-damage — 300,000 with one wizard, 150,000 each with two, 99,000 each with three-plus. Solo or duo, a single minion kill ends the fight; just blade up and kill one Image.

name: Dream Freddie

stats.resist: 24% to all schools54% to all schools

cheats: Confusion
At the beginning of the first Round, Confusion will cast Saga of Heroes and will recast this spell when replaced.
Beguile Cheats - While Dream Freddie is alive, Confusion will cast Spells that benefit whichever side has Beguiled it, while it has an Aura up. This prevents attacks on allies and heals on enemies.
[Enemy] - If Dream Freddie casts Beguile (Sun) on Confusion, it will cast a "Freddie's Influence" Empowerment Aura on itself.
[Friend] - If a Wizard casts Beguile on Confusion, Confusion will cast a "Wizard's Influence" Infallible Aura on itself.
Spells with Beguile as a Secondary Effect, such as Crow, will trigger this cheat.
Confusion will target and heal either side when not Beguiled.
If Dream Freddie is defeated, Confusion will cast a Star version of Supernova, removing its Aura and dealing no Damage to itself, ending it's Beguile cheats.
Dream Freddie
"Well, when you say it like that..." - At the beginning of every fourth Round, Dream Freddie casts Beguile (Sun) on Confusion.
"I'm a BAD guy!" - If Dream Freddie is attacked, he will cast a Tower Shield (Sun -90%) on himself.
Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward can be used to remove this Shield.
Damage over Times will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Hit with Myth (+30%); never Storm (54% resist), and everything else is 24% resist.
Beguile the Confusion minion to flip it to your side — a wizard Beguile gives it an Infallible aura and it works for you. Freddie re-Beguiles it every 4th round, so refresh control.
When you attack Freddie he throws up a -90% Sun Tower Shield; strip it with Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward before your real hit, or chip with a DoT (DoTs don't trigger the shield).
Blade and trap up, remove the shield, then land a Myth hit.
Killing Freddie ends Confusion's beguile cheats — it Supernovas its aura off.
cheatNotes: Confusion casts Saga of Heroes round 1 and recasts it if replaced.
Whoever Beguiles Confusion gets its spells — controlled by the boss it blocks your hits and heals him, controlled by you it helps you.
If Freddie Beguiles it, it gives itself an Empowerment aura for his side.
If you Beguile it, it gives itself an Infallible aura and works for you — do this.
Spells that Beguile as a side effect (like Crow) also flip it.
When not Beguiled it just heals whichever side it likes — take control.
Killing Freddie makes Confusion Supernova its aura off and end its beguile cheats.
Every 4th round Freddie Beguiles Confusion back to his side — re-Beguile to keep it.
Attack Freddie and he shields himself with a -90% Sun Tower Shield.
Strip that shield with Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward before your main hit.
DoTs don't trigger the shield, so they chip him freely.

name: Drowned Dan (Tier 1)
locations: Celestia: Science Center - Drowned Dan's Chamber
cheats: [Aura] "Ye must sink the sams to learn me code" - Drowned Dan will cast an Invulnerability -300% Brace Aura making him Immune to Damage.

[Round] "The code is #-#-#-#" - At the start of the second Round, Drowned Dan will reveal the first number of the code. Each time a Minion is defeated, the next number of the code is revealed at the start of the following Round. The code represents four different Spell pip costs that Drowned Dan must be targeted with in order to deactivate the Invulnerability Aura.
List of Codes:
Drowned Dan will use one of the following nine codes:
Correct Responses:
"Blast! Ye be correct!" - is his response if answered correctly the first time.
"Yar! A lucky guess!" - is his response if answered correctly the second time.
"Blimey! They've figured it out!" - is his response if answered correctly the third time.
"Nooo! My protection!"' - is his response if answered correctly the fourth time, and casts a version of Supernova on himself that deals no Damage.
Incorrect Responses:
"Incorrect! Back to the start with ye!" - is his response if answered incorrectly, starting over the original sequence.
strategy: Drowned Dan is immune to damage under an Invulnerability aura. You cannot hurt him until you solve his four-number code.
The code is four pip costs. The first number is revealed at Round 2; each time you defeat his Sunken Sam minion the next number appears the following round.
To unlock him, target HIM with spells whose pip cost matches each code number in order (for example, code 1-5-2-7 means hit him with a 1-pip, then 5-pip, then 2-pip, then 7-pip spell).
Kill the minion, read the next digit, feed the matching pip-cost spell. A wrong answer resets the whole sequence, so go carefully.
Once all four are correct the aura drops. Then hit with Death (+30%) or any non-Life school to finish him off.
cheatNotes: He wears an Invulnerability aura making him immune to all damage until you crack his code. Do not waste hits before it is down.

The code is four pip costs revealed one at a time: the first at Round 2, each next digit after you defeat a Sunken Sam. Cast spells at him whose pip cost matches each number in order to strip the aura.
A wrong pip-cost answer resets the sequence to the start. Track the revealed digits carefully before responding.

name: Dumoozid
cheats: [Late] "More mess?? I'll have none of that!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Dumoozid will cast Lord of Winter at the Wizard.
[Storm][Manipulation] "Behold the power of pure clean!" - Dumoozid will randomly interrupt-cast a Storm version of Empower that gives him 1 Pip.
[Damage] "Let's clean this up!" - Dumoozid will randomly interrupt-cast a version of Tempest which removes all Charms and Wards from all Wizards.
Aegis can be used to prevent the removal of the Charm
[Storm][Manipulation] "So much mess, I can't take it!" - Dumoozid will randomly interrupt-cast a Storm version of Mana Burn
strategy: Enter on Round 1. Late arrivals eat Lord of Winter.
He is stunable and beguilable. A stun or beguile buys a clean setup or hit Round.
Keep setup short. He randomly Tempests off all your charms and wards and Mana Burns your pips.
Aegis your main blade so his Tempest cannot strip it, then hit Fire or Myth, since he takes +15% from each.
Prism or avoid Ice and Storm. He resists both 30%.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets you hit with Lord of Winter. Come in on Round 1.
He randomly interrupts with a Storm Empower to gain a pip, so he can out-pip you occasionally.
He randomly interrupts with a Tempest that strips every charm and ward off all Wizards, so your blades can vanish before you hit.
Put Aegis on your key blade so the Tempest cannot remove it.
He also randomly Mana Burns you to dump your pips. Do not over-bank pips; hit before he can.

name: Duncan Grimwater

cheats: "Here's how the Cabal fights!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Duncan Grimwater will cast a -100% version of Doom and Gloom.

"Ha! I knew you'd try that!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will place a -55% Infection on the Wizard.

Race him down before he banks two Shadow Pips. At that point he casts Dark Fiend with a +40% Shadowblade for a big hit.
Stack blades over two or three rounds and deliver one large hit. With healing off the table, a fast kill is the goal.

Casting any Heal gets you a -55% Infection, further crushing healing. Rely on shields and absorbs instead.
Even pet may-cast heals trigger the Infection, so consider swapping to a non-healing pet.
Once he banks two Shadow Pips he casts Dark Fiend with +20% Backlash and gives himself a +40% Shadowblade. Kill him or shield up before it lands.
