strategy: This is a 19,600-health Shadow boss - bring a team with one hitter and support. Everyone joins from the start to avoid Mana Burn.
Hit with a school she does not resist; she only resists 35% Myth and takes +10% Storm. Storm is a safe primary damage school here.

Do not stack normal traps on her. Each trap gives her a stacking +10% Amplify (shown as Berserk, 99 rounds) that keeps growing her damage. Use Mass Feint (+75%) instead - it does not trigger the Amplify.
Be careful with damage blades and globals: they curse the caster and weaken the wizard who received the blade. Multi-target blades like Bladestorm dump both the Curse and Weakness on the caster.

Never try to remove her Amplify aura - each removal attempt jumps it to +50%, then +100%. Leave it alone, set up with Mass Feint, and burst her before her every-5-round 500 Death AoE grinds the team down.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins late gets hit with Mana Burn (Shadow), draining pips. Enter from the start.
Every fifth round she casts a Shadow Diablo Qarin dealing 500 Death damage to all wizards.
She casts that 500 Death AoE once per wizard present, up to four times, so it hits harder in a full group.
Casting a damage-type blade or a global places a protected Curse on the caster and a protected Weakness on the wizard who received the blade.
Secondary blade or global effects instead give her the Weakness and hand the caster a Shadow Trap.
Multi-target blades like Bladestorm put both the Weakness and the Curse on the caster.
Placing a harmful trap on her gives her a +10% Amplify (99 rounds) that grows another 10% with each additional trap. Avoid normal traps.
Mass Feint (+75%) is the exception - it does not trigger the Amplify, so use it for your setup.
Trying to remove her Amplify aura backfires: the first attempt makes it +50%, further attempts make it +100%. Do not touch it.

name: Eirikur Axebreaker (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 1)
cheats: Receives a Frozen Armor on self on 1st Round that absorbs 200 Damage.
Receives a second Frozen Armor when two of his Minions are defeated that absorbs 400 Damage.
Receives a third Frozen Armor when his final Minion is defeated that absorbs 800 Damage.
Defeating all of his Minions or Beguiling him will make him add the last two Absorbs.
If a Wizard uses Steal Ward on the first Absorb, he will then cheat-cast Brimstone Revenant, breaking the Frozen Armor and giving 240 not absorbed Fire Damage.
The Brimstone Revenant will not leave behind a Fire Trap.
Pierce and Shatter can also remove the Frozen Armor.
strategy: Fire boss (3200) that is stunable and beguilable. Hit with Balance or Life (+20%); avoid Ice (50%), Fire (30%), and Death (20% resist).

He wears a Frozen Armor absorb (200) from round 1, gaining a 400 absorb when two minions die and an 800 absorb when the last minion dies. Use Pierce or Shatter to strip these rather than wasting hits into them.
Do not Steal Ward his first absorb; that makes him Brimstone Revenant for 240 unblocked Fire damage. Pierce or Shatter instead.
Beguiling him or clearing all his minions stacks the last two absorbs at once, so time your break for after they are all up if you want one clean strip.
Stack blades, strip the armor, and burst him down.
cheatNotes: He starts with a 200-damage Frozen Armor. Chip or pierce it before your real hit or the shot is wasted.
Killing two of his minions gives him a 400 absorb. Factor it in before committing a big hit.
Dropping his last minion grants an 800 absorb, his biggest shield. Plan a Shatter for this.
Clearing all minions or beguiling him adds both remaining absorbs at once, so you can break them in a single Shatter.
Do NOT Steal Ward the first absorb; he counters with Brimstone Revenant for 240 unblockable Fire damage. Use Pierce or Shatter.
Pierce and Shatter cleanly remove the Frozen Armor with no counter. These are your tools for every absorb.

name: Eirikur Axebreaker (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 2)
cheats: On the first Round, receives a Frozen Armor on self that absorbs 400 Damage.
When two of his Minions are defeated, receives a second Frozen Armor that absorbs 800 Damage.
When his final Minion is defeated, receives a third Frozen Armor that absorbs 1,600 Damage.
Defeating all of his Minions, or Beguiling him, will cause him to add the last two Absorbs.
If a Wizard uses Steal Ward, Eirikur will cast Brimstone Revenant dealing 500 Fire Damage, without the Fire Trap, and then cast Steal Ward to take back the Absorb.
strategy: Attack with Life or Balance (+15%); he resists Fire, Ice, and Death.
He stacks Frozen Armor absorbs: 400 on round 1, +800 when two minions die, +1,600 when the last minion dies. Killing his minions makes him tankier, so do not clear them early.
Beguiling him or wiping all minions at once immediately dumps the full 800 plus 1,600 absorb on him, so save that for after your setup.
Do not use Steal Ward on his absorb: he counters with Brimstone Revenant (500 Fire) and steals it right back. Use Pierce or just out-damage the absorb.
Stack blades and a Feint, leave the minion alive, then land one hit big enough to eat the 400 absorb and kill him.
cheatNotes: He starts round 1 with a 400-absorb Frozen Armor, so your first hit must clear that plus his health.
Killing two of his minions adds an 800 absorb, a reason to leave minions up until your kill shot.
Downing his final minion adds a 1,600 absorb on top; clearing minions makes him much harder to kill.
Beguiling him or wiping all minions at once immediately loads both the 800 and 1,600 absorbs onto him.
Steal Ward on his absorb backfires: he hits you with Brimstone Revenant (500) and steals the ward back. Use Pierce or raw damage instead.

name: Eirikur Axebreaker (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 3)
cheats: On the first Round, receives a Frozen Armor on self that absorbs 800 Damage.
When two of his Minions are defeated, receives a second Frozen Armor that absorbs 1,600 Damage.
When his final Minion is defeated, receives a third Frozen Armor that absorbe 3,200 Damage.
Defeating all of his Minions, or Beguiling him, will cause him to add the last two Absorbs.
spellNotes: Natural Attack (Eirikur Ice) applies a universal Pierce to all allies.
strategy: Attack with Storm or Life (his listed boost schools); he resists Fire, Ice, and Death.
Do NOT clear his minions or beguile him — killing minions and beguiling both stack extra Frozen Armor absorbs on him, making him far tankier.
He opens with only one 800-damage absorb, so focus Eirikur directly through that single shield while leaving the Thundersnow Trolls alive.
Stack blades, traps, and a Feint, then hit for well over 800 to punch through the opening absorb and into his health.
Watch his natural attack, which applies a universal pierce to his side; keep your own resist in mind.
cheatNotes: He opens with a Frozen Armor absorbing 800 damage — your first hit needs ~800 of overkill just to start denting his health.
Kill two of his minions and he gains a second absorb (1,600). Leave minions up if you can and focus him directly.
Killing his last minion hands him a third absorb (3,200). Clearing the minions makes him tankier, not easier.
Beguiling him also triggers those last two absorbs (4,800 total) — don't beguile this boss; just burst him through the single opening absorb.

name: Eirikur Axebreaker (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 4)
cheats: [Absorb] Starts the first Round with Frozen Armor.
When all of his Minions are defeated, Eirikur Axebreaker (Tier 4) casts two Frozen Armors on himself.
[Manipulation] If a Wizard uses Steal Ward, he will then cheat-cast Brimstone Revenant, that deals 100 Fire Damage without the Trap, and take back his Frozen Armor.
Pierce and Shatter can overcome this cheat.
spellNotes: Natural Attack (Eirikur Ice) is 6 Pips maximum.
strategy: Team of 4: a hitter plus supports. Hit with Life or Balance (he takes +35% from both) and never into Ice (80% resist).
He opens with a Frozen Armor Absorb. Break it with Shatter or armor-piercing, not Steal Ward.
Do not Steal Ward his Frozen Armor. It makes him hit the caster with Brimstone Revenant for 100 Fire and take the Absorb back. Pierce and Shatter get around it.
Leave the Thundersnow Troll alive until your burst is ready. The moment all his minions die, he gives himself two fresh Frozen Armors.
He is stunable, so a stun can buy a setup round. Stack blades, Shatter the Absorb, then land one Life or Balance hit.
cheatNotes: He begins with a Frozen Armor Absorb shielding his health. Shatter or pierce it.
Killing all his minions makes him cast two more Frozen Armors on himself. Only clear the Troll when you are ready to kill him.
Steal Ward on his Frozen Armor backfires: he casts Brimstone Revenant on the caster for 100 Fire and takes the Absorb back.
Pierce and Shatter remove the Frozen Armor without triggering the Steal Ward punish.

name: Eirikur Axebreaker (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 5)
cheats: [Absorb] Receives a Frozen Armor on self on the first Round that absorbs 1,500 Damage.
Receives a second Frozen Armor when two of his Minions are defeated that absorbs 3,000 Damage.
Receives a third Frozen Armor when his final Minion is defeated that absorbs 6,000 Damage.
Bug: Frozen Armor doesn't visually display on the Boss.
Beguile may trigger this final Frozen Armor.
[Manipulation] Casts Steal Ward any time that a Wizard casts a Ward Spell. This also triggers a version of Brimstone Revenant that deals 1,500 Damage.
strategy: Team gauntlet (24k with escalating absorbs). Hit with Life or Balance (+40%); avoid Ice (80%), Fire (70%), and Death (60%).
Don't cast ward/shield spells — every ward makes him Steal Ward and counter with Brimstone Revenant for 1,500. Survive on resist and heals instead.
He gets a 1,500 Frozen Armor on round 1, a 3,000 absorb after two minions die, and a 6,000 absorb when the last minion dies — plan a hit big enough to break the current absorb.
Time the last minion's death and your burst so you punch through the 6,000 absorb in one hit rather than feeding it chip damage.
Funnel blades and Feints to one hitter and overshoot the absorb.
cheatNotes: He shields himself with a 1,500-absorb Frozen Armor on round 1 — overshoot it.
When two of his minions die he gains a 3,000 absorb.
When his last minion dies he gains a 6,000 absorb — line up a hit big enough to break it.
Beguiling can trigger the final 6,000 absorb early.
Any ward/shield you cast makes him Steal Ward and hit back with Brimstone Revenant for 1,500 — skip shields.

name: Elana Darksun
locations: Khrysalis: Kondha Desert - Chamber of the Sun's Shadow
cheats: "Come, join me!" - At the end of the first Round, Elana will summon a Deer Mouse Revenant.
spellNotes: Uses a version of Shadow Seraph that gives +15 Backlash.
Uses a Natural Attack that does not affect Shadow Creature Backlash.
strategy: High-health Khrysalis fight (18,365) with a Wraith Knight and Deer Mouse Revenant already out. Bring a team with a hitter, supports, and a healer.
At the end of Round 1 she summons another Deer Mouse Revenant, so expect the board to fill. Decide whether to AoE the minions down or funnel one big hit through them onto her.
She is not stunnable. Her Shadow Seraph gives +15 Backlash, so shadow-form hitters take extra backlash. Have non-shadow damage ready or heal the backlash.
Supports blade the hitter and Feint Elana; the healer covers the Deer Mouse and Wraith Knight hits.
Set up 2-3 rounds, then the hitter lands an AoE so the minions die alongside her.
cheatNotes: Her only listed cheat is summoning a Deer Mouse Revenant at the end of Round 1. Plan for one extra minion and either AoE it down or push a single hit through to her.

name: Elephant Spectral Guardian
cheats: At the beginning of the battle, the Elephant Spectral Guardian will cast a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself. He replaces this Tower Shield every time he is attacked, until all of the other Spectral Guardians are defeated.
Pierce, Steal Ward and Shatter can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield. (He will still replace it when attacked.)

"Only charms can save you now!" - The Elephant Spectral Guardian will mark a random Wizard for 2 Rounds. During that time, if a charm is not cast upon the marked Wizard, then the other Spectral Guardians will cheat-cast the following spells at the marked Wizard:
"Are you still marked?" - After these spells are cast or a charm is cast, the Elephant Spectral Guardian will remove the mark and all wards (including traps) from the marked Wizard.
If a charm is cast on the marked Wizard, this cheat will not activate.
Pet spells will not affect this cheat. Pet Charms will not remove the mark.
Stun shields can be removed by Shatter.
"We can't fail!" - If all the other Spectral Guardians have been defeated, the Elephant Spectral Guardian will remove all wards from himself (including his Tower Shield).
"Your pursuit ends here!" - After all the other Spectral Guardians have been defeated, he will cast Ominous Scarecrow (Death) every three Rounds.
strategy: Ignore the Elephant at first. His -90% Tower Shield refreshes whenever he is hit and cannot be removed by damage while the other Guardians live.
Kill the Gorilla, Lion, and Zebra Guardians first. This is a kill-order fight.
When a wizard gets marked with 'Only charms can save you now!', cast any charm on that wizard within 2 rounds to cancel the punishment.
If you must hit the Elephant early, strip his Tower Shield with Pierce, Steal Ward, or Shatter first.
Once all three other Guardians are down, the Elephant strips his own shields. Pour blades and traps and hit him then.
After that he casts Ominous Scarecrow (Death AoE) every 3 rounds. Shield and finish the 7390 health fast.
cheatNotes: He wears a -90% Tower Shield he re-applies every time he is hit, and it will not come off through damage while the other Guardians live.
Pierce, Steal Ward, or Shatter safely remove that Tower Shield, though he re-shields when attacked again.
He marks a random wizard for 2 rounds. If no charm is placed on that wizard, the other Guardians blast them.
Once the punishment fires or a charm is cast, he strips the mark and all wards and traps off that wizard, so do not stack traps on a marked wizard.
Casting any charm on the marked wizard cancels the whole punishment. Keep a spare blade handy.
Pet spells do not count; a pet charm will not clear the mark, so cast it yourself.
His stun shields can be removed with Shatter.
When the other three Guardians are dead he strips all wards off himself, including his Tower Shield. That is your window to hit unshielded.
After the minions die he casts Ominous Scarecrow (Death) every third round. Shield for it and burst him down.

name: Empress of Flames

stats.resist: 36% to all schools66% to all schools

cheats: [Damage over Time] "My rage burns hotter than any sun!"- At the beginning of the first Round, and every six Rounds after, she casts a Damage Over Time dealing 12,610 Damage over five Rounds to all enemies.
If the Damage Over Time is removed or pushed away from all Wizards, the Cheat cycle will reset two turns later, replacing the Damage Over Time.
strategy: Expect a Fire DoT for 12,610 over 5 rounds on the whole team at round 1 and every 6 rounds. Keep a healer rolling HoTs and heal through it.
Do not chase removing the DoT: pushing or cleansing it off all wizards just resets the cycle to replace it 2 turns later, so heal rather than strip.
Hit her with Ice, which she takes +30% from; she resists Fire 66%.
Race the 6-round timer. Blade and Feint fast and land a big Ice hit before the second DoT wave stacks on the first.
Team: healer sustains against the DoT, supports blade the hitter, hitter bursts within the first cycle.
cheatNotes: At round 1 and every 6 rounds after, she lays a Fire DoT dealing 12,610 over 5 rounds on all wizards.
Removing or pushing the DoT off every wizard just resets the cycle, and she replaces the DoT two turns later.

name: Endurzaga
cheats: "You have served your purpose!" - At the end of a Round, Endurzaga will randomly cast Draw Power on a Dunes Djinn, or a random Wizard when there are no Dunes Djinns left.
"Pet! Destroy that!" - If a Wizard casts a positive ward (i.e. Shields), his Pet Cave Chameleon will cast Pierce at the Wizard that received the ward.
"Pet! Shift that back!" - If a Wizard hits Endurzaga while a Damage over Time is on him, his Pet, Cave Chameleon, will cast Shift, and transfer the remaining ticks to the Wizard.
spellNotes: Endurzaga's Pet Cave Chameleon can cast Hydra.
strategy: Hit with Storm, he takes +15%, and avoid Myth (30% resist).
Skip shields for defense, casting a positive ward makes his Cave Chameleon Pierce it off you. Use resist gear and heals instead.
Do not DoT him. If you hit him while a DoT is on him, his pet Shifts the remaining ticks back onto you, so use burst damage.
He drains pips via Draw Power each round (his Djinns first, then you when they are gone), so do not sit on a huge pip pool, spend it.
Stack blades and a Feint, then land one direct Storm hit, and watch for the pet's Hydra.
cheatNotes: Each round he Draws Power from a Dunes Djinn, or from a random wizard once the Djinns are gone, do not hoard pips.
Cast a shield or any positive ward and his Cave Chameleon Pierces it off you. Rely on resist and heals, not wards.
Hit him while a DoT is on him and his pet Shifts the remaining ticks onto you, use burst, not DoTs.

name: Enki the Heretic
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Tomb of Light
cheats: [Damage][Late] "Feel the bite of Raven!" - After Enki attacks, or if a Wizard joins the duel late, Enki will cheat-cast Scald.
[Round] Cycle:
"To face the wolf, bare the claws." - At the beginning of every second Round, Enki the Heretic will cheat-cast a +25% Fireblade or +25% Deathblade on each Wizard.
"The Light burns!" - At the beginning of the every fifth Round, Enki will cheat-cast Incindiate.
"The Light blesses you." - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm, Enki will cheat-cast an all enemies version of Sprite, restoring 500 Health over 4 Rounds on each Wizard.
strategy: Do not join late - a latecomer eats a cheat Scald (Fire DoT). Enter on time and pack Triage.
Kill the Fallen Luphilim minion early or AoE both together; it hits Ice-Myth.
Hit with Ice - he takes 30% more from Ice and resists Fire 55%. Prism if you are a Fire wizard.
He hands each wizard a +25% Fireblade or Deathblade every 2nd round; only useful if you hit Fire or Death, otherwise ignore it and use your own Ice blades.
Expect a cheat Scald after he attacks and Incindiate every 5th round (both boost his Fire hits) - keep Fire shields up.
Buff freely: casting a positive charm just makes him heal YOU 500 over 4 rounds, which is harmless.
cheatNotes: After Enki attacks, or if you join late, he cheat-casts Scald (a Fire damage-over-time) on you. Enter on time and pack Triage.
Every 2nd round he gives each wizard a +25% Fireblade or Deathblade. Useful only if you hit Fire or Death; otherwise ignore it.
Every 5th round he casts Incindiate, boosting his own Fire damage. Keep Fire shields up around those rounds.
Casting a positive charm makes him cast an all-wizards Sprite that heals YOU 500 over 4 rounds - harmless, so buff freely.

name: Entrance Exam (Standard)

stats.resist: 42% to all schools72% to all schools

cheats: [Round] At the beginning of the first Round, Entrance Exam will cast one of the following on itself:
[Shield] Seven -80% Damage Wards, one for each School including Balance.
[Heal over Time] Four Heal over Time effects that each restore 52 Health over 50 Rounds.

"You missed the lesson. We must drive home its effect!" - If Entrance Exam is defeated while any of these hanging effects still exist, it will revive itself with half Health, reapply the effects, and give each Wizard the corresponding Spell that can be used to remove the effects.
[Shatter (Battle Card)][Incapacitate (Death)]

[Charm][Trap] "A conventional technique. We are prepared." - Each time a Wizard casts a Damage Charm on themself or a Damage Ward on Entrance Exam, each Wizard will take Damage equal to 20% of their max Health.
"*Grim Laughter*" - This will become the new line of the previous cheat if triggered more than 2 times.
strategy: Read its Round 1 opener. It casts one of two things on itself, and both must be cleared before it dies, or it revives.
If it opens with seven -80% damage shields (one per school), strip them with the Shatter card it hands you before you commit your hit.
Keep setup minimal. Every damage charm you put on yourself and every damage trap you put on it costs you 20% of your max Health, so use a couple of high-value buffs, not a full stack.
Attack with Life (it takes +30% Life); never Death (72% resist) and expect 42% resist on the other schools.
Clear the opening hanging effects (Shatter the shields or remove the heal-over-times with the given cards), then hit through to the kill so it cannot revive.
At 35,000 Health and not stunable, run this as a team: one hitter, supports keeping buffs light, and the removal cards ready before the finisher.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 it casts one of two openers on itself (see the next two lines), and both must be cleared before it dies.
One opener is seven -80% damage shields, one for every school including Balance, so any single hit is heavily blocked. Shatter them off first.
The other opener is four heal-over-times restoring 52 Health each over 50 Rounds. Minor, but they still count as the hanging effect that triggers its revive.
If you defeat it while either opener still hangs, it revives at half Health, reapplies the effects, and hands everyone a spell to remove them, so remove them before the kill.
Every damage charm you put on yourself or damage trap you put on it costs you 20% of your max Health, so blade and Feint sparingly.
After it triggers that self-damage cheat more than twice it just changes its taunt line; the effect is the same.

name: Ervin the Barbarian
locations: Wysteria: Pegasus Place - Tower of the Helephant
cheats: Ervin the Barbarian
At the start of the battle, Ervin cheat-casts a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself. If removed by Damage, he cheat-casts it again as long as at least one Minion is alive.
Spells such as Steal Ward or Pierce can be used to safely remove the shield, but he will still replace it when he takes Damage.
Ervin will still cast the shield on the first Round even if all of the Minions are missing due to being defeated in a prior attempt.
"Hah! Your miss leaves you open!" - Whenever a Wizard fizzles, Ervin cheat-casts a 0-Pip Skeletal Dragon on the Wizard who fizzled, dealing 800 + 1,998 Death Damage over 3 Rounds.

"I needed those thieves!" - After all of the Minions are defeated and repeating every second round, Ervin cheat-casts a 0-Pip Leviathan at the start of the round that deals 2,030 Storm Damage to the highest threat Wizard.
Although this cheat is not cast, it may still be dispelled with Dissipate.
If all of the Minions were defeated in a prior attempt, this cheat will first trigger on the third Round (although the line is still spoken without any associated spellcast on the first Round).
If a Trap is placed on Ervin while any of the Minions are alive, then he will cast Cleanse Ward on himself.
Vaporize can be used to dispel this cheat.
For Elemental Trap and Spirit Trap, only the last Trap of the three (Storm, Life) is removed.
Multi-Trap Spells (such as Windstorm or Mass Death Prism) do not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
Taurus the Clever
At the start of every third round, Taurus cheat-casts Smoke Screen (-50%), making it easier for Ervin to use Skeletal Dragon.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.

"This would look better on Ervin." - If a Wizard casts any Charm, Taurus will cheat-cast Disarm on the recipient and give a Deathblade to Ervin the Barbarian. If Ervin the Barbarian is defeated, Taurus will still cast Disarm without casting Deathblade.
Dissipate can be used to prevent this cheat. If Disarm is dispelled, Taurus will not give Ervin a Deathblade.
For Elemental Blade and Spirit Blade, only the first Blade of the three (Fire, Life) is removed.
For multi-Charms like Bladestorm, only the caster's Charm is removed.
Spells such as Plague and Smoke Screen activate this cheat and Taurus will cast Disarm on the caster.
Aegis can be used to avoid this cheat.
Charms cast by Shadow Creatures such as Shadowblade will trigger this cheat.
Pet spells do not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Accuracy first. Any fizzle gets the fizzler a 0-pip Skeletal Dragon (800+1998 Death over 3 rounds), so enchant hits with accuracy and do not gamble low-accuracy spells.
Ervin keeps a -90% Tower Shield he recasts whenever damaged as long as a minion lives. Kill the minions first, or use Steal Ward or a pierce hit to get through.

Set up safely: single traps make him Cleanse Ward, so use Indemnity, Vaporize, or multi-trap spells like Windstorm (which do not trigger it). Any charm makes Taurus Disarm you and hand Ervin a Deathblade, so Aegis-protect blades or Dissipate the Disarm.
Quench Taurus's Smoke Screen (-50%) every third round; it sets up Ervin's Skeletal Dragon.
Clear the minions, then blade up and hit Ervin with Ice or Life (+35%, resists Death 80%). After minions die he throws a 0-pip Leviathan every other round, so do not stall.
cheatNotes: He wears a -90% Tower Shield and recasts it whenever damaged while a minion is alive, so kill the minions or you fight through the shield every hit.
Steal Ward or a pierce hit removes the shield without the damage-recast, though he still replaces it once he takes damage again.
Every fizzle earns the fizzler a 0-pip Skeletal Dragon for 800+1998 Death over 3 rounds, so stack accuracy and avoid low-accuracy hits.
Once the minions are gone he throws a 0-pip Leviathan (2030 Storm) at the highest-threat wizard every second round, so finish quickly.
That Leviathan can be dispelled with Dissipate even though it is a cheat-cast.
A single trap on Ervin makes him Cleanse Ward it off while any minion lives; use Indemnity or a multi-trap spell instead.
Vaporize dispels the Cleanse Ward cheat.
Multi-trap spells like Windstorm or Mass Death Prism do not trigger the Cleanse Ward, so use them to trap him safely.
Indemnity on your trap avoids the Cleanse Ward entirely.
Taurus drops a -50% Smoke Screen every third round to set up Ervin's hits; Quench it to keep your accuracy.
Quench removes Taurus's Smoke Screen.
Any charm you cast makes Taurus Disarm the recipient and hand Ervin a free Deathblade; Aegis your blades to protect them.
Dissipate stops this, and dispelling the Disarm also denies Ervin the Deathblade.
Even Plague or Smoke Screen count as charms here and get you Disarmed, so watch what you cast.
Aegis on the charm avoids Taurus's Disarm.
Shadow-creature charms like Shadowblade also trigger the Disarm.

name: Estrakir Gloomthorn
cheats: If Estrakir is attacked (or defeated) before Knuckles McCloud, then Knuckles will give him a Helping Hands out of turn. Estrakir will then cast Fire Dragon out of turn.
Meteor Strike will trigger this cheat if Estrakir casts this Spell.
Estrakir will sometimes cast Fire Dragon even when he is not hit.
Morganthe's Requiem does not trigger either cheat.
Estrakir will cast Cleanse Ward on himself or any minion of his that receives a Shield.
Only the first Trap of a Tri-Trap Spell will be removed.
All-Enemy Trap Spells, such as Windstorm or Mass Fire Prism, will not trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat, though he may randomly remove the Traps later on.
Secondary Spell effects will also be removed.(e.g. Brimstone Revenant, Keeper of the Flame).

"Power Spell!" - If a Global Spell is not active around the battlefield (even if the Global Spell is active(?)), Estrakir will cast a Fire version of Power Nova dealing 770 Fire Damage every three turns.
If a Wizard casts a Global Spell, Estrakir or a Wizard will shout out, "Interrupt Charge!".
"Power Spell!" - Sometime after his Minions have been defeated, Estrakir will start using a version of Scarecrow (600) every other turn.
On their first turn, the Rat Illusionists will shield Knuckles McCloud and Estrakir with -90% Tower Shields, and will replace these out of turn whenever they are broken.

Each of the Rat Illusionists will choose either Knuckles or Estrakir, and cast the Tower Shield on that Boss. If only one of the Rats is defeated, then the remaining Rat will cast a Shield on Knuckles before Estrakir.
Defeating Estrakir or Knuckles will end this cheat.
"Yeah, we can Heal Too!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Knuckles will cast a 750 Healing Rebirth (without Absorb Shields).
Pet heals will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Hit with Ice - he takes +50% from it and resists 80% of Fire; never attack into his Fire resist.
Handle the Rat Illusionists' -90% Tower Shields: they reshield Knuckles and Estrakir every turn until a boss dies, so either kill both Rats first or plan an AoE that clears everything in one round.
Set traps with all-enemy spells like Windstorm or Mass Fire Prism - he Cleanse Wards single traps and shields but cannot remove AoE traps.
Do not heal - Knuckles answers any heal with a 750 Rebirth; use absorbs or resist instead.
Clear Knuckles before landing a big single hit on Estrakir, or attacking Estrakir first gives him a free out-of-turn Fire Dragon.
Keep a Global on the field to stop his 770 Fire Power Nova, and burst fast once minions fall since he starts Scarecrow-draining every other turn.
cheatNotes: Attacking or defeating Estrakir while Knuckles McCloud is still up makes Knuckles hand him a Helping Hands and Estrakir fire a Fire Dragon out of turn. Clear Knuckles first, or AoE both at once.
His own Meteor Strike also sets off that out-of-turn Fire Dragon.
He may throw a Fire Dragon at you even on rounds you did not hit him, so keep some resist or a shield up.
Morganthe's Requiem is a safe hit - it does not set off the Helping Hands or Fire Dragon cheat.
He Cleanse Wards off any Shield placed on him or his minions, so single shields on him are wasted.
Only the first trap of a tri-trap spell gets removed, so the other two stick.
All-enemy trap spells like Windstorm or Mass Fire Prism are NOT cleansed - use these to keep traps on him.
Pet-cast spells do not trigger the cleanse, though he may still strip the traps on a later turn.
Linked secondary effects (Brimstone Revenant, Keeper of the Flame) get removed too.
If no global is on the field he casts a Fire Power Nova for 770 to the team every three turns, so keeping a global up suppresses it.
Once his minions are down he starts casting Scarecrow (600 drain) every other turn, so finish him quickly after.
The Rat Illusionists put -90% Tower Shields on Knuckles and Estrakir on turn one and instantly replace any that break.
Each Rat picks one boss to shield; if only one Rat is left it shields Knuckles before Estrakir.
Defeating Estrakir or Knuckles ends the Rat shielding loop.
If any wizard heals, Knuckles counter-casts a 750 Rebirth heal, so avoid healing spells.
Pet heals do not set off Knuckles' counter-heal.

name: Evandyr HalfEar
locations: Khrysalis: Kondha Desert - Dry Gulch Cave
cheats: [Minion] "Left Jab!" - At the end of Round 2, he shouts "Left Jab!" and summons Ali LeftJab (which isn't active until the next Round).
[Minion] "Right Hook!" - At the end of Round 4, he shouts "Right Hook!" and summons Rocky RightHook (which isn't active until the next Round ).
[Minion] "Upper Cut!" - At the end of Round 6, he will summon Tyson UpperCut (which isn't active until the next Round).
[Minion][Afterlife] If a Minion is defeated, he will resummon it (in the same order as above, if more than one is defeated).
strategy: This is an 18,060-health boss with three summoned boxers, so run it as a team: a hitter, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer. He resists Myth 80%, so hit with Storm or Death (+35%) and never Myth.
He summons a minion every two rounds: Ali LeftJab at the end of round 2, Rocky RightHook at the end of round 4, Tyson UpperCut at the end of round 6.
Do not waste turns killing the boxers one by one. Any minion you defeat is resummoned in the same order, so funnel damage into Evandyr instead.
Set up rounds 1 through 3, then burst Evandyr down before round 6 when all three boxers are out, using an AoE to clear whichever minions are up on the kill turn.
Expect Humongofrog AoEs from him, so keep the team topped up while you build blades.
cheatNotes: At the end of round 2 he summons Ali LeftJab, active the next round.
At the end of round 4 he summons Rocky RightHook, active the next round.
At the end of round 6 he summons Tyson UpperCut, active the next round.
Any boxer you defeat is resummoned, in the same order if several fall. Ignore the minions and focus the boss.

name: Exemplar Commander
locations: Empyrea: Nimbus Citadel - New School of Magic
cheats: If a Wizard joins late, Exemplar Commander casts Bad Juju (Shadow) on the Wizard.
At the start of combat, Exemplar Commander casts a Shadow Spell to summon Giles.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Deathblade (+40%), Stormblade (+30%), and Bladestorm.
strategy: Attack with Myth or Life (+5% each) and stay off Storm and Death, which he resists 70%.
He is stunable and beguilable; stun him or beguile Giles to control the fight.
He opens by summoning Giles, so bring AoE or plan to split damage.
He self-casts protected Weakness, protected Deathblade, Stormblade, Bladestorm, and a protected Mass Feint; use Cleanse Charm for the Weakness and expect the Mass Feint to amplify his hit.
His incoming healing is cut 45%, so lean on shields if you need to out-sustain him.
Blade up, Cleanse his Weakness off you, then burst him and Giles.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets Bad Juju cast on you; enter on time.
He summons Giles at the start, so be ready to handle two targets with AoE or focus fire.

name: Failed Ritual Oni
cheats: [Ward][Jinx] "My tactics are flawless!" - At the beginning of every Round, Failed Ritual Oni will interrupt-cast a -25% Shield on itself, and a +15% Trap on all Wizards.
[Healing] If Failed Ritual Oni is defeated while their Shield and Trap cheat is still active, they will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "I'm failure incarnate! So here's a card!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, or after it respawns, Failed Ritual Oni will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Balance] "No, I've made a mistake!" - When Locust Swarm (Tier 2b) is used, Failed Ritual Oni will remove all Wards from itself, remove all Traps from all Wizards, and stop its Shield and Trap cheat.

"I did not see that coming!" - Other Balance Spells that Echo Traps will cause Failed Ritual Oni to remove all Wards from itself, remove all Traps from all Wizards, and stop its Shield and Trap cheat.
strategy: Balance boss (4730). It takes +30% from Balance damage and only resists 4% off-school, so a Balance hit is actually your best option.
Every round it self-shields -25% and lays a +15% trap on every wizard. Those traps help your damage, but the shield state is what keeps it alive.
Do not kill it while its shield and trap cheat is still running, or it revives at full health.

The counter: cast Locust Swarm (Tier 2b), or another Balance echo-trap spell, to make it strip its own wards, remove all wizard traps, and permanently stop the cheat. It gives you the card on round 4.
With the cheat disabled, stack up and land your Balance hit to finish it for good.
cheatNotes: Each round it shields itself -25% and lays a +15% trap on every wizard. The traps actually help your damage, but the shield state is what keeps it alive.
Kill it while that shield and trap cheat is still active and it revives at full health. You must switch the cheat off first.
On round 4 (or after it respawns) it hands each wizard a card. This is the Balance echo-trap spell used to disable its revive.
Casting Locust Swarm (Tier 2b) makes it drop all wards, clear your traps, and permanently stop the shield and trap cheat. Do this before you try to kill it.
Any other Balance spell that Echoes traps has the same effect and disables the revive cheat. Then finish it off.

name: Fairy Queen (Rank 7)
cheats: "Fairies! Protect your Queen!" - At the start of the Round, this Boss will interrupt-cast a Life Spell which summons two Rank 7 Fairies of a random School.

"You'll never be as charming as Rattlebones!" - If a Blade or Global Spell is cast, then there will be a random chance of the Fairy Queen interrupt-casting Enfeeble on the Wizard who received the Blade or cast the Global Spell.
Using Bladestorm will prompt the Fairy Queen to remove the Blade from the caster only.
"Why don't we trade?" - When a Feint is placed on the Fairy Queen, then she will interrupt-cast Swap Ward which switches the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Melt can be used to avoid this cheat.
spellNotes: Casts a Satyr which restores 1,075 Health.
Casts a version of Sprite Trap which places a Death Trap on all Wizards, despite the Spell card's description.
strategy: She has no resists, so hit with your strongest school; Balance and Myth get a small +10%.
She summons two fairies at the start of the round, so she keeps adding bodies. Bring an AoE finisher to clear fairies and Queen together rather than grinding single-target.
Blades and Globals risk an Enfeeble on the receiver; Bladestorm only strips the caster's own blade, so blade from a safe slot or Aegis the hitter.
Do not Feint her normally: she Swap Wards it so the bigger trap ends up on you. Cast Feint with Melt available to keep the trap on her.
Race her Satyr heal (1,075): stack fast and hit before she stabilizes.
cheatNotes: She summons two random-school fairies at the start of the round, so expect a steady stream of adds. Plan an AoE kill.
Blades and Globals have a chance to be Enfeebled off the receiver or caster. Aegis your key blade.
Bladestorm only loses the caster's copy, so use it to blade someone else safely.
Feint her and she Swap Wards it so the larger trap lands on you. Use Melt to keep it on her.
Keep a Melt handy; it removes the swapped trap and undoes this cheat.

name: Fellspawn
locations: Wizard City: The Catacombs - Fathomless Tomb
cheats: If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Fellspawn casts Call of Khrulhu (Shadow).
At the start of the first Round, Fellspawn summons any 3 of the following Minions:
Balance Tentacular Horror
Death Tentacular Horror
Fire Tentacular Horror
Ice Tentacular Horror
Life Tentacular Horror
Myth Tentacular Horror
Shadow Tentacular Horror
Storm Tentacular Horror
At the end of the third Round, after a Minion is killed, a random school Minion is summoned. After this, another Minion will be summoned at the end of every fourth Round.

At the start of the first Round, Fellspawn casts an Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 9,999. Damage cannot go above this limit while Global is present. If the Global is replaced, it will be recast.
At the start of the fifth Round, Fellspawn summons an Unhelpful Spawn on the Wizard's team if all duel slots are not filled.
Sacrificial Spells, such as Sap Power, will not remove the Minion from the duel slot.
If the Wizard in the first duel slot flees, this summon will disappear.
Fellspawn will resummon the Minion soon after it is defeated.
At the end of every fifth Round, Fellspawn will cheat-cast a Black Mantle, Hex, and Weakness, each on a random Wizard, and cast Bladestorm and Mass Tower Shield (Ice).
Every six Rounds, Fellspawn will Polymorph into a Moon form lasting for three Rounds, starting on Round 3, with different cheats.
Shadow-Storm:
If a Wizard casts a Blade, Fellspawn will Mana Burn (Shadow) the caster, dealing 600 Shadow Damage per Pip, and Enfeeble (Shadow) the receiver of the Blade.
If an all-allies Spell such as Bladestorm is cast, only the caster's Blade will be removed.
Non-Blade Charms such as Guiding Light will not activate this cheat.
Moon Polymorph Form:
While in Polymorph form, Fellspawn loses all stats and cannot gain Power Pips or Shadow Pips.
If a Wizard casts a Trap, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps from the target of the Trap, and cast Stun (Shadow) that Stuns the caster for two Rounds.
Spells with a Trap after effect, such as Brimstone Revenant, will trigger this cheat.
Prisms will not trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: Backlash from Dark Fiend can exceed the Damage cap.
strategy: Team speed run of four: three supports and one hitter in the last position. The whole plan exploits his 9,999 damage cap, so everyone must join on Round 1 or the strategy fails (spells after round 4 trigger the trap-removal cheats).

The core trick is Nightbringer: enchant it onto the hitter's school rank-7 AoE (Fire Dragon, Frost Giant, etc.). When that hit lands while a bubble goes up the following round, Nightbringer replaces the damage cap and lets a massive hit through. Nightbringer must be unlocked (35 spellements) and you need pierce and damage stacked.
Round 1: supports 1-2 cast Donate Power TC to fuel the hitter; support 3 plays Sharpened Bladestorm or Feint; hitter casts Frenzy.
Round 2: supports 1-2 stack Potent Feint; support 3 casts Mass Hex or Mass Feint; hitter casts the Nightbringer-enchanted school AoE.
Round 3: supports cast Mass Feint, Aegis Blades, or globals; the hitter casts a 2-pip bubble (Wyldfire, Balefrost, etc.) to trigger the stored Nightbringer damage and blow past the cap.

Do not blade the hitter without Aegis (only supporter Bladestorms are safe), and remember critical hits do nothing here regardless of rating. Minions spawn round 2; same-school adds raise your damage requirement, so time the burst before they pile up.
cheatNotes: Join after round 2 and he casts Call of Khrulhu — enter early.
At the start of round 1 he summons three Tentacular Horrors, so expect a crowded board you have to manage.
After a minion dies at the end of round 3 he resummons, then refills a minion every 4th round. The adds keep coming; don't over-invest in clearing them.
He casts Equalize capping all damage at 9,999, so you cannot one-shot his 46k — plan multiple capped hits, and he recasts it if the Global is replaced.
Round 5 he sticks an Unhelpful Spawn in your team's open slot — dead weight that he resummons if killed.
Every 5th round he throws Black Mantle, Hex, and Weakness on random wizards and casts Bladestorm plus Mass Tower Shield (Ice) on his side. Cleanse the debuffs and pierce his tower before hitting.
Every 6 rounds (starting round 3) he polymorphs into a Moon form for three rounds with different cheats — always check which phase he is in.
In his Shadow-Storm phase, casting a Blade gets the caster Mana Burned (600 per pip) and the blade's receiver Enfeebled. Do not blade during this phase.
An all-allies blade like Bladestorm only loses the caster's blade to this cheat, so the rest of the team keeps theirs.
In his Moon polymorph form he loses all stats and cannot gain power or shadow pips — a safe window to unload your capped hits.
In Moon form, casting a Trap makes him Cleanse all traps off the target and Stun the caster for two rounds. Don't trap during Moon form.
Prisms do not trigger the Moon-form trap cheat, so use prisms to set up your damage school safely.

name: Flevur Flave
cheats: [Round] Will interrupt cast Gnomes! on a random Wizard at the beginning of the battle.
[Manipulation] Will interrupt cast Steal Charm (for no Pips) when a Wizard casts a Charm on itself or an Ally, and Steal Ward (for no Pips) when a Wizard casts a Ward on itself or an Ally.
Global Damage Spells will trigger this cheat.
Volcanic Golem doesn't trigger this cheat.
If a Spell such as Guidance or Legion Shield is cast, he will only cast Steal Charm/Ward on the Wizard who cast the Spell. Rebirth will also trigger this cheat because of its absorbs.
If a Spell such as Elemental Blade or Spirit Shield is cast, he will only steal the first of the Blade or the Shield (Fire and Life).
strategy: Hit with Ice or Life (+35%); never attack into Death (80% resist).

He Steal Charms any blade and Steal Wards any shield you put on yourself or an ally — so minimize single buffs. Cast Elemental or Spirit Blade and he only steals the first part (Fire, or Life), leaving the rest.
Trap him directly — Feint and traps placed ON him aren't stolen (only charms and wards on you or allies are).
Protect a key blade with Aegis, or set up and hit the same round so there's nothing to steal.
Multi-blade, Feint him, then land one big Ice/Life hit.
cheatNotes: He opens by interrupt-casting Gnomes! on a random wizard — expect the round-1 hit.
Any charm (blade) or ward (shield) you cast on yourself or an ally gets stolen for free — trap him directly or use Aegis instead.
Global damage spells also trigger the steal.
Volcanic Golem doesn't trigger it.
Team buffs like Guidance or Legion Shield only get stolen from the caster; Rebirth triggers it via its absorbs.
Cast Elemental Blade or Spirit Blade and he only steals the first part (Fire or Life), so you keep the rest.

name: Freddie Kroaker (Challenge)

stats.resist: 37% to all schools57% to all schools

cheats: Freddie Kroaker:

[Dispel][Ward] "One, two, I'm coming for you!" - At the beginning of the the first Round, Freddie Kroaker will cast a Shadow Spell that applies a Dispel Charm of the Wizard's School on each Wizard, and then place a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on a random Wizard.

[Charm][Dispel] "Let the malus grow! Let it feed my beast!" - At the beginning of every Round after the second Round, Freddie Kroaker casts a Moon version of Balanceblade that says "GROWTH," and then a Dispel Charm of the Wizard's School on each Wizard.
The above cheat will not activate if a "GROWTH" Charm was cleared by his cheats the previous turn.

[Damage] "It lives! My Juggernaut Crab of Malus lives! The Great Barrier will fall!" - At the end of each Round, if Freddie Kroaker has 3 "GROWTH" Charms, he will cast a Moon version of Call of Khrulhu that deals 1,000,000 Damage on all Wizards.

[Healing] - "This better not be love conquering all!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, a positive Healing Charm, a Healing Global, or an outgoing Healing Aura, then Freddie Kroaker will cast a version of Enfeeble that removes all Charms from himself, and a version of Cleanse Ward that removes all Wards (including the 'Nightmare Prone' mark) from the receiver.

[All Friends (Squared)] - "You're spread too thin! Three, four, we've only started our war!" - If a Wizard casts an All Allies Spell, Freddie Kroaker will cast an Equalize Global, setting the Damage cap to 3,000.
[Charm] - "Blades are the forte of Storm, Death and Life!" - If a Wizard casts a Helpful Charm and is not Storm, Death, or Life, Freddie Kroaker will cast Ice Wyvern (Tier 5b) on the receiver.

[Curse] - "Negative Charms are the forte of Death, Storm and Ice!" - If a Wizard casts a Harmful Charm and is not Death, Storm, or Ice, Freddie Kroaker will cast Stone Colossus (Tier 5b) on the caster.
[Ward] - "Shields are the forte of Ice, Myth and Death!" - If a Wizard casts a Helpful Ward and is not Ice, Myth, or Death, Freddie Kroaker will cast Helephant (Tier 5b) on the receiver.
[Jinx] - "Traps are the forte of Myth, Fire and Ice!" - If a Wizard casts a Harmful Ward and is not Myth, Fire, or Ice, Freddie Kroaker will cast Centaur (Tier 5b) on the caster.
[Damage over Time] - "DoT's are the forte of Fire, Myth and Life!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time and is not Fire, Myth, or Life, Freddie Kroaker will cast Stormzilla (Tier 5b) on the caster.

[Heal over Time] - "Heals are the forte of Life, Storm and Fire!" - If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time and is not Life, Storm, or Fire, Freddie Kroaker will cast Skeletal Pirate (Tier 5b) on the receiver.
[Pip] - "Pips are the forte of Balance!" - If a Wizard casts a Pip manipulation Spell, such as Empower or Dark Surge, and is not Balance, Freddie Kroaker will cast Mana Burn on the receiver.
Arcane Toad (Star-Storm, Challenge):
[Blade] "Too many blades!" - If a Wizard has three or more Helpful Charms, Arcane Toad (Star-Storm) will cast Banshee (Tier 5b) on the receiver of the Charm.
[Trap] "Too many traps!" - If an enemy has three or more Harmful Wards, Arcane Toad (Star-Storm) will cast Immolate (Tier 5b) on the caster of the Ward.
[Damage] "One, two, Freddie's not done with you!" - If Freddie Kroaker is targeted or an All Enemies Spell is used, Arcane Toad (Star-Storm) will give all Wizards a Living Nightmare card:
[Living Nightmare]
Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm, Challenge)

[Curse][Ward] "Sweet dreams, wizard." - At the beginning of the first Round, and at the end of every fourth Round, Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) casts Smoke Screen (Shadow), and then places a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on a random Wizard.
Cleanse Ward can be used to remove the mark.

[Round] "One, two, here's a nightmare for you!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has the 'Nightmare Prone' mark, Arcane Toad (Moon-Storm) casts one of the following Spells on the marked Wizard:
Call of Khrulhu
Monster Mash (Tier 5a)
Shift Grey Wolf
Winged Sorrow
strategy: Team fight (17,140 health, two Arcane Toads). The fight is school-gated: each action type is allowed only for certain schools, and off-school actions get you hit by a Tier 5 minion. Match casters to allowed schools (blades: Storm/Death/Life; shields: Ice/Myth/Death; traps: Myth/Fire/Ice; DoTs: Fire/Myth/Life; heals: Life/Storm/Fire; pip spells: Balance).
The kill clock is his GROWTH charms: he adds one Balanceblade GROWTH to himself each round after round 2, and at 3 GROWTH charms he wipes the team for 1,000,000 damage.

Exploit the healing cheat: any heal you cast makes him Enfeeble himself, removing all his charms including the GROWTH stacks, and Cleanse Wards the target, clearing Nightmare Prone marks. Use a heal to reset his GROWTH before it reaches 3.
Watch the Nightmare Prone stun marks (round 1 and every fourth round). A marked wizard gets hit at end of round; Cleanse Ward removes the mark, or the healing cheat clears it.
Do not cast All-Allies spells (Equalize caps your damage at 3,000), and mind the toad limits: 3+ helpful charms draws Banshee, 3+ traps on an enemy draws Immolate.
Set up within the school rules, keep GROWTH cleared with heals, and burst him down between resets.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he puts a Dispel of your own school on each wizard and a Nightmare Prone stun mark on a random one.
Each round after round 2 he adds a Balanceblade GROWTH to himself and re-Dispels every wizard.
He skips that GROWTH cast if a GROWTH charm was cleared off him the previous turn.
At 3 GROWTH charms he ends the round with Call of Khrulhu for 1,000,000 damage to all wizards.

Any heal (spell, heal charm, heal global, or outgoing-heal aura) makes him Enfeeble off all his own charms and Cleanse Ward all wards off the target. Use it to strip his GROWTH and clear Nightmare marks.
Casting an All-Allies spell makes him Equalize the damage cap down to 3,000.
A helpful charm from a non-Storm/Death/Life wizard draws Ice Wyvern on the receiver.
A harmful charm from a non-Death/Storm/Ice wizard draws Stone Colossus on the caster.
A helpful ward from a non-Ice/Myth/Death wizard draws Helephant on the receiver.
A harmful ward from a non-Myth/Fire/Ice wizard draws Centaur on the caster.
A DoT from a non-Fire/Myth/Life wizard draws Stormzilla on the caster.
A HoT from a non-Life/Storm/Fire wizard draws Skeletal Pirate on the receiver.
A pip-manipulation spell from a non-Balance wizard draws Mana Burn on the receiver.
A wizard holding 3+ helpful charms gets Banshee from the Star-Storm toad.
An enemy holding 3+ harmful wards gets its caster hit with Immolate.
Targeting Freddie or using an All-Enemies spell makes the toad give every wizard a Living Nightmare card.
Round 1 and end of every fourth round, the Moon-Storm toad casts Smoke Screen and marks a random wizard Nightmare Prone.
Cleanse Ward removes the Nightmare Prone mark.
End of each round, a marked wizard gets hit with one of the toad's listed nightmare spells.

name: Freddie Kroaker (Standard)
cheats: Freddie Kroaker:

"One, two, I'm coming for you!" - At the beginning of the first Round, or if a Wizard joins late, Freddie Kroaker will cast a Shadow Spell that applies a Dispel Charm of the Wizard's school on each Wizard, and then place a 'Nightmare Prone' Stun Shield on a random Wizard.
"One, two, here's a nightmare for you!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has the 'Nightmare Prone' mark, Freddie Kroaker casts one of the following Spells on the marked Wizard:
Call of Khrulhu
Monster Mash (Tier 5a)
Shift Grey Wolf
Winged Sorrow
"Let the malus grow! Let it feed my beast!" - At the beginning of every second Round, Freddie Kroaker casts a Moon version of Balanceblade that says "GROWTH".

"It lives! My Juggernaut Crab of Malus lives! The Great Barrier will fall!" - At the end of each Round, if Freddie Kroaker has 3 "GROWTH" Charms, he will cast a Shadow version of Skeletal Dragon that deals 40% max Health Damage on all Wizards.
Impair or Enfeeble can be used to counter this cheat

"This better not be love conquering all!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, a positive Healing Charm, a Healing Global, or an outgoing Healing Aura, then Freddie Kroaker will cast a version of Enfeeble that removes all Charms from himself, and a version of Cleanse Ward that removes all Wards (including the 'Nightmare Prone' mark) from the caster.
Aegis can be used to prevent removal.

"Three, four, we've only started our war!" - If a Wizard casts an All Allies Spell, including Living Nightmare, Freddie Kroaker will cast a Shadow Spell that applies a Dispel Charm of the Wizard's school on each Wizard.
Monstrology minions of Astral or Shadow schools will not receive a Dispel.
Arcane Toad (Star-Storm):
"One, two, Freddie's not done with you!" - If Freddie Kroaker is targeted or an All Enemies Spell is used, Arcane Toad (Star-Storm) will give all Wizards a Living Nightmare card:
[Living Nightmare]
This cheat can still be cast even if Freddie Kroaker is defeated.
strategy: Team fight (17,135, Moon). He takes +30% from Life and resists Death 67%. Hit with Life or another non-Death school.
He builds GROWTH charms (a Moon Balanceblade) every second round. At 3 GROWTH stacks he fires a Shadow Skeletal Dragon for 40% of everyone's max health. Keep the count under 3.

The clean counter: a healing spell makes him Enfeeble himself, removing ALL his charms including every GROWTH stack, and Cleanse Ward removes all wards from the caster, clearing their Nightmare Prone mark. Time a heal to wipe his GROWTH before the third stack.
Alternatively, Impair or Enfeeble from your side stops the Skeletal Dragon cheat, and Aegis protects a blade from his removal.
Watch the Nightmare Prone mark: the marked wizard eats a random heavy Shadow spell at the end of each round until it is cleared (a heal clears it).
Avoid All-Allies spells; each one dispels the whole team's school. Astral and Shadow monstrology minions are immune to the dispel.
Supports blade the hitter, the healer doubles as GROWTH-cleaner, and the hitter lands a Life AoE. Note the Arcane Toad keeps handing out Living Nightmare cards even after Freddie dies.
cheatNotes: At the start, or on late join, he dispels each wizard's school and drops a Nightmare Prone mark on someone. Expect a fizzle risk and a marked target to protect.
While marked, that wizard eats a random heavy Shadow spell (Call of Khrulhu, Monster Mash, etc.) at the end of every round. Clear the mark fast; a heal removes it.
Every second round he adds a GROWTH charm to himself. Track the stacks.
At 3 GROWTH charms he hits the whole team for 40% max health with a Shadow Skeletal Dragon. Strip his charms or Impair/Enfeeble him before the third stack.
Impair or Enfeeble on him stops the 3-GROWTH Skeletal Dragon. Keep one ready.

This is the key trick: any heal makes him Enfeeble away ALL his own charms (all GROWTH stacks) and Cleanse Ward removes all wards from the caster, clearing their Nightmare mark. Heal to reset his GROWTH and your mark at once.
Aegis on a blade stops his removal from stripping it.
Casting an All-Allies spell dispels every wizard's school. Avoid team-wide buffs unless you can eat the dispel.
Astral or Shadow monstrology minions dodge the dispel.
Targeting Freddie or using an all-enemies spell makes the Arcane Toad hand everyone a Living Nightmare card.
The Toad's card cheat still fires even after Freddie is dead, so finish the fight expecting it.

name: Frog Prince
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Humongishfrog Battlefield
cheats: [Trap] "*Noms*" - If a Trap is placed on him, he will cheat-cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) to remove one Trap.
Mass Feint (+75%) activates cheat.
Multi-Trap Spells, such as Fuel, will have only one Trap removed.
This can be avoided with Indemnity.
[Late] "*Croaks*" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, he will cast Medusa (Shadow) on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Sunblade (+35%) and Elemental Blade.
strategy: Bring your whole team in on time. A late wizard eats a Shadow Medusa, so avoid joining after the fight starts.
Protect your Feint. Any Trap placed on him triggers a Cleanse Ward that removes one Trap, so cast Feint under Indemnity so it survives the strip.
Prefer blades over trap stacks. He only removes one Trap per trigger even from multi-trap spells, so a single Indemnity-protected Feint plus blades on your hitter beats loading many traps.
Pick a boosted school. He is Sun-flagged but resists Fire, Myth, and Life at 70% and takes small boosts from Ice, Storm, and Death, so hit with Ice, Storm, or Death.
He is stunnable and beguilable, so a stun or beguile can buy you a setup round; watch his protected Sunblade and Elemental Blade plus a 480 Sun natural attack.
Set up in two or three rounds behind Indemnity, then burst him and Humongishfrog together with one AoE.
cheatNotes: Placing any Trap on him makes him Cleanse-Ward off one Trap. Protect your Feint with Indemnity so it stays.
Mass Feint (+75%) still counts as a Trap and sets off the removal cheat.
Even multi-trap spells like Fuel only lose one Trap to the cheat, so the strip is limited but real.
Indemnity on your Feint prevents it from being the Trap that gets removed.
If a wizard joins the duel late, he casts Shadow Medusa on that wizard; be present at the start.

name: Frostbranch
cheats: Interrupt Cycle
"You have little time to hurt me!" - At the start of the duel (and every five Rounds thereafter), Frostbranch will cheat-cast two Smoke Screens (-50%) at each Wizard.
The first Smoke Screen cheat-cast has a casting animation; however, the second one does not.
Quench can be used to Dispel the Smoke Screen.

"Who doesn't have a Mark of Courage?" - At the start of every fifth Round, Frostbranch will cheat-cast Efreet dealing 895 Fire Damage applying a -90% Weakness at any Wizard who does not have a "Mark of Courage". On the same Round, Frostbranch will use its turn to cast Earthquake (310) without using Pips, regardless of if any Wizard in the battle has a "Mark of Courage".
Vaporize can be used to Dispel the Earthquake.
Quench can be used to Dispel Efreet, even though it has no casting animation.
One Quench is required for every Efreet Dispelled.

"A true warrior!" - If a Wizard attacks Frostbranch with a single-target Spell, he will remove all Smoke Screens from the Wizard, and place a "Mark of Courage" on the Wizard in the form of a Stun Shield.
Pet Maycast Talents will not activate this cheat.
If Frostbranch has reached the "Who doesn't have a Mark of Courage?" phase of his cycle most recently (he has not recast his Smoke Screens yet), direct attacks will not yield any Stun Shields.

Since the Marks of Courage are Stun Shields, they are not removed by Earthquake nor stolen by Thieving Dragon. This means that wizards need only attack directly once to avoid Frostbranch's Efreet cheat.
strategy: He opens by throwing two -50% Smoke Screens on each wizard, wrecking your accuracy.
Attack him once with a single-target spell to strip your Smoke Screens and earn a Mark of Courage.
That Mark (a Stun Shield) makes you immune to his round-5 Efreet (895 Fire plus -90% Weakness), so grab it before round 5. It survives Earthquake and cannot be stolen.
Hit with Fire or Myth (+30% incoming); avoid Ice (80% resist).
Blade or Feint, then burst the 4435 health with one clean hit.
cheatNotes: At the start and every 5 rounds he lays two -50% Smoke Screens on each wizard, tanking your accuracy.
Quench dispels the Smoke Screen if you would rather cleanse than attack through it.
Every fifth round he Efreets (895 Fire plus -90% Weakness) any wizard without a Mark of Courage, plus a free Earthquake. Have your Mark up.
Vaporize dispels that Earthquake.
Quench dispels the Efreet even though it shows no animation.
You need one Quench per Efreet you want to dispel.
Attacking him with a single-target spell clears your Smoke Screens and grants a Mark of Courage (a Stun Shield). Do this early to dodge the Efreet.
Pet may-casts will not earn the Mark, so make the attack yourself.
During the Mark-of-Courage round itself, direct attacks grant no Mark. Get yours in an earlier round.
The Mark is a Stun Shield, so Earthquake and Thieving Dragon cannot remove it. One direct hit early keeps you safe from the Efreet.

name: Furryosa
cheats: "AAAAHHHHHH!" - At the start of the first Round, Furryosa casts Quicken.
Furryosa will recast this Spell at the start of every third Round.
strategy: She has no listed school resist, so hit with your strongest attack school.
She casts Quicken on Round 1 and re-casts it every third round. It is her buff, so do not waste turns replacing it, just out-damage her.
Spend two to three rounds stacking blades and a Feint.
Land one large hit. At 12,355 health, bring enough damage or split the kill across two bursts.
Keep shields and heals ready between her Quicken cycles on Rounds 1, 4, 7 and so on.
cheatNotes: She casts Quicken at the start of Round 1, it is her global, not a threat you need to answer.
She re-casts Quicken every third round (4, 7, 10 and so on), ignore it and keep to your own setup-then-hit plan.

name: General Benedict Brutus von Booth
cheats: Cheat Cycle:
"Get your paws off me!" - At the start of Round 1, Benedict will cast an Invulnerability -200% Brace on himself.
Using Supernova will not remove his Aura.
Avoiding joining until pips appear at everyone's feet will prevent this cheat from taking place.
"Fine! Let's get uncivilized" - At the end of Round 3, Benedict casts Furnace (6 Rounds), making him vulnerable.
"Burn curs!" - At the end of Round 4, Benedict casts Fire Dragon and Incindiate.
The Round after the cheat cycle will restart.
strategy: He is immune to damage under a -200% Invulnerability aura from Round 1, so do not attack yet. Spend Rounds 1-3 stacking blades and Feint.
At the end of Round 3 he casts Furnace and becomes vulnerable, then the cheat cycle restarts, so burst him in the Round 4 window before he re-shields.
Hit with Ice (+10%, he resists everything else 40%); make it one big blade-stacked hit while he is exposed.
Expect Fire Dragon and Incindiate at the end of Round 4, so shield Fire and heal before your hit.
Supernova will not remove the aura, so do not bother; just wait out the invulnerable window.
cheatNotes: He is immune to damage under a -200% Invulnerability aura from Round 1, so spend the opening rounds setting up rather than hitting.
Supernova will not strip this aura, so do not waste a turn trying.
You can skip the aura entirely by not joining until pips appear at everyone's feet.
At the end of Round 3 he casts Furnace and becomes vulnerable; that is your damage window before the cycle restarts.
End of Round 4 he throws Fire Dragon and Incindiate, so shield Fire and heal going into it.
The cheat cycle restarts afterward and he re-shields, so land your kill in the Round 4 vulnerability window.

name: General Lightbane (Tier 1)
locations: Khrysalis: Last Wood - General Lightbane's Chamber
cheats: "Scour the Light and Raise the Shadow!" - At the beginning of the first Round, General Lightbane will cast one of the versions of these following Spells. This cheat will repeat after attacking.
Dark Nova, Dark Shepherd, Dark Fiend
These Spells are accelerated, adding a +40% Shadowblade at the end of Round 1, and the second effect Shadowspear and Shadow Trap Round two and attacking the same Round.
General Lightbane will cast a Shadow Spell to summon one of the following Minions, and they will affect the Spells it casts accordingly.
These cheats repeat after the two Round Shadow-Creature Spell cycle.

General Lightbane will use his turns casting Super Shadowplume & Headless Horseman (Shadow).

General Lightbane will use his turns casting Vitamins & Regenerate.

General Lightbane will use his turns casting Storm Elf (Shadow) & Heck Hound.

General Lightbane will use his turns casting Dragonblade & Chromatic Blast.
[Curse] "Beware, my thrall, of Curses" - Cursed Shrike (Tier 1) [Shadow][Storm].
General Lightbane will use his turns casting Weakness & Virulent Plague.

General Lightbane will use his turns casting Tower Shield & Frozen Armor.
[Trap] "Beware, my thrall, of Jinxes" - Trapper Shrike (Tier 1) [Shadow][Myth].
General Lightbane will use his turns casting Hex & Mass Feint (+70%).
[Pacify] "Beware, my thrall, of Pacify" - Mollifying Sentinel (Tier 1) [Shadow][Life].
General Lightbane will use his turns casting Pacify & Tranquilize.
[Threat] "Beware, my thrall, of Taunts" - Mocking Sentinel (Tier 1) [Shadow][Ice].
General Lightbane will use his turns casting Taunt & Mega Taunt.
[Global] "Beware, my thrall, of Globals" - Seraph of Sacred Ground (Tier 1) [Shadow][Balance].
General Lightbane will use his turns casting Doom and Gloom & Shadow Global.
[Aura] "Beware, my thrall, of Auras" - Seraph of Blessings (Tier 1) [Shadow][Star].
General Lightbane will use his turns casting Frenzy & Frenzy (5 Pip).
"Your magic feeds the Shadow" - Using the wrong Spell type against a certain Cycle Phase will trigger the following response casts from General Lightbane:
[Healing] - Healing will trigger Blessing on his minion.
[Damage] - Damage will trigger Super Shadowplume.
[Heal over Time] - Heal over Time will trigger Sprite on his minion.
[Damage over Time] - Damage over Time will trigger Scald.
[Blade] - Blades will trigger Balanceblade.
[Curse] - Negative Damage Charms will trigger Plague.
[Ward] - Shields will trigger Legion Shield.
[Trap] - Traps will trigger Mass Hex.
[Pacify] - Pacifies will trigger Subdue.
[Threat] - Taunts will trigger Taunt.
[Global] - Globals will trigger Shadow Global.
[Aura] - Auras will trigger Frenzy.
"Gah!" - Casting the opposite Spell Type of a given Minion will cause it to use Sacrifice on itself, losing 20% maximum Health. This will increase the backlash of the Boss by +10.
(Needs confirmation)
Seraph of Salvation -> Damage
Frenzied Shrike -> Healing
Mending Trickster -> Damage over Time
Sly Trickster -> Heal over Time
Charming Trickster -> Negative Damage Charm
Cursed Shrike -> Blade
Mighty Sentinel -> Trap
Trapper Shrike -> Positive Ward
Mollifying Sentinel -> Taunt
Mocking Sentinel -> Pacify
Seraph of Blessings -> Global
Seraph of Sacred Ground -> Aura
spellNotes: Frenzy (5 Pip) provides +40% outgoing Damage, and Doubles Pip gain for 4 Rounds.
strategy: Lightbane resists all damage 300%, so you cannot simply hit him down; the fight is about matching the active phase and forcing his minion to Sacrifice.
Each cycle he announces a phase ('Beware, my thrall, of ...') and summons a themed Shadow minion. Read the phase before you cast.
Do not cast the phase's own spell type; casting the wrong type for the active phase triggers a punish cast from Lightbane, such as a Heal in the Healing phase triggering Blessing on his minion.
Cast the exact opposite spell type of the active minion to force it to Sacrifice for 20% of its Health, whittling it down, though this raises his backlash by +10.

He opens Round 1 with an accelerated Shadow AoE (Dark Nova, Dark Shepherd, or Dark Fiend) backed by a +40% Shadowblade, Shadowspear, and a Shadow Trap, and repeats it after attacking, so keep your Health buffered.
Coordinate as a team so each wizard casts the correct counter-type without tripping the wrong-type punish.
cheatNotes: He opens Round 1 with an accelerated Shadow AoE (Dark Nova, Dark Shepherd, or Dark Fiend) and repeats it after every attack.
The opening is buffed: he adds a +40% Shadowblade at the end of Round 1, then Shadowspear and a Shadow Trap on Round 2 before hitting.
He summons a themed Shadow minion whose type dictates which spells he casts each cycle.
The summon-and-cast pattern repeats every two Rounds.
Healing phase: he summons Seraph of Salvation to counter your heals.
During the Healing phase he spends turns casting Minor Blessing and Fairy.
Those support heals land on his minion, keeping it alive.
Damage phase: he summons Frenzied Shrike.
During the Damage phase he casts Super Shadowplume and a Shadow Headless Horseman.
HOT phase: he summons Mending Trickster.
During the HOT phase he casts Vitamins and Regenerate.
DoT phase: he summons Sly Trickster.
During the DoT phase he casts a Shadow Storm Elf and Heck Hound.
Charm phase: he summons Charming Trickster.
During the Charm phase he casts Dragonblade and Chromatic Blast.
Curse phase: he summons Cursed Shrike.
During the Curse phase he casts Weakness and Virulent Plague.
Ward phase: he summons Mighty Sentinel.
During the Ward phase he casts Tower Shield and Frozen Armor.
Jinx/Trap phase: he summons Trapper Shrike.
During the Trap phase he casts Hex and a +70% Mass Feint.
Pacify phase: he summons Mollifying Sentinel.
During the Pacify phase he casts Pacify and Tranquilize.
Threat phase: he summons Mocking Sentinel.
During the Taunt phase he casts Taunt and Mega Taunt.
Global phase: he summons Seraph of Sacred Ground.
During the Global phase he casts Doom and Gloom and a Shadow Global.
Aura phase: he summons Seraph of Blessings.
During the Aura phase he casts Frenzy and 5-pip Frenzy.
Casting the wrong spell type for the active phase triggers a punish cast from Lightbane, listed below.
Casting a Heal triggers Blessing on his minion.
Casting Damage triggers Super Shadowplume.
Casting a Heal over Time triggers Sprite on his minion.
Casting a Damage over Time triggers Scald.
Casting a Blade triggers Balanceblade.
Casting a negative damage Charm triggers Plague.
Casting a Shield triggers Legion Shield.
Casting a Trap triggers Mass Hex.
Casting a Pacify triggers Subdue.
Casting a Taunt triggers Taunt.
Casting a Global triggers a Shadow Global.
Casting an Aura triggers Frenzy.
Casting the exact opposite spell type of the active minion makes it Sacrifice for 20% of its Health but raises the boss's backlash by +10.

spellNotes: Frenzy (5 Pip) provides +40% outgoing Damage, and Doubles Pip gain for 4 Rounds.
strategy: Team fight at 45,000 Health with 300% resist to every school. Raw hits barely scratch him, so this is a setup war.
Read the summoned minion each cycle. It names both the spell type he punishes and the opposite spell that hurts him.
Never cast the punished type during its phase. That triggers his backlash cast, such as Super Shadowplume for damage or Balanceblade for blades.
Instead cast the minion's opposite type (Damage versus Salvation, Healing versus Frenzied Shrike, and so on) to force it to Sacrifice 20% of its health and raise his backlash.
Because of the 300% resist, funnel every blade and Feint onto one hitter and only fire when the stack is huge.
Watch Round 2. His accelerated Shadowblade and Shadowspear combo spikes his own damage, so keep the healer ready.
cheatNotes: He opens by casting Dark Nova, Dark Shepherd, or Dark Fiend, and repeats this attack cycle after each time he hits.
His attack is accelerated: a +40% Shadowblade at the end of Round 1, then Shadowspear and Shadow Trap on Round 2, hitting the same Round. His damage spikes on Round 2.
He summons a Shadow minion that dictates which spells he casts and which spell type he punishes.
The minion and spell pattern repeats after each two-Round shadow-creature cycle.
Seraph of Salvation (Life) marks the phase where healing is punished. He supports it with Minor Blessing and Fairy.
Frenzied Shrike (Death) marks the damage-punished phase. He casts Shadowplume and Headless Horseman.
Mending Trickster (Life) marks the heal-over-time-punished phase. He casts Vitamins and Regenerate.
Sly Trickster (Fire) marks the damage-over-time-punished phase. He casts Storm Elf and Heck Hound.
Charming Trickster (Storm) marks the charm-punished phase. He casts Dragonblade and Chromatic Blast.
Cursed Shrike (Storm) marks the curse-punished phase. He casts Weakness and Virulent Plague.
Mighty Sentinel (Balance) marks the ward-punished phase. He casts Tower Shield and Frozen Armor.
Trapper Shrike (Myth) marks the trap-punished phase. He casts Hex and Mass Feint.
Mollifying Sentinel (Life) marks the pacify-punished phase. He casts Pacify and Tranquilize.
Mocking Sentinel (Ice) marks the taunt-punished phase. He casts Taunt and Mega Taunt.
Seraph of Sacred Ground (Balance) marks the global-punished phase. He casts Doom and Gloom and Shadow Global.
Seraph of Blessings (Star) marks the aura-punished phase. He casts Frenzy and Frenzy (5 Pip).
Casting the spell type the current phase punishes triggers a backlash cast from him.
Healing during its phase makes him Blessing his minion.
Damage during its phase triggers Super Shadowplume.
A heal-over-time during its phase triggers Sprite on his minion.
A damage-over-time during its phase triggers Scald.
A blade during its phase triggers Balanceblade.
A negative damage charm during its phase triggers Plague.
A shield during its phase triggers Legion Shield.
A trap during its phase triggers Mass Hex.
A pacify during its phase triggers Subdue.
A taunt during its phase triggers Taunt.
A global during its phase triggers Shadow Global.
An aura during its phase triggers Frenzy.
Casting the opposite spell type to the current minion makes it Sacrifice itself for 20% of its health and raises the boss's backlash by +10. This is how you wear him down.
Against Seraph of Salvation, cast Damage to force the Sacrifice.
Against Frenzied Shrike, cast Healing.
Against Mending Trickster, cast a Damage over Time.
Against Sly Trickster, cast a Heal over Time.
Against Charming Trickster, cast a Negative Damage Charm (weakness).
Against Cursed Shrike, cast a Blade.
Against Mighty Sentinel, cast a Trap.
Against Trapper Shrike, cast a Positive Ward (shield).
Against Mollifying Sentinel, cast a Taunt.
Against Mocking Sentinel, cast a Pacify.
Against Seraph of Blessings, cast a Global.
Against Seraph of Sacred Ground, cast an Aura.

spellNotes: Frenzy (5 Pip) provides +40% outgoing Damage, and Doubles Pip gain for 4 Rounds.
strategy: Team fight: 60,000 health and 300% resist to every school, so this is a long attrition duel - bring a dedicated hitter, supports who feed blades and Feint, and a utility wizard to read phases.

Each cycle he summons one guardian minion; the minion's category names the guarded spell type for that phase (see cheat notes). Casting the guarded type earns an extra punish, so identify the newest minion before anyone acts.

Whittle guardians with the exploit: cast the OPPOSITE type of the current minion so it Sacrifices itself for 20% of its max health (Seraph of Salvation dies to Damage, Cursed Shrike to a Blade, and so on). This also raises his backlash by +10.
Watch the accelerated shadow attack: a +40% Shadowblade lands end of Round 1, then Shadowspear and Shadow Trap plus a hit on Round 2. Pacify or taunt-tank that window and keep the hitter healthy.
Because his resist is so high, the hitter must overstack - many blades, stacked Feints when the Trap phase is not active, and shadow or pierce - before committing one massive hit.
Do not blanket-heal or shield on reflex; heals, HoTs, blades, shields, traps, globals, and auras are each the guarded type during some phase, so use each tool only when its phase is safe.
cheatNotes: The fight opens with a Shadow attack (Dark Nova, Dark Shepherd, or Dark Fiend) and this pattern repeats each cycle, so expect a hit roughly every two rounds.
He accelerates: a +40% Shadowblade lands end of Round 1, then Shadowspear and Shadow Trap go up Round 2 and he attacks that same round. Blunt that burst window.
Each cycle he summons one guardian minion; the minion's type tells you which spell category is guarded that phase.
The summon-and-attack pattern loops every two-round cycle, so re-read the newest minion each cycle to know the current phase.
Healing phase (Seraph of Salvation): heals are guarded now, so hold any healing until the phase changes.
During the Healing phase he wastes turns on Minor Blessing and Fairy instead of hitting hard.
Those heals land on his own minion, keeping the guardian alive.
Damage phase (Frenzied Shrike): plain attacks are guarded, so set up instead of hitting this phase.
In the Damage phase he throws Super Shadowplume and Headless Horseman (Shadow) at the team.
HoT phase (Mending Trickster): heal-over-time spells are guarded, so avoid Regenerate-type casts.
During the HoT phase he casts Vitamins and Regenerate on his side.
DoT phase (Sly Trickster): damage-over-time is guarded, so hold Fire and Bug DoTs this phase.
In the DoT phase he uses Storm Elf (Shadow) and Heck Hound against you.
Charm phase (Charming Trickster): blades and charms are guarded, so do not blade up this phase.
During the Charm phase he stacks Dragonblade and Chromatic Blast on his own side.
Curse phase (Cursed Shrike): negative charms like Weakness are guarded this phase.
In the Curse phase he applies Weakness and Virulent Plague to the team.
Ward phase (Mighty Sentinel): shields and positive wards are guarded, so do not shield up now.
During the Ward phase he shields with Tower Shield and Frozen Armor.
Trap phase (Trapper Shrike): traps and jinxes are guarded, so hold Feints this phase.
In the Trap phase he casts Hex and a +70% Mass Feint that stacks a dangerous trap on the whole team.
Pacify phase (Mollifying Sentinel): pacifies are guarded here.
During the Pacify phase he casts Pacify and Tranquilize to choke your pips.
Threat phase (Mocking Sentinel): taunts are guarded this phase.
In the Threat phase he throws Taunt and Mega Taunt.
Global phase (Seraph of Sacred Ground): globals are guarded, so do not drop a global now.
During the Global phase he lays Doom and Gloom and a Shadow Global.
Aura phase (Seraph of Blessings): auras are guarded this phase.
In the Aura phase he buffs with Frenzy and the 5-pip Frenzy (+40% damage, doubled pips).
Casting the guarded spell type for the current phase triggers an extra response cast, listed below - one more reason to match the phase.
Healing during the wrong phase makes him cast Blessing on his minion.
Attacking during the wrong phase triggers Super Shadowplume back at you.
A heal-over-time in the wrong phase triggers Sprite on his minion.
A DoT in the wrong phase triggers Scald.
A blade in the wrong phase triggers Balanceblade on his side.
A weakness or negative charm in the wrong phase triggers Plague.
A shield in the wrong phase triggers Legion Shield on him.
A trap in the wrong phase triggers Mass Hex.
A pacify in the wrong phase triggers Subdue.
A taunt in the wrong phase triggers a Taunt back.
A global in the wrong phase triggers a Shadow Global.
An aura in the wrong phase triggers Frenzy, buffing his damage.
