The exploit: cast the OPPOSITE spell type of the current guardian and it Sacrifices itself, losing 20% of its max health and adding +10 to the boss's backlash. Use this to grind minions down safely.

name: General Stormclaw
cheats: At the beginning of the first Round, General Stormclaw will cast a -65% Tower Shield on himself, and replace it whenever he is hit.
He will place another Tower Shield on himself every three to four Rounds.
Using Steal Ward will bypass this cheat.
At the end of the first Round, he will cheat-cast Earthquake and summon his Minions.

He will cast a version of Cleanse Ward that removes two negative Wards if a Trap is placed on him or his Minions, and removes Weakness from himself and his Minions, and even sometimes from Wizards, but stops removing them after his Minions have been defeated.
"I saw that!!" - If a Wizard attacks Tired Fighter then the following cheats will occur by interrupt.
Lazy Henchmen will cheat-cast Rebirth healing 750 without absorbs.
Half-Witted Skeleton cheat-casts Colossus after Rebirth cheat.
All Enemy Spells from the Wizard's team don't trigger this cheat.
Whenever Half-Witted Skeleton casts Blizzard, or Tired Fighter casts Tempest, only the Rebirth cheat will trigger.
"Help me!" - If General Stormclaw is attacked, his Minions will cast the following spells:
Lazy Henchman will cheat-cast Rebirth (without absorbs). It will also cheat-cast this Spell if the Half-Witted Skeleton or Tired Fighter is attacked.
Tired Fighter will cheat-cast Storm Lord.
For Spells that hit more than once (e.g. Chimera, Hydra, Orthrus, Minotaur, Damage over Times etc.), the Minions will cheat-cast their Spells for each time the Boss was hit.
The Round after all three Minions have been defeated, he will cheat-cast Frost Giant dealing 475 Damage.
If all Minions are defeated in the same Round going first, he will activate this on the same turn, not the one after.
strategy: Hit with Storm; he takes +50% and resists Myth 80%.
He shields himself with a -65% Tower Shield on Round 1 and re-shields when hit or every few Rounds; use Steal Ward to bypass it rather than wasting a hit on the shield.
At the end of Round 1 he Earthquakes and summons three minions that constantly heal and buff him and each other.
Do not poke the Tired Fighter or Stormclaw directly early; his minions cheat-cast Rebirth heals, Colossus, and Storm Lord in response. Clear the minions first.
Because minion cheats fire once per hit, use single-hit spells while minions are alive, not multi-hit spells like Hydra.
After all three minions die he cheat-casts Frost Giant (475); with the board clear, Steal Ward his shield and finish with a Storm hit.
cheatNotes: He wears a -65% Tower Shield and replaces it when hit; Steal Ward it off instead of hitting into it.
He also refreshes a Tower Shield every three to four Rounds, so keep a Steal Ward handy.
Steal Ward removes the shield without triggering a replacement; it is the clean answer to his shielding.
At the end of Round 1 he Earthquakes (stripping your blades) and summons his three minions; delay heavy setup until after.
Placing a Trap on him or his minions makes him Cleanse two wards and strip Weaknesses; avoid traps while minions live.
Attacking the Tired Fighter sets off interrupt heals and buffs from the other minions; leave it for last or clear the board together.
The Lazy Henchman cheat-heals 750 with no absorbs when you poke the minions.
The Half-Witted Skeleton follows with Colossus after that Rebirth.
All-enemy spells from your team do not trigger this interrupt, so AoE is safer than single-target pokes.
When the skeleton casts Blizzard or the fighter casts Tempest, only the Rebirth heal fires.
Hitting Stormclaw himself makes his minions respond with heals and Storm Lord, so kill minions first.
The Lazy Henchman Rebirth-heals whenever Stormclaw or any minion is attacked.
The Tired Fighter answers with Storm Lord on your team.
Multi-hit spells like Hydra make the minions cheat once per hit, multiplying their heals; use single hits while minions live.
Once all three minions are down he cheat-casts Frost Giant for 475; expect it the Round after you clear them.
If you clear all minions in one Round while going first, the Frost Giant lands that same turn.

name: General Tung-Ak
locations: Novus: Stone of Heaven - Tung-Ak's Stronghold
cheats: "My armory is infinite" - At the beginning of every Round, General Tung-Ak casts one of the following Shadow Spells.
Protected 1,500 Absorb Damage Ward.
Protected +500 Flat Damage Charm.
"Even the ancestors are mine to command!" - General Tung-Ak casts Guardian Spirit (20%) on himself.
strategy: Storm boss (11145). Hit with Myth (+14%); he resists 36% across the board, so blades and traps matter a lot to punch through the universal resist.

Every round he adds either a protected 1500 absorb ward or a protected +500 flat-damage charm. The absorbs stack fast, so do not drip-feed hits. Set up fully and hit once, hard enough to blow through the absorbs in one shot.
He gives himself Guardian Spirit, so he revives once. Your finisher or a follow-up must account for a second life bar.
His charms and wards are protected, so removal is inefficient. Out-damage them with heavy blade and trap stacking and Myth hits.
cheatNotes: Every round he casts one of two protected buffs below. They pile up, so a slow chip strategy loses. Burst him.
One option is a protected 1500-damage absorb. Multiple can stack, so your hit must overkill the total absorb to connect.
The other option is a protected +500 flat-damage charm that makes his attacks hit noticeably harder. Keep heals ready.
He self-casts Guardian Spirit, so he revives once at low health. Plan a second hit to put him down for good.

name: Ghost Dog (Shadow)
cheats: "Devote your body and soul to your master!" - At the end of the first Round, Ghost Dog will summon two Hagakure Blades Minions and one Seijun Killer Minion. They become active during the second Round.

"Feel the poetry of war!" - At the end of the second Round, and every five Rounds thereafter, Ghost Dog will interrupt-cast a Damage over Time Spell that deals 400 Death Damage per Round for ten Rounds, or 575 Death Damage per Round in Shadow form.
When this cheat is triggered, Ghost Dog will target the Wizard with the most generated threat, or the Wizard in the first position (Sun spot) of the Duel by default.

"The way of the Samoorai is found in death!" - At the end of the fifth Round, and every five Rounds thereafter, Ghost Dog will interrupt-cast a Death version of Detonate on the Wizard who received the Damage over Time effect, dealing 2,800 Death Damage.
If this cheat is Dispelled, then Ghost Dog will interrupt-cast Detonate again to hit his target.
"Be resolved from the beginning!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, then Ghost Dog will interrupt-cast Shatter on the Wizard that cast the Ward.
Pet spells do not trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: This Boss uses Shadow Shrike MOB with +15 Backlash.
strategy: Keep a minimal deck of blades, Feints, and one primary attack so you draw what you need. This is a fast burst fight: get your buffs on and get out.
Send one wizard in first (Solo Scout) to draw the minions' opening spells like Leviathan and Storm Lord alone, preserving the team's blades.
Clear the minions the boss summons at the end of round 1 (two Hagakure Blades and a Seijun Killer) before they land their big hits, then focus the boss.
Do not shield. He ghost-casts Shatter on any helpful Ward, so avoid Rebirth and Spirit Armor healing; heal with flat heals and use Triage or Strangle to manage his Death DoT instead.

Manage the DoT cheat: at the end of round 2 (and every 5 rounds after) he lays a 575 Death-per-round DoT for 10 rounds on the wizard with the most threat or the first (Sun) spot. Triage it off, because at round 5 he Detonates it for 2,800 Death on that same wizard.
Build blades and Feints on one Primary Hitter while the Support cleanses DoTs, account for his Fortify raising his effective 21,760 health, then burst him down with one large single-target hit.
cheatNotes: At the end of round 1 he summons two Hagakure Blade minions and one Seijun Killer, which act starting round 2. Plan to clear them or tank their hits.
At the end of round 2, and every 5 rounds after, he casts a Death DoT (400 Death per round, 575 in Shadow form, for 10 rounds). Remove it with Triage or block it with Strangle.
That DoT lands on whoever has generated the most threat, or the first-spot (Sun) wizard by default, so your hitter is the likely target. Cleanse it off them.
At the end of round 5, and every 5 rounds after, he Detonates the DoT for 2,800 Death damage on the wizard who took it. Triage the DoT before this fires.
If you Dispel that Detonate, he simply re-casts it to hit his target anyway, so dispels do not save you here. Remove the DoT instead.
If any wizard casts a helpful Ward, he interrupt-Shatters it on the caster. Skip shields and Spirit Armor healing entirely.
Pet-cast wards do not trigger the Shatter cheat, so pet shields are safe to use.

name: Gibson O'Leary (Rank 11)
cheats: "Uh oh, you dropped your little Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Gibson O'Leary will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade, then he will proceed to destroy it.
"I think that Blade would look better on me!" - If a universal Blade, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade.
"You can't mess with us!" - If any type of universal Trap is placed on an enemy, he will cheat-cast Backdraft on the Wizard who cast the Trap.
"Allow me to lower the boom!" - When a single Trap or DoT is placed on a Wizard, he will cheat-cast Detonate on the Wizard.
strategy: Hit Ice. He resists 80% Fire and takes boosted Ice damage, so Ice is your clean route through his 6,145 health.
Do not rely on blades. Any blade he steals off the recipient and then destroys, so stacked charms are wasted here.
Do not use universal traps. A universal trap on him puts Backdraft on the caster, and a single trap or DoT on a wizard makes him Detonate that wizard.
Skip the whole blade-and-trap loop. Instead lean on flat Ice damage, an aura, or a shadow/critical hit to build a hit without feeding his steal and Detonate cheats.
With no minions and low health, a couple of setup rounds using untouched tools plus one strong Ice hit ends it before his counters matter.
cheatNotes: Casting any blade makes him Steal Charm it off the recipient, then destroy it.
Casting a universal, Elemental, or Fireblade makes him Steal Charm it off the recipient.
Placing any universal trap on an enemy makes him cast Backdraft on you, the caster.
Placing a single trap or DoT on a wizard makes him cast Detonate on that wizard.

name: Gibson O'Leary (Rank 14)
cheats: "Uh oh, you dropped your little Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Gibson O'Leary will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade, then he will proceed to destroy it.
"I think that Blade would look better on me!" - If a universal Blade, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade.
"You can't mess with us!" - If any type of universal Trap is placed on Gibson, he will cheat-cast Backdraft on the Wizard who cast the Trap.
"Allow me to lower the boom!" - When a single DoT is placed on a Wizard, he will cheat-cast Detonate on the Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Ice or Storm (both +30%). Never Fire; he resists it 80%.

Blades are stolen. Any Blade you cast gets Steal Charm'd off the receiver, and universal, Elemental, or Fireblades go straight onto him. Protect your key blade with Aegis or lean on traps he does not punish.
Avoid universal Traps on Gibson. Each one hits the caster with Backdraft. Use school-specific traps instead.
Do not leave a single Damage-over-Time on any Wizard; he Detonates it for burst.
Stack an Aegis-protected blade plus safe traps, then fire one Ice or Storm hit.
cheatNotes: Casting any Blade lets him Steal Charm it off the receiver and destroy it. Aegis-protect the blade you need.
Universal, Elemental, or Fireblades get stolen straight onto him, buffing his hits. Prefer other blades or protect them.
A universal Trap on him triggers Backdraft on the caster. Use school-specific traps instead.
A single DoT sitting on a Wizard gets Detonated for instant damage. Do not leave DoTs up.

name: Gladiator Dimachaerus (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Mount Olympus (Challenge) - Pit of the Noxii (Challenge)

stats.resist: 7% to all schools27% to all schools

cheats: [Round] "I can smell your weakness" - At the beginning of every Round, Gladiator Dimachaerus will cast his Natural Attack on the Wizard with the lowest current health, which deals 180 Balance Damage and has an additional effect based on the attacked Wizard's School:
[Storm] or [Death] - A +55% version of Hex
[Ice] or [Myth] - A Damage over Time that deals 150 Balance Damage over 3 Rounds
[Life] or [Fire] or [Balance] - A -35% version of Weakness
strategy: Every round he hits the lowest-health wizard for 180 Balance plus a school-based rider. Keep everyone topped so the target stays predictable and the riders stay manageable.
Low 3,220 health means you do not need much setup; one or two blades and a hit end it before riders pile up.
Balance resists itself 27%, so hit with an off-school or Spectral hit.
Clear the Doomed Bones minion in the same AoE.
cheatNotes: Every round he Natural-Attacks the lowest-health wizard for 180 Balance, adding a rider based on that wizard's school.
A Storm or Death wizard takes a +55% Hex from the rider.
An Ice or Myth wizard takes a DoT dealing 150 Balance over 3 rounds.
A Life, Fire, or Balance wizard takes a -35% Weakness.

name: Gobblorian Bandit

stats.resist: 40% to all schools70% to all schools100% to all schools

cheats: The Wizard can cast Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, and War Oracle as inspired Casts during this battle.
At the end of every Round, Gobblorian Bandit will cast Power Current on itself.
Boss sometimes uses Sprite on himself to set up a Gambit.
spellNotes: Casts a 6 Pip version of Blizzard that deals 555 Ice Damage and removes 3 positive Charms from all enemies.
Casts a 6 Pip version of Ratatoskr's Spin that deals 555 Life Damage, and removes 3 negative Wards from all allies.
strategy: Voracious Void raid boss, so coordinate as a team with a hitter, blading supports, and utility.
Attack with Ice or Death — those are only 40% resisted, while Fire and Life are fully immune (100%) and Storm, Myth, Balance, and Astral/Shadow are 70% resisted.
Lean on the inspired casts unlocked here (Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle) since your normal schools are heavily resisted.

He self-buffs Power Current every round and can Sprite himself to set up a Gambit, so don't let the fight drag and watch for his charm/ward strips (Blizzard removes 3 of your charms, Ratatoskr's Spin removes wards).
Hold blades to the hitting round so his Blizzard doesn't strip them early, then commit one big Ice or Death hit.
cheatNotes: You get access to inspired casts here (Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle) — lean on them since your normal schools are heavily resisted.
He stacks Power Current on himself every round, building his own damage and gambit — don't let the fight drag; his hit grows each round.
He may Sprite himself to set up a Gambit — treat the heal-over-time as a tell that a gambit payoff is coming.

name: Golden Horde Captain
cheats: "Feel the wrath of the Horde" - At the end of every two Rounds, Golden Horde Captain will cast one of the following spells:
Unicorn, Sacred Charge, Centaur, Headless Horseman,
Chivalrous Centaur, Snow Angel, Shift Otomo, Shift Margherita
strategy: Hit with Death — he resists every other school 50%, but Death isn't on his resist list, so it lands full.
He has +100% outgoing healing and casts heal spells like Unicorn and Sacred Charge, so pack Infection or Doom and Gloom to cut his healing.
Every two rounds he casts a random attack (Unicorn, Centaur, Headless Horseman, and others) — keep your health buffered.
He can't be stunned but can be Beguiled — a Beguile buys you setup rounds.
Blade and Feint (Death or universal), then land a big Death hit through his healing.
cheatNotes: Every two rounds he casts one of these spells at random — a mix of Life hits and shifts, so stay topped up.

name: Golden Horde Colonel
cheats: [Round] "I empower the horde!" - At the end of every two Rounds, Golden Horde Colonel will cast Donate Power.
strategy: He resists 40% to every school with no weakness, so stack armor piercing and blades rather than hunting a boosted school.
He recasts Donate Power every 2 rounds; with no minions present it just cycles pips, so ignore it and focus your setup.
He protects a charm with Dwarven Aegis, so a single Steal or removal will not strip it - do not waste a turn trying.
Stack blades plus Feint over 2-3 rounds and land one big pierced hit.
cheatNotes: Every 2 rounds he casts Donate Power to buff his side. With no minions present it has little effect - do not react to it.

name: Golden Horde Storykeeper
locations: MooShu: Catmandu - Temple of Purrfect Harmony
cheats: [Round] "I can't read and now neither can you!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Golden Horde Storykeeper will cast a Shadow Spell to give all Wizards three of a random assortment of cards.
If a Wizard joins the battle late, they will receive these three cards at the beginning of the next Round.
[Uioweajdsfamdsa][Reqpivunadhfjkw][Vzpoaiejfandvjha]
[Round] "Burn, books, burn!" - At the start of every fifth Round, Golden Horde Storykeeper will cast Scald.
strategy: Discard the junk. On Round 1 he jams three random cards into every hand, so drop them before they clog your draws.
He resists all schools 40% and only gives a small +10% boost to Ice, so bring Ice or use a prism to convert.
Keep Triage or heals ready for his Scald, a Fire DOT he casts every fifth Round.
Blade and Feint onto one hitter, clear the junk cards, and land your hit before the round-five Scald pressure stacks.
He is not stunable, so you cannot skip his Scald turns; time your kill window instead.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 he shadow-casts three random junk cards into everyone's hand, so discard them to keep your draws clean.
A wizard who joins late gets the same three cards on their next Round.
Every fifth Round he casts Scald, a Fire damage-over-time, so keep Triage or heals ready.

name: Goliath

stats.resist: 30% to all schools60% to all schools

cheats: [Harmful Aura] "I may be demimortal, but you will be half of even that!" At the beginning of the first Round, Goliath will cast a Spell that deals 50% max Health Damage to all Wizards, and applies a -50% outgoing Damage Aura for 99 Rounds on all Wizards.
[Late] If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Goliath will cheat cast this on any late Wizard, but will not re-cast it on any Wizards already present in the battle.
[Aura] "In my presence, you shall always be semi-demi-mortal!" If a Wizard casts a helpful Aura Spell, Goliath will re-cast the -50% outgoing Damage Aura on them.
Removing the Aura using conditional Spells such as Poison (Tier 2b) or Shu (Tier 2b) will not result in Goliath re-casting the Aura.
strategy: Attack with Myth; Goliath takes +30% from it and resists Storm 60%, everything else 30%. He is not stunable.
Round 1 he hits the whole team for 50% max health and applies a -50% outgoing damage aura for 99 rounds, so enter at full health and expect your damage halved until you clear it.
Strip the -50% aura with a conditional spell like Poison (Tier 2b) or Shu (Tier 2b). Those remove it without him recasting it.
Do not cast any helpful Aura of your own (Berserk, Empowerment). He re-applies the -50% aura on anyone who does.
Once the aura is cleared with Poison or Shu, stack blades and finish with one big Myth hit.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he deals 50% max-health damage to all wizards and applies a -50% outgoing damage aura for 99 rounds. Come in at full health; your damage is halved until you clear the aura.
Late arrivals get the same round-1 hit and aura, but present wizards are not re-hit. Enter as a group at the start.
Casting any helpful Aura (Berserk, Empowerment, and the like) makes him re-cast the -50% aura on you. Skip auras entirely.
Removing the aura with a conditional spell like Poison (Tier 2b) or Shu (Tier 2b) does NOT make him recast it. Use those to clear it safely.

name: Goofang
cheats: [Late] "Lateness is beneath us." - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second round, Goofang will cast a Balance version of Steal Pips dealing Balance Damage at the Wizard.
[Blade] "I... do not please" - Sometimes, when Goofang casts Fireblade or Iceblade, he will cheat-cast the opposite School version of that Blade.
This cheat will sometimes chain multiple times in a row.
[Damage] "How dare you strike a Sayamaeez!" - Sometimes, when Goofang casts one of the following Attack Spells, he will cheat-cast the opposite School version of the same Attack:
[Spell] "An experimental effect!" - Sometimes, when Goofang casts one of his Spells, he will cheat-cast the opposite School version of that Attack.
He may also steal Blades.
strategy: Solo-friendly (7,000 health). He mirrors his own casts into the opposite school and can steal your blades, so protect your key blade with Aegis.
He only resists Fire and Ice 15% and has no boost weakness, so hit with any off-Fire/Ice school you like.
Do not over-stack blades he can steal. Keep one Aegis-protected blade and a Feint, then hit.
He is stunable, so stun to skip a turn and deny his cheat-casts.
Enter on time (late entry costs you pips) and finish with one strong hit.
cheatNotes: Joining after round 2 gets you hit with a Balance Steal Pips.
When he casts Fireblade or Iceblade he sometimes also cheat-casts the opposite blade.
That blade cheat can chain several times in a row.
When he casts certain attacks he sometimes also cheat-casts the opposite-school version.
When he casts his spells he sometimes cheat-casts the opposite-school version.
He may also steal your blades.

name: Gorilla Spectral Guardian
cheats: This boss will cast a -50% no pip Smoke Screen if a player is marked and does not cast a charm within 2 rounds of being marked.
strategy: Small Fire boss (3,380). He takes +30% from Ice and Storm; hit with either.
His one cheat: if you are marked and do not cast a charm within two rounds, he lands a -50% Smoke Screen. After being marked, just cast any blade within two rounds to avoid it.
Blade up (which also satisfies the charm requirement), then hit with an Ice or Storm attack.
cheatNotes: If you are marked and cast no charm within two rounds, he hits you with a -50% Smoke Screen. Simply blade within the window; your setup blade doubles as the counter.

name: Gorn Hits-With-Sticks
cheats: Gorn Hits-With-Sticks

[Round] "Sticks and stones won't break these bones." - At the beginning of the first Round, or any Round Gorn doesn't have an Aura, he will cheat-cast an "Invulnerability" -300% Brace Aura for 99 Rounds.
This cheat will stop after Pork removes Gorn's Aura.
[Damage] "I shall hit you with sticks!" - Gorn will say this when he casts his Natural Attack.
Gorn's casts are scripted:
Round 1: Nature's Wrath
Round 2: Nature's Wrath (Tier 5a)
Rounds 3 & 4: Drop Bear Fury (Tier 4a)
Rounds 5 & 6: Centaur (Tier 5a)
Round 7: Forest Lord (Tier 5a)
Round 8: Natural Attack (Gorn Hits-With-Sticks)
Rounds 9 & 10: Bonetree Lord
Round 11+: Natural Attack (Gorn Hits-With-Sticks)
Captain Pork
[Minion] "But I can... with some preparation. Hold your ground, Wizard, I'll think of something." - Captain Pork will say this when he joins the battle.
[Afterlife] If Captain Pork is defeated before he removes Gorn's Aura, he will cast a version of Blessing, restoring himself to 100% Health.
Round 1 - "Wizard, I need a Life Pip, quick!":
[Pork's Request (Life)]
Round 2 - "Fire Pip incoming... right?":
[Pork's Request (Fire)]
Round 3 - "Just a little Ice Pip to top this off.":
[Pork's Request (Ice)]

"And now... try these sticks and stones, Gorn!" - When Captain Pork has a Life, Fire, and Ice Pip, he will use his turn to cast Pork's Plan, which removes an Aura from, deals 3,001 Fire Damage to, and applies a +30,000 Damage Trap on all enemies.
[Damage] If Captain Pork is defeated either on the turn he removes Gorn's Aura or afterwards, the battle will be declared lost on the turn after and the Wizard will be sent back to The Oasis.
spellNotes: The Spells in the cast list below will be used during the Rounds specified in the cheat notes regardless of the caster's Pips.
As a Monstrology Minion, Gorn can only cast his Natural Attack.
strategy: Do not try to damage Gorn. He self-casts a -300% Invulnerability Brace aura for 99 rounds and re-casts it whenever he has no aura, so your attacks do almost nothing.
This fight is won by Captain Pork, not by you. Your job is to feed him the pips he asks for so he can cast Pork's Plan, which strips Gorn's aura, deals 3,001 Fire, and traps him for +30,000 damage.
Answer Pork's requests in order: a Life Pip on Round 1, a Fire Pip on Round 2, an Ice Pip on Round 3, using the Pork's Request cards he provides. Once he holds all three he casts Pork's Plan.

Keep Captain Pork alive. If he dies before removing the aura he revives at 100% via Blessing, but if he dies on or after the turn he removes the aura, the battle is lost and you are sent back to the Oasis.
Survive Gorn's scripted casts (Nature's Wrath, Drop Bear Fury, Centaur, Forest Lord, Bonetree Lord) on their fixed rounds; his damage is on a timer regardless of pips, so shield and heal through it.
After Pork's Plan lands the aura is gone and the +30,000 trap is up. Then any hit ends Gorn; he is Life with 71% Life resist, so swing with a boosted school (Death) into that trap.
cheatNotes: On Round 1, or any round he has no aura, Gorn self-casts a -300% Invulnerability Brace for 99 rounds, making him effectively immune to your damage until the aura is removed.
The invulnerability cheat only stops once Captain Pork strips Gorn's aura, which is the real win condition.
This line just flags his natural attack; expect scripted damage on the timer below.
When Pork joins he tells you he needs preparation; you supply the pips he requests over the next rounds.
If Pork dies before he removes the aura, he revives himself to 100% with Blessing, so an early death is recoverable.
On Round 1 Pork asks for a Life Pip; give it using the Pork's Request (Life) card.
On Round 2 Pork asks for a Fire Pip; give it using the Pork's Request (Fire) card.
On Round 3 Pork asks for an Ice Pip; give it using the Pork's Request (Ice) card.
Once Pork holds a Life, Fire, and Ice Pip he casts Pork's Plan: it removes Gorn's aura, deals 3,001 Fire, and lays a +30,000 damage Trap on all enemies. Hit Gorn right after.
If Pork dies on the turn he removes the aura or any turn after, the fight is declared lost the next turn, so protect him hardest at the finish.

name: Gourmand Ramsey
cheats: [Round] On Round 1, Gourmand Ramsey starts the Round by casting a Shadow Spell, forcing all Wizards to pass.
[Minion] "Does this chicken look cooked to you?!?" - Uses a Shadow Spell to summon Apollo Bright One (Rank 21).
Apollo Bright One uses an Intercept 100% Damage Shadow Sentinel.
[Spell] "You've used so much thyme, The Professor arrived in the Telegraph Box to save us all!" - Casts a Shadow Spell that does ???

[Charm][Trap][Global] "There's enough garlic in here to defeat every Vampire in Darkmoor!"- If a Wizard uses a positive Charm, Trap, or Global, Ramsey will casts a Shadow version of Monster Mash that removes 25% of the Wizard's max Health on the Wizard with the lowest health, and deals 1 Shadow Damage to self.
(Because this Spell removes max Health, this will ignore any Wards or Traps on the targeted Wizard.)
For Charms, the Monster Mash will be cast on the receiver of the Charm.
Spells such as Volcanic Golem will trigger this cheat.
The following Spells do not trigger this cheat.
AoE traps, such as Mass Feint and Mass Fire Trap
Power Play
Frostfeather
King Artorius
Rebirth
Attenuate
Frenzy
strategy: Expect to pass Round 1; his opening Shadow spell forces all wizards to pass.
Most positive Charms, Traps, and Globals trigger a Shadow Monster Mash that strips 25% max Health from your lowest wizard and ignores wards, so buff with the safe options only.
Use the exempt spells to set up: AoE traps like Mass Feint and Mass Fire Trap, Power Play, Frostfeather, King Artorius, Rebirth, Attenuate, and Frenzy do not trigger the cheat.
Apollo Bright One intercepts with a 100% Shadow Sentinel, so clear or play around the Sentinel before your real hit lands on Ramsey.
Ramsey resists everything 40% except Ice (10% more), so hit with Ice and burst him after your safe setup.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 he casts a Shadow spell that forces everyone to pass, so you lose the opening Round.
He summons Apollo Bright One with a Shadow spell.
Apollo uses a 100% Damage Shadow Sentinel to intercept your attacks, soaking hits meant for Ramsey.
He casts an unknown Shadow spell tied to 'The Professor' flavor; the effect is undocumented.
Cast a positive Charm, Trap, or Global and he answers with a Shadow Monster Mash that removes 25% max Health from your lowest-Health wizard and deals 1 Shadow to himself.
Because it removes max Health, that Monster Mash ignores any wards or traps on the target.
For Charms, the Monster Mash lands on whoever received the Charm.
Even spells with secondary buffs, like Volcanic Golem, set off this cheat.
A handful of spells are exempt and will not trigger the Monster Mash.

name: Grandfather Spider
cheats: "You're late... or are you early?" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Grandfather Spider will interrupt-cast a Shadow version of Insane Bolt on the Wizard, dealing 2,000 Shadow Damage and removing 3 pips.

"You will not defeat me!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Grandfather Spider will summon a Spiderling Minion. The Minion will become active on the next Round and interrupt-cast a permanent version of Shadow Sentinel which provides Intercept 90%, +80 Critical Block, and -30% Incoming Damage.
If the Spiderling is defeated, then Grandfather Spider will summon another one three Rounds later.
Only one Spiderling can be up at a time.

Grandfather Spider will only summon as many Spiderlings as there are Wizards engaged in the duel. Once that number of Spiderlings has been defeated, he will unsuccessfully attempt to summon a Minion every three Rounds.
"Spin the wheel! I'm enjoying this!" - At the end of every third Round, Grandfather Spider will interrupt-cast a Spell that switches the first turn of the duel.

"Time, time, time, see what's become of me" - At the end of a number of Rounds dependent on the number of Wizards engaged in the duel, Grandfather Spider will interrupt-cast a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that steals all positive charms and wards from all Wizards.
Every 8 Rounds with 1 Wizard present.
Every 7 Rounds with 2 Wizards present.
Every 6 Rounds with 3 Wizards present.
Every 5 Rounds with 4 Wizards present.
"Surprised? So am I!" - Grandfather Spider will use his Natural Attack on a random Wizard and then cast Healing Current on himself.
"You had your chance, I'm ending this" - Grandfather Spider will cast an Iron Sultan.
"That's the spirit!" - If a Feint is placed on Grandfather Spider, then he will cast Swap Ward to switch the traps with the Wizard who cast it.
This can be avoided with Indemnity or Melt.
"Time needs chaos! One direction is boring!" - If a Bad Juju is placed on Grandfather Spider, then he will interrupt-cast Sacrifice on himself.
spellNotes: Casts an Aegis-protected Shadowblade, Stormblade (+30%), and Reaperblade.
Reaperblade incorrectly states +35% Storm Damage on both Blades.
Casts an Indemnity-protected Windstorm (+25%), and Mass Feint (+70%/+30%).
Deals 110 Shadow Damage per Pip, despite the Spell card's 3-Pip maximum description.
strategy: Coordinate entry so everyone is in by Round 2. Any wizard who joins after Round 2 is interrupt-hit by a Shadow Insane Bolt for 2,000 damage and loses 3 pips. Have your slowest member port out and Dungeon Recall to control battle order.

Kill the Spiderling first. On Round 2 it casts a permanent Shadow Sentinel that intercepts 90% of your damage to the boss and gives it -30% incoming, so hits barely land until the minion is gone. Stack Feints on the Spiderling and finish it with a 4-5 pip spell.

Beat the Feint-swap with Melt. Feinting the boss makes him cast Swap Ward, flipping your 70% trap onto you as a weak one. Cast Melt first to strip a shield, or use an Indemnity Feint or item/pet/TC Feint - none of those get swapped.

Run the universal 5-round setup: R1 melt/melt/trap/trap (add a prism if you are Storm), R2 Feint the minion x3 and kill it, R3 feint/feint/trap/trap, R4 Indemnity Feint then blades, R5 blades then main hit.

Blade and hit before Dimension Shift fires. He steals every positive charm and ward from all wizards every 8/7/6/5 rounds for 1/2/3/4 wizards. Time your buff-and-hit to land inside that window so the steal grabs nothing.

Pierce his resistances. He resists Storm 40% and Fire/Ice/Life/Death/Balance 20%, but boosts Myth 10%. Attack with Myth if you can, otherwise add Infallible to punch through. Bring Doom/infection because his 'Surprised?' cheat self-heals with Healing Current.

Do not place Bad Juju on him - it makes him interrupt-cast Sacrifice on himself. Keep your setup to blades, traps, and Feints (via Melt) and end the fight in one big AoE so the Spiderling dies with him.
cheatNotes: Latecomers are interrupt-hit by a Shadow Insane Bolt for 2,000 damage and lose 3 pips, so make sure everyone is in the duel by the end of Round 2.

On Round 1 he summons a Spiderling that becomes active Round 2 and casts a permanent Shadow Sentinel (90% intercept, +80 block, -30% incoming), which is why the minion must die before you try to hit the boss.
Kill the Spiderling and he simply summons a replacement three rounds later, so plan to clear it early rather than repeatedly.
Only one Spiderling exists at a time, so you never have to deal with more than a single active minion.
He only summons as many total Spiderlings as there are wizards (four total in a full team); once that many are dead, his summon attempts fail every three rounds.
Every third round he swaps who goes first, so a plan that relies on hitting before or after him can flip - account for both orders.
His Shadow Dimension Shift strips all positive charms and wards from the whole team, so you must blade and hit in the same window rather than banking buffs across rounds.
With one wizard the steal fires every 8 rounds.
With two wizards the steal fires every 7 rounds.
With three wizards the steal fires every 6 rounds.
With four wizards the steal fires every 5 rounds, the tightest window, so a full team must burst fast.
His 'Surprised?' cheat hits a random wizard with his Natural Attack and then heals himself with Healing Current, so bring Doom/Gloom or an infection if the fight drags.
When he says 'You had your chance,' he casts Iron Sultan as a large attack - be topped up on health going into long fights.
Placing a Feint on him triggers Swap Ward, moving your trap value onto you, so never Feint him raw.
You can avoid the Swap-Ward by using an Indemnity Feint or by casting Melt first, since Melt-protected traps and indemnified Feints cannot be swapped.
Placing Bad Juju on him makes him interrupt-cast Sacrifice on himself, so keep Bad Juju out of your deck for this fight.

name: Grandfather Spider (Challenge)
cheats: Shadowbones
[Ward] "Tag... You're it!. - At the beginning of the first Round and every Round after his Mark has been removed, Shadowbones will mark a random Wizard with a Stun Block named "Tag. I'm it!".
[Damage] "You're too fast!" - If a Marked Wizard damages Shadowbones with a single-target Spell on the next turn, he will remove the Mark.
[Damage][All Enemies (Squared)] "They're not playing!" - If an All Enemies Spell is used, Shadowbones will restore himself to full Health and give a Shadow Pip to Grandfather Spider.
[Late] "Too slow." - At the end of the Round after a Mark is cast, if the Marked Wizard failed to damage Shadowbones, he will remove the Mark and cast Lord of Winter (Tier 5a) on them.
"You're not it!" - If a Wizard that isn't Marked targets Shadowbones, he will restore himself to full Health.

"If you're not going to play..." - If Shadowbones is defeated by a Wizard that isn't Marked, he will restore himself to full Health, cast Scarecrow (Tier 4a), and give a Shadow Pip to Grandfather Spider.
Bort Shadowmane
[Shield] "Check out my armor!" - At the beginning of the first Round and two Rounds after his Shields removed, Bort will cast a -80% Damage Ward of a random School on himself.
"You broke my armor, lucky guess!" - If Bort's Shields are removed, he will say this.
"Poor showmanship" - If Bort is defeated while he has a Shield, he will restore himself to full Health, cast Elemental Shield or Spirit Shield on himself, and give a Shadow Pip to Grandfather Spider.
Shadowwock
[Aura] - At the beginning of the first Round and the next Round after the Screeching Crimson Aura is cast, Shadowwock will cast a green Verdant Action Aura on itself.
[Aura] - At the beginning of a random Round, Shadowwock will cast a yellow Golden Caution Aura on itself.
[Aura] - At the beginning of the Round after the Golden Caution Aura is cast, Shadowwock will cast a red Screeching Crimson Aura on itself.

"You lose" - If any enemies take Damage while the Screeching Crimson Aura is active, Shadowwock will restore itself to full Health, cast Fire Dragon (Tier 5a), and give a Shadow Pip to Grandfather Spider.
Grandfather Spider
[Shadow Pip] - At the end of every Round, Grandfather Spider will gain a Shadow Pip.
"Be patient, you have other playmates." - If Grandfather Spider is damaged or targeted before his Minions have been defeated, he will restore himself to full Health.
"Spin the wheel! I'm enjoying this!" - At the end of every third Round, Grandfather Spider will cast a Spell that switches the first turn of the duel.
As Challenge Mode uses a snaking turn order, this does nothing.

"Oh this has looked like fun! My turn!" - At the beginning of the Round after his Minions have been defeated, Grandfather Spider will restore himself to full Health and gain the cheats of two of his Minions at random from the start of the next turn.
spellNotes: Casts a protected version of Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).
strategy: Team fight and a kill-order puzzle. All three minions must die before you touch Grandfather Spider, who fully heals if damaged or even targeted early.
Handle Shadowbones with the tagged Wizard only. Whoever holds the 'Tag, I'm it!' mark hits him single-target each Round and lands the kill. Never AoE him.
Strip Bort Shadowmane's -80% shield before killing him, or he revives and feeds the Spider a Shadow Pip.
Watch Shadowwock's auras. Green and yellow are safe, but deal zero damage the Round his red Screeching Crimson aura is up.
Line the minions up to die the same Round if you can, since the Spider gains a Shadow Pip every Round and steals two minion cheats once they fall.
The Round the minions die, unload your fully-stacked hitter on the Spider before his heal and new cheats matter.
cheatNotes: Shadowbones marks a random Wizard with a 'Tag, I'm it!' Stun Block and re-marks whenever it is gone. Only the marked Wizard can safely fight him.
The marked Wizard must hit Shadowbones with a single-target spell to remove the mark. Do that every time you are tagged.
Never use an AoE on Shadowbones. Any all-enemies spell fully heals him and feeds Grandfather Spider a Shadow Pip.
If the marked Wizard fails to damage Shadowbones that Round, the mark comes off and they eat Lord of Winter.
If an unmarked Wizard targets Shadowbones, he heals to full. Only the tagged player touches him.
If an unmarked Wizard lands the kill, he revives full, casts Scarecrow, and feeds the Spider a pip. The marked Wizard must be the one to finish him.
Bort shields himself with a random-school -80% ward on Round 1 and two Rounds after each shield is removed.
If Bort is defeated while shielded, he revives full and feeds the Spider a pip. Strip his shield before the killing blow.
Shadowwock puts a green Verdant aura on itself on Round 1 and again the Round after each red aura.
At a random Round it swaps to a yellow Golden Caution aura, your warning.
The Round after the yellow aura, it goes red (Screeching Crimson).
Do not deal any damage while the red aura is up. Any enemy taking damage revives Shadowwock full, casts Fire Dragon, and feeds the Spider a pip.
Grandfather Spider gains a Shadow Pip every Round, so the fight has a clock.
Damaging or even targeting the Spider before all three minions are dead fully heals him. Clear minions first.
Every third Round he tries to switch first turn.
Once all minions are dead he heals to full and steals two random minion cheats, so the kill on him must come fast the Round after the minions fall.

name: Grandmother
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Grandma's House
cheats: "Let's teach you some manners!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Grandmother will Polymorph the Wizard into Grandmother.

"The curse spreads..." - Starting at the end of Round 1, Grandmother will Polymorph the Wizard into Grandmother for four Rounds with 7,500 Health, giving them the following crazy Spells they can cast while also confusing them.
Beguile can prevent this cheat.
Polymorph Spells include:
Curse of Grandma - Spell triggers Grandmother to Polymorph the Wizard.
Knitted Sweater - Stuns target for one Round.
Amigurumi - Restores 1,500 Health to self, and -90% Weakness to target.
Overfeed - Deals an unknown Shadow Damage over three Rounds.
Smooches! - Deals an unknown Shadow Damage to target.
Shiny Nickel - Deals an unknown Shadow Damage to target and -25% Weakness to target.
"Yes" - Will say this if a Wizard casts Curse of Grandma Spell, which resets the Polymorph onto a different Wizard, polymorphing and confusing them.
If Spell is cast on someone already Polymorphed, it will reset the turns the Polymorph lasts, while not confusing them, unless the Polymorph is on the last turn.
spellNotes: Deals 420 Shadow Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: This is a two-stage fight: clear the Big Bad Wolf (~4,780 HP) first, then Grandmother, a rank 17 Shadow boss.

She resists 70% to nearly every school and only takes a slight boost from Life and Balance, and has just 45% incoming healing, so prism into Life/Balance or bring reliable hits and don't count on out-healing her.
She is beguilable: Beguile shuts off the polymorph cheat entirely. Otherwise plan around it, since from the end of Round 1 she turns one wizard into a confused Grandmother for four rounds.

When polymorphed solo, cast Knitted Sweater (it only ever stuns Grandmother, never you) or Amigurumi to heal 1,500; avoid the self-damaging cards and carry a Cleanse Charm to strip the Bad Juju weakness Amigurumi leaves.
In a team, the polymorphed player should spam Curse of Grandma every turn to just pass the curse along while the rest keep blading and Feinting the boss normally.
Otherwise play the standard loop: stack blades, Feint, then hit. She mostly passes and casts predictable Storm's Iron Giant and Glowbug Squall, so she is not threatening between polymorphs.
cheatNotes: Join before the duel starts; late arrivals get polymorphed into a Grandmother copy right away.
From the end of Round 1 she polymorphs one wizard into a confused Grandmother (7,500 HP) for four rounds, handing them a special junk deck.
Beguiling her prevents the polymorph cheat entirely, and she is beguilable, so this is a clean counter.
Curse of Grandma makes her re-polymorph a wizard. As the polymorphed player, spamming it just passes the curse instead of hurting teammates.
Knitted Sweater stuns for one round, and cast while polymorphed it only ever stuns Grandmother, never you, making it the safe pick.
Amigurumi heals 1,500 to self but applies a -90% Weakness (Bad Juju) to the target, so keep a Cleanse Charm ready.
Overfeed lays a three-round Shadow poison DoT on a target.
Smooches deals Shadow damage to a target.
Shiny Nickel deals Shadow damage plus a -25% Weakness to the target.
If a wizard casts Curse of Grandma she says 'Yes' and resets the polymorph and confusion onto a different wizard.
Cast on an already-polymorphed wizard it refreshes the polymorph's duration without confusing them, unless it lands on the last turn.

name: Gray Man Golem (Challenge)
cheats: [Manipulation] "You cannot kill the undying" - At the beginning of the first round, each Gray Man Golem will receive a Guardian Spirit effect

[Round] "Magic mine... your power... mine!" - At the end of each round, if there is at least one wizard with a Positive Charm, a Gray Man Golem will target a random wizard and receive a number of Power Pips depending on the amount of Positive Charms.
1 or 2 charms = 1 Power Pip
3 charms = 3 Power Pips
4+ charms = 4 Power Pips

[Round] "Pain... makes... power!" - At the beginning of each round, if a Gray Man Golem is not at full health, it will cast a special version of Bladestorm that increases in value the lower his health is.
The minimum blade value is 20% and the maximum blade value is 100%. If the blade is 100%, it will also be Protected.

If there are multiple Gray Man Golems under full health, the value of the blade will be based on the first one in the duel circle. This cheat will only activate one per round, regardless of how many Gray Man Golems are in the battle.
Further testing is required to determine blade values between the minimum and maximum and their corresponding HP thresholds.
strategy: This is a multi-golem Darkmoor Challenge fight - bring a team with an AoE hitter, since each golem gets Guardian Spirit and revives once on death.
Hit Life. The golems take boosted Life damage and resist Death, so Life is your damage school.
Minimize positive charms. At the end of each round, if any wizard holds a positive charm, a golem steals power pips - more charms means more pips fed to them.

Because a golem's self-blade grows as its health drops (up to a protected 100% Bladestorm near death), do not leave one lingering at low health. Plan an AoE that finishes them together rather than chipping.
Set up with Feints and traps (not blades) to avoid the pip-steal, then land one big Life AoE. Account for Guardian Spirit - be ready to hit again to put them down past the revive.
cheatNotes: On round 1 each Gray Man Golem gains Guardian Spirit, so it revives once when defeated. Plan to finish them past that.
At the end of each round, if any wizard holds a positive charm, a golem steals power pips from a random wizard - more charms steal more pips.
While below full health, each round a golem self-casts a Bladestorm that grows the lower its health falls.
The self-blade ranges from 20% up to 100%, and a 100% blade is also protected - so a low-health golem gets very dangerous.
Only one golem blades per round, based on the health of the first golem in the circle.

name: Gray Man Golem (Standard)

stats.resist: 49% to all schools79% to all schools

cheats: [Manipulation] "You cannot kill the undying" - At the beginning of the first Round, each Gray Man Golem will cast a Death version of Guardian Spirit that revives themself with 15% Health.

[Round] "Magic mine... your power... mine!" - At the end of each Round, if there is at least one wizard with a helpful Charm, a Gray Man Golem will target a random Wizard and receive a number of Power Pips depending on the amount of helpful Charms.
1 Charm = 1 Power Pip
2 Charms = 2 Power Pip
3 Charms = 3 Power Pips
4+ Charms = 4 Power Pips

[Health] "Pain... makes... power!" - At the beginning of each Round, if a Gray Man Golem is not at full Health, it will cast a special version of Bladestorm that increases in value the lower its Health is.
The minimum Blade value is +20% and the maximum Blade value is +100%. If the Blade is +100%, it will also be protected.

If there are multiple Gray Man Golems under full Health, the value of the Blade will be based on the first Golem in the duel circle. This cheat will only activate one per Round, regardless of how many Gray Man Golems are in the battle.
Further testing is required to determine Blade values between the minimum and maximum and their corresponding Health thresholds.
strategy: Team fight (~26,000 health, multiple Golems): one hitter, supports who funnel blades late, and a healer to manage the Life burst.
Hit with Life. The Golems resist Death 79% and take +30% Life.
Each Golem self-revives once with Guardian Spirit on Round 1. Plan to kill each Golem, then finish the revived 15% remainder. Do not stop at the first kill.
Do not leave helpful Charms up between rounds. Any Wizard holding blades feeds the Golems Power Pips (up to 4) at the end of the Round. Blade and hit in the same round.
A wounded Golem casts a scaling Bladestorm each Round, stronger the lower its health. Burst each Golem down fast rather than chipping it.
cheatNotes: Each Golem casts Guardian Spirit on Round 1, self-reviving once at 15% health. Kill through the revive.
At the end of each Round, if any Wizard holds helpful Charms, a Golem steals Power Pips scaled to the charm count. Blade and hit the same round.
One held Charm feeds a Golem one Power Pip.
Two held Charms feed two Power Pips.
Three held Charms feed three Power Pips.
Four or more held Charms feed the maximum four Power Pips.
A Golem below full health casts a scaling Bladestorm each Round, stronger the lower its health. Burst it fast instead of chipping.
The self-buff ranges from +20% at high health to +100% at low health, and the +100% version is protected.
With several wounded Golems, the blade uses the first Golem's health and fires only once per Round.

name: Gretta Darkkettle
locations: Wizard City: Firecat Alley - Cabal Safehouse
cheats: "You too? What a treat!" - If a Wizard joins after the battle has started, she will cast both a Curse (+25%) and a Hex (+35%), on the Wizard who joined late.

"You could have been one of us!" - In the first Round (and every third Round thereafter), she will cast Beguile on a random target (also includes Minions). This cheat only activates when two or more positions are filled in the duel.
If three or more Wizards are present, this cheat will trigger every Round.
"What is this, amateur hour?" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Gretta Darkkettle, she will cast Sacrifice (Death) to remove it.
Mass Feint (+75%) will trigger this cheat.
"Feel the power of the Cabal!" - At the end of every fifth Round, she will cast a Mass Infection.
spellNotes: Her Black Cat Pet may cast either Black Cat (80-120) or Black Cat (660-700).
strategy: Use a hard-hitting off-Death school, she is Death with no listed weakness.
Both Feint and Mass Feint make her Sacrifice to remove them, so protect your Feint with Indemnity, or skip traps and stack blades instead.
Solo, her Beguile cheat is dormant since it needs two or more filled positions, so a solo run avoids the mind-control entirely.
She casts Mass Infection at the end of every fifth round, halving your healing, cleanse it or time heals off that round.
Set up two to three rounds with blades (Indemnity-Feint if used), then land one big hit.
cheatNotes: Join late and she hits you with a +25% Curse and a +35% Hex.
From Round 1 and every third round she Beguiles a random target, but only when two or more positions are filled, solo this never fires.
With three or more wizards the Beguile fires every round, stay aware of mind-control in a full group.
Feint her and she Sacrifices to strip it, protect it with Indemnity.
Mass Feint also triggers the Sacrifice, so even the AoE trap gets removed unless protected.
She casts Mass Infection at the end of every fifth round, cutting your healing in half, cleanse before you heal.

name: Grisletusk
cheats: "A Charm! Let's even the playing field!" - If a Wizard casts a Charm Spell, then Grisletusk will interrupt-cast a +35% version of Hex on them.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
Pet Maycast Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A Ward! Let's even the playing field!" - If a Wizard casts a Ward Spell, then Grisletusk will interrupt-cast a +35% version of Hex on them.
strategy: Low health (3580), so this is a fast kill. Skip blades and shields; every charm or ward you cast makes him Hex you for +35% incoming damage.
Set up with traps instead. Feint and other traps do not trigger the cheat, so trap him and hit.
Hit with Fire or Death (he takes +35% from both, resists Life 80%).
Globals also trigger the Hex, so avoid global buffs; a couple of traps plus one hit ends it.
cheatNotes: Casting any charm, including blades, makes him Hex you for +35% incoming damage; set up with traps instead of blades.
Global spells also trigger the Hex, so skip global buffs.
Casting any ward or shield also triggers the +35% Hex on you, so do not shield up here.

name: Grizzlebort the Unsociable
locations: Krokotopia: Marketplace of Ideas - Den of the Storyteller
cheats: [Curse] "All are weak against Grizzlebort!" – At the beginning of every Round, Grizzlebort interrupt-casts Plague.

"Ha-ha! Puny human!" - Grizzlebort casts Plague after a Wizard casts a Spell, even if they fizzle. He will cast it twice if both he and Grizzletroll are hit by the Spell. He will also cast it on a Wizard every Round they have a Damage over Time effect on them.
Pet casts will trigger this cheat.
Shields, and Spells that create multiple Charms or Traps, like Elemental Blade will not trigger this cheat.
[Healing] If Grizzlebort is defeated while his Plague cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.

[Spell] "Sticks and stones may break my bones, but something, something" – At the beginning of the fourth Round (after spells are chosen), or when he revives, Grizzlebort will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Stone Colossus (Tier 2b)]
[Myth] "I feel woozy. Where did that mountain come from?" - When hit by Stone Colossus (Tier 2b), Grizzlebort will remove all harmful Charms from Wizards, and stop his Plague cheat.

"Wait... you aren't supposed to do that!" - If a Wizard casts another Myth Damage Spell that removes harmful Charms on Grizzlebort, such as King Artorius (Myth) (Tier 2b), he will remove all harmful Charms from Wizards, and stop his Plague cheat.
strategy: He interrupt-casts Plague every round and again whenever you cast anything (even fizzles and pet casts), so expect constant Weakness. Shields and multi-charm spells like Elemental Blade do not trigger the per-cast Plague.
Do not kill him while his Plague cheat is active; he revives at full health. You must shut the cheat off first.
At round 4 he hands every wizard Stone Colossus (Tier 2b). Cast it back on him to strip your Plagues and stop the Plague cheat.
Any Myth damage spell that removes harmful charms (like King Artorius Myth Tier 2b) works the same way if you have it.
With the cheat off, blade up and finish him; he takes +35% from Ice and Myth and resists Life and Storm 35%.
cheatNotes: At the start of every round he interrupt-casts Plague, blanketing wizards in Weakness.
He also Plagues you after every spell you cast, even on a fizzle, twice if the spell hits both him and Grizzletroll, and each round you carry a DoT.
Pet casts count as casts and trigger the Plague too.
Shields and spells that create multiple charms or traps at once, like Elemental Blade, do not trigger the per-cast Plague.
If you defeat him while his Plague cheat is still running, he revives at full health.
At round 4 (or when he revives) he gives every wizard a Stone Colossus (Tier 2b) spell to use.
Hitting him with Stone Colossus (Tier 2b) makes him strip all harmful charms from wizards and stop the Plague cheat.
Any other Myth damage spell that removes harmful charms, like King Artorius Myth (Tier 2b), does the same.

name: Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet - Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: "Oh no, ye found me one weakness: showing up late." If a Wizard is late to combat, he'll cast a Moon Spell giving all Wizards Banish! (7 Pip).
[Banish! (7 Pip)]
This spell seems to be automatically cast when selected rather than targeting.
"Ach! I cannae be beaten, only banished! - " After the end of the first Round, Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 1) will give Wizards Banish! (7 Pip).
"Ach! Get 'Em, Bitey!" - If Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 1) is attacked, he will interrupt-cast Orthrus.
"Hide behind yer shields then!" - If a Wizard casts a Shield, Boss will cast Guidance on all Wizards.
This will only trigger once per Round.
"Time for a controlled Mana Burn" - Boss will cast an all enemies version of Mana Burn (Shadow) that also removes 1 Shadow Pip.
This spell will be cast repeatedly.
"Think ye can banish me? Try!" - Boss will say this line after he uses Mana Burn.
strategy: He is immune to globals, so skip Doom and Gloom or global buffs; they will not stick.
He repeatedly casts an all-team Mana Burn (Shadow) that also strips a Shadow Pip, so do not hoard pips - build blades and hit before he burns them off.
Attacking him makes him interrupt-cast Orthrus back at the team, so expect a counter every time you commit a hit.
Shielding is fine and even helpful - it makes him cast Guidance (accuracy) on all wizards - but that response only fires once per round.
At 4,500 health with no listed resist, stack a couple of blades plus Feint and end it in one hit before the pip drain and Banish cards pile up.
cheatNotes: Join late and he hands the whole team a Banish! (7 Pip) card - arrive on time to skip it.
The Banish card auto-fires when picked rather than letting you aim it.
After Round 1 he gives every wizard a Banish! (7 Pip) card regardless, so expect it in your hand.
Any attack on him makes him interrupt-cast Orthrus at the team.
If a wizard shields, he casts Guidance (accuracy buff) on all wizards - harmless to you.
That Guidance response only fires once per round.
He repeatedly casts an all-team Mana Burn (Shadow) that also strips one Shadow Pip, so do not sit on pips.
Expect the Mana Burn to keep coming round after round.

name: Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet - Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: [Late] "Oh no, ye found me one weakness: showing up late." If a Wizard is late to combat, Grounds-Creeper Willie will cast a Shadow Spell giving all Wizards Banish! (1 Shadow Pip).
[Banish! (1 Shadow Pip)]
[Spell] "Ach! I cannae be beaten, only banished! - " After the end of the first Round, Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 2) will give Wizards Banish! (1 Shadow Pip).
[Damage] "Ach! Get 'Em, Bitey!" - If Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 2) is attacked, he will interrupt-cast Orthrus.
[Healing] "Oh, Wizard need a heal? Maybe a blankie too?" - If a Wizard heals, Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 2) will cast a Shadow Spell giving Wizards Donate Shadow (3 Pip).
[Donate Shadow (3 Pip, Given)]
[Manipulation] "Time for a controlled Mana Burn" - Grounds-Creeper Willie will cast an all enemies version of Mana Burn (Shadow) that also removes 1 Shadow Pip.
"Think ye can banish me? Try!" - Grounds-Creeper Willie will say this line after he uses Mana Burn.
strategy: Every time he is attacked he interrupt-casts Orthrus back on you, so avoid chip damage - set up fully and end it in as few hits as possible.
Do not join late; latecomers get handed Banish cards, and he also hands out Banish after round 1.
He is immune to globals, so do not waste a turn on Sun/Balance global buffs.
His Mana Burn hits all wizards and strips a Shadow Pip, so do not hoard shadow pips waiting to spend them.
Stack blades and a Feint, heal or shield for the Orthrus counter, then land one large hit.
cheatNotes: Join late and he gives every wizard a Banish card (costs a shadow pip to use). Enter on time.
After round 1 he hands wizards a Banish card - a shadow-pip spell dropped into your hand.
Whenever you damage him he interrupt-casts Orthrus back on you. Hit big and rarely rather than chipping.
If a wizard heals, he gives wizards Donate Shadow (grants shadow pips) - harmless to you.
He casts an all-wizards Mana Burn that also removes a Shadow Pip. Spend pips before he burns them.

name: Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet - Unforgiven Dead Gauntlet (Tier 3)
cheats: Oh, ye found me one weakness: showing up late! If a Wizard is late, or flees and returns, the Boss will give Wizards the following spell.
[Banish! (2 Shadow Pip)]
"Ach! I cannae be beaten, only banished! - " After the end of the first Round, Grounds-Creeper Willie (Tier 3) will give Wizards Banish!.
"Ach! Get 'Em, Bitey!" - If he is attacked, he will interrupt-cast Orthrus.
"Hide behind yer shields then!" - If a Wizard casts a Shield, he will give a +25% version of Guidance to the Wizard's team.
"Oh, wizard need a heal? Maybe a blankie too?" - If the Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will give the Wizard the following spell.
Notes: Only the Wizard receiving the Heal is the one that will receive the Donate Shadow Spell and Maycast Healing Pets don't activate this cheat.
[Donate Shadow (3 Pip, Given)]
"Time for a controlled Mana Burn" - At the beginning of every third Round, he will cast an All Enemies version of Mana Burn (Shadow) that also removes 1 Shadow Pip.
"Think ye can banish me? Try!" - Boss will say this line after he uses Mana Burn.
strategy: He is immune to Globals, so do not waste turns on Auras or Globals like Doom and Gloom; they will not stick.
Do not shield or heal casually. Shielding makes him hand your team a +25% Guidance (a wasted trigger), and healing gives the healed wizard a Donate Shadow card.
Every third Round he casts an all-enemy Mana Burn that also strips a shadow pip, so spend pips before that Round rather than banking them.
He is Myth with 50% armor piercing and 150% damage; keep resist up and a heal ready.
Attacking him makes him interrupt with Orthrus, so expect a counter-hit when you strike.
Stack blades and Feint, then land one big hit timed around his Round-three Mana Burn so you are not caught pip-starved.
cheatNotes: Arriving late or fleeing and returning hands you a Banish! card; just enter and stay.
After Round one he gives everyone a Banish! spell; it is his own gimmick, not a threat.
Hitting him triggers an interrupt Orthrus back at you; keep a heal or shield ready when you attack.
Casting a Shield makes him give your team +25% Guidance; it wastes the trigger but does no harm, so shield only when needed.
Healing makes him hand the healed wizard a Donate Shadow card; heal deliberately.
Every third Round he Mana Burns the whole team and strips a shadow pip; spend your pips before that Round so you lose nothing.

name: Guaman Skyfall
locations: Azteca: Floating Mountains - Seven Stabs of the Knife Temple
cheats: "Trying to soften me up?" - If a Wizard casts a Wand attack at Guaman, he will place a Stun Shield "mark" on the Wizard, indicating it is safe to attack.

If a Wizard has been marked and casts a single-target attack Spell at Guaman (excluding Wand attacks), he will remove the Stun Shield mark and all other Wards. Another Wand attack must be used in order to obtain the mark again.
Only one mark can be present on a Wizard at any given time.

"Your wand might help... next time!" - If a Wizard has not been "marked" with a Stun Shield and casts a single-target attack Spell at Guaman (excluding Wand attacks), he will cheat-cast a 0-Pip enhanced version of Leviathan, which deals 2,030 Storm Damage.
All Enemy Spells, such as Scarecrow and Humongofrog, will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: High-health Storm boss (18040). Hit with Myth or Life (+35%); never Storm (80% resist).
Easiest line: hit him with All-Enemies (AoE) attacks. The Leviathan punish and the wand-mark gate only apply to single-target attacks, so an AoE finisher skips the whole mechanic.
If you do want single-target damage, first land a Wand attack to earn the Stun Shield mark, then attack. An unmarked single-target hit eats a 2030 Storm Leviathan.
A single-target hit also strips the mark and all your wards, so you would have to re-wand each time. Prefer AoE.
Stack Myth or Life blades and traps, then finish with a big AoE hit.
cheatNotes: Landing a Wand attack marks you as safe to hit him with single-target spells. Wand first if you plan to go single-target.
A single-target hit spends the mark and strips all your other wards, so you must re-wand before your next single-target attack. AoE avoids this.
Hit him single-target without a mark and he counters with a 2030 Storm Leviathan. Either wand-mark first, or just use AoE attacks, which never trigger it.
Enemy-cast spells like Scarecrow and Humongofrog do not trigger the punish, and neither do AoE attacks. An all-enemies hit sidesteps the wand mechanic entirely.

name: Guardian of Life
cheats: "Life will persevere." - At the beginning of Round 1, Guardian of Life will cast a Life Spell that restores 10 Health over 10 Rounds to self, and then casts an Equalize Global which sets the Damage cap to 1.

"I survive!" - If Guardian of Life is defeated while the Equalize Global is active, Guardian of Life will cast a version of Blessing, restoring 1 Health, and then restores 10 Health over 10 Rounds to self.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Major Vibrato that deals 5% max Health.
Casts a version of Leprechaun that deals 20% max Health.
Casts a version of Nature's Wrath that deals 30% max Health.
strategy: On round 1 it sets an Equalize Global capping all damage to 1 and starts regenerating about 1 health per round, so brute force barely works and it revives if you kill it under the Equalize.
Replace the Equalize Global with your own Global spell first. That lifts the damage cap so a normal hit ends it, and the I survive revive (which only triggers while Equalize is active) never fires.
If you cannot overwrite the Global, out-pace the regen: with the cap at 1, several wizards each landing a 1-damage hit per round beats its ~1-per-round heal.
Watch its percent-max-health attacks (Leprechaun ~20%, Nature's Wrath ~30%) and keep the group healed.
Coordinate the kill so it happens after the Global is replaced.
cheatNotes: Round 1 it heals 10 over ten rounds and drops an Equalize capping damage to 1. Overwrite that Global with your own so you can actually deal damage.
If you kill it while the Equalize is still up, it revives at 1 health and heals again. Replace the Global before the killing blow.

name: Guardian of Myth
cheats: "Stories last forever." - At the beginning of Round 1, Guardian of Myth will cast a Myth Spell to place a Myth Prism on a random Wizard in combat 8 times, and then cast an Invulnerability Aura on itself.
strategy: It has only 100 health but shields itself with an Invulnerability Aura on round 1, so raw damage does nothing until that aura is gone.
Overwrite or remove its Invulnerability Aura first (cast your own aura over it, or use an aura-stripping effect like Supernova).
Once the aura is gone, any small hit finishes its 100 health.
The 8 Myth Prisms it stacks on a wizard convert Myth damage, so a Myth hit into a prism can be the intended finisher once the invuln is off.
cheatNotes: On round 1 it stacks 8 Myth Prisms on one wizard and gives itself an Invulnerability Aura. Overwrite or remove that aura before attacking — until then its 100 health is untouchable.

name: Guardian of Storm
cheats: "I Am The Forever Storm!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Guardian of Storm will cast 8 Guiding Light blades on self, and then casts an Invulnerability Aura.
"Curse The Harsh Light Of Day!" - When Guardian of Storm has no Charms, it will remove the Invulnerability Aura.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Major Flux that deals 5% max Health.
strategy: Raid puzzle boss: it's invulnerable while it holds charms. Round 1 it gives itself 8 Guiding Light charms and an Invulnerability Aura.
As a team, strip all 8 charms off it (Steal Charm, Cleanse Charm, or other charm removal) — the instant it has zero charms the Invulnerability Aura falls off.
With the aura gone, any hit kills it (only 100 health).
Keep health up while you strip charms — it casts a Major Flux for 5% of your max health.
cheatNotes: Round 1 it stacks 8 Guiding Light charms on itself plus an Invulnerability Aura — it can't be damaged yet.
The instant it has no charms the Invulnerability Aura falls off, so remove all 8 charms and then hit it.

name: Hades the Unseen (Tartarus)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus - Hall of Hades
cheats: Hades may gain an extra Pip or Power Pip at the start of each Round.
"The sands of your time are running out!" - Hades will cast Impending Doom on a random Wizard.
If the Damage over Time effect is shifted to any of the Bosses, Hades will use a Triage to remove it.
If any Wizard is defeated, Hades will cast a 1,890 Healing over 3 Rounds to all allies.
If all three Bosses are still in the battle, he will cast this Spell three times on each Boss.
If two Bosses are still remaining, he will cast the Spell two times on each Boss.
If only Hades remains, he will cast the Spell once on himself.
If a Wizard is not defeated on the Round the attack was cast, this cheat will not activate.
If a Wizard is defeated by a Damage over Time, this cheat will not activate.
If a Wizard defeats themselves, this cheat will activate, regardless of whether the Wizard is subsequently revived.
The above cheat is also noted to happen if Hades is still in the battle, and Zeus is defeated.
Poseidon may gain an extra Pip or Power Pip at the start of each Round.
"We Immortals are infallible!" - Poseidon will cast Infallible on his first turn and any other turn when he does not have an Aura. (Poseidon will also not cast Infallible unless he has no Aura.)

"You seek to weaken me? Drink from the depths!" - If any single-target Charm or Trap is cast on Poseidon, he will respond by using his Natural Attack, which removes all Traps that were placed on him. Multi-target Spells such as Windstorm or others can still be used.
Spells with single-target secondary effects (such as Efreet, Gnomes!, and Dr. Von's Monster) will trigger this cheat.
Fizzling will trigger this cheat.
Single-target Prisms will also trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to protect Charms and Wards from removal.
Zeus may gain an extra Pip or Power Pip at the start of each Round.
"You hesitate too much!" - If a Wizard casts a Spell that costs 6 or more Pips, Zeus will cheat-cast a Minotaur on the Wizard.
Healing Spells will trigger this cheat.
Shadow Spells will trigger an additional Minotaur.
This cheat will activate regardless of which Boss is targeted.
strategy: Three-boss Tartarus finale (Hades, Poseidon, Zeus). All take +30% from Myth, Life, and Death and resist Balance 80%. Hitters use Myth, Life, or Death.
Never let a wizard fall: any wizard defeated makes the surviving bosses heal 1,890 over 3 rounds each (up to three casts each while all three live). Keep the healer active.
Zeus punishes big spells: anything 6+ pips (including heals) draws a Minotaur on the caster, and Shadow spells draw an extra one. Keep utility cheap and save one expensive hit for the kill.

Poseidon removes single-target traps with his Natural Attack (fizzling and single-target prisms trigger it too) and keeps Infallible up. Use Indemnity-protected or multi-target traps like Windstorm, and Feint through Indemnity.
Watch Hades' Impending Doom on a random wizard and all three bosses grabbing free extra pips each round. Pre-heal the doomed wizard.
Assign a kill order, funnel blades to one hitter, and burst each boss with a 6+ pip hit only when you can absorb the Minotaur, keeping everyone alive to deny the heals.
cheatNotes: Hades can grab a bonus pip most rounds, so he out-paces you on pips. Expect faster boss hits.
He drops Impending Doom on a random wizard. Pre-heal or shield the target.
If you shift a DoT onto the bosses he Triages it off. DoTs are wasted here.
Any wizard defeated makes the bosses heal 1,890 over 3 rounds. Keep everyone alive to deny it.
With all three bosses up, that heal is cast three times each. Losing a wizard nearly full-heals the room.
With two bosses left it heals twice each.
With only Hades left it heals him once.
The heal only fires if a wizard actually dies the round the killing hit lands; surviving the round denies it.
A wizard killed by a DoT does not trigger the heal.
Self-defeats still trigger the heal even if you revive after. Do not sacrifice a wizard.
It also fires if Zeus dies while Hades remains, so mind your kill order.
Poseidon also steals bonus pips each round.
Poseidon keeps Infallible up whenever he lacks an aura, so his hits will not fizzle. Plan for reliable damage from him.
Any single-target charm or trap on Poseidon makes him Natural Attack and strip all his traps. Use Indemnity or multi-target traps like Windstorm instead.
Single-target secondary effects (Efreet, Gnomes, Dr. Von's) also trigger Poseidon's trap-strip.
Fizzling on Poseidon triggers the strip too, so keep accuracy up against him.
Single-target prisms trigger it as well; prism him with multi-target options or not at all.
Indemnity protects your charms and wards from Poseidon's removal.
Zeus also grabs bonus pips each round.
Any spell costing 6+ pips draws a Minotaur onto the caster. Save your expensive hit for a round you can absorb it and keep utility cheap.
Heals count as spells here; a 6+ pip heal also draws a Minotaur.
Shadow spells draw an extra Minotaur. Weigh whether the shadow hit is worth two Minotaurs.
The Minotaur fires no matter which boss you targeted; it is about the pip cost, not the target.

name: Hades the Unseen (Tartarus, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Hall of Hades (Challenge)
cheats: [Round] "The sands of your time are running out!" - At the beginning of every Round, Hades will cast a +70% incoming Death Damage Aura for 4 Rounds on a random Wizard.
[Aura] "We Immortals are Infallible" - At the beginning of the first Round, Poseidon will cast Infallible.
[Blade][Aura] "Death is Eternal" - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Charm or helpful Aura, Hades will cast Tower Shield on himself.
[Harmful Aura] "Death is Vengeful" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Aura, Hades will cast his Natural Attack on the caster.
Auras that increase incoming Damage such as Berserk or Galvanic Field will also trigger this cheat.
[Heal] "Death is Patient" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Hades will cast Deathblade on himself.
[Shield] "Your punishment shall be swift!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Zeus will cast Minotaur on the recipient.
[Death] "Ahhh, the glorious taste of defeat!" - Whenever a Wizard, Poseidon, or Zeus are defeated, Hades will cast a protected +40% version of Deathblade on himself.
strategy: Attack Hades with Life. He resists Death 80% and takes boosted Life damage, so Life is your damage school.

Play with minimal buffs. A helpful charm or aura gives him a Tower Shield, a healing spell gives him a Deathblade, a helpful ward makes Zeus hit you with Minotaur, and a harmful aura makes him hit the caster.
Avoid mid-fight healing. It hands Hades free Deathblades, and pet may-cast heals count too, so bring shields and pre-heals instead.
Watch the +70% incoming Death aura he drops on a random wizard each Round for 4 Rounds. That wizard takes a much bigger hit, so keep them topped up rather than over-committed.
Burst everything together. Whenever a wizard, Poseidon, or Zeus dies, Hades gains a protected +40% Deathblade, so do not kill his minions early unless you can finish the whole board.
This is a team Challenge: one hitter, supports funneling blades, and a healer who avoids triggering his heal cheats while managing the incoming Death aura.
cheatNotes: Each Round he puts a +70% incoming Death damage aura on a random wizard for 4 Rounds, so that wizard takes a much bigger hit; keep them healed and shielded.
Poseidon casts Infallible on Round 1, giving the enemy side armor piercing.
Casting a helpful charm or aura makes Hades Tower Shield himself, blocking your next hit, so avoid self-blades and auras.
Casting a harmful aura makes Hades hit the caster with his Natural Attack.
Damage-increasing auras like Berserk or Galvanic Field count as harmful here and trigger that same counterhit.
Casting any healing spell gives Hades a Deathblade, so avoid mid-fight heals.
Casting a helpful ward makes Zeus hit the recipient with Minotaur, so skip shields.
Every time a wizard, Poseidon, or Zeus is defeated, Hades gains a protected +40% Deathblade, so killing his minions makes him hit harder; burst everything together.

name: Hades the Unseen (Zeus Exalted Challenge)
cheats: "Tartarus is the true victor in war!" - If Hades and Ares Savage Spear are both present, Hades will interrupt at the start of the Round to use his Natural Attack on a random Wizard, that deals 4 Death Damage and applies 4 Damage over Time effects on the target. Then, Ares will use his Natural Attack that Detonates all Damage over Time effects, dealing 1,175 then 1, 2, and 3 Death Damage.
"The selfish belong in Tartarus!" - If a Healing Spell is cast on themself, Hades will cast a Shift Wraith Spell that deals 335 Death Damage and applies a -90% Infection on the caster.
This cheat will trigger with AoE Healing Spells.
This cheat will not trigger if a Wizard heals another player other than themselves.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Tartarus appreciates your donation." - If a Wizard or a Boss is defeated, Hades will cheat-cast Donate Power to whoever cast the defeating spell.
"Zeus sends his regards from Tartarus." - If Zeus is defeated, Hades will cast his Natural Attack on a random Wizard every two rounds.
spellNotes: Casts Lernaean Hydra and applies a -45% Accuracy Charm on all Wizards.
Casts Lycian Chimera and applies a -45% Damage Charm on all Wizards.
strategy: Exalted challenge team fight: hitter, blading supports, and a healer. Hades has no resist, so hit with your strongest school.
Never self-heal. Every self-heal, including AoE heals and pet heals, triggers Shift Wraith (335 Death plus a -90% Infection on the healer). Heal teammates instead, which is safe.
Avoid DoTs while Ares is up. Hades stacks DoTs on you and Ares detonates them for heavy Death damage.
Mind kill order: defeating an enemy hands the killer a Donate Power, and killing Zeus makes Hades natural-attack a random wizard every 2 rounds.
Funnel blades and Feint to the hitter and burst each target down.
cheatNotes: While Ares (Savage Spear) is alive with Hades, they combo: Hades stacks 4 DoTs on a wizard, then Ares detonates them all for heavy Death damage. Keep DoTs off yourself.
Self-healing triggers Shift Wraith: 335 Death and a -90% Infection on you. Heal teammates, not yourself.
AoE heals also count as self-heals here and will trigger it.
Healing another player, not yourself, is safe and will not trigger the Wraith.
Pet heals trigger it too, so watch may-cast heals.
Whoever lands a killing blow gets a Donate Power from Hades, so the killer gains pips.
Once Zeus is defeated, Hades natural-attacks a random wizard every 2 rounds. Keep shields and heals ready.

name: Half-Witted Skeleton
cheats: "I saw that!!" - After a Tired Fighter is damaged, this Creature casts Colossus after Lazy Henchman uses Rebirth.
strategy: Attack with Fire; it takes +40% and the skeleton resists Ice 40%.
