With only 1,115 health, one bladed Fire hit should end it, so do not overinvest in setup.
Its only cheat is a reactive Colossus tied to a Tired Fighter being damaged, so focus it down before that chain matters.
cheatNotes: After a Tired Fighter is damaged, this skeleton follows up with Colossus once Lazy Henchman uses Rebirth. With only 1,115 health it dies to one Fire hit, so drop it fast.

name: Headless Horseman

stats.resist: 49% to all schools79% to all schools

cheats: [Global] "This will be your grave. Your head will be my trophy." - At the beginning of the first Round, the Headless Horseman will cast a version of Doom and Gloom.
If the Doom and Gloom is replaced, the Horseman will recast Doom and Gloom.

[Round] "The Headless Horseman rides again!" - At the end of the first Round, the Horseman will cast a special version of Headless Horseman that deals 605 Death Damage to all enemies and applies a -30% Damage Charm on all enemies.
This will repeat every 5 Rounds.
[Spell] "LAUGHS" - The Horseman says this after he casts either Wraith or Monster Mash.
After the next time this is triggered, he will cast his special version of Headless Horseman at the end of the following Round.

[Round] "Don't lose your head! Here, have mine!" - At the end of the Round, if the Horseman did not cast his special Headless Horseman cheat, the Horseman will cast his Natural Attack on the Wizard with the current highest Health, which removes two helpful Charms from the target to apply a -35% Damage Charm per Charm removed. This also applies a -10% Healing Charm on all Wizards.

[Round] "Death Calls for the Weak and Unwary." - At the beginning of the Round, if a Wizard has six or more harmful Charms on them, the Horseman will cast a version of Mortality that deals 100% max Health Damage to the Wizard.
[Friend][Afterlife] "And Death comes for you all." - The Horseman says this after a Wizard is defeated.
[Feint] If a Wizard casts a Feint or Backdraft, the Horseman will cast a Death version of Cleanse Ward on himself which clears all harmful Wards on him, including protected ones.
[Dispel] If a Wizard casts a Dispel, the Horseman will remove all harmful Charms from himself, including protected ones.
spellNotes: The Headless Horseman's Natural Attack deals 666 Death Damage to target and applies a -10% Healing Charm on all enemies.
strategy: Team fight (150,000 health). Put all blades on one hitter using Life or a Life prism: he takes +30% Life and resists Death 79% and everything else 49%.
Round 1 he casts Doom and Gloom for the whole fight and recasts it if replaced, so plan around cut healing rather than fighting it.
Feint does not work here: casting Feint or Backdraft makes him Cleanse Ward all traps off himself, even protected ones. Rely on blades, not traps.
He strips your blades. His end-of-round natural attack removes two helpful charms and applies -35% damage per charm, so protect the hitter's key blades with Aegis or re-blade the same round you hit.
Watch your harmful-charm count: at 6+ harmful charms on a wizard he casts Mortality for 100% max health. Cleanse the -damage and -heal charms he piles on.
His special Headless Horseman AoE lands at end of round 1 and every 5 rounds, plus after his Wraith or Monster Mash LAUGHS, so time setup and the big hit around those windows.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he casts Doom and Gloom to cut all healing.
Replace his Doom and Gloom and he just recasts it.
End of round 1 he casts a special Headless Horseman for 605 Death to all and a -30% damage charm on all.
That special Headless Horseman attack repeats every 5 rounds.
He says LAUGHS after casting Wraith or Monster Mash.
The next time that LAUGHS triggers, his special Headless Horseman lands at end of the following round.
If he did not cast the special that round, he hits the highest-health wizard, removing two blades for -35% damage each and putting -10% healing on all wizards.
At the start of a round, a wizard carrying 6+ harmful charms gets Mortality for 100% of max health.
He taunts after a wizard is defeated, with no direct effect.
Casting Feint or Backdraft makes him Cleanse Ward off all his traps, even protected ones.
Casting a Dispel makes him remove all his harmful charms, even protected ones.

name: Heidi
cheats: "ALL WIZARDS WILL BURN!" - If the battle is joined after Round 2, Heidi will cast Fuel and Quench on the late Wizard/Wizards.
Note: Heidi casts her Balanceblade cheat before her turn, which is after the Wizard's turn if the Wizard goes first.

"I AM THE AVATAR OF THE INFERNO!" - At the start of Round 1 and every 3 Rounds after, Heidi will summon a Fire Elemental Minion if one isn't already present in the battle using her turn not using Pips.
Heidi will say this line when the Fire Elemental casts Raging Bull to re-summon another Fire Elemental.
After the Fire Elemental casts Raging Bull, it is defeated.

"LET THE FLAMES DANCE AND WRITHE!" - When a Wizard who is currently affected by a Damage over Time Spell which is not Fire Dragon (may be a bug) has their turn, Heidi will cast Balanceblade on the caster. This cheat will trigger once for each Damage over Time Spell on the Wizard, regardless of what the Wizard does for their turn. This includes casting a Spell, passing, fizzling, etc.
Aura Cycle: - After the Fire Elemental is defeated by her, she will cast the following Amplify spells.
If the Minion was defeated by the Wizard, she will not cast Amplify.
"I AM THE FIRE THAT MELTS STEEL!" - 1st Minion defeated - +20% outgoing Damage lasting 99 Rounds.
"ALL TRAPS MELT IN MY WAKE!" - 2nd Minion defeated - +40% outgoing Damage lasting 99 Rounds.
3rd Minion defeated - +60% outgoing Damage lasting 99 Rounds.
4th Minion defeated - +80% outgoing Damage lasting 99 Rounds.
5th Minion defeated - +100% outgoing Damage lasting 99 Rounds.
These Auras have the appearance of Berserk.
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Fire Trap (+25%).
Can cast Shadow Spells without Shadow Pips (may be a bug).
strategy: Bring your full team in from Round 1; anyone who joins after Round 2 eats Fuel and Quench.

Put a cheap Damage over Time on yourself early (anything except Fire Dragon). Every turn you take with a DoT ticking, Heidi casts a free Balanceblade on you, one per DoT. This is the fight's best exploit.
Kill each Fire Elemental yourself before it casts Raging Bull. If it dies to her side, Heidi gains a stacking +20% to +100% permanent damage aura per minion lost.
She resists Fire 70% and takes +5% from Ice. Hit with Ice or any non-Fire school. She cannot be stunned.
Once you have farmed two or three Balanceblades and are keeping the Elemental down, stack your normal blades and finish her with one big hit.
cheatNotes: Anyone entering after Round 2 gets Fuel and Quench cast on them. Start the fight with your whole team in the circle.
Timing note: if you go first, her Balanceblade lands after your turn, so plan to spend each farmed blade the following round.
Expect a Fire Elemental on Round 1 and a replacement every 3 rounds whenever one is missing. It costs her turn, not pips, so it never slows her damage.
The Elemental re-summons a replacement by casting Raging Bull. Kill it before it gets the chance.
Raging Bull is a suicide cast; the Elemental dies after using it, which counts against you and feeds her Amplify cycle.
Free blades: while any DoT except Fire Dragon is ticking on you, Heidi casts Balanceblade on you every turn you take, once per DoT. Deliberately keep a cheap DoT on yourself and farm blades.
If the Elemental dies to Raging Bull or to her side, she gains a permanent damage aura. Killing each Elemental yourself denies the whole cycle.
Defeating the minion with your own spells skips the Amplify entirely. Always take the Elemental out yourself.
Let the first Elemental die to her and she gains +20% outgoing damage for the rest of the fight.
+40% on the second. The auras stack, so her damage snowballs fast.
+60% on the third.
+80% on the fourth.
+100% on the fifth. With her base 135% boost her hits become lethal; never let it get here.
Cosmetic note: the auras look like Berserk, but treat them as the +damage Amplify described above.

name: Helios
locations: Celestia: The Floating Land - Helios's Hangout
cheats: "A Charm! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Blade will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat, but Aura Spells will not.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A ward! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Trap (including Feint) or a Shield will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
The text for this cheat only appears after Helios casts the Hex.
strategy: Skip blades, traps, and shields — every one you cast gets answered with a +35% Hex on you. Pack an aura for your damage buff instead; auras do not trigger him.
Hit with Ice — he takes +35% from it. Never Fire into his 35% resist.
Open with an aura, build pips for two or three rounds, and just attack. At 3,435 health he dies to one solid Ice hit without any trap setup.
If you must shield or trap, do it knowing his next hit on you lands +35% harder through the Hex — heal margin accordingly.
cheatNotes: Cast a Blade and he instantly puts a +35% Hex trap on you — his next hit on you does 35% more. Buff with auras instead.
Globals count as blades for this cheat, but auras are exempt — auras are your only free buff.
Pet may-cast blades do not trigger the Hex, so pet buffs are free value.
Traps (Feint included) and shields draw the same +35% Hex. Shielding still works — you just pay a Hex for it.

name: Hercules Poirot
locations: Empyrea: Southwest Aero Plains - Cretan Bull Field
cheats: "Late! Feel the power of my mythical strength!" - If a Wizard joins the duel late after the second Round, he casts Athena Battlesight, Witch's House Call (992-1,132), and Orthrus on the Wizard.
"I Feel Great!" If at full Health at the end of a Round, he casts a protected Balanceblade (+100%).

"This Will Not Do..." If not at full Health, he casts a unique Blessing which heals him for 10% of his total Health which is 1,518. This Heal is unaffected by any modifiers, such as Sanctuary or Infection, and it is not dispelled by Entangle.
strategy: Enter the fight on time with your team. Joining late after Round 2 triggers Athena Battlesight, Witch's House Call (992-1,132), and Orthrus dumped onto the latecomer.
Keep at least some damage landing on Hercules every single round. As long as he takes damage he cannot end the round at full health, which shuts off his protected +100% Balanceblade cheat.

The cleanest way to keep chip damage flowing is a low-damage off-school damage-over-time each cycle: Fire Elf, Frostbite, Poison, Death Bat, Black Widow, or Steel Giant. The DoT ticks while your team blades safely.

In a group, assign one wizard as the consistent attacker who pokes Hercules each round, a hitter who charges multi-hits like Bugs, Raging Bull, or Mystic Colossus, and a healer running Satyr with Life mastery.
Wear Tower or Dream Shields and pack healing. Hercules has 35% universal pierce, so raw shields alone leak damage. Shadow spells like Shrike add pierce for the final hit.

Note his heal cheat when he is not at full health: a flat 1,518 Blessing that ignores Infection and Sanctuary and is not removed by Entangle. Do not waste rounds trying to block it; just out-damage it and burst him down.
cheatNotes: If you enter after Round 2, Hercules immediately loads the latecomer with Athena Battlesight, a Witch's House Call hit for 992-1,132, and an Orthrus. Load in with your team from the start.
Any round he ends at full health, he gives himself a protected +100% Balanceblade. Keep a hit or a damage-over-time landing on him every round so he is never at full health at round's end.

When he is below full health he casts a special 1,518 Blessing (10% of his health) that ignores Infection, Sanctuary, and Entangle. You cannot stop this heal, so plan for extra damage rather than trying to block it.

name: Hierarch Lucien
cheats: There may be a bug in which he'll state two rules at once, which can lead to you being rewarded and punished or punished twice.
[Late] "LATE! YOU ARE LATE!" - If a Wizard is late he'll cast a Storm spell that applies -50% Weakness, a -100% Black Mantle, and also cast Beguile on the late Wizard.
[Blade] "New Rule: You must Blade!" - At the end of the first round he'll shout this out.
"Yes, obedience is key!" - If a Wizard Blades, he'll place a Tower Shield on them.
"That is against the rules." - If a Wizard casts a spell that isn't a Blade, Hierarch Lucien will cast Weakness (-50%) on them.
Maycasts will cause Lucien to cast this twice.
[Heal] "New Rule: You must Heal!" - At the end of X Round, he'll shout this out.
"Yes, obedience is key!" - If a Wizard Heals, he'll place a +50% Universal Lightning Strike on them.
"That is against the rules." - If a Wizard doesn't Heal, he'll place a -100% Black Mantle on them.
[Manipulation] "New Rule: Strike ALL your friends and be rewarded!" - At the beginning of the 3rd Round he'll cast Beguile on a random Wizard.
"Yes, obedience is key!" - If a Wizard casts an AoE Damage spell if there are targets to hit, he'll cast a -33% Health Insane Bolt on himself removing 33% of his total Health.
While Beguiled, casting a spell that is against the rules is allowed until the effect wears off.
spellNotes: Casts an AoE version of Disarm (1 Pip).
strategy: Play by his rules each round. Lucien announces a rule, rewards obeying it, and punishes breaking it, so follow the current rule for the buff and hit only when the rule allows.
Under the Blade rule, cast a blade to earn a Tower Shield; casting anything else draws a -50% Weakness. Use these forced blade turns to stack your hitter.
Under the Heal rule, heal to earn a +50% Universal Lightning Strike (a free damage boost); not healing gives you a -100% Black Mantle. Bank that Lightning Strike for your big hit.

Exploit the Manipulation rule: when he demands you strike your friends, casting an AoE damage spell that has targets makes him Insane Bolt himself for 33% of his own Health. Line up a real AoE finisher for that round.
He is Storm with 73% Storm resist and takes only 5% Myth, plus strong outgoing and incoming heals (41%/46%), so pack a Myth hitter or prisms and an Infection/Doom to cut his healing.

Come in on time (late wizards get Weakness, Black Mantle, and Beguile) and watch for the two-rule bug; when Beguiled you may break rules freely, so use those turns to set up, then burst him with your rule-sanctioned AoE.
cheatNotes: A known bug can make him state two rules at once, so you may be rewarded and punished, or punished twice, in a single round.
A late wizard is hit with a -50% Weakness, a -100% Black Mantle, and Beguile; join before the fight begins.
At the end of Round 1 he declares the Blade rule.
While the Blade rule is active, blading earns you a Tower Shield.
Casting any non-blade under the Blade rule draws a -50% Weakness on you.
Maycast effects can make him apply the Weakness twice, so watch your pet procs.
At the end of a later round he declares the Heal rule.
While the Heal rule is active, healing earns you a +50% Universal Lightning Strike, a free blade for your next hit.
Not healing under the Heal rule gives you a -100% Black Mantle.
At the start of Round 3 he Beguiles a random wizard and demands you strike your friends.
Casting an AoE damage spell with valid targets under this rule makes Lucien Insane Bolt himself, removing 33% of his own Health. Save a real AoE for this round.
While Beguiled, you are allowed to cast rule-breaking spells until the Beguile wears off, so use those turns freely.

name: Hierophant of Destruction (Standard)
locations: Darkmoor: Black Lagoon - Bog of Vengeance (Standard)
cheats: "No child dares disobey the Scholomari" - Hierophant of Destruction will say this at the beginning of the first round with no effect. Whenever Hierophant of Destruction casts a buff, her minions will cheat-cast similar buffs of the same type.
[Global] = Stormblade
[Aura] = Magnify, Sleet Storm, or Empowerment
[Trap] = Ice Trap or Storm Trap
[Blade] = Balanceblade, Iceblade (+45%), or Stormblade
[Random] "My turn is when I say it is." - Randomly, Hierophant of Destruction will interrupt and cheat-cast Chain Lightning.
[Round] Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle begins on the first round and repeats from the beginning starting on the next turn after the cycle ends.
"You will cast what I tell you!" - Hierophant of Destruction will interrupt and force a random wizard to cast one of the following spells:
[Elemental][Weakness] A special 15% version of Elemental Weakness
[Deferred Damage] A triple Bomb DoT that deals 50 Fire damage after 5 rounds.
[Heal over Time] A triple HoT that heals 50 health over 5 rounds.
[Spirit][Blade] A special 15% version of Spirit Blade

[Round] "The willful student hurts themselves" - At the end of the round two rounds after forcing a wizard to cast a spell (e.g. round 3 for the first cycle), Hierophant of Destruction casts a special Ice (if the hanging effect was blades or weaknesses) or Storm (if the hanging effect was DoTs or HoTs) damage spell that clears the hanging effects that wizards were forced to cast and deals 10% of the wizard's max health per hanging effect cleared.
strategy: Team fight. Bring a Fire hitter — she takes +30% from Fire and resists everything else (75% Ice, 63% Storm, 45% to the rest).
Supports stack blades on the hitter and Feints on the boss. She cannot be stunned, and her random Chain Lightning interrupts land whenever they like — keep the team topped up.

Let her forced-cast cycle play out. Each cycle she forces a random wizard to cast a minor effect: a 15% Elemental Weakness, a tiny Fire bomb DoT, a small HoT, or a 15% Spirit Blade. None of these matter much on their own.

Two rounds after the forced cast she clears those effects and deals 10% of max health per effect cleared. Spend or remove the forced effects before that round (use the blade, cleanse the weakness) to shrink the punish.
If minions are present, remember they copy every buff she casts — blades, traps, auras, even a Stormblade global. Clear them early or the enemy side snowballs together.
Build to one big Fire AoE and end it. Do not trade chip damage into 20,220 health and 45%+ resist.
cheatNotes: Whenever she buffs herself, any minions with her immediately copy the same kind of buff. Her setup rounds buff the whole enemy side, not just her.
The minions' copy when she casts a Global: Stormblade.
The minions' copy when she casts an Aura: Magnify, Sleet Storm, or Empowerment.
The minions' copy when she casts a Trap: Ice Trap or Storm Trap.
The minions' copy when she casts a Blade: Balanceblade, Iceblade (+45%), or Stormblade.
At random she interrupts with Chain Lightning. You cannot prevent it — keep health high enough to absorb a surprise Storm hit.
The forced-cast cycle starts on round 1 and loops for the whole fight.
She takes over a random wizard's turn and makes them cast one of four minor spells. You do not choose the target or the spell.
One forced option: a 15% Elemental Weakness. Weak on its own, but it counts as a hanging effect for her punish two rounds later.
One forced option: a triple bomb DoT dealing only 50 Fire after 5 rounds. The damage is trivial — the punish counting is what matters.
One forced option: a small HoT healing 50 over 5 rounds. Harmless, but it still counts toward the punish.
One forced option: a 15% Spirit Blade. Use it on your next attack if you can — a spent blade cannot be cleared for punish damage.
Two rounds after the forced cast she clears all remaining forced effects and deals 10% of your max health per effect cleared. Spend or remove the forced effects early to blunt this hit.

name: High Loremagus (Tier 1)
locations: Dragonspyre: The Atheneum - High Loremagus' Chamber
cheats: "Only a true master of lore can defeat me!" - At the start of every Round when a Wizard or Minion lacks an Aura, High Loremagus (Tier 1) will cast a Star School Spell on each Wizard or Minion, applying a -200% Accuracy Aura of the targets School that lasts for 99 turns.
Summoned Monstrology Minions that are Astral or Shadow School will be targeted by this cheat, but no Aura will be applied to them as there's no Aura for those Schools.
Summoned Monstrology Minions that are dual schooled will get a -200% Accuracy Aura for their second school.
"You know nothing of lore. Do your research!" - If a Wizard or Minion casts an Aura, High Loremagus (Tier 1) will recast the Aura on them.
"Uh oh... Watch out for vermin!" - If a Feint is cast on her, she will cast Camp Bandit (Tier 4a).
Mass Feint can not be used to avoid this cheat.
strategy: Expect a -200% accuracy aura of your own school on everyone from round 1 - she applies it to anyone without an aura, and casting your own aura just gets the aura recast on you. Plan around it instead of fighting it.
Pack off-school hits (treasure cards or a secondary school). The aura only debuffs spells of the target's own school, so off-school spells still land.
Skip Feint entirely - any Feint on her triggers Camp Bandit, and Mass Feint does not avoid it. Use regular traps and blades instead.
She takes +15% from Myth, Life, and Death and resists Balance 40% - pick your off-school cards accordingly.
Clear the three Fonts with an AoE so they die with her. 7,000 health falls to a modest blade-and-trap setup once your accuracy problem is solved.
cheatNotes: From round 1, anyone without an aura gets a -200% accuracy aura of their own school for the rest of the fight. Spells from other schools are unaffected, so off-school hits are the safe play.
Astral or Shadow summoned minions get targeted but nothing sticks - no aura exists for those schools.
Dual-school summoned minions get the -200% accuracy aura for their second school.
Casting your own aura just makes her recast the aura on you, so you cannot overwrite the accuracy debuff. Do not waste turns on Star spells.
Any Feint on her is answered with Camp Bandit. Leave Feint out of your deck.
Mass Feint still triggers the Camp Bandit response - there is no Feint workaround.

name: High Loremagus (Tier 2)
locations: Dragonspyre: The Atheneum - High Loremagus' Chamber
cheats: "Only a true master of lore can defeat me!" - At the start of every Round when a Wizard or Minion lacks an Aura, High Loremagus (Tier 2) will cast a Star School Spell on each Wizard or Minion, applying a -200% Accuracy Aura of the targets School that lasts for 99 turns.
Summoned Monstrology Minions that are Astral or Shadow School will be targeted by this cheat, but no Aura will be applied to them as there's no Aura for those Schools.
Summoned Monstrology Minions that are dual schooled will get a -200% Accuracy Aura for their second school.
"You know nothing of lore. Do your research!" - If a Wizard or Minion casts an Aura, High Loremagus (Tier 2) will recast the Aura on them.
"Uh oh... Watch out for vermin!" - If a Feint is cast on her, she will cast Camp Bandit (Tier 4a).
Mass Feint can not be used to avoid this cheat.
strategy: Keep an Aura on yourself at all times. If you start any round without one, she gives you a -200% Accuracy Aura for 99 rounds and you fizzle everything. Open round 1 with an Aura and never let it drop.
She recasts your Aura on you when you cast one, so refreshing is cheap; just make sure one is always active.
Do not Feint her: any Feint, Mass Feint included, triggers Camp Bandit. Use other traps or stack blades instead.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (+15%); she resists Balance 40% and hits for +150%, so shield up.
Handle the three Fonts and finish with a boosted hit while your Aura keeps your accuracy safe.
cheatNotes: If you start any round without an Aura, she gives you a -200% Accuracy Aura for 99 rounds. Keep an Aura up from round 1 on.
Astral or Shadow summoned minions get targeted but take no aura, since none exists for those schools.
Dual-school minions catch the -200% accuracy on their second school.
Casting an Aura makes her recast it on you, which is harmless and keeps you covered against the accuracy cheat.
Feinting her triggers Camp Bandit, so skip Feint entirely.
Mass Feint does not dodge it either, so use no Feint of any kind.

name: High Loremagus (Tier 3)
locations: Dragonspyre: The Atheneum - High Loremagus' Chamber
cheats: "Only a true master of lore can defeat me!" - At the start of the First Round, and every round thereafter for any Wizard or Minion lacking an Aura, High Loremagus (Tier 3) will cast a Star School Spell on that Wizard or Minion, applying a -200% Accuracy Aura of the target's School that lasts for 99 turns.
Summoned Monstrology Minions that are Astral or Shadow School will be targeted by this cheat, but no Aura will be applied to them as there's no Aura for those Schools.
Summoned Monstrology Minions that are dual schooled will get a -200% Accuracy Aura for their second school.
"You know nothing of lore. Do your research!" - If a Wizard or Minion replaces the given Aura, High Loremagus (Tier 3) will recast the Aura on them.
"Uh oh... Watch out for vermin!" - If a Feint or Mass Feint is cast on her, she will cast Camp Bandit (Tier 4a).
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Run a team of four: three supports (not Balance) and one hitter who attacks from a school other than their own. The whole fight forces you off your primary school.
Keep an Aura on yourself at all times. Round 1 and every round after, anyone without an aura gets a -200% accuracy aura for 99 turns and fizzles everything, so pre-load Amplify, Fortify, or Berserk.
Round 1: Support 1 lands a spirit/elemental trap and Support 2 a potent spirit/elemental blade on the boss, Support 3 casts Mass Feint, and the hitter uses any 0-pip buff (not Frenzy).

Round 2: all three supports pile spirit/elemental blades onto the hitter, then the hitter fires a 7-pip AoE from the off-school for a 2-turn kill. A Death wizard with 160% Storm damage hits roughly 16k on turn two.
Equip a mastery amulet for that second school so the hitter can cast its 7-pip AoE reliably.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (she takes +15% from each; she resists Balance 40%). Overwrite her aura only on the round you plan to hit — replacing it makes her recast it next round.
Do not Feint her after round 1 beyond the opening Mass Feint plan, and never leave yourself auraless while setting up.
cheatNotes: If you ever lack an Aura, she hits you with a -200% accuracy aura for 99 turns and you'll fizzle everything. Keep your own aura (Amplify, Fortify, Berserk) up at all times.
Replacing her aura makes her recast it next round — so overwrite it with your own aura on the same round you plan to attack, then hit before she reapplies.
Feint or Mass Feint makes her summon a Camp Bandit — avoid the normal Feint here.
Use the Balance version of Feint instead; it traps her without triggering the Camp Bandit summon.

name: High Priest Ixta

stats.resist: 45% to all schools65% to all schools85% to all schools

cheats: "Death is the test. I will pass it." - At the beginning of each Round, Ixta will add a Heal over Time effect that restores 500 Health over 5 Rounds on all allies. (940 Health with Outgoing Healing)

"Only the strong will survive." - When Ixta has 2 or more Heal over Times on himself, he will cast a version of Sun Serpent that hits all enemies once, and then removes all Heal over Time effects from himself.
spellNotes: Casts Meteor Strike that deals 440 Fire Damage.
Casts Fire Dragon that deals 780 Fire Damage.
Casts Raging Bull that deals 1,110 Fire Damage.
strategy: Raid fight. Hit with Death — he resists it only 45% versus 65% on most schools and 85% on Ice/Myth.
He puts a heal-over-time on himself every round; once he holds two he fires a Sun Serpent at the whole team and clears them, so the AoE lands every other round — pre-shield and keep health buffered.
Bring Infection or Doom and Gloom to blunt his self-healing.
He can't be stunned, so time your burst for the round right after his Sun Serpent when he's reset.
Blade the hitter, Feint him, and land a big Death hit.
cheatNotes: Every round he puts a ~500 (940 buffed) 5-round heal-over-time on himself — chip his healing with Infection or Doom and Gloom.
Once he holds two heal-over-times he hits the whole team with Sun Serpent and clears them, so expect that AoE every other round and pre-shield.

name: High Priest Yetaxa
cheats: "Accept Xibalba's truth!" - At the start of every Round, Yetaxa will add two Ice Traps and two Fire Traps on two random Wizards in battle.

"Let the comet of ice rain fire!" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has four Ice Traps or four Fire Traps on them, Yetaxa will cast an all enemies version of Lord of Winter, then remove all Wards from all enemies.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Frost Giant without the Stun.
strategy: This is a raid boss with 16,760 health. Bring a full group with set roles: one hitter, blade supports, and someone assigned to watch trap counts.
Every round he spreads two Ice Traps and two Fire Traps across two random wizards. Track the count on every player, every round.
Never let anyone reach four Ice Traps or four Fire Traps. At four, he fires an all-enemy Lord of Winter and then strips every ward from your team.
Supports clear traps before anyone hits four while the hitter builds blades. Don't invest in shields you can't afford to lose if the ward wipe fires.
He is Ice school, so favor Fire damage. Set up quickly and hit hard; the trap pressure only builds the longer the fight runs.
cheatNotes: Start of every round, two random wizards gain Ice and Fire Traps. In a full group the traps spread out more slowly per player; count them carefully anyway.
The fourth Ice or Fire Trap on any one wizard triggers an all-enemy Lord of Winter plus a full ward wipe on your team at the end of that round. Clear traps before anyone reaches four.

name: High Priestess Autloc
cheats: "Life is random..." - At the start of every Round, Autloc randomly casts one of the following:
Applies a +15% Damage Trap on all enemies and a -25% Damage Ward on all allies.
Applies a -15% Damage Charm on all enemies and a +15% Damage Blade on all allies.
Deals 228 Balance Damage over 3 Rounds to all enemies, and restores 113 Health over 3 Rounds to all allies.
"...but it always leads to death." - When ???, Autloc will cast a Damage-Only version of Sabertooth on all enemies.
"Balance is all things." - Once per Round:
If a Wizard casts a Blade, Autloc will cast a +40% Bladestorm that applies two Damage Blades.
If a Wizard casts a Trap, Autloc will cast a +50% Mass Hex that applies two Traps.
If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time, Autloc will cast a Balance spell that deals ~280 Balance Damage over 3 Rounds on all Wizards.
strategy: Raid boss at 15,355 health — bring a full team: hitter, blade support, and a healer.

Her 'Balance is all things' counters mirror every blade, trap, and DoT, but only once per round — compress setup: the whole team blades or traps in the same round so she answers once instead of three times.
Ban DoTs from every deck — each one costs the whole team roughly 280 Balance damage over 3 rounds.
Prefer Sharpened/Potent enchanted buffs — fewer total casts means fewer mirrored Bladestorms and Mass Hexes for her side.
Healer keeps everyone topped: her random round-start roll can be a team-wide DoT, and the Sabertooth cheat's trigger is unknown, so treat it as always loaded.
Hit as soon as a clean window exists — the longer the fight runs, the more her random blades and your mirrored hexes pile up against you.
cheatNotes: Every round opens with one of three random effects (below) — expect swingy rounds and plan margins for the bad rolls.
Bad roll: +15% traps on your whole team and -25% damage wards on hers — your team takes more, deals less. Shield up on these rounds.
Bad roll: a -15% weakness on your team while her side gains +15% blades — cleanse the hitter or power through.
Mixed roll: 228 Balance damage over 3 rounds on your team plus a heal-over-time on hers — Triage whoever is low.
The trigger is unknown — assume a damage-only Sabertooth on the whole team can come at any time and keep health high.
Her copycat counters fire once per round — if the team must set up, stack those casts into the same round so she only answers one of them.
Each Blade you cast gives her side a +40% Bladestorm plus two extra blades — blade sparingly, and all in one round.
Each Trap you cast answers with a +50% Mass Hex plus two extra traps on your team — favor single enchanted traps over trap stacks.
Each DoT you cast hits your whole team for ~280 Balance over 3 rounds — leave DoTs out entirely.

name: High Priestess Cameca
cheats: "Life wears on us all!" - At the start of every Round, Cameca will add two -25% Damage negative Charms and two -50% Healing Charms on 2 random Wizards in battle.

"Accept your end." - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has 4+ negative Healing Charms or 4+ negative Damage Charms, Cameca will cast an all enemies version of Storm Owl, and then removes all Charms from all enemies.
spellNotes: Casts Blizzard that deals 365 Ice Damage.
strategy: Raid boss. Hit with your strongest school (no listed resist) and bring a team with a hitter and support.

Every round she stacks two -25% damage and two -50% healing charms on two random wizards. Track them, because at 4+ negative damage or 4+ negative healing charms on any wizard she Storm Owls the whole team then clears all your charms.
Her Storm Owl reset also wipes your blades, so time the hitter's burst to land BEFORE anyone hits the 4-charm threshold, or right after she resets you.
Spread the incoming charms since they hit random wizards, and cleanse damage charms off the hitter before the big hit.
Blade the hitter, Feint the boss, and burst in one round between her Owl resets.
cheatNotes: Each round she piles two -25% damage and two -50% healing charms onto two random wizards, your damage and healing erode fast.

The round any wizard reaches 4+ negative healing or 4+ negative damage charms, she Storm Owls the whole team and strips ALL your charms, blades included. Burst before that threshold or right after the reset.

name: Hisser
cheats: "I thought you came here to hurt me!" - If Hisser does not take Damage in a Round, he will cast a 0-Pip Tempest at the end of the Round, which deals 80 Storm Damage per Pip, based on the number of pips he has at the end of the Round. Power Pips count as normal Pips.
"Oh no you don't!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time (DoT) Spell, he will cast a 0-Pip Shift to the Wizard at the end of the Round following the first Damage tick.
spellNotes: The Tempest Spell that is cheat-cast, was observed to do the originally intended Damage amount, despite Resist. One Pip Tempest will deal 80 Storm Damage, despite the Wizard's Resist.
strategy: Hit him with Ice or Storm — he takes +35% from both and resists Fire 80%.
Damage him every single round, even if it is just a wand hit. If he takes no damage in a round, he casts a free Tempest dealing 80 Storm per pip he holds — and it ignores your resist.
Leave DoTs out of your deck. Any DoT gets Shifted back onto you after its first tick.

Blade up while poking him each round, then finish with big direct Ice or Storm hits. He is stunable and beguilable if you need breathing room, but keep the chip damage going regardless — 18,720 health takes a few cycles.
cheatNotes: Poke him every round — a wand hit counts. If a round ends without him taking damage, the free Tempest hits for 80 Storm per pip he is holding, and your resist will not reduce it.
Do not cast DoT spells on him. After the first damage tick he Shifts the whole DoT back onto you.

name: Honeycomb Horror

stats.resist: 40% to all schools70% to all schools100% to all schools

cheats: The Wizard can cast Brush Fire, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, and War Oracle as inspired Casts during this battle.
At the end of every Round, Honeycomb Horror will cast Power Current on itself.
After casting Doom Oni, Honeycomb Horror will cast Energy Wall on itself.
spellNotes: Casts a Death version of Sabertooth.
strategy: This is a Raid fight, so coordinate roles and use the free inspired casts (Brush Fire, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle) as your no-cost setup and damage.
It resists Storm and Life 100%, Fire, Death, Balance and the astral and shadow schools 70%, and Ice and Myth 40%, so hit with Ice or Myth and never into Storm or Life.
It buffs itself with Power Current every Round, so do not stall; build blades on your hitter and end the fight before its charge snowballs.
After it casts Doom Oni it shields with Energy Wall, so break that wall or pierce through before your finisher.
cheatNotes: You get inspired casts of Brush Fire, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, and War Oracle during this fight; use them, since they cost you nothing.
At the end of every Round it casts Power Current on itself, steadily building a charge.
After it casts Doom Oni it puts up an Energy Wall on itself.

name: Hook Worm
locations: Empyrea: Southwest Aero Plains - Hook Worm Tunnel
cheats: [Late] If a Wizard joins after Round 1, Hook Worm will cast Sand Wurm on the late Wizard.
spellNotes: Deals 420 Death Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Enter on Round 1. Late arrivals get hit with Sand Wurm.
He self-casts a protected all-enemies Feint that boosts his hits on you, so keep a shield or heal ready for the follow-up natural attack (up to 420 Death).
He is stunable and beguilable. Use either to skip a threatening turn while you set up.
Hit anything but Death. He resists Death 70%, so prism if you are a Death wizard.
Stack blades over two or three Rounds and drop one hit. At 9,370 Health he goes down solo.
cheatNotes: The only cheat is a punish for arriving late: join before Round 2 or eat a Sand Wurm.

name: Howling Chaney
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Castle Darkmoor (Dungeon)
cheats: "You like the taste of Life?" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Howling Chaney will cheat-cast Gnomes! (860-960) on the Wizard.
Pet and Wand may-cast Spells will trigger this cheat.
"I sharpen my claws!" - At the beginning of the second Round (and every other Round thereafter), Howling Chaney will interrupt cast a +12% version of Lifespear on himself.
"Tremble under the power of life!" - At the beginning of the third Round (and every three Rounds thereafter), he will interrupt cast a Life Fuel (+35%) on a random Wizard.
strategy: Swap out pets with may-cast heals before entering - every heal, including may-casts, draws a Gnomes! counter for 860-960.
Set roles: two supports feed blades and Feints to one hitter, and the healer holds heals unless someone is in real danger.
When you must heal, do it big and once - each healing cast eats a ~900 Gnomes! counter, so one large heal beats three small ones.
Do not stall: he gains a +12% Lifespear every other round and puts Life Fuel (a +35% trap) on a random wizard every third round, so his damage keeps climbing.
Hitter finishes with Fire or Death (+30%) and clears the Hound of Heck with the same AoE. Life is resisted 80%, so Life wizards bring a prism.
cheatNotes: Every heal you cast is answered with Gnomes! for 860-960 on that wizard. Heal rarely and big, and be ready to absorb the counter.
May-cast heals from pets and wands count - bring a pet without healing may-casts or you will eat random Gnomes! counters.
He gains a +12% Lifespear on round 2 and every other round after, so his hits get stronger the longer the fight runs.
Every third round a random wizard gets Life Fuel, a +35% trap - that player takes his next Life hit much harder, so shield them or finish fast.

name: Huac Cries Blood (Challenge)
locations: Housing Instance: Huac Cries Blood Rematch

stats.resist: 32% to all schools12% to all schools

cheats: [Afterlife] "Let the Old Day Die!" - If anyone on the Wizard's team is defeated, the Boss that defeated them will cast a version of Meteor Strike that deals 100% max Health Damage to all Wizards.

[All Enemies (Squared)] "Together, we return!" - If more than one enemy is defeated on the same turn, including if all enemies are defeated at the same time, all enemies will cast a version of Blessing, restoring themselves to 100% max Health.
[Ward] "No defense against the inevitable" - If a Wizard casts a Shield, Huac Cries Blood will cast Helephant (Tier 5b) on the Wizard with the lowest Health.
[Heal over Time] "All succumb to decay" - If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time, Skurkis Screaming Moon will cast Skeletal Pirate (Tier 5b) on the Wizard with the lowest Health.
[Curse] "No curse can snare me!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm on Yacate Farwalker, Yacate Farwalker will cast Stone Colossus (Tier 5b) on the Wizard with the lowest Health.
Dispels and harmful Accuracy Charms do not trigger this cheat.

[Curse] "No curse can snare me!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm on Huac Cries Blood or Skurkis Screaming Moon, Yacate Farwalker will cast Glacial Fortress on the Boss who receives the harmful Charm.

[Dispel] Dispels will trigger this cheat. If the harmful Charm is a Dispel cast on Huac Cries Blood, he will cast a protected +35% Fireblade on himself. If the harmful Charm is a Dispel cast on Skurkis Screaming Moon, she will cast a protected Deathblade (+40%) on herself.
Harmful Accuracy Charms do not trigger this cheat.
If a Spell is cast that applies harmful Charms on multiple enemies, such as Plague, Yacate Farwalker will only cast Stone Colossus and will not cast Glacial Fortress.
Yacate Farwalker's Glacial Fortress can be dispelled with Melt.
strategy: This is a three-enemy puzzle fight (Huac plus Skurkis and Yacate) with a lethal kill-order trap - bring a full team with a hitter, support, and a healer running strong absorbs.
Hit Life. Huac takes +55% Life and +8% Storm; he only resists 32% Ice and 12% elsewhere, so Life is your damage school.

Do not AoE them down together. Defeating two or more enemies on the same turn (or all at once) makes every enemy full-heal with Blessing. But defeating just one triggers a 100%-max-health Meteor Strike on the whole team.
Because both a single kill and a multi-kill are punished, kill them one at a time with big absorbs/shields already up to survive each Meteor, and save Huac for last.

Avoid the trigger cheats while setting up: no shields (Helephant on lowest health), no Heal-over-Times (Skeletal Pirate on lowest health), no harmful charms on Yacate (Stone Colossus), and no dispels or curses on the bosses (Glacial Fortress or free protected blades). Melt can remove Yacate's Glacial Fortress if it goes up.
cheatNotes: Defeating any one enemy makes the boss that scored the kill hit the entire team with a Meteor Strike for 100% of max health. Keep absorbs up before any kill.
Defeating two or more enemies on the same turn - or all at once - makes every enemy cast Blessing and heal back to full. Never AoE them down together.
Casting a Shield makes Huac drop a Helephant on your lowest-health wizard.
Casting a Heal-over-Time makes Skurkis drop Skeletal Pirate on your lowest-health wizard.
A harmful charm on Yacate makes Yacate cast Stone Colossus on your lowest-health wizard.
Dispels and harmful accuracy charms do not trigger that Stone Colossus cheat.
A harmful charm on Huac or Skurkis makes Yacate shield that boss with Glacial Fortress.
A Dispel specifically makes the dispelled boss self-blade: a protected +35% Fireblade on Huac, or a protected +40% Deathblade on Skurkis.
Harmful accuracy charms do not trigger that self-blade cheat.
A multi-target curse like Plague makes Yacate cast only Stone Colossus and skip Glacial Fortress.
Yacate's Glacial Fortress can be removed with Melt.

name: Hugh
cheats: Hugh:
"Play for them their swan song!" - If a Wizard casts an all allies Healing Spell, Hugh will cast Sirens on the casting Wizard.
"You want to surrender your pips!" - At the end of the 3rd Round, Hugh will cast a Balance version of Steal Pips, stealing 3 Pips from and dealing 80 Damage per Pip to a random Wizard.
Jack:
"Time to bear the claws!" - If a Wizard casts a Rank 7 Spell or higher, Jack will cast Shift Chaney on the casting Wizard.
"Quickly, use your accelerated healing." - When Jack reaches below 80% Health, he will cast a Life Spell that restores protected 10,000 Health over 10 Rounds on himself.
Mann:
"I am the Master of Mentalism." - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Mann will cast a Fireblade on himself.
"Behold, the power of illusion!" - Unknown Trigger: Mann will cast Pigsie.
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Hex.
strategy: Three-boss Wallaru fight (Hugh, Jack, and Mann). Hugh is Life (22005). Funnel blades to one hitter and pick targets deliberately.
Avoid all-allies heals. Hugh answers a mass heal with Sirens on the healer, so use single-target heals instead.
Watch spell ranks. Jack Shift-Chaneys any wizard who casts a Rank 7 or higher spell, so keep setup low-rank and save the big spell for the kill turn.
Kill Jack in one burst, or before he drops below 80% health. Under 80% he heals himself 10000 over 10 rounds.
Mann Fireblades himself whenever a wizard casts a blade, so expect his damage to climb. Blade the same round you hit.
Hugh steals 3 pips from a random wizard at the end of round 3, so the hitter should not be holding the whole kill setup that turn.
cheatNotes: Cast a mass heal and Hugh hits the healer with Sirens. Stick to single-target healing.
At the end of round 3 Hugh steals 3 pips (and deals damage) from a random wizard. Do not sit on your full kill setup that turn.
Jack Shift-Chaneys anyone who casts a Rank 7 or higher spell. Keep setup low-rank and unload the big spell only when ready to end it.
Once Jack drops below 80% health he heals 10000 over 10 rounds. Take him from healthy to dead in a single burst rather than chipping.
Every blade a wizard casts makes Mann Fireblade himself, ramping his damage. Blade the same turn you hit so he gets fewer stacks.

name: Hunhau
cheats: At the beginning of every Round, Hunhau will cheat one of the following:
Note: Hunhau may target any Wizard.
[Fire] - "Let all flesh burn away!" - Deals 300 Fire Damage over 10 Rounds on the Wizard, applies a 250 Healing Trap on the Wizard, and restores 400 Health over 10 Rounds to self.
[Ice] - "Accept Death's cold embrace." - Applies a 250 Healing Trap on the Wizard, a -80% Life Shield on self, and a -50% negative Healing Charm on the Wizard.
[Storm] - "Thunder gives way to silence." - Applies a Stormblade on self, restores 400 Health over 10 Rounds to self, and applies a -50% negative Healing Charm on the Wizard.
[Myth] - "Even your story will wither." - Deals 300 Fire Damage over 10 Rounds on the Wizard, a 250 Healing Trap on the Wizard, and a -80% Life Shield on self.
[Life] - "Life consumes Life." - Applies a Stormblade on self, deals 300 Fire Damage over 10 Rounds on the Wizard, and restores 400 Health over 10 Rounds to self.
[Death] - "Death is inexorable." - Applies a Stormblade on self, a -80% Life Shield on self, and a -50% negative Healing Charm on the Wizard.
"And now... your suffering ends." - At the end of the Round, when ???, he'll cast a Death version of Meteor Strike that deals 100% max Health Damage on all Wizards.
spellNotes: Casts an all enemies version of Catch of the Day that deals 615 Storm Damage and applies a +45% Death Blade on self.
Casts an all enemies version of Beary Surprise.
Casts a version of Deer Knight that deals 390 Death Damage to all enemies and applies a -20% Damage Charm on one target.
strategy: This is a raid DPS race with heavy anti-healing. Hunhau seeds Healing Traps and -50% Healing Charms on wizards and often heals himself 400 per round, so bring Doom and Gloom or infections to shut down his healing and Cleanse Charm to clear the anti-heal off your team.
Do not attack with Life; many of his cheats give himself a -80% Life Shield. Hit with Storm, Fire, Ice, Myth, or Balance instead.

Every round he casts one of six random elemental effects mixing a Fire DoT, a self-shield, a self-heal, or a Stormblade. Expect a self-blade or shield most rounds and strip or pierce before the hitter goes.
Cleanse the 250 Healing Traps off wizards before your healer acts, or heals get undercut.
Prepare for his end-of-round Death Meteor Strike that hits all wizards for 100% max health. Keep everyone shielded, absorbed, and topped when it looms.
Hitter funnels blades and bursts; supports strip his self-shields and blades; healer fights the anti-heal.
cheatNotes: Every round he fires one of the six elemental cheats below at a random wizard, so plan for a mix of self-buffs and anti-heal each turn.
His per-round cheat can land on any wizard, so nobody is safe from the healing debuffs.
Fire cheat: a 300 Fire DoT and a 250 Healing Trap on a wizard plus a 400 self-heal. Cleanse the trap and cut his heal.
Ice cheat: a Healing Trap on a wizard, a -80% Life Shield on himself, and a -50% Healing Charm on the wizard. Do not hit into that Life shield.
Storm cheat: a Stormblade on himself, a 400 self-heal, and a -50% Healing Charm on a wizard. Strip the blade before he swings.
Myth cheat: a 300 Fire DoT and Healing Trap on a wizard plus a -80% Life Shield on himself.
Life cheat: a Stormblade and self-heal for himself and a 300 Fire DoT on a wizard.
Death cheat: a Stormblade and -80% Life Shield on himself and a -50% Healing Charm on a wizard.
His end-of-round Death Meteor Strike hits every wizard for 100% max health. Be at full with shields up before it fires.

name: Iron Face

Mandar
Quartermane

strategy: 100,000 health, 50% resist to everything, 101% damage boost — full team fight: hitter, two supports, healer.

Survive the every-2nd-round Superhero Strike casts — healer pre-heals into even rounds; 225 critical and 16% pierce make these hurt.
His round-1 Chi aura guts his outgoing healing (-300%), so your damage sticks — no Doom and Gloom needed.
Once he sits at 60,000, stop stalling: his Berserk/Frenzy stacking from Mandar snowballs, and long fights get lethal.
Bring pierce for the 50% universal resist (no boost school exists) and burst the remaining 60,000 with a fully bladed hit through Feints.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he gives himself a 'Chi!!' aura at -300% outgoing healing — he cannot meaningfully heal himself for the whole fight.

He otherwise stacks Berserk, then Frenzy — once his self-damage stops at 60,000, every extra round makes him hit harder. Don't stall past that point.
