cheats: "You are late!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, he will cast Iron Sultan (Shadow).

"Clever, but that is why I have a Golden Dragon!" - On the first Round, his Gold Dragon (Tier 4) uses a Shadow Sentinel on itself that provides 75% Intercept, 100 Critical Block, and -30% Incoming Damage.
"I am METAL!" - Boss will use Cleanse Ward twice on himself to remove all negative Wards, making Indemnity ineffective.
AoE Traps such as Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.
"This one goes up to eleven!" - After X Rounds, Boss will cast a +35% version of Darkwind.
"Heavy fists of lead, fill my victums full of dread!" - After he casts the Global, he will cast Iron Sultan on the Wizard with the lowest Health.
strategy: The Gold Dragon takes 75% of the hits aimed at the Sultan (Shadow Sentinel) and has -30% incoming, so single-target hits mostly land on the Dragon - deal with it first or hit AoE.
Do not rely on single Feints: he Cleanse Wards himself twice to wipe negative wards, which even beats Indemnity.
Use AoE traps like Windstorm instead - they do not trigger his double cleanse, so your traps stick.
Expect a +35% Darkwind (accuracy debuff) after several rounds, followed by an Iron Sultan hit on the lowest-health wizard - keep everyone topped and consider accuracy support.
Set up with AoE traps and blades, then clear the Gold Dragon and Steeleton along with the Sultan in one AoE hit through his 8,800 health.
cheatNotes: Join late and he casts the Shadow version of Iron Sultan - arrive on time.
On Round 1 the Gold Dragon gives itself a Shadow Sentinel: 75% intercept, 100 critical block, and -30% incoming, so it soaks single hits meant for the Sultan.
He casts Cleanse Ward twice on himself to strip all negative wards, so Indemnity will not save a Feint.
AoE traps such as Windstorm do NOT trigger that double cleanse - use them to keep traps on him.
After several rounds he casts a +35% Darkwind, cutting the team's accuracy.
After the global he casts Iron Sultan on whoever has the lowest health - keep everyone topped up.

name: Iron Sultan (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Sinbad & the Iron Sultan Gauntlet - Metallos Summit
cheats: "You are late!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, then the Iron Sultan will interrupt-cast Iron Sultan (Shadow) on the late Wizard.

"Clever, but that is why I have a Golden Dragon!" - At the beginning of the first Round, the Gold Dragon will interrupt-cast a version of Shadow Sentinel on itself that provides 75% Intercept, 100 Critical Block, and -30% incoming Damage.

"Time to strike while the Iron Sultan is hot!" - If Wizards cast an all-enemies or targeted enemies Spell, then the Iron Sultan will interrupt-cast a version of Dryad that restores 800 Health to himself and all allies.
"I am METAL!" - If a Feint or Mass Feint is cast on the Iron Sultan, he will use Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) twice on himself to remove all harmful Wards.
"This one goes up to eleven!" - At the end of every fifth Round, the Iron Sultan will interrupt-cast a +35% version of Darkwind.
"Heavy fists of lead, fill my victums full of dread!" - Immediately after Darkwind is cast, the Iron Sultan will cast Iron Sultan on the Wizard with the lowest Health.
strategy: Kill the Gold Dragon first. On Round 1 it self-casts a Shadow Sentinel giving 75% Intercept (plus 100 Critical Block and -30% incoming), so it soaks 75% of any damage aimed at the Sultan or Steeletons. Use a modest AoE with a few blades to drop the Dragon before hitting anything else.
Hit with Myth (+10%) and never attack into his 60% Storm resist.
Don't chip. Any all-enemy or targeted-enemy spell makes him interrupt-cast Dryad, healing himself and all allies 800, so set up fully and land one killing hit rather than several small ones.
Skip Feint entirely. A Feint or Mass Feint makes him Cleanse Ward twice and wipe all your traps, so blade up and use non-Feint traps for the setup.
Once the Dragon is down, funnel blades to the hitter and stack non-Feint traps on the Sultan and Steeleton(s), then finish both together with one big AoE. His incoming healing is already cut 50%.

Race the Round-5 window. At the end of every fifth Round he casts a +35% Darkwind and immediately follows with Iron Sultan on the lowest-health wizard for 3,000+ damage. Try to win before it, keep that wizard topped, and bring a healer if the fight runs long. Never flee and return, as a late wizard is instantly hit by Iron Sultan (Shadow).
cheatNotes: Don't flee and rejoin, and don't lag entering. A late wizard is immediately hit by Iron Sultan (Shadow).
Round 1 the Gold Dragon shields itself with 75% Intercept, 100 Critical Block, and -30% incoming, so it eats 75% of any damage meant for the Sultan or Steeletons. Kill the Dragon first.
Any all-enemy or targeted-enemy spell makes him interrupt-Dryad, healing himself and both minions 800. Don't chip him; set up fully and land one killing hit.
Feinting backfires. A Feint or Mass Feint makes him Cleanse Ward (-All) twice and remove all harmful wards, so use non-Feint traps instead.
At the end of every fifth Round he casts a +35% Darkwind on himself, boosting his next hit.
Right after that Darkwind he hits your lowest-health wizard with Iron Sultan for over 3,000 damage. Shield and heal that wizard on Round five, or win before it.

guide.title: Sinbad and the Iron Sultan Gauntlet

name: Ivan
locations: Polaris: Frigid Maw - Frigid Mine Docks
cheats: In the first Round, Ivan will cast a Balance Spell to summon three Pit Fiend Minions.They become active in Round 2.
strategy: Round 1 Ivan summons three Pit Fiends that act from Round 2. Expect four incoming attacks per round after that.
He resists Fire 80% and takes +40% from Ice and Storm. Bring Ice or Storm.
Use the free Round 1 (minions inactive) to blade, then shield or heal through rounds 2-3 while you add a Feint.
Finish with one big Ice or Storm AoE so the Pit Fiends die with him.
cheatNotes: He opens by summoning three Pit Fiend minions; they sit out Round 1 and act from Round 2. Use that quiet round to start setup, and plan an AoE finish so they all die together.

name: Ixcax CursedWing (Tier 1)
locations: Azteca: Three Points - Ixcax CursedWing's Chamber
cheats: [Feint] "Two can play that game" - If a Wizard casts a Feint on Ixcax, she will cast a Feint on the Wizard.
[Aura] "Vengeance will be mine!" - If a Tzeltan Cohort is defeated, Ixcax will cast Berserk, and the remaining Minions will cast Fortify.

[Manipulation] "Time to mix things up" - At the end of every fifth Round, Ixcax casts a version of Cleanse Ward (-All) that removes all harmful Wards from her team, and casts a unique Storm School Mass Prism on all enemies that converts any incoming Damage to another School of magic (i.e Death, Fire, Storm, Life, Ice, or Myth).
strategy: Attack with Myth or Life; she takes +35% from both and resists Storm 80%. Never hit into Storm.
Do not Feint her. She Feints you back, so stack blades on your hitter instead of trapping her.
Do not pick off the Tzeltan Cohort early. Killing it makes Ixcax cast Berserk and the rest Fortify, a damage spike. Clear both together with an AoE.
Beat her before the end of round 5. At every fifth round she strips all traps off her team and throws a Mass Prism that reroutes your incoming damage to a random school.
Stack blades over rounds 1 through 4, then land one big Myth or Life AoE that kills her and the Cohort together.
cheatNotes: Feinting Ixcax makes her Feint you back. Skip Feint and stack blades on your hitter instead.
Killing the Tzeltan Cohort makes Ixcax cast Berserk and the remaining minions Fortify, a damage spike. Do not pick the minion off early; clear both with one AoE.
At the end of every fifth round she strips all traps off her team and casts a Storm Mass Prism that reroutes your incoming damage to a random school. Land your hit before round 5.

name: Ixcax CursedWing (Tier 2)
locations: Azteca: Three Points - Ixcax CursedWing's Chamber
cheats: "Death had no hold on me!" - If a Wizard casts a Deathblade, or if a Tzeltan Cohort casts a Deathblade on Ixcax CursedWing, she will cast a version of Disarm on herself to remove the Deathblade and cast Virulent Plague.
"Vengeance will be mine!" - If an ally or enemy is defeated, she will cheat-cast Berserk, and the remaining Minions will cast Fortify.
"Two can play at that game!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Ixcax, she will cheat-cast a Feint on the Wizard.

"Time to mix things up" - At the end of every fifth Round, Ixcax cheat-casts a version of Cleanse Ward (-All) that removes all negative wards from her team, and casts a unique Storm School Mass Prism on all enemies that converts any incoming Damage to another School of magic (i.e Death, Fire, Storm, Life, Ice, or Myth).
strategy: Team fight — 19,200 health plus Tzeltan Cohorts. Hit with Myth or Life (+35%); never Storm into her 80% resist.

Work in 5-round windows: at the end of every 5th round she cleanses every negative ward off herself and mass-prisms your whole team into a random school. Traps go on early in the window; the hit lands before the round-5 reset.
No Deathblades from anyone — she Disarms them and answers with Virulent Plague.
Feints get counter-Feinted onto the caster — if you Feint her, hit the same round or shield the Feinted wizard immediately.
Any kill — a Cohort or a teammate — triggers her Berserk while surviving minions Fortify. Clear the Cohorts early, shield through the spike, then set up the boss window.
If the fight crosses a round-5 prism, check what school your damage now converts to before committing the big hit.
cheatNotes: Any Deathblade — even one a Cohort puts on her — gets Disarmed off and answered with Virulent Plague (-healing on your team). Keep Death charms out of team decks.
Every defeat on either side makes her cast Berserk while remaining minions Fortify — expect a damage spike the round after anything dies; pre-shield when you kill a Cohort.
Feint her and she Feints you back — a +70% trap on your head with minions up is dangerous. Feint only on the same round as the hit, or shield the marked wizard.

End of every 5th round she cleanses all negative wards off her team and casts a mass Storm prism that converts your incoming damage to a random school — traps placed on round 5 are wasted, and post-prism hits may change school. Set up and strike inside each 5-round window.

name: Ixcax CursedWing (Tier 4)
locations: Azteca: Three Points - Ixcax CursedWing's Chamber
cheats: "Vengeance will be mine!" - If an ally or enemy is defeated, she will cheat-cast Berserk, and the remaining Minions will cast Fortify.
"Two can play at that game!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Ixcax, she will cheat-cast a Feint on the Wizard.

"Time to mix things up" - At the end of every fifth Round, Ixcax cheat-casts a version of Cleanse Ward (-All) that removes all negative Wards from her team, and casts a unique Storm School Mass Prism on all enemies that converts any incoming Damage to another School of magic (i.e.; Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, or Death).
strategy: Run a full team of Support / Support / Healer / Hitter; the beguile pressure makes soloing unreliable.
Kill the three Tzeltan Cohort minions first (7,440 HP each). They spam Winter Moon and Death Crow, which beguile your wizards, so removing them cuts the crowd control.
Supports spend Rounds 1-3 stacking Potent Feint / TC Feint plus blades (and a prism if the hitter is resisted) while everyone buffs at once. Hit with Myth or Life; she takes +35% from both.
Do not cast a plain Feint on Ixcax; she Feints you back. Use Potent or treasure-card Feints so you still stack roughly +80% and +30% trap value.

Carry Storm Prisms if hitting Storm through her 80% Storm resist, keep a couple of cleanse charms for the Virulent Plague she throws at Death blades, and never stand directly in front of her because of her Enfeeble.

Time the hit before the end of Round 5: at that point she cleanses all traps and prisms off her team and casts a Mass Prism that flips your damage school, so land the kill first or reset your setup after.
Expect a spike whenever anything dies: she casts Berserk on herself and her minions Fortify, so heal through it.
cheatNotes: Whenever any wizard or minion is defeated, Ixcax casts Berserk on herself (more damage and pierce) and the surviving minions cast Fortify (less damage taken), so expect a damage spike right after a kill.
If you place a normal Feint on her she immediately Feints you back; use Potent or treasure-card Feints instead so the retaliation matters less.

At the end of every fifth round she cleanses all negative Wards (your traps) off her team and casts a unique Mass Storm Prism that converts your incoming damage to a random school, so hit before that or re-set afterward.

name: Izft
locations: Krokotopia: Upper Zigazag - House of Scales
cheats: "You can have this one!" - If a Damage over Time is cast on Izft, he will cast Shift on a random Wizard.
"Your usefulness has run out!" - When any Minion gets less than 25% of its maximum Health, Izft will cheat-cast a Draw Power to the damaged Minion, one Minion per Round.
If a Minion is defeated by Izft and Izft gets defeated in the same Round, the Minion will respawn.
Izft will cast Draw Power on himself if there are only two Minions and both get defeated on the same Round. (One gets sacrificed by Izft, and the other is defeated by a Wizard).
Izft will respawn after the battle.
strategy: Hit with Storm or Death — +30% to both. His Myth resist is 80%.
No DoTs. Any DoT on Izft gets answered with a Shift onto a random wizard.
Do not whittle the Condemned Manders below 25% health — Izft answers by cheating Draw Power on the damaged minion, sacrificing it to fuel himself.
The clean kill is one bladed Storm or Death AoE that drops Izft and the minions together. His respawn cheats only matter around him sacrificing minions, so a single burst sidesteps the whole mess.
He crits often (97 critical rating) — keep your health up while setting up.
cheatNotes: Skip DoT spells entirely. Casting one on Izft gets it Shifted onto a random wizard.
If any minion drops under 25% health, Izft cheats Draw Power on it — sacrificing it for his own benefit, one per round. Do not leave minions barely alive; kill them outright or leave them healthy.
Edge case: if Izft sacrificed a minion and you defeat Izft the same round, that minion comes back. Kill minions with your own damage, or take Izft down on a round he has not just sacrificed one.
If only two minions remain and both die in one round (one sacrificed, one killed by you), he Draw Powers himself. Another reason to burst everything at once instead of trading kills.

name: Jabberwock
cheats: [Round] At the end of the 3rd round, and every third round afterwards (6, 9, 12, etc), he will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Meteor Strike that deals 1,500 Fire damage. A +200% universal Trap will appear on him afterward. (Any Wizards in Shadow Shrike form at this time will receive -10 backlash.)
[Manipulation] At the start of the 6th round, and every third round afterward (9, 12, 15, etc), he will cheat-cast a version of Cleanse Ward (-All) to remove all Traps and Prisms from himself.
strategy: Pack Ice or Storm - he takes +55% from both. Fire is resisted 120%, so Fire wizards need a prism.

Blade rounds 1-3 and budget a shield or heal for the end of round 3: he cheats an enhanced Meteor Strike for 1,500 Fire there and every third round after. Stay out of Shadow Shrike form on those rounds (-10 backlash).
Exploit the aftermath: a +200% universal trap appears on HIM after each Meteor. Rounds 4-5 are your kill window - unload into that free trap.
Do not bank your own traps or prisms long: starting round 6 (then 9, 12), he Cleanse Wards everything off himself at the start of the round. Place Feints and prisms the same round you hit.
Ideal solo line: set up rounds 1-3, eat the round-3 Meteor, then kill through the +200% trap on round 4 or 5 before the round-6 cleanse.
cheatNotes: Every third round ends with a 1,500 Fire Meteor Strike - plan shields or heals for rounds 3, 6, 9 - and a +200% universal trap then appears on him. That trap is your kill window: hit hard the round after each Meteor. Avoid Shadow Shrike form on Meteor rounds (-10 backlash).
From round 6 on, every third round starts with him cleansing all traps and prisms off himself. Set traps the same round you hit, and spend the free +200% trap before the cleanse takes it.

name: Jack of Knaves
locations: Arcanum: Lower Decks - Lumi Hostage Room

stats.resist: 40% to all schools70% to all schools

cheats: [Round] "I am the master of all!" - At the beginning of every Third Round, Jack of Knaves:
casts a Balance Spell that applies one of the following effects at random:
Damage over Time effect that deals 462 Fire Damage over 5 Rounds
-80% Storm Shield
Stormblade
+30% Myth Trap
Heal Over Time effect for 327 Health over 5 Rounds
-50% Healing Charm
And then casts one of the following Spells at random:
Krampus (Tier 3b)
Reindeer Knight (Tier 2c)
Catch of the Day (Tier 5c)
Keeper of the Flame (Tier 5a)
Goat Monk (Tier 5a)
Headless Horseman (Tier 3b)
strategy: Solo-friendly (15,935 health, no minions). Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (all +30%); he resists Balance 70% and everything else 40%.
His only cheat fires every third round: a random hanging effect (DoT, shield, blade, trap, HoT, or -50% heal charm) followed by a random summon or hit like Krampus, Goat Monk, or a Headless Horseman.
Use rounds 1 and 2 to blade and Feint, then land your hit, timing big hits to avoid feeding the round-3 summon a fresh target.
Carry Triage for the possible 462 Fire DoT and Cleanse Charm for the -50% healing charm.
Stack blades onto a single strong hit and burst him between his third-round cheats.
cheatNotes: Every third round he casts a random hanging effect and then a random summon or attack spell.

name: Jackson Daytona

stats.resist: 43% to all schools73% to all schools

cheats: [Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Jackson Daytona or his minions are hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on themself:
If they are not defeated by the attack, they will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If they are defeated by the attack, they will cast a version of Minor Blessing on themself that heals for 100% of their max Health.

[Spell] "Is that the sun?!" - If either Jackson Daytona or his Minions are hit by a Spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal Dispel Charm on themself.
strategy: Do not drain and do not use Sun-symbol spells. Both trigger self-buffs or protection cheats, so build damage the plain way with blades and Feints.

Skip Death drains entirely. Hitting Jackson or his minions with a Drain either gives him an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade (if it doesn't kill) or a full heal (if it does), so a Scarecrow-style finisher backfires.
Avoid Sun-symbol spells (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) unless they kill outright, since a survivor self-casts a protected universal Dispel that eats your next hit.
He is Death with 73% Death resist and takes 30% boosted Life, so bring a Life hitter or Life prisms and a normal (non-drain) attack school for the finish.
This is a team fight: 17,140 Health and three minions. One hitter, supports Feint the boss and blade the hitter, healer manages the group.
Stack blades and traps on one non-drain, non-Sun AoE and clear Jackson with his minions (Nandoor, Nadya, Robin) in a single burst to avoid the heal-on-kill cheat retriggering.
cheatNotes: If Jackson or a minion is hit by a Drain spell, that enemy self-casts in response, so avoid Death drains as your damage.
If the drain does not kill the target, it gains an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade, making it hit much harder.
If the drain does kill the target, it instead full-heals via Minor Blessing, undoing your damage.
If Jackson or a minion is hit by a Sun-symbol spell (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) and survives, it self-casts a protected universal Dispel that blocks your next attack.

name: Jacques the Scratcher (Rank 11)
cheats: "For me? You shouldn't have!" - If any type of universal Shield is cast, Jacques the Scratcher will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield.
"No need for such things here!" - If any type of School-specific Shield is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield, and then proceed to destroy it.
"You can't have nice things when there are cats around!" - If any type of Heal over time spell is cast, he will cheat-cast Snow Drift on the receiver of the heal.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Allow me to scratch some of that dirt off you, friend." - If an enemy takes Damage from a DoT spell, he will cheat-cast Cooldown on the ally.
strategy: Do not shield: universal Shields get stolen off you, and school Shields are stolen and then destroyed, so rely on resist rather than Towers.
Avoid Heal-over-Times, including pet heals, since each one draws a Snow Drift onto the healed wizard.
He resists Ice 80% and takes 50% more from Fire, so bring Fire and skip Ice attacks.
Blade up a Fire hitter and burn him down directly rather than leaning on shields, HoTs, or DoTs.
cheatNotes: Cast any universal Shield and he cheat-casts Steal Ward on whoever received it, taking it for himself.
Cast any school-specific Shield and he steals it and then destroys it outright.
Cast any Heal-over-Time and he answers with Snow Drift on whoever got the heal.
Even pet HoTs trigger the Snow Drift cheat.
When an enemy takes damage from your DoT, he casts Cooldown on his ally.

name: Jacques the Scratcher (Rank 14)
cheats: "For me? You shouldn't have!" - If any type of universal Shield is cast, Jacques the Scratcher will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield.
"No need for such things here!" - If any type of School-specific Shield is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield, and then proceed to destroy it.
"You can't have nice things when there are cats around!" - If any type of Heal over time Spell is cast, he will cheat-cast Snow Drift on the receiver of the heal.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Allow me to scratch some of that dirt off you, friend." - If an enemy takes Damage from a DoT Spell, he will cheat-cast Cooldown on the ally.
strategy: Exalted challenge team fight. Hit with Fire (boosted); avoid Ice (80% resist).
Do not cast shields. Universal shields get stolen, and school shields are stolen then destroyed. Rely on direct heals and fast damage.
Avoid Heal-over-time spells and pet HoTs; each triggers a Snow Drift on the target. Use direct heals instead.
Skip DoTs. When his side takes DoT damage he casts Cooldown (a heal) on that ally.
Blade and Feint funneled to the hitter, then burst the 13,850 health.
cheatNotes: Any universal shield you cast gets stolen off the receiver immediately. Do not bother shielding.
School-specific shields are stolen and then destroyed, so they are gone for good. Skip shields entirely.
Heal-over-time spells make him Snow Drift the target. Use direct heals instead.
Pet HoTs trigger the Snow Drift too.
Landing a DoT on his side makes him Cooldown that ally, healing it. Avoid DoTs and hit direct.

name: Jade Oni (Challenge)
cheats: Jade Oni
"Jade ancestors, protect me!" - On the fifth Round, Jade Oni will cast a Spirit Armor on himself.
"Your Doom is Imminent" - Jade Oni casts a Spirit Blade on self, warning of the impending gambit.
"You were warned. Bwahaha!" - If Jade Oni reaches a gambit of having four Blades, he will cast Primal Oni. Blades will be removed after this gambit has been used.
Death Oni
"Spirits of the Dead Protect Me!" - Death Oni casts a Spirit Shield on himself after using a Shield spell.
"Think you're tough?" - If Death Oni reaches a gambit of having four Shields, he will cast Doom Oni. Wards will be removed after this gambit has been used.
Plague Oni
"A pox upon your house!" - On the third Round, Plague Oni casts Plague.
"Your Doom is Imminent" - Plague Oni casts a Balanceblade on itself and Mass Infection, when it has a Blade, and a Wizard has a negative Charm. This warns of the impending gambit.
This Balanceblade can stack with the Balanceblade he casts from his hand.

"Greed will be your downfall!" - If Plague Oni reaches a gambit of having two Blades on itself, and 2 harmful Charms on the Wizard, he will cast Tribunal Oni. All Charms will be removed after this gambit on both teams.
War Oni
"Let the war rage on!" - War Oni casts Sprite on himself after he has a HOT on himself.
"Survival is the dominian of the fittest!" - If War Oni reaches a gambit of having two HOTs, he will cast Infernal Oni. HOTs will be removed after this gambit has been used.
strategy: He resists 20% to every school, so any strong hit works. Expect big AoE Oni gambits from each minion.

Each Oni charges a gambit and unleashes an AoE at its threshold: Jade at 4 Spirit Blades (Primal Oni), Death at 4 Shields (Doom Oni), Plague at 2 blades plus 2 harmful charms on you (Tribunal Oni), War at 2 HoTs (Infernal Oni). The Your Doom is Imminent line warns a gambit is one step away.
Kill the minions before they complete gambits, prioritizing whichever is closest. AoE damage thins all of them at once.
Deny Plague Oni's Tribunal by keeping harmful charms off yourself, cleanse weaknesses and infections so its condition never completes.
Jade Oni self-heals hard (60% outgoing healing, Spirit Armor on Round 5), so save your burst to drop him after the minions and hit through his heals with one large blow.
cheatNotes: On Round 5 Jade Oni shields himself with Spirit Armor.
Jade Oni gives himself a Spirit Blade, warning his 4-blade gambit is near.
At 4 blades Jade Oni casts Primal Oni (big AoE), then loses the blades, kill him or strip blades before he gets there.
Death Oni adds a Spirit Shield to himself whenever he shields.
At 4 shields Death Oni casts Doom Oni (AoE), then loses the wards, pierce or race it down.
On Round 3 Plague Oni casts Plague on your team.
Plague Oni casts Balanceblade and Mass Infection when it has a blade and you carry a negative charm, warning its gambit is building.
That Balanceblade stacks with the one it casts from hand, so it ramps damage quickly.
At 2 blades on itself plus 2 harmful charms on you, Plague Oni casts Tribunal Oni and wipes all charms on both sides, keep harmful charms cleansed to deny it.
War Oni adds a Sprite HoT to itself whenever it has a heal-over-time.
At 2 HoTs War Oni casts Infernal Oni (AoE), then loses the HoTs, kill it or apply Infection to block its heals.

name: Jade Oni (Standard)
cheats: [Ward] "Jade ancestors, protect me!" - On the fifth Round, Jade Oni will cast a Spirit Armor on himself.
[Trap] "Your Tricks become my Treasure" - If a Trap is placed on Jade Oni, he will cast Oni's Naturalism.
Multi-Target Spells, such as Windstorm, will trigger this cheat.
[Damage over Time] "Sharing is Caring. Bwahaha!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time on Jade Oni, he will cast Camp Bandit (Tier 4b).
"Your Doom is Imminent" - Jade Oni casts a Spirit Blade on self, warning of the impending Gambit.
"You were warned. Bwahaha!" - If Jade Oni reaches a Gambit of having four Blades, he will cast Primal Oni. All Blades will be removed from all enemies after this Gambit has been used.
[Blade] "You dare mock Death?" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Death Oni will cast Putrefaction on the receiver.
Aegis will not protect Blades from removal.
Spells with secondary effects will trigger this cheat.
Multi-Target Blade Spells will trigger an AoE version of Putrefaction.
[Heal over Time] "You must play by the Death Oni's Rules!" - If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time, Death Oni will cast Oni's Morbidity.
[Ward] "Spirits of the Dead Protect Me!" - Death Oni casts a Spirit Shield on himself after casting a Shield, warning of the impending Gambit.
"Think you're tough?" - If Death Oni reaches a Gambit of having four Shields, he will cast Doom Oni. All Wards will be removed from all enemies after this Gambit has been used.
[Curse] "A pox upon your house!" - On the third Round, Plague Oni casts Plague.
[Blade] "Those with the Most have the Most to lose." - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Plague Oni will cast Oni's Shadow.
[Trap] "Time for the tables to turn" - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Plague Oni will cast Righting the Scales on the caster.
Multi-Target Spells will trigger this cheat.
"Your Doom is Imminent" - Plague Oni casts a Balanceblade on itself and Mass Infection, when it has a Blade, and a Wizard has a negative Charm, warning of the impending Gambit.
This Balanceblade can stack with the Balanceblade he casts from his hand.

"Greed will be your downfall!" - If Plague Oni reaches a Gambit of having two Blades on itself, and 2 harmful Charms on the Wizard, he will cast Tribunal Oni. All Charms will be removed from all enemies and allies after this Gambit has been used.
[Ward] "Your armaments are no match for the Heat of the Forge" - If a Wizard casts a Shield, War Oni will cast Oni's Forge on the receiver.
"Let the war rage on!":
[All Enemies] If War Oni is damaged with an AoE Spell, he will cast Pigsie (Tier 5b).
If War Oni is defeated with an AoE Spell, he will instead cast a version of Pigsie that restores 100% Health.
[Heal over Time] If War Oni has a HoT, he casts Sprite on himself, warning of the impending Gambit.
"Survival is the dominion of the fittest!" - If War Oni reaches a Gambit of having two HoTs, he will cast Infernal Oni. All HoTs will be removed from all enemies after this Gambit has been used.
strategy: The guide runs a four-player team: one Balance blader/support, a second blader of any school, and two hitters from different schools, funneling blades to the hitters.

Its opener is a Bladestorm chain — Round 1 Pet Bladestorm and Sharpened Blade Bladestorm; Round 2 Item/TC Bladestorm and Sharpened variants (a Violet Hood of Bitter Truth adds +25 to Bladestorm) — while the hitters prep Frenzy, Scarecrow, or Storm Lord.

Heed the guide's own caveat: the pure Bladestorm route is unreliable now, and because nearly every action you cast triggers an Oni counter (blades draw Putrefaction/Oni's Shadow, traps draw Naturalism/Righting the Scales, shields draw Oni's Forge, HoTs and DoTs also punish), keep setup minimal and coordinated.

Watch the Gambit warnings ('Your Doom is Imminent'). A filled gambit wipes all blades (Primal Oni), wards (Doom Oni), charms (Tribunal Oni), or HoTs (Infernal Oni) from the team, so land your hit before a gambit completes.
Kill War Oni single-target first, as the commenter notes — AoE on him makes him Pigsie-heal, and killing him with AoE fully heals him.
Funnel blades to a hitter in one round and burst Jade Oni with Death; heal through Plague on Round 3 and expect his Spirit Armor absorb on Round 5, so time the kill before then.
cheatNotes: On Round 5 Jade Oni shields himself with Spirit Armor, so plan your big hit before then or expect an absorb.
Trapping Jade Oni triggers Oni's Naturalism, so go easy on traps against him.
Multi-target spells like Windstorm count as trapping him and trigger it too.
A DoT on Jade Oni summons Camp Bandit, so avoid damage-over-time hits here.
This warns that Jade Oni is one step from his blade gambit.
When Jade Oni reaches four blades he casts Primal Oni and strips every blade from your team, so hit before he fills it.
Any blade you cast gets Putrefaction from Death Oni on the recipient, and Aegis will not save it.
Spells with secondary effects also count as blades for this cheat.
Multi-target blades trigger an AoE Putrefaction on the whole team.
Heal-over-time spells trigger Death Oni's Oni's Morbidity, so avoid HoT heals.
Death Oni shields himself toward a ward gambit whenever he shields.
At four shields Death Oni casts Doom Oni and removes all wards from your team.
Plague Oni casts Plague on Round 3, so expect an early accuracy and weakness debuff.
Blades also make Plague Oni cast Oni's Shadow, another reason to keep blades few.
Trapping makes Plague Oni cast Righting the Scales on you.
Multi-target spells trigger this too.
This warns that Plague Oni is building toward his charm-removal gambit.
When Plague Oni has two blades and you have two harmful charms he casts Tribunal Oni, wiping all charms from everyone.
Shielding makes War Oni cast Oni's Forge on the recipient, so limit shields.
Damaging War Oni with an AoE makes him Pigsie-heal, so hit him single-target instead.
Killing War Oni with an AoE fully heals him, so finish him with a single-target hit.
A HoT on War Oni builds his gambit, another reason to skip HoTs.
At two HoTs War Oni casts Infernal Oni and strips all HoTs from the team.

strategy: Rematch team fight: Jade Oni resists 40% of every school and has three Oni minions that each punish a different setup tool - coordinate roles and a kill order.

Almost every tool is punished: DoTs get shifted back to the caster (Jade), blades draw Putrefaction from Death Oni (Aegis will not stop it), HoTs draw Oni's Morbidity, shields draw War Oni's Forge, and traps draw counters even from AoE like Windstorm.
Kill Death Oni early so the hitter can blade safely, then favor auras (Frenzy, Amplify) over charms where possible to buff without triggering punishes.
Avoid HoTs and DoTs entirely; heal with direct heals only, and note that casting Feint may make him Dark Pact himself (unconfirmed).
Jade Oni casts Spirit Armor on Round 5 - burst him before or straight through that absorb, and AoE to clear the minions with him.
Watch Plague on Round 3 and Tribunal Oni aimed at the highest-pip wizard; keep the hitter from over-stacking pips before the strike.

Even AoE traps like Windstorm set off that Naturalism heal, so there is no safe trap here.

War Oni heals itself with Sprite.

name: Jake the Smoke Monster
cheats: "Who misses Malistaire? I do!" - At the beginning of every second Round, Jake the Smoke Monster will cast Shift Malistaire on a random Wizard, dealing 85 Death Damage per Pip.
"In my version... Xibalba hit Celestia!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura, Jake the Smoke Monster will cast Mass Supernova.
"Everything Old is mine now!" - Jake the Smoke Monster will summon a Spiderling Minion.
"Behold my originality!" - If no Minions are left, Jake the Smoke Monster will summon another Jake the Smoke Monster (Minion).
"Electromagnetism!" - If a Wizard casts a negative Charm, Jake the Smoke Monster will cast a version of Insane Bolt (Moon), dealing either 550, 1,050, or 1,550 Damage to all enemies.
Spells that apply negative Charms as a secondary effect will trigger this cheat.
"Boom! Morganthe is back!" - After an unknown number of turns, Jake the Smoke Monster will cast a version of Death Morganthe Attack that deals 100% of the wizard's health.
When 2 or more Wizards are present
"Ooooh mystery!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Global, or a Trap Spell, Jake the Smoke Monster will respond with this.
"I dont like themes!" - If another Wizard casts a second Blade Spell, Global, or Trap on the same turn:
Second Blade and/or Global Spell: Jake the Smoke Monster will use an Ice version of Enfeeble that removes all Blades from all enemies.
Second Trap Spell: Jake the Smoke Monster will use a Fire version of Cleanse Ward that removes all Traps from all allies.
Note: Both a Blade/Global and a Trap can be cast in the same turn. The cheat only triggers if two of the same hanging effects are cast.
strategy: He constantly summons: a Spiderling and, when no minions remain, another copy of himself; carry AoE to keep the board clear.
Do not cast Auras. An Aura makes him respond with Mass Supernova.
Do not cast negative Charms on him. Any negative charm, even as a secondary effect, triggers Insane Bolt for up to 1,550 to your whole team.
Every second Round he casts Shift Malistaire for 85 Death per pip; keep shields up on that timer.
After some turns he casts a Death Morganthe attack for 100% of a wizard's health, so keep everyone healthy and do not overextend.
Solo him if you can; with two or more wizards he punishes stacked blades, Globals, and traps by removing them.
cheatNotes: Every second Round he hits a random wizard with Shift Malistaire for 85 Death per pip; expect chip damage on that timer.
Casting an Aura makes him retaliate with Mass Supernova; skip Auras here.
He summons a Spiderling minion; keep AoE ready.
With no minions left he summons another copy of himself, so the board rarely stays empty.
Any negative Charm you place makes him Insane Bolt your whole team for 550, 1,050, or 1,550; avoid weaknesses, mantles, and infections.
Spells that apply a negative charm as a side effect also set off the Insane Bolt; check secondary effects before casting.
After an unpredictable number of turns he casts a Morganthe attack for 100% of a wizard's health; keep everyone near full and end the fight before it stacks up.
With two or more wizards, the first blade, Global, or trap each turn just gets a verbal response; the danger is a second one below.
If a second wizard casts the same hanging-effect type that turn, he punishes it, so coordinate to avoid doubling up.
A second blade or Global in one turn makes him Enfeeble off all your blades; stagger your buffs across turns.
A second trap in one turn makes him Cleanse all your traps; place traps one wizard per turn.

name: Jamburglar
cheats: "More Customers? You must pay for that!" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, Jamburglar will cast a single target Shadow version of Power Nova that takes 100% of their max health, instantly defeating them.

"Prepare to be Jamburgled!" - At the end of each Round after the first, Jamburglar casts a Shadow version of Power Nova that takes 15% of each Wizard's max Health and 10% of his own.
This Spell can trigger Jamburglar's Adapt, giving him a Power Pip from being hit by this Spell.
Spells that take a percentage of Health ignore all stats and hanging effects.
"Gum's Away!" - If anyone in the Wizard's side doesn't have Impede active, Jamburglar will cast it on them.
This cheat applies to summoned minions.
Bug: Dead Wizards will cause Jamburglar to repeatedly shout this cheat line as he detects them not having the aura active.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Shadowblade and Shadow Trap.
strategy: Shadow boss (11000) with 70% universal resist. Over-stack blades and traps; +5% Balance is the only boost school, so lean on setup rather than raw school damage.
Round 1 he wipes everyone's pips, gives all wizards 7 Power Pips, and slaps a 99-round Impede aura on you that chokes pip gain. Treat those 7 power pips as your budget: build and spend them.
Every round after the first he Power Novas the team for 15% of max health. Your healing is cut 45%, so race him and hit before the chip adds up.
Do not waste turns replacing the Impede aura; he re-applies it immediately. Work through it.
Stack blades and a Feint in one or two rounds and unload your 7-pip hit.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins after round 1 is instantly defeated by a full-health Power Nova. Everyone must be in from the start.

Round 1 he zeroes your pips, hands everyone 7 Power Pips, applies a 99-round Impede that throttles pip gain, and Blinding Lights you. Plan to win on those 7 pips and carry accuracy for the fizzle risk.
Every round after the first he Power Novas the whole team for 15% max health (and 10% of his own). Your healing is reduced 45%, so end it fast.
His own Power Nova can feed him a Power Pip via Adapt, so he may hit harder than expected. Do not stall.
If anyone loses the Impede aura he immediately re-applies it. Do not waste a turn clearing it; play through the pip choke.

name: Kain von Shane (Challenge)

stats.resist: 33% to all schools53% to all schools73% to all schools

cheats: [Feint] "I am Lord Kain von Shane and I deserve dignity!" - If a feint is cast on Kain von Shane, he will cast a version of Dark Tribute on himself. This is an old version of Dark Pact that removes universal traps and applies a +30% balance blade on Shane.
[Curse] "I will not bow down to you!" - If a harmful Charm is cast on Kain von Shane, then he will interrupt-cast Disarm on the wizard who cast the Charm.
[Steal] "Don't drain me!" - If a Drain spell is cast on Kain von Shane, he will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
[Heal] "More life for me!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, then Kain von Shane will interrupt-cast a version of Vampire that deals Death Drain Damage and returns half Health to the caster.
Pet Maycast Heals will trigger this cheat.
This cheat will not trigger for Beguiled Wizards.
[Spell] "Who let the sun in here?" - If a Wizard casts as spell that has a sun in it (such as Power Nova,) Kain von Shane will cast a Protected Universal Dispel on himself.
Interrupt Cycle: This cycle begins on the second round and repeats every 3 rounds until it is reset.

[Blade] "Build my strength..." - At the beginning of the first round in this cycle, Kain von Shane will interrupt-cast a Star version of Supercharge that applies a +10% per pip universal damage charm on himself and gives himself a pip.
[Dispel] "...stun my prey..." - At the beginning of the second round in this cycle, Kain von Shane will interrupt-cast a Star spell that applies a Dispel to every wizard of their own school.
[Friend][Damage] "...and now the strike!" - At the beginning of the third round in this cycle, Kain von Shane will interrupt-cast Monster Mash on a random wizard.
Cycle Reset:
[Damage] "*hiss* Where was I?" - If Kain von Shane is targeted with an attack spell, then his cheat cycle will pause for one round and reset to the beginning.
[Damage over Time] Damage over time spells will only reset his cheat cycle on the turn they are cast.
strategy: Attack with Ice or Myth, his lowest resists at 33%; avoid Storm and Life (73% resist) and expect 53% on most other schools.
Keep steady hits on Kain to stall his interrupt cycle. Attacking him pauses and resets the cycle, which is the cleanest way to stop the Round 3 Monster Mash from ever landing.
Do not Feint or Drain him. Feinting removes your universal traps and gives him a +30% Balance blade, and Draining gives him an Aegis-protected +100% Balance blade.
Avoid unnecessary heals and weaknesses. Healing triggers a Vampire interrupt, harmful charms trigger a Disarm, and sun-enchanted spells like Power Nova make him Universal Dispel himself.
Beat his cycle-round-two own-school Dispel by hitting off-school or with a prism so your intended school is not blocked.
At 16,110 Health and not stunable, run it as a team: one hitter keeping pressure on Kain, supports funneling blades without Feints, and careful healing.
cheatNotes: Feinting him triggers Dark Tribute: he removes all universal traps and gives himself a +30% Balance blade. Skip Feint here.
Casting a weakness or other harmful charm on him makes him interrupt-Disarm the caster.
Casting a Drain spell on him gives him an Aegis-protected +100% Balance blade, so do not drain him.
Casting any heal makes him interrupt with a Vampire that drains him and returns half the Health to the caster, so avoid unnecessary heals.
Pet may-cast heals also trigger the Vampire interrupt, so watch your pet.
Beguiled wizards do not trigger the heal cheat.
Casting a spell with a sun enchant, like Power Nova, makes him put a protected Universal Dispel on himself, so avoid sun-enchanted attacks.
Cycle Round 1: he Supercharges himself, gaining a +10%-per-pip universal damage charm and a pip.
Cycle Round 2: he Dispels every wizard's own school, so hit with an off-school or prism to avoid the Dispel.
Cycle Round 3: he casts Monster Mash on a random wizard, the payoff you want to prevent.
Attacking Kain directly pauses his cheat cycle for a Round and resets it to the start, so keep steady hits on him to stop Monster Mash from landing.
Damage-over-time spells only reset the cycle on the Round you cast them, not while they tick.

name: Kaizoku (Tier 1)
cheats: [Shield] At the beginning of the first Round, Kaizoku will interrupt-cast a -70% version of Tower Shield on himself.
[Damage] If Kaizoku is damaged by any Spell, then he will interrupt-cast to replace the Tower Shield.
[Manipulation] Manipulation Spells that remove helpful Wards can be used to remove Kaizoku's Tower Shield without causing him to replace it.
This cheat will stop occurring after all of Kaizoku's Minions have been defeated.
[Damage] If Kaizoku is damaged by an all-enemies Damage Spell, then he will interrupt-cast a -60% version of Black Mantle on the caster.
[Damage] At the beginning of every second Round, Kaizoku will interrupt-cast a Star Spell that deals 75 Star Damage to all Wizards.

The first time this cheat occurs, Wizards will be damaged in the normal order from right to left. The second time this cheat occurs, Wizards will be damaged from left to right. The order in which Wizards are damaged alternates every time this cheat occurs.
Sometimes, the Spell will deal 600 Star Damage instead of the usual 75 Star Damage.
[Minion][Afterlife] After all of Kaizoku's Minions have been defeated, Kaizoku will begin interrupt-casting this Spell every Round, and twice in a row every fourth Round.

[Ward] "You stopped my Halberd?" - If a Wizard that is damaged by this cheat had any Wards, not including Damage Absorption Wards, then the Spell will not damage any other Wizards, and Kaizoku will interrupt-cast a Spell to remove all Wards from that Wizard and himself. This will not trigger if the Wizard that has a Ward is the last Wizard to be damaged.
[Minion][Afterlife] "Time to turn things up!" - After all of Kaizoku's Minions have been defeated, he will remove all Wards from himself.
strategy: Low health (795), so one solid Ice hit ends it, but he keeps a -70% Tower Shield he replaces whenever damaged.
Strip the shield with a manipulation spell like Steal Ward (that removes it without the replace), then hit with Ice (+20%, resists Fire 40%).
Or just hit hard enough with Ice to punch through the -70% shield in one blow.
Expect a Star attack (about 75, occasionally 600) to all wizards every second round, so keep healed. Note wards on you can trigger him to strip all wards when his Star hits.
cheatNotes: He shields with a -70% Tower Shield on Round 1; either strip it or hit hard enough to break through.
Damaging him makes him replace the Tower Shield, so a chip hit just resets it; use one big hit or remove it first.
Manipulation spells like Steal Ward take the shield off without triggering the replace.
Hitting him with an all-enemies spell gets you a -60% Black Mantle, so single-target may be safer.
Every second round he hits the whole team with a Star spell (about 75 damage), so keep healed.
That Star spell occasionally hits for 600 instead of 75, so keep a buffer of health.
After the minions die he casts the Star attack every round, and twice on every fourth round.
If a wizard hit by the Star has any wards, he strips all wards from that wizard and himself, so do not over-shield.
