name: Kallah Silverback
locations: Zafaria: Drum Jungle - Kallah Silverback's Shack
cheats: "Your school magic barely tickles me!" - If a Wizard casts a single-target Damage Spell on Kallah without a Prism, then he will interrupt-cast Minor Blessing on himself.
Pet Spells will not trigger his cheat.

"Oof. I wasn't expecting that!" - If a Prism is on Kallah when attacked by any single-target Damage Spell, then he will hit himself with an Ice Blast for 500 Damage of an unknown School of Magic, and then remove one Prism from himself.
This cheat will activate even if the Prism that is active on Kallah is not used up by the Damage Spell.
spellNotes: Deals 280 Star Damage for 3 Pips maximum.
strategy: Always keep a Prism on Kallah before you attack — hitting him without a prism makes him self-heal with Minor Blessing.
With a prism up, every single-target hit also makes him blast himself for 500 and strip a prism, and that self-damage lands even if your attack did not consume the prism.
So keep prisms stocked and let him chip his own health down alongside your hits.
Stack blades and traps, then hit single-target (pet spells don't count for either cheat); he has no school resist, so any school works.
With only 5,910 health, a couple of prism-backed hits plus his own self-blasts finish him fast.
cheatNotes: Attack him single-target without a prism on him and he heals himself with Minor Blessing — always prism him first.
With a prism on him, each single-target hit makes him Ice Blast himself for 500 and remove a prism — free extra damage, so keep prisms stocked.
The 500 self-hit happens even when your attack didn't use up the prism, so you get the bonus regardless — reapply and keep swinging.

name: Khai Tengri
locations: MooShu: Jade Palace - Khai Tengri's Dojo
cheats: "A Charm! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Blade will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat, but Aura Spells will not.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A ward! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Trap (including Feint) or a Shield will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
The text for this cheat only appears after Khai casts the Hex.
strategy: Hit with Ice (+35%); he resists Fire 35%.
Every blade you cast makes him Hex you (+35% incoming damage), and every trap or shield does the same — so minimize charms and wards.
Use Aura spells for your damage boost instead — auras don't trigger the Hex, and pet-cast blades are safe too.
He's low health (3,435); a single buffed Ice hit ends it.
If you must blade, accept one Hex and hit the same round before it matters.
cheatNotes: Cast a blade and he interrupt-Hexes you (+35% incoming damage) — lean on auras instead, which don't trigger it.
Global spells also trigger the Hex; auras do not.
Pet-cast spells won't trigger it.
Cast a trap (Feint included) or a shield and he Hexes you the same way — keep setup minimal.

name: Khan
locations: Empyrea: Aeriel Jungle - Tower of Sycorax
cheats: "Don't insult my intelligence!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, Khan will cast a Moon version of Mana Burn on the Wizard that removes 4 Pips and summons one of the Minions listed below.
At the beginning of the duel, he will use his turn casting a Moon Spell to summon at least one of the following Minions:
A Moon School version of Cleanse Ward is also cast on him after Summoning his first minion, before the Wizard can cast spells.

"I'm afraid it's more difficult than you think, Wizard!" - If two or more Wizards are present in the duel, he will summon an additional random Minion. And if a Minion is defeated, Khan will summon another random Minion from the list above.
"From Tartarus' heart I will grapple with thee!" - At the end of every 5th Round, Khan will do the following:
Casts a Moon version of Steal Health that sacrifices a random Minion, healing Khan for 7,000 - 11,000 Health.

Casts an all enemies version of Wraith that gives Khan a +500% Blade first, then deals 500 damage of that School, without the Steal effect. The School of the Blade and Wraith depends on the Minion that Khan sacrifices.
"Is that the best you can do? I'm laughing at your superior magic!" - If a negative Ward is placed on Khan, then his minion will cast an Ice spell to steal the trap onto themselves.
Mass Feint (+75%) triggers this cheat.
Cheat does not trigger when there are no minions present.
Only one Trap is swapped onto minion when this Spell is cast.
This can be avoided with Indemnity.
strategy: He takes +10% from Ice and Myth and resists Fire and Storm 50%. Hit with Ice or Myth.
Every 5th round he sacrifices one of his Fury minions to heal 7,000-11,000 and then hits the team with a +500% self-blade Wraith for 500. This is his big swing; try to kill him before Round 5.
Keeping minions dead reduces his sacrifice fuel, but he resummons, so the real answer is speed. Burst him down inside the first five rounds.
Traps on Khan get stolen onto a minion (even Mass Feint +75% triggers it here). Protect your Feint with Indemnity, or note the cheat cannot trigger when no minions are present.
Blade heavily, Indemnity your Feint, and land an Ice or Myth nuke before his 5th-round heal. Join on time; late arrival costs 4 pips.
cheatNotes: Joining late costs you 4 pips (Mana Burn) and summons a minion. Arrive on time.
He opens by summoning at least one Fury minion, so expect an add from turn one.
He Cleanse Wards himself right after his first summon, stripping your early traps. Re-apply after his opener rather than before.
With two or more wizards he summons an extra minion, and replaces any minion you kill. Do not expect the board to stay clear.
Every 5th round he sacrifices a minion to heal 7,000-11,000 then buffs himself, his key swing. Kill him before Round 5 or burst through it.
A negative ward on Khan makes his minion steal the trap onto itself. Protect it with Indemnity.
Even Mass Feint (+75%) triggers this trap-steal here, unlike most bosses.
It cannot trigger when no minions are out, a window to trap him safely if you clear the board.
Only one trap is swapped per cast, so heavy trap stacks partially survive.
Indemnity prevents the trap-steal entirely.

name: Khonsu (Challenge)
cheats: "Combat prowess is inversely proportionate to adorability" - At the beginning of the first Round, Khonsu will Polymorph each Wizard into one of the following forms at random for 99 Rounds and remove all of their Pips:
[Fire] Marshfellow (Challenge) - Mallow Inferno
[Ice] Snowball (Challenge) - New Ice Age
[Storm] Glowbug Zapper (Challenge) - Zap-aclysm
[Myth] Snipe (Challenge) - Snipe Hunter
[Life] Horned Frog (Challenge) - Plague of Frog
[Death] Gummy Bunny (Challenge) - Hare Razer
[Balance] Wombat (Challenge) - Wombat Evolved
If a Wizard casts their above Spell and does not meet its condition to deal Damage, a copy of the Spell will be added to their hand.

"Hmm. Perhaps I should increase the adorability quotient" - After a Wizard successfully attacks Khonsu with one of the above Spells, Khonsu will Polymorph the Wizard who attacked him into another one of the above forms, remove their current Pips and give them 2 Power Pips. After Khonsu has been hit the same number of times as there are Wizards, Khonsu will unpolymorph all Wizards, and Polymorph himself into one of the following forms at random for 100 Rounds:
"I will rain down death..." - Hunhau
"Feel hatred, Wizard" - Malus
"Your rules do not apply to me" - Personal Daemon
"All shall be consumed" - The Devourer
strategy: Team challenge, 80,000 health. Round 1 everyone is Polymorphed into a random school form (Marshfellow, Snowball, and so on) with all pips removed. You fight using the form's signature spell.
Cast your form's spell meeting its damage condition to hit Khonsu. If you miss the condition, you get a copy back to try again.
Each successful hit re-polymorphs that wizard into a new form and gives 2 power pips, so adapt to the new kit each time.
Coordinate so Khonsu is hit once per wizard. Then he unpolymorphs the team and polymorphs himself into a boss form (Hunhau, Malus, Personal Daemon, or The Devourer).
He has no resist. Sustain, heal, and keep landing the polymorph hits through the long grind.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he polymorphs every wizard into a random school form for 99 rounds and strips all pips. You fight through the form's spell.
If you cast your form's spell without meeting its damage condition, you get another copy in hand to retry.
Landing a hit re-polymorphs that wizard (new form plus 2 power pips). Once he is hit once per wizard, he unpolymorphs everyone and polymorphs himself.

name: Khonsu (Standard)
cheats: "Combat prowess is inversely proportionate to adorability" - At the beginning of the first Round, Khonsu will Polymorph each Wizard into one of the following forms at random and remove all of their Pips:
[Fire] Marshfellow (Standard) - Marsh of War (added to hand by Cuteness Overload)
[Ice] Snowball (Standard) - Splat Flurry
[Storm] Glowbug Zapper (Standard) - Zap, Crackle, Pop!
[Myth] Snipe (Standard) - Fur Baby (cast on Minion after meeting condition)
[Life] Horned Frog (Standard) - Everyone Croaks (added to hand by Nature Call)
[Death] Gummy Bunny (Standard) - Sweet Death
[Balance] Wombat (Standard) - Wombat Frenzy
If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Khonsu will Polymorph each Wizard into one of the above forms at random and remove all of their Pips, then heal himself to 100% Health.
The Snipe Polymorph will not be assigned to any Wizard if there are 4 Wizards present.

"Hmm. Perhaps I should increase the adorability quotient" - If there is more than 1 Wizard in battle when attacked, Khonsu will Polymorph the Wizard who attacked him into another one of the above forms and remove their Pips.
Sometimes this cheat may activate more than once from only a single attack, which will lower the amount of times needed to attack before the following cheat activates.
After the above cards have been used the same number of times as wizards in the duel (IE if there are 2 wizards in the duel, 2 spells need to be used), Khonsu will give all Wizards the following:
[Break Free (Moon)]
It is not necessary to use the 'Break Free' Spell. Wizards may continue to obtain copies of their Spell and fight as their Polymorph, regardless of the number of Wizards present.
Polymorph Cycle
After Wizards have been given a 'Break Free' Card, Khonsu will begin to Polymorph into one of the following forms on that Round and the end of every 5th Round afterward.
"If I can't change you, I will upgrade myself" - Polymorphs into Malistaire Drake for 3 Rounds.
"The Shadow shall fall!" - Polymorphs into Morganthe for 3 Rounds.
"Perhaps something more chaotic..." - Polymorphs into Grandfather Spider for 3 Rounds.
"You will learn to fear Nothing..." - Polymorphs into Dasein for 3 Rounds.
strategy: Team fight. Khonsu Polymorphs every wizard into a cute form and strips all pips on Round 1, so you fight as your form using its one signature spell.
Never let anyone join after Round 2, a late wizard makes Khonsu heal to 100% and re-polymorph everyone.

Cast your polymorph's signature spell (Splat Flurry as Snowball, Zap Crackle Pop as Glowbug, and so on). After the team casts these as many times as there are wizards, Khonsu hands out Break Free, though you can keep fighting as the polymorph.
Attacking while more than one wizard is present re-polymorphs the attacker and strips their pips, so coordinate hits and expect resets.

After Break Free he cycles into Malistaire, Morganthe, Grandfather Spider, and Dasein forms (3 rounds each, every 5th round). Time the team's biggest burst into his weaker windows and chip the 50,000 down steadily.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 he Polymorphs every wizard into a random cute form and removes all pips, you fight as that form with its one signature spell.
Join after Round 2 and he heals to full and re-polymorphs everyone, do not come in late.
With 4 wizards the Snipe/Myth form is never assigned.
Attack while more than one wizard is present and he re-polymorphs the attacker and strips their pips.
That re-polymorph can fire more than once per attack, speeding up the Break Free counter.
Once the team has cast its polymorph spells as many times as there are wizards, he gives everyone Break Free.
You do not have to use Break Free, you can keep fighting as your polymorph.
After Break Free is handed out, he starts polymorphing himself on that round and every 5th round after.
He becomes Malistaire Drake for 3 rounds.
He becomes Morganthe for 3 rounds.
He becomes Grandfather Spider for 3 rounds.
He becomes Dasein for 3 rounds.

name: Killer Krok
cheats: [Late] "Tick-tock, Tick-tock, says the Krok" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, Killer Krok will cast Bad Juju on the late Wizard.
[Trap][Curse] "That won't even chip my scales" - If a Trap or Harmful Charm is placed on Killer Krok, he will cast Sacrifice (Death).
[Shadow] "Now you'll see why they call me Killer Krok" - He will cast Dark Fiend.
[Manipulation] "*Growls*" - After Killer Krok casts Bad Juju, he will follow up with Sacrifice (Death).
strategy: Solo-friendly (11,705 health). Do not trap or weaken him: any trap or harmful charm makes him cast Sacrifice to recover. Stack blades instead.
He resists Death 50% and takes only +10% from Life, so hit with a strong off-Death school and blade heavily rather than trapping.
Expect Dark Fiend, a shadow hit, and a Bad Juju into Sacrifice chain, so keep a shield or resist up.
Enter on time: joining late gets you Bad Juju.
Set up a trap-free burst with blades, then land one big hit.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets the late wizard hit with Bad Juju.
Any trap or weakness placed on him makes him cast Sacrifice to recover. Skip traps and weaknesses.
He casts Dark Fiend, a shadow attack.
After Bad Juju he follows up with another Sacrifice.

name: King Avernus
cheats: [Random] "King Avernus casts Gut Punch of Paralysis. Does it succeed?" - At the end of every second Round, King Avernus will cast an Ice Spell on the Wizard with the highest Health that will randomly do one of the following effects:
"Critical success! You're stunned!" - The targeted Wizard will take 200 Ice Damage and is Stunned.
"It does succeed! You take damage!" - The targeted Wizard will take 200 Ice Damage.
"It fails!" - Nothing happens.
"Critical fail! He stuns himself!" - King Avernus is Stunned.
King Avernus' Relic Cheats

[Afterlife] "I call upon the Relic of Eternal Avernus to revive me!" - At the beginning of the first Round, if "Seek Blessing" (Middle Room) was not chosen first, King Avernus will cast a version of Guardian Spirit that restores 17% of his maximum Health (362 Health) upon being defeated.
If King Avernus' Afterlife is removed by Clerical Terror, this cheat will retrigger.

[Shield] "Emblem of Avernal Shielding, protect me!" - If "Find Treasure" (Left Room) was not chosen second, the first time King Avernus is targeted or Damaged by an attack Spell each turn, he will cast a -50 Damage Ward on himself.

[Shadow Creature] "Medallion of Shadow Summon, bring me a thrall from the void!" - At the beginning of the third Round, if "Sneak Around" (Right Room) was not chosen third, King Avernus will cast a version of Dark Fiend that deals 200 Shadow Damage to all enemies with no additional effects.
Wizards' Relic Cheats

[Afterlife] "You have my Relic of Eternal Avernus? No!" - At the beginning of the first Round, if "Seek Blessing" (Middle Room) was chosen first, a version of Guardian Spirit will be cast on all Wizards that restores 50% of their maximum Health upon being defeated.

[Shield] "No, Emblem of Avernal Shielding, don't protect them!" - If "Find Treasure" (Left Room) was chosen second, after a Wizard is targeted by an attack Spell, a -50 Damage Ward is applied on them.
If the Wizard's team is targeted with an All Enemies Spell, all Wizards will receive a -25 Damage Ward.

[Shadow Creature] "You have stolen my Medallion of Shadow Summon?" - At the beginning of the third Round, if "Sneak Around" (Right Room) was chosen third, a random Wizard will cast a version of Dark Fiend that deals 1,700 Shadow Damage to King Avernus and has no additional effects.
spellNotes: Casts a 0-Pip Storm Spell that applies a +25 Damage Charm.
Casts a 1-Pip version of Qismah's Curse that deals 50 Death Damage to all enemies.
Casts a 2-Pip version of Deer Knight that deals 75 Death Damage to and applies a -25 Damage Charm on all enemies.
Casts a 2-Pip version of Scarecrow that deals 50 Death Drain Damage to all enemies and heals the caster for the Damage dealt.
strategy: This gauntlet is shaped by which room choices you made. Pick 'Seek Blessing', 'Find Treasure', and 'Sneak Around' in the right order and Avernus's relic cheats flip to your side instead.
Choose 'Seek Blessing' first so the Guardian Spirit revives your team at 50% instead of reviving Avernus at 17%; otherwise he comes back on defeat.
Choose 'Find Treasure' second so damage wards land on your wizards; otherwise Avernus shields himself with a -50 Damage Ward the first time he is attacked each turn.
Choose 'Sneak Around' third so on Round 3 a wizard fires a 1,700 Shadow Dark Fiend at Avernus; otherwise he Dark Fiends your team for 200 each.
He is Death with no resist and takes boosted Ice and Myth (+25 each), so an Ice or Myth hitter melts his 2,135 Health fast; he is stunnable, so a stun can skip his Gut Punch round.
Play through his weak natural attacks, watch the Round-2 Gut Punch on your highest-Health wizard, and burst him down once the relic cheats are working for you.
cheatNotes: At the end of every second round he casts a random Ice spell on your highest-Health wizard, which can stun and deal 200, deal 200, do nothing, or backfire and stun himself.
Critical success stuns that wizard and deals 200 Ice.
A plain success just deals 200 Ice damage.
On a fail, nothing happens.
On a critical fail, Avernus stuns himself instead, giving you a free round.
If you did not pick 'Seek Blessing' first, Avernus revives to 17% (362 Health) on defeat via Guardian Spirit.
If his Afterlife is stripped by Clerical Terror, the revive cheat re-arms.
If you did not pick 'Find Treasure' second, the first attack on him each turn triggers a -50 Damage Ward he self-shields with.
If you did not pick 'Sneak Around' third, on Round 3 he Dark Fiends your whole team for 200 Shadow each.
If you picked 'Seek Blessing' first, all wizards get Guardian Spirit reviving them at 50% max Health.
If you picked 'Find Treasure' second, your wizards gain a -50 Damage Ward after being targeted by an attack.
Against an all-enemies spell, each wizard instead gets a -25 Damage Ward.
If you picked 'Sneak Around' third, on Round 3 a random wizard fires a 1,700 Shadow Dark Fiend straight at Avernus.

name: King Borr (Tier 2)
locations: Grizzleheim: Savarstaad Pass - Savarstaad Pass Gold Key Room
cheats: If you're late, you must wait! - If a Wizard joins the battle late, King Borr (Tier 2) will cast Medusa (865) on the Wizard who joined late. Grendelynn (Tier 2) will cast Shadow Sentinel that provides 75% Intercept on King Borr after being revived.
At the start of the first Round, Grendelynn (Tier 2) will cast Shadow Sentinel that provides 75% Intercept on King Borr.
I call Your Feint! - If a Feint is cast on King Borr, he will cast an AoE Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1).

You Won't Fall Until I Command It! - If Grendelynn (Tier 2) is defeated before the Boss, King Borr (Tier 2) will use a Moon Blessing on Grendelynn (Tier 2) restoring 100% Health, and Grendelynn will recast Shadow Sentinel on King Borr.
Avenge Me! - King Borr will say this when he's defeated.
strategy: Grendelynn keeps a 75% Intercept Shadow Sentinel on King Borr, so single-target hits get soaked. Use AoE to hit past it.
Do not kill Grendelynn first; King Borr revives her at 100% health and she re-casts the Sentinel. Kill Borr through AoE or drop both in the same round.
Feint triggers an AoE Cleanse Ward, so skip Feint or protect it with Indemnity; blade-only setup is cleaner.
Blade up and AoE with Storm (+15%, resists Myth 50%).
cheatNotes: Latecomers get hit with Medusa, so join on Round 1.
Grendelynn's Shadow Sentinel intercepts 75% of single-target hits on Borr; use AoE to reach him.
Feinting Borr makes him AoE Cleanse Ward it off, so skip Feint or protect it with Indemnity.
Killing Grendelynn before Borr fully heals her and she re-Sentinels, so take Borr down with AoE instead.

name: King Borr (Tier 3)
locations: Grizzleheim: Savarstaad Pass - Savarstaad Pass Gold Key Room
cheats: You are my Shield! - At the start of the first Round, Grendelynn (Tier 3) will cast Shadow Sentinel that provides 75% Intercept on King Borr.
I call Your Feint! - If a Feint is cast on King Borr, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) that removes all traps.

You Won't Fall Until I Command It! - If Grendelynn (Tier 3) is defeated before the Boss, King Borr (Tier 3) will use a Moon version of Blessing on Grendelynn (Tier 3), restoring 100% Health, and Grendelynn will recast Shadow Sentinel on King Borr.
If you're late, you must wait! - If a Wizard joins the battle late, King Borr (Tier 3) will cast Medusa (865) on the Wizard.

Avenge Me! - After King Borr (Tier 3) is defeated, Grendelynn (Tier 3) will be defeated. This cheat may be bugged though, because the You Won't Fall Until I Command It cheat will activate, restoring the Minion's Health again. Needs verification.
spellNotes: Deals 460 Myth Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Hit with Storm — he takes +10% from it — and never attack into his 60% Myth resist.
Do not Feint him. Any Feint triggers Cleanse Ward that strips all traps off him. Stack blades instead of traps.
Grendelynn casts a 75% Intercept Shadow Sentinel on King Borr at the start, so single-target hits mostly land on the minion. Use an AoE to damage the boss directly.
Do not kill Grendelynn first — King Borr heals her to 100% and she recasts the Sentinel. Focus the boss; when he dies she is defeated too (Avenge Me).
Blade up and drop one boosted Storm AoE to finish King Borr through the intercept.
cheatNotes: Grendelynn puts a 75% Intercept Sentinel on King Borr, so most of a single-target hit is soaked by the minion. Use AoEs to hit the boss directly.
Feinting him backfires: he Cleanse Wards off all your traps. Skip traps and use blades.
If you kill Grendelynn before the boss, he heals her to full and she re-shields him. Do not bother killing the minion.
Join on time or the latecomer eats a Medusa (865).
Killing King Borr defeats Grendelynn automatically, so you never need to kill the minion yourself.

name: King Borr (Tier 4)
locations: Grizzleheim: Savarstaad Pass - Savarstaad Pass Gold Key Room
cheats: "You are my shield!" - Grendelynn (Tier 4) will cast a Shadow Sentinel that provides 75% Intercept.
If Grendelynn is Beguiled, the Intercept protects the Wizard team for as long as the Beguile is active.
"If you're late, you must wait!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, King Borr will cast Medusa (865) on the Wizard.
"You won't fall until I command it!" - If Grendelynn is defeated before King Borr, a Moon version of Blessing will be cast on Grendelynn that restores 100% Health.
If both King Borr and Grendelynn are defeated in the same round, this cheat still activates.
"I call your Feint!" - If a Feint is cast, King Borr will cast an AoE version of Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all unprotected Traps.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
"Avenge Me!" - King Borr will say this if he is defeated before Grendelynn.
strategy: Enter on time. Wizards who join after the second round get hit with Medusa.
Kill order: King Borr must die before Grendelynn. If Grendelynn dies first she revives/heals, so pile your damage onto Borr and hit as hard as you can.
Lead with an Indemnity-protected Feint as your very first trap. If you place any other trap first he Cleanse Wards them all off; the Indemnity Feint has to go down before the rest of your stack.
Beguile Grendelynn on the same turn as your hit. This disables her Shadow Sentinel, which otherwise Intercepts 75% of all damage aimed at King Borr, and lets your full hit land on the boss.
Never Feint him unprotected - he responds with a mass Cleanse Ward. Only use Indemnity-protected traps (or the Balance version of Feint, which does not trigger the cleanse).
Stack blades, keep the Indemnity Feint and traps up, disable the Intercept with the beguile, then burst King Borr down before you finish Grendelynn.
cheatNotes: Grendelynn shields with a 75% Intercept Sentinel, absorbing your hits aimed at King Borr.
Beguiling Grendelynn flips her Intercept to protect your team for as long as the beguile holds; a strong opener.
Joining late gets Medusa (865) cast on you; enter on time.
Kill Grendelynn before King Borr and she full-heals; keep them even and finish together.
If you drop both in the same Round the avenge and heal still fire, but with both gone it no longer matters; aim for a simultaneous kill.
Feinting him makes him Cleanse all unprotected traps off; use Balance Feint or protect traps with Indemnity.
Mass Feint also triggers the trap cleanse, so avoid it here.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger the cleanse; it is the safe way to trap him.

name: King Detritus
locations: Wizard City: The Drains - Abandoned Backyard
cheats: "Your tardiness is disgusting!" - If any Wizards join the duel after the second Round, King Detritus will summon an additional Minion as well as cast Steal Health on one of his Minions and move up to the next Polymorph stage.
If there are no Minions present, King Detritus will cast Steal Health on a random Wizard.

"Ah ah ah!" - If a Feint is cast on King Detritus, one of his Minions will cast a Shadow Spell twice to move the Trap onto itself. Feints can be safely cast during the first Round, while the Minions are not yet active.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
"Refuse, unite!" - At the beginning of the first Round, King Detritus will cast a Sun Spell that summons random Trash Minions equal to the number of Wizards present.
"Your sacrifice is for the greater garbage good!" - King Detritus will cast a Sun version of Steal Health on his Minion to heal himself 100% of the Damage dealt to the Minion.
"The trash is piling up!" - King Detritus will summon another random Trash Minion if he is out of Minions.

Polymorph Cycle - In this cycle, King Detritus will Polymorph into a larger version of himself, which gives him an Aura but loses all of his stats, including Power Pips, Shadow Pips, Masteries, Incoming Healing, Critical rating, Critical Block, Pierce, Outgoing Damage, Incoming Boosts, and Resistances. The following stages will show the Aura's stats along with the dialogue:
"The trash man cometh!" - Gives +5% Damage, +5% Armor Piercing, and +5% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
"Now is the day of the discarded!" - Gives +10% Damage, +10% Armor Piercing, and +10% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
"I love the smell of garbage in the morning" - Gives +15% Damage, +15% Armor Piercing, and +15% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
"This fight has become quite the dumpster fire" - Gives +20% Damage, +20% Armor Piercing, and +20% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
"Time for the trash to take you out!" - Gives +25% Damage, +25% Armor Piercing, and +25% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
After these 5 cheats, lines will recycle giving King Detritus the following Auras:
Stage 6 - +30% Damage, +30% Armor Piercing, and +30% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
Stage 7 - +35% Damage, +35% Armor Piercing, and +35% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
Stage 8 - +40% Damage, +40% Armor Piercing, and +40% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
Stage 9 - +45% Damage, +45% Armor Piercing, and +45% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
Stage 10 - +50% Damage, +50% Armor Piercing, and +50% Resistance, for 99 Rounds.
spellNotes: Casts a +30% version of the Spell Mass Hex
strategy: Huge Sun boss (39680) that gets stronger every round via a Polymorph cycle (+5% damage, pierce, and resist per stage, up to +50%). This is a race, so set up efficiently and burst.
Hit with Ice, Storm, or Death (+5%); avoid Fire, Myth, and Life (70% resist). Funnel blades to one hitter.
Cast Feints only on round 1, while his Trash minions are not active yet. After that, a Feint (or Mass Feint) makes a minion shift the trap onto itself.
He Steal-Healths his own minions to heal himself and re-summons Trash whenever he runs out. Do not chase minions endlessly; AoE them alongside him.
Because he escalates, plan your kill window early. Over-stack blades and traps on the hitter and drop one massive hit before his auras stack too high.
cheatNotes: A late joiner makes him summon an extra minion, Steal Health, and jump a polymorph stage (stronger). Everyone in on time.
With no minions out he Steal-Healths a random wizard instead, healing himself. Expect self-healing either way.
Feint him after round 1 and a minion shifts the trap onto itself. Cast Feints only on round 1 while minions are still inactive.
Mass Feint triggers the same trap-dodge. Same rule: round 1 only.
Round 1 he summons one Trash minion per wizard. Bring AoE to manage the board.
He sacrifices his own minions with Steal Health to heal 100% of the damage dealt. His minions are healing batteries, so AoE them with him rather than one at a time.
Whenever he is out of minions he summons another, so you cannot fully clear the board. Focus the boss.
He Polymorphs into bigger forms that trade his stats for escalating damage, pierce, and resist auras. The fight gets harder each stage, so win quickly.
Stage 1 aura is +5% damage, pierce, and resist. Every stage adds more, so treat the clock as your enemy.
By the later stages he is at +30% and climbing to +50%. If the fight has run this long, reset your setup and commit to a kill.

name: King Tusk
cheats: [Jinx] "Watch out for the traps" - At the beginning of each Round, King Tusk will interrupt-cast one of the following Spells:
Myth Trap
Mythic Fuel (+25%)
Yellow Troll
Keeper of the Flame (Tier 5)
Hex
[Fire] If a Wizard casts a Fire Damage Spell that removes harmful Wards on King Tusk, he will remove all harmful Wards from Wizards and deactivate his Trap cheat.
[Afterlife] If King Tusk is defeated while his Trap cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "You fell right into my trap. Try harder" - When King Tusk revives, he will cast Mythic Fuel (+25%) on the Wizard that defeated him, then give Wizards the following Spell:
[Immolate (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
strategy: Attack with Fire; it is a boost school and it is the key to shutting off his revive.
He keeps a Trap cheat active, jinxing himself each round with Myth Trap, Hex, Mythic Fuel and the like. If you kill him while that cheat is on, he revives at full health.
To disable it, cast a Fire damage spell that removes harmful wards from him. That clears the jinxes off your team and turns off the Trap cheat. Do this before your kill shot.
After the trap cheat is off, finish him. Otherwise he revives, buffs whoever killed him with Mythic Fuel, and hands out Immolate cards.
Stack blades, disable the cheat with a ward-clearing Fire hit, then land the finisher.
cheatNotes: Each round he jinxes himself with one of the listed spells as part of his Trap cheat; this is the cheat that must be turned off before he can die for good.
A Fire damage spell that strips harmful wards off him clears the jinxes from your team and deactivates the Trap cheat. That is your setup step before killing him.
Kill him while the Trap cheat is still active and he revives at full health; disable it first.
When he revives he puts Mythic Fuel (+25%) on whoever killed him and hands wizards an Immolate card.

name: Kobra Kommander
cheats: [Late] "No tolerance for tardiness!" - If a Wizard joins the duel late, Kobra Kommander will cast a Balance version of Steal Pips, which gives him 3 pips.
[Damage] "Here's a Kobra special!" - At the end of the fifth Round, he will cast a multi-target version of Wild Bolt that hits all Wizards.
[Shadow Pip] "Now for my secret weapon!" - When he receives two Shadow pips, he will cast Dark Fiend with +20% backlash and receive a +40% Shadowblade.
strategy: Join on time to deny him the free Steal Pips, and plan to win before or right at Round 5, when he throws a multi-target Wild Bolt at everyone.
He is stunable and beguilable, so a stun or beguile is a strong way to skip his Wild Bolt turn or his Dark Fiend.
Watch his Shadow pips: at two pips he casts a self-buffed Dark Fiend with a +40% Shadowblade, so try to finish him before he charges it.
He resists Storm 30% and takes 15% more from Myth, so hit with Myth, stack blades, and burst inside five Rounds.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard lets him cast a Balance Steal Pips for 3 pips.
At the end of Round 5 he hits all wizards with a multi-target Wild Bolt.
Once he has two Shadow pips he casts Dark Fiend with +20% backlash and gives himself a +40% Shadowblade.

name: Kormak Shadowclaw
cheats: "Help each other to defeat me!" - At the start of each battle, Kormak will cast a -65% Tower Shield.
Steal Ward and Pierce can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield.
After an attack against him (not including subsequent Damage over Time), he replenishes his -65% Tower Shield by interrupt.
If you use an Attack Spell that hits more than once, such as Minotaur or Hydra, he will put up Shields to match how many times he was hit.

"Now's your chance!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a single-target Charm on a teammate, he will interrupt cast a version of Cleanse Ward to remove all Shields and Traps (including Prisms) that are on him.
Spells such as Bladestorm do not trigger this cheat.
Spells from Pets do not trigger this cheat.
Vaporize can dispel this cheat.
strategy: He opens with a -65% Tower Shield and re-shields after every hit, so bring Pierce or Shatter, or use Steal Ward to strip it safely (and keep the shield yourself).
Avoid multi-hit spells (Minotaur, Hydra) — each hit makes him add another shield.
Attack with Myth or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Storm resist without a prism.
In a group you can strip his shields by casting a single-target charm on a teammate (he Cleanses all his own wards) — but it also removes your traps on him, so time it before the hitter goes.
Stack blades, break the Tower the same round, and land one clean single hit.
cheatNotes: He starts with a -65% Tower Shield. Pierce or shatter through it, or Steal Ward it off (and keep the shield yourself).
Steal Ward and Pierce remove the Tower safely without provoking a replacement — use them over raw damage.
He re-casts the -65% Tower by interrupt after every attack on him, so break the shield the same round you commit your big hit.
Multi-hit spells (Hydra, Minotaur) make him stack a shield per hit — use a single-hit attack instead.
Casting a single-target charm on a teammate makes him Cleanse off all his own shields, traps, and prisms — handy to clear his Tower, but it wipes your traps on him too.
Vaporize dispels this Cleanse cheat if you want to stop him from stripping his own wards.

name: Krampus (Tier 1)
locations: Wizard City: Ravenwood - Krampusgarten,Colossus Boulevard - Krampusgarten
cheats: "Naughty naughty! Punctuality is a virtue!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Krampus will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Power Link on the Wizard.
"A gift for Krampus? You shouldn't have!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, he may cast a version of Steal Charm (Shadow) that steals all Charms from the Wizard who cast the Blade.
He will occasionally cast this throughout the battle.
Pet Spells can trigger this cheat.
"Naughty, naughty! Krampus see's all!" - If a Wizard casts a Feint Spell, he will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward two times (which makes Indemnity ineffective).

"Krampus is everywhere!" - At the beginning of every third Round, he will cast a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that transfers all negative hanging effects that are on him (i.e. Traps) to a random friend or foe.
"'Tis the season of Krampus!" - At the beginning of Round 5, he will cast a Shadow Spell to summon any combination of Chilling Helpers and Stormy Helpers.
Whenever a Minion is defeated, he will resummon another one after one Round.
spellNotes: The Lump of Coal Pet casts the Krampus Spell which acts like a Natural Attack.
strategy: He steals all your charms when you cast a blade (and occasionally on his own), so blade and hit the same round, or protect with Aegis.
Feint is double-Cleansed and Indemnity won't save it — trap him with regular traps or rely on blades timed with the hit instead.
At the start of every 3rd round he Dimension Shifts the traps hanging on him onto a random target, so don't over-invest in traps on him before round 3, 6, or 9.
Round 5 he summons Chilling/Stormy Helpers and resummons any defeated minion after a round — AoE to clear them with him.
Blade up and finish Krampus (3,920) with one AoE before the minion cycle drags on.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get an enhanced Power Link — join on time.
Cast a blade and he may Steal Charm all your charms — hit the same round or protect with Aegis.
He also throws this steal out occasionally on his own.
Pet-cast blades can trigger the steal too.
Cast Feint and he Cleanse Wards twice, and Indemnity won't protect it — don't rely on Feint here.
Every 3rd round he Dimension Shifts the traps on him to a random friend or foe, so traps on him may vanish or move.
Round 5 he summons Chilling and Stormy Helpers.
Any defeated minion is resummoned one round later — AoE everything together.

name: Krampus (Tier 2)
locations: Wizard City: Ravenwood - Krampusgarten,Colossus Boulevard - Krampusgarten
cheats: "Naughty, naughty! Punctuality is a virtue!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Krampus will cheat-cast a Shadow version of Scald dealing Fire Damage and a -75% version of Smoke Mantle on the Wizard.
"A gift for Krampus? You shouldn't have!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, he may cast a Shadow version of Steal Charm that steals all Charms from the Wizard who cast the Blade.
He will occasionally cast this throughout the battle.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
"Naughty, naughty! Krampus sees all!" - If a Wizard casts a Feint Spell, he will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward twice (which makes Indemnity ineffective).

"Krampus is everywhere!"" - At the beginning of every third Round, he will cast a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that transfers all negative hanging effects that are on him (i.e. Traps) to a random friend or foe.
"'Tis the season of Krampus!" - At the beginning of Round 5, he will summon any combination of Chilling Helper (Rank 10) and Stormy Helper (Rank 10).
Whenever a Minion is defeated, he will resummon it after one Round.
spellNotes: The Damage portion of Sacrifice (1,000) can be used on any ally.
The Lump of Coal Pet casts the Krampus Spell, which acts like a Natural Attack.
strategy: Krampus punishes setup hard: casting a blade may trigger Steal Charm (he takes ALL your charms), and Global spells trigger it too. Minimize blades and hit the same round you blade so he cannot steal first.
Feint is a trap here; he Cleanse Wards twice on a Feint, which defeats Indemnity. Skip Feint and rely on hit-round blades, auras, or a Sun-enchanted big hit.
Every third round he Dimension Shifts his own traps onto a random friend or foe, so traps you place can vanish or bounce. Time traps to hit before a third-round shift.
He is Fire; bring a non-Fire hit or prism.
At Round 5 he summons Chilling and Stormy Helpers and resummons them when killed. Do not grind the adds; burst Krampus down. Join on time to avoid the late-arrival Scald and Smoke Mantle.
cheatNotes: Late arrival eats a Shadow Scald plus a -75% Smoke Mantle. Zone in on time.
Casting a blade can trigger Steal Charm, taking ALL your charms. Blade only in the same round you hit so he cannot steal them first.
He recasts this steal at random through the fight, so never leave charms sitting for a full round.
Global spells also trigger the charm-steal. Avoid dropping globals mid-setup.
Casting Feint makes him Cleanse Ward twice, which beats Indemnity. Do not Feint him; use other setup.
Every third round he Dimension Shifts his traps onto a random friend or foe. Land your hit before the shift or the traps scatter.
At Round 5 he summons Chilling and Stormy Helpers. Do not grind them; pressure Krampus.
Killed helpers resummon after one round, so racing Krampus beats clearing adds.

name: Krampus (Tier 3)
locations: Wizard City: Ravenwood - Krampusgarten,Colossus Boulevard - Krampusgarten
cheats: "Naughty, naughty! Punctuality is a virtue!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Krampus will cheat-cast a Shadow version of Scald dealing 750 Fire Damage over three Rounds and a -75% version of Smoke Mantle on the Wizard.
"A gift for Krampus? You shouldn't have!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, he may cast Steal Charm (Shadow -All) that steals all Charms from the Wizard who cast the Blade.
He will occasionally cast this throughout the duel.
Pet Spells can trigger this cheat.
"Naughty, naughty! Krampus sees all!" - If a Wizard casts a Feint Spell, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) two times (which makes Indemnity ineffective).
Mass Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Balance Feint does not trigger this cheat.

"Krampus is everywhere!"" - After Krampus has summoned his Minions and has Traps, Krampus will cast a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that transfers all negative hanging effects that are on him (e.g. Traps) to a random ally.
"'Tis the season of Krampus!" - At the beginning of Round 5, and randomly thereafter, when he has no Minions, he will cast a Shadow Spell to summon 3 random minions.
Chilling Helper (Tier 3)
Stormy Helper (Tier 3)
Whenever a Minion is defeated, he will resummon another after one Round.
spellNotes: The Damage portion of Sacrifice (1,500) can be used on any ally.
The Lump of Coal Pet casts the Krampus Spell, which acts like a Natural Attack.
strategy: Fight as a team: three supports stacking blades on one hitter plus a healer. Arrange positions Support - Support - Support/Healer - Hitter.
Krampus is Fire and resists Fire and Death 50% (Storm/Myth/Balance 30%); he takes +15% from Ice and Life, so bring an Ice or Life hitter.
Only ever blade the hitter. Blading yourself triggers Steal Charm, which removes ALL your charms; use Aegis-protected blades and avoid may-cast blade pets, which also trigger it.
Do NOT cast single-target Feints: any Feint triggers a double Cleanse Ward that even beats Indemnity. If you must trap, use Mass Feint or Balance Feint, which don't trigger it.

Round plan: R1 everyone lays Aegis blades on the hitter, R2 three keep blading while one drops an aura, R3 the hitter unloads a single-target hit with ~9 stacked blades (school blade, Elemental/Spirit Blade, Balance Blade, Dark Pact at ~100% gear damage).
Use single-target spells only. Avoid AoE hits and Bladestorms, because a random Beguile cheat can turn them onto your own team.

Race to finish before Round 5: with no minions he summons 3 (each ~5,024 HP) and resummons one a round after any is killed. Also don't leave traps on him, since his Shadow Dimension Shift dumps them onto a random ally.
cheatNotes: Join before the fight starts. Late arrivals eat a Shadow Scald (750 Fire over three rounds) plus a -75% Smoke Mantle.
Blade yourself and he may cast Steal Charm to take ALL your charms, so only blade your hitter, never the caster.
He throws this charm-steal out occasionally throughout the duel, not just once.
Pet-cast blades also trigger the steal, so watch out for may-cast blade pets.
Feints are forbidden: any single-target Feint triggers two Shadow Cleanse Wards (-1), which defeats Indemnity protection.
Mass Feint is safe, it does not trigger the cleanse cheat.
Balance Feint is also safe from the cleanse cheat.
Once he has minions and traps on him, a Shadow Dimension Shift moves all his traps onto a random ally, so time your traps to hit before this fires.
At the start of Round 5 (and randomly after) if he has no minions, he summons 3 random minions.
Kill a minion and he resummons a replacement one round later.

name: Kravenly the Hunter
cheats: At the beginning of every round, Kravenly will cast Natural Attack (Kravenly) at a random Wizard.
Stunning him does not prevent this cheat from occurring.
This cheat can be dispelled.
When reaching the following Health thresholds, Kravenly the Hunter will cast the following spells.
Spirit Blast dealing 1,120 Death Damage, 1,095 Life Damage, or 1,190 Myth Damage to all enemies.
Spectral Blast dealing 1,320 Fire Damage, 1,095 Ice Damage, or 1,650 Storm Damage to all enemies.
"Health at 75%" - Once his health reaches 75% (11,280 remaining), he will interrupt cast his Spirit Blast.
"Health at 50%" - When his health reaches 50% (7,520 remaining), he will interrupt cast his Spirit Blast.
His Spectral Blast may trigger from this instead.
"Health at 25%" - When his health reaches 25% (3,760 remaining), he will interrupt cast his Spirit Blast.
If multiple health cheats are triggered at once, the Spirit Blasts may not occur immediately together. One will trigger the same round and the others on following rounds.
spellNotes: Deals 153 Life Damage for 1 Pip, 245 Life Damage for 2 Pips, or 340 Life Damage for 3 Pips + 630 Life Damage over 3 Rounds.
strategy: This is the final fight of the Banyan Tower; a team of Ice, Life, and Storm wizards clears the tower well. Kravenly is Rank 12 Life.
The winning plan is a one-shot: take him out in one hit and his interrupt Blasts and heals never come into play.
Blade up across the setup rounds and Feint him, then land one big attack. Life wizards must bring a Life Prism through his 80% Life resist; Fire and Death are boosted 35% against him.
Send a tank in alone the first round to soak the opening Natural Attack and avoid a double Entangle before the rest of the team engages.
Build some Life resist and lean on pet heals to shrug off his round-start Natural Attack DoT (up to 340 Life plus 630 over 3 rounds); you can also dispel that cheat outright.

If you cannot one-shot, expect an interrupt Spirit Blast at 75%, 50%, and 25% health (AoE up to 1,190 across schools) and possibly a Spectral Blast at 50% (up to 1,650 Storm), so heal and shield before crossing each threshold.
cheatNotes: Every round he opens by casting Natural Attack on a random wizard, hitting for up to 340 Life plus 630 Life over three rounds; Life resist and pet heals cover it.
Stunning Kravenly does not stop the round-start Natural Attack, so do not rely on stuns to shut it down.
The Natural Attack cheat can be dispelled (a Life dispel) to turn it off.
His Spirit Blast is a team-wide AoE dealing 1,120 Death, 1,095 Life, or 1,190 Myth damage.
His Spectral Blast is a team-wide AoE dealing 1,320 Fire, 1,095 Ice, or 1,650 Storm damage.
At 75% health (11,280 remaining) he interrupt-casts Spirit Blast, so blowing past this threshold in one hit avoids it.
At 50% health (7,520 remaining) he interrupt-casts Spirit Blast again.
The 50% trigger may fire the Spectral Blast instead of Spirit Blast.
At 25% health (3,760 remaining) he interrupt-casts another Spirit Blast.
Crossing several thresholds in one hit spreads the Blasts out over the following rounds rather than all at once, so a clean one-shot avoids them entirely.

name: Kravenly the Hunter (Gallery)
cheats: Kravenly will remove any negative charms that are placed on him (e.g. Weakness or dispels).
At the beginning of every other Round, Kravenly will cheat-cast a Natural Attack (Kravenly) at a random Wizard, which deals 340 initial Life damage plus 630 Life damage over 3 Rounds.
Stunning him does not prevent this cheat from occurring.

"Health at 75%" - Once his health reaches 75% (19,100 health remaining), he will cast an multi-target version of Spirit Blast, that deals 1,120 Death Damage, 1,095 Life Damage, or 1,190 Myth Damage on all Wizards.
"Health at 50%" - When his health reaches 50% (12,733 health remaining), he will cast his Spirit Blast.
"Health at 25%" - When his health reaches 25% (6,366 health remaining), he will cast his Spirit Blast.
His Backlash can trigger this cheat.
Bringing his health down to 50% or 25% from one attack, will still trigger each stage of this cheat to be cast consecutively.
"Your body and soul are mine once again!" - At the end of Round 1, Ghost Dog will summon one Seijun Killer Minion and one Hagakure Blade Minion. (They become active in Round 3.)

"Again feel the poetry of war!" - At the end of Round 2 (and every 5 Rounds thereafter), he will cast a Damage over Time (DoT) spell that deals 400 Death Damage per Round for 10 Rounds (575 Death damage in Shadow form).

"The way of the Samoorai is found in death!" - At the end of Round 5 (and every 5 Rounds thereafter), he will cast a version of Detonate (Death) that detonates 5 DoTs on all Wizards dealing 2,800 Death Damage. This includes the "poetry of war" DoT, but DoTs placed before this cheat DoT will remain.
The target of these attacks will be the "most intimidating" Wizard (most blades or casted traps), or the Wizard in the first position (Sun spot) by default.

If the Detonate (Death) cheat fizzles, he'll cast it again to hit his target, then recast it two more times (for a total of 4 casts - though nothing actually occurs the last two times). This may be intentional, for the purpose of removing any Strangle dispels that are placed on him.
Shift or Triage can be used to safely remove the DoT (make sure to time it in between the Detonate (Death).
"I retain my resolve!" - If a Wizard casts a positive ward (e.g. shields), he will cast a 0-pip Shatter to remove the shields.
Shields placed on other Wizards will not trigger this cheat.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
Multi-target spells, such as Legion Shield or the Spirit Armor applied by Rebirth, will trigger this cheat, but only for the Wizard that cast the spell.
strategy: Kravenly punishes shields with Shatter and cleans off any weakness or dispel you place, so protect the team by racing damage and using Triage rather than wards.
He fires Spirit Blast at the whole team at 75%, 50%, and 25% health, so burst through each threshold in one hit rather than parking just above it.

Manage the DoTs: he lays a 400-Death-per-round DoT at the end of Round 2 and every 5 rounds after, and detonates 5 DoTs for 2800 Death at the end of Round 5 and every 5 after. Use Shift or Triage to clear the DoT between detonates.
The hitter holding the most blades or traps draws his attacks, so keep that wizard healed and funnel blades to them late.
Blade the hitter and hit with Fire or Death (+35%, resists Life 80%), aiming to cross the 50% and 25% thresholds with big hits.
cheatNotes: Weakness, dispels, and other negative charms on him are removed instantly, so do not rely on them.
Every other round he hits a random wizard with 340 Life plus a 630 DoT, so keep the team topped up.
Stunning him does not stop that attack.
At 75% health he Spirit Blasts the whole team for over 1000, so heal for it or push past 75% fast.
At 50% he Spirit Blasts again; blow through this threshold in one hit.
At 25% he Spirit Blasts a third time; burst through it too.
His own Backlash can drop him past a threshold and trigger the blast.
Crossing multiple thresholds in one hit fires each blast in turn, so plan heals for a possible double blast.
End of Round 1 the Ghost Dog summons two minions that go active on Round 3, so clear or manage them.
End of Round 2 and every 5 rounds after he lays a 400-Death-per-round DoT for 10 rounds.
End of Round 5 and every 5 after he detonates 5 DoTs for 2800 Death to all, so clear DoTs before it lands.
Whoever holds the most blades or traps is his target, so keep that wizard healed.
Shift or Triage safely removes his DoT; time it between the Round-5 detonates.
Casting a shield makes him Shatter it off with a 0-pip cheat, so skip shields.
Mass Feint triggers the Shatter too, so protect it or avoid it.
Team shields like Legion Shield or Rebirth's armor trigger the Shatter for the caster only.

name: Krokodile Hunter
cheats: "Let the show begin!" - At the end of Round 1, Krokodile Hunter will cast a protected Balanceblade (+100%) on himself, a Feint (Shadow), a +200% outgoing Damage Berserk and a 0 Damage Tempest to all enemies.
"That's right- showed you!" - After the Tempest, Krokodile Hunter will cast a Star version of Supernova, removing his Aura and dealing no Damage to himself.

"You're caught in the jaws of the Krok mate!" - At the beginning of every Round, Krokodile Hunter will cast a version of Spinysaur that deals 100 Life Damage to a random enemy. (214 with Outgoing Damage)
strategy: Hit with Storm — he takes +30% from it — and avoid his 64% Myth resist.
Let Round 1 play out. At the end of it he loads a +100% Balanceblade, a Feint, a +200% outgoing Berserk aura, and a harmless 0-damage Tempest. The Berserk is the only real threat.
His own Supernova immediately strips that Berserk aura off him, so the big-hit danger is gone by Round 2 while his Balanceblade stays.
He is stunable — stun him if you need to skip a dangerous turn while you set up.
Stack blades over the next rounds and end it with one boosted Storm hit. His per-round Spinysaur only chips ~214 to a random player, so just stay healed.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1 he buffs himself: +100% Balanceblade, a Feint, a +200% outgoing Berserk, and a 0-damage Tempest. Only the Berserk makes his hits scary.
Right after, his own Supernova removes the Berserk aura from him, so the +200% damage threat is gone entering Round 2.
Every round he throws a small Spinysaur (~214 Life) at a random player. Minor chip damage; just keep the team topped up.

name: Krokopatra (Challenge)
cheats: [Late] "Your Queen has no patience for tardiness!"' - If a Wizard is late to combat, Krokopatra casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.

[Damage] "You dare strike your Queen?!" - If Krokopatra is attacked, she will cast a version of Healing Current that heals for 110, 440, or 1,100 Health, before casting a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
[Damage over Time] "I shrug off such paltry magic"' - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect on Krokopatra, she will cast Stormzilla (Tier 5b).
[Blade] "You can't charm me!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global, Krokopatra will either cast an AoE Moon version of Enfeeble, or Healing Current on herself.
strategy: Set up in one Round. Every blade or global you cast makes her Enfeeble your team or heal, so blade and hit the same turn.
Skip damage-over-time entirely. It triggers Stormzilla back at you.
Expect a heal-then-Insane-Bolt every time she is struck, so aim to kill in a single big hit rather than chipping her.
She resists all schools only 10%, so use your strongest school and pile blades and traps for one burst.
Keep the team healthy for the Moon Insane Bolt retaliation, which hits all for up to 1,550.
cheatNotes: Arriving late triggers a Moon Insane Bolt on the whole team for up to 1,550. Come in on Round 1.
Every time you attack her she first heals herself, then Insane Bolts the whole team. Plan for the retaliation and burst her down fast.
Do not use damage-over-time on her. It makes her cast Stormzilla back.
Every blade or global you cast makes her Enfeeble your team or heal herself. Blade the same Round you hit.

name: Krokopatra (Rank 14)
cheats: Krokopatra
"More fodder! Protect your Queen!" If a Minion is defeated, Krokopatra will summon another random School Minion which will become active after two Rounds.

"Time to ride the Lightning!" - Various triggers can occur: If no Minions are present, if she has been defeated and healed, if some Minions are defeated and healed, Krokopatra will shout this phrase and use her turn to cast Insane Bolt. Needs confirmation on triggers.
"Why don't we direct all that potential?" Krokopatra will randomly cast Beguile on the Wizard with the most Blades at the beginning of the Round.
"Loose the winds of destruction!" - Krokopatra will interupt-cast Windstorm (+25%).
"I'm crackling with rage!" - Krokopatra will interupt-cast a zero-Pip Supercharge for the amount of Pips she has. Needs confirmation on trigger.
"Beware! There's a storm brewing!" Krokopatra will cheat-cast Astraphobia.
Queen's Thrall (Death) (Rank 14)
"Anything for my Queen" If Krokopatra receives a Charm, this Minion will cheat-cast Dark Pact on her.
"No one threatens my queen!" - The Wizard with the most Blades may randomly get a Bad Juju by this Minion at the beginning of the Round.
Queen's Thrall (Life) (Rank 14)
'We must protect ourselves to protect the Queen!" If a DoT Spell is cast, this Minion will cheat-cast Mass Triage.
"Our Queen needs her army!" - If Krokopatra or a Minion is attacked, this Minion will cheat-cast Unicorn.
Queen's Thrall (Storm) (Rank 14)
"Protect the Queen!" - If Krokopatra is attacked, this Minion will cheat-cast Healing Current.
"Stand Strong for the Queen!" - If a Dispel is placed on Krokopatra or a Minion, this Minion will cheat-cast Cleanse Charm.
strategy: The guide's recommended team is two Fire wizards plus one wizard of any school. The Fire wizards are the damage dealers; the third slot is flexible support. Traps are your best friend in this fight.
Turn 1 setup: Fire wizard 1 casts Potent Backdraft, Fire wizard 2 and the third wizard each lay any Fire trap, and the Fire hitter casts Phoenix into the stacked traps.
Backdraft on a Fire wizard amplifies the trap damage enough to end the fight in one or two turns; the author's 180% damage Fire hit did about 11k against the roughly 11,040 health boss.
If you are short on Fire wizards, use your own school's traps instead and just take one extra setup turn to stack enough before the hit.

Avoid the punish cheats: Feints trigger dual Insane Bolts, DoTs prompt a Kraken removal, Juju or weakness spells trigger Hammer of Thor, and Blades or global spells provoke Enfeeble. Stick to traps plus one big hit.
Spread setup so no single wizard is the obvious Beguile target, then funnel the Backdraft-fed Phoenix (or your school's AoE) for the kill before her thralls out-heal you.
cheatNotes: Killing a thrall summons a new random-school one that wakes up two Rounds later; you cannot clear the board for good, so plan to kill her directly.
With no minions, or after being healed, she may burn her turn on Insane Bolt; a self-inflicted risk that can help you.
She Beguiles the wizard carrying the most blades; spread blades or funnel them late so no single wizard is the obvious target.
She interrupt-casts a +25% Windstorm to buff her Storm damage.
She can interrupt with a zero-pip Supercharge scaling to her pip count, powering a big follow-up.
She can cheat-cast Astraphobia on the team; keep shields up.
If Krokopatra receives a charm, the Death thrall Dark Pacts her, buffing her at a health cost.
The Death thrall may Bad Juju the wizard with the most blades; another reason not to over-stack blades on one player.
Casting a DoT makes the Life thrall Mass Triage it off the whole enemy side; skip DoTs and burst instead.
Attacking Krokopatra or a minion makes the Life thrall heal the enemy side with Unicorn; hit hard enough to out-pace it.
Attacking Krokopatra makes the Storm thrall Healing-Current her; combine kills into one big hit.
Dispelling Krokopatra or a minion makes the Storm thrall Cleanse it off; Dispels are wasted here.

name: Krokopatra (Rank 8)
cheats: Krokopatra will cheat-cast Beguile before a Wizard attacks with an Attack-All-Enemies Spell, causing the Wizard to attack allies that Round.
This cheat does not activate if soloing.
"More fodder! Protect your queen!" If one of Krokopatra's Minions is defeated before her, she will randomly summon another Minion at the end of the next Round.
"Loose the winds of destruction!" She will randomly cast Windstorm.

"Why don't we redirect all that potential?" She will cheat-cast Beguile at the beginning of the Round if there is any Blade on a Wizard. This will repeat every other Round until there are no more Blades on the Wizard.
"I'm crackling with rage!" Krokopatra will randomly cheat-cast Supercharge (+12%). Its percentage is still based on her number of Pips, however.
"Stand strong for the Queen!"If a Wizard casts a negative Charm (e.g. Plague), this Queen's Thrall will cast Cleanse Charm on the receiver of the Charm.
"Our Queen needs her army!" Interrupt casts Unicorn when any other Thralls are defeated in the same Round. Stuns do not prevent this cheat.
"We must protect ourselves to protect the Queen!" If a Damage over Time Spell is cast, this Minion will cheat-cast Mass Triage.
"Anything for my queen!" Every other Round, this Queen's Thrall will cast Dark Pact (+30%) on Krokopatra.
This Thrall can defeat itself using this cheat.
strategy: Storm boss (2400). Hit with Myth (+40%); never Storm (70% resist). She is fragile, so focus her, not the Thralls.

Blades are a trap here: if you have a blade on you she Beguiles you, and repeats every other round until the blade is gone. Either skip blades and lean on traps and Feint, or blade and hit the same round.
Avoid AoE in a group; she Beguiles the AoE caster before it fires. Use single-target on her.
Do not use DoTs (Thrall Mass Triage), and expect her to Supercharge and get Dark Pact from her Thralls, so keep healthy.
Trap her and land one Myth hit to end it before the Thralls snowball.
cheatNotes: In a group she Beguiles you right before you fire an all-enemies spell, turning it on your team. Use single-target attacks on her.
Kill a Thrall before Krokopatra and she summons a replacement. Do not waste time on Thralls; kill her first.
If you are carrying a blade she Beguiles you, and keeps doing it every other round until the blade is gone. Blade and hit the same round, or skip blades.
She randomly Supercharges herself, scaling with her pips, so her hits can jump. Keep your health up.
Throw a Weakness or Plague and a Thrall Cleanses it off. Damage charms are wasted here.
Killing Thralls together makes a Thrall Unicorn-heal the team. Another reason to ignore them and focus the boss.
Cast a DoT and a Thrall Mass Triages it away. Skip damage-over-time.
A Thrall Dark-Pacts Krokopatra every other round, giving her pips at the cost of its own health. Expect her to hit sooner than her pip count suggests.

name: Krokopatra (Standard)
cheats: [Late] "Your Queen has no patience for tardiness!"' - If a Wizard is late to combat, Krokopatra will cheat-cast a Moon version of Insane Bolt, dealing 250, 500 or 1,000 Moon Damage on all enemies.

[Damage] "You dare strike your Queen?!"' - If a Wizard Damages Krokopatra, she will cheat-cast a version of Healing Current, restoring 100, 400, or 1,000 Health to herself. Then, Krokopatra casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt, dealing 250, 500 or 1,000 Moon Damage on all enemies.
[Damage over Time] "I shrug off such paltry magic" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect on Krokopatra, she will cheat-cast Stormzilla (Tier 5b).
[Blade] "You can't charm me!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or outgoing Damage Aura, Krokopatra will cheat-cast an Ice version of Enfeeble on all Wizards.
This cheat cannot be Dispelled.
Aegis will not bypass this cheat.
[Trap] "Your clumsy traps are no match for my grace" - If a Wizard casts a Trap or incoming Damage Aura, Krokopatra will cheat-cast a Life version of Cleanse Ward to remove all Traps on herself.
Indemnity will not bypass this cheat.
strategy: This is a one-shot fight. Any damage to her makes her heal (up to 1,000) and fire an Insane Bolt AoE, so you must go from full health to dead in a single hit - never chip her.
Hit Myth. She takes +30% Myth and resists 33% Storm, so Myth is your kill school.
Do not use Damage-over-Time - it makes her summon Stormzilla. And skip traps: any trap makes her Cleanse Ward all traps off herself, and Indemnity will not stop it.

Blades work but cost you: each blade or outgoing-damage aura makes her Enfeeble the whole team (a weakness you cannot dispel, and Aegis will not block). Stack your blades in one turn, then Cleanse the weaknesses off just before you swing.
Enter on time to dodge her opening Insane Bolt, build every blade you can in a single setup turn, clear the Enfeebles, then land one overwhelming Myth hit that kills her outright.
cheatNotes: A late joiner gets hit with a Moon Insane Bolt for 250, 500, or 1,000 to all enemies.
Any time you damage her she heals herself 100, 400, or 1,000, then fires a Moon Insane Bolt at the whole team. Kill her in one hit instead of chipping.
A Damage-over-Time on her makes her summon Stormzilla (Tier 5b).
Any blade or outgoing-damage aura makes her cast an Ice Enfeeble on all wizards, weakening your whole team.
That Enfeeble cannot be dispelled.
Aegis will not stop the Enfeeble either.
Any trap or incoming-damage aura makes her Cleanse Ward all traps off herself.
Indemnity will not stop that trap cleanse.

name: Krokopatra (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Krokopatra Rematch - Krokopatra Challenge (Tier 2)
cheats: "Your Queen has no patience for tardiness!"' - If a Wizard is late to combat, Krokopatra (Tier 2) casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
"I feel alive!" - At the end of every Round Krokopatra (Tier 2) has a Heal over Time, she casts Thundering Jinn.
"Your dare strike your queen?!"' - If a Wizard Damages her, she casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
"I shrug off such paltry magic"' - If a Wizard casts a DoT on Krokopatra (Tier 2), she casts Kraken (Tier 5b).
"You can't charm me!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global, she casts either an AoE Moon version of Enfeeble, or Healing Current on herself.
"You can't weaken the queen!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage Debuff on Krokopatra (Tier 2), she casts Hammer of Thor (Tier 5b).
"Your clumsy feints are no match for my grace" - If a Wizard casts a Feint, she casts Insane Bolt, then casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
"I will not be triumphed over!" - If a Wizard casts Dissipate, she casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
strategy: She punishes almost every action, so keep your deck lean: fewer, bigger plays beat lots of small ones.
Damaging her triggers a Moon Insane Bolt AoE (random 550, 1,050, or 1,550) on your team, so shield the group and be ready to heal after each hit.
Skip DoTs (she counters with Kraken), damage debuffs (Hammer of Thor), and extra Feints (Insane Bolt twice); each just eats a punish.
Blades and Globals trigger an AoE Enfeeble or a self-heal via Healing Current. With 45% incoming healing she heals well, so bring Doom and Gloom or infection and blade the same round you hit.
She has no boost school and 20% resist to everything, so attack with your strongest school, stack a couple of blades, and burst her down accepting the Insane Bolt return.
cheatNotes: Arriving late gets your whole team hit with a Moon Insane Bolt (550, 1,050, or 1,550). Join on time.
If she has a Heal-over-Time ticking at end of round she casts Thundering Jinn, so do not hand her healing to protect.
Any damage on her returns a Moon Insane Bolt AoE on your team; shield before you hit.
A DoT on her triggers Kraken, so leave DoTs out.
Blades and Globals make her cast an AoE Enfeeble or heal herself with Healing Current. Blade the same round you hit and pack an infection.
A damage debuff (weakness type) triggers Hammer of Thor, so do not debuff her damage.
Feinting her triggers Insane Bolt plus a Moon Insane Bolt AoE, the priciest punish. Avoid Feint.
Casting Dissipate triggers another Moon Insane Bolt AoE.

strategy: She counters almost everything with an AoE Moon Insane Bolt (550/1,050/1,550), so plan to kill in as few actions as possible and keep the team's health buffered.

Set up with Auras and at most one trap instead, since those are your only quiet tools.
She resists all schools 40% and self-pierces 60%, so bring raw damage and accuracy rather than a stacked charm setup.
Enter on time (late is a free Insane Bolt), then land one big single hit and heal through the counter.
cheatNotes: Join late and she opens with an AoE Moon Insane Bolt (550/1,050/1,550) — always enter on time.

name: Krokosphinx (Balance)
cheats: 1st Riddle

[Round] "I am caustic and invisible; I can be measured, but never touched; and I am infinite, but always in short supply. What am I?" - At the end of the first Round, Krokosphinx will change each Wizards' Spell Decks, giving all Wizards the following Spells and 20,000 Health:
[Candy][Crowns][Space][Summer][Time]
Correct Answer: Time
"Correct. Time rots the world, renders empire into ruins." - If answered correctly, Krokosphinx will cast a +100% Damage Trap on itself.
"WRONG!" - Krokosphinx will say this if answered incorrectly.
2nd Riddle

[Round] "What is a bringer of death and life, light and shadow, and energy and exhaustion?" - At the end of the second Round, Krokosphinx will change each Wizards' Spell Decks, giving all Wizards the following Spells and 20,000 Health:
[Conversation][Exercise][Sugar][The Moon][The Sun]
Correct Answer: The Sun
"Yes, the sun enables, but also burns. Its light casts shadow. And its energy brings wearying heat." - If answered correctly, Krokosphinx will cast a +100% Damage Trap on itself.
3rd Riddle

[Round] "I haunt like a ghost, ensnare like a trap and lie to my creator. I bring gentle comfort or bitter sickness. I am faded, but forever. What am I?" - At the end of the third Round, Krokosphinx will change each Wizards' Spell Decks, giving all Wizards the following Spells and 20,000 Health:
[Hunger][Memory][Pictures][Poltergeist][Regret]
Correct Answer: Memory
"Indeed, memory is a fickle thing. It can deceive us, dismay us or remind us of happier days." - If answered correctly, Krokosphinx will cast a +100% Damage Trap on itself.
strategy: This is a riddle fight. Answer each round with the given card to load damage traps onto the Sphinx.
Round 1, cast Time. Round 2, cast The Sun. Round 3, cast Memory. Each correct answer stacks a +100% Damage Trap on Krokosphinx.
You are handed a fresh deck and 20,000 health each riddle, so survival is not the concern; answering right is.
After the three traps are up, hit with Myth, Life, or Death (each +30%); avoid Balance (70% resist).
Stack your own blades alongside the three cheat traps, then land one massive hit into the 100,000 health.
cheatNotes: First riddle, answered at the end of Round 1. The answer is Time.
Cast Time to answer correctly.
A correct answer stacks a +100% Damage Trap on the Sphinx, free setup for your hit.
Wrong answers earn nothing; only the correct card loads a trap.
Second riddle, answered at the end of Round 2. The answer is The Sun.
Cast The Sun to answer correctly.
Another correct answer adds a second +100% Damage Trap.
Third riddle, answered at the end of Round 3. The answer is Memory.
Cast Memory to answer correctly.
The third correct answer adds a third +100% Damage Trap. Now hit.

name: Kurokage (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Midnight Sun Pagoda - Midnight Sun Pagoda (Tier 1)
cheats: "You won't last long without shields!" - If a Wizard attacks him or his allies without a shield, he interrupt-casts a Vampire dealing 350 Death Drain Damage, healing for half the damage dealt, against the Wizard that attacked.
This cheat does not always occur when someone attacks.
Absorbs can't prevent this cheat.
Every other Round, he casts a version of Cleanse Ward to remove all wards (shields and traps) on a random Wizard.
strategy: Keep a shield on yourself before you attack — hitting him or his allies without a shield up triggers a 350 Death Vampire on the attacker.
He Cleanse Wards a random wizard every other round, stripping shields and traps, so re-shield before you hit.
Hit with Storm (+10%); he resists Myth 40%.
The whole fight is low health — blade and AoE the board quickly.
cheatNotes: Attack him or his minions without a shield on yourself and he Vampires the attacker for 350 — always keep a shield up.
Absorbs won't stop that Vampire, so use an actual shield.
Every other round he strips all shields and traps off a random wizard — reapply your shield before attacking.

name: Kurokage (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Midnight Sun Pagoda - Midnight Sun Pagoda (Tier 2)
cheats: If a Wizard attacks Kurokage without a Shield, he will interrupt-cast Vampire at the Wizard.
Starting Round two, every other Round, he casts a Myth spell to remove all Wards (Shields and Traps) on a random Wizard.
strategy: Small Myth boss (1,965). He takes +20% from Storm and resists Myth 50%. Hit with Storm or a non-Myth school.
Attacking him without a Shield up triggers Vampire on you, so keep a shield active on the turn you swing.
From Round 2, every other round he strips all wards (shields and traps) off a random wizard. Re-shield after these wipes and do not over-invest in traps that may be removed.
Blade, keep a shield up, and hit with Storm.
cheatNotes: Attacking him with no Shield up triggers Vampire on you. Keep a shield active on the round you hit.
Starting Round 2, every other round he removes all shields and traps from a random wizard. Re-shield after each wipe rather than stacking wards.

name: Kurokage (Tier 3)
cheats: [Damage] "You won't last long without shields!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage Spell on Kurokage and does not have a Shield, then Kurokage will interrupt-cast a version of Vampire on the Wizard that damaged him.

strategy: Always keep a Shield on yourself before you damage Kurokage. Hitting him unshielded triggers a Vampire drain back on you.
He strips all Wards from a random wizard every other round, so do not over-invest in shields; refresh the one you attack behind.
He resists Myth 70% and takes +40% from Storm, so hit with Storm.
Only 1,935 health across the boss; blade once and AoE to clear him and the treant, monk, and wildclaw minions together.
cheatNotes: Damaging Kurokage while you have no Shield up makes him interrupt-cast Vampire on you, draining you.
Every other round he casts a Myth spell that removes all Wards from a random wizard.

Starting Round two, every other Round he casts a version of Cleanse Ward to remove all wards (shields and traps) on a random Wizard.
strategy: Always have a shield up before you attack him or a minion. Attacking unshielded triggers Vampire (600 Death steal) on you.
From round 2, every other round he strips all shields and traps off a random wizard. Reapply your shield and time traps to hit the same round you set them.
Hit with Storm or Death (+30% incoming); avoid Myth (40% resist).
AoE the three minions with the boss, or shield up and focus him.
Blade and Feint behind a shield, then burst the 7960 health.
cheatNotes: Attacking him or a minion without a shield on you triggers a 600-damage Vampire steal. Keep a shield up before every attack.

name: Kurokage (Tier 5)
