(Cheat only triggers with 6+ Ranked Spells?)
This cheat doesn't activate for damage over time spells.
[Manipulation] Every other Round, Kurokage will interrupt-cast a Myth Spell that removes all Wards from a random Wizard.
strategy: Always keep a shield on yourself before attacking. Hitting him with a 6+ pip spell while you have no shield triggers a 600-damage Vampire counter on the attacker.
Damage-over-time spells and low-rank spells do not trigger the counter - an alternative if you cannot stay shielded.
Hit with Storm or Death - he takes 35% more from both and resists Myth 80%.
He strips all wards from a random wizard every other round, so reapply your shield right before you hit rather than early.
He is stunable and beguilable - stun to skip his ward-strip turn while you set up.
cheatNotes: Attacking him with a 6+ pip spell while you have no shield up triggers an interrupt Vampire dealing 600 to you. Keep a shield on yourself.
The counter appears tied to 6+ pip spells; smaller spells do not seem to trigger it.
Damage-over-time spells do not trigger the Vampire counter - a safe way to deal damage unshielded.
Every other round he strips all wards from a random wizard. Re-shield right before you attack.

name: Kus'Bhid
cheats: "We all give to the Toll Taker!" - The Ravenous Tooth (Life) will cast Brilliant Light (triggering Kus'Bhid to cast Link).
This cheat has no effect if Kus'Bhid is not present.
"The Toll Taker accepts!" - Kus'Bhid replies to his Minions by casting Power Link on a random Wizard.
"Time to pay the Toll!" - At the end of every second Round, Kus'Bhid will cast Brimstone Revenant on a random Wizard.
"No one crosses the Toll Bridge for free" - At the end of every fourth Round, Kus'Bhid will cast Efreet on a random Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Ice, he takes +50%, and never Fire (80% resist).
Kill the Ravenous Tooth minion early. Its Brilliant Light triggers his Link, and his cheats need him present anyway.
Expect a Brimstone Revenant on a random wizard every second round and an Efreet every fourth, so shield for Fire and heal off those beats.
At only 5,030 health, stack one round of Ice blades and burst him down before the Round 4 Efreet.
Use an Ice AoE to hit boss and minion together if you cannot split focus.
cheatNotes: His Ravenous Tooth casts Brilliant Light, which makes Kus'Bhid cast Link, kill the minion to break this loop.
The Link cheat does nothing if Kus'Bhid himself is gone.
He answers his minion by Power Linking a random wizard, buffing himself.
At the end of every second round he drops a Brimstone Revenant DoT on a random wizard.
At the end of every fourth round he casts Efreet on a random wizard, shield for Fire before Round 4.

name: Lady Stonegaze (Tier 1)
locations: Wizard City: Garden of Hesperides - Stonegaze's Chamber
cheats: "Mind your gaze, lest ye be turned to stone!" - Lady Stonegaze (Tier 1) gives a warning at the beginning of the first Round.
"Haha! Another statue for my garden!" - If a Wizard casts any Spell on Lady Stonegazze (Tier 1), including AoE Spells, she will Polymorph the Wizard into Statue for 99 Rounds.
Polymorph has a deck of 77 Cards, all being Pass Cards, except for one Break Free Card.
If Lady Stonegaze (Tier 1) was defeated by a Spell that is not from the Blindfolded Hits, she will use a Moon version of Blessing to restore 100% max Health on herself.
At the end of every other Round, one of the following Spells is given to all Wizards who aren't Polymorphed:

[Blindfolded Hit (Balance, Tier 1)][Blindfolded Hit (Death, Tier 1)][Blindfolded Hit (Fire, Tier 1)][Blindfolded Hit (Ice, Tier 1)][Blindfolded Hit (Life, Tier 1)][Blindfolded Hit (Myth, Tier 1)][Blindfolded Hit (Storm, Tier 1)]
strategy: Do not cast any of your own spells on her. Anything you cast, even AoE, turns you into a Statue for 99 rounds (the deck is all Pass cards plus one Break Free).
She hands every non-polymorphed wizard a Blindfolded Hit card at the end of every other round. Attack only with these.
She must be defeated with a Blindfolded Hit; killing her with any other spell full-heals her via Moon Blessing.
Pass and bank Blindfolded Hits, then finish her with them; she is only 2700 health.
cheatNotes: Casting any spell on her, even AoE, polymorphs you into a Statue for 99 rounds, so never cast your own spells at her.
The Statue deck is 77 cards, all Pass except one Break Free, so you are stuck until you draw it.
Killing her with anything other than a Blindfolded Hit fully heals her, so only finish with the Blindfolded Hits she gives you.
She hands out a Blindfolded Hit to each un-polymorphed wizard every other round; these are your only weapons.

Stack self-blades between hands so one Blindfolded Hit finishes her (4,500 health, no resist).

Every other round each wizard who is not a statue receives one Blindfolded Hit of a random school — your only legal way to damage her.

name: Lady Stonegaze (Tier 4)
locations: Wizard City: Garden of Hesperides - Stonegaze's Chamber
cheats: "Mind your gaze, lest ye be turned to stone!" - Lady Stonegaze (Tier 4) gives a warning at the beginning of the first Round.
"Haha! Another statue for my garden!" - If a Wizard casts any Spell on Lady Stonegaze (Tier 4), including AoE Spells, Traps, or Charms, she will Polymorph the Wizard into Statue for 99 Rounds.

Polymorph has a deck of 77 Cards, all being Pass Cards, except for one Break Free Card. The Pass Spells can be discarded and the Wizard may Pass normally to avoid a spell casting animation. The Wizard can be healed while polymorphed.
Stonegaze's Shadow-Enhanced Spells will remove Polymorph effects from the Wizard.
If Lady Stonegaze (Tier 4) is damaged by a Spell that is not from the Blindfolded Hits, she will use a Moon version of Blessing to restore 100% max Health on herself.

strategy: Myth boss (12425) with a hard rule: only the 0-pip Blindfolded Hit cards she hands out can damage her. She gives them to all non-polymorphed wizards at the end of every other round.

Never target her with your own spells. Any spell you aim at her (attack, trap, charm, even an AoE) turns you into a Statue for 99 rounds, and any normal damage that lands makes her Moon-Blessing to full health.

Because the Blindfolded Hits cost 0 pips, build a support-heavy deck — blades, shields, and heals with room to spare. Frenzy, Blade Storm, Dark Pact, and Balance/Spirit/Elemental Blade are the core buffs.
Buff only yourself: self-blades land on you, not her, so they are safe. Stack self-blades, then unload the Blindfolded Hits the round she provides them.
Boost the Blindfolded Hits with universal (all-school) damage gear, since it lifts every card variant equally.

If you get statued, your deck becomes 77 Pass cards plus one Break Free (50% accuracy). Discard Passes to dig for it, or have a teammate's Shadow-Enhanced spell free you — you can still be healed while a statue.

Watch your pet: passives with pierce or enfeeble can count as targeting her and statue you. A dedicated healer alongside the damage dealers keeps a mixed-level team steady since the fight scales to the lowest player.
cheatNotes: Cast ANY spell targeting her (attack, trap, charm, even an AoE) and you are polymorphed into a Statue for 99 rounds. Only ever hit her with the Blindfolded Hits she provides.
As a statue your deck is 77 Pass cards and one Break Free. Discard Passes to dig for Break Free; you can still be healed while statued.
A Shadow-Enhanced spell removes the Statue polymorph, so a teammate can free you. Solo, rely on finding Break Free.
If she takes damage from anything other than a Blindfolded Hit, she heals to full. Never attack her with your own spells.
Every other round she hands each free wizard a Blindfolded Hit. These are the ONLY spells that damage her, so save your pips for them.

name: Lambent Fire (Tier 2)
locations: Avalon: Crystal Caves - Crystal Caves Gold Key Room
cheats: "Fire pierces all!" - After Lambent Fire casts a Damage Spell, he receives a Firespear.
"The spirits of the past strike you down!" - If a Wizard casts any non-Fire, non-universal Blade, Lambent Fire will cast Brimstone Revenant on the receiver.

"The Fire hungers." - Casting any Fire Damage Blade Spell, including Elemental Blade, randomly triggers the Steam Charm cheat.
Pet May-Casts will trigger this cheat.
"Your world shall burn!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Trap on Lambent Fire, he will cast Immolate on the caster.
Mass Feint and Mass Hex will not trigger this cheat.
When any Skirker Buster casts Natural Attack (Fire 2,050), it will cast a version of Immolate that sacrifices 100% Max Health on itself.
"Come to the light!" - At the end of every Round, Lambent Fire will resummon all Skirker Buster Minions that were defeated or missing on the battlefield.
Death Interrupt Cycle:
When all three Skirker Busters are present, this cheat will cycle at the end of each round and repeats itself after three rounds.
"I give you my strength!" - At the end of the first round, the first Skirker Buster will cast Dark Pact (+30%) on Lambent Fire.
"I give you my health!" - At the end of the second Round, the second Skirker Buster will cast Sacrifice (Death) on Lambent Fire.
"I give of myself to curse you!" - At the end of the third Round, the third Skirker will cast Bad Juju on a random Wizard.
The third Round will not trigger if any Wizards flee the duel.
strategy: Trap him with Mass Feint or Mass Hex — those slip past his cheats, while any universal single-target trap makes him Immolate you.

Avoid stacking blades on yourself: non-Fire blades make him Brimstone Revenant you, universal blades get Steal-Charmed off, and Fire blades can trigger the Steam cheat. Lean on mass traps and a Feint instead.
Ignore the Skirker Busters — he resummons all of them every round; just race Lambent.
Attack with Ice (he takes +30%); he resists Fire 60%.
His Skirker death cycle buffs him (Dark Pact round 1, Sacrifice round 2) and Bad Jujus a wizard on round 3, so burst him down fast and pack a Cleanse.
cheatNotes: After he casts a damage spell he gains a Firespear, so his hits ramp up the longer the fight runs.
A non-Fire, non-universal blade on a wizard makes him Brimstone Revenant the receiver — don't stack school blades openly.
A universal blade (Elemental, Balanceblade) gets Steal-Charmed off the receiver, handing him your blade.
Any Fire damage blade (including Elemental Blade) can randomly trigger the Steam Charm cheat — avoid Fire blades here.
A universal Trap on him makes him Immolate the caster — use Mass Feint or Mass Hex instead.
Mass Feint and Mass Hex slip past that Immolate cheat — they're your safe trapping tools.
He resummons all downed Skirker Busters every round — don't bother clearing them, focus Lambent.
End of round 1 a Skirker Dark Pacts him for +30% — expect a bigger hit; burst him fast.
End of round 2 a Skirker Sacrifices to heal him — another reason to race him down.
End of round 3 a Skirker throws Bad Juju on a random wizard — have a Cleanse ready or finish before round 3.

When all three Skirker Busters are present, this cheat will cycle at the end of each round and repeats itself after three rounds.
[Blade] "I give you my strength!" - At the end of the first round, the first Skirker Buster will cast Dark Pact (+30%) on Lambent Fire.

spellNotes: Deals 350 Fire Damage for 3 Pips maximum
strategy: Plan is simple in shape: blade a single hitter and finish with an AoE that one-shots Lambent Fire and the Skirker Buster minions together. Bring a tank/healer because his blade and trap punishments deal constant damage.
Lambent Fire has 60% Fire resist and takes 30% extra from Ice, so hit with Ice rather than Fire.

Blade very carefully. Non-Fire, non-universal blades make him cast Brimstone Revenant on the wizard who got the blade; universal blades make him Steal Charm it; even Fire damage blades (including Elemental Blade) randomly trigger the steal. Keep blade counts low, lean on an Aura for damage, and let the healer cover the Brimstone hits.

Do not use universal traps. Any universal Trap, Mass Feint, or Mass Hex on Lambent Fire makes him Immolate the caster. Set up with school-specific Ice Traps instead of trapping him with universal traps.

Account for the minions' death cycle: end of Round 1 a Skirker Dark Pacts the boss (+30%), end of Round 2 a Skirker Sacrifices to heal him, end of Round 3 a Skirker throws Cursed Juju on a random wizard. He also resummons dead Skirkers each round, so wipe them in the same AoE as the boss.
Burst everything down in one AoE round rather than grinding, since he re-Firespears himself after every damage spell and keeps resurrecting minions.
cheatNotes: Every time Lambent Fire casts a damage spell he gives himself a Firespear (armor pierce), so his return hits punch through your shields. Keep resist and healing up.
If you put any non-Fire, non-universal blade on someone (like a Stormblade or Iceblade), he casts Brimstone Revenant on that wizard. Blade sparingly and heal the recipient.
If you put a universal blade (like Balanceblade) on someone, he Steal Charms it right off them, so universal blades are usually wasted.
If you use a spread blade like Bladestorm, only the actual caster gets hit by the blade-punishment cheat, not the whole team.
Casting any Fire damage blade, Elemental Blade included, has a random chance to trigger the Steal Charm cheat, so even on-type blades aren't safe.
Pet may-cast blades count too and can randomly set off the Steal Charm cheat. Watch what your pet throws out.
If you put a universal Trap on Lambent Fire, he Immolates the wizard who cast it. Use school-specific Ice Traps instead.
Mass Feint and Mass Hex both count as universal traps here and will trigger the Immolate-on-caster punishment.
When a Skirker Buster uses its 2,050 Fire Natural Attack, it also Immolates itself for 100% of its max health, so the minions damage themselves after hitting you.
At the end of each round Lambent Fire resummons every Skirker Buster that died or is missing, so killing them alone accomplishes nothing. Kill them in the same AoE as the boss.
This resummon is currently bugged so the Skirkers cannot actually join the duel circle.
With all three Skirker Busters present, this death cycle runs at the end of each round and repeats every three rounds.
At the end of Round 1, the first Skirker casts Dark Pact (+30%) on Lambent Fire, boosting his damage.
At the end of Round 2, the second Skirker casts Sacrifice (Death) to heal Lambent Fire.
At the end of Round 3, the third Skirker casts Cursed Juju on a random wizard, weakening the team.
That Round 3 Cursed Juju is skipped if any wizard flees the duel.

name: Lamia
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea - House of Lost Sailors
cheats: On the first Round, Lamia will cast a +35% Death Damage Global Spell.
"You are not the master down here!" - If the Global is replaced, she will re-cast her Global.
Strangle will dispel this cheat.

"You are slow to take in the world around us!" - If you have not tried to replace this Global (Globals don't need to stay active), she will cheat-cast Scarecrow, that deals 620 Health Steal Damage to all enemies and returns half to the caster approximately every four Rounds. However, from the 30th Round onward, she will cast the Scarecrow regardless of whether you have tried to replace her Global or not.
strategy: She takes +35% from Ice and Life and resists Death 80%. Hit with Ice or Life. She is stunnable, so a stun can buy a setup round.
Round 1 she casts a +35% Death-damage global. Since you are not attacking with Death, it barely helps her; leave it up rather than fighting it.

If you do NOT try to replace the global she periodically Scarecrows the team (620 drain, about every 4 rounds), and from Round 30 she Scarecrows regardless. Heal through the occasional Scarecrow and race her down before Round 30.
If you replace her global she simply recasts it; Strangle dispels that recast if you want it gone.
Blade up, stun if handy, and land an Ice or Life nuke.
cheatNotes: Her Round 1 +35% Death global only helps Death damage, which you are not using. Leave it and save cards.
Replace her global and she just recasts it. Do not waste turns fighting it.
Strangle dispels her recast global if you insist on removing it.
Leaving the global alone lets her Scarecrow the team every ~4 rounds, and always from Round 30 on. Heal through it and kill her before Round 30.

Blade and hit in the same round, or Aegis your blade. Any helpful Charm or Aura makes her Starfall the recipient and strip the buff.
Do not heal (she answers with a Pathogen DoT) and do not cast harmful Auras on her (she answers with Monk of Mourning on the caster).
Beat her around round 4. Every fourth round she casts Scarecrow, an AoE drain that heals her. AoE the Storm Shark down with her.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she puts up a +35% Deadzone global that boosts Death and cuts healing. Plan to out-damage, not out-heal.
She recasts Deadzone if you replace it with your own global, so do not bother overwriting it.
Any helpful Charm or Aura makes her Starfall the recipient (or the aura caster), stripping the buff. Blade and hit the same round, or Aegis the blade.
Casting a harmful Aura on Lamia makes her hit the caster with Monk of Mourning (Tier 2b).
Any heal gets the recipient a Pathogen DoT (or the caster on an all-allies heal). Avoid healing.
At the start of the fourth round and every fourth round after, she casts Scarecrow, an AoE drain that heals her. Kill her before it swings the fight.

name: Lazy Henchman
cheats: "I saw that!!" - This Creature casts Rebirth healing 750 without absorbs after Tired Fighter is Damaged.
All Enemy Spells don't trigger this cheat.
This cheat also triggers whenever Half-Witted Skeleton uses Blizzard, or Tired Fighter uses Tempest.
strategy: Hit with Death (+40% incoming); avoid Life (40% resist).
He heals the group for 750 (Rebirth) whenever Tired Fighter takes damage. Kill the Lazy Henchman early to stop the heals.
He is only 1115 health, so one solid attack removes him. Focus him down first.
Then clean up the rest with AoE.
cheatNotes: He Rebirth-heals 750 (no absorbs) whenever Tired Fighter is damaged, undoing your progress. Kill him early to shut it off.
His own side's spells do not trigger the heal, only your attacks on Tired Fighter.
It also fires when Half-Witted Skeleton casts Blizzard or Tired Fighter casts Tempest.

name: Le Pingouin
cheats: "Le whu! Whu!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already started, Le Pingouin will cast Winter Moon at the Wizard.
"Seek and destroy, mes petites!" - At the end of each Round, if there are fewer Minions than Wizards currently in the duel, Le Pingouin will cast a Moon Spell to summon one of the following Minions:
"Whu! Whu!" - Every three Rounds, Le Pingouin will trigger his Minions to cast the following spells.
Clockwork Spanner (Death) will cast a multi-target version of Poison, which deals max Health Damage to self, and 700 Death Damage to all Wizards.
Clockwork Spanner (Fire) will cast a Sun multi-target version of Immolate, which deals max Health Damage to self, and 700 Fire Damage to all Wizards.
"Borealis is hard to pierce, eh?" - Clockwork Spanner (Storm) will cast a Star version of Insane Bolt which deals max Health Damage to self, and deals 700 Storm Damage to all Wizards.
strategy: Hit with Ice or Myth, he takes +10% from each, and avoid Storm (45%) and Fire (50% resist).
He keeps his minion count up to your team size, re-summoning at the end of any round he is short. Do not chase minions, they self-destruct on their own.
Every third round his Clockwork Spanners sacrifice themselves to blast all wizards for 700 each (Death, Fire, or Storm). Shield the whole team before Rounds 3, 6, 9 and so on.
Build blades and a Feint across the safe rounds, then land one big Ice or Myth hit.
Heal up right after each triple-Spanner detonation window.
cheatNotes: Join late and he hits you with Winter Moon.
At the end of any round he has fewer minions than wizards, he summons another Clockwork Spanner.
Every three rounds his Spanners self-destruct to blast the team, pre-shield on Rounds 3, 6, 9 and so on.
The Death Spanner casts Poison for 700 Death to all wizards while killing itself.
The Fire Spanner casts Immolate for 700 Fire to all wizards while killing itself.
The Storm Spanner casts Insane Bolt for 700 Storm to all wizards while killing itself.

name: Leadite (Portal of Peril, Tier 3)
locations: Portal of Peril: Sinbad & the Iron Sultan Gauntlet (Portal of Peril) - Metallos (Portal of Peril)
cheats: "Lead is heat resistant!" - If Leadite is damaged by a Damage over Time Spell, then he will interrupt-cast the following Spells in order:
Chromatic Blast
Frenzy
Iron Curse (Tier 5)
"My half-life means you're half done!" - When Leadite is defeated, at the end of the Round, two Uraniumites will join the duel.
"Ha! Even my half-lives have half-lives." - When a Uraniumite is defeated, at the end of the Round, two Protactimites will join the duel.
strategy: Avoid DoT spells on Leadite. A damage-over-time makes him chain Chromatic Blast, Frenzy, and Iron Curse, so use direct-damage hits.
Killing Leadite spawns two Uraniumites, and each Uraniumite killed spawns two Protactimites, so plan an AoE-heavy finish to clear the cascade rather than leaving stragglers.
Blade up and hit with Life (he takes +30%, resists Death 66% and everything else 36%).
Save AoE for the add waves after he dies.
cheatNotes: Hitting him with a DoT makes him chain Chromatic Blast, Frenzy, and Iron Curse, so stick to direct damage.
Defeating him spawns two Uraniumites, so be ready with AoE for the next wave.
Each Uraniumite you kill spawns two Protactimites, so clear them together with AoE to avoid an endless cascade.

name: Lion Spectral Guardian
cheats: This boss will cast a 0-pip Orthrus on a player who is marked and does not cast a charm within 2 rounds of being marked.
strategy: Hit with Storm or Death — he takes +30% from both — and avoid his 40% Myth resist.
If you get marked, cast a charm (any blade counts) within two rounds or he drops a free 0-pip Orthrus on you.
Since you are blading every round anyway, the mark never fires — just keep laying charms while you build up.
Stack blades and finish with one boosted Storm or Death hit; at 4,225 health it does not take much.
cheatNotes: Being marked means you must cast a charm within two rounds or take a free Orthrus. A single blade satisfies it, so keep laying blades and it never triggers.

name: Loon Knight (Challenge)
cheats: Loon Knight

[Round] "Become Khonsu's Avatars!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Loon Knight will Polymorph each Luna-Tick Minion into one of the following forms for 99 Rounds, depending on the number of Wizards present:
2 or fewer: Old Quack (Challenge)
3 or more: Old Quack (Challenge), Quentin Quail (Challenge) and Drake Lockley (Challenge).
[Round] "I am the Beak of Vengeance!" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, Loon Knight will cast Shatterhorn (Moon) on a random Wizard.

[Polymorph] "The Moon Phases..." - If there are no Luna-Tick Minions present, Loon Knight will Polymorph into one of the above forms, retaining all cheats listed above while gaining all cheats of that Polymorph.
[Polymorph] "QUACK!" - Loon Knight will Polymorph into another one of the above forms, gaining the cheats of both that form and the previously Polymorphed form.
Old Quack

[Charm] "That's not the real you!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Helpful Charm, or a multi-blade Spell, such as Elemental Blade, Old Quack will cast an Ice version of Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
If a multi-target Blade Spell is cast, such as Bladestorm, Old Quack will cast an all enemies version of Enfeeble.
A wizard casting a blade-type spell that is not of their original school will also trigger this cheat.
Spells with secondary effects, such as Savage Paw, will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis will not prevent the removal of Blades.
Quentin Quail
"Let me see. Oh, yes! That's the ticket!" - If a Wizard casts one of the following hanging effects, Quentin will cheat-cast one of the following Spells:
Secondary effects, such as the Blade on Sound of Musicology, will trigger this cheat.
[Friend][Charm] Single Target Helpful Charm - Weakness
[All Friends (Squared)][Charm] Helpful Charm on All Allies - Mass Weakness
[Enemy][Curse] Single Target Harmful Charm - Balanceblade
Starfall will also trigger this cheat.
[All Enemies (Squared)][Curse] Harmful Charm on All Enemies - Bladestorm (+25%)
[Friend][Ward] Single Target Helpful Ward - Hex
[All Friends (Squared)][Ward] Helpful Ward on All Allies - Mass Hex
[Enemy][Jinx] Single Target Harmful Ward - Tower Shield (Balance)
Attenuate will also trigger this cheat.
[All Enemies (Squared)][Jinx] Harmful Ward on All Enemies - Legion Shield
Drake Lockley

[Blade][Trap][Aura][Global] "Time for the old one, two PUNCH!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Pierce Charm, Trap, Aura, or Global, Drake will cast a Storm version of Duck Savage that deals 800 Storm Damage on the receiver.
Multi-Target Spells will result in the Duck Savage being cast on the caster.
Secondary effects will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Loon Knight polymorphs its Luna-Tick minions into duck forms (Old Quack, and with 3+ wizards also Quentin Quail and Drake Lockley), each punishing a different action; the fight is about avoiding their triggers.
Watch your buffs: Old Quack Enfeebles off any universal helpful charm, multi-blade, or off-school blade, and Aegis will not save the blade; use plain single-school blades or bank buffs.
Around Quentin Quail, every hanging effect you cast (charm, ward, jinx) draws a counter-spell such as Weakness, Balanceblade, Hex, or shields; keep setup minimal.
Around Drake Lockley, any damage or pierce charm, trap, aura, or Global earns an 800 Storm Duck Savage on the caster; avoid those categories.
Every third Round Loon Knight hits a random wizard with Shatterhorn (strips shields), and when minions run out it Polymorphs itself into the duck forms, keeping their cheats.
Set up as little as possible, kill the duck minions to force Loon Knight to polymorph, then burst it down.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 it turns its minions into duck forms; the number depends on party size, and each form has its own punish cheat.
Every third Round it Shatterhorns a random wizard, stripping shields; keep a heal ready rather than relying on shields.
When no minions remain it Polymorphs itself into a duck form, keeping all its cheats plus that form's; killing minions does not remove the danger.
It can Polymorph again into a second form, stacking both forms' cheats; expect layered punishes late.
Old Quack Enfeebles off any universal helpful charm or multi-blade you cast; use single-school blades, and Aegis will not protect them.
A multi-target blade makes it Enfeeble your whole team's blades; avoid Bladestorm-type buffs.
Casting a blade outside your own school also triggers the Enfeeble.
Blades that are secondary effects, like Savage Paw's, do not trigger the Enfeeble.
Aegis does not stop this blade removal, so do not rely on it here.
Quentin Quail answers each hanging effect you cast with a matching counter-spell; keep your charms and wards to a minimum.
Even secondary hanging effects, like a spell's built-in blade, trigger Quentin's counters.
A single helpful charm earns a Weakness on the receiver.
A team helpful charm earns a Mass Weakness.
A single harmful charm on an enemy gives it a Balanceblade.
Starfall also triggers the harmful-charm counter.
A harmful charm on all enemies gives them a +25% Bladestorm.
A single helpful ward earns a Hex.
A team helpful ward earns a Mass Hex.
A single harmful ward on an enemy gives it a Tower Shield.
Attenuate also triggers the harmful-ward counter.
A harmful ward on all enemies gives them Legion Shield.
Drake Lockley hits the caster with an 800 Storm Duck Savage for any damage or pierce charm, trap, aura, or Global; avoid those categories.
Multi-target versions land the Duck Savage on the caster too.
Secondary effects also trigger Drake's Duck Savage, so check side effects before casting.

name: Loon Knight (Standard)
cheats: Loon Knight

3 or more: Old Quack (Standard), Quentin Quail (Standard) and Drake Lockley (Standard).

strategy: Team fight (24,500 health). His Luna-Tick minions are polymorphed into ducks (Old Quack, Quentin Quail, Drake Lockley) that punish setup, so coordinate who casts what.
Hit with Fire (+30%); he resists Ice, Myth, and Death 70% and everything else 40%.
Blading is dangerous: universal, off-school, or multi-blades draw Ice Enfeeble from Old Quack, and Aegis will not save the blade. Use single mono-school blades of your own school.

Charms, wards, and traps trigger Quentin's counters (Weakness, Hex, Balanceblade, shields), and any damage charm, trap, aura, or global draws Drake's 800 Storm Duck Savage. Keep setup minimal and clean.
Every third round he Shatterhorns a random wizard, so keep shields refreshed.
Funnel safe mono-school blades to the Fire hitter and burst the boss and ducks together.

name: Loother Ray
cheats: [Late] "Another combatant? Bring it on!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Loother Ray will cast Bad Juju (Shadow) at the Wizard.
[Minion] "Come out and play!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Loother Ray casts a Shadow Spell that summons a random combination of two of the following: Fangs Guards and/or Fangs Assaulters.
[Feint] "You'll pay for that!" - If a Feint is placed on Loother Ray, he will cast a Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) that removes all unprotected Traps and Prisms on himself.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can prevent the Feint from being removed.
[Heal] "I saw that coming" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cast Mass Infection (-65%).
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
[Steal] "Experience the strength of street knowledge" - He will randomly cheat-cast Steal Health.
spellNotes: Casts a multi-target version of Poison (45+450).
strategy: Protect your Feint with Indemnity. Any Feint on Loother triggers a Cleanse Ward that removes all unprotected Traps and Prisms, so Indemnity keeps your key trap alive.
Or sidestep the cheat entirely: Mass Feint (+75%) and the Balance version of Feint do not trigger the trap wipe, so those are safe to stack.
Do not heal mid-setup. Healing (including pet heals) makes him cast Mass Infection (-65%), gutting your next attack, so heal before you commit or bring Cleanse Charm.
He is Death with 30% Death resist and takes 15% boosted Life, so use a Life hitter or Life prisms; watch his random Steal Health and his multi-target Poison (45+450).
Come in on time. A late wizard eats Shadow Bad Juju, and at Round 1 he summons two Fangs (Guards and/or Assaulters), so plan to clear minions with AoE.
Set up behind Indemnity or Mass Feint plus blades, avoid healing into the Infection, then AoE Loother and his Fangs down together.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard is hit with Shadow Bad Juju, so be present when the fight starts.
On Round 1 he summons two minions in a random mix of Fangs Guards and Fangs Assaulters; expect an AoE to clear them.
Placing a Feint on him triggers a Cleanse Ward that strips all unprotected Traps and Prisms. Protect your Feint with Indemnity.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger the trap-removal cheat, so it is a safe trap source.
The Balance version of Feint also does not trigger the cheat.
Indemnity on your Feint stops it from being removed by the Cleanse Ward.
Any heal you cast makes him drop Mass Infection (-65%) on your team, so avoid healing right before your hit.
Pet heal procs also set off the Mass Infection cheat, so mind your pet.
He randomly cheat-casts Steal Health to top himself off, so expect some self-healing.

name: Lord Barghest (Challenge)

stats.resist: 25% to all schools45% to all schools65% to all schools

cheats: [Trap] "You think to trap the hunters?" - Triggers the first time a Negative Ward is applied to Lord Barghest or his minion(s).

"Your traps become our tools!" - Triggers the second time a Negative Ward is applied to Lord Barghest or his minion(s). When this cheat is triggered, all traps will be removed from Lord Barghest and his minion(s).
"*growl* I'll get you yet!" - After attacking Lord Barghest while he has a trap, Lord Barghest's minion will say this to indicate that it is safe to trap again.
Cheat Cycle

[Protected Damage over Time] "Now the real hunt begins!" - At the beginning of the first round, Lord Barghest will cast a protected Damage Over Time effect that deals 17,775 damage over 60 rounds, followed by a protected Damage Over Time effects that deals 118 damage over 60 rounds.

[Polymorph] "The coin flips in your favor, for now." - At the end of the third round, Lord Barghest will polymorph into his human form and cast a protected Damage Over Time effect that deals 17,775 damage over 60 rounds, followed by a protected Damage Over Time effects that deals 118 damage over 60 rounds.
Fire dispels can prevent the above cheats.
"*snarl*" - If a wizard casts a Positive Charm, this cheat will be triggered one round early.
In his human form, Lord Barghest's school changes to Ice and his boost/resist values are different.
[Polymorph] "And now... the coin flips to my favor." - At the end of the sixth round, Lord Barghest will polymorph back into his wolf form and cast a Sun version of Incindiate.
"*hrumph*" - If a wizard casts a Positive Charm, this cheat will be triggered one round early.
Minion Cheats
At the beginning of the first round and every other round afterwards, Lord Barghest's minion will cheat-cast one of the following cheats.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - Lord Barghest's minion will cast a special version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% damage charms to all allies.
[Shield] "Brace yourself!" - Lord Barghest's minion will cast Mass Tower Shield.
[Heal] "You will not fall!" - Lord Barghest's minion will cast a version of Pigsie that heals all allies and applies 3 heal over times for 4 rounds.
[Afterlife] "*whimper*" - After being defeated at the end of the turn, Lord Barghest's minion will revive itself for 100% of its max HP and will leave combat.
Helpful Cheats
In the earlier stages of the dungeon, three clues are spread throughout the area, each of which corresponds to one NPC. If the clue is found, that means the corresponding NPC is innocent.
Tranett = Silver Necklace
Tawni = Lock of Hair
Ignacio = Red Cloth
Dimiti = Strange Footprints

If the correct NPC is chosen, the following helpful cheats may activate. Each incorrect choice typically removes one helpful cheat, but helpful cheats frequently won't trigger even if no incorrect choices were made. More testing is needed to determine what causes cheats not to trigger.
[All Friends (Squared)][Aura] "Here to help, wizard!" - At the end of the first round, a special version of Quicken that lasts 6 rounds will be applied to all wizards.
[All Friends (Squared)][Manipulation] "Now, Barghest, your end comes." - At the end of the first round, a special version of Guardian Spirit that restores 100% hp will be applied to all wizards.
[All Friends (Squared)][Aura] "Don't hit me!" - Before Lord Barghest cheat-casts Incindiate, a special version of Fortify that reduces incoming damage by -135% will be applied to all wizards.
[All Friends (Squared)][Aura] "Now, you face Ignacio. Pity you." - After Lord Barghest casts Incindiate for the first time, the 6-round Quicken will be re-applied to all wizards.
strategy: This Challenge boss layers huge protected damage-over-time (17,775 over 60 Rounds) at Round 1, again at the end of Round 3 when he polymorphs to human (Ice) form, and returns to wolf form with an Incindiate at the end of Round 6. Fire dispels can prevent the DoT cheats, so bring them.

Trap carefully: the first negative ward is a warning, but the second time you trap him or his minion he removes all traps. Wait for the minion's '*growl* I'll get you yet' after you attack to know it is safe to trap again.
Positive Charms trigger the polymorph cheats a Round early, so coordinate blading with the phase timing.
The minion re-buffs him every other Round with a double +30% Bladestorm, Mass Tower, and a Pigsie group heal, and revives once at full Health before leaving, so deal with it but expect it back once.

strategy: Darkmoor team fight (25000). He swaps forms: wolf form is Fire (hit him with Ice, +30%; he resists Fire 64%), human form is Ice (hit with Fire; he resists Ice 76%). Match your hitter's school to his current form.
Fire Dispels prevent his big protected Damage-over-Time cheats (17775 over 60 rounds) at round 1 and when he polymorphs. Bring them, and heal the team if a DoT lands.
Go easy on positive charms: every round a wizard casts one, his polymorph and Incindiate cheats trigger a round earlier. Blade minimally, right before the hit.
Traps: the first negative ward on him is fine, but the second removes ALL traps from him and his minion. Keep to a single trap at a time and re-trap only after his minion signals it is safe.
His minion buffs, heals, and shields the enemy side and revives once. Clear or out-pace it, and remember the dungeon clue NPCs grant your team helpful auras if chosen correctly.
Funnel blades to the hitter and burst him within a single form, healing through the DoTs.
cheatNotes: The first trap on him or his minion is allowed, so placing one is fine.
The second trap triggers removal of ALL traps from him and his minion. Never stack more than one trap at a time.
His minion says a line to signal it is safe to trap again after you have hit. Wait for it before re-trapping.
Round 1 he lays a protected DoT dealing 17775 over 60 rounds. A Fire Dispel beforehand prevents it; keep the team healed if it lands.
At the end of round 3 he becomes human (Ice school) and stacks another 17775/60 DoT. Switch your hitter to Fire and Dispel if you can.
Fire Dispels stop these DoT cheats. Carry a couple for the team.
Each positive charm a wizard casts pulls his next form or DoT cheat one round earlier. Minimize blades and cast them just before hitting.
In human form he is Ice: hit him with Fire; he now resists Ice 76% and boosts 30% Fire.
At the end of round 7 he reverts to wolf and casts a Sun Incindiate. Have the team's incoming-damage buffs or heals ready.
Every round with a positive charm keeps pulling the Incindiate and form cheats earlier. Keep charm use lean.
His minion acts every other round from round 1 with one of the support cheats below. Account for enemy buffs and heals.
The minion can Bladestorm the enemy side with two +30% charms, so his hits ramp. Re-Feint and keep healing.
The minion can Mass Tower Shield the enemy side. Over-stack blades or pierce through it.
The minion can Pigsie-heal his allies with heal-over-times. Burst the boss faster than the minion heals.
The minion revives once at full HP then leaves at end of turn. Do not over-invest in killing it.
The dungeon hides three clues; finding one clears an NPC as innocent. Correct choices unlock the helpful cheats below.
Choosing correctly can grant your team a 6-round Quicken at the end of round 1, a big tempo boost.
You may also get a Guardian Spirit that restores 100% HP to all wizards. Rely on it only if your choices were right.
Before his Incindiate you may get a Fortify cutting incoming damage -135%, which blunts that hit. Time your defense around it.
After the first Incindiate the 6-round Quicken can re-apply. Use the tempo to set up your kill.

name: Lord Nightshade (Challenge)
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness is unacceptable" - If a Wizard is late after the second Round, Boss will cast a Moon version of Ship of Fools dealing 100,000 Moon Damage. If a Damage cap is active, the Damage will be set to the maximum.
[Global] Equalize Cycle
"Your doom approaches" - At the start of Round 1, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 250.
"Closer" - At the start of Round 3, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 500.
"and closer..." - At the start of Round 5, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 1,000.
"It's almost here" - At the start of Round 7, the Boss will cast a Death version Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 2,000.
"Your doom is NOW!" - At the start of Round 10, the Boss will cast Doom and Gloom (Moon).

"You wish to hasten your doom?" - If a Wizard changes the Global, Lord Nightshade will immediately replace the Equalize, resetting the Damage cap to 250. The cycle will continue normally, and future Equalize casts will set the Damage cap to the proper limit for that round.
[Round] End of Round Cycle

"Kneel! For I am the harbinger of your demise!" - At the end of every Round, Lord Nightshade will interrupt with a Moon version of Deer Knight dealing 100,000 Moon Damage. If a Damage cap is active, the Damage will be set to the maximum.
If a Wizard changes the global, the Deer Knight will deal 250 damage due to the Equalize being temporarily reset.
strategy: Come in on Round 1. A late Wizard eats a Ship of Fools (capped, but massive).
His Equalize caps all damage: 250 (R1), 500 (R3), 1,000 (R5), 2,000 (R7). This limits your hits too, so plan to strike many times, not once.
Every Round he Deer Knights the team for the current cap, so the longer the fight the harder those hits. Race the clock.
Optional exploit: replacing his global temporarily resets the Equalize to 250, dropping his next Deer Knight to 250, though it also caps your own hits at 250 that Round.
Kill before Round 10, when he casts Doom and Gloom to shut off healing.
cheatNotes: A late Wizard after Round 2 eats a Moon Ship of Fools; with a damage cap active it deals the cap instead. Come in on Round 1.
Round 1 Equalize sets the damage cap to 250 for both sides.
Round 3 Equalize raises the cap to 500.
Round 5 Equalize raises the cap to 1,000.
Round 7 Equalize raises the cap to 2,000.
Round 10 he casts Doom and Gloom (Moon) to cut healing.
Changing the global makes him immediately re-cast Equalize, resetting the cap to 250, then the cycle continues normally.
Every Round he interrupts with a Moon Deer Knight set to the current damage cap.
If you change the global, that Deer Knight only deals 250 because the Equalize is briefly reset to the 250 cap.

name: Lord Nightshade (Rematch)

The -85% Tower Shields can each be dispelled with Melt.

strategy: Join by round 2; arriving later triggers a 100,000 Moon Ship of Fools that wipes you.
He runs an escalating Equalize cycle capping your damage: 250, then 500, 1,000, 2,000, then Doom and Gloom, and finally a 100,000 Deer Knight every round that ends the fight. You have a hard timer.
With only 4,500 health, set up blades during the low-cap rounds and burst him during the 2,000-cap window (a few 2,000 hits clear him) before the doom phase.
Do not change the Global; it triggers a -70% Tower Shield plus two -85% Tower Shields on him (the -85% ones need Melt) and does not even remove the Equalize.
Stack your setup early, then unload the moment the cap allows a kill.
cheatNotes: Join after round 2 and he casts a 100,000 Moon Ship of Fools, a guaranteed wipe. Be on time.
He caps your damage at 250 early, so do not try to burst yet; just set up.
The cap rises to 500.
Then to 1,000.
Then to 2,000; this is your kill window, and a couple of hits clear his 4,500 health.
After that he casts Doom and Gloom, cutting healing, so be past the kill by now.
Changing the Global makes him drop a -70% Tower Shield and two -85% Tower Shields on himself without removing the Equalize. Do not touch the Global.
Those -85% Tower Shields can each be removed with Melt if you are forced to deal with them.
His finisher is a 100,000 Moon Deer Knight every round, so kill him before this phase or you die.

strategy: The guide runs a four-wizard team: three supports and one hitter (its example uses a Storm hitter).
The key trick is Nightbringer — the hitter casts it to nullify the bubble/Equalize cheat, which removes the damage cap and makes the fight simple.
Round 1: supports cast Elemental Blade, Feint, and Storm blade onto the hitter; the hitter opens with Frenzy.
Round 2: supports stack Sharpened Elemental Blade, Potent Feint, and Sharpened Storm blade; the hitter casts the Nightbringer 3-pip hit to strip the bubble.
Round 3: supports add TC/alternative Elemental Blade, Feint, and Storm blade; the hitter drops a 2-pip bubble and lands the big uncapped hit.
With enough stats the hitter clears it in one blow — the guide reports ~45,000 damage at 175% damage and 38% pierce even through a -40% weakness.
Do not otherwise replace the Global on your own; only Nightbringer is the intended way to clear the cap, and enter on time so the Equalize is up before you join.
cheatNotes: Join late after round 2 and he casts a 100,000 Moon Ship of Fools — enter on time so the Equalize cap is protecting you.
Round 1 he caps all damage at 250. Your hits are limited too, so plan a many-round grind, not a burst.
Round 3 the cap rises to 500 — your per-hit ceiling climbs with it.
Round 5 the cap is 1,000.
Round 7 the cap is 2,000 — and his end-of-round Deer Knight now hits for 2,000, so aim to finish around here.
Round 10 he casts Doom & Gloom (Moon), cutting healing — try to win before this.
Don't replace the Global; changing it makes him recast Equalize.
Every round he interrupt-casts a Deer Knight (capped to the current damage cap) — that steady chip is your damage-race clock, so keep healed each round.

Every round he interrupts with a Deer Knight that would deal 100,000 Moon, limited to the current cap. Early rounds you only take about 500, but by Round 7 you take up to 4,000 per round, so kill him before the cap climbs too high.

Blade hard in the low-cap rounds, then land your hits on rounds 5-7 when the cap (2,000-4,000) lets your attacks connect for real damage, racing him down before the Deer Knight outpaces your healing. His Deathblade is Aegis-protected.

name: Lord Ombra
locations: Khrysalis: The Hive - Night Orb Chamber
cheats: "Your power belongs to me!" - At the start of every fourth Round, Lord Ombra will interrupt-cast a Balance version of Steal Pips at the Wizard with the most Pips, which steals three Pips.
Sometimes this cheat will not damage the Wizard.
He will not cast this cheat if all Wizards have 4 Pips (in any combination of Power Pips and regular Pips) or less.
He does cast the normal version of Mana Burn, though not as the cheat.
strategy: Team fight at 17,365 health: hitter, supports, and a healer. Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+30% incoming); never attack into his 80% Balance resist.
He steals 3 pips from whoever has the most at the start of every 4th round. Plan the big hit for round 3 or 4 so the hitter spends pips before the steal.
If you cannot hit in time, keep pips low: he skips the steal entirely when every wizard has 4 or fewer pips.
Supports Feint and blade the hitter; clear the Arachna Gladiator with AoE or focus the boss.
Funnel to one big hit to grind the 17,365 health.
cheatNotes: Every 4th round he steals 3 pips from the wizard holding the most. Time your hit before it, or keep the hitter's pips spent.
Sometimes the pip-steal deals no damage, just takes pips.
If every wizard has 4 or fewer pips, he skips the steal entirely, a way to deny it.
He also casts a normal Mana Burn separately from the cheat, so expect occasional pip loss anyway.

name: Lord Very-Tall
cheats: "Power Pips? What a puny plan!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Lord Very-Tall will cast a Sun School version of Power Play that applies -100% Power Pip gain, and then a Fire School version of Mana Burn on all Wizards, removing all their pips.
This Global gets recast whenever the Global is changed.
strategy: On Round 1 he strips all your pips and applies -100% power pip gain, so you build only 1 plain pip per round all fight. Plan a long, efficient game.
Do not try to overwrite his Power Play global, he re-casts it whenever it is changed. Work under it instead.
Use low-pip attacks, blades, and traps that fit a starved pip economy, and let a pet or wand hits help while pips are scarce.
Deal with the Emu Rider and Punk Wallaby minions with a cheap AoE as your pips allow.
Stack blades over several rounds and land your biggest affordable hit once you have saved enough plain pips.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he casts Power Play (-100% power pip gain) and Mana Burns away all your pips, you will gain only 1 plain pip per round all fight, so plan a slow, efficient build.
Overwriting his global just makes him recast it, leave it up and play around the pip starvation.

name: Loremaster (The Atheneum)
cheats: The Font of Strength Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +50%) on the Loremaster every three Rounds out of turn, starting on the first Round.
strategy: Bring a team; the guide says this is not recommended to solo and is best farmed with other players. Loremaster has 7,000 health and 80% Balance resist, so hit with Myth, Life, or Death (17% boost) and never attack into Balance without a prism.

Kill order matters: the Font of Strength feeds the boss a +50% Sun Dragonblade every three rounds, and the Font of Life spams Dryad, Rebirth, Satyr, and Sprite heals. Take out those support fonts before you try to burst the boss.
Carry infections or Doom and Gloom to shut down the Font of Life's healing, and keep a shield up against the Font of Weakness's Mana Burn, Plague, and Virulent Plague.
Stack blades and Feints on your hitter over 2-3 setup rounds, then land one big Myth/Life/Death AoE so the fonts die alongside the boss.
Time your kill window so the boss has not just received a fresh +50% Dragonblade; the Font of Strength recasts it on rounds 1, 4, 7, etc., so burst on a round when the blade has not just refreshed.
cheatNotes: Every third round (rounds 1, 4, 7...) the Font of Strength stacks a +50% Sun Dragonblade on Loremaster for free, so an unattended boss hits progressively harder. Kill or Beguile the Font of Strength early, or burst the boss down before the blades pile up.

name: Lost Maiden (Challenge)
cheats: "You dare raise arms to me?" - At the end of each Round, if a Wizard has a helpful Charm or helpful Aura Spell, Lost Maiden will cast Ra (Tier 2b) on all Wizards.
strategy: Hit Myth, Life, or Death - all three take boosted damage. She resists 80% Balance, so never attack into Balance.
Never let a helpful charm or aura sit on your team at the end of a round. If one is present, she casts Ra on all wizards.
Blade and hit on the same turn so no charm ever lingers into the end-of-round check.
Set up with traps and Feints instead of leaving blades hanging, since traps are not part of the punish here.
With only 4,325 health, one clean same-turn blade-and-hit takes her and the two minions down before Ra becomes a problem.
cheatNotes: At the end of each round, if any wizard holds a helpful charm or helpful aura, she hits the whole team with Ra (Tier 2b). Blade and attack on the same turn so nothing lingers.
