"This is my show! Mine!" - At the start of every fourth Round, Lucia (Tier 6) will cast a Star version of Steal Charm that steals all positive Charms, and Steal Ward (Star, -All) that steals all positive Wards on all Wizards.
"I've no time for such frivolity!" - If a Feint is placed on Lucia (Tier 6), she will cast Cleanse Ward (Star, -All) on herself, then proceed with the cheat below.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
"The star is only as good as her supporting cast!" - If a Trap is placed on Lucia (Tier 6), she will cast a Star Spell that gives the following Charms to all allies.
+30% Damage, +20% Accuracy, +10% Armor Piercing, +30% Healing.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Fire Damage.
strategy: Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+15%); avoid Fire, Ice, and Storm (45% resist).
Every 4th round she steals ALL your positive charms and wards — set up and land your hit before round 4 (or right after a steal on round 5+).

Don't trap her: any trap buffs all her minions (+30% damage, +20% accuracy, +10% pierce, +30% healing), and Feint additionally makes her Cleanse Ward herself. Protect a Feint with Indemnity if you must, otherwise rely on blades.
She's low health (5,045) — blade up fast and burst her before the round-4 steal.
AoE to hit her and the Clockwork minions together.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get Mana Burned — join on time.
She summons a Clockworker at the start, and more minions if more than two wizards are present.
Every 4th round she strips all your positive charms and wards — hit before round 4.
Put a Feint on her and she Cleanse Wards herself, then runs the trap-buff cheat below — use Indemnity or skip Feint.
Indemnity avoids that Feint cheat.
Any trap on her buffs all her minions with damage, accuracy, pierce, and healing charms — don't trap her.

name: Ludus the Beastmaster
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Ludus the Beastmaster will cheat-cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
"My beasts will defend me!" - Every 4 Rounds, he will cast Attack Sea Lion which summons an Attack Sea Lion.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Feint is placed on Ludus, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward which swaps the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Melt can be used to Dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
"I alone can unleash these beasts!" - If a Trap is placed on an Attack Sea Lion, he will interrupt-cast Cleanse Ward.
Vaporize can be used to Dispel this cheat.
"Ha! We shake off your measly charms!" - Whenever a single target negative Charm (excluding Dispel) is placed on a Minion, he will cast Cleanse Charm.
Secondary Spell debuffs will trigger this cheat.
"No one hurts my beasts but me!" - If a Wizard attacks one of the Attack Sea Lions, he will interrupt-cast Berserk.
"My beast! Must... Avenge... GRR!" - If an Attack Sea Lion is defeated, he will cast an enhanced version of Berserk (+45%/+35%).
spellNotes: His Beastmaster's Eagle Pet casts Bladestorm.
strategy: He takes +15% from Storm and resists Myth and Balance 80%. Hit with Storm.
Do not single-Feint him: he Swap Wards it back onto you. Use Mass Feint (+75%), which does not trigger the swap, or protect Feint with Indemnity. Melt also dispels the swap cheat.
Leave his Attack Sea Lions alone. Attacking one makes him Berserk, and killing one gives him an enhanced Berserk (+45%/+35%). Trapping or charming the lions triggers Cleanse Ward or Cleanse Charm.
He summons a new Sea Lion every 4 rounds, so focus all damage on Ludus and ignore the pets.
Blade up, Mass Feint or Indemnity-Feint, and hit Ludus directly with Storm. Join on time; late arrival eats a Mana Burn.
cheatNotes: Late arrival eats a Mana Burn. Zone in on time.
He summons an Attack Sea Lion every 4 rounds. Ignore them and keep hitting Ludus.
A Feint on Ludus gets Swap Warded back onto you. Use Mass Feint (+75%) instead, which does not trigger it.
Melt dispels this swap cheat if you want to Feint normally.
Indemnity also prevents the Feint swap.
Mass Feint (+75%) is safe; it never triggers the swap.
Trapping a Sea Lion makes him Cleanse Ward. Do not bother trapping the pets.
Vaporize dispels that Cleanse Ward cheat.
A single-target negative charm on a minion makes him Cleanse Charm. Keep debuffs off the pets.
Even secondary-effect debuffs trigger the Cleanse Charm. Aim clean single hits at Ludus.
Attacking a Sea Lion makes him Berserk (a damage buff). Never hit the pets.
Killing a Sea Lion gives him an enhanced Berserk (+45%/+35%). Leave the lions alive and burst Ludus.

name: Luminous Prophet
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Field of Reeds
cheats: "Death rules here. I doubt even Taweret could shield you." - At the beginning of the first Round, Luminous Prophet casts a negative version of Acrimony on all Wizards that turns all incoming Healing into Damage for 99 Rounds.
Luminous Prophet will not recast the Aura if replaced.
"WE ARE DEATH!" - At the beginning of the first Round and every 6th Round afterwards, Luminous Prophet will cast a version of Anubis that deals 666 Death Damage to all Wizards.
"Allow us to help you..." - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm, Luminous Prophet will cast a Shadow version of Blessing that restores 666 Health to all Wizards.
"You will destroy yourself." - If Forgotten Soul is defeated, it will cast Regenerate (Tier 5a) on Luminous Prophet.
spellNotes: Casts an all enemies version of Shadow Trap.
strategy: Round 1 he casts a negative Acrimony that turns all your incoming healing into damage. Replace it immediately with your own aura; he will not recast it, so one aura fixes healing for the whole fight.
Until Acrimony is gone, do not heal and do not cast a positive Charm; his 'Blessing' becomes 666 damage under Acrimony.
Hit with Life (+30% incoming); avoid Death (69% resist).
He hits the team with Anubis (666 Death) on round 1 and every 6th round. Shield for those.
If a Forgotten Soul appears, do not kill it unless you are bursting the Prophet down right after, since it Regenerates him.
Blade, Feint, and burst the 16,945 health while watching the 6-round Anubis timer.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he lays a negative Acrimony aura: all your incoming healing becomes damage for 99 rounds. Replace it with your own aura fast.
He will not recast Acrimony once you replace it, so a single replacement aura permanently fixes your healing.
He casts Anubis (666 Death to all wizards) on round 1 and every 6th round after. Shield up on those rounds.
Casting a positive Charm makes him heal the team 666, but under his Acrimony that is 666 damage. Do not trigger it until Acrimony is gone.
If a Forgotten Soul is defeated, it Regenerates the Prophet. Do not kill the soul unless you are finishing him right after.

name: Luska Charmbeak
cheats: Luska will remove traps that are placed on him.
Pet spells will not trigger this cheat.
Only the first trap will be removed from spells that apply more than one trap.
Traps with Indemnity enchantment will prevent any traps from being removed.
AoE traps such as Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.

"Only attack if you have been inked!" - At the beginning of each Round, Luska will "Ink" the Wizard in the first spot (Sun spot), which applies a Smoke Mantle. All other Wizards will receive a Feint (this does not apply the +30% trap on himself).
"Still fighting? Have some more ink!" - When he loses 25% of his health (12,690 health remaining), he will also Ink the second Wizard (Eye spot) each Round.
When he loses 50% of his health (8,460 health remaining), he will also Ink the third Wizard (Star spot)each Round.
He will never Ink the fourth Wizard (Moon spot).

"If you can still see me, don't look at me!" - If a non-inked Wizard casts a single-target spell at Luska (e.g. attacks, traps, negative charms), he will interrupt to remove all wards from himself and all Wizards, then cast either Skeletal Dragon (at the Wizard who cast the spell), dealing 1,200 Death damage or Power Link (at the Wizard who cast the spell), dealing 2,100 Fire damage and 600 healing for 3 rounds.
Only Wizards who have been Inked can cast single-target spells at Luska without retaliation. He will still remove traps from himself.
Any Wizard can cast attack all-enemy spells without triggering this cheat.
strategy: Waterworks team fight (16,920 health). Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+20%), never Balance (80% resist).
He Inks the first-spot wizard each round with a Smoke Mantle, that inked wizard is your designated hitter and can single-target Luska safely. Everyone else attacks only with all-enemy AoEs.
If a non-inked wizard single-targets Luska, he strips all wards from everyone and answers with Skeletal Dragon (1,200 Death) or Power Link (2,100 Fire). Keep non-hitters on AoE and support only.
Traps get removed unless they are AoE traps (Windstorm) or Indemnity-protected, so use Windstorm feints and funnel blades to the inked hitter.
As he drops past 75% and 50% he Inks the second and third spots too, so concentrate blades on the inked hitter and burst him down before the whole team is debuffed.
cheatNotes: He removes single traps placed on him, use AoE traps or Indemnity to make them stick.
Pet-cast traps do not trigger the removal.
On multi-trap spells he only strips the first trap.
Indemnity-protected traps are safe from his removal.
AoE traps like Windstorm are never removed, this is the way to trap him.
Each round he Inks the first-spot wizard with a Smoke Mantle (your safe hitter) and puts a Feint on every other wizard.
At 75% health (12,690) he also Inks the second-spot wizard every round.
At 50% (8,460) he also Inks the third-spot wizard.
He never Inks the fourth (Moon) spot, that wizard can never safely single-target him.
A non-inked wizard single-targeting him strips all wards from everyone and triggers Skeletal Dragon (1,200 Death) or Power Link (2,100 Fire), only the inked hitter should single-target Luska.
Only Inked wizards can single-target him safely, and he still removes traps even from them.
All-enemy AoE attacks never trigger the retaliation, non-hitters should stick to AoE.

name: Luther Hexdragon
cheats: [Charm] "I summon the blades from the boulders" - At the beginning of every Round, Luther Hexdragon will cast one of the following Spells:
Catch of the Day (Tier 5b)
Dragonlance
Storm Beetle
Stormblade
[Curse] Casting a Death Damage Spell that removes helpful Charms on Luther Hexdragon will disable his Blade cheat.
[Afterlife] If Luther Hexdragon is defeated while his Blade cheat is still active, then he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "As long as I hold a blade, I cannot fall" - Whenever Luther Hexdragon revives, Wizards will be given the following Spells:
[Scarecrow (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)][Skeletal Dragon (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
strategy: Know the trap before you swing: he blades himself every Round, and if you kill him while that blade cheat is active he revives at full Health.
Disable it by hitting him with a Death damage spell that removes helpful charms. Scarecrow is ideal because it strips his blades and shuts off the revive.
Strip his blades first, then finish him. If he does revive, he hands you Scarecrow and Skeletal Dragon, so use them to clear blades before the next kill.
Attack with Death or Ice, his boosted schools; avoid Fire and Myth (his resist schools).
At only 4,940 Health, a stripped and bladed hit ends it fast once the blade cheat is disabled.
cheatNotes: Every Round he blades himself with one of Catch of the Day, Dragonlance, Storm Beetle, or Stormblade, and these blades keep him alive through his revive cheat.
Hitting him with a Death damage spell that removes helpful charms, like Scarecrow, disables his blade cheat, which is the key to killing him for good.
If you defeat him while the blade cheat is still active, he revives at full Health, so always strip his blades first.
Whenever he revives he hands every wizard the tools to fix it: Scarecrow and Skeletal Dragon.

name: Lyon Lorestriker
locations: Wysteria: Pegasus Place - Tower of the Helephant
cheats: Minion Cycle:
"Welcome to the party!" - At the end of every Round, he will summon one Lyon Lorestriker (Minion) starting with 1,200 Health.

”Friends, stay a bit longer!” - If Lyon has 50% or less of his total health remaining, the new minions summoned start with 2,000 Health and an extra Power Pip. Any existing minions will not be replaced until they are defeated.
Rarely, Lyon Lorestriker may summon multiple minions, or not summon a minion at all.
"Tsk tsk. Another friend for you!" - Lyon Lorestriker will summon an additional minion during the same round if the Wizard casts any following spells:
If a Wizard uses a Heal Over Time spell (e.g. Regenerate, Sprite), he will cast a Life Spell that removes the HoT on the Wizard who received it.
Pet spells do not trigger this cheat.
Availing Hands does not trigger this cheat.
If a Trap or Prism is placed on him, he will use Cleanse Ward.
Multi-target Traps or Prisms such as Windstorm and Mass Storm Prism do not activate this cheat.
This cheat can be avoided using Indemnity or Vaporize.
"Crowds just warm my icy heart!" - Once Lyon Lorestriker acquires three minions, he will cast a version of Blizzard dealing 750-790 Ice Damage at the beginning of every Round.
"We need a bigger crowd!" - If all Minions are defeated, he will once again summon them at the end of each Round.
strategy: Do not try to clear the minions. Lyon resummons one every round, and once three are up he Blizzards the whole team every round. Race the boss instead.
Keep his health above 50% until you can burst; below 50% his new minions come in beefier (2000 health, extra pip).

Avoid triggering extra summons: HoTs make him remove the heal and summon another minion, and single traps or prisms make him Cleanse Ward. Use Indemnity or Vaporize, or multi-target versions like Windstorm and Mass Storm Prism.
Blade up and hit Lyon with Myth or Life (+35%, resists Storm 80%); AoE trims minions while you damage him.
Burst him down before the Blizzard chip adds up.
cheatNotes: He summons a 1200-health minion at the end of every round, so the board refills; focus him, not the adds.
Below 50% health his new minions arrive with 2000 health and an extra power pip, so try to burst him past 50%.
Any HoT you cast gets removed and makes him summon an extra minion, so heal with direct heals instead.
A single trap or prism on him triggers Cleanse Ward; use Indemnity or a multi-target version.
Multi-target traps or prisms like Windstorm and Mass Storm Prism do not trigger the Cleanse Ward.
Indemnity or Vaporize also avoids the Cleanse Ward.
Once he has three minions he Blizzards the team (750-790 Ice) every round, so do not let the board fill; AoE it down.
Clearing all minions just makes him resummon them each round, so race the boss rather than the adds.

name: MacDeath (Tier 1)
cheats: "May a choir of understudies sing thee to my rest" - MacDeath will cast a Moon Spell to summon a MacDeath Understudy. MacLife Understudy, or MacStorm Understudy.
A second Minion will be summoned after the first one arrives, if 3 Wizards are present.
MacDeath will summon a replacement for any Minion that is defeated.
"Wizards die a thousand times before their death!" - If a Damage over Time Spell is cast on MacDeath, then he will immediately Shift it back on the casting Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Life — he takes +20% from it — and avoid his 50% Death resist.
Do not waste turns picking off the Understudy minions. He resummons any that die and adds more with 3 wizards, so an AoE that clears everything at once is far better.
Never cast a DoT on MacDeath — he immediately Shifts it back onto you. Use direct hits only.
He is stunable if you need to buy a turn. Blade up, then finish with one Life AoE to take out MacDeath and his minions together (2,400 health).
cheatNotes: He summons an Understudy minion (Death, Life, or Storm). Do not over-invest in killing minions.
With 3 wizards he summons a second minion, so expect a fuller enemy board.
Any minion you defeat gets replaced the next turn, which is why an AoE finish beats killing them one at a time.
A DoT cast on him bounces straight back onto you. Skip DoTs and use direct damage.

name: MacDeath (Tier 2)
cheats: "Late arrivals will not be seated" - MacDeath (Tier 2) will cast Feint (+75%), Curse (+25%), and Hex (+35%) on any Wizard who joins the battle after the second turn.
"May a choir of understudies sing thee to thy rest" - MacDeath (Tier 2) will cast a Moon Spell to summon one of the following Minions:
MacDeath Understudy (Tier 2), MacLife Understudy (Tier 2), MacStorm Understudy (Tier 2),
"Out, out, brief candle!" - MacDeath (Tier 2) casts Doom and Gloom (-75%), if a Global or Healing Spell is cast.
strategy: Hit with Life; he takes +50% and resists Death 80%.
He is stunable and beguilable; stun or beguile to control him.
He summons a random understudy minion; clear it or focus MacDeath.
Do not cast Globals or Healing spells; either makes him drop a -75% Doom and Gloom that cripples your own healing.
Blade up, avoid Globals and heals, then finish with one Life hit.
cheatNotes: Joining after turn two gets Feint, Curse, and Hex stacked on you; enter on time.
He summons a random understudy minion; deal with it or race him down.
Casting a Global or a heal makes him cast a -75% Doom and Gloom, gutting your own healing; avoid both.

name: MacDeath (Tier 3)
cheats: "May a choir of understudies sing thee to thy rest!" - MacDeath will cast a Moon Spell to summon one of the following Minions:
MacDeath Understudy (Tier 3), MacLife Understudy (Tier 3), MacStorm Understudy (Tier 3).
"This is your moment in the spotlight!" - If a Feint is cast on MacDeath, a Minion will cast an Ice Spell to move the Trap to themselves. This includes Mass Feint.
"Late arrivals will not be seated!" - Casts Feint (+75%), Curse (+25%), and Curse +35% on the late Wizard.
"Out, out, brief candle!" - MacDeath will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%) if a Healing or Global Spell is cast.
This will trigger if MacLife Understudy casts Giant Spider.
"We few, we happy few, we band of beguileds" - MacDeath will cast Beguile on a Wizard.
strategy: Death boss (4560) that is stunable and beguilable. Hit with Life (+50%); never Death (80% resist).
Do not Feint him. A minion shifts the trap onto itself (Mass Feint too). Use blades and hit instead.
Avoid healing and Global spells; he answers with Doom and Gloom (-75% healing). Top off before you engage, not mid-fight.
Expect a summoned Understudy minion and a Beguile on a wizard. Beguile or stun him back to control the tempo.
Stack blades and land a Life hit. He is low health.
cheatNotes: He summons a Death, Life, or Storm Understudy minion. Be ready to clear it or AoE.
Feint him (including Mass Feint) and a minion shifts the trap onto itself. Skip Feint here.
Late joiners eat Feint and two Curses. Arrive on time.
Cast a heal or a Global and he drops Doom and Gloom (-75% healing) on everyone. Top off before the fight, not during.
He Beguiles a random wizard. Stun or beguile him first, and do not rely on a beguiled wizard for the kill.

name: MacDeath (Tier 4)
cheats: [Late] "Late arrivals may not be seated!" - If a Wizard joins the Duel after the first Round, then MacDeath will interrupt-cast a +75%/+20% version of Feint, a +25% version of Curse, and a +35% version of Hex on the late Wizard.
[Aura] "To be or not to be? To be obviously" - At the beginning of the first Round, MacDeath will interrupt-cast a -100% version of Fortify.

[Damage][Steal] "Et tu, Wizard?" - If a Wizard casts a Damage or Steal Spell on MacDeath, then he will interrupt-cast a version of Fortify that is 10% less effective than his current Aura. This cheat will repeat every time that MacDeath is damaged until his Aura reduces incoming Damage by -10%.
[Minion] "May a choir of understudies sing thee to my rest" - MacDeath will interrupt-cast a Moon Spell that summons a MacStorm, MacLife, or MacDeath Understudy.
[Global][Healing] "Out, out, brief candle!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing or Global Spell, then MacDeath will interrupt-cast a -75% version of Doom and Gloom.
[Manipulation] "We few, we happy few, we band of beguileds" - MacDeath will interrupt-cast Beguile on a Wizard.
[Feint] "This is your moment in the spotlight!" - If a Wizard casts Feint on MacDeath, then one of his Understudies will interrupt-cast Swap Ward on him.
[Damage over Time] "Wizards die a thousand times before their death!" - If a Damage over Time effect is cast on MacDeath, then he will interrupt-cast Shift on the caster.
Spells that apply Damage over Time effects on all enemies may result in the effect being shifted to a Wizard that was not the caster.
strategy: Enter on time. A late join eats a stacked Feint, Curse, and Hex.
Attack with Life; he resists Death 80% and takes +35% from Life. He is stunable, so a stun can skip one of his interrupts.

His round-1 near -100% Fortify makes early hits barely dent him. Each Damage or Steal you land drops that Fortify 10%, so chip him repeatedly (multi-hit spells are ideal) until it is down to -10%, then land your real hit.
Do not cast Globals or Heals (he answers with -75% Doom and Gloom), do not Feint him (an understudy Swap Wards it off), and do not use DoTs (he Shifts them back onto the caster).
Clear his summoned MacStorm, MacLife, and MacDeath understudies with AoE and expect a Beguile on a random wizard.
cheatNotes: Late arrivals eat a stacked +75%/+20% Feint, +25% Curse, and +35% Hex. Enter at the start.
Round 1 he puts up a -100% Fortify, so early hits barely dent him.
Each time you Damage or Steal on him, his Fortify drops 10% in strength. Keep hitting (multi-hit spells help) to peel it down to -10% before your kill shot.
He summons a MacStorm, MacLife, or MacDeath Understudy via a Moon spell. Clear them with AoE.
Casting any Heal or Global triggers a -75% Doom and Gloom. Skip globals and healing.
He Beguiles a random wizard, so expect to lose a turn to mind control.
Feinting MacDeath makes an understudy Swap Ward it off him. Blade your hitter instead of trapping him.
A DoT on MacDeath gets Shifted back onto the caster. Avoid damage-over-time.
An all-enemy DoT can Shift onto a wizard who was not the caster.

name: MacDeath (Tier 5)
cheats: "Late arrivals may not be seated!" - MacDeath will cast Feint (+75%), Curse (+25%), and Hex (+35%) on the late Wizard.

"May a choir of understudies sing thee to thy rest" - MacDeath will cast a Moon Spell to summon one of the following Minions: MacDeath Understudy (Tier 5), MacLife Understudy (Tier 5), MacStorm Understudy (Tier 5).
"To be or not to be? To be obviously" - Casts a -100% version of Fortify with a 99 Round timer.
"Et tu, Wizard?" - Recasts previous Aura to be by 10% lower. Cheat repeats until Boss has a -10% Incoming Aura which expires after 4 Rounds.
"Out, out brief candle" - Boss cheat-casts a -75% Doom and Gloom, if a Wizard casts any Healing Spell.
"We few, we happy few, we band of beguileds" - Boss will cast Beguile on a Wizard.
strategy: He is Death, so hit with Life. His Aegis-protected Deathblade means his hits stay strong, so shield.

He hides behind a -100% Fortify that halves incoming damage, but his own Et tu cheat keeps recasting the aura 10% weaker each time until it is only -10% and expires after four rounds. Bait it down or strip the aura, then burst while it is weak.
Do not heal unless needed: any heal triggers a -75% Doom and Gloom that cuts your own healing anyway.
He can Beguile a wizard and summons Understudy minions, so clear adds and watch for a beguiled ally hitting you.
Stack Life blades and a Feint while his Fortify decays, then hit once it is down to -10%.
cheatNotes: Show up on time; a late wizard gets Feint (+75%), Curse (+25%), and Hex (+35%) dumped on them.
He summons a MacDeath, MacLife, or MacStorm Understudy minion. Clear it or AoE through.
He puts a -100% Fortify on himself, halving your damage. Wait for it to decay or strip it before hitting.
His Et tu cheat recasts that aura 10% weaker each round until it is just -10% and expires after four rounds. Bait it down, then burst.
Casting any heal triggers a -75% Doom and Gloom that cripples healing, so avoid healing when you can.
He can Beguile one of your wizards into attacking the team, so be ready to shield.

name: MacDeath (Tier 6)
cheats: "Late arrivals will not be seated!" - Casts Feint (+75%), Curse (+25%), and Hex (+35%) on the late Wizard.
"May a choir of understudies sing thee to thy rest" - MacDeath will cast a Moon Spell to summon one of the following Minions.
MacDeath Understudy (Tier 6), MacLife Understudy (Tier 6), MacStorm Understudy (Tier 6).
Although the speech will continue, Understudies will cease to be summoned after a certain number, probably based on the number of Wizards in the duel. "Needs confirmation."
Hiring a Henchman also appears to trigger additional Understudies to be summoned.
"This is your moment in the spotlight!" - If a Feint is cast on MacDeath, a Minion will cast an Ice Spell to move the Trap to themselves. This includes Mass Feint
"To be or not to be? To be obviously" - Casts a -100% version of Fortify.
"Et tu, Wizard?" - Recasts previous Aura to be 10% lower after a Damage Spell is cast on him. Cheat repeats until Boss has a -10% Incoming Aura.
Weapon-Casts trigger this cheat also.
Health Steal Damage Spells do not trigger this cheat (ex: Ghoul).
"Wizards die a thousand times before their death!" - Boss will Shift any Damage Tick placed on him to the caster of the DoT.
"Out, out, brief candle!" - MacDeath (Tier 6) casts Doom and Gloom (-75%) if a Global or Healing Spell is cast.
Pet Spells will activate this cheat.
If a Strangle then a Global Spell is cast, Wizards can Heal without Doom and Gloom (-75%).
"We few, we happy few, we band of beguileds" - Boss will cast Beguile on a Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Feint (+70%/+30%) that affects all enemies.
strategy: He sits under a -100% Fortify aura, and every damage spell you land (wands included) knocks that aura down 10% toward -10% — so chip him with cheap hits and wands first, then unload your big hit once the aura is nearly gone.
Don't Feint him (a minion just takes the trap) and don't DoT him (he Shifts the tick back to you); Ghoul-type health-steal hits don't erode the aura, so use plain direct damage to lower it.
Cast Globals and Heals sparingly — either triggers Doom & Gloom -75%. If you must heal, Strangle him first, then cast a Global to heal safely.
Attack with any non-Death school (he resists Death 80%); he is stunable, so stun to skip a turn.
Enter on time — late wizards eat a +75% Feint, +25% Curse, and +35% Hex.
cheatNotes: Late wizards eat a +75% Feint, +25% Curse, and +35% Hex — enter on time or your first hit takes big bonus damage.
Feint on MacDeath (including Mass Feint) gets moved onto a minion — don't rely on Feint; kill the understudies or trap around them.
He carries a -100% Fortify aura, so your hits land reduced until you erode it (see next cheat).
Each damage spell you land drops his incoming-damage aura by 10%, down to -10% — chip him with cheap hits and wands first, then unload once the aura is nearly gone.
Weapon (wand) casts also erode the aura, so wand him each setup round to grind it down.
Health-steal hits like Ghoul do NOT erode the aura, so use plain damage for that job.
Any DoT tick on him gets Shifted back onto you — skip damage-over-time here.
Casting a Global or a Heal triggers Doom & Gloom -75%, gutting your healing — cast heals sparingly.
If you Strangle him and then cast a Global, you can heal without the -75% Doom & Gloom — the workaround for emergency heals.
He'll Beguile a wizard, turning them against the team for a turn — keep that in mind in group play.

name: Mad Prophet

stats.resist: 13% to all schools43% to all schools

cheats: [Curse] "Your weakness is our salvation!" - At the beginning of every Round, Mad Prophet will interrupt-cast Virulent Plague.
[Healing] If Mad Prophet is defeated while his Virulent Plague cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "As long as you are weak, we shall survive!" - At the end of the 3rd Round, and after Mad Prophet revives, he will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Evil Snowman (Tier 2b)]
[Ice] "You defy prophecy!" - If Evil Snowman (Tier 2b) is used, Mad Prophet will remove all negative Charms and stop his Virulent Plague cheat.
strategy: Solo-friendly (7,270 health). Do not kill him while his Virulent Plague cheat is running, or he revives at full health.
Every round he interrupt-casts Virulent Plague, so you always carry a fresh weakness. Plan around reduced hit damage.
Survive to end of round 3: he hands every wizard an Evil Snowman card. Cast it to strip your weaknesses and permanently shut off his Plague cheat.
Only after the Snowman disables the cheat should you go for the kill, or he revives.
Hit with Ice or Myth (+9%) and avoid Storm and Life (43% resist). Blade up during the first three rounds, then finish once the cheat is off.
cheatNotes: Every round he interrupt-casts Virulent Plague, stacking a weakness on you.
Kill him while that Plague cheat is still running and he revives at full health.
At end of round 3, and after any revive, he hands every wizard an Evil Snowman card.
Cast that Evil Snowman: it strips your weaknesses and permanently shuts off his Plague cheat, so cast it before you go for the kill.

name: Malificus Mangemort

stats.resist: 15% to all schools35% to all schools55% to all schools

cheats: [Spell] "Oh, fie, it seems I dropped something." - At the beginning of every fourth Round, Malificus Mangemort will give all Wizards one of the following Cards at random.
[Attenuate (Storm)][Balanceblade (Death)][Brace (Storm)][King Artorius (Myth, 2,090)][Sprite (30+15,000)][Woolly Mammoth (1,750)]
"What? How did you get that? Did I do that?" - Malificus Mangemort will say this if Woolly Mammoth (1,750) is cast.
strategy: Settle in for a long fight. Malificus has 40,000 Health, 104% boost, and no boost school for you, so this is a team grind: one hitter, supports blade and Feint, healer sustains.
Use his free cards. Every fourth round he hands all wizards a random card, and one of them is Woolly Mammoth (1,750), a strong free hit you should save and fire on your setup.
Do not waste the good rolls. When you get Woolly Mammoth or Sprite (30+15,000) from his handout, hold them for a big turn rather than dumping them.
He is Death with 55% Life resist and only 15% Myth resist (his lightest), so a Myth hitter or Myth prisms cut deepest; avoid attacking into his heavier resists.
Clear Fleshless Chattel with AoE so the minion does not chip your team while you build the long stack on your hitter.
Feint and blade the hitter over several rounds, then unload one large Myth AoE, supplementing with his own gifted Woolly Mammoth cards to speed the 40k down.
cheatNotes: At the start of every fourth round he gives all wizards one random card from his list; some are strong free hits worth banking.
He remarks on it when a wizard casts the Woolly Mammoth (1,750) card he handed out, which is a solid free attack to save for your setup.

name: Malistaire Drake (Challenge)
cheats: Malistaire Drake:
"Late? Have a taste of the Dragon Titan's power!" - If a Wizard is late, he'll interrupt with Fire Dragon.
"As long as Sylvia and I are together- Nothing can stop us!" - When Malistaire is together with Sylvia at the beginning of every Round, Virulent Plague (Shadow, -100%) is cast on all Wizards.
"Charms? I'll take those" - If a Wizard casts a Charm, Malistaire will cast Jinn's Larceny.

"Can you fathom the pain of our parting?" - When Malistaire is alone without Sylvia, he will remove 25% max Health from himself and use an AoE version of Mana Burn (Shadow) that also removes 1 Shadow Pip.
"NOOOOO!!!!!" When Sylvia Drake is defeated, any remaining harmful Charms will be removed from Wizards in the fight.
Sylvia Drake:
My love...I can't hold on...any longer" - After a few Rounds, Sylvia will cast a version of Dark Pact that removes 100% of her max Health and provides a +100% Balanceblade on Malistaire Drake.
This currently is bugged and will do nothing.
strategy: Team fight (24k): run 3 supports and 1 hitter. Enter on time - late wizards eat a Fire Dragon.
Kill Sylvia Drake first. While she is alive Malistaire Virulent Plagues (-100%) your whole team every round, so a support must Cleanse Charm the hitter each round to keep the damage up.

While waiting on Sylvia, trap-stack Malistaire: supports layer Feint, Potent Feint, and school traps onto him each round rather than blading (charms just get stolen by Jinn's Larceny). The hitter builds pips, then casts Frenzy to buff.
Do not over-blade. Any charm you cast triggers Jinn's Larceny to steal it, so lean on traps for the setup instead.
Once Sylvia is dead all harmful charms clear off your team, but Malistaire then self-strips 25% health and AoE Mana Burns you (removing a shadow pip), so rebuild pips before the finish.

His incoming healing is cut 45%. With the trap stack up, the hitter single-targets Malistaire (resists all only 20%); aim for high pierce - the guide hit ~30k on a Storm wizard with 174% damage and 45% pierce, ending the fight in 2-4 turns.
cheatNotes: Late wizards eat a Fire Dragon — join on time.
While Sylvia is alive he Virulent Plagues (-100%) the whole team every round — kill her to stop it.
Cast a charm and he steals it with Jinn's Larceny.
Once he's alone he removes 25% of his own health and AoE Mana Burns you, stripping a shadow pip.
When Sylvia dies, all harmful charms are cleared off your team.
Sylvia eventually Dark Pacts herself to death to blade him, but it's bugged and does nothing.

name: Malistaire Drake (Khrysalis)

cheats: "Curious... a vulnerability" - At the beginning of every fourth Round, Malistaire will cheat-cast a Death Prism.
At the end of Round 11 the duel ends automatically.
strategy: You have a hard time limit: the duel ends automatically at the end of Round 11, so kill Malistaire (4,800 health) before then.
Every fourth round he cheat-casts a Death Prism. If you hit with Death, expect the prism to convert your damage, so plan around it or use another school.
Blade up quickly and land a hit well before Round 11; his natural attack is modest, so prioritize speed over heavy setup.
cheatNotes: Every fourth round he cheat-casts a Death Prism. If you hit with Death, expect the prism to convert your damage; plan around it or use another school.
The duel auto-ends at the end of Round 11. Burst his 4,800 health down before the timer, and do not over-setup.

This summon can occur if Malistaire already has a Sylvia Minion.

strategy: The core loop is splitting the couple: while Malistaire and Sylvia are together, everyone eats a -100% Virulent Plague every Round, and Sylvia keeps him alive.
Sylvia revives or heals Malistaire when he is hit while she lives, and can even revive him if he dies first, so bring her down or force her Sacrifice before committing to killing Malistaire.
When Malistaire is alone he strips 25% of his own Health and Mana Burns a wizard, and after Sylvia is gone he Cleanse-Charms your weaknesses off, so time your Feints for after that cleanse.
Avoid casting Charms or Auras carelessly; each one lets Malistaire steal two blades from you.
He takes 50% more from Life, so a Life hitter is ideal; funnel blades, clear Sylvia, then burst Malistaire in one big hit.
Note he can re-summon Sylvia with a Moon spell even if a Sylvia minion is already out, so keep infection and pressure up to blunt the plagues.

He casts a Moon spell to summon Sylvia Drake.

Cast a Charm or Aura and he steals two blades from you.

This is currently bugged and may never trigger or fail on trigger.
If Malistaire is hit while Sylvia is alive, she Sacrifices to heal him, and this can even revive him if he dies first.
