name: Malistaire the Undying
cheats: Malistaire the Undying will defeat himself the Round after all his Minions are defeated.
spellNotes: This Boss uses a +75%/+25% version of Feint.
strategy: This is the whole fight: the round after all three minions are defeated, Malistaire defeats himself. Do not waste damage on Malistaire.
Kill Grim Calaca, Xipe Flayed One, and Youala Night Drinker in the same round. Have supports funnel blades and traps onto the hitter, then drop one AoE to wipe all three minions together.
Supports blade the hitter and can Feint or trap the minions; the healer keeps the team up and watches for his +75% Feint landing on players.
He is immune to Globals, so skip Doom and Gloom style plays. He is stunable if you need to stop a big hit while you set up the AoE.
Once the AoE clears every minion, hold position — Malistaire kills himself next round.
cheatNotes: This is the win condition: the round after every minion is dead, Malistaire defeats himself. Put all damage on the three minions, not on Malistaire, and clear them in one AoE.

name: Malistaire the Undying (Shadow)
cheats: [Manipulation] "I cannot be stopped" - At the beginning of the first Round, Malistaire will cheat-cast a Shadow Mega Taunt on the first Wizard.
This cheat restores Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) to 100 health and spawns as Haunted Malistaire after being defeated for the first time.

[Blade] [Trap] "What was yours is mine" - If a Wizard casts a universal Damage Blade (e.g. Balanceblade) or universal positive Damage Ward, he will cast Steal Charm or Steal Ward on the Wizard that received the effect.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade or Ward Spell, such as Bladestorm or Legion Shield is used, only the caster will be affected.
Secondary Spell effects, such as the Ward from Abominable Weaver, will not trigger this cheat.
If a universal Trap (e.g. Feint) is placed on Malistaire, he will cast Cleanse Ward on himself.
If a universal negative Charm (e.g. Black Mantle) is placed on Malistaire, he will cast Cleanse Charm on himself.
Pet Spells, Life Feint, and secondary Spells other than negative Damage Charms will trigger this cheat.
All Enemy Charms and Wards, (e.g. Mass Feint), and negative Health Charms (e.g Infection) will not trigger this cheat.
If Elemental Defuse or Spirit Defuse is used, Malistaire the Undying will use a version of Cleanse Charm to remove all three Dispel Charms.
[Weakness] "I grow stronger every moment" - If any Weakness is placed on Malistaire, he will cast a version of Empower that deals 300 Death Damage on himself and gains 3 pips.
Weakness-based pet spells, roshambo spells (even if no Weakness is placed on Malistaire), and secondary Spell effects (e.g. Efreet) will trigger this cheat.
[Damage] "Can you face my stare?" - If a Wizard uses a single target Damage Spell against Malistaire, he will cast Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip) on the Wizard after the attack.
The Secondary Interrupt Cycle after attacking Malistaire before the last round can remove the Mantle from the Wizard.
Interrupt Cycles Malistaire's first form revolves around two cheat cycles; a "Primary Interrupt Cycle" and a "Secondary Interrupt Cycle." Both cycles overlap each other throughout the battle.

[Moon] "Fear the power of Dragonspyre" - At the end of the third Round in this cycle, Malistaire will polymorph into a Black Dragon and attack a random Wizard, dealing 535 Moon Damage and destroying any Aura effects.
[Sun] "A gift from Tartarus" - At the end of the fourth Round, he will cast a Sun version of Deadly Minotaur on a random Wizard.
[Star] "My curse upon you!" - At the end of the fifth Round, he will cast an Aura that grants him +20%, +15%, and +10% outgoing Death Damage and three +15% Power Pip Chance for five Rounds.
"Its power shakes your spells apart!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round, Malistaire's Skeletal Dragon will remove all Charms and Wards from each Wizard.
Wizards using Empowerment or Adapt will gain one Pip or Power Pip, respectively.

"I touch Shadow!" - Haunted Malistaire, a Shadow Boss, will spawn within the battle after Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) has been defeated or reaches below 1,000 health. The Boss interrupts a Shadow Spell at the beginning of the round that sets its max health to 48,000 and restores 99,999 Health, followed by a Secondary Interrupt Cycle.
This cheat occurs once, even if Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is not defeated.

If Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is not defeated and only Haunted Malistaire is defeated first, he will revive himself with 100 Health, retain his stats from Haunted Malistaire, and delay his Shadow version of Primary Interrupt Cycle by one round.
Malistaire's Health after healing can be decreased to 25,050 if a Treasure Card Doom and Gloom is present when Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is defeated.
A regular Doom and Gloom will not reduce his health below 48,000.
If Malistaire the Undying (Shadow) is not defeated, the health can be reduced further using negative Healing Charms, such as Mass Infection.
Haunted Malistaire retains all the above cheats except his Primary Interrupt Cycle, along with the one cheat below.
"Curse you beast! Do not defy me!" - Malistaire's Skeletal Dragon will randomly place a +100% universal Trap on Malistaire and his allies.
Interrupt Cycles The "Primary Interrupt Cycle" is replaced with a Shadow version. The "Secondary Interrupt Cycle" stays the same.
"I call upon Shadow" - At the end of the first Round, he will cast Shadowblade on himself.
"I call upon Shadow" - At the end of the second Round, he will cast Shadowspear on himself.
"I call upon Shadow" - At the end of the third Round, he will cast an All Enemies version of Shadow Trap.
"I will show you the Shadow" - At the end of the fourth Round, he will cast Shadow Steal, which deals 1,600 Shadow Damage, and converts 100% of the Damage to Health.
"Its power shakes your spells apart!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, Malistaire's Skeletal Dragon will remove all Charms and Wards from each Wizard.
spellNotes: Even though the Spell text does not indicate this, it has been observed that Skeletal Dragon (666+333) does Damage to All Enemies.
Lord of Night (625) applies a -40% Healing Ward to the target.
strategy: The Castle Darkmoor Graveyard finale, the most famous team dungeon in the game. Normally a 4-person run: a hitter, supports feeding blades and Feints, and a healer riding out the interrupt cycles. It is a two-phase fight (24,000, then Haunted Malistaire heals to 48,000). (An expert solo clear is possible; the guide shows a Life solo route that leans on Sanctuary and Guardian Spirit to survive.)
First form boosts Life (+40%), so skip prisms and Shrike here and just stack Life blades and hit with Forest Lord. Ice, Myth, and Balance also work (+20%); avoid Death and Storm.

Don't cast universal blades or wards on yourself (he Steals them), don't Feint or place negative charms on him (he Cleanses them), and never place a Weakness (any Weakness Empowers him +3 pips). Use school-specific setup and Indemnity on Feints. He opens with a Mega Taunt, so plan the forced targeting.

Play around the Skeletal Dragon strip. It removes all charms and wards on Round 5 in phase one (Round 4 in phase two), so run Regenerate the turn before the dragon, then 3 blades and a hit the round after.
At below 1,000 HP he becomes Haunted Malistaire, heals to 48,000, and now resists Life. Switch to traps, a Life prism, and Shrike, and use King Artorius cards for shadow pips.

In phase two watch the end-of-Round-4 Shadow Steal (1,600 damage, heals him) and the dragon strip; keep your health up or carry Guardian Spirit, and hold a Treasure-Card Doom and Gloom active on the kill to cap his revive at 25,050.
cheatNotes: He Mega Taunts the first wizard, forcing your hits onto that player; keep them healed and shielded.
He doesn't stay dead; the first defeat revives him as Haunted Malistaire, so plan for a second phase.
Casting a universal blade or positive ward on yourself makes him Steal it; use school-specific Life blades or protect them.
Pet-cast blades and wards also get Stolen, so watch your pet's may-casts.
On a multi-target blade or ward, only the caster's copy is stolen; anyone else keeps theirs.
Wards that are secondary effects, like Abominable Weaver's, do not trigger the steal.
Placing a universal Trap like Feint makes him Cleanse it off; protect Feints with Indemnity.
A universal negative Charm makes him Cleanse it; avoid loose weaknesses and mantles.
Pet spells, Life Feint, and secondary charm effects also get Cleansed.
All-enemy charms and wards like Mass Feint, and Infections, do not trigger the cleanse; those are the safe ways to debuff him.
Elemental or Spirit Defuse makes him Cleanse all three dispels at once; single Dispels are more reliable.
Any Weakness on him makes him Empower for 300 Death and +3 pips; never Weaken him.
Weakness pets, roshambo spells, and secondary weakness effects (Efreet) also feed his Empower; keep all weakness sources off him.
A single-target hit earns you a -55% Black Mantle afterward; keep an accuracy buffer or Cleanse it.
His own later interrupt cycle can strip that Mantle back off you before the last round.
At the end of the third cycle Round he becomes a Black Dragon and hits a random wizard for 535 Moon, destroying auras; re-apply auras after.
At the end of the fourth Round he Deadly Minotaurs a random wizard; keep your health up.
At the end of the fifth Round he auras up his own Death damage (+20/+15/+10%) and pip chance for five Rounds; burst him before it pays off.
At the start of Round 5 his Skeletal Dragon removes all charms and wards from every wizard; land your setup and hit the round after, not before.
If you carry Empowerment or Adapt, you gain a pip or power pip when he strips you; a small consolation.
When his first form dies, or drops below 1,000, Haunted Malistaire spawns at 48,000 and full heal; save burst for this phase.
This revive happens only once, even if you didn't fully defeat the first form.
If you kill only Haunted first, he revives at 100 health with Haunted's stats and delays his shadow cycle a Round; a small opening.
A Treasure-Card Doom and Gloom present at his defeat cuts the revive to 25,050; bring one to ease phase two.
A regular Doom and Gloom won't push the revive below 48,000; the treasure version is what shrinks it.
Infections and other negative healing charms can lower his revived health further if the first form is still up.
Haunted keeps all the above cheats except the primary interrupt cycle.
His Skeletal Dragon randomly drops a +100% universal trap on Malistaire and allies; that's a free trap you can hit into.
In phase two he Shadowblades himself at the end of Round 1.
He Shadowspears himself at the end of Round 2, stacking damage.
At the end of Round 3 he drops an all-enemies Shadow Trap on you.
At the end of Round 4 he casts Shadow Steal for 1,600 and heals the full amount; keep your health up and time your kill before it lands.
At the start of Round 4 in phase two his dragon again strips all charms and wards; land your finisher before it.

name: Malus (Challenge)

stats.resist: 18% to all schools38% to all schools58% to all schools

cheats: [Damage] "Their hatred is a tonic. It fuels me." - At the beginning of the first Round, if Malus has any Minions, he will cast a Death version of Meteor Strike that deals 100% max Health Damage to all Wizards. Max Health Damage ignores all Resist.
[Round] "Embrace your hatred!" - At the beginning of every Round, Malus will cast one of the following Spells:
Black Mantle (-55%, 3 Pip)
Choke
Efreet (1,000) - Targets Wizard with the lowest amount of Health
Monster Mash (1,170) - Targets Wizard with the highest amount of Health
Muzzle
Tower Shield (Sun -90%)
Dispels can prevent these cheats.
[Aura] "Let your rage drive you. Strike me down!" - Randomly, at the end of a Round, Malus will cast a "Flames of Hatred" Aura on himself.
[Manipulation] "Heroism is vanity. FIGHT ME!" - If a Wizard targets Malus while his "Flames of Hatred" Aura is active, he will cast a 0 Damage Star version of Supernova on himself, removing the Aura.
[Afterlife] "Good. AGAIN!" - If Malus is defeated while the "Flames of Hatred" Aura is active, Malus will heal himself for 50% Health, and applies a +200% outgoing Damage Aura on himself.
strategy: Team fight at 62,930 Health. Survive the Round-1 Meteor Strike first: it deals 100% max Health ignoring resist, so open with team Absorbs. Tower and resist stacking does not help since it ignores resist.
Keep Malus dispelled. Dispels stop his Round-start Choke, Efreet, Monster Mash, and shield cheats.
Hit Storm. He resists Storm only 18% versus 38% to 58% elsewhere, so prism if off-school.
Watch his Flames of Hatred aura. Do not target or hit him while it is up, or your action is wasted, and killing him then triggers a 50% heal plus a +200% damage aura.
Strip Flames of Hatred, or wait it out, before the killing blow, then have the hitter unload a fully-stacked Storm hit.
Keep the lowest-Health member healed since Efreet targets them, and clear the three Ghost minions along the way.
cheatNotes: If minions are up at the start of Round 1, he Meteor Strikes everyone for 100% of max Health, ignoring all resist. Stack Absorbs or a big shield before it lands.
Every Round he opens with one of the spells below. Dispels prevent these casts.
Efreet (1,000) hits whoever has the lowest Health, so keep your low-Health member topped up.
Monster Mash (1,170) hits whoever has the highest Health.
Dispels on Malus prevent his Round-start cheat casts. Keep him dispelled.
Randomly he puts a Flames of Hatred aura on himself.
If you target Malus while Flames of Hatred is up, he Supernovas it off himself for 0 damage, so targeting him is wasted that Round.
Never kill Malus while Flames of Hatred is active. He heals 50% and gains a +200% outgoing damage aura. Strip the aura first, then finish him.

name: Malus (Standard)

stats.resist: 59% to all schools29% to all schools

cheats: "Their hatred is a tonic. It fuels me." - At the beginning of the first Round, if Malus has any Minions, he will cast a Death version of Meteor Strike that deals 100% max Health Damage to all Wizards. Max Health Damage ignores all Resist.
"Embrace your hatred!" - At the beginning of every Round, Malus will cast one of the following Spells:
Black Mantle (-55%, 3 Pip)
Choke
This version of Efreet leaves a -95% Curse [Curse] on the target and Malus will always target the Wizard with the lowest amount of Health [Health].
This version of Monster Mash beguiles the target and Malus will always target the Wizard with the highest amount of Health [Health].
Muzzle
Tower Shield (Sun -90%)
Dispels can prevent some of these cheats.
"Let your rage drive you. Strike me down!" - Randomly, at the end of a Round, Malus will cast a "Flames of Hatred" Aura on himself.
"Heroism is vanity. FIGHT ME!" - If a Wizard targets Malus while his "Flames of Hatred" Aura is active, he will cast a 0 Damage Star version of Supernova on himself, removing the Aura.
"Good. AGAIN!" - If Malus is defeated while the "Flames of Hatred" Aura is active, Malus will heal himself for 50% Health (75,000 Health), and applies a +200% outgoing Damage Aura on himself.
strategy: Wallaru finale (150000). Full team: a hitter, supports funneling blades, and a healer/utility on cheats. Hit with anything but Fire (he resists Fire 59%, everything else 29%).

Watch his Flames of Hatred aura. Attacking him while it is up makes him self-Supernova and negate the moment, and KILLING him while it is active revives him with 75000 health and a +200% damage aura. Clear the aura with a cheap throwaway hit first, then commit real damage.
Round 1, if he has Ghost minions out, he Meteor Strikes the team for 100% max health (ignores resist). Bring absorbs, heals, or Dispels for the opening.

Every round he casts a control spell (Black Mantle, Choke, Efreet with a -95% Curse on the lowest-health wizard, Monster Mash beguiling the highest-health wizard, Muzzle, or a Tower Shield). Dispels prevent several, and the utility wizard should cleanse the -95% Curse.
Supports over-stack blades and Feints on the hitter between his cheats; the healer keeps the team above his Meteor and Nova math.
Once Flames of Hatred is cleared and blades are stacked, the hitter lands one massive hit. Never take the kill shot while that aura is up.
cheatNotes: If he has minions on round 1 he Meteor Strikes the whole team for 100% max health, ignoring resist. Open with absorbs and heals, or Dispel it.
Every round he casts one control spell from the list below. Keep Dispels and cleanses ready.
One option is a -55% Black Mantle (accuracy down). Carry accuracy so your hitter does not fizzle the kill.
He may Choke a wizard. Plan around a lost turn.
His Efreet leaves a -95% Curse and always targets the lowest-health wizard. Cleanse it off the hitter before they swing.
His Monster Mash beguiles the highest-health wizard. Keep your hitter from being the top-health target that turn.
He may Muzzle a wizard. Do not rely on one wizard holding the only answer.
He may drop a -90% Sun Tower Shield on himself. Over-stack blades or pierce through it.
Dispels can stop several of these cheats. The utility wizard should keep them up.
Randomly he gives himself a Flames of Hatred aura. Do NOT attack or kill him while it is up.
Hitting him while Flames of Hatred is active makes him self-Supernova and wipe the aura for no damage. Use a cheap throwaway to clear it, then hit for real.
Killing him while the aura is up revives him at 75000 health with a +200% damage aura, a near-loss. Always clear Flames of Hatred before the kill shot.

name: Mandrake the Maligician
locations: Lemuria: Wildlands - Heart of the Wilds
cheats: "Late Arrivals Disrupt the Show" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Mandrake will cast Camp Bandit (Tier 5a) on them.

"I Am Unstoppable!" - At the start of the battle, Mandrake puts on a -10% version of Fortify that lasts four Rounds. Each succeeding Round that he takes new Damage, Mandrake will cast a new version of the Aura Spell that increases his Resist by -10%, until it is at -100% Damage. (Note: This Resist Aura is not always shown in the Creature's effects.)
Later ticks of Damage over Time Spells do not appear to trigger this increase.
"Feel the Power of Mandrake" - If Mandrake is at full health at the end of a Round, he'll cast a -15% Health Leafstorm that removes 15% of the total Health of the target to all enemies.
After this spell is cast, Enfeeble will be cast on all enemies.
Leafstorm will trigger Adapt's spell requirement giving a Power Pip.
If Mandrake is not at full health at the end of a Round, he'll cast a version of Blessing to restore 5% of his total Health (+698).
Entangle will not prevent this heal.
"By the Power of Mali-Magic" - If a Healing Spell is cast, Mandrake will cast a +20% Dragonspear on the healer.
Pet May Casts will trigger this cheat.
"I knew you would try that!" - If Mandrake's Aura is removed or changed, he will change his Aura to a -100% version of Fortify.
If Mandrake's Aura is replaced with a negative Aura, such as with Pathogen, this cheat will be activated and the Aura's appearance will be altered, but the effects of it will not.
If Humango Assistant is not defeated by the time Mandrake's -10% Aura expires, this cheat will be triggered.

If Humango Assistant is defeated by the time Mandrake's -10% Aura expires, Mandrake will replace it with a -20% version of Fortify, triggering this cheat 1 Round later. (needs verification for trigger)
spellNotes: Casts a version of Life Feint that affects all enemies.
strategy: Attack with Death (+5%) or use a prism; he resists Life 73%.

The core trap: every time he takes new damage he stacks a +10% resist aura, up to -100% and fully immune. You get essentially one real hit, so never chip him. Stack all your blades and Feints first, then land one massive hit.
Do not remove or change his aura; that triggers an instant -100% Fortify. Leave it alone.
Keep him below full health at end of round: at full health he Leafstorms your team for 15% of your health and Enfeebles everyone, while below full he only heals about 5% (+698).
Avoid healing; any heal, including pet may-casts, puts a +20% Dragonspear on the healer. Survive with shields.
He is stunable and beguilable, so stun to buy safe setup rounds, then unload your one big burst.
cheatNotes: Arriving late gets you Camp Bandit, so join on time.
Each time he takes new damage he adds +10% resist (up to -100% and immune). Do not chip him; set up fully and hit once, hard.
Later ticks of a DoT do not add to that resist stack, so a DoT's follow-up ticks are free damage.
At full health at end of round he Leafstorms your team for 15% of your health, so keep him below full.
That Leafstorm is followed by an Enfeeble on all enemies, gutting your damage.
His Leafstorm also feeds his Adapt requirement, giving him a power pip.
If he is not at full health at end of round he just heals about 5% (+698) instead of Leafstorming.
Entangle will not stop that heal, so do not count on it.
Any heal you cast puts a +20% Dragonspear on the healer, buffing his next hit. Avoid healing.
Even pet may-cast heals trigger the Dragonspear cheat.
Removing or changing his aura triggers an instant -100% Fortify. Leave his aura alone.
Even overwriting it with a negative aura like Pathogen triggers the cheat, keeping the resist effect underneath.
If his Humango Assistant is still alive when his opening -10% aura expires, this immunity cheat fires, so pressure that add.
If the Assistant is dead in time, he swaps to a -20% Fortify and the cheat comes a round later instead.

name: Marcio
cheats: During the first Round, Marcio will use his turn to cast a -5% version of Doom and Gloom.
If a Wizard casts a Global Spell, then Marcio will interupt-cast the -5% version of Doom and Gloom.
If a Trap Spell is placed on Marcio, he will interrupt-cast Cleanse Ward to remove it.
If a multi-Trap Spell such as Elemental Trap is cast, then Marcio will remove only the last Trap cast.
Multi-target Trap Spells such as Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.

"Your desperation pains me!" - If a Wizard attacks Marcio when they have 1/3 health, then he will interrupt to damage himself for the following amount based on the number of spots filled by the Wizard team:
800 Ice Damage (1 Wizard present)
500 Random Damage (2 Wizards present)
This attack is currently bugged and does not deal Ice Damage, nor does it deal damage of any of the seven primary Schools of Magic.
400 Ice Damage (3 Wizards present)
300 Ice Damage (4 Wizards present)
Even though the Spells that deal Ice Damage are not cast by Marcio, they can still be dispelled.
strategy: Trap him with multi-target spells (Windstorm, Mass Feint) — single traps on Marcio get Cleansed off immediately.
Attack with Fire (he takes +35%); he resists Ice 35%.
Plan heals around his round-1 Doom & Gloom (-5%, minor) and don't try to clear it by swapping Globals (he recasts it).
Optional: attacking him while you sit at about 1/3 health makes him damage himself (800 Ice solo) — a small free chunk.
Stack blades and a multi-target trap, then burst his 9,005 health.
cheatNotes: He opens round 1 with a -5% Doom & Gloom, lightly cutting healing — minor, but plan heals around it.
Casting a Global makes him re-cast the -5% Doom & Gloom — don't try to clear it by swapping globals.
A single Trap on Marcio gets Cleansed off immediately — use multi-target traps instead.
Multi-trap spells like Elemental Trap only lose their last trap to the Cleanse — the earlier traps stick.
Multi-target trap spells like Windstorm don't trigger the Cleanse at all — your safest trapping tool here.
If you attack him while at about 1/3 of your health, he damages himself — a small free chunk you can exploit.

name: Marcus
locations: Krokotopia: Underground Market - Shrine of Marcus

stats.resist: 42% to all schools72% to all schools

cheats: [Round] "WOOF! Let's play!" - Marcus will say this at the beginning of the first Round.
[Blade][Global][Trap] "Fetchy-Fetchy!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Global, or Trap, Marcus will cast a version of Frozen Armor on himself that absorbs 800 Damage.
"Zoomies!" - If there are 3 or more helpful Wards on Marcus at the end of a Round, he will cast a protected Iceblade (+40%) on himself.
[Damage] "SQUIRREL!" - If Marcus is damaged by Ratatoskr's Spin, he will cast Freeze on himself.
strategy: This is a 19,660-health Ice boss - bring a team with a Fire hitter and support. He is stunable, so stuns can buy setup rounds.
Hit Fire. He takes +30% Fire and resists 72% Ice and 42% most schools, so Fire is your clean damage.
Every blade, global, or trap you cast gives him an 800-damage Frozen Armor absorb, and these stack. Keep setup lean and plan a hit big enough to punch through the absorbs you generate.
Do not let 3 or more helpful wards sit on him at end of round, or he self-casts a protected +40% Iceblade.
Because setup feeds his absorbs, funnel to a single Fire hitter, minimize charm/trap count, and overpower his stacked Frozen Armor with one large hit. Ratatoskr's Spin will Freeze him if you have it.
cheatNotes: Any blade, global, or trap you cast gives him a Frozen Armor absorbing 800 damage. These stack, so overpower them with a bigger hit rather than chipping.
If 3 or more helpful wards are on him at the end of a round, he self-casts a protected +40% Iceblade.
Damaging him with Ratatoskr's Spin makes him Freeze himself.

name: Margherita Vizzini
cheats: "Riposte!" - If Margherita is damaged by a Spell, then she will interrupt-cast Mythblade on herself, then use her Natural Attack on the Wizard that attacked her.
This Mythblade will stack with her regularly cast Mythblade.
If Margherita is damaged more than once by a single Spell, then she will use this cheat for every time that she is damaged on her attacker.
Ticks from Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
If this cheat triggers while Margherita has no Pips, then her Natural Attack will act as though she has one Pip.
Although her Natural Attack is not cast when used through this cheat, it can still be Dispelled.
"I have studied in Crab Alley!" - Margherita will interrupt-cast Storm Prism and then use Triton on a Wizard due to a few triggers.
If there are two Wizards present in the duel, then this cheat will trigger on the first Wizard to cast any kind of Spell.
May-Cast Spells will trigger this cheat.

If there was initially only one Wizard present in the duel, and a second joins later or a Minion is summoned, then this cheat will activate on the first Wizard to cast any kind of Spell when the second character becomes active in the duel.
This cheat will not activate if there are initially more than three Wizards in the duel and a Wizard(s) flee, leaving only two Wizards left.
If a Lanyon Scratcher is defeated, then this cheat will trigger on whoever defeated the Scratcher.
This cheat will only trigger once if more than one Laynon Scrathcer is defeated with a single Spell.
This cheat will only trigger once if there are only two Wizards present in the duel and the first Spell cast by the Wizard team defeats a Lanyon Scratcher(s).
This cheat will not trigger if a Lanyon Scratcher is defeated by a Damage over Time effect.
Although Triton is not cast, it can be Dispelled.

"I pierce your defenses!" - If Margherita uses her Natural Attack on a Wizard and they still have a Shield or Trap placed on them after her Natural Attack, then she will interrupt to use Pierce on the Wizard that she attacked.

"I disarm your words!" - If Margherita uses her Natural Attack on a Wizard and they still have a Shield, Trap, or positive Charm placed on them after her Natural Attack, or there is an active Global Spell, and her previously used Natural Attack activated her Pierce cheat, then she will interrupt to use Disarm on the Wizard that she attacked.
Although Disarm is not cast, it can be Dispelled.
"I invoke the power of Myth!" - At the beginning of the Round after Margherita's Health drops to 50% or lower, she will interrupt-cast a +35% version of Time of Legend.
"I'll back you into a trap!" - At the beginning of every Round after Margherita's Health drops to 50% or lower, she will interrupt-cast Myth Trap on a random Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+40%); she resists Myth 70%.
She's low health (3,620) — blade up over a round or two and end her in one big single hit; multi-hit spells make her counter-attack for each hit.
Damaging her makes her Mythblade herself and natural-attack you, but DoT ticks don't trigger it — a DoT or a stun (she's stunable) avoids the counter.
Watch the Storm Prism trigger — if she prisms herself your Storm converts to resisted Myth, so consider stunning or hitting through it.
Below 50% health she casts Time of Legend and Myth Traps a wizard every round, so burst her from above 50% straight to dead.
cheatNotes: Damage her and she Mythblades herself then natural-attacks you — end her in one hit or stun her first.
That extra Mythblade stacks with her normal one, so she hits harder each time.
A multi-hit spell makes her counter once per hit — use a single big hit instead.
DoT ticks don't trigger the counter — a DoT chips her safely.
Her counter natural attack can still be Dispelled.
On certain triggers she Storm Prisms herself and Tritons a wizard — the prism turns your Storm into resisted Myth.
May-cast pet spells trigger it.
Defeating a Lanyon Scratcher triggers it on whoever killed it.
Killing a Scratcher with a DoT won't trigger it.
Her Triton cheat can be Dispelled even though it isn't cast.
After her natural attack, if you still have a shield or trap she Pierces you.
If you still have a shield/trap/charm or a global is active and her last natural triggered Pierce, she Disarms you.
That Disarm can be Dispelled.
The round after she drops to 50% health she casts a +35% Time of Legend.
Every round below 50% she Myth Traps a random wizard — burst her past 50% to dead.

name: Margherita Vizzini (Challenge)
cheats: [Myth][Ward] "Tropement!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Margherita Vizzini will cast Myth Trap on the recipient of the Ward.
[Myth][Curse] "Derobement!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm, Margherita Vizzini will cast Myth Trap on the caster.
[Damage] "Riposte!" - If Margherita is damaged by a Spell, she will cast Myth Trap and then her Natural Attack on the Wizard that attacked her.
If Margherita is damaged more than once by a single Spell, she will use this cheat for every time that she is damaged.
Ticks from Damage over Time effects will not trigger this cheat.
If this cheat triggers while Margherita has no Pips, then her Natural Attack will act as though she has one Pip.
[Damage] "I pierce your defenses!" - After Margherita Vizzini casts her Natural Attack, if her target has a helpful Ward, she will cast Pierce.
[Spell] "I have studied in Crab Alley!" - When a Wizard defeats a Lanyon Scratcher, Margherita will cast Storm Prism and then Triton on the Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Fire or Life (+12%). She barely resists most schools, so your damage school is flexible.

She Ripostes: every time a spell damages her she drops a Myth Trap on the attacker and hits back with her Natural Attack. Use one single-hit spell, not a multi-hit attack; each separate damage instance triggers a fresh Riposte.
Avoid helpful Wards (she answers with a Myth Trap on you and Pierces the ward after her Natural Attack) and harmful Charms (Myth Trap on the caster).
At 3,520 health, set up a blade or two and end it in one hit before the Ripostes add up.
cheatNotes: Casting a helpful Ward drops a Myth Trap on the warded Wizard. Skip shields here.
Casting a harmful Charm drops a Myth Trap on the caster. Skip weaknesses.
Any spell that damages her triggers a Myth Trap plus her Natural Attack on the attacker. Hit once, hard.
A multi-hit spell Ripostes once per hit, so multi-hit attacks get punished repeatedly.
Damage-over-Time ticks do not trigger the Riposte.
If she has no Pips when it triggers, her Natural Attack fires as though she had one Pip.
After her Natural Attack, if you are holding a helpful Ward she Pierces it. Do not sit on shields.
Defeating a Lanyon Scratcher makes her cast Storm Prism then Triton on that Wizard.

name: Master Tonkatsu
cheats: At the end of each Round, Master Tonkatsu will interrupt and beckon Hamakala to deal 100 Balance damage to all Wizards.
Although this Spell is not cast, it can be dispelled with Strangle.
strategy: Small Storm boss (2,320) with Spirit, Mind, and Body Bowl minions. Nothing here stops a straightforward blade-and-hit.
Each round he beckons Hamakala for 100 Balance damage to all wizards, minor chip. Strangle dispels it if you want, otherwise heal through it.
Blade up and AoE the bowls down with him.
cheatNotes: Each round he pings the whole team for 100 Balance damage. It is small; heal through it or Strangle it away.
Though it is not a normal cast, Strangle can dispel this 100-damage beckon if it bothers you.

name: Matilda Collie
cheats: "Money does buy healthiness!" - If Matilda Collie takes Damage, she will cast a version of Leprechaun that restores 420 Health to herself.
Multi-Hit Spells, like Minotaur will only trigger this cheat once.
strategy: Every time she takes damage she heals 420 (Leprechaun), so avoid chipping her with many small hits.
Stack blades and Feint, then land one large hit; the single 420 heal is trivial against a big burst.
Multi-hit spells like Minotaur only trigger the heal once, so they are fine.
Bring enough setup to one-shot or near-one-shot her 12,205 health.
cheatNotes: Any damage she takes heals her 420. Burst her with one big hit rather than many small ones.
Multi-hit spells only trigger the 420 heal a single time, so they are safe to use.

name: Matkis Axethief
cheats: [Round] During the first Round, Matkis Axethief will use his turn to cast Reshuffle for zero pips on all Wizards.
[Jinx] If a single target Trap or Prism is cast on Matkis Axethief, he will cheat-cast a version of Cleanse Ward that removes all harmful Wards from himself.
Spells such as Mass Fire Prism and Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.

[Manipulation] "Don't Get Too Comfortable!" For every Spell cast by Wizards (including Pet casts) or enemies, there is a random chance Matkis Axethief will cheat-cast Reshuffle for zero Pips on all Wizards.
This cheat can repeat multiple times in a row.
Wizards under the effect of Empowerment will gain a Pip.
[Damage] "Hah! Your miss leaves you open!" If a Wizard fizzles, then Matkis Axethief will cast an enhanced version of Link on the Wizard who fizzled (100+600 Fire Damage, 30+600 Health).
strategy: Use Mass traps and Mass prisms only (Mass Fire Prism, Windstorm). A single-target Trap or Prism makes him Cleanse off all his harmful Wards.
He resists Fire 80%; convert with a mass prism and hit Ice or Storm, which he takes +35% from.
Avoid fizzling. A fizzle triggers an enhanced Link on you (about 700 Fire, healing him ~630), so run accuracy and do not swing at low base chance.
Expect frequent free Reshuffles on your team (round 1 and randomly per cast), so keep key cards early and do not rely on a stable hand.
Set up with mass effects, then burst; 9,040 health falls to one blade-stacked hit.
cheatNotes: During round 1 he casts a free Reshuffle on all wizards, redrawing your hands.
Casting a single-target Trap or Prism on him makes him Cleanse away all his harmful Wards.
Mass versions like Mass Fire Prism and Windstorm do not trigger that Cleanse, so use those instead.
For any spell cast by wizards, pets, or enemies there is a random chance he free-Reshuffles all wizards.
That random Reshuffle can trigger several times in a row.
Wizards under an Empowerment aura gain a pip when he Reshuffles them.
If a wizard fizzles, he casts an enhanced Link on them for about 700 Fire while healing himself ~630.

name: Maxine Rockhoppierre
locations: Novus: La Ville Rose - Maxine's Office
cheats: [Aura] "Dasein protects me!" - Starting on Round 1, Maxine interrupt casts an Invulnerability -200% version of Brace on herself.
The Aura cannot be removed.
[Minion] "Vive la revolution!" - After all Minions are defeated, she casts a Shadow Spell to Summon Aberrant Maxine.
In addition, she'll cast a version of Frenzy using her turn, and recasts every 4 Rounds.
strategy: Round 1 she puts an unremovable Invulnerability aura (-200% Brace) on herself that heavily cuts your damage. Do not waste turns trying to strip it; stack blades and pierce and wait for it to expire, then hit.
She resists 45% to every school, so pile on armor piercing rather than chasing a weakness.
Killing her Aberrant Pinguoin makes her summon Aberrant Maxine and cast Frenzy, so expect a fresh minion and a damage spike.
Set up a big pierced hit for when the aura drops; funnel blades to one hitter.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she casts an Invulnerability -200% Brace aura that sharply reduces incoming damage. Set up and hit once it expires.
Once all her minions are defeated she summons Aberrant Maxine. Killing the Pinguoin just brings a replacement.
She also Frenzies herself on that turn and recasts Frenzy every 4 rounds, spiking her damage - shield up.

name: Mayor McCreary
cheats: "My people won't let me fall!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Mayor McCreary will cast a Sun spell to summon Enthralled Villager (Balance), Enthralled Villager (Life), and Enthralled Villager (Storm) and will cast an Invulnerability Aura lasting indefinitely.
The aura can be replaced with negative auras like Attenuate without penalty, but the Invulnerability effect will not be removed.

"You think I was their only enabler?" - At the beginning of the Round after all Enthralled Villager minions are defeated, Mayor McCreary will cast Frenzy, replacing his Invulnerability Aura. If Mayor McCreary is not defeated within three Rounds of his Frenzy cast (including the round he casts it), he will re-summon his minions and Invulnerability Aura.
strategy: Team fight. Hit with Death (he resists Life). He is damage-immune under an Invulnerability aura until his three Enthralled Villagers die.
Focus and kill all three Villagers first, AoE clears them together. The aura cannot be removed by replacing it (Attenuate and the like will not drop the immunity).
The round after the last Villager falls he casts Frenzy and drops his invulnerability, this is your only window. He hits harder under Frenzy, so shield up.
You have about three rounds (including his Frenzy round) before he re-summons the Villagers and re-shields, so have the whole team pre-bladed to burst the moment he is vulnerable.
Supports blade the hitter and Feint him during the Villager clear, the healer keeps everyone up through Frenzy, then the hitter dumps one massive Death hit inside the window.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he summons three Enthralled Villagers and an indefinite Invulnerability aura, he cannot be damaged until all three Villagers are dead.
You can overwrite the aura slot with a negative aura, but the Invulnerability effect itself will not come off, you must kill the Villagers.
The round after the last Villager dies he Frenzies and loses invulnerability. Kill him within about three rounds or he re-summons the Villagers and re-shields, burst him fully bladed in that window.

name: Medulla (Challenge)
cheats: After Sparck's first cycle, Medulla will command these cheats.
"Mr. Sparck, man the Essence Cannon" - This is a one Round warning before Sparck fires the Essence Cannon.
"Fire!" - At the end of the Round, Sparck will use a Shadow Spell to fire the Essence Cannon, which deals 1,050 Shadow Damage to all Wizards and removes one Shadow Pip from each Wizard.
Before Beans' Blossom's Thorns cheat, Medulla will use the following cheat.
"Prepare to be mind-meddled!" - Medulla will cast a Shadow Spell to randomly 'Confuse' a Wizard for three Rounds (begins in the following Round).
All Spells have a chance of targeting a random ally or enemy.
Pet may-casts will keep their original target.
If a Wizard attempts to pass while 'Confused', a random Spell will be selected from the Wizard's hand.
This action will not take place if the Wizard is stunned.
"Sparck! I need technical support" - Medulla will shout this out whenever a Wizard casts an Aura, and Sparck will cast Supernova (535, 75%).
Cycle 1 - Sparck will begin the first cycle
"Medulla is my captain now" - Sparck will cast Magnify (6 Rounds).
"As you wish, Boss" - Sparck will cast Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip).
"You're up Mr. Sparck" - Medulla will shout this out, and Sparck will cast Niles' Knock-Out.
Beans
"What’s up doc?” - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Beans will cast an all allies Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2).
"Beans, Beans. He's good for the heart!" - Medulla will shout this out whenever a Wizard casts Weakness, Beans will cast an all allies Shadow version of Double Cleanse Charm.
Cycle 2 - Beans will begin the second cycle.
"Anything you say, Boss" - Beans will cast Goat Monk (Tier 5b)
"I must heal my master...bc I'm a Dr...Darnit" - Beans will cast Pigsie (Tier 5b)
"Your turn doctor" - Medulla will shout this out, and Beans will cast Blossom's Thorns
Cycle 3 - Pork will begin the third cycle
"I live to serve" - Pork will cast Scald
"Yes Boss" - Pork will cast Fire Dragon
Old Cheats
Medulla
"You can't outsmart me!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already started, Medulla will cast a Shadow Spell to 'Confuse' the Wizard for two Rounds (begins in the following Round).
"Prepare to be mind-meddled!" - He will cast a Shadow Spell to randomly 'Confuse' a Wizard for three Rounds (begins in the following Round).
If a Wizard casts a negative Charm or Trap on Medulla after Beans is defeated, they will not be removed.
"[Aftershock]" - He will shout this out after using Earthquake (525).
"Your combat skills are laughable, ha!" - Once all his Minions are defeated, further attempts to Feint will result in all negative Wards and Charms being removed from Medulla.
Beans (Enemy)
"Beans, Beans. He's good for the heart." - If a negative Charm is placed on Medulla, Beans will remove all negative Charms from him.
"What’s up doc?” - If a Trap is placed on Medulla, Beans will cast a double Cleanse Ward.
Beans (Ally)
"Hold still--I'll have you fixed in a jiffy!" - Beans will randomly cast Pigsie, restoring 550 Health to all Wizards.
This cheat can consume Brilliant Light from Medulla.
Pork (Enemy)
"Do your worst, Pork!" - Pork will cast Incindiate.
Pork (Ally)
"We must work together!" - Randomly at the beginning of the Round, Pork will cast Smoke Screen (-45%) at the enemy team.
Sparck (Enemy)
Sparck (Ally)

"Preparing to fire the Essence Cannon" - At the end of the Round, Sparck will cast a Shadow Spell to fire the Essence Cannon, which deals 6,000 Shadow Damage to all enemies and removes one Shadow Pip from each enemy.
This cheat will consume both Mythblades and Shadowblades from Medulla.
spellNotes: Casts an Aegis-protected Reaperblade (Myth-Shadow), and Bladestorm.
strategy: A 125,000-health Myth boss with three minions, so bring a full team. Hit with Storm (he takes +10%, resists Myth and Shadow 50%) and assign a hitter, supports, and a healer.

Beans punishes setup: traps make Beans Cleanse Ward the team's traps, and negative charms on Medulla get removed while Beans lives. Protect Feints with Indemnity and save weakening for after Beans dies.
Manage Confuse. Medulla randomly Confuses a wizard for three rounds so their spells hit random targets; do not let the hitter act while confused, and stunning a confused wizard skips the forced cast.
Avoid auras; any aura makes Sparck Supernova the caster.
Heed the Essence Cannon warning ('man the Essence Cannon'). Next round Sparck fires 1050 Shadow to all and strips a shadow pip, so be at full health and do not bank on shadow pips landing.
Kill the minions to progress, then funnel blades to the hitter and burst Medulla in big Storm hits; his incoming healing is cut 75%, so damage sticks.
cheatNotes: This is your one-round warning before the Essence Cannon, so heal to full now.
Sparck fires the Essence Cannon for 1050 Shadow to the whole team and strips a shadow pip, so be topped up and do not count on shadow pips surviving.
Medulla Confuses a wizard for three rounds so spells may hit random targets; do not let your hitter act while confused.
A stunned wizard skips the forced random cast, so stunning a confused hitter can save the turn.
Any aura you cast makes Sparck Supernova the caster, so leave auras at home.
Casting a trap makes Beans Cleanse Ward the whole team's traps, so keep traps minimal or protect with Indemnity.
Casting Weakness makes Beans Double Cleanse Charm the team, wiping your weakness setup.
Late joiners get Confused for two rounds, so everyone join on Round 1.
He also randomly Confuses a wizard for three rounds, so plan around forced random casts.
After Beans is defeated, negative charms and traps on Medulla are no longer removed, so save Feints for after Beans dies.
Once all minions are down, Feinting him strips all negative wards and charms off him, so trap him before then or accept the loss.
While Beans lives, a negative charm on Medulla makes Beans remove all his negative charms.
A trap on Medulla makes Beans double Cleanse Ward it off, so protect with Indemnity.
Pork casts Incindiate, boosting fire damage against you, so expect a spike.

name: Medulla (Standard)
cheats: At the end of the first Round, all Wizards will be given 3 of the following Spell, which instantly removes Pork, Beans, or Sparck from combat, and moves them to the Wizard team:
[Clear Mind]
If Pork, Beans, or Sparck is healed on the same Round that Clear Mind is cast on them, they regain their prior Health, and remain on Medulla's team.
"Pork!" / "You freed my mind, Wizard. Let's finish this!" - When Pork is defeated, he will assist the Wizard team.
"We must work together!" - Randomly at the beginning of the Round, Pork will cast Smoke Screen (-45%) at the enemy team.
"Beans!" / "The doctor is in!" - When Beans is defeated, he will assist the Wizard team.
"Hold still - I'll have you fixed up in a jiffy!" - Beans will randomly cast a version of Pigsie, restoring 550 Health to all Wizards.
"Sparck!" / "Medulla's mind-control spell is broken. Fascinating!" - When Sparck is defeated, he will assist the Wizard team.

"Preparing to fire the Essence Cannon" - At the end of the Round, Sparck will cast a Shadow Spell to fire the Essence Cannon, which deals 6,000 Shadow Damage to all enemies and removes one Shadow Pip from each enemy.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Reaperblade (Myth-Shadow) and Bladestorm.
Casts a Mass version of Hex (+30%).
strategy: Normally a team fight: Medulla is a high-HP dungeon finale flanked by Pork, Beans, and Sparck. Standard setup is a hitter, supports feeding blades and Feints, and a healer who plans around Sparck's Essence Cannon. (An expert solo clear is possible; the guide shows a Storm solo route that keeps Aura of Fortification and Aura of Brace up to cancel the pierce and buy a free cast when Confused.)
Use the three Clear Mind cards handed to your team at the end of Round 1 to pull Pork, Beans, or Sparck off Medulla's team and onto yours, flipping their cheats to your side.
Never heal a minion on the same round you Clear Mind it, or it snaps back to full health and stays on Medulla's team.
Kill order: Sparck first. His Essence Cannon deals 6,000 Shadow to your whole team and strips a Shadow Pip, so convert or drop him before he fires.
Beans second: his Pigsie heals otherwise keep the enemy team topped off, and he pulls traps off Medulla.
Take Pork third, then Medulla last. Between Sparck's Essence Cannon rounds, have supports stack Feints and blades so the hitter can burst each target down.
Once the minions are gone the fight opens up. Funnel the stacked Feints and blades into Medulla and hit with Storm (+10% in) around her 50% Myth resist to finish.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1 your team is handed three Clear Mind cards; casting one on Pork, Beans, or Sparck instantly pulls that minion off Medulla's team and onto yours.
If you heal a minion the same round you cast Clear Mind on it, it regains its old health and stays loyal to Medulla, so never heal them on the conversion round.
When Pork is defeated (or freed) he switches to fight for the wizard team.
While allied to you, Pork randomly opens the round with Smoke Screen (-45% accuracy) on Medulla's team.
When Beans is defeated (or freed) he joins the wizard team.
Beans randomly casts a version of Pigsie, healing all wizards 550; this same healing makes him a priority kill while he is on Medulla's side.
When Sparck is defeated (or freed) he joins the wizard team.
Sparck's Essence Cannon is a Shadow spell fired at end of round that deals 6,000 Shadow to the whole enemy team and removes one Shadow Pip from each, which is why you eliminate or convert him first.

guide.title: Brainstorm: A Storm Solo of Medulla

name: Melanj
locations: Mirage: Caterwaul Canyons - Cavern of the Wurm
cheats: "*Melanj Mana Burn*" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Melanj will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
"*Melanj rules only*" - At the beginning of Round 1, Melanj will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%).
If a Wizard tries to replace the Doom and Gloom with another type of Global Spell, Melanj will recast Doom and Gloom.
This cheat can be bypassed by using Strangle.
If Melanj replaces the Doom and Gloom with another type of Global Spell (e.g. Gaze of Fate.), the Global Spell won't change.
"*Melanj spice strike*" - At the end of every fifth Round, Melanj will cast Sand Wurm at a random Wizard.
"*Wurm domination*" - If a Wizard uses a Blade Spell, then Melanj will interrupt-cast her Natural Attack, Steal Charm (Shadow), and a Weakness at the Caster.
A Global Spell, such as Darkwind, will trigger this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade Spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), Melanj will only target the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Shadow Shrike's Dragonspear will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis will prevent the blade from being removed but the weakness will still be placed.
Blades given from spells will not trigger this cheat.
Pet casts will trigger this cheat.
"*Wurm plague*" - If a Wizard uses a Feint, there is a chance that Melanj will cast Virulent Plague.
Life Feint will trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: Casts a multi-target version of Poison (45+450).
strategy: Balance boss; she opens Round 1 with a -75% Doom and Gloom that cuts healing. Clear it with Strangle rather than a Global, which she just recasts.
Do not cast blades. A blade makes her interrupt with her natural attack, Steal Charm, and a Weakness on the caster; Aegis saves the blade but not the weakness.
Set up with traps instead of blades; traps do not trigger her, and blades given by spells or Shadow Shrike's Dragonspear are also safe.
Feints are risky too; a Feint gives a chance of Virulent Plague, and Life Feint also triggers it.
Every fifth Round she casts Sand Wurm at a random wizard; keep a heal ready on that timer.
Trap up, Feint sparingly, and finish with one hit.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets you Mana Burned; enter on time.
She opens with a -75% Doom and Gloom that cripples healing; clear it with Strangle rather than a Global.
If you overwrite the Doom and Gloom with another Global, she just recasts it; use Strangle instead.
Strangle bypasses the Doom and Gloom cheat cleanly.
If she does keep a replacement Global, it stays put; still, Strangle is the reliable answer.
Every fifth Round she hits a random wizard with Sand Wurm; keep a heal ready on that timer.
Casting a blade makes her interrupt with a hit, Steal Charm, and a Weakness on you; set up with traps instead.
A Global like Darkwind also triggers that punish.
On a multi-target blade she only hits the caster.
Shadow Shrike's Dragonspear does not trigger the blade punish.
Aegis protects the blade from the steal, but she still lands the weakness.
Blades handed to you by spells do not trigger the punish.
Pet-cast blades do trigger it, so watch your pet.
Feinting her risks a Virulent Plague; Feint sparingly.
Life Feint also risks the Plague.

name: Meowiarty (Challenge)
cheats: [Stun] "I've taken the liberty of inviting some of your old friends" - At the beginning of the first Round, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast a Star Spell to summon three Protection Pox (Challenge) minions, and a version of Blinding Light that doesn't place a stun shield.

[Afterlife] "Settle in. We've much to catch up on." - At the beginning of the second Round, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast a Star version of Guardian Spirit that restores 100% Health after defeat on all allies.
[Shield] "Shields up!" - At the beginning of the second Round, and every three Rounds after, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast Legion Shield.
strategy: Plan around Guardian Spirit. On Round 2 he gives his whole side a revive-at-100% effect, so kill everything in the same round or your kills reverse.
He resists all schools only 20%, so any strong school works; set up and AoE the entire board at once to beat the revive.
Clear or hit through his shields. He reapplies Legion Shield on Round 2 and every third Round after, so Shatter or pop them before your big swing.
Handle the three Protection Pox he summons on Round 1 with your AoE rather than picking them off one at a time.
At 6,115 Health this is soloable with a strong AoE hitter, but the whole board must fall together.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he summons three Protection Pox minions and casts a Blinding Light that leaves no stun shield.
Round 2 he gives his entire side Guardian Spirit, reviving them at 100% Health after defeat, so kill everything at once or remove it before finishing them.
Round 2 and every third Round after, he casts Legion Shield on his side, so clear it or hit through before your big swing.

name: Meowiarty (Rank 12)
cheats: Meowiarty will cleanse any negative Charms off of him and his allies.
Dr. Von Katzenstein will use this cheat to clear off any negative Charms.

"Let's start this party off with a bang!" - At the start of Round 1, he will attack a random Wizard, leaving a DoT around them for five Rounds. On the fifth Round, the DoT will detonate and deal ~2,500 Myth Damage to the affected Wizard, then place +10% "lock" Traps on him and his allies.
"Tisk, tisk!" - Using Triage or Mass Triage will trigger a 2,000 Shadow Damage Insane Bolt on the receiver.
Gibson O'Leary who cast Detonate on a Wizard with a DoT will not trigger this cheat.

Shift and Cooldown do not trigger this cheat. Shift works. Cooldown is trickier. It must be cast between the 4th and the 5th tick. The turn Meowiarty casts it is turn 1. To remove the last tick from a Wizard before removing it from your Wizard, cast Cooldown on turn 4, because the 4th tick has already happened. If the receiver of the cheat follows you in the battle circle, cast Cooldown on turn 5 (the 4th tick happened last turn), and the 5th tick is about to happen. This does not change even if Cooldown is cast more than once during that cycle. The last large tick will be removed if Cooldown is cast between the 4th and 5th tick.

"Blast! You foiled my ingenious plan!" - If all the "locks" are removed, he will cheat-cast Mass Triage on himself and proceed to throw the Myth DoT again on a Wizard (identical to the one thrown in the cheat "Let's start this party off with a bang!").
"Poor Wizard, you can't win for losing." - If the last "lock" is removed by an AoE spell, he will cheat-cast his Shadow Insane Bolt on the Wizard who cast the AoE.
Scald does not trigger this cheat.
"Tick... Tock... Boom! Too late!" - If any of the "locks" remain after five Rounds, he will remove all negative and positive Wards on him and his allies, and replace the "locks".
"Every star fears the Supernova!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell, the O'Leary Thug will cheat-cast Supernova on the Wizard.
He will also say this if a Wizard casts Shadow Shrike, but will not actually perform the cheat.
"A thug's job is to balance the fight!" - If a Wizard has five or more Pips after casting a Spell, he will cheat-cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"Keep up the good work!" - If an enemy successfully casts an attack, the Midnighter will cheat-cast Empower on the ally.
"Bad luck abounds!" - If an enemy Spell fizzles, he will cheat-cast Black Cat (100-140) on the fizzled Spell's target (or a random Wizard if the Spell was intended for an ally).
"Sharp move, my friend!" - If an enemy casts a Blade Spell, he will cheat-cast Dark Pact on the receiver of the Blade.
"Tis a good time for my monster to come out to play!" - At the end of every third Round, Dr. Von Katzenstein will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster on a random Wizard.
"Don't worry, there's time for everyone to play!" - At the start of every fourth Round, he will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard.
"It's not safe to run with Blades!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, he will cheat-cast Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
"One must keep a sanitary work environment!" - If a Wizard casts a negative Charm, he will use Cleanse Charm on the receiver of a negative Charm.
"Uh oh, you dropped your little Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Gibson O'Leary will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the blade, then he will proceed to destroy it.
"I think that Blade would look better on me!" - If a universal Blade, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade.
"You can't mess with us!" - If any type of universal Trap is placed on an enemy, he will cheat-cast Backdraft on the Wizard who cast the Trap.
"Allow me to lower the boom!" - When a single Trap or DoT is placed on a Wizard, he will cheat-cast Detonate on the Wizard.
"For me? You shouldn't have!" - If any type of universal Shield Ward is cast, Jacques the Scratcher will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield.
Legion Shield only affects the caster.
"No need for such things here!" - If any type of School-specific Shield Ward is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield, and then proceed to destroy it.
"You can't have nice things when there are cats around!" - If any type of Heal over time spell is cast, he will cheat-cast Snow Drift on the receiver of the heal.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Allow me to scratch some of that dirt off you, friend." - If an enemy takes Damage from a DoT Spell, he will cheat-cast Cooldown on the ally.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage.
strategy: Myth challenge boss (7990) with a pack of gimmick minions. Hit with Storm (+30%); never Myth (80% resist).

Round 1 he tags a wizard with a 5-round Myth DoT that detonates ~2500 and drops +10% lock traps on the team. Do not Triage it (that triggers a 2000 Insane Bolt). Manage it with Cooldown timed between the 4th and 5th tick, or Shift.
Do not clear the locks with an AoE; removing the last lock by AoE brings an Insane Bolt on the caster. If any locks remain after 5 rounds he strips all wards and re-locks, so handle the DoT cleanly.
Watch your pip count: end a turn with 5+ pips and the O'Leary Thug Mana Burns you. Skip Auras (Supernova counter) and go light on blades (they get stolen, Dark-Pacted, or Enfeebled).
Because setup is so punished, keep it minimal: a couple of traps and one strong Storm hit, healing through the DoT windows.
cheatNotes: He and his minions cleanse negative charms off themselves, so Weakness or Plague on them will not stick.
Round 1 he plants a 5-round Myth DoT (~2500 on detonation) and +10% lock traps on the team. Do not panic-remove it; manage the timing.
Using Triage or Mass Triage on that DoT triggers a 2000 Shadow Insane Bolt on the target. Never Triage it.
Shift and Cooldown are the safe ways to remove the DoT; Cooldown must land between the 4th and 5th tick. Time it rather than Triaging.
Remove all the locks and he Mass Triages himself and re-throws the DoT. Clearing locks just restarts the cycle.
If an AoE removes the last lock, he Insane Bolts the AoE caster. Do not AoE the locks off.
Leave any lock up past 5 rounds and he strips all wards from both sides and re-locks. Deal with the DoT within the window.
Cast an Aura and the O'Leary Thug Supernovas you. Skip auras.
End a turn holding 5 or more pips and he Mana Burns you. Spend down before passing your turn.
When one of his side lands an attack, the Midnighter Empowers it. Expect their damage to climb.
If one of his spells fizzles he Black Cats a wizard. Minor, but keep healed.
Dr. Von hits a random wizard every 3rd round with his Monster. Heal into rounds 3, 6, and 9.
Monster Mash lands on a random wizard every 4th round. Expect it.
Casting a blade gets its recipient Enfeebled. Blade the same round you hit.
Negative charms on the enemy get Cleansed off. Do not rely on Weakness or Plague.
Gibson steals and destroys a blade you cast. Aegis the important one or blade-and-hit the same round.
Universal, Elemental, or Fire blades get stolen. Prefer single-school blades you can hit with immediately.
Place a universal Trap on an enemy and he Backdrafts the caster. Use school-specific traps.
A single Trap or DoT on a wizard gets Detonated. Be careful stacking effects on your own team.
Universal Shields get stolen by Jacques. Use school shields or heal instead.
School-specific Shields also get stolen and destroyed. Defense is hard here; race him instead.
Heal-over-times get Snow Drifted off (pet heals too). Use direct heals.

name: Meowiarty (Rank 14)
cheats: "Let's start this party off with a bang!" - At the start of the first Round, he will attack a random Wizard, leaving a defered DoT that deals 2,345 Shadow Damage after 3 Rounds.
"Tisk, tisk!" - Using Triage or Mass Triage will trigger a 2,000 Shadow Damage Insane Bolt on the caster.
After every five Rounds, Meowiarty will place +10% "lock" Traps Damage Ward on him and his Allies.
"Blast! You foiled my ingenious plan!" - If all the "locks" Damage Wards are removed, he will cheat-cast Mass Triage on himself.

"Poor Wizard, you can't win for losing." - If the last "lock" Damage Ward is removed by an Attack All Enemies Spell, he will cheat-cast a 2,000 Shadow Damage Insane Bolt on the Wizard who cast the AoE.
"Tick... Tock... Boom! Too late!" - If any of the "locks" Damage Wards remain after five Rounds, he will remove all "Locks" and apply the same Shadow defered DoT and replace the "locks."
"Every star fears the Supernova!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell, the O'Leary Thug will cheat-cast Supernova (535, 85%) on the Wizard.
"A thug's job is to balance the fight!" - If a Wizard has five or more Pips while casting a Spell, he will cheat-cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"Keep up the good work!" - If an enemy successfully casts an attack, the Midnighter will cheat-cast Empower on the Ally.

"Bad luck abounds!" - If an enemy Spell fizzles, he will cheat-cast a version of Black Cat dealing 1,000 Damage on the fizzled Spells' target (or a random Wizard if the Spell was intended for an Ally).
"Sharp move, my friend!" - If an enemy casts a Blade Spell, he will cheat-cast Dark Pact (+30%) on the receiver of the Blade.
"Tis a good time for my monster to come out to play!" - At the end of every third Round, Dr. Von Katzenstein will cheat-cast Dr. Von's Monster (1,005) on a random Wizard.
"Don't worry, there's time for everyone to play!" - At the start of every fourth Round, he will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard.
"It's not safe to run with Blades!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Damage Charm, he will cheat-cast Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
"One must keep a sanitary work environment!" - If a Wizard casts a negative Damage Charm, he will cast an AoE version of Cleanse Charm.

"Uh oh, you dropped your little Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Damage Charm, Gibson O'Leary will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade, then uses a Fire version of Cleanse Charm to remove the Blade from himself.
"I think that Blade would look better on me!" - If a universal Blade Damage Charm, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Charm on the receiver of the Blade.
"You can't mess with us!" - If any type of universal Trap Damage Ward is placed on an enemy, he will cheat-cast Backdraft on the Wizard who cast the Trap.
"Allow me to lower the boom!" - When a single Trap Damage Ward or DoT is placed on a Wizard, he will cheat-cast Detonate on the Wizard.
"For me? You shouldn't have!" - If any type of universal Shield Ward is cast, Jacques the Scratcher will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield.
"No need for such things here!" - If any type of School-specific Shield Ward is cast, he will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the receiver of the Shield, and then proceed to destroy it.
"You can't have nice things when there are cats around!" - If any type of Heal over time Healing Spell is cast, he will cheat-cast Snow Drift on the receiver of the Heal.
Pet Heals will trigger this cheat.
"Allow me to scratch some of that dirt off you, friend." - If an enemy takes Damage from a DoT Spell, he will cheat-cast Cooldown on the Ally.
strategy: The guide runs this as two max-level wizards (dual-box), but the tactics scale to any small team. Pack 4-pip hitting spells for steady damage plus blades and feints for the finisher.

Round 1 the boss summons nine minions and stuns everyone: three Koolakamba Warriors (Fire, 3,845 HP), three Numbskull Ogres (Fire, 2,880 HP), and three Protection Pox (Balance, 785 HP). Clearing minions is the first order of business.
Round 2: wizard 1 casts an AoE (the author used Orthrus) while wizard 2 buffs that attack to hit both the minions and the boss hard.

Round 3: wizard 2 repeats the AoE to finish the minions before Guardian Spirit revives them to full health. The Fire minions stun and smoke-spam, the Balance Pox spam shields, so kill them fast rather than letting the fight drag.
Rounds 4-6: with the board clear, both wizards spam 4-pip hitting spells straight into Meowiarty.
Round 7: execute the alpha strike. Blade one hitter and Feint on the other, then land the killing blow. Expect a roughly 7-round clear, faster with more players.
cheatNotes: He opens by hitting a random wizard with a deferred DoT that deals 2,345 Shadow damage after three rounds. Shield and heal it; do not Triage.
Using Triage or Mass Triage returns a 2,000 Shadow Insane Bolt on the caster, so never cleanse with it.
Every 5 rounds he places +10% lock damage wards on himself and his allies.
If all the lock wards are removed at once he Mass Triages himself, so do not clear them all in one shot.
If the last lock is removed by an all-enemies AoE, he hits that caster with a 2,000 Shadow Insane Bolt.
If any locks remain after 5 rounds he reapplies the Shadow deferred DoT and replaces the locks, so chip them down in time.
Casting an Aura makes the O'Leary Thug hit you with Supernova (535, 85%), so avoid auras.
Casting a spell with 5 or more pips triggers Mana Burn on you, so keep pips low when you cast.
When one of his side lands an attack, the Midnighter Empowers an ally.
Any fizzle triggers a 1,000-damage Black Cat on the fizzled spell's target, so keep your accuracy high.
Casting a blade triggers Dark Pact (+30%) on the receiver.
Every 3rd round Dr. Von's Monster (~1,005) hits a random wizard.
Every 4th round Monster Mash (~1,170) hits a random wizard.
A blade also draws an Enfeeble onto the receiver.
Putting a negative damage charm on an enemy makes him Cleanse Charm the whole team.
A blade can be Steal Charmed off the receiver, then Cleansed off him.
Universal blades, Elemental Blade, or Fireblade get Steal Charmed off the receiver.
Any universal trap on an enemy triggers Backdraft on the wizard who cast it.
A single trap or DoT on a wizard triggers Detonate on that wizard.
Any universal shield gets Steal Warded off the receiver.
A school-specific shield gets Steal Warded off the receiver and destroyed.
Any Heal-over-Time triggers Snow Drift on the receiver.
Pet heals trigger that Snow Drift too, so watch your pet.
When an enemy takes DoT damage, an ally gets Cooldown.

name: Meowiarty (Rematch)
cheats: "I've taken the liberty of inviting some of your old friends" - At the start of the duel, Boss casts a Star spell to summon three Protection Pox minions, and use Blinding Light on all Wizards.
"Settle in. We've much to catch up on." - At the start of the second Round, Boss casts an AoE Star version of Guardian Spirit that restores 100% Health after defeat.
strategy: Round 1 he summons three Protection Pox and Blinding-Lights the whole team (accuracy down) — overwrite the Blinding Light with your own Aura before attacking, or pack accuracy so you don't fizzle.
Round 2 he blankets his side with Guardian Spirit (revive at 100% health on defeat), so either strip those Guardian Spirits or plan to kill each target twice.
He has no school resist (only 20% to Globals), so hit hard with your best school.
Use a blade-stacked AoE to clear the Protection Pox alongside him, accounting for the Guardian Spirit revive.
Burst his 6,000 health once the revives are handled.
cheatNotes: He opens by summoning three Protection Pox and Blinding-Lighting the team (accuracy down) — replace the Blinding Light with your own Aura or bring accuracy so you don't fizzle.
Round 2 he casts AoE Guardian Spirit (revive at 100% health on defeat) across his side — strip it or expect to kill each target twice; a single big hit won't stick.

name: Meowiarty (Standard)
cheats: At the beginning of the first Round, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast Blinding Light.

[Afterlife] "Settle in. We've much to catch up on." - At the beginning of the second Round, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast a Star version of Guardian Spirit that restores 100% Health after defeat on all allies.
[Shield] "Shields up!" - Starting at the beginning of the second Round, and every three Rounds afterward, Meowiarty will interrupt-cast Legion Shield.
strategy: Attack with Storm; he takes +30% from it and resists Myth 36%, everything else only 6%.
Round 1 Blinding Light lowers your accuracy, so bring accuracy enchants or Sniper to avoid fizzles.
From round 2 he carries Guardian Spirit, a full-health revive after the first defeat. Plan to kill him twice.
Break his shields before hitting. Starting round 2 and every three rounds he casts Legion Shield on himself.
Stack blades, pop the Legion Shield, hit once, then set up again and kill him a second time after the Guardian Spirit revive.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he interrupt-casts Blinding Light, dropping your accuracy. Bring accuracy enchants or Sniper to avoid fizzles.
Round 2 he gives himself a Star Guardian Spirit, a full-health revive after the first defeat. Expect to kill him twice.
Starting round 2 and every three rounds he casts Legion Shield. Pop the shields before your hit.

name: Meowiarty (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Meowiarty Rematch - Meowiarty Challenge (Tier 2)
cheats: "Late? I'm quaking in my boots" - After his Minions are defeated, if a Wizard is late to combat, Meowiarty (Tier 2) will cast Earthquake.

strategy: Round 1 he summons three Numbskull Ogres and Blinding Lights the team (accuracy down) — use accuracy enchants or clear the debuff.
Round 2 he gives his whole side Guardian Spirit (revives once at 100% health after defeat) — so your first kill on each enemy is undone. Plan to kill each target twice.
He resists all schools 30% and hits hard (+100% outgoing), so hit Storm into his Myth and keep shields up.
Blade and AoE the ogres plus Meowiarty together, then repeat the AoE to finish the revived enemies.
He can't be stunned, so rely on shields for his big hits.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get an Earthquake once his minions are down — join on time.
He opens by summoning three Numbskull Ogres and Blinding Lights the whole team (lowered accuracy).
Round 2 he casts an AoE Guardian Spirit on his side — every enemy revives once at full health, so plan to kill each twice.

name: Meowiarty (Tier 3)

cheats: "Late? I'm quaking in my boots" - After his Minions are defeated, if a Wizard is late to combat, Meowiarty (Tier 3) will cast Earthquake.

strategy: He resists all schools 50% (global) and has 40% pierce. Bring a strong single hit and expect to punch through resistance.
He opens by summoning three Koolakamba Warriors and Blinding Light on all wizards (accuracy debuff). Carry accuracy enchants or a Sun accuracy buff so your hit does not fizzle.
On Round 2 he casts an AoE Guardian Spirit, so he and his minions each revive once at 100% health after defeat. Plan for a second kill; do not spend everything on the first knockdown.
His Mythblade is Aegis-protected. Blade up, hit through Guardian Spirit once to burn the revive, then finish.
Clear or AoE the Koolakamba alongside him.
