name: Metallossus (Portal of Peril, Tier 3)
locations: Portal of Peril: Sinbad & the Iron Sultan Gauntlet (Portal of Peril) - Metallos (Portal of Peril)
cheats: "Only Dispels can pierce my metal!" - At the beginning of the first Round, the Metallossus will cheat-cast a version of Mighty Judgement that damages all enemies and applies the same two Dispels on all enemies.
"Oof, Smart Move!" - If Metallossus is hit, the Metallossus will steal all Dispels from that Wizard and place them onto himself.
"Trying to pierce through my defense?" - If a Wizard casts an Armor Piercing Charm, Metallossus will cheat-cast Enfeeble (Shadow) on the caster.
Aegis is ineffective in protecting against this cheat.
"Only Dispels can supress my magic!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm that is not a Dispel, Metallossus will cheat-cast Cleanse Charm (Shadow), removing all harmful Charms from himself.
"Argh- 6 dispels is too much to handle!" - At the end of a Round, if the Metallossus has 6 or more Dispels, then he will remove all Dispels on himself to apply a +300% Armor Piercing Ward on himself.
"Fragment!" - At the beginning of the next Round, the Metallossus will summon a Runic Fragment.
An additional Runic Fragment is summoned when the Metallossus is defeated.
"My Fragment protects me from Dispels!" - If a Dispel is placed on then Metallossus while a Runic Fragment is in the duel, then the Metallossus will push all Dispels to the Runic Fragment.
strategy: Portal of Peril team gauntlet. He resists 300% to every school, so raw attacks do almost nothing; the fight is about Dispels, the only thing that pierces his metal.
Do not hit him carelessly. Any attack makes him steal all your Dispels onto himself.
Do not cast Armor Piercing charms (he Enfeebles you, and Aegis will not stop it) or non-dispel harmful charms (he Cleanse-Charms them off himself).
Watch the dispel count. If he reaches 6 or more Dispels he converts them into a +300% pierce ward and summons a Runic Fragment, which then soaks any further dispels you place on him.
Coordinate Dispels carefully as a team and time your damage windows. Expect an extra Runic Fragment when he dies.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he hits all wizards with Mighty Judgement and stamps two Dispels on each of you.
Attacking him makes him steal all Dispels off that wizard onto himself. Do not swing loosely.
Casting an Armor Piercing charm gets you Enfeebled.
Aegis will not shield you from that Enfeeble.
Casting a harmful charm that is not a Dispel makes him Cleanse-Charm all harmful charms off himself. Only Dispels stick.
If he ends a round with 6 or more Dispels, he clears them for a +300% pierce ward on himself. Do not over-stack dispels past 5.
The next round he summons a Runic Fragment.
Defeating him summons one more Runic Fragment.
With a Fragment on the field, any Dispel you place on him is pushed onto the Fragment instead. Deal with the Fragment to land dispels on him.

name: Millispeeder
cheats: "Your magic is useless here" - Casts an all enemies triple Dispel matching the School of the Wizard at the start of combat.
"There's no escape!" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, Millispeeder casts the same triple Dispel matching the School on all Wizards.
spellNotes: Casts a protected and unprotected Shadowblade.
strategy: Raid boss with 100% resist to every school, so you must break his resist with Shatter or heavy pierce and traps before damage lands. Bring a coordinated team.
He triple-Dispels your school at the start of combat and again every third round, so your school's spells get eaten. Attack with an off-school spell or a prism-converted hit that dodges the Dispels.
Have supports stack pierce and Shatter and traps, the hitter lands off-school damage once his resist is cracked.
Refresh your setup after each Round-3 Dispel wave, do not queue your main-school nuke into a Dispel.
Healer keeps the team alive through his 200% outgoing damage, burst him in the windows between Dispels.
cheatNotes: At the start of combat he lands a triple Dispel on your school, so your next same-school casts get eaten, attack off-school or through a prism.
He re-Dispels your school every third round, do not queue your main-school nuke into Rounds 3, 6, 9 and so on.

name: Mimic
cheats: [Polymorph] "A treasure of tricks" - At the beginning of Round 1, each Mimic will interrupt-cast a Moon spell and Polymorph into a random School of magic, which include the following forms.
[Fire] Mimic (Fire)
[Ice] Mimic (Ice)
[Storm] Mimic (Storm)
[Myth] Mimic (Myth)
[Life] Mimic (Life)
[Death] Mimic (Death)
While Polymorphed, they lose the ability to gain power pips, likely due to a bug.
[Late] "A surprise for you too" - If Wizards join the duel after it has begun, each Mimic will cast Steal Pips (Moon) which each steals one pip from all late Wizards.
Cheat still activates when Mimic has 7 power pips, but does not gain a pip even though text says "Gained 1 Pip!".
[Polymorph] "I can keep this up all day!" - At the beginning of every third Round, the Mimics will interrupt-cast their Polymorph spell.
strategy: Solo-friendly (4,000 health, stunable). Round 1 each Mimic polymorphs into a random school and, due to a bug, cannot earn power pips while transformed, so their damage output stays slow.
It has no resist, so hit with your strongest school and burst before the round-3 re-polymorph.
Stun to skip their turns and keep the pressure on.
Enter on time: late entry lets each Mimic steal a pip.
Blade up rounds 1 and 2 and finish before round 3.
cheatNotes: Round 1 each Mimic transforms into a random school; you will not know which until it flips.
While transformed they cannot gain power pips, so their attacks come slowly.
Wizards joining late each get a pip stolen by every Mimic.
Every third round they re-cast their polymorph.

name: Mina Clarke

stats.resist: 37% to all schools67% to all schools

cheats: [Steal] "You don't drain us, we drain you!" - If either Mina Clarke or her minions are hit with a Drain spell, the enemy that was hit will cheat-cast one of the following spells on themself:
If they are not defeated by the attack, they will cast an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade on himself.
If they are defeated by the attack, they will cast a version of Minor Blessing on themself that heals for 100% of their max health.

[Spell] "Is that the sun?!" - If either Mina Clarke or her minions are hit by a spell with a sun in it (such as Sun Serpent, Ra, or Climaclysm) and are not defeated by it, they will cast a protected universal dispel on themself.
strategy: Do not drain and do not use Sun-symbol spells. Both feed her cheats, so attack the plain way with blades, Feints, and a non-drain finisher.
Skip Death drains. Hitting Mina with a Drain either gives her an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade (if she survives) or full-heals her (if it kills), so a Scarecrow finish backfires.
Avoid Sun-symbol spells (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) unless they kill outright; a survivor self-casts a protected universal Dispel that eats your next hit.
She is Death with 67% Death resist and takes 30% boosted Life, so bring a Life hitter or Life prisms plus a non-drain damage school for the finisher.
Stack blades and Feint over a few rounds, then swing one large non-drain, non-Sun hit to burst her 16,190 Health.
With 6 starting pips and high resist she hits back hard, so keep a healer topped and blade only right before the swing.
cheatNotes: If Mina or a minion is hit by a Drain spell, that enemy self-casts in response, so keep Death drains out of your kit.
If the drain does not kill, she gains an Aegis-protected +100% Balanceblade and hits much harder.
If the drain does kill, she full-heals via Minor Blessing, erasing your progress.
If Mina or a minion is hit by a Sun-symbol spell (Sun Serpent, Ra, Climaclysm) and survives, she self-casts a protected universal Dispel that blocks your next attack.

name: Mister Utterson
cheats: Mister Utterson
"Time to burn it to the ground." - If Mister Poole regularly casts Fuel on a Wizard, then Mister Utterson will interrupt-cast a -35% version of Legion Shield.
"I'll follow your lead" - If Mister Poole regularly casts Fire Trap on a Wizard, then Mister Utterson will interrupt-cast a +35% version of Ice Trap on the same Wizard.
"We'll have hard words indeed!" - If Mister Poole regularly casts his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then Mister Utterson will interrupt-cast his Natural Attack on the same Wizard.
If this cheat triggers while Mister Utterson has no Pips, then his Natural Attack will act as though he has one Pip.
Mister Poole
"I'll match you, sir." - If Mister Utterson casts Iceblade, then Mister Poole will interrupt-cast a +40% Fireblade on himself.
"I'll follow your lead" - If Mister Utterson regularly casts Ice Trap on a Wizard, then Mister Poole will interrupt-cast a Fire Trap (+35%) on the same Wizard.
"Time to armor up!" - If Mister Utterson regularly casts Legion Shield, then Mister Utterson will interrupt-cast a Fuel (+35%) on a random Wizard.
"Let's have some hard words!" - If Mister Utterson regularly casts his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then Mister Poole will interrupt-cast his Natural Attack on the same Wizard.
If this cheat triggers while Mister Poole has no Pips, then his Natural Attack will act as though he has one Pip.
strategy: Two bosses that mirror each other's attacks: Utterson (Ice) and Poole (Fire). Expect their natural attacks and traps to double up on whoever they focus.
Hit Utterson with Fire (he takes +40%, resists Ice 70%); hit Poole with Ice.
Blade up and AoE to pressure both, healing through their paired natural attacks.
Nothing you cast directly triggers a punish here (their combos are between themselves), so play a standard blade-and-hit.
cheatNotes: Their natural attacks chain; if one lands its natural attack on a wizard, the other follows with its own on the same wizard, so that wizard takes two hits.

name: Mister Utterson (Challenge)
cheats: [Spell] Mister Utterson
[Blade] "I'll match you, sir." - Whenever Mister Poole casts Fireblade, then Mister Utterson will cast Icespear on himself.
[Trap] "I'll follow your lead" - Whenever Mister Poole casts Fire Trap on a Wizard, then Mister Utterson will cast Ice Trap on the same Wizard.
[Damage] "We'll have hard words indeed!" - Whenever Mister Poole casts his Natural Attack on a Wizard, then Mister Utterson will cast his Natural Attack on the same Wizard.
[Spell] Mister Poole

[Fire][Jinx] "Attack and follow through" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, Mister Poole will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (120 Fire Damage when including the Mister Poole's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.

[Fire][Ward] "I shall circumvent that!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Mister Poole will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (120 Fire Damage when including the Mister Poole's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.

strategy: This is a two-boss mirror: Utterson (Ice) and Poole (Fire) copy each other's blades, traps, and attacks, and both punish your wards.
Casting a harmful or helpful Ward makes Poole plant a delayed Fire hit (75, or 120 with his boost) that lands on all wizards after 4 Rounds, so minimize ward use or be ready to heal the delayed tick.
They feed each other spears and traps automatically, so expect matching Ice and Fire debuffs to stack on you regardless of what you do.
This is a low-Health Challenge (3,450 each), so blade a hitter and burst them down quickly rather than out-trading their reactive casts.
Utterson is Ice, so hit him with Fire; hit Poole with Ice, and try to drop them close together.
cheatNotes: When Poole casts Fireblade, Utterson answers with Icespear on himself.
When Poole casts Fire Trap on a wizard, Utterson adds Ice Trap on the same wizard.
When Poole lands his natural attack on a wizard, Utterson hits the same wizard with his own attack.
Casting a harmful Ward makes Poole plant a delayed Fire hit (75, or 120 with his boost) that strikes all wizards after 4 Rounds.
Casting a helpful Ward makes Poole plant the same delayed Fire hit on all wizards after 4 Rounds.
When Utterson casts Iceblade, Poole answers with Firespear on himself.
When Utterson casts Ice Trap on a wizard, Poole adds Fire Trap on the same wizard.
When Utterson casts Legion Shield, he follows with Fuel on a random wizard.
When Utterson lands his natural attack on a wizard, Poole hits the same wizard with his own attack.

name: Mithraya
locations: Celestia: Trial of the Spheres - Sanctum of the Sun
cheats: During the first Round, Mithraya will use her turn to cast a -50% version of Smoke Screen on all Wizards.
If a Wizard successfully casts a Spell, then Mithraya will interrupt-cast a -50% version of Smoke Mantle on the caster.
Pet Maycasts will trigger this cheat.

"The cards align! Now's your chance!" - If a Wizard has at least one Blade placed on them and Mithraya has at least one Trap placed on her, then Mithraya will no longer interrupt-cast Smoke Mantle upon successful casts.
strategy: Pack Storm hits (she takes +35% from Storm) plus accuracy enchants, and avoid Fire and Myth (35% resist). She cannot be stunned.
Expect the Round 1 -50% Smoke Screen on your whole team, so do not throw a big hit while your accuracy is down.
Turn off her interrupt loop fast: put one Blade on yourself and one Trap on her. Once both are up, she stops interrupt-casting Smoke Mantle on every cast.
With the accuracy loop shut off, stack the rest of your Storm blades and traps safely.
Finish with one enchanted Storm hit.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she hits your whole team with a -50% Smoke Screen, so expect early fizzles; carry an accuracy enchant before committing a big hit.
Every spell you land earns the caster a -50% Smoke Mantle accuracy debuff, so fizzle chance climbs the more you cast; set up in as few casts as possible.
Even a pet may-cast counts as a successful cast and stacks another accuracy debuff on you.
This is the exploit: get one blade on yourself and one trap on her and she stops interrupt-casting Smoke Mantle entirely. Blade up, trap her, and the rest of your casts land clean.

Kill Astraeus before its auras stack (they grow +10% per Round to +50% for it and -10% per Round for you), but hit after its death Supernova clears the board, not before.
Expect Ptolemos to Polymorph whoever kills Astraeus into a 300-resist form. Kill Ptolemos next to remove it.
With Sun mutates in hand, stack blades and Feint, then fire a mutated Sun hit on Mithraya.
cheatNotes: Killing either minion hands you Mutate spells that convert an attack to Sun damage, and Sun is the only school Mithraya does not resist.
Balance wizards get Mutate Ra to fire it as a Sun Ra.
Death wizards get Mutate Dragon for a Sun Dragon.
Fire wizards get Mutate Efreet for a Sun Efreet.
Ice wizards get Mutate Snow Angel for a Sun Angel.

Defeating Ptolemos removes the Polymorph. If Astraeus dies first, kill Ptolemos next to free the polymorphed Wizard.
On Round 1 Astraeus casts a +10% damage Reinforce on its team and a -10% damage Bulwark on all Wizards.
It repeats every Round, growing the auras 10% each Round up to 50%. Kill Astraeus before that gap gets large.
When Astraeus dies it Supernovas everyone for no damage, clearing auras and blades, so hit after that, not before.

Race him down fast; the incoming Storm damage adds up, so minimize setup Rounds.
Stack a couple of Myth blades and Feint, then end quickly with one Myth hit.

Every second Round he adds a 775-835 Stormzilla; between this and Leviathan, keep heals ready and end the fight quickly.

Casts a Shift Mockquistan Spell that deals Myth Damage and applies a -50% Damage Ward on itself.
strategy: Team fight (~16,000 health, three Bear minions): hitter, supports, and a healer. Hit with Storm (+30%); the Bears resist Myth 69%.

Nearly every action you take is punished by Happy Bear: Wards, Traps, Prisms, and Stun Blocks give the Bears Legion Shield, Charms give a +35% Bladestorm, and Heals give Pigsie. Keep setup minimal and deliberate, and heal only when needed.

Sunburn Bear runs a global cycle that triggers Moonbeam's, then Happy's, then Lucky's Natural Attacks, eventually landing all three the same round with Mass Hex, Mass Weakness, and Bladestorms. Pre-heal and shield before that burst turn.
Focus the support Bears, especially Sunburn and Happy, to shut down the globals and punishes before committing to Moonbeam.
Once the Bears are down or controlled, stack blades and finish Moonbeam with one big Storm hit.
cheatNotes: Moonbeam's Natural Attack hits a Wizard and lays a +50% Mass Hex on your team.
Sunburn casts Time of Legend as a global buff. You can overwrite it with your own global.
The round after, Sunburn triggers Moonbeam's Natural Attack.
Sunburn casts Balance of Power as a global.
The round after, Sunburn triggers Happy Bear's Natural Attack.
Sunburn casts Circle of Thorns as a global.
The round after, Sunburn triggers Lucky Bear's Natural Attack.
These stack up so all three Bears' Natural Attacks land together, then the cycle repeats. Expect a burst turn and heal and shield for it.
Replacing his globals is fine, but it does not stop the Natural Attack triggers.
Happy's Natural Attack hits a Wizard and lays a -50% Mass Weakness on your team.
Casting any Ward or Trap makes Happy cast Legion Shield on the Bears.
Prisms and Stun Blocks count as Wards here and also trigger the Legion Shield.
Casting any Charm makes Happy give the Bears a +35% Bladestorm.
Healing makes Happy cast Pigsie on the Bears. Time heals carefully.
Lucky's Natural Attack hits a Wizard and gives the Bears a +50% Bladestorm.
Each round Lucky picks one of several scripted spells; testing is unclear whether it is random or patterned.
On 'Ooh, unlucky you!' Lucky attacks a Wizard with one of the spells below.
On 'Lucky us!' Lucky casts one of the support spells below on a Bear.

name: Moonbeam Bully
locations: Krokotopia: Garden of Khonsu - Shrine of the New Moon
cheats: [Spell] "Vengeance empowers me!" - Each time a Wizard casts a Spell, Moonbeam Bully will cheat-cast one of the following:
Dark Tribute
Deathblade
Donate Power
Iceblade
Mass Death Trap
Mass Ice Trap
Mass Myth Trap
Moon Blade
Mythblade
An all enemies version of Moon Trap
strategy: Every spell you cast lets Moonbeam Bully cheat-cast a self-buff or a trap on your team, so cast as few times as possible: minimal setup, then one big hit.
Some of his cheats are Mass Death, Ice, and Myth Traps plus an all-enemy Moon Trap on your team, raising the damage you take; clear them or just burst before they matter.
Hit with Fire, which he takes +30% from; avoid Ice (73% resist).
Team fight at 19,450 health plus three Tut Tough minions: funnel blades to one hitter, keep support casts low, and finish with an AoE so the minions die too.
cheatNotes: Each time a wizard casts a spell, he cheat-casts one of the listed effects: blades and power for himself, or traps on your team.

name: Morganthe (Challenge)

stats.resist: 19% to all schools300% to all schools

cheats: "Feed the Darkness":
At the beginning of the first Round, Morganthe will give all Wizards two Shadow Empower cards:
[Shadow Empower]
At the beginning of the second Round, Morganthe will give all Wizards two Dark Nova cards:
[Dark Nova (Given)]
"Submit to Shadow" - At the end of every Round, Morganthe will cast a Shadow Natural Attack that deals 300 Shadow Damage or 600 Shadow Damage per Shadow Pip to all enemies.
strategy: Shadow rematch (48880) that resists every normal school 300%. Your regular spells barely scratch her; you win with the Shadow cards she gives you.
Round 1 she hands everyone two Shadow Empower cards; round 2, two Dark Nova cards. Use Shadow Empower to build shadow pips, then hit with Dark Nova (Shadow, only 19% resisted).
Every round she hits all wizards with a Shadow attack scaling per shadow pip (300, or 600 per shadow pip). Keep the team healed and do not over-hoard shadow pips into her scaling hit.
Coordinate: everyone Empowers, then unloads Dark Nova together for a burst that adds up across the team.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she gives everyone two Shadow Empower cards. Use them to build the shadow pips you need for Dark Nova.
Round 2 she gives two Dark Nova cards each. These Shadow hits are your real damage; she resists Shadow only 19% versus 300% for everything else.
Every round she hits all wizards with 300 Shadow, or 600 per shadow pip. Do not sit on shadow pips longer than needed and keep the team topped up.

name: Morganthe (Death)
cheats: [Manipulation] If a prism is placed on Death Morganthe (including Mass Prisms), she will cast a DoT on herself that deals 350 Damage over one Round, and use Cleanse Ward to remove the Prism. (DOT does not deal Death Damage).
[Global] Storm Morganthe places this DoT on herself after a Global is used.
[Damage] Ice Morganthe reacts to any enemy attacks.

[Damage] At the beginning of every fourth Round, Ice Morganthe will cast a Shadow Spell that summons Shadow Morganthe (the giant spider in the background) to hit all Wizards for 600 Shadow Damage at the same time.

If Ice Morganthe is defeated, Death Morganthe will take over casting the Shadow Morganthe cheat. If Death Morganthe is defeated, Storm Morganthe will cast it, and finally Balance Morganthe will cast the cheat if all other forms have been defeated.

[Damage] Each Morganthe has a Natural Attack, and a special personal attack. The Natural Attack sends spiders to wrap the Wizard in a web. The special attack morphs many spiders into one giant shadow spider (does not do Shadow Damage though). The following is a list of the special personalized attacks:
Balance Morganthe Attack
Death Morganthe Attack
Ice Morganthe Attack
Storm Morganthe Attack
[Shadow] When each Morganthe has 2 Shadow Pips, they will use their turn to cast the following Shadow Spells, even if they have existing pacts:
Balance Morganthe and Ice Morganthe will cast Dark Fiend.
Death Morganthe will cast Dark Shepherd.
Storm Morganthe will cast Dark Nova.

Because of changes to Shadow Creatures and updates, attempting to cast their respective shadow spells (with the exception of Dark Nova) will cause each Morganthe (except Storm) to always pass due to a targeting bug.
strategy: Run a 4-wizard team: a Storm or Fire hammer with 60%+ damage and Colossal/Epic-tier AoE, plus three supports who Feint and blade. The Storm player takes the last position in turn order to avoid Enfeeble debuffs.

Do not put prisms on Death Morganthe. A prism makes her Cleanse Ward it off and put a 350 DoT on herself, so it just wastes your setup. Keep Globals off the field too, since they punish Storm Morganthe for no gain to you.
Target Ice Morganthe. She is the one casting the every-fourth-round Shadow Morganthe hit for 600 to all wizards, and her roughly 27,390 health is what your one big hit must exceed.

Spend Rounds 1-3 stacking Feints on the minions and elemental/Storm blades on the hammer: Feint on Ice and Balance early, then Sharpened Elemental Blade, item blades, and TC blades funneled to the hammer while the hammer self-blades with Stormblade.
Round 4, fire Colossal Sirens off the stacked buffs. With full stacking it hits roughly 33,229, clearing Ice Morganthe's 27,390 pool and heavily denting the rest.
Watch the 4-round Shadow timer: if you cannot kill in one shot, the 600 shadow hit lands on the whole team, so keep buff discipline and be ready to heal through it.
cheatNotes: Putting any prism (including Mass Prism) on Death Morganthe makes her Cleanse Ward it away and take a 350 non-Death DoT on herself, so the prism is wasted. Don't prism her.
Storm Morganthe punishes Globals by dropping the same DoT on herself after any Global is cast. Keep Globals off the field to avoid feeding this.
Ice Morganthe counters your offense: she reacts to any enemy attack, so expect a response whenever you hit.
Every fourth round Ice Morganthe summons the giant background Shadow Morganthe to hit all wizards for 600 Shadow at once. Time your kill to land before this, or heal through it.
The every-fourth-round 600 Shadow hit does not stop when Ice dies. Death picks it up next, then Storm, then Balance, so killing one form just passes the duty along.
Each Morganthe has a normal spider-web Natural Attack plus a personalized giant-spider special attack (neither deals actual Shadow damage). These are her filler turns.
Once a Morganthe reaches 2 Shadow Pips she spends her turn on a Shadow Spell instead of her normal cast, even if she already has a pact active.
Balance and Ice Morganthe are set to cast Dark Fiend at 2 Shadow Pips, though a targeting bug usually makes them pass instead.
Death Morganthe is set to cast Dark Shepherd at 2 Shadow Pips, but the targeting bug usually makes her pass instead.
Storm Morganthe actually lands her Shadow cast, Dark Nova, at 2 Shadow Pips, so she is the one whose Shadow spell to respect.
Because of Shadow Creature changes, every form except Storm just passes when it tries its Shadow spell, so in practice only Storm's Dark Nova goes off.

name: Morganthe (Standard)
cheats: [Spell] "Feed the Darkness":
At the beginning of the first Round, Morganthe will give all Wizards two Shadow Empower cards:
[Shadow Empower]
At the beginning of the second Round, Morganthe will give all Wizards two Dark Nova cards:
[Dark Nova (Given)]
[Damage] "Submit to Shadow" - At the end of every Round, Morganthe will cast a Shadow Natural Attack that deals 300 Shadow Damage or 600 Shadow Damage per Shadow Pip to all enemies.
strategy: Big-health rematch (140,000) and a team fight. She resists 80%, so lean on the free shadow cards she gives you rather than your own school hits.
Round 1 take the two Shadow Empower cards she hands you and bank shadow pips; round 2 she gives two Dark Nova cards - your main damage.
Cast the given Dark Novas once you have shadow pips built to hit her hard through her resist.
Every round she Shadow natural-attacks the whole team for 300-600 per Shadow Pip she holds - keep the team healed and shielded and do not let the fight drag.
Funnel blades to hitters and time the Dark Novas together to burst her 140,000 down.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she gives every wizard two Shadow Empower cards. Use them to build shadow pips.
Round 2 she gives every wizard two Dark Nova cards - a shadow AoE. These are your main damage through her 80% resist.
Every round she hits all wizards with a Shadow natural attack scaling 300-600 per Shadow Pip she holds. Keep the team topped and end the fight quickly.

name: Morp
locations: Wallaru: Eucalyptus Forest - Great Barrier Gatehouse
cheats: "You shall not, you know, pass." - At the beginning of Round 1, Morp will cast Shadow Sentinel MOB, lasting three Rounds.
"It seems like you're still trying to pass." - If Morp is hit for the first time, she will Stun the caster.
"I thought we discussed this passing business." - If Morp is hit for the second time, she will Stun the caster.
"And now you're gone! I did warn you." - If Morp is hit for a third time, she will force all Wizards to pass, then use Exit Combat on each Wizard, which ends the fight.
strategy: You get only two hits before Morp ends the fight — her third hit forces all wizards to pass and Exit-Combats everyone, so you must kill her within two hits (ideally one).
Round 1 she summons a Shadow Sentinel (three rounds) that intercepts single-target hits, so spend rounds 1-3 stacking every blade, trap, and Feint while it expires.
Attack with Death (she takes +30%; she resists Life 71% and everything else 40%).
Once the Sentinel is gone, unload one fully-stacked Death nuke to kill her outright — each hit also Stuns you, so make it count.
Never chip her; every poke burns one of your two allowed hits.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she summons a Shadow Sentinel for three rounds that intercepts single-target hits — use rounds 1-3 to stack buffs and let it expire, or go around it with an AoE.
Your first hit on her Stuns you, so expect to lose the following turn — make that hit count.
Your second hit Stuns you again — you really only get one or two swings.
Your third hit ends the fight outright (all wizards pass, combat exits) — you must kill Morp within two hits, so build one fully-stacked Death nuke and drop it.

name: Moth-Amon
cheats: "Your tardiness offends me!" - If a Wizard enters the duel late, Moth-Amon will interrupt the duel and polymorph the tardy Wizard into a Polymorph Egg.
"Swarm to me!" - At the start of the duel, Moth-Amon casts a Moon spell to summon the following Minions.
Moth-Amon is not targetable while he has Minions. Single target hits are canceled on the first round, and AoE hits don't hit him.
Projection (Balance)
Projection (Life)
Projection (Storm)
"You're the fool, sorcerer! Now I will finish you at last!" - At the start of the duel, Mandar the Barbarian is summoned as an ally to aid Wizards in the fight.
If the Wizard in the first duel slot flees, this summon will disappear.
If the Wizard in the first duel slot summons a Minion, it will replace Mandar the Barbarian.
Wizards who cast after the initial caster can summon Minions without replacing Mandar the Barbarian.
"No blade can slay me!" - If a Blade or Global is cast by an ally or foe, Moth-Amon will cast an AoE Moon Enfeeble on all Wizards.
Pet 'May Cast' Blades will trigger this cheat.
Aegis can prevent them from being removed once.
Armor Piercing Charms will not trigger this cheat.
"No, my guards!" - The round after his Minions have been defeated, he'll shout this out.
Boss will become targetable the next Round after he says this.
strategy: Kill the three Projections first — Moth-Amon can't be targeted while minions are alive (single hits cancel, AoE won't touch him).

Don't waste blades early: any blade or global (yours or an ally's) makes him AoE Enfeeble all wizards, stripping charms. Protect with Aegis, use Armor Piercing charms (which don't trigger it), or blade and hit the same round once he's targetable.
Keep Mandar the Barbarian alive as your ally — the first-slot wizard should NOT summon a minion, or Mandar is replaced.
After the minions die he shouts and becomes targetable the following round — set up that round and burst.
Hit with Fire or Life (+5%); avoid Ice and Death (73% resist).
cheatNotes: Late wizards get polymorphed into a helpless Polymorph Egg — join on time.
He opens by summoning his Projection minions.
He's untargetable while minions are up — single hits cancel and AoE misses him, so clear the Projections first.
He also summons Mandar the Barbarian as an ally for you — keep him around.
If the first-slot wizard flees, Mandar disappears.
If the first-slot wizard summons a minion, it replaces Mandar — don't do that.
Wizards after the first can summon minions without losing Mandar.
Any blade or global cast makes him AoE Enfeeble all wizards — blade and hit the same round or use Aegis.
Pet may-cast blades trigger the Enfeeble too.
Aegis protects your charms from being stripped once.
Armor Piercing charms don't trigger the Enfeeble — safe to use.
The round after his minions die he shouts 'No, my guards!'
He becomes targetable the round after that — set up then.

name: Mother Ghulture
cheats: "Squawk!" - Mother Ghulture will shout this before the following cheats:
[Minion] At the beginning of the first three Rounds, she will cast a Shadow Spell to summon either a Death or a Myth Ghulture Minion.
[Afterlife] When the three Minions have been summoned, she will cast a version of Guardian Spirit for 33% Health restored after defeat on them.
Entangle can be used to dispel one Guardian Spirit per dispel.
[Late] If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, she will cast Luminous Weaver on the Wizard.
strategy: Expect a Ghulture minion each of the first three Rounds, then Guardian Spirit on all three (revive at 33% after defeat).
Carry Entangle to strip one Guardian Spirit per cast before you kill the minions, or AoE the whole board at once so they fall together.
Attack with Fire, Ice, Storm, or Myth. She only resists Life and Death (15%), so hit into anything else.
At 4,560 Health, blade once and AoE everything after clearing the Guardian Spirits.
Enter on time so you do not eat her late-join Luminous Weaver.
cheatNotes: On each of the first three Rounds she shadow-summons a Death or Myth Ghulture minion.
Once all three are out she gives them Guardian Spirit, reviving each at 33% Health after defeat.
Cast Entangle to strip one Guardian Spirit per cast before you kill the minions.
A wizard who joins late gets hit with Luminous Weaver.

name: Mother Sunn (Challenge)
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Crypt of Ra (Challenge)

stats.resist: 13% to all schools33% to all schools53% to all schools

cheats: [Round] "... You can die!" - If either Mother Sunn or Ghost Mander is not attacked during the Round, Mother Sunn will cheat-cast a Sun version of Ra (Tier 5a) that deals 460-540 Sun Damage to and applies a -65% Accuracy Charm on all enemies at the end of the Round.
Damage over Time effects will not prevent this cheat.
[Round] Cycle:
"Don't stay out in the sun too long." - At the beginning of every second Round, Mother Sunn will cheat-cast a Sun version of Scald that deals 375 Sun Damage over 3 Rounds to all enemies.

"In star's birth, you die." - At the beginning of every fourth Round, Mother Sunn will cheat-cast a Sun version of Power Nova that deals 665 Sun Damage to and applies a -40% Damage Charm on all enemies.
strategy: This is a 21,000-health Challenge boss, so run it as a team: a hitter, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer to manage her DoTs. She has no boost school and resists everything, with Storm the least (13%), so hit with Storm.
Attack Mother Sunn or Ghost Mander with a real hit every single round. If neither is attacked during a round, she Ra's the whole team for 460-540 plus a -65% accuracy charm.
A ticking DoT does not count as attacking, so keep a fresh chip hit going every round while your hitter builds.
Bring Triage for her every-second-round Scald (375 over 3 rounds) and cleanse the -40% damage charm from her every-fourth-round Power Nova before your big hit.
Stack blades and Feints while chipping each round, then finish with one large Storm AoE.
cheatNotes: If nobody attacks Mother Sunn or Ghost Mander during a round, she Ra's the whole team for 460-540 Sun damage plus a -65% accuracy charm. Land a real hit every round.
A ticking DoT does not count as attacking. You still need a fresh hit each round to dodge the Ra.
Every second round she Scalds the team for 375 Sun damage over 3 rounds. Bring Triage.
Every fourth round a Sun Power Nova hits for 665 and stacks a -40% damage charm on everyone. Cleanse it before your big hit.

name: Mother Sunn (Standard)
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Crypt of Ra (Standard)
cheats: [Round] Cycle:
"Don't stay out in the sun too long." - At the beginning of every second Round, Mother Sunn will cheat-cast a Sun version of Scald that deals 375 Sun Damage over 3 Rounds to all enemies.

"In star's birth, you die." - At the beginning of every fifth Round, Mother Sunn will cheat-cast a Sun version of Power Nova that deals 665 Sun Damage to and applies a -40% Damage Charm on all enemies.
strategy: Team fight (21,000 health, no minions). Hit with Ice (+30%); she resists Fire 59% and everything else 29%.
She hits the whole team with a 375 Sun Scald DoT every second round and a 665 Sun Power Nova plus -40% damage charm every fifth round.
Time your big hit before the round-5 Nova, or cleanse the -40% damage charm off your hitter afterward so your blades are not gutted.
Bring Triage for the Scald DoT and keep the team healed through the recurring AoE.
Funnel Ice blades to one hitter and unload between the Scald ticks.
cheatNotes: Every second round she casts a Sun Scald dealing 375 Sun over 3 rounds to all enemies.
Every fifth round she casts a Sun Power Nova for 665 Sun and a -40% damage charm on all enemies.

name: Mr. Cane
cheats: "Careful now. Mind the Hostages." - At the beginning of Round 1, Mr. Cane will cast a 6,000 "Restraints" Damage Absorb Virulence Aura for 99 Rounds on both Hostages.
The Aura can sometimes prevent Criticals from doubling Damage, or prevent Traps from being activated.
If Mr. Cane is defeated while either of the Hostages are still in combat, he will use a Sun version of Blessing, restoring 100% max Health to all allies, and will recast the Auras.
Removing the Aura will also trigger this cheat.

"Worthless hostages can't even stay captured!" - If a Hostage takes more than 0 Damage (more than 6,000 after the Absorb Aura) in one hit, Mr. Cane will force the Wizard to cast Exit Combat on the Hostage, removing them from combat.
Drain Spells will go directly through the Aura, triggering this cheat.
"I'll show you corruption!" - At the beginning of every third Round, Mr. Cane will cast Poison.
strategy: He takes +30% from Life and resists Death 62%. Hit with Life or a non-Death school.

The core mechanic: two Hostages each carry a 6,000 Damage Absorb aura. If you kill Mr. Cane while a Hostage is still in combat, he fully heals everyone and recasts the auras. Get the Hostages out first.
Remove a Hostage by dealing more than 6,000 in one hit (which force-exits them from combat), or use Drain spells, which pass straight through the absorb and trigger the exit.
Do NOT try to strip the Restraints aura; removing it also triggers his full-heal cheat.
Once both Hostages are cleared, focus Mr. Cane. Expect a Poison every third round; carry Triage or heal through it. Blade up, drain or burst the Hostages out, then land a Life hit on Cane.
cheatNotes: At Round 1 both Hostages get a 6,000 Damage Absorb aura. You must remove the Hostages before killing Cane, not just chip them.
That absorb can also stop criticals from doubling and block trap activation on the Hostages. Big single hits or drains are the way through.
Kill Cane while a Hostage is still in combat and he full-heals everyone and recasts the auras. Clear both Hostages first.
Removing the absorb aura also triggers his full-heal. Never strip it; remove the Hostages themselves instead.
A hit dealing more than 0 after the 6,000 absorb (over 6,000 in one hit) forces the Hostage to Exit Combat. That is how you clear them.
Drain spells bypass the absorb entirely and trigger the Exit Combat. Drains are the easy way to remove Hostages.
Every third round he casts Poison. Carry Triage or heal through the DoT.

name: Mr. Carver
cheats: [Life] Mr. Carver
Every Round, before casting a Spell, Mr. Carver will say one of the following lines:

"I will do whatever I can!" - Mr. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. Before his next turn, Mr. Carver will cast version of Imp that deals 85 Life Damage with no additional effects.

"Please, just help me, help yourself" - Mr. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. Before his next turn, Mr. Carver will cast version of The World that deals 930 Life Damage with no additional effects.
[Ice] Ms. Carver
Every Round after the first, before casting a Spell, Ms. Carver will say one of the following lines:

"I will never let my child go!" - Ms. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. After her next turn, Ms. Carver will cast version of Frost Beetle that deals 85 Ice Damage with no additional effects.

"You do not do enough!" - Ms. Carver will cast a Daybreaker-enchanted Spell on a random Wizard. After her next turn, Ms. Carver will cast version of The Hierophant that deals 930 Ice Damage with no additional effects.
strategy: Expect two enemies. Mr. Carver is Life and Ms. Carver is Ice, and both throw Daybreaker-enchanted spells at random wizards plus follow-up hits, so this is a team fight with sustained incoming damage.

Watch their tells. Carver's 'whatever I can' line means a small Imp (85) next; his 'help yourself' line means a hard The World (930) is coming. Ms. Carver's lines similarly telegraph a small Frost Beetle (85) or a big The Hierophant (930).
Keep the healer active. Damage comes every round from both Carvers on random targets, so spread-shield and pre-heal rather than reacting after the 930s land.
Play the schools: Mr. Carver is Life and takes boosted Death, Ms. Carver is Ice and takes boosted Fire. Split hitters or use prisms so both fall on the same burst.
Stack on one hitter, blade and Feint over two or three rounds, then AoE both Carvers together so neither is left to keep chipping the team.
Since both attack from turn one (Ms. Carver from Round 2), do not over-extend setup; burst on schedule before the 930-damage hits stack up.
cheatNotes: The 'whatever I can' line telegraphs a small follow-up: he casts an Imp for 85 Life before his next turn.
The 'help yourself' line telegraphs a heavy follow-up: he casts The World for 930 Life before his next turn.
The 'never let my child go' line telegraphs a small follow-up: Frost Beetle for 85 Ice after her next turn.
The 'do not do enough' line telegraphs a heavy follow-up: The Hierophant for 930 Ice after her next turn.

name: Mr. Montgomery (Challenge)
cheats: "You do not have that power!" - At the beginning of each Round, Mr. Montgomery will cast the following Spells:
On Round 1: Stormblade
On Round 2: Two Stormblades that stack
On Round 3: Two Stormblades that stack, Darkwind
On Round 4: Two Stormblades that stack, Darkwind, Balanceblade
On Round 5: Two Stormblades that stack, Darkwind, Balanceblade, Balanceblade (+35%)
On Round 6: Two Stormblades that stack, Darkwind, Balanceblade, Balanceblade (+35%), Dragonlance
On Round 7: Two Stormblades that stack, Darkwind, Balanceblade, Balanceblade (+35%), Dragonlance, Stormspear, Storm Lord (965) without the Stun.
After this cheat, Mr. Montgomery will cast a 100% max Health Damage version of Sacrifice on himself, defeating him.
"You cannot take it from me! You do not have the power!" - At the end of each Round, Mr. Montgomery will cast a version of Storm Lord (965) without the Stun that cannot be Dispelled.
"Send in more disposables!" - At the beginning of Round 6, Mr. Montgomery will summon 'Mr. Montgomery's Shadow, who becomes active on the next Round.
strategy: Team challenge, 100,000 health. This is a burn-and-survive race: he stacks blades every round and at the end of round 7 unloads a fully-buffed Storm Lord before Sacrificing himself.
Every round's end he lands an undispellable Storm Lord (965 Storm) on the team. The whole team needs Storm shields and heals stacked constantly.
Race his health down before round 7, or be prepared to survive the round-7 burst.
On round 6 he summons Mr. Montgomery's Shadow, active the next round. AoE it or keep pressure on the boss.
Funnel all blades and Feints to one hitter for a shadow-enhanced burst each window; the healer keeps everyone above his Storm Lord damage.
cheatNotes: Each round he self-blades, ramping his Storm damage. The longer the fight, the harder his hits.
By round 7 he is fully stacked and casts a huge Storm Lord (965, no stun). Have max Storm resist and heals ready.
Right after, he Sacrifices himself for 100% of his health, ending his turn, so the fight resolves at round 7 regardless.
Every round's end he lands an undispellable Storm Lord (965 Storm) on the team. Keep shields and heals up constantly.
On round 6 he summons his Shadow, active the next round. Expect a second attacker late.

name: Mr. Montgomery (Standard)
cheats: "You cannot take it from me! You do not have the power!" - At the end of each of the first four Rounds, Mr. Montgomery will cast Tempest (6 Pip).
"You do not have that power!" - At the beginning of each Round, Mr. Montgomery will cast the following Spells:
On Round 1: Stormblade
On Round 2: Two Stormblades that stack
On Round 3 and 4: Two Stormblades that stack and Darkwind
On Round 5: Two Stormblades that stack, Darkwind, and Tempest (6 Pip)
"Aargh- You do have that power!" - At the end of the fifth Round, Mr. Montgomery will cast a 100% max Health Damage version of Sacrifice on himself.
strategy: He kills himself at the end of Round 5 with a 100% max-health Sacrifice, so the whole fight is a survival check. You can win by simply staying alive to Round 5.
He stacks two Stormblades every round and casts Tempest at the end of each of the first four rounds, so his AoE gets bigger each round. Stack Storm shields and heal every round.
He is stunable and beguilable, so chain stuns or Beguile him to skip his turns and blunt the escalating Tempests.
If you would rather race him, he is immune to globals, so rely on blades and traps and burst him before his Round 4-5 Tempests land.
Either way, pre-shield for Storm on Rounds 4 and 5 when his damage peaks.
cheatNotes: He casts a 6-pip Tempest at the end of each of the first four rounds, its damage climbs as his Stormblades stack, shield for Storm each round.
Each round he stacks Stormblades (and later Darkwind) on himself, ramping his AoE, a good reason to stun or beguile him.
Round 1: one Stormblade.
Round 2: two stacking Stormblades.
Rounds 3-4: two stacking Stormblades plus Darkwind, his Tempest gets dangerous here.
Round 5: two Stormblades, Darkwind, and a Tempest all at once, peak damage, shield heavily.
At the end of Round 5 he Sacrifices himself for 100% of his health and dies, just survive to here and you win.

name: Mr. Stone

stats.resist: 46% to all schools76% to all schools

cheats: [Polymorph] "By sun's light, I am as stone" - At the end of the first and fourth Round, and every fifth Round after (1, 4, 9, 14, etc.), Mr. Stone will cast Power Nova on himself. This deals no damage and Polymorphs him into his Statue form for 2 Rounds.
When in this Statue form, Mr. Stone is immune to all damage.
[Damage] "*Low unhappy growl*" - If Mr. Stone is attacked while in his statue Polymorph, he will unpolymorph himself, making him vulnerable to damage again.

[Polymorph] "You sat idle like stones, so stones you shall become!" - If Mr. Stone’s Statue Polymorph expires naturally (meaning he was not attacked during the Statue phase), he will cast Medusa on the Wizard. This deals no damage but Polymorphs the Wizard into a Statue for 99 Rounds.

Polymorph has a deck of 77 Cards, all being Pass Cards, except for one Break Free (Myth) Card. The Pass Spells can be discarded and the Wizard may Pass normally to avoid a spell casting animation. The Wizard can be healed while polymorphed.
If all Wizards are already a Statue, Mr. Stone will not cast Medusa after his Statue Polymorph expires naturally.
This cheat can be dispelled with Vaporize.
strategy: He turns to Statue form and becomes immune to all damage at the end of Round 1, Round 4, and every fifth Round after (1, 4, 9, 14...), for 2 Rounds each time.
Attack him during the Statue phase to force him straight out of it, making him vulnerable again, so do not waste your big hit into the immune window.

If the Statue phase expires without you hitting him, he casts Medusa and turns a wizard to stone for 99 Rounds; you can still be healed while statued and can Pass through the 77-card Pass deck or play the single Break Free.
You can dispel the Statue polymorph with Vaporize to keep the pressure on.
He resists Ice 76% and other schools 46% but takes 30% more from Fire, so bring Fire; set up while he is stone-immune, then hit the Round he is exposed.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1, Round 4, and every fifth Round after, he casts Power Nova on himself, dealing no damage but turning to Statue form for 2 Rounds.
While in Statue form he is immune to all damage.
Attacking him during the Statue phase forces him out of it, making him vulnerable again.
If the Statue phase expires without you hitting him, he casts Medusa and turns a wizard to stone for 99 Rounds.
The polymorph deck is 77 Pass cards plus one Break Free (Myth); you can discard and Pass normally, and you can be healed while statued.
If every wizard is already a statue, he skips Medusa after the Statue phase ends naturally.
This Medusa cheat can be dispelled with Vaporize.

name: Ms. Felicity (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Great Sky Train Robbery Gauntlet - Great Sky Train Robbery Gauntlet (Tier 3)
cheats: "Blades off the table!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade on another Wizard, Ms. Felicity (Tier 4) will cheat-cast Disarm on the Wizard who received the Blade.
"Nobody likes a cluttered battle"' - If a Wizard casts a Trap, Ms. Felicity will cheat-cast Cleanse Ward on the target of the trap.
"Your invitation has been rescinded" - If Kenny Rooster (Tier 4)'s Health falls below ~20%, Ms. Felicity (Tier 4) will cast a Shadow Spell that instantly expels him from the duel.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Balance Damage.
strategy: Blade yourself, not teammates. Blading another wizard makes her Disarm the recipient; solo this is a non-issue.
Traps get Cleanse Warded off their target, so keep trap setup minimal or protect it.
She is Balance (resists Balance), so hit with an off-school or Spectral hit; stack blades and burst.
Note she will expel Kenny Rooster from the duel once he drops below about 20% health.
cheatNotes: Blading another wizard makes her Disarm the recipient, so blade yourself instead.
Any trap you cast gets Cleanse Warded off its target, so keep traps to a minimum or protect them.
She expels Kenny Rooster from the fight once he falls below about 20% health, so you may not get to finish him.

name: Mulleted Dr. Neaux (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Night Mire Gauntlet - Night Mire Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: "You're just in time to bask in my full, lustrous glory!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Mulleted Neaux will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.
"Argghh... I didn't ask for a trim" - If Mulleted Neaux takes more than 50% of his max Health, he will cast Choke.
"No matter, hair grows back!" - Mulleted Neaux will cast a version of Heartbeat that restores 1,500 Health every Round until he is fully healed.
"Glory be! My mullet is safe!" - Mulleted Neaux will respond with this when he's fully healed, restarting the cheat cycle.
"Fool! You must trim before you shave!" - If Mulleted Neaux is defeated before his trim cheat is triggered, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.
strategy: Arrive on Round 1. A wizard who joins late makes him Blessing back to 100% health.
He restores 1,500 health per round with Heartbeat until he is full, so a slow grind never works; build toward one burst window.
Do not try to kill him from full in a single shot. Defeating him before the trim cheat has triggered gives a full heal, so first chip him below 50% health to trigger Choke.
Once the trim cheat has fired, stack your blades and traps and finish him before Heartbeat tops him back up.
Clear or ignore the Hairball minions as needed while you set up.
cheatNotes: Arrive on Round 1; a wizard who joins late makes him Blessing back to full health.
Once he has taken over half his max health in damage he casts Choke. This is the 'trim' cheat you must trigger before you can safely kill him.
Heartbeat restores 1,500 health per round until he is full, so grinding him down never works; burst him in one window.
If you defeat him before the trim/Choke cheat has fired he Blessings back to full, so always chip him past 50% first, then kill.

name: Mulleted Dr. Neaux (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Night Mire Gauntlet - Night Mire Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: [Late] "You're just in time to bask in my full, lustrous glory!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Mulleted Neaux will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.
[Health] "Argghh... I didn't ask for a trim" - If Mulleted Neaux takes more than 50% of his max Health, he will cast Choke.
This cheat can be dispelled with Quench.
[Healing] "No matter, hair grows back!" - Mulleted Neaux will cast a version of Heartbeat that restores 1,500 Health every Round until he is fully healed.
"Glory be! My mullet is safe!" - Mulleted Neaux will respond with this when he's fully healed, restarting the cheat cycle.
[Afterlife] "Fool! You must trim before you shave!" - If Mulleted Neaux is defeated before his trim cheat is triggered, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.
strategy: Enter on Round 1. Arriving late fully heals him.
Cut his healing. He Heartbeats 1,500 per Round to full, so land Doom and Gloom or an infection before you commit.

You must trigger his trim cheat before he dies. Land one hit taking more than 50% of his health, which Chokes your attacker (clear it with Quench), then kill him next Round. Killing him before that hit revives him to full.
Hit Death or Storm, since he takes +5% from each, and never Life, which he resists; prism if you are Life.
Clear the two Hairballs with AoE and burst the boss in the follow-up.
cheatNotes: A late Wizard makes him Blessing himself back to full Health. Enter on Round 1.
Taking more than 50% of his max Health in one shot triggers Choke on the attacker.
That Choke can be removed with Quench.
He heals himself 1,500 per Round with Heartbeat until full, so bring an infection or Doom and Gloom to blunt it.
You must land the hit that triggers his trim cheat before killing him. If he is defeated before that cheat fires, he revives to full.

name: Mulleted Dr. Neaux (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Night Mire Gauntlet - Night Mire Gauntlet (Tier 3)
cheats: "You're just in time to bask in my full, lustrous glory!" - If a Wizard joins after the first Round, Mulleted Neaux will cast a Moon Blessing restoring 100% of his Health.
"Argghh... I didn't ask for a trim" - If Mulleted Neaux takes more than 50% of his max Health, he will cast Choke.
This cheat can be prevented by dispelling it with Quench.
"No matter, hair grows back!" - Mulleted Neaux will cast a version of Heartbeat that restores 1,500 Health every Round; (3,000 with Outgoing Healing) until he's fully healed.
Doom and Gloom will reduce the healed amount.
Entangle will prevent this cheat.
"Glory be! My mullet is safe!" - Mulleted Neaux will respond with this when he's fully healed, restarting the cheat cycle.
"Fool! You must trim before you shave!" - If Mulleted Neaux is defeated before his trim cheat is triggered, he will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his Health.
strategy: Life boss with a heavy healing gimmick; hit with Storm or Death (+6%) and avoid the schools in his 44% resist.
His Heartbeat heals 1,500 per Round (3,000 with his outgoing healing) until full; shut it down with Entangle, which prevents the cheat, and lean on Doom and Gloom or infections to cut healing.
Do not chip him past 50% of his max health carelessly; taking more than 50% makes him Choke you, which you can pre-empt by dispelling with Quench.
He also Blessing-heals to full if he is defeated before his trim cheat triggers, so work him down rather than bursting from full unexpectedly.
Enter on time; a late wizard makes him full-heal immediately.
Suppress his healing with Entangle and Doom and Gloom, stack Storm or Death blades, then finish him.
cheatNotes: Joining after Round one makes him full-heal; enter with the group.
Taking more than half his max health in one blow makes him Choke you; pre-dispel it with Quench.
Quench dispels the Choke before it lands.
His Heartbeat heals 1,500 a Round (3,000 with his boost) until full; block it with Entangle and cut it with Doom and Gloom.
Doom and Gloom lowers how much his Heartbeat restores; keep one active.
Entangle stops the Heartbeat cheat entirely; it is the key tool for this fight.
If you kill him before his trim and Choke cheat has triggered, he Blessing-heals to full; do not burst him from full unexpectedly, work him down.

name: Mykedee
locations: Empyrea: Aeriel Shores - Beastman Fight Club
cheats: [Late] "You ready to fight for your right to be tardy?" - If a Wizard enters the duel late, Mykedee will cheat-cast Metalops that leaves a +200% protected Death Trap on the Wizard and Beguiles the Wizard for three Rounds.
Cheat will also trigger if Yowck and Adrok are defeated, leaving Mykedee the last one in the duel, and it will trigger on all Wizards that aren't already Beguiled.
[Blade] If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell on another target, Mykedee will perform one of the following actions:
"No way, Wizard" - The first time, Mykedee will cast a Bad Juju on the target.
"This ain't no Charm School" - The second time, Mykedee will cast Virulent Plague.
"Third time ain't a charm!" - The third time, Mykedee will cast Metalops. After the third time, the cycle begins again.
Damage Spells with a secondary effect Blade, like on Savage Paw, will result in the action being cast on the target of the Damage.
All Ally Blade Spells will not trigger this cheat.
[Healing] If a Wizard casts a Healing spell, Mykedee will perform one of the following actions:
"No healing, it time to get ill!" - The first time, Mykedee will cast Infection on the Wizard.
"Feeling better? I fix that!" - The second time, Mykedee will cast Infection on the Wizard.
"Ha! Now everyone get ill!" - The third time, Mykedee will cast Mass Infection. After the third time, the cycle begins again.
"Hold it -- now hit it!" - After casting his Natural Attack, Mykedee will follow up with a Feint (+75%) on the target.
"Time for one last Beatdown" - Randomly, if Beets is in the battle, Mykedee will use his turn to cast Guardian Spirit (20%) on Beets.
spellNotes: Casts an all enemies version of Curse (95%).
strategy: Death boss (19825) with 100% incoming healing, so never heal him and expect drains to rebound. Hit with Life (+10%); never Death (75% resist).
Blading a teammate triggers a cycle on that target: Bad Juju, then Virulent Plague, then Metalops. Use All-Ally blade spells (Bladestorm etc.), which do not trigger it, or blade-and-hit in one round.
Do not chain heals: the first two heals get you Infected, the third Mass-Infects the team. Cleanse infections and heal only when needed.
After his Natural Attack he Feints his target for +75%. The hitter should avoid being the follow-up target, or clear it before the next big incoming hit.
Funnel blades to the hitter via all-ally spells and burst him. Watch for his Beguile on late joiners.
cheatNotes: A late joiner (or the last wizard standing) gets Metalops: a +200% protected Death Trap and a 3-round Beguile. Arrive on time.
Blading another wizard triggers a punish on that target. Use all-ally blade spells or blade the same round you hit.
First offense: Bad Juju on the target.
Second offense: Virulent Plague, cutting that wizard's damage.
Third offense: Metalops. Then the cycle repeats.
All-Ally blade spells like Bladestorm do not trigger this, so they are the safe way to buff your hitter.
Healing triggers an infection cycle on the healer or target.
First heal: Infection on you.
Second heal: another Infection.
Third heal: Mass Infection on the whole team. Cleanse and heal sparingly.
After his Natural Attack he Feints his target +75%. If that is your hitter, clear it before the next big enemy hit lands.

cheats: N. Bison

Daniel McCoil

Kenny Rooster

spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage.
strategy: Attack with Storm (+50%); N. Bison resists Myth 80%.
He confuses each wizard on round 1 and every 7 rounds for two rounds, so expect random targeting; AoEs care less about aim.
Do not blade openly: Ms. Felicity Disarms single blades off the receiver, though multi-target blades are safe.
Do not cast Auras: Kenny Rooster destroys them with a Natural Attack.
His minions chain-kill their own low members below ~20% health. Bring an AoE to whittle the low-HP gauntlet (Bison only 1,915) and finish everything together.
Be careful attacking Bison directly, since Psycho Deflection returns 20% of your max health. Shield or burst him in one hit.
cheatNotes: Late arrivals get confused for two rounds, so join on time.
He confuses every wizard on round 1 and every 7 rounds after for two rounds. Plan AoEs that do not depend on aim.
Attacking him returns 20% of your max health via Psycho Deflection, so burst him (only 1,915 HP) rather than poking.
Single blades get Disarmed off the receiver by Ms. Felicity, so use multi-target blades.
If Kenny Rooster drops below ~20% health, Ms. Felicity instantly finishes him; the gauntlet chain-kills its own low members.
Daniel hands every wizard a Free Sample card on round 1 and every five rounds.
If Ms. Felicity falls below ~20%, Daniel instantly defeats her.
Casting an Aura makes Kenny destroy it with a Natural Attack, so do not rely on auras.
If Daniel drops below ~20%, Kenny instantly defeats him.

With only 2,595 health and low-health minions, stack blades and drop a blade-boosted AoE to clear the whole gauntlet at once; attack with Storm (+20%).

"I'm afraid you're of no further use" - If Ms. Felicity's Health falls below ~20%, Daniel McCoil will cast a Shadow Spell that instantly defeats her.
