"The chickens come home to roast!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura, Kenny Rooster will cast a Natural Attack dealing 705 Damage, which destroys the Aura for 705 Damage.
"Time to fold 'em, partner" - If Daniel McCoil's Health falls below ~20%, Kenny Rooster will cast a Shadow Spell that instantly defeats him.
strategy: Team gauntlet (four enemies). Hit Storm into Bison (+5%); avoid Myth (70%). He's stunable and beguilable — control him during setup.
Attacking Bison makes him retaliate for 20% of the attacker's max health, so only hit him with shields up and when committing the kill.
Blade the hitter with multi-target blades (Elemental/Spirit) — Ms. Felicity Disarms single blades cast on another wizard and Cleanse Wards single traps placed on other enemies.
Don't use Auras — Kenny Rooster natural-attacks (705) and destroys them.
Expect two-round Confusion on the team from round 1 onward; heal and push through. The minions execute each other under 20% health, so focus fire and let the chain thin them.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get Confused for two rounds — join on time.
Round 1 (and periodically) he Confuses every wizard for two rounds — expect fizzles and misdirected casts.
Attack Bison and he retaliates for 20% of the attacker's max health — only hit him with shields up and when finishing.
Cast a blade on another wizard and Ms. Felicity Disarms the receiver — use multi-target blades instead.
Multi-target blades don't trigger the Disarm.
Place a trap on another enemy and she Cleanse Wards it off.
A multi-trap spell only loses one trap to her.
If Kenny Rooster drops below 20% health she executes him.
Round 1 and every 5th round Daniel gives each wizard a junk Snake Oil card.
If Ms. Felicity drops below 20%, Daniel executes her.
Cast an Aura and Kenny natural-attacks for 705 and destroys the aura — don't use auras.
If Daniel drops below 20%, Kenny executes him.

name: Narallor Nightborn
cheats: If Narallor Nightborn is damaged by any of the seven primary Schools of Magic, he will interrupt-cast a Blade of the opposite School used to attack him on himself.
[Fire] - Fireblade & Helephant
[Ice] - Iceblade & a version of Colossus that deals 460-540 Ice Damage
[Storm] - Stormblade & a version of Stormzilla that deals 650-730 Storm Damage
[Life] - Lifeblade & Centaur
[Myth] - Mythblade & Orthrus
[Death] - Deathblade & Wraith
Balance attacks will result in Narallor Nightborn receiving a Deathblade.
Each tick from Damage over Time (DoT) Spells can trigger this cheat, although he will apply the Blade after his regular turn as opposed to before if he goes second.
Dispels can prevent this cheat.
If Narallor Nightborn is damaged by the same primary School twice in a row, he will interrupt-cast a 0 Pip Damage Spell of the opposite School used to attack him on the caster.
Balance Damage attacks that are used twice in a row will trigger Narallor Nightborn to use Wraith on the caster.
Each tick from Damage over Time (DoT) Spells can trigger this cheat, although he will use the attack after his regular turn as opposed to before if he goes second.
Dispels can be used to prevent this cheat.
strategy: He takes +35% from Fire and resists Ice and Death 35%. Fire is your best school, but read his counters first.

When you damage him with a primary school he gives himself a blade and hits back with a matching creature (for example, an Ice hit brings Iceblade and a Colossus). Hitting with the SAME school twice in a row fires a 0-pip counter spell at you.
The clean solution is one big single hit to kill or near-kill, so his self-blade and counter never matter. Vary schools if you need multiple hits, and use Dispels to prevent the counter entirely.
Avoid DoTs; each tick counts as a hit and triggers the counter.
Blade up and drop one large Fire hit rather than pecking him repeatedly.
cheatNotes: Damaging him with any primary school gives him a blade and a counter hit. Do not peck him; one big hit avoids the fallout.
The Fire entry: a Fire hit results in Fireblade and a Helephant in the exchange.
An Ice hit brings Iceblade and a Colossus (460-540 Ice).
A Storm hit brings Stormblade and a Stormzilla (650-730).
A Life hit brings Lifeblade and a Centaur.
A Myth hit brings Mythblade and an Orthrus.
A Death hit brings Deathblade and a Wraith.
Balance attacks give him a Deathblade.
Each DoT tick counts as a hit and triggers the blade and counter. Avoid damage-over-time.
Dispels prevent this counter. Keep one up if you must hit repeatedly.
Hitting him with the SAME school twice in a row fires a 0-pip opposite-school spell at you. Alternate schools or one-shot him.
Balance twice in a row draws a Wraith on you.
DoT ticks also trigger this same-school counter.
Dispels prevent this one too.

name: Neberyx
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, Neberyx will cast a Moon School version of Insane Bolt on the Wizard, dealing 1,500 Moon Damage.
"Enough of this!" - Sometimes, if a Wizard casts a Damage Blade or Global Spell, Neberyx will cast a version of Earthquake that deals 600 Myth Damage.
Pet casts may trigger this cheat.
"How do you like this?" - Neberyx will say this when he casts his Natural Attack.
spellNotes: Natural Attack deals 100 Shadow Damage per Pip and applies a -40% Damage Charm on all Wizards.
strategy: Normally a team fight against a 44,000-health boss. Run a trap strategy: supports feed a single hitter, and any type of Feint is allowed here, which makes setup easy. Stack traps matching the hitter's school, keep blades light, and finish in one big hit.
Enter on round 1. A latecomer eats a Moon-school Insane Bolt for 1,000-1,500 damage.

Carry Vaporize. When you cast a damage Blade or a Global, Neberyx may interrupt with an enchanted Earthquake (600 Myth) that strips your blades; Vaporize clears the fallout and the cheat does not fire every time.
Bring Shatter to blow off any shields, and Cleanse Charm to clear the -40% damage charm his natural attack (plus Virulent Plague) stacks on the whole team.
A Myth wizard is useful if you want to carry charms into this fight, since Myth handles the Earthquake school and can pressure through his resists.
Stack traps and Feints on one hitter, keep blades minimal to avoid triggering Earthquake, then finish with one big hit.
cheatNotes: Any wizard joining after round 2 gets hit by a Moon Insane Bolt for about 1,000-1,500 damage. Enter on round 1.

Casting a damage Blade or a Global can make him interrupt with an enchanted Earthquake dealing 600 Myth damage that also strips your charms. It does not trigger every time, and Vaporize counters it, so lean on traps over blades.
Your pet's charm or global casts can also set off the Earthquake cheat, so be aware your pet may trigger it unexpectedly.
This is just his callout when he uses his natural attack, which deals 100 Shadow damage per pip and stacks a -40% damage charm (with a Virulent Plague) on all wizards. Clear it with Cleanse Charm.

name: Ned Collie
cheats: "I'll take that!":
If a Wizard casts a Positive Charm, Ned Collie will cast Steal Charm on the caster.
For any Spell that applies multiple Blades, only the first Blade is removed.
Spells that provide these as a secondary effect, such as Shadow Shrike, will trigger this cheat.
Aegis can be used to protect against this cheat.
If a Wizard casts a positive Ward, Ned Collie will cast Steal Ward on the caster.
If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time, Ned Collie will cast Snow Drift on the caster.
strategy: Hit with Life (+30%), avoid Death (67% resist), and note he pierces 27% and hits at +119% damage, so shield up.
He steals any positive charm you cast (Steal Charm, so he keeps your blade). Feint and other traps go on him safely, so lean on traps over self-blades.
When you do blade, use multi-blade spells (Elemental or Spirit Blade), he only removes the first blade so you keep the rest. Aegis fully protects a key blade.
Avoid positive wards (he Steal-Wards them) and heal-over-time (he answers with Snow Drift), use flat heals and resist instead.
Stack traps on him plus an Aegis-blade, then land one big Life hit.
cheatNotes: Cast a positive charm and he Steal-Charms it, taking your blade for himself, protect blades with Aegis or use traps instead.
On multi-blade spells he only steals the first blade, so an Elemental or Spirit Blade lets you keep the rest.
Secondary-effect blades like Shadow Shrike also trigger the steal.
Aegis fully protects a charm from the steal, put it on your key blade.
Cast a positive ward and he Steal-Wards it, skip shields on yourself and rely on resist.
Cast a heal-over-time and he answers with Snow Drift on you, use flat heals instead.

name: Nergal the Burned Lion
cheats: "My Shadow Fire will make you weak!" - On the first Round, Nergal will use his turn to attempt to cast Weakness on the last Wizard to enter battle. Every 4 Rounds after, he will use his turn to attempt to cast Weakness on whomever he is focused on.

"No more of that!" - Every other time that Nergal is attacked, if he is damaged by a Primary School of Magic, then he will cast a dispel of the same School that he was damaged with on the Wizard that damaged him.
Damage over Time (DoT) spells will not trigger this cheat.
If the School of damage is changed by a Prism, then Nergal will use a dispel that matches what the Prism converted the damage into.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Fire Damage.
strategy: Low health (3890), so set up and end it in one hit. He dispels your attacking school every other time he is hit, so a single big blow avoids the dispel.
Hit with Ice or Storm (+35%, resists Fire 80%). DoT spells do not trigger the dispel if you prefer that route.
Expect a Weakness on the last wizard to enter on Round 1 and every 4 rounds after; Cleanse it or blade over it.
If you prism your damage, he dispels the converted school instead, so account for that.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he Weakens the last wizard to enter, and every 4 rounds Weakens his focus target, so cleanse or blade over it.
Every other hit that damages him with a primary school gets you a dispel of that school, so land one big hit rather than chaining same-school spells.
Damage-over-time spells do not trigger the dispel, so DoTs are a safe alternative.
If you use a prism, the dispel matches the converted school, so plan your follow-up accordingly.

name: Nethellnax
locations: Celestia: Stormriven Hall - Storm Surge Cavern
cheats: [Heal over Time] "The Life Energy won't stop!" - At the beginning of every Round, Nethellnax will interrupt-cast Ha-ha! A 1-Up! on himself.
[Healing] If Nethellnax is defeated while his Heal over Time cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "I am not this, I am Death!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, and when Nethellnax revives, he gives Wizards the following Spell:
[Skeletal Pirate (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
[Death] "Is that one of my tricks?" - If a Wizard casts Skeletal Pirate (Tier 2b) on Nethellnax, he removes all Heal over Time effects on himself and stops his Heal over Time cheat.

"What... what just happened?" - Other Spells that remove Heal over Time effects for Weaknesses or Shields, such as Dr. Von's Monster (Tier 2b) or Primal Stampede, will trigger Nethellnax to remove all Heal over Time effects on himself and stop his Heal over Time cheat.
strategy: Hit with Storm or Death (he takes +30% from both) and avoid Fire and Myth (45% resist). He cannot be stunned.
He puts a Heal-over-Time on himself every round and revives at full health if killed while it is active, so raw damage alone will not finish him.
On Round 4, and on any revive, he hands your team a Skeletal Pirate (Tier 2b) card. Keep it.
Cast that Skeletal Pirate on Nethellnax to strip all his Heal-over-Time and shut off the HoT cheat, which kills the revive. Dr. Von's Monster or Primal Stampede do the same.
With the HoT gone, stack Storm or Death blades and traps and kill him for good.
cheatNotes: He self-casts a Heal-over-Time every round; on its own it is minor, but it powers his revive.
As long as that HoT is ticking, defeating him just revives him at full health, so you must remove it before the killing blow.
At the start of Round 4 and on every revive he gives your team a Skeletal Pirate (Tier 2b) card; hold onto it.
Cast that Skeletal Pirate on him to wipe every HoT and permanently stop the heal cheat; do this, then kill him.
Other HoT-strippers like Dr. Von's Monster or Primal Stampede shut off the same cheat if you do not have the card ready.

name: Neumian Lion
locations: Empyrea: Southwest Aero Plains - Meowl Den
cheats: "*ROAR*" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, the Boss will cast Impede and two Indemnity protected universal Traps for +25% and +35%.

"*GROWL*" On the first turn, the Neumian Lion will cast Tower Shield (Star) on itself, as well as a -50% version of Fortify. Aura will be re-cast on the last Round of its cycle. Supernova makes the Boss recast Aura.
"*GRAWWWW*" - Every tenth Round, starting on the ninth for the first cast, the Boss will cast Chimera (Star) on a random Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Balance; he takes +75% from it. Fire, Myth, and Life also work (+5%); avoid Ice, Storm, and Death (70% resist).

Clear the Round-1 Tower Shields with double Shatter. He opens with six -50% shields; two Shatters (or Shatter Treasure Cards if no Myth wizard is present) strip them. Once gone, he never recasts them.

Play around the -50% Fortify aura. Healing, blading, and trapping all re-trigger it, so accept it will be up and plan to punch through rather than remove it. Do not cast Supernova, which only makes him recast the aura.
Use Shadow Shrike to beat the aura. Have the hitter cast Shadow Shrike before the big hit; support can add Shatter on the boss.
Blade and Feint like a normal boss once the shields are down, then a few rounds in have the hitter Shrike and land the kill. He is stunable, so stuns can buy setup Rounds.
He carries an Aegis-protected Balanceblade, so a single steal won't take it. Keep a heal ready for the Round-9-then-every-10 Chimera cast.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets Impede plus two Indemnity-protected universal traps (+25% and +35%) on you; enter on time.

On turn 1 he puts up a Star Tower Shield (six -50% shields total) and a -50% Fortify aura, recast at the end of each cycle; casting Supernova forces an extra recast. Double Shatter removes the shields for good, and Shadow Shrike punches through the aura.
Every tenth Round (starting Round 9) he Chimeras a random wizard; keep a heal ready on that timer.

name: Nightwyng
cheats: "For the Bat" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Nightwyng will cast a version of Insane Bolt that deals 1,000 Moon Damage on the Wizard.

"I have trained under the Bat, a child of Grandfather Spider, I know more of Shadow Magic than you can imagine, can cast spells you've never seen, behold my power!!! Aw, nuts!" - When Nightwyng gains a Shadow Pip, he will attempt to cast Infinite Shadow, but it will always fizzle and uses a Spell that removes his Shadow Pip.
"This time I'll get it! Bam! Pow! Zowie!" "Aw, nuts!"- Nightwyng will state this every other attempt to cast Infinite Shadow after his first.
"Holy ineffectual wizard buffs!" - If a Wizard casts a positive or negative Charm, then Nightwyng will cast Steal Charm on the Wizard who cast the Spell. (Sometimes this cheat doesn't activate)
This cheat will only be triggered by the first Wizard to cast a Charm each Round.
Damage Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
AoE Charms will only remove from the caster.
"You can't trap a disciple of the Bat!" - If a Damage Debuff such as Weakness is placed on Nightwyng he will cast Cleanse Charm to remove it from himself. (Sometimes this cheat doesn't activate)
Using a Damage Debuff can sometimes trigger the Steal Charm cheat, as well as only trigger Steal Charm.
strategy: Shadow boss (7080). Hit with Myth (+10%); avoid Storm and Shadow (50% resist).
Free tempo: whenever he gains a Shadow Pip he tries Infinite Shadow, always fizzles, and loses the pip. He burns turns on this, so use them to set up.
Careful with charms: the first wizard to cast a Charm each round gets it stolen (Damage Globals count; AoE charms only lose the caster's copy). Blade and hit the same round, or Aegis the key blade.
Do not bother with Weakness-type debuffs; he Cleanses them off and may Steal Charm instead.
Stack a couple of blades and land a Myth hit. He is low health and wastes turns on his own fizzle.
cheatNotes: Late joiners take a 1000 Moon Insane Bolt. Be in on round 1.
Every time he gains a Shadow Pip he tries Infinite Shadow, always fizzles, and loses the pip. Free wasted turns for him, so use them to build.
The first wizard to cast any Charm each round gets it stolen. Blade and hit the same round, or Aegis your main blade.
Only the first charm each round is punished, so a second wizard can blade safely after the first draws the steal.
Damage Globals also count as charms for the steal. Watch what you open with.
AoE charms only lose the caster's copy, so a team blade still buffs everyone else.
Put a Weakness on him and he Cleanses it off. Skip damage debuffs.

name: Nimbari Exemplar
locations: Empyrea: Nimbus Citadel - Military Training Grounds
cheats: "You can't outsmart me!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, she will use Bad Juju (Shadow) on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts an Aegis-protected Deathblade, Stormblade, and Bladestorm;
Casts an Indemnity-protected Weakness and Mass Feint (70%/30%);
Deals 500 Storm Damage for 4 Pips max.
strategy: Join on time; her only cheat punishes latecomers with Bad Juju.
Attack with Myth or Life (+5%); she resists Storm and Death 70%. She is stunable and beguilable, so a stun buys safe setup rounds.

She self-blades with Aegis-protected Deathblade, Stormblade, and Bladestorm and drops an Indemnity-protected Mass Feint (70%/30%) on you, so her hits are big. Shield heavily and consider stunning her before she cashes in.
The Feint she places is Indemnity-protected, so clear it with Cleanse Charm or shield through the amplified hit.
Stack your own blades and a Feint, then burst her down between her stun windows.
cheatNotes: Her only cheat hits latecomers with Bad Juju, so just join on round 1 and it never matters.

name: Nordri
cheats: At the start of Round 1, Nordri will use his turn to cast a -65% Tower Shield on himself.
At the start of Round 2, Nodri he will cast a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself.
This Shield will be replaced every time Nordri is hit.
Spells such as Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward can be used to remove the Tower Shield, but will still trigger the "Tag" cheat.
"Tag! You are It!" - At the start of the fight, and every three Rounds thereafter, one of the Bosses will cast a Stun Shield labeled "Tag! I'm It!" on a random brother, "tagging" them.
On repeats of this cheat, the previously "tagged" Boss will have all Wards on him removed, including the "Tag! I'm It!" Stun Shield.
"Wrong brother! I'm it!" - Casting Spells at any Boss who is not "tagged" will trigger an interrupt-attack from the "tagged" boss. The attack varies depending on which boss is "tagged".
May Cast Spells will trigger this cheat.
Multi-target, all-enemies attack Spells will not trigger this cheat.
This cheat will stop when Nordri is defeated.
"Now where did my brothers go?!" - When all of Nordri's brothers are defeated, he will remove all Wards from himself at the beginning of the following Round.
"You hurt my brothers!" - Four rounds after all of his brothers have been defeated, and every three Rounds thereafter, Nordri will cheat-cast a version of Meteor Strike that deals 500 Fire Damage.
strategy: Use all-enemies AoE attacks — they completely ignore the 'Tag' cheat, so you can hit every brother at once without the tagged one interrupt-attacking you.
If you must single-target, only hit the currently 'tagged' brother; hitting an untagged boss triggers a retaliation from the tagged one.
Nordri self-casts a Tower Shield (-65% round 1, -90% round 2) that refreshes when hit, so pierce/shatter it or overpower it with a blade-stacked AoE.
Attack with Ice or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Death resist without a prism.
Aim to kill everything together with one big AoE, since clearing the brothers first makes Nordri drop his wards and start Meteor Striking.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he puts up a -65% Tower Shield on himself — pierce it or overhit.
Round 2 he upgrades to a -90% Tower Shield — small single hits are nearly useless; break it with Pierce/Shatter or a big blade-stacked attack.
He refreshes the Tower every time he's hit, so break it the same round you commit your big hit.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward remove the Tower but still trigger the Tag cheat — pair them with an all-enemies attack, not a single hit on an untagged brother.
Every 3 rounds a brother gets 'tagged' with a Stun Shield — that tagged brother is the only one safe to single-target.
Single-target attacks on a non-tagged boss trigger a counter-attack from the tagged brother — AoE avoids this entirely.
All-enemies attack spells don't trigger the counter — this is why AoE is the safe answer here.
Once all his brothers are defeated he drops all his own wards the next round, opening him up.
A few rounds after his brothers die he starts Meteor Striking for 500 Fire every 3 rounds — kill him before this ramps, ideally with the AoE that clears the brothers.

name: Noxious Gas Golem
cheats: "Alert: Noxious Gas Release Imminent" - At the beginning of the second Round, two Gas Elementals will join the duel, and will become active during the third Round.

"Initiate: Support Maintenance" - At the beginning of the Round after one of the Gas Elementals reaches 50% Health or lower, the Noxious Gas Golem will interrupt-cast Take Power on each of the Gas Elementals individually. Two Rounds after the Elementals are sacrificed, additional Elementals will join the duel, and become active on the third Round after this cheat occurs. This cheat will repeat endlessly until the Noxious Gas Golem is defeated.
If the Chief Whip is defeated before the Noxious Gas Golem, then a third Gas Elemental can join the duel when the Elementals are recycled.
If Wizards flee and then return to this area so that Gas Elementals are present from the beginning of the Duel, then Gas Elementals will not be recycled once defeated.
"Initiate: Defensive Screen" - At the beginning of every fourth Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will interrupt-cast Smoke Screen (-45%).
Chief Whip
"Chip up!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage Spell, there is a chance that the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Spirit Armor on the target of the attack.
If a multi-target Damage Spell triggers this cheat, then the Spirit Armor will be cast on whoever is in the first spot.
"Hold the line!" - Chief Whip can interrupt-cast Rebirth and revive fallen companions.
"I'll catch you up!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, then the Chief Whip will interrupt-cast Entangle on the Wizard.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Hit with Ice (+40%); the Golem resists Fire 70%.
Kill the Chief Whip early — it Spirit Armors whatever you attack (eating your hits), Rebirths fallen enemies, and Entangles you when you heal.
Gas Elementals keep recycling until the Golem itself is dead, so don't chase adds — focus the Golem and Chief Whip.
Expect a -45% Smoke Screen every 4th round; use accuracy enchants or heal off the fizzles.
Blade up and AoE Ice to hit the Golem, Chief Whip, and Elementals together.
cheatNotes: Round 2 two Gas Elementals join and go active round 3.
When an Elemental hits 50% health he Take-Powers them and cycles in fresh Elementals endlessly until the Golem is dead — kill the Golem, don't chase adds.
Every 4th round the Golem casts a -45% Smoke Screen — bring accuracy.
When you cast a damage spell the Chief Whip may Spirit Armor your target, absorbing the hit — kill the Whip to stop this.
On a multi-target hit the Spirit Armor lands on the first spot.
The Chief Whip can Rebirth to revive fallen enemies.
Heal and the Chief Whip Entangles you — another reason to drop it first.
Pet-cast heals trigger the Entangle too.

name: Noxious Gas Golem (Challenge)
cheats: Noxious Gas Golem

[Fire][Jinx] - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (110 Fire Damage when including the Golem's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.

[Fire][Ward] - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast a Fire Spell that deals 75 Fire Damage (110 Fire Damage when including the Golem's Damage boost) after 4 Rounds to all Wizards.
[Round][Minion] "Alert: Noxious Gas Release Imminent!" - At the beginning of the first Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will summon two Gas Elementals, which become active during the second Round.
[Round][Trap] "Initiate: Defensive Screen" - At the end of the second Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast Mass Fire Trap.
[Round][Damage] "Initiate: Offense!" - At the end of the third Round, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast Meteor Strike.
Chief Whip
[Life][Jinx] - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, the Chief Whip will cast a Life Spell that restores 75 Health over 3 Rounds to himself.
[Life][Damage over Time] - Whenever a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect, the Chief Whip will cast a Life Spell that restores 75 Health over 3 Rounds to himself.
[Minion][Afterlife] "Hold the line!" - The first time that an enemy is defeated, the Chief Whip will cast a Spell that restores 15% max Health to all allies, reviving the defeated enemy.
strategy: Avoid casting Wards. Any Ward, harmful or helpful, makes the Golem set a delayed ~110 Fire AoE for 4 rounds later, and a harmful Ward also heals the Chief Whip.
Skip DoTs as well; they heal the Chief Whip.
Watch the Golem's timer: two Gas Elementals summon at round 1 (active round 2), Mass Fire Trap lands at the end of round 2, and Meteor Strike at the end of round 3. Plan to burst before the Meteor.
The Chief Whip revives the first defeated enemy once and heals the team 15%, so expect one enemy to come back, then clean up.
Hit with an AoE to clear the Golem and Elementals together; the boss only has 3,430 health.
cheatNotes: Casting a harmful Ward makes the Golem set a Fire hit that lands ~110 on all wizards 4 rounds later.
Casting a helpful Ward triggers the same delayed ~110 Fire hit on all wizards.
At round 1 the Golem summons two Gas Elementals that become active in round 2.
At the end of round 2 the Golem casts Mass Fire Trap on the wizards.
At the end of round 3 the Golem casts Meteor Strike.
Casting a harmful Ward also heals the Chief Whip 75 over 3 rounds.
Casting a DoT heals the Chief Whip 75 over 3 rounds.
The first time an enemy is defeated, the Chief Whip heals all allies 15% and revives that enemy, once.

name: Numbskull Chieftain
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, the Numbskull Chieftain will interrupt to place a +30% Spirit Trap on the Wizard.

"Don't do that!" / "I'm warning you." / "I warned you!" - If a Wizard casts a Charm Spell (e.g. Blades), he will shout a warning. After the third Charm is cast, he will cheat-cast Chivalrous Centaur on the Wizard who cast the Spell, then proceed to destroy three Charms on the Wizard.
Pet Blades will trigger this cheat.
"Do your worst!" - If a Trap is placed on the Numbskull Chieftain, he will cheat-cast Legend Shield and Dream Shield on himself.
"Heals? I can play that game too!" - If a Healing Spell is used, his Pet will cheat-cast Guiding Light on him.
Pet Heals will trigger this cheat.
"I hunger for your pain!" - He will cast Hungry Caterpillar.
strategy: Life boss (10,240). Hit with Death or a non-Life school (no boost is listed, so use your strongest off-Life hitter or a prism).
Charm limit: he warns on each charm and after your THIRD charm he Chivalrous Centaurs you and destroys three of your charms (pet blades count). Keep to two blades, then hit.
Do not trap him; a trap makes him Legend Shield and Dream Shield himself.
Avoid heals: a healing spell (pet heals too) makes his pet Guiding Light him. Pre-shield instead of healing during setup.
Two blades, no traps, then land a big hit; watch for his Hungry Caterpillar DoT.
cheatNotes: Late arrival gets a +30% Spirit Trap slapped on you. Zone in on time.
After your third charm he Chivalrous Centaurs you and destroys three of your charms. Stop at two blades, then hit.
Pet-cast blades count toward that third-charm trigger. Watch your pet.
Trapping him makes him Legend Shield and Dream Shield himself. Skip traps and rely on blades.
Any heal makes his pet Guiding Light him. Pre-shield during setup instead of healing.
Pet heals also trigger the Guiding Light. Turn off healing pets here.
He casts Hungry Caterpillar for DoT pressure. Carry Triage or heal after his charm count resets.

name: Nyx Night Bringer (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Forbidden Library Gauntlet - Forbidden Library Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: [Global] "Nightfall is inevitable!"' - At the start of combat, Nyx Night Bringer casts a version of Doom and Gloom (Moon).
[Harmful Aura] "The cloak of night obscures all sight!" - At the end of Round 1, Nyx Night Bringer will cast Darkness: -50% Accuracy Acrimony Auras on all Wizards for 99 Rounds.
[Damage] "The worst monsters prowl at night!" - Casts a versions of Dark Nova, Dark Fiend & Dark Shepherd that immediately attack, or a Natural Attack that also steals pips.

name: Oblongata

stats.resist: 44% to all schools64% to all schools84% to all schools

cheats: "Ice freezes Storm." - If a Wizard casts a Blade, Oblongata will cast Wall of Blades or Oni's Destruction.
"Ice survives Death." - If a Wizard casts a Weakness or Dispel, Oblongata will cast Glacial Fortress or Jinn's Vexation.

"Don't... mind if I do!" - Oblongata will cast either a helpful Charm, or helpful Ward, or Heal over Time effect on himself, or a harmful Charm, or harmful Ward, or Damage over Time effect on a random Wizard. These effects do not interact with any Damage Spells, and the Heal and Damage over Time effects restore no Health and deal no Damage, and last forever, they are labelled "Brain Power".

Removing one of these effects with one of the following Spells will cause Oblongata to apply a +200% Damage Ward on himself. This 200% Trap is unaffected by Stack Overflow, so it can be a 5th Trap, however if you apply a 6th Trap (including from Jinn's Reversal), it will be removed by Stack Overflow.
Jinn's Affliction
Jinn's Defense
Jinn's Larceny
Jinn's Restoration
Jinn's Reversal
Jinn's Vexation
spellNotes: Casts a Balance Spell that gives all enemies a +40% Pierce Trap.
strategy: Raid boss, 90,000 health, coordinated team. He resists most schools heavily; Fire and Myth are only 44% resisted while Ice, Life, Storm, Death, and Balance are 64 to 84%. Check the resist line and prism accordingly.
Centerpiece exploit: he randomly puts 'Brain Power' effects on himself or wizards. Remove one with a Jinn's spell and he slaps a +200% Damage Trap on himself, a massive free trap for your hitter.
That +200% trap ignores Stack Overflow and can be your 5th trap, but keep the total at 5. A 6th trap triggers Stack Overflow and removes it.
Minimize loose Blades (he answers with Wall of Blades or Oni's Destruction) and stray Weakness/Dispel (he answers with Glacial Fortress or Jinn's Vexation).
Funnel blades to the hitter and fire through the +200% trap for a raid-sized burst; the healer sustains the team.
cheatNotes: Casting a Blade makes him shield up with Wall of Blades or Oni's Destruction. Do not over-blade.
Casting a Weakness or Dispel makes him cast Glacial Fortress or Jinn's Vexation.
He randomly applies 'Brain Power' effects that deal and heal nothing and last forever. Harmless on their own, but the setup for the exploit.
Remove a Brain Power effect with a Jinn's spell and he puts a +200% Damage Trap on himself. It survives as a 5th trap, but a 6th trap triggers Stack Overflow and removes it.

name: Old Quack (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade] "That's not the real you!" - If a Wizard casts a universal Helpful Charm, or a multi-blade Spell, such as Elemental Blade, Old Quack will cast an Ice version of Enfeeble on the receiver of the Blade.
If a multi-target Blade Spell is cast, such as Bladestorm, Old Quack will cast an all enemies version of Enfeeble.
A wizard casting a blade-type spell that is not of their original school will also trigger this cheat.
Spells with secondary effects, such as Savage Paw, will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis will not prevent the removal of Blades.
strategy: Solo-friendly (12,515 health). Blading is punished: universal blades, multi-blades, or any blade of a school other than your own draw Ice Enfeeble, and Aegis will not protect it.
Use only single mono-school blades of your own school; blades with secondary effects like Savage Paw are also safe.
Hit with Fire (he takes boosted Fire damage and resists Ice); use a Fire prism if you are an Ice wizard.
Set up with safe blades and a Feint, then land one big Fire hit.
Avoid Elemental, Spirit, and universal blades entirely.
cheatNotes: Casting a universal blade or a multi-school blade like Elemental Blade gets the recipient hit with Ice Enfeeble; use single mono-school blades of your own school.
Multi-target blades like Bladestorm draw an all-enemies Enfeeble.
Even a normal blade of a school other than your own triggers it.
Blades with secondary effects like Savage Paw are safe from this cheat.
Aegis will not save a blade here; it gets removed anyway.

strategy: Team fight. Hit with Fire (+30%), never Ice (76% resist).
Use single-school blades of your OWN school. Universal charms (Balanceblade, elemental), multi-blade spells (Elemental Blade), and off-school blades all make him Enfeeble the blades off the receiver.
Multi-target blade spells (Bladestorm) make him Enfeeble the whole team, so avoid group-blading, blade wizards individually with same-school blades.
Secondary-effect blades like Savage Paw do not trigger the cheat, so they are a safe way to buff.
Aegis will not stop the Enfeeble, so do not rely on it. Funnel clean same-school blades to the hitter and land one big Fire hit.
cheatNotes: Cast a universal charm (Balanceblade) or a multi-blade spell (Elemental Blade) and he Enfeebles the blades off whoever received them, use single same-school blades instead.
Multi-target blade spells like Bladestorm make him Enfeeble the entire team, never group-blade him.
Blading with a charm outside your own school also triggers the Enfeeble.
Secondary-effect blades like Savage Paw do NOT trigger it, a safe way to buff.
Aegis will not save your blades from this Enfeeble, so do not rely on it.

name: Omen Stribog
locations: Polaris: River of Frozen Tears - Omen's Hideout
cheats: Omen Stribog
"Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has already started, then Omen will cast an enchanted version of Mana Burn dealing 1,500 base Damage.
Unbalance can be used to bypass this cheat.
"We are not easily fooled" - If a Wizard casts Feint on any enemy, then Omen will then remove the Feint from himself or his allies, unless it's protected.
Mass Feint will remove the trap placed on the user of the spell.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this.
"You are darkened by the Shadow as well?" - If a Wizard casts a Shadow Creature Spell, then Omen will cast a Moon Spell that adds +40 Backlash to the Wizard.
Because of updates to Shadow mechanisms this cheat no longer increases Backlash beyond 30.
If Omen is the last Boss remaining, he will cast these variations of Amplify. (Needs clarification on how often Aura changes)
+10% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+20% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+30% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+40% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
+50% outgoing Damage for 99 Rounds.
This Aura has the appearance of Berserk.
If a Wizard removes the Aura, he will recast it.
Dark Sentinel
At the beginning of the first Round, Dark Sentinel will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Shadow Sentinel, which intercepts 75% Damage, lasts 99 Rounds, and has no Backlash.
If Dark Sentinel is revived from a Heal All Spell, then it will not cheat-cast Shadow Sentinel.
"Your parlor tricks are to no avail!" - If a Wizard casts any type of Charm or Global Spell, Dark Sentinel will cheat-cast Enfeeble (Shadow) on both the caster and receiver.
If a Wizard casts a Blade on themself, then the Dark Sentinel will cast Enfeeble on them twice.
Negative charms such as Entangle will result in all blades being removed from the targeted opponent as well as the caster.
If a multi-target Spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), then the Dark Sentinel will only remove the Blade from the Wizard who cast the Spell.
If a Wizard casts any type of Ward Spell, there is a chance that Dark Sentinel will cheat-cast Steal Ward on the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Dark Seraph
Whenever the Dark Seraph uses a Healing Spell, an Absorb will be placed on all enemies, similar to the Shadow Seraph effect.
spellNotes: Shadow Decrease Backlash gives -10 Backlash to any Shadow Spells on himself.
Deals 100 Shadow Damage per Pip despite the Spell's 3-Pip maximum description.
The Natural Attack is a Steal that heals 100% of the Damage dealt.
strategy: Fight this as a full team of four and join on the very first round. Anyone who joins late eats an enchanted Mana Burn for 1,500 base damage; casting Unbalance can bypass that penalty.

Kill the Dark Sentinel first. At the start of round 1 it cheat-casts an enhanced Shadow Sentinel that intercepts 75% of your damage for 99 rounds, so nothing sticks to the real targets until it is dead. Note: if you revive it with a Heal All, it will NOT re-cast the Sentinel.

Assign roles by column as the guide's chart shows: a Fire hitter is ideal for Backdraft traps, a Balance support for Hex traps and Bladestorms, plus a dedicated healer. Spend rounds 1-2 loading traps.

Handle blades carefully. If anyone casts a charm or global, the Dark Sentinel cheat-casts Enfeeble on caster and target (twice if you self-blade), so protect single-target blades with Aegis and have everyone else use Bladestorm instead of self-blades.
Feint must be Indemnity-enchanted, since Omen removes any unprotected Feint from himself and allies. The Balance version of Feint does not trigger the removal at all.
Never cast a Shadow Creature spell; Omen answers with a Moon spell adding backlash to that wizard (capped at 30 now). Also expect the Dark Seraph to drop an Absorb on all enemies whenever it heals.

Build to a burst: around round 4 Shatter and Aegis-blade, round 5 stack blades, then round 6 land the big AoE hit while feints are up. If Omen is the last one alive he stacks a Berserk-looking Amplify aura (+10% up to +50% damage) and recasts it if removed, so close him out fast rather than trading turns.
cheatNotes: Join before the duel starts. A wizard who runs in after combat begins gets hit with an enchanted Mana Burn for 1,500 base damage, draining pips and health.
Casting Unbalance neutralizes the late-join Mana Burn cheat if someone does arrive late.
Any unprotected Feint you place on Omen or his allies gets stripped off immediately. Enchant your Feint with Indemnity so it survives.
Mass Feint is punished by also removing the trap from the wizard who cast it, so it is not a safe way to feint here.
The Balance-school version of Feint is the exception: it does not trigger Omen's feint-removal cheat at all.
If you cast a Shadow Creature spell, Omen replies with a Moon spell that adds backlash to you. Avoid Shadow creature hits.
That backlash cheat is now capped and will not push your backlash past 30, so it is less punishing than it used to be.
When Omen is the only boss left he buffs himself with an Amplify aura; the exact tier and refresh timing vary.
The self-buff aura looks like Berserk but is purely an outgoing damage boost, without Berserk's incoming-damage downside.
Removing Omen's damage aura is wasted effort because he simply recasts it. Just race him down instead.

At the start of round 1 the Dark Sentinel shields the enemy team with an enhanced Shadow Sentinel that soaks 75% of incoming damage for 99 rounds. Kill the Sentinel first so your hits land on the real targets.
If you accidentally revive the Dark Sentinel with a Heal All spell, it will not re-cast its Shadow Sentinel, so a revived Sentinel is far less of a problem.
Casting any charm (blade) or global spell makes the Dark Sentinel Enfeeble both the caster and the receiver. Minimize charm and bubble casts near the Sentinel.
Self-blading is especially punished: the Sentinel casts Enfeeble on you twice. Use Aegis-protected blades from teammates or Bladestorm instead.
Negative charms like Entangle backfire, stripping every blade off both the target and the caster. Avoid casting negative charms on the enemies.
Multi-target buffs like Bladestorm are safer: the Sentinel only removes the blade from the caster, not the whole team, so most of your blades survive.
Casting any ward or shield risks the Dark Sentinel stealing that ward off you. Do not rely on shields for defense here.
Whenever the Dark Seraph heals, it also drops an Absorb on all enemies, adding a damage shield you must chew through. Expect to overhit or strip it around Seraph heals.

Use Feints and traps instead of charms where you can, since traps do not trigger the charm cheat.
It is Balance with 35% Balance resist and takes 35% boosted Myth, Life, and Death, so a Myth, Life, or Death hitter is ideal.

name: Orrick Nightglider
locations: Wizard City: Colossus Boulevard - Briskbreeze Tower
cheats: If a Trap is placed on Orrick, he will use Cleanse Ward on himself to remove one Trap on himself.
Prisms and mass Traps, such as Windstorm, do not trigger this cheat.
This cheat can be Dispelled or prevented with Indemnity.

Every 3 Rounds, while Orrick's health is above 4,000, he will interrupt-cast a Heck Hound on the Wizard dealing 1,820 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds. This is the greatest threat in this battle. This cheat can be Dispelled.
This Heck Hound will still be cast on a Wizard even if Orrick is Beguiled.
Starting on Round 4, Orrick will interrupt with Meteor Strike dealing 750 Fire Damage.
This cheat can be Dispelled.
Beguile can delay this cheat by one round when he has no allies.
Under 4,000 Health:
When Orrick's health drops below 4,000 during a round, then three Stompers join the duel at the end of the round and become active 2 rounds later.
If Wizards flee and return, the Stompers will still remain.
When Orrick's health drops below 4,000, he will use Meteor Strike (750) at the start of every 3 Rounds once the current four round cycle completes.
When Orrick's health is between 4,000 and 2,000, he will interrupt to use a -65% Tower Shield on himself whenever he is damaged.
Drain Spells do not trigger this cheat.
If Orrick is hit with a multi-hit spell, he will use Tower Shield (-65%) on himself for every hit.
This also includes Damage over Times, which will be cast after his turn ends.
Under 2,000 Health:

At the start of the round Orrick's health drops below 2,000, Orrick will interrupt with an Earthquake that only deals 750 Myth Damage. Spell doesn't remove charms or wards. This cheat can be Dispelled.
In addition every 3 Rounds, while Orrick's health is below 2,000, he will interrupt-cast a Heck Hound on the Wizard dealing 1,820 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds.
strategy: Death boss: hit with Life (+50%); Death is resisted 80%. He is stunable if you need to buy a round.
The biggest danger is Heck Hound (1,820 Fire over 3 rounds) every 3 rounds while he is above 4,000, and again below 2,000. Pack Triage or Fire resist, or Dispel it.
Single traps get Cleanse Warded off him, so set up with prisms or mass traps like Windstorm, or lock a trap in with Indemnity.
Between 4,000 and 2,000 health he Tower Shields (-65%) every time he takes damage, and multi-hit spells shield him per hit, so hit with one big single Life spell, not chip or multi-hit.
Dropping him below 4,000 spawns three Stompers that go active two rounds later, so plan your burst to blow through 4,000 down toward 2,000 in one hit.
Below 2,000 expect a 750 Myth Earthquake and another Heck Hound; finish him quickly.
cheatNotes: Single traps get Cleanse Warded off him, so set up with prisms or mass traps, or lock a trap in with Indemnity.
Prisms and Windstorm-style mass traps do not trigger the Cleanse, so they are your safe trap options.
You can Dispel the Cleanse cheat or block it with Indemnity.
Heck Hound deals 1,820 Fire over 3 rounds every 3 rounds while he is above 4,000, the fight's biggest threat; Dispel it or carry Triage.
Beguiling him does not stop the Heck Hound from landing on a wizard.
From Round 4 he interrupts with Meteor Strike for 750 Fire.
That Meteor Strike can be Dispelled.
Beguile buys one round of delay on it when he has no minions out.
Dropping him below 4,000 brings in three Stompers that go active two rounds later; plan for the adds before you cross that line.
Fleeing and returning will not clear the Stompers.
Below 4,000 he resumes Meteor Strike every 3 rounds once the cycle resets.
Between 4,000 and 2,000 he Tower Shields (-65%) every time he takes damage; hit him with one big spell, not chip damage.
Drain spells do not trigger that Tower Shield.
Multi-hit spells such as Minotaur give him a shield per hit, so avoid them in this health range.
Damage-over-time also triggers the shield, applied after his turn.
Below 2,000 he interrupts with a 750 Myth Earthquake that leaves your charms and wards alone; it can be Dispelled.
Below 2,000 the 1,820 Heck Hound returns every 3 rounds, so burst him out of this range fast.

Global Spells will trigger this cheat, but Aura Spells will not.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A ward! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Trap (including Feint) or a Shield will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
The text for this cheat only appears after Osseus casts the Hex.
strategy: Hit with Life (+35%) and avoid Death (35% resist).
Do not cast blades or Globals on yourself; each makes him Hex you (+35% trap on you). Auras are the safe exception.
Do not cast Traps or Shields either; those also draw a +35% Hex on the caster.
Set up with Auras (which do not trigger him) and pet-cast blades (which also do not trigger), then land one Life hit.
At 3,955 health a single blade-and-hit setup ends him fast.
cheatNotes: Casting a blade makes him Hex you with a +35% trap; buff with Auras instead, which do not trigger it.
Globals trigger the Hex but Auras do not, so lean on Auras for setup.
Pet-cast blades do not trigger the Hex; a safe way to gain a blade.
Casting a Trap (even Feint) or a Shield also earns you a +35% Hex; avoid them and burst him.

name: Overlord Xerxes
cheats: "Another intruder! Interesting" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, then Overlord Xerxes will cast a version of Steal Health on the Wizard, which deals 1,596 Damage and Pierces through all Resist, healing him for 100% of the Damage.

If a Minion has the first position, then the Minion will not be sacrificed.
If no Minions are present, Xerxes will cast a version of Blessing on himself that restores 10,000 Health.
"Your modern tactics are weak" - If a Feint is placed on Xerxes or a Minion, he will cast Sacrifice (Death) on himself.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
"Let's see how you handle this" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, he will cast a Steal Charm (Star) that steals all Blades from all Wizards.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
Pet casts will trigger this cheat.
Aegis can be used to protect your Blades once. Casting a second Charm will cause Xerxes to remove the first Charm.
spellNotes: Casts an all-enemies version of Curse (95%).
Casts a +70% / +30% version of a Spell similar to Mass Feint.
strategy: Death boss (14000). Hit with Life (+35%); avoid Death (35%) and Balance (40% resist).

Blades are dangerous: any Blade (or Global, or pet cast) makes him steal ALL blades from every wizard. Aegis protects one blade, but a second charm makes him remove the first, so Aegis one key blade and hit, or stack blades and hit the same round.
Do not Feint him or his minions (Mass Feint included); he answers by Sacrificing himself, resetting your trap plan.
He opens round 1 by Steal-Healthing his own Ossuary Conscripts to heal up (or Blessing for 10000 if none are out). Expect him near full early, so favor clean setup over chipping.
Aegis a blade, add a Life hit through a trap, and burst him.
cheatNotes: Late joiners take a 1596 pierce Steal Health that heals him. Arrive on time.
Round 1 he sacrifices his own minions with a 9378 Steal Health, healing himself fully. Expect him topped up early.
A minion in the first slot is not sacrificed, so one may survive the opening.
With no minions out he Blessings himself for 10000 instead. Either way he will be near full, so do not count on early chip damage.
Feint him or a minion and he Sacrifices himself, wrecking your trap setup. Skip Feint.
Mass Feint triggers it too. Use plain blades and hit.
Any Blade makes him steal ALL blades from the whole team. Aegis one key blade, or blade-and-hit in a single round.
Damage Globals trigger the blade-steal as well. Be careful what you open with.
Even pet-cast charms trigger the steal, so a May Cast blade can set it off unexpectedly.
Aegis saves one blade from the steal, but casting a second charm afterward makes him strip the first. Protect one, then hit.

name: Paranoia

stats.resist: 24% to all schools54% to all schools

cheats: "HUH?":
At the beginning of the first Round, Paranoia will cast Astraphobia and will recast this spell when replaced.
At the beginning of the fourth Round, and every two Rounds thereafter, Paranoia will cast a Dispel determined by the School of the Wizard's highest number of School Pips.
If a Wizard casts a single Blade, Paranoia will cast a -80% Shield of the Blade's School, or a -70% Tower Shield for Balanceblades.
If a Wizard casts Elemental Blade or Spirit Blade, Paranoia will cast Elemental Shield or Spirit Shield, respectively.
If a Wizard casts Star Blade, Moon Blade, or Sun Blade, Paranoia will cast the corresponding -50% triple Shields.
Alternative Blades, such as Raindrop Blade, Catch of the Day (Tier 2b), etc. don't trigger this cheat.
strategy: Attack with Myth (+30%); he resists Storm 54% and everything else 24%.

His key cheat: every normal blade you cast makes him shield the matching school. Use alternative blades like Raindrop Blade or Catch of the Day, which do not trigger any shield, so you can set up freely.
He Dispels the school you have the most pips in starting round 4 (every 2 rounds after), so set up and hit before round 4, or avoid over-relying on your main school's pips.
He recasts Astraphobia (round 1) whenever it is replaced, so do not fight the Global.
Blade with alt-blades and a Feint, then land one big Myth hit before the Dispel cycle bites.
cheatNotes: He casts Astraphobia round 1 and recasts it whenever replaced, so do not waste turns changing the Global.

Elemental or Spirit Blade makes him cast Elemental or Spirit Shield.
Star, Moon, or Sun Blade triggers the matching -50% triple shields.
Alternative blades like Raindrop Blade or Catch of the Day do not trigger any of it, so build your setup with these.

name: Pendragon
cheats: [Dispel] At the start of the duel, the Pendragon will interrupt-cast an Entangle Treasure Card on all Wizards.
[Damage over Time] During Round 1, he will use his turn to cast Fire Dragon (650+20,000) which deals 100 Damage Over Time per Round lasting 200 Rounds. This can 'Critical' for double Damage.
Triage and Shift can be used to safely remove the DoT.
Shift and Detonate can be used as a counter attack to this cheat.
Quench can be used to dispel the Fire Dragon if Wizard has the initial cast.
On the fifth Round, Pendragon will use his turn to recast this Spell if initially dispelled.
[Ice] If a Wizard attacks the Pendragon, he will cast an Ice Wand attack that doesn't deal Ice Damage dealing 500 Damage on himself.

If a Wizard attacks the Pendragon with the Sword of Kings Wand hits, he will re-cast the Wand hit as a Natural Attack on himself, and sometimes take the 500 Damage from the Ice Wand attack that doesn't deal Ice Damage.
The Sword of Kings Wand attacks will always trigger this cheat.
[Steal] If a Wizard casts a Rank 6 or higher single-target Spell (including X-Pip), he will interrupt-cast an Ominous Scarecrow dealing 550 Damage.
strategy: Round 1 he Entangles the whole team (a skipped turn) and lands a Fire Dragon DoT (100/round) — clear the DoT with Triage or Shift (Quench works if you have the first cast) and plan your setup around losing round 1.
Avoid Rank 6+ single-target attacks — they make him interrupt with Ominous Scarecrow (550) on you. Use an AoE finisher or stack blades onto a lower-rank hit.
Exploit his self-damage: attacking him makes him hit himself for ~500, and Sword of Kings wand hits always trigger it — wand him for free chip damage.
Attack with Ice or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Death resist without a prism.
Stack blades and traps, then finish with an AoE or mid-rank hit to skip the Scarecrow counter.
cheatNotes: He opens by Entangling the whole team (a skipped turn) — plan your setup around losing round 1.
Round 1 he casts Fire Dragon for a long 100/round DoT (200 rounds, can crit) — clear it fast so it doesn't grind you down.
Triage or Shift safely removes that Fire Dragon DoT — bring one.
You can also Shift then Detonate the DoT back onto him as a counter-attack.
Quench dispels the Fire Dragon if you have the initial cast — an alternative to Triage.
Attacking him makes him hit himself for ~500 — free chip damage every time you swing.
Sword of Kings wand hits get reflected onto him as self-damage — wand him for extra free damage.
A Rank 6+ single-target spell makes him interrupt with Ominous Scarecrow (550) — use an AoE or lower-rank hit, or accept the 550.

[Myth] Black Dragon - Black Dragon punishes Single Target spells as followed.

"You are myopic, too focused on one and not the whole"' - If a Wizard casts one of the following single target effects, Black Dragon will cast one of the following Shadow removal Spells based upon the amounts of effects the spell placed. These will ignore protection.

Heal over Times included.

[Blade] - Casting a multi-target Blade, an AoE Disarm will remove 1 or 2 Blades depending on how many Blades were cast on all Wizards.
[Ward] - Casting a multi-target Shield, an AoE Pierce will remove 1 or 2 Wards depending on how many Wards were cast on all Wizards.

This also triggers multi-target Damage the cheat below.

Prisms are unaffected.
[Curse] - If a Wizard casts a multi-target negative Charm on White Dragon, Cleanse Charm will remove 1, 2, or 3 negative Charms depending on how many Charms were cast on White Dragon.
strategy: His resist is even (18% to most, 38% Life), so hit with any school except Life; Myth gets a small boost.
Never hit Pendragon with a spell costing more than 3 Pips. He answers with a 1,000 Death-drain Scarecrow that heals him. Chip him with cheap sub-4-pip spells.

name: Personal Daemon

stats.resist: 44% to all schools64% to all schools84% to all schools

cheats: "Ice freezes Storm." - If a Wizard casts a Helpful Charm, Personal Daemon will cast Wall of Blades or Oni's Destruction on all Wizards that clears all positive Charms for 3 -20% damage shields or 3 +15% ice damage traps.

"Ice outlasts Death." - If a Wizard casts a Harmful Charm, Personal Daemon will cast Glacial Fortress on itself or Jinn's Vexation on the casting wizard that clears all negative Charms for 3 -20% damage shields or 3 -10% damage weaknesses.
"Stack overflow" - If one of the following effects goes over a certain threshold, the extra effects will be removed.
Over 4 Helpful Wards on Wizards.
Over 4 Heal over Times on Wizards.
Over 4 Damage over Times on Daemon.
Over 4 Harmful Wards on Personal Daemon.
traps on Personal Daemon placed as part of his interrupt cycle are ignored for this count
Interrupt cycle Personal Daemon revolves around a interrupt cycle repeating every 5 rounds
At the beginning of the first round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to remove all harmful wards from itself.
At the beginning of the second round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to place a cloaked trap on itself.
The cloaked trap is a +0% trap of the school corresponding to the school that primarily owes the hanging effect that the hit for the cycle counters
Fire: Damage over Times
Ice: Helpful Wards
Life: Heal over Times
Myth: Harmful Wards
Balance: Pips
The Bat Bot minions may change their self-buffing behavior if this trap is removed on the same turn it is placed
At the beginning of the third round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to place a cloaked trap on itself.
The cloaked trap is a +0% trap of the school corresponding to the school of the hit of the cycle

Removing this trap on the same turn it is placed will cause Bat Bot minions with shielding capabilities to interrupt cast a -125% damage shield of the school of the hit on the next turn, overriding their default behavior
At the beginning of the fourth round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to place a cloaked trap on itself.
The cloaked trap is a +0% trap of the school corresponding to the school that primarily owes the hanging effect that the hit for the cycle generates
All the school/effect pairings noted on turn one are used plus the following new options
Storm: Helpful Charms
Death: Harmful Charms
Shadow: Shadow Pips

"Execute background process" - At the end of the fifth round of the cycle, Personal Daemon will interrupt to cast one of the following spells based on the self applied traps. All of these spells have 45 additional pierce.

A version of Earth Walker that deals 6,666 Life Damage to all enemies, and removes up to three Damage over Time effects from itself to apply a Damage over Time effect on all enemies dealing 130 Life Damage over 3 Rounds.
A version of Centaur that deals 6,666 Life Damage to all enemies, and removes up to five harmful wards from itself to apply a +25% damage charm on itself per trap removed.
A version of Akori Nirini that deals 6,666 Fire Damage on all enemies, and removes up to five harmful wards from itself to apply a +30% damage trap on all enemies per harmful ward removed.

A version of King Artorius (Fire) that deals 6,666 Fire Damage on all enemies and removes up to helpful wards from all enemies per shield removed to apply a Heal over Time that restores 700 Health over 5 Rounds.

A version of King Artorius (Storm) that deals 6,666 Storm Damage to all enemies, and removes up to three Heal over Time effects from enemies to apply a Heal over Time effect on itself restoring 300 Health over 4 Rounds.
A version of Leviathan that deals 6,666 Storm Damage to all enemies, and removes up to three Damage over Time effects from itself to apply a -25% damage weakness on all enemies.
A version of Basilisk that deals 6,666 Myth Damage to all enemies, and removes up to eight helpful wards from enemies to apply a -50% Damage shield on itself per shield removed.

A version of Skeletal Pirate that deals 6,666 Death Damage to all enemies, and removes up three Heal over Time effects from enemies to apply a -50% damage shield on itself per Heal over Time effect removed.
A version of Hypothermi-HIYA! that deals 6,666 Ice Damage to all enemies, and removes 3 pips from all enemies and gives itself 3 pips.
A version of Clockwork Spider (355) that deals 6,666 Balance Damage to all enemies, and removes 3 pips from all enemies and gives itself a shadow pip.

At any point, if one of Personal Daemon's self placed traps are removed without hitting, by gambit, swap or clear, Personal Daemon will interrupt to skip the rest of the cycle and start the "Execute background process" phase instantly.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Freeze Ray that deals 920 Ice Damage for 5 Pips and 2 Shadow Pips.
strategy: Raid boss with 96,000 health - a full team job. He resists Storm/Death 84% and Ice 64%, so hit with Fire or Myth (44%) and stack armor piercing.
Watch what you cast: a helpful charm makes him strip your charms and shield/trap himself, and a harmful charm makes him cleanse and shield. Coordinate so only planned buffs go up.
Keep effect counts at or under 4 - more than 4 helpful wards/HoTs on wizards, or DoTs/harmful wards on him, get auto-removed by "Stack overflow." His own cycle traps are exempt.

He runs a 5-round interrupt cycle: cleanses himself, then places three cloaked +0% traps that telegraph his round-5 "Execute background process" 6,666-damage AoE (+45 pierce). Read the trap schools to know which hit is coming and pre-shield.
Do NOT remove his self-placed traps early (gambit, swap, or clear) - that skips the cycle and fires the 6,666 AoE immediately, and can trigger Bat Bot shields.
Burst him in planned windows between Execute phases; have the healer pre-shield the whole team for each 6,666 hit.
cheatNotes: Casting a helpful charm makes him clear your positive charms and put up 3 damage shields or 3 Ice traps (Wall of Blades / Oni's Destruction). Only blade the turn you plan to hit.
Casting a harmful charm (weakness) makes him cleanse it and shield/weaken via Glacial Fortress or Jinn's Vexation. Avoid loose weaknesses.
"Stack overflow": he auto-removes any of the listed effects once they go over a threshold of 4.
More than 4 helpful wards across wizards and the extras are stripped. Keep shields at 4 or fewer.
More than 4 heal-over-times on wizards and the extras are stripped.
More than 4 damage-over-times on him and the extras are removed.
More than 4 harmful wards on him and the extras are removed.
His own cycle-placed traps are exempt from these overflow counts.
His core mechanic is a 5-round interrupt cycle, detailed below.
Cycle round 1: he removes all harmful wards from himself. Do not waste traps the turn before.
Cycle round 2: he places a cloaked +0% trap on himself.
That round-2 trap's school tells you which hanging effect his upcoming hit will counter (pairings below).
Fire trap = his hit will counter Damage-over-Times.
Ice trap = counters Helpful Wards.
Life trap = counters Heal-over-Times.
Myth trap = counters Harmful Wards.
Balance trap = counters Pips.
Removing this trap the same turn it is placed can change Bat Bot minion behavior.
Cycle round 3: he places another cloaked +0% trap, this one matching the school of the incoming hit itself.
So the round-3 trap's school is the damage school of the Execute hit - pre-shield it.
Removing the round-3 trap the same turn makes a Bat Bot interrupt-cast a -125% shield of that school next turn.
Cycle round 4: he places a third cloaked +0% trap, keyed to the effect his hit will generate.
That round-4 trap's school tells you what the hit produces (pairings below).
All the round-2 school/effect pairings apply here plus three more.
Storm trap = the hit generates Helpful Charms for him.
Death trap = generates Harmful Charms on you.
Shadow trap = generates Shadow Pips.
End of cycle round 5, "Execute background process": he casts one of the spells below, each 6,666 damage with +45 pierce. Pre-shield the whole team.
Earth Walker: 6,666 Life AoE, converts up to 3 DoTs on himself into a 130 Life DoT on your team.
Centaur: 6,666 Life AoE, turns up to 5 harmful wards on him into +25% damage charms on himself.
Akori Nirini: 6,666 Fire AoE, turns up to 5 harmful wards on him into +30% traps on your team.
King Artorius (Fire): 6,666 Fire AoE, strips your shields to heal himself 700 over 5.
King Artorius (Storm): 6,666 Storm AoE, strips up to 3 HoTs from you to heal himself 300 over 4.
Leviathan: 6,666 Storm AoE, converts up to 3 DoTs on himself into -25% weaknesses on your team.
Basilisk: 6,666 Myth AoE, strips up to 8 of your shields to give himself -50% shields per shield removed.
Skeletal Pirate: 6,666 Death AoE, strips up to 3 of your HoTs to give himself -50% shields per HoT.
Hypothermi-HIYA!: 6,666 Ice AoE, steals 3 pips from your team and gives himself 3.
Clockwork Spider: 6,666 Balance AoE, steals 3 pips from your team and gives himself a shadow pip.
Removing any of his self-placed traps without hitting (gambit, swap, or clear) skips the rest of the cycle and fires the Execute hit instantly. Never strip his traps.

Triage or Mass Triage can be used to remove the Damage over Time effect.

Indemnity can be used to prevent this switch.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.

name: Poison Oak (Cabal's Revenge)

stats.resist: 14% to all schools34% to all schools54% to all schools74% to all schools94% to all schools

cheats: "Nature adapts. Can you?" - If a Wizard damages Poison Oak, he'll cast a triple -999% Shield on himself based on the school's Damage.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Ratatoskr's Spin that deals 365 Life Damage.
strategy: This is an 84,750-health raid boss, so run it as a full team: a hitter, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer. Death is his least-resisted school (14%); avoid Fire (94%) entirely.
Every time you damage him he stacks three -999% shields against that damage school, so a second hit of the same school is wasted into them.
Rotate damage schools or bring Shatter and Steal Ward to strip the triple shield before your real hit.
Have one wizard bait the shields with a hit, then Shatter them the same round so the hitter's Death nuke lands clean.
Stack blades and Feints through the setup, keep the shield-strip and the big hit on the same round, and repeat to grind his large health pool down.
cheatNotes: Every time you damage him he stacks three -999% shields against that damage school. Rotate schools or Shatter/strip the shields before your real hit; Death is his least-resisted school at 14%.

name: Ponce de Gibbon
cheats: Interrupt Cycle This battle revolves around a cheat cycle that spans the length of five Rounds. This cheat repeats itself every five Rounds.
At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle (starting in Round 6), Ponce will remove marks he placed during the second Round in this cycle (see below). This is considered a normal Round.
"First, Let's cloud the mind!" / "Mind Weakened" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Ponce will place a "mark" on a random Wizard, in the form of a Stun Shield.
Melt can be placed on Ponce in order to Dispel this cheat.
The Stun Shield marker does not block Stuns.

The third Round in this cycle is considered a normal Round. However, it is the last Round for a marked Wizard to damage Ponce, in order to get rid of the mark. Failure to do so results in the worst part of his cheat to take place in the fourth and fifth Rounds of this cycle.
"Bah! You are too strong willed!" - If the marked Wizard attacks him, he will remove the Stun Shield mark and any other Wards from the Wizard. Stun Shields aren't removed from this spell.
"Are you sure you won't join us?" - In Round 4, if a marked Wizard did not attack Ponce by the end of the third Round in this cycle, he will Beguile the Wizard.

"If you aren't with us, you're against us!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Sacrifice on the marked / Beguiled Wizard, stealing Health from the Wizard and Healing himself for 2,090 Health (as though the Wizard used Sacrifice on him).
Universal and Death Blades that are active on the Wizard, will increase the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
Universal and Death Shields that are active on the Wizard will decrease the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
Infection will not lessen this Damage, however, it will decrease the Healing effect by -50%. Needs confirmation.
strategy: He takes +35% from Storm and Death and resists Myth 80%. Hit with Storm or Death.

The fight runs a 5-round cycle. On the second round of each cycle he marks a random wizard with a Stun Shield. The marked wizard MUST damage Ponce by the end of the third round of the cycle to clear it.
If the mark is not cleared, Round 4 he Beguiles the wizard and Round 5 he Sacrifices them (drain plus heals himself 2,090). Always land a hit on Ponce during your marked window.
Melt on Ponce dispels the marking cheat if you want to skip the dance.
Keep Universal and Death blades off yourself when marked (they boost his Sacrifice) and keep a shield up (shields reduce it). Blade up, clear each mark with a Storm or Death hit, and burst him down.
cheatNotes: At the start of each cycle he clears the marks he placed. This is a normal round; use it to set up.
On the second round of the cycle he marks a random wizard with a Stun Shield. That wizard must hit Ponce before the cycle's third round ends.
Melt on Ponce dispels this marking cheat entirely if you would rather not chase it.
The Stun Shield mark does not actually block stuns; it is only the cheat timer.
The third round of the cycle is the marked wizard's last chance to damage Ponce and clear the mark.
If the marked wizard attacks him in time, he removes the mark and their other wards. Just get a hit in.
If the mark is not cleared by then, Round 4 he Beguiles the marked wizard. Do not miss your hit window.
Round 5 he Sacrifices the marked or beguiled wizard, draining them and healing himself 2,090. Clearing the mark avoids all of this.
Universal and Death blades on the marked wizard increase that Sacrifice damage; shed them if you fall behind the mark.
Universal and Death shields on the marked wizard reduce the Sacrifice. Keep a shield up if you might get caught.
Infection does not lessen the damage but cuts his heal from it by half.

name: Ponce de Gibbon (Rank 22)
cheats: Interrupt Cycle This battle revolves around a cheat cycle that spans the length of five Rounds. This cheat repeats itself every five Rounds.
At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle (starting in Round 6), Ponce will remove marks he placed during the second Round in this cycle (see below). This is considered a normal Round.
"First, Let's cloud the mind!" / "Mind Weakened" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Ponce will place a "mark" on a random Wizard, in the form of a Stun Shield.
The Stun Shield marker does not block Stuns.
Earthquake can remove this marker and will prevent the rest of this cycle.

The third Round in this cycle is considered a normal Round. However, it is the last Round for a marked Wizard to damage Ponce, in order to get rid of the mark. Failure to do so results in the worst part of his cheat to take place in the fourth and fifth Rounds of this cycle.
"Bah! You are as strong-willed as I remember" - If the marked Wizard attacks him, he will remove the Stun Shield mark and any other Wards from the Wizard. Stun Shields aren't removed from this Spell.
"I can't control you, but I can confuse you!" - In Round 4, if a marked Wizard did not attack Ponce by the end of the third Round in this cycle, he will Confuse the Wizard for 2 Rounds.

"If you aren't with us, you're against us!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Sacrifice on the marked / Confused Wizard, stealing Health from the Wizard and Healing himself for 2,090 Health (as though the Wizard used Sacrifice on him).
Universal and Death Blades that are active on him will increase the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
Universal and Death Shields that are active on the Wizard will decrease the amount of Damage dealt by the Sacrifice.
strategy: He resists 45% of most schools including Myth, with no clear boost school, so prism or brute-force with your strongest hit.
His five-round cycle marks a random wizard (a Stun Shield marker) on the cycle's second round. That wizard must attack Ponce by the next round to clear the mark.
If the mark is not cleared, round 4 Confuses that wizard and round 5 hits them with an enhanced Sacrifice that heals Ponce about 2,090. Always attack with the marked wizard in time.
Earthquake removes the mark and cancels the rest of the cycle outright, a clean answer.
Keep a Death or universal shield on the marked wizard to blunt the Sacrifice if it does land; blade, Feint, and burst the 11,940 health.
cheatNotes: At the start of each cycle (from round 6) he clears the previous mark, a normal round to set up.
On the cycle's second round he marks a random wizard with a Stun Shield marker, putting them on a timer.
The marker does not actually block stuns, it is just the mark.
Earthquake removes the marker and prevents the rest of the cycle, the cleanest counter.
The marked wizard must damage Ponce by the cycle's third round to shed the mark.
When the marked wizard attacks him, he removes the mark but also strips that wizard's other wards (not stun shields).
If the mark is not cleared, round 4 Confuses that wizard for 2 rounds.
On round 5 he Sacrifices the marked or confused wizard, stealing health and healing himself about 2,090. Do not let it get this far.
Universal and Death blades on Ponce increase that Sacrifice's damage, so do not blade him before it.
Universal and Death shields on the wizard reduce the Sacrifice; shield the marked wizard as insurance.

name: Poseidon Earth Shaker (Atlantea)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea - Palace of Atlantea
cheats: "We immortals are infallible!" - During the first Round, and any other Round when he does not have an Aura, Poseidon will use his turn to cast Infallible.

"You seek to weaken me? Drink from the depths!" - If any single-target Charm, Prism, or Trap is cast on Poseidon, he will use his Natural Attack, which removes all Traps that were placed on him. Multi-target Spells, such as Windstorm or others, can still be used.
Spells with single-target secondary effects will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Trap Poseidon with multi-target spells only. Any single-target charm, prism, or trap makes him natural-attack and wipe all traps off himself; Windstorm and other AoE traps are safe.
He recasts Infallible whenever he has no aura, so he will not fizzle; do not rely on accuracy debuffs.
He is stunable, so a stun buys the team a safe setup round.
Hit with Myth or Life (+35%, resists Storm 80%); funnel blades to the hitter and use AoE traps.
Burst him down and clear the Atlantean Trierarch with the same AoE.
cheatNotes: He recasts Infallible whenever he lacks an aura, so he will not fizzle; accuracy debuffs are wasted on him.
Any single-target charm, prism, or trap makes him natural-attack and strip all traps off himself, so trap him only with multi-target spells like Windstorm.
Spells with single-target secondary effects also trigger the trap-wipe, so watch enchanted hits.

name: Poseidon Earth Shaker (Atlantea, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea (Challenge) - Palace of Atlantea (Challenge)
cheats: [Aura] "We immortals are infallible!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Poseidon will cast Infallible.
strategy: Solo-friendly (7,345 health). His only cheat is an opening Infallible on round 1, so his early attack will not fizzle and pierces some resist. Shield up early instead of hoping he misses.
Hit with Ice or Death (+20%); he resists Fire, Storm, and Life 20%.
Clear or ignore the Atlantean Trierarch minion with an AoE alongside the boss.
Blade up over two or three rounds behind a shield, then land one strong hit.
Feint is safe here with no punish cheat, so add it for extra damage.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he casts Infallible, so his attack will not fizzle and ignores some of your resist. Shield rather than rely on a miss.

name: Poseidon Earth-Shaker (Zeus Exalted Challenge)
cheats: "Observe the might of a true weapon!" - If a Blade Spell is used, Poseidon will shift into Atlantean Trierarch, then cast Enfeeble on the Wizard who received the Blade(s).
Aegis-enchantment can be used to prevent any positive Charms from being removed.
Bladestorm will only affect the caster.
Pet casts will also trigger this cheat.
"The Sea is but a puddle without the stars." - If an Aura or Shadow Spell is used, Poseidon will place a Tower Shield on the caster.
"The Sea mourns for mighty Zeus!" - If Zeus is defeated, Poseidon will cast his Tempest Natural Attack every three rounds.
spellNotes: Casts an enhanced AoE version of Snow Shark that steals 1 Positive Ward and deals 650 Ice Damage to all enemies.
Casts an enhanced version of Tempest dealing 960 Storm Damage that steals 1 Pip from all Wizards and gains 4 Pips afterwards.
strategy: Team fight (20,000 health). Hit with Myth or Life (+35%), never Storm (80% resist).
Any blade makes him shift into Atlantean Trierarch and Enfeeble the blade off whoever received it. Protect key blades with Aegis, or use Bladestorm, which only blades the caster.
Pet-cast blades also trigger the Enfeeble, so manage your pet.
Casting an aura or shadow spell just gives the caster a Tower Shield, which is harmless and even helps survival, so cast those freely.
Once Zeus is defeated Poseidon casts his Tempest Natural Attack every three rounds, so shield the team and funnel Aegis-blades to one hitter for the kill between AoEs.
cheatNotes: Cast any blade and he shifts form and Enfeebles it off the receiver, protect your key blade with Aegis.
Aegis stops the Enfeeble from removing that charm, use it on the hitter's main blade.
Bladestorm only blades the caster here, a clean way to buff one hitter.
Pet-cast blades also trigger the Enfeeble, so watch your pet.
Casting an aura or shadow spell just gives you a Tower Shield, harmless and even helpful, so cast freely.
After Zeus dies, Poseidon casts his Tempest AoE every three rounds, shield the team and burst between them.

name: Power Draconian
cheats: Power Draconian
"Oh look, a wizard with attitude!" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Power Draconian casts Impede on the Wizard.
"Power Equalize!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Power Draconian will cast a Sun Global Spell called Equalize, which sets the Damage limit to 3,000.
"Bubble Morph!" - If a Wizard tries to replace the Equalize Spell with another type of Global Spell, he will recast Equalize.
"Power Stunner!" - At the beginning of every third Round, Power Draconian casts Power Stunner, which Stuns for 1 Round and applies a -40% Accuracy Charm on all enemies.
"Power Recharge!" - If Power Draconian is damaged by an All Enemies Spell, Power Draconian casts a Shadow version of Blessing restoring 100% max Health on all allies.
All Enemies Spells that deal Drain Damage, like Scarecrow, do not trigger this cheat.
White Tiger
"*GROWL*" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, White Tiger casts Sabertooth on the Wizard.
"*WARNING ROAR*" - White Tiger will say this the turn before their *ROAR* cheat.
"*ROAR*" - At the end of every two Rounds, White Tiger casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt to all enemies dealing 550, 1,050 or 1,550 Moon Damage.
"*WHIMPER*" - If White Tiger is hit by an attack, their *ROAR* cheat is delayed, and the two Round cycle is reset.
"*SNARL*" - White Tiger will cast Empower (Moon) on itself after these following triggers.
If a negative Charm is cast on White Tiger.
After White Tiger casts Grim Reader or Power Draconian casts Lord of the Jungle.
strategy: Fire boss: hit with Ice, the only school he takes extra from (+5%); Fire is resisted 73%. Neither he nor the White Tiger can be stunned.
Equalize caps all damage at 3,000, so forget one-shots and plan to chip both enemies down under the cap.
Never hit with an all-enemies spell: Power Recharge heals every enemy to full when he is hit by AoE. Use single-target hits, or a drain AoE like Scarecrow, which does not trigger it.
Every third round Power Stunner stuns your team for a round and adds -40% accuracy, so bring stun blocks and accuracy.
