Hitting the White Tiger resets and delays its ROAR (Insane Bolt, up to 1,550 Moon to your team), so chip the Tiger to keep pushing that nuke back.
Grind both enemies down under the 3,000 cap and finish.
cheatNotes: Join by Round 2 or he hits the latecomer with Impede.
Equalize caps every hit at 3,000 damage, so big single-shot kills are off the table; plan to chip.
Trying to swap out his Equalize global just makes him recast it, so leave it.
Every third round Power Stunner stuns your whole team for a round and adds -40% accuracy; pack stun blocks.
Hitting him with any all-enemies spell heals every enemy to full, so only ever use single-target hits here.
Drain AoEs like Scarecrow are the exception and do not trigger the full heal.
A late wizard also eats a Sabertooth from the White Tiger.
When the Tiger says WARNING ROAR, its big ROAR nuke lands next turn.
ROAR is a Moon Insane Bolt to your whole team for 550, 1,050, or 1,550 every two rounds.
Hitting the Tiger delays ROAR and resets its two-round timer, so chip it to stall the nuke.
SNARL means the Tiger Empowered itself; watch for a stronger follow-up.
Putting a negative charm on the Tiger triggers that Empower.
The Tiger also Empowers after it casts Grim Reader or after Draconian casts Lord of the Jungle.

name: Preceptor of Order (Challenge)
cheats: [Shield] "Be thorough!" - After casting any spell that places a shield on himself, the Preceptor of Order will cast either Celestial Intervention (Tier 5b) or Frozen Armor.
strategy: Bring Fire. The Preceptor is Ice and takes boosted Fire, so a Fire hitter or Fire prisms carry this Challenge; do not attack into his Ice resist.

Punish through his shields, not around them. Any self-shield he casts triggers a follow-up (Celestial Intervention Tier 5b or Frozen Armor), stacking more defense, so carry Shatter or pierce to strip them before your hit.
Do not chip at him repeatedly and let shields pile up. Build one large stacked hit that blows through the layered wards in a single swing.
This is a team fight: 20,325 Health in a Darkmoor Challenge. Funnel blades to one Fire hitter, have supports Feint the boss, and keep a healer on the group.
Stack blades and Feint over a few rounds, hold a Shatter for the turn you swing, then burst him down before the shield-stacking cheat can rebuild.
Since his shield cheat re-arms on every self-shield, time your finisher to land right after you strip, not before he re-shields.
cheatNotes: Any spell that puts a shield on himself makes him immediately follow up with Celestial Intervention (Tier 5b) or Frozen Armor, stacking extra defense. Carry Shatter or pierce and hit through it in one big swing.

name: Preceptor of Order (Standard)

stats.resist: 53% to all schools83% to all schools

cheats: [Ice] Preceptor of Order
[Blade] "Be unyielding!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm, the Preceptor of Order will cast Ice Wyvern (Tier 5b) on the recipient of the Charm.
[Curse] "Be forceful!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm on the Preceptor of Order, the Preceptor will cast Woolly Mammoth (Tier 5b) on the caster of the Charm.
[Shield] "Be thorough!" - After casting a Shield, the Preceptor of Order will cast Frozen Armor, which will not use any Pips.
[Myth] Preceptor of Deviousness
[Shield] "Subvert defenses!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, the Preceptor of Deviousness will cast Minotaur (Tier 5b) on the recipient of the Ward.
[Blade] "Catch them unawares!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm while the Wizard has a Damage over Time effect on them, the Preceptor of Deviousness will cast Incindiate.
[Life] Preceptor of Decadence
[Trap] "Always respond with excess!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Ward on the Preceptor of Decadence, the Preceptor will cast Centaur (Tier 5b) on the caster.
[Damage over Time] "Never accept little bites!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect on the Preceptor of Decadence, the Preceptor will cast Spinysaur (Tier 5b) on the caster.
[Afterlife] "Always overindulge!" - When the Preceptor of Decadence is defeated for the first time, it will cast a Spell that restores 50% max Health to all of the Preceptors.
[Storm] Preceptor of Savagery
strategy: Every wizard action draws a themed Ice Wyvern, Woolly Mammoth, Minotaur, or Centaur cheat cast, so plan who casts what and keep buffing tight.

Helpful Charms make the Order Preceptor Wyvern the recipient, harmful Charms on it draw a Woolly Mammoth on the caster, and shields, wards, traps, and DoTs each trigger their own punish, so avoid stacking these on the wrong Preceptor.
Kill the Life Preceptor of Decadence carefully: the first time it dies it heals all Preceptors 50% max Health, so time it to die last or burst it through with the others.
The Order boss resists Ice 83% and other schools 53% but takes 30% more from Fire, so bring Fire hitters.
Funnel blades to one hitter, Feint the target the same Round you hit to dodge the reactive casts, and AoE the Preceptors down together where possible.
cheatNotes: Cast a helpful Charm and the Order Preceptor throws an Ice Wyvern at whoever received it.
Cast a harmful Charm on the Order Preceptor and it hits the caster with Woolly Mammoth.
After you cast a Shield, the Order Preceptor casts Frozen Armor for free.
Cast a helpful Ward and the Deviousness Preceptor throws a Minotaur at whoever received it.
Cast a helpful Charm while you have a damage-over-time on you and the Deviousness Preceptor casts Incindiate.
Cast a harmful Ward on the Decadence Preceptor and it hits the caster with Centaur.
Cast a damage-over-time on the Decadence Preceptor and it hits the caster with Spinysaur.
The first time the Decadence Preceptor dies, it restores 50% max Health to all of the Preceptors.

name: Prince Viggor
locations: Wizard City: The Catacombs - Viggor's Tomb
cheats: "Knights of Dragonspyre, to me!"' - Casts a Sun Spell to summon a combination of the following Minions:
Summons 1 Minion if only 1 Wizard is present.
Summons 2 Minions if 2 Wizards are present.
Viggor's Crusader (Death)
Viggor's Crusader (Ice)
Viggor's Vanguard (Fire)
Viggor's Vanguard (Life)
Viggor's Lorekeeper (Myth)
Viggor's Lorekeeper (Storm)
Viggor's Havox (Balance)
Viggor's Havox (Shadow)
"The cycle repeats..."' - Starting on Round 3, Prince Viggor will recast his Sun Summon Spell to summon one additional Minion.
"That knowledge is beyond you!" - If a Feint is cast on Boss, he will cast a Shadow Spell to remove all Traps.
Indemnity can be used to preserve the Feint if Viggor's Lorekeeper isn't present.
"Steel yourselves!" - At the end of Round 2, Prince Viggor will cast a -55% Mass Tower Shield (Shadow).
"This is for your own good" - Prince Viggor will cast a -30% Mass Weakness.
"Attack!" - Viggor will cast Bladestorm (+25%).
"Feel our fury!" - Viggor will cast +70% Feint (Shadow) universal Trap on all Wizards.

"You can't sneak up on me!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Prince Viggor will cast +25% Bladestorm, -30% Mass Weakness, a -55% Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) that applies 3 shields per ally, and an AoE +70% Feint (Shadow).
Life and Death Minions
If a Shield is cast, this Minion will use Pierce (Star) to remove 1 Shield then proceed to cast Tower Shield (-55%) on themselves.
AoE Shields only trigger this spell on the caster.
Fire and Ice Minions
If a Blade is cast, this Minion will use Cleanse Charm (Star) to remove all Negative Charms from the receiver of the Blade and then proceed to cast Balanceblade (+30%) on themselves.
For multi-target spells such as Bladestorm, only the caster will be cleansed.
Storm and Myth Minions
If a Trap is cast, this Minion will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) to remove 1 Trap from their ally and places a Feint (+75%) on the Wizard that cast the Trap.
Multi-target Traps such as Windstorm will cause the Minions to cast this spell on the caster.
Balance and Shadow Minions
If a Negative Charm is cast, this Minion will use Cleanse Charm (Star) on the target it was used on then proceed to cast a -30% Weakness on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Stun (80%).
strategy: Big Catacombs fight: Prince Viggor at 41,300 who continually summons school-paired Crusader, Vanguard, Lorekeeper, and Havox minions. Run a full team. Hit with Storm or Life (+5%); avoid Myth and Death (70% resist).
He summons on the opening and again from Round 3 on, so expect a growing board; the hitter bursts Viggor while supports manage minions, which each punish setup by school.
Do not Feint him. A Feint makes him Shadow-cast off all traps; Indemnity saves a Feint only if no Storm/Myth Lorekeeper is present.

His minions counter your setup: shields draw Pierce, blades draw Cleanse Charm, traps draw a Cleanse Ward plus a Feint back on you, and negative charms draw a Cleanse plus Weakness. Keep setup minimal and funneled.
He layers team debuffs (Mass Weakness, -55% Mass Tower Shield, Bladestorm, +70% Feint on all wizards) and a Mass Stun; keep stun blocks and Cleanse ready.
Funnel blades to one hitter, protect a Feint with Indemnity, clear or ignore minions as needed, and deliver one large Storm or Life hit.
cheatNotes: He summons school-paired minions based on party size; expect a board of adds from the start.
From Round 3 he summons another minion each cycle, so the board keeps growing; kill or out-race the adds.
Feinting him makes him Shadow-cast off all your traps; protect Feints with Indemnity or skip them.
Indemnity only saves the Feint when no Storm/Myth Lorekeeper is present; otherwise the trap still goes.
At the end of Round 2 he drops a -55% Mass Tower Shield on his side; use Pierce or Shatter to punch through.
He casts a -30% Mass Weakness on your team; Cleanse it off your hitter before the big shot.
He buffs his side with a +25% Bladestorm; expect harder enemy hits.
He drops a +70% Feint on all wizards, setting up his own big hit; heal or shield through it.
Joining late gets Bladestorm, Mass Weakness, a triple Mass Tower Shield, and a +70% Feint dumped on you; enter on time.
Casting a Shield makes the Life/Death minion Pierce one shield off and Tower Shield itself; shield sparingly.
Team shields only trigger this on the caster.
Casting a blade makes the Fire/Ice minion Cleanse the receiver's negative charms and Balanceblade itself; funnel blades late.
Multi-target blades only cleanse the caster.
Casting a Trap makes the Storm/Myth minion strip a trap and Feint you back; avoid traps while they live.
Multi-target traps make the minion Feint the caster.
Placing a negative charm makes the Balance/Shadow minion Cleanse it and Weaken the caster; keep debuffs off unless you can burst immediately.

name: Procyon
cheats: "Here's to your health!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, then Procyon will use a version of Meteor Strike that deals damage for the following amount based on the number of spots filled by the Wizard team:
Pet Heals will not trigger this cheat.
Even though these Spells are not cast by Procyon, they can still be dispelled.
strategy: Myth boss (7690). Hit with Storm (+35%); avoid Myth (35% resist).
Do not cast heals: any heal makes him Meteor Strike the team (bigger the more wizards present). Heal before engaging, use pet heals (which do not trigger it), or Dispel the Meteor.
Stack blades and traps and land a Storm hit. Keep healing out of the fight and he is straightforward.
cheatNotes: Any healing spell triggers a Meteor Strike on the whole team, scaling with how many wizards are present. Avoid heals or use pet heals instead.
Pet heals do NOT trigger the Meteor, so lean on your pet for healing here.
The Meteor Strike can be Dispelled even though he cheats it. A Dispel up front lets you heal safely once.

name: Professor Fuzzlewort
cheats: [Ward] "Nothing can take my shields!" - At the beginning of each Round, Professor Fuzzlewort will cast one of the following Spells:
Reindeer Knight (Tier 3b)
Shield Wall
Star Shield
Tower Shield
A version of Volcanic Golem (Tier 5b) that deals 400 Damage
[Jinx] If a Wizard casts a Minotaur (Tier 2b) or a Basilisk (Tier 2b) on Professor Fuzzlewort, he will cast a version of Shatter that affects all allies, and deactivate his Shield cheat.
[Afterlife] If Professor Fuzzlewort is defeated while his Shield cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "Ha! Even Death bounces off my shields!" - When Professor Fuzzlewort revives, he will cast Shield Wall on himself, and give Wizards the following Spells:
[Minotaur (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)][Basilisk (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
strategy: He self-shields every Round, so raw hits get eaten, and killing him while the shield cheat is active revives him to full.
Cast the given Minotaur or Basilisk on him. It Shatters his shields and turns off the shield cheat.
He hands out those Minotaur and Basilisk cards when he revives, so the practical line is to hit him down once, let him revive, then use the given card to disable the cheat and finish him.
Hit Fire or Myth, his listed weak schools, and avoid Ice and Storm.
Watch for his Volcanic Golem (400 damage) on Rounds he attacks instead of shielding.
cheatNotes: Each Round he shields himself with one of the following, or attacks with Volcanic Golem.
His Volcanic Golem deals 400 damage on the Round he attacks instead of shielding.
Casting the given Minotaur or Basilisk on him makes him Shatter his own shields and shuts off the shield cheat. This is the key to the fight.
If you kill him while the shield cheat is still active, he revives to full. Turn the cheat off with Minotaur or Basilisk first.
On reviving he Shield Walls and hands every Wizard the Minotaur and Basilisk cards.

name: Ptolemos
locations: Celestia: Trial of the Spheres - Lunar Sanctum
cheats: "Your charm has worn thin!" - During the first Round, and after each direct attack against him, Ptolemos will cast a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself.
Melt can be used to prevent this cheat.
Pierce or Steal Ward can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield.
All Enemy Spells and Steal Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"You have charmed your way into my heart!" - If a Wizard casts a Charm Spell, Ptolemos will remove all Wards from himself.
Global Spells, such as Sanctuary, will trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
Ptolemos will remove harmful Wards that are placed on him (e.g. Traps, Prisms, etc.).
Vaporize can be used to prevent this cheat.
Multi-target Trap Spells, such as Mass Feint, will not trigger this cheat.
Only the first Ward is removed from a multi-Trap Spell, such as Spirit Trap.
Indemnity will prevent Trap removal once.
strategy: Attack with Fire (+35%); he resists Ice and Death 35%.
He shields himself with a -90% Tower Shield on round 1 and after every direct attack, so do not chip him with single hits.
Exploit his charm cheat: casting a Charm (a blade) or a Global makes him strip all wards off himself, including that Tower Shield. Blade up to clear his shield, then hit.
Alternatively use an All-Enemy spell or Steal spell, which do not trigger the shield replace, or Pierce and Steal Ward to remove the shield safely.
Note casting a Charm also removes your own traps from him, so rely on blades, then finish him.
cheatNotes: He drops a -90% Tower Shield round 1 and after every direct attack, so single hits just refresh it. Strip it before hitting.
Melt prevents this shield cheat.
Pierce or Steal Ward removes the Tower Shield without triggering a replace.
All-enemy spells and Steal spells do not trigger the shield at all, so they are safe ways to damage him.
Casting any Charm makes him strip every ward off himself, including his Tower Shield. Blade to clear his shield, then hit.
Globals like Sanctuary also trigger that self-strip.
Pet-cast spells do not trigger the ward-strip cheat.
He will also strip harmful wards like traps and prisms off himself when this triggers, so your traps come off too.
Vaporize prevents the ward-strip cheat.
Multi-target traps like Mass Feint do not trigger the strip, a way to trap him safely.
On a multi-trap spell only the first ward gets removed.
Indemnity protects a trap from removal once.

name: Pumpkin Head
locations: Wizard City: Ravenwood - Hallowe'en Master Tower
cheats: "Less Heals! More Damage!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he cheat casts Hex on the healed Wizard.
For group Healing Spells, only the caster receives a Hex.
Pet Heals do not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Attack with Ice (he takes +50%); he resists Fire 80%.
Avoid healing spells — he Hexes whoever heals. Use pet heals instead (they don't trigger it).
Stack blades and traps, then burst his 3,040 health quickly.
If you must heal, use a group heal so only the caster eats the Hex, or route it through your pet.
cheatNotes: Casting a heal makes him Hex the healed wizard — skip healing spells or route healing through your pet.
With a group heal only the caster gets Hexed — the healed allies are fine.
Pet heals don't trigger the Hex, so heal through your pet if you need it.

name: Puzzle Piece Monster
cheats: "Bzzzzzt" - At the end of Round 1, Puzzle Piece Monster casts Moon Blade.
"Whrrrrrr" - At the end of Round 2, Puzzle Piece Monster casts Moon Trap.
"Foosshhhh" - At the end of Round 3, Puzzle Piece Monster casts one of the following spells:
Elemental Defuse, Spirit Defuse, Unbalance
strategy: No resist or boost listed — use your strongest reliable school.

He builds a Moon Blade on himself round 1 and a Moon Trap round 2, then end of round 3 he Defuses your blades (Elemental Defuse, Spirit Defuse, or Unbalance) — set up and hit by round 3 before your blades get stripped.
Pick blades the defuse won't catch (a single-school blade rather than the matching Elemental/Spirit/Balance stack he removes).
Blade over rounds 1-2 and land your big hit end of round 2 or start of round 3.
He can't be stunned, so rely on speed rather than control.
cheatNotes: End of round 1 he gives himself a Moon Blade.
End of round 2 he lays a Moon Trap.
End of round 3 he Defuses your blades with one of the following — hit before then.

name: Qhatlady
cheats: [Late] "You've got to be kitten me..." - If a Wizard joins the duel after Round 1, Qhatlady will cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
[Aura] "You're purrfect without that!" - If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell, she will cast Supernova on the Wizard.
[Manipulation] "Sparkly!" - After casting her Balance Natural Attack, Qhatlady will cast Steal Charm and Steal Ward on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts a Death version of Sabertooth.
strategy: This is an 11,705-health Empyrea boss with two minions - bring a team with a hitter and support, and everyone joins from the start to dodge Mana Burn.
Hit Life or Myth. She takes +10% Life and +5% Myth while resisting 50% Balance and 45% Death, so avoid those two.
Do not use auras. Any aura makes her hit you with Supernova.
After her Balance natural attack she Steal Charms and Steal Wards from a wizard, so do not over-invest blades and shields on someone she is about to swing at.
Keep setup aura-free: stack blades and Feints on your hitter, clear the two Globulin minions with an AoE, and burst her before her charm and ward steals unwind your setup.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins after round 1 gets hit with Mana Burn, draining pips. Enter from the start.
Casting any aura makes her hit you with Supernova. Skip auras.
After her Balance natural attack she Steal Charms and Steal Wards from the wizard, so do not stack extra blades or shields on the target she just hit.

name: Quartermaster Rackum
cheats: When his minion has less than ~50% Health, the minion will cheat-cast Sacrifice (Death) to heal Quartermaster Rackum.
Using Strangle on the minion will dispel this cheat. Hitting again will re-trigger this cheat.
Whenever the minion takes damage, it will cheat-cast a Regenerate on itself.
strategy: Storm boss (18,495) with a healing minion. The minion is the problem: below ~50% health it Sacrifices itself to heal Rackum, and it Regenerates itself whenever it takes damage.
Strangle the minion to shut off the Sacrifice heal (hitting again re-triggers it, so re-apply Strangle as needed).
He is stunnable; a stun can steal a turn during your burst.

Either kill the minion outright in one big hit (so it cannot Sacrifice-heal at low health) or Strangle it and focus Rackum. Blade up and burst; do not let the fight drag while the minion recycles heals.
cheatNotes: When the minion drops below ~50% health it Sacrifices itself to heal Rackum. Kill it in one hit or Strangle it to stop the heal.
Strangle on the minion dispels the Sacrifice heal, but hitting it again re-triggers the cheat. Re-apply Strangle as needed.
The minion Regenerates itself whenever it takes damage, so chip damage is wasted. Burst it down or leave it Strangled.

name: Quincy Morris

stats.resist: 42% to all schools72% to all schools

cheats: Quincy Morris
[Polymorph] "*Rrrrroooooaaaarrrrr!*" - At the beginning of the first Round, Quincy Morris will polymorph into Werewolf form.
This cheat is re-triggered at the beginning of the fifth Round after unpolymorphing.
[Aura] "No buffs can pierce this hide!" - After polymorphing into werewolf form, Quincy Morris will cast an aura limiting incoming Damage to 1,000.
After Quincy Morris takes six turns, he will revert back to his human form at the end of the Round, and then cast Amplify.
[Trap] "Gah! Snagged! I will recover!" Casting a Trap on Quincy Morris will decrease the time it takes for him to unpolymorph by a single Round.
Spells that apply Traps on all enemies, such as Mass Feint or Windstorm, will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that apply multiple Traps on a target, such as Elemental Trap, counts as a single Trap, and only decreases the time of unpolymorphing by a single Round.

[Harmful Aura] "The wilds make us stronger, we endure." Attempting to replace this Aura, such as with Starfall, will cause Quincy to heal himself to full health, recast his damage cap Aura, and then cheat cast Orthrus.
John Seward
[Polymorph] "*Hoooowwwwllllll!*" - At the beginning of the first Round, John Seward will polymorph into Were-Bear form.
"My power will return..." - At the end of the 6th Round, John Seward will unpolymorph.
This line will also play when an Aura Spell, such as Starfall, is used on him when in Were-Bear form. It will not unpolymorph him early, however.
After John Seward takes six turns, he will revert back to his human form at the end of the Round.
[Curse] Casting a harmful Charm on John Seward will decrease the time it takes for him to unpolymorph by a single Round.
Spells that apply harmful Charms on all enemies, such as Mass Infection, will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that apply multiple harmful Charms on a target, such as Threefold Fever, counts as a single harmful Charm, and only decreases the time of unpolymorphing by a single Round.
[Damage] "The wilds make us stronger, we endure." Using a Damage Spell on John Seward, while in Were-Bear form, will cause him to heal for 780 Health.
spellNotes: Casts a version of Lifeclops that summons a Life Cyclops Minion when there are two or more Wizards present. This fails when there is only one Wizard in battle or if a Life Cyclops is already in the battle.
strategy: Team fight (~22,000 health, plus John Seward): hit with Death; Quincy resists Life 72% and takes +30% Death.
Both bosses polymorph on Round 1, Quincy to a Werewolf (incoming damage capped at 1,000) and John to a Were-Bear (damage on him heals him 780). Don't try to burst through the forms.
Speed the forms off: cast Traps on Quincy to cut his unpolymorph timer by a Round each, and harmful Charms on John to cut his. Single-target only; all-enemy versions don't count.
Do not replace Quincy's damage-cap Aura (for example with Starfall); he heals to full, recasts it, and cheat-casts Orthrus. And do not damage John while he is a Were-Bear.
Wait for the human windows (they revert after six turns; Quincy then Amplifies), then unload blades and Feints into one big Death hit.
cheatNotes: Quincy turns into a Werewolf on Round 1.
He re-polymorphs on the 5th Round after each time he reverts.
In Werewolf form he caps all incoming damage at 1,000. Don't try to burst him then.
After six of his turns he reverts to human at the end of the Round and casts Amplify. Hit during the human window.
Each Trap on Quincy shortens his Werewolf time by a Round. Use traps to strip the form faster.
All-enemy trap spells like Mass Feint or Windstorm don't trigger the speed-up.
Multi-trap spells like Elemental Trap count as one and shorten it by just one Round.
Replacing his damage-cap Aura (for example with Starfall) makes him heal to full, recast it, and cast Orthrus. Never overwrite it.
John Seward turns into a Were-Bear on Round 1.
He unpolymorphs at the end of Round 6.
Using an Aura on him in Were-Bear form plays his line but does not revert him early.
After six of his turns he reverts to human at the end of the Round.
Each harmful Charm on John shortens his Were-Bear time by a Round.
All-enemy curse spells like Mass Infection don't trigger the speed-up.
Multi-charm spells like Threefold Fever count as one and shorten it by one Round.
Damaging John while he is a Were-Bear heals him 780. Wait until he is human.

name: Ra
locations: Krokotopia: The Oasis - Ra's Reading Room
cheats: "Let us accelerate this contest" - At the beginning of the first Round, Ra will interrupt-cast Balance of Power.
"No no, we play by my rules" - If a Wizard attempts to replace the Global, Ra will recast Balance of Power.
"Accuracy is such a fickle thing" - Randomly, Ra will use his turn to cast Black Mantle or a +10% version of Precision, either of which can affect either all allies or all enemies.
If Ra is Spied when this cheat occurs, only the normal versions of Precision or Black Mantle will be revealed.

"Let's balance things out a bit!" - At the end of every fourth Round, Ra will interrupt-cast a Spell that applies an Aura on all participants in the duel. The first Aura that is cast will be Amplify, then Fortify, and then it will cycle between the two until the duel ends.
strategy: Ra is a Rank 4 Balance boss with only 3,000 health behind a wooden skeleton key, meant for level-20-ish wizards. An overleveled wizard ends this almost instantly, so bring a strong hitter and just plan around the auras.
Bring a balance dispel. Ra opens by interrupt-casting Balance of Power and recasts it any time you try to replace the Global, so a dispel is the only clean way to shut it off.
Wear high-accuracy gear or pack Precision. Ra randomly throws Black Mantle or a +10% Precision that can hit either side, so accuracy insurance keeps your setup from fizzling.

Plan burst around the aura cycle. At the end of every fourth round Ra interrupt-casts an aura on everyone: Amplify first (round 4), then Fortify (round 8), alternating. Try to land your big hit on an Amplify round for free extra damage.

Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+10% incoming) and avoid Balance (30% resist). Clear the three minions (Sia ~920, Hu ~800, Heka ~880) with an AoE alongside your hit on Ra, and carry heals for the low-level version of the fight.
cheatNotes: At the very start Ra interrupt-casts Balance of Power as a Global. A balance dispel removes it.
If you try to swap out his Global with your own, Ra just interrupt-recasts Balance of Power. Dispel it rather than trying to overwrite it.
At random, Ra casts Black Mantle or a +10% Precision that can land on either all allies or all enemies, so your accuracy may drop unexpectedly. Bring accuracy gear.
If you Spy Ra during that accuracy cheat, only the plain versions of Precision or Black Mantle are shown, hiding the +10% detail.

At the end of every fourth round Ra interrupt-casts an aura on everyone, starting with Amplify (round 4), then Fortify (round 8), then alternating. Amplify rounds boost damage for both sides, so time your hit there.

name: Ra (Rank 23)
locations: Krokotopia: Grand Exposition - Theater of War
cheats: "The Night serves to balance, to match, the Day." - Ra will say this at the beginning of the first Round.
[Round] "Can you complete the Day?" - At the end of the first Round, Ra will place three protected positive Charms on himself or three protected negative Wards on all Wizards.
These effects indicate the exact amount of helpful Charms or harmful Wards Wizards need before damaging Ra.
These effects are Cloaked and will not be consumed until removed by the cheat below.

[Damage] "Night must be equal to Day, which must match Night." - If a Wizard damages Ra without exactly matching his protected hanging effects, he will cheat-cast his Natural Attack on the offending Wizard, dealing 1500 Balance Damage, then remove all Charms and Wards.
[Afterlife] "You shouldn't skip the story." - If Ra is defeated while his hanging effects are not matched, he will cast Availing Hands, then remove all Charms and Wards.
[Damage] "And thus the Day is complete." - If a Wizard damages Ra while exactly matching his protected hanging effects, he will remove all Charms and Wards, then give the caster a +100% Frenzy Aura.
The hanging effects do not have to be consumed when dealing Damage, and any hanging effects consumed will count towards the total.
After receiving this cheat, all other cheats will stop.
strategy: At the end of round 1 Ra shows the requirement: either three protected positive Charms on himself (you need exactly 3 helpful Charms up) or three protected negative Wards on your team (you need exactly 3 harmful Wards). They are cloaked and are not consumed.
Match that exact count before you attack: stack precisely the required number, no more and no less.
When you damage Ra while your effects exactly match, he wipes everything and hands the hitter a +100% Frenzy Aura, and all his cheats stop.
Never hit him unmatched. An unmatched hit Natural-Attacks for 1,500 and wipes all charms and wards, and killing him unmatched makes him cast Availing Hands and wipe them too.
After the Frenzy reward, blade freely and finish him; Balance resists itself 67%, so hit off-school or with Spectral.
cheatNotes: At the end of round 1 he places either three protected positive Charms on himself or three protected negative Wards on all wizards.
That number is the exact count of helpful Charms or harmful Wards you must have before you damage him.
These effects are cloaked and are not consumed until the matching cheat below removes them.
Damaging him without exactly matching his hanging effects makes him hit the offender for 1,500 Balance and wipe all charms and wards.
Killing him while unmatched makes him cast Availing Hands, then wipe all charms and wards.
Damaging him while your effects exactly match makes him wipe all charms and wards and give the caster a +100% Frenzy Aura.
You do not have to spend the hanging effects to match; any you do consume still count toward the total.
Once you earn the Frenzy reward, all his other cheats stop for the rest of the fight.

name: Rage

stats.resist: 27% to all schools57% to all schools

cheats: "AAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!!!":
At the beginning of the first Round, Rage will cast Combustion and will recast this spell when replaced.
2 Quench spells are required to dispel this cheat.
Casting any Global-type spell will trigger the boss to re-cast Combustion if it is not active.
At the start of every Round, if a Wizard has six or more Pips, Rage will cast Scald.
Power Pips and School Pips count as two Pips.
Shadow Pips do not count toward this cheat.
If a Wizard casts a Spell that costs seven or more Pips, Rage will cast Incindiate.
If a Damage Blade, Damage Trap, Global, or Aura is cast, including secondary effects, Rage will cast a Firecharm giving a random charm as followed:
Firespear: 5%, 10%, 15%
[Pacify] - If a Pacify effect is cast, Rage will cast Pacify, and then cast 3 Quenchs on itself. Any additional Pacify effects will only produce 1 Quench.
[Threat] - If a Taunt effect is cast, Rage will cast Taunt, and then cast Fuel (+35%) on the caster.
strategy: Hit with Ice (+30%), never Fire (57% resist).
Keep your pips under 6. At 6+ pips (power and school pips count double) at the start of a round he Scalds you, so spend pips before they pile up. Shadow pips do not count.
Never cast a 7-pip-or-higher spell or he answers with Incindiate, so win with several smaller Ice hits rather than one huge one.
Avoid feeding him: any damage blade, damage trap, global, or aura you cast gives Rage a random Fireblade or Firespear. Skip self-blades and Feint, rely on flat Ice hits and prisms.
Exploit Pacify: casting a Pacify makes Rage cast 3 Quenches on himself, and 2 Quenches dispel his Combustion, so you can strip his own damage global. Chip him down with sub-6-pip Ice hits.
cheatNotes: He casts Combustion on Round 1 and re-casts it whenever it is replaced.
It takes 2 Quenches to dispel his Combustion, note his own Pacify response casts 3 Quenches on himself.
Casting any global re-triggers his Combustion if it is down, so do not rely on overwriting it.
At the start of any round you hold 6+ pips, he Scalds you, spend pips and stay under 6.
Power pips and school pips each count as two toward that 6-pip limit.
Shadow pips do not count toward the Scald threshold.
Cast a 7+ pip spell and he answers with Incindiate, keep spells cheaper than 7 pips.
Any damage blade, trap, global, or aura you cast (even secondary effects) gives Rage a random Firecharm, avoid buffing in front of him.
Cast a Pacify and he Pacifies then Quenches himself 3 times, which strips his own Combustion, a way to disarm him.
Cast a Taunt and he Taunts then Fuels (+35%) himself, buffing his damage, avoid taunting him.

name: Rattlebones (Rank 14)
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, Rattlebones will cast a Mana Burn at the Wizard, which deals 3,000 Damage.
Unbalance can be used to dispel this cheat.
"Rattlebones will be avenged!" - The Fairy Queen will cast this cheat if Rattlebones has been defeated.
"Let's dampen the Heals!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cheat-cast Doom and Gloom.
Strangle can be used to dispel this cheat.
Once dispelled, heals can be cast successfully (for an unconfirmed amount of time).
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Feint is placed on Rattlebones, he will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.

"Fairies! Protect your Queen!" - At the beginning of the first Round (and every 4 Rounds after a Minion's death), the Fairy Queen will summon 2 Fairies (Rank 14) of random Schools, which become active the following Round, and have 4,160 Health.
Entangle will not dispel this cheat, but instead will cause the Minions to become active two Rounds later.
"You'll never be as charming as Rattlebones!" - If a Charm or Global spell is cast, there is a random chance that she will cast Enfeeble on the Wizard who received the Charm or cast the Global spell.
Dissipate can be used to dispel this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), then she will only remove the Blade from the Wizard who cast the Spell.
Plague and Virulent Plague can trigger this cheat.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on the Fairy Queen, she will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.

"We fight as a team this time. Our victory is assured." - The Fairy Queen will cast Satyr (990) on Rattlebones, which heals an amount equivalent to the Backlash damage dealt by the previous Shadow Sentinel.
The following Fairies will cast these cheats.
Balance, Death, Ice, Life, Myth, Storm

"I shall protect you at all costs, my queen!" - Whenever the Fairy is under ~25% Health, she'll cast a version of Sacrifice that deals 2,100 Death Damage to herself to heal Fairy Queen (Rank 14) for 700 Health; (1,050 with Fairy Queen's Incoming Heal).
Fire

"Ashes to ashes, we all burn down!" - Whenever the Fairy is under ~25% Health, she'll cast a version of Immolate that deals 2,100 Fire Damage to herself to deal 600 Fire Damage to the Wizard who last attacked her.
This spell isn't cast when the Wizard who attacked her is defeated.
spellNotes: Casts a multi-target version of Feint.
Casts a -75% version of Doom and Gloom.
Casts a version of Gold Skeleton which deals 490 Death Damage.
strategy: Run a four-wizard team: Balance, Ice, Storm (hitter), and Death. Balance and Ice carry the fight with their dispels; Death traps and Storm hits.
Everyone joins in the first two Rounds. Joining late triggers a 3,000-damage Mana Burn (Unbalance dispels it if you must arrive late).

Kill the Fairy Queen first. She summons two 4,160-HP fairies on Round 1 and every 4 Rounds after a minion dies, Enfeebles anyone who blades or casts globals, and Satyr-heals Rattlebones, so free blading only comes after she's gone.
Both bosses swap your Feints so you keep the 70% and they get 30%. Enchant Feints with Indemnity, or dispel with Ice Melt / Balance before feinting.
Don't heal with pets or spells near Rattlebones; it cheat-casts Doom and Gloom. Strangle dispels it, otherwise leave healing pets home and use potions.

Layer protected Feints and blades (Aegis/Indemnity) on the Fairy Queen and hit her down, then blade Rattlebones and finish him and any minions with an AoE like Glowbug Squall. Make sure nobody dies on the kill Round or the chest is lost.
cheatNotes: Joining after Round 2 gets you a 3,000 Mana Burn, so join early.
Unbalance dispels the late-join Mana Burn.
If Rattlebones dies while the Fairy Queen lives, she casts an avenge cheat; another reason to drop her first.
Any heal you cast triggers Doom and Gloom (no healing), so Strangle it off or pre-heal before it lands.
Strangle dispels the Doom and Gloom so heals work again for a while.
Once dispelled, heals can be cast successfully for a time.
Even pet heals trigger the Doom and Gloom, so leave healing pets home.
Feinting Rattlebones makes him Swap Ward the 70% trap onto you, so protect it first.
Melt dispels the Swap Ward.
Indemnity on your Feint prevents the Swap Ward entirely.
The Fairy Queen summons two 4,160-HP Fairies on Round 1 and every 4 Rounds after a minion dies, so keep AoE ready and kill her to stop the flow.
Entangle won't stop the summon, only delays the new fairies becoming active by two Rounds.
Casting a charm or global gives a random chance she Enfeebles that wizard, so don't over-rely on globals while she lives.
Dissipate dispels that Enfeeble.
A multi-target blade (like Bladestorm) only loses the caster's copy, so the rest of the team keeps their blades.
Plague and Virulent Plague also trigger the Enfeeble cheat.
Feinting the Fairy Queen also gets Swap Warded onto you, so protect it with Indemnity.
The Fairy Queen Satyr-heals Rattlebones (990) off Shadow Sentinel backlash, so burst him rather than chipping.
A fairy below ~25% health Sacrifices for 2,100 to heal the Queen 700 (1,050 with her incoming heal), so burst fairies down rather than chipping.
A Fire fairy below ~25% Immolates for 2,100 to itself to hit your last attacker for 600 Fire, unless that wizard is already defeated.

name: Rattlebones (Rank 7)
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Rattlebones will cast a Mana Burn at the Wizard.
Unbalance can be used to dispel this cheat.
The Fairy Queen will cast this cheat if Rattlebones has been defeated.
"Lets dampen the Heals!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cheat-cast Doom and Gloom.
Strangle can be used to dispel this cheat.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Why don't we trade?" - When a Feint is placed on Rattlebones, he will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.

"Fairies! Protect your Queen!" - At the beginning of the first Round (and every four Rounds after a Minion is defeated), the Fairy Queen will summon two Fairies (Rank 7) of random Schools, which become active the following Round, and have 1,750 Health.
Entangle will not dispel this cheat, but instead will cause the Minions to become active two Rounds later.

"You'll never be as charming as Rattlebones!" - If a Blade or Global Spell is cast, there is a random chance that the Fairy Queen will cast Enfeeble on the Wizard who received the Blade or cast the Global Spell.
Dissipate can be used to dispel this cheat.
If a multi-target Blade spell is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), then she will only remove the Blade from the Wizard who cast the Spell.
"Why don't we trade?" - When a Feint is placed on the Fairy Queen, she will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts an all-enemies version of Feint.
When revealed from the Monstrological Summon, the Spell resembles a normal Feint.
The Gold Skeleton, which resembles his single-hit Natural Attack, has been observed to deal 470 damage.
strategy: Death boss that resists Death and Ice 80%, so hit with Fire or Life (each +10%) and never attack into Death or Ice. He cannot be stunned.
Feint gets Swap Warded straight back onto you, so protect it with Indemnity, dispel with Melt, or skip Feint and stack blades instead.
Do not heal in the open: any heal, pets included, triggers Doom and Gloom. Bring Strangle to dispel it or top up before it lands.

The Fairy Queen summons two 1,750-health Fairies on Round 1 and every 4 rounds after a minion dies, and can Enfeeble the wizard who blades or casts a global, so spread setup out or Dissipate the Enfeeble.
Funnel blades to one hitter, keep the Fairy adds in check, then land one big Fire or Life hit.
cheatNotes: Join before combat starts or the latecomer gets Mana Burned.
You can Unbalance to dispel that Mana Burn.
If Rattlebones is already down, the Fairy Queen throws the Mana Burn instead.
Any healing spell makes him drop Doom and Gloom to choke your heals.
Strangle removes that Doom and Gloom.
Even pet may-cast heals set off the Doom and Gloom.
Feint him and he Swap Wards it, moving the bigger trap onto you, a bad trade.
Melt can dispel the swapped-back trap.
Indemnity stops the Feint swap entirely, the cleanest fix.
The Fairy Queen summons two random-school 1,750-health Fairies on Round 1 and again every 4 rounds after a minion dies.
Entangle will not stop the summon, it just delays the Fairies going active by two rounds.
Blading or casting a global can draw an Enfeeble onto that wizard from the Queen.
Dissipate removes the Enfeeble.
With a mass blade like Bladestorm she only Enfeebles the caster, not the whole team.
Feint on the Fairy Queen gets swapped back the same way, so protect or skip it there too.

name: Ravik Dreamscape
cheats: "Behold the horrors!" - At the beginning of the first Round and every 5th Round, Ravik will interrupt-cast a -5% version of Doom and Gloom.

"Your curse is not complete!" - At the beginning of the 3rd Round and every other Round thereafter, Ravik will summon a new Cursed Mouse Knight. If his first Minion has been defeated or the battle reaches the 10th Round, Ravik will summon a new Cursed Mouse Knight at the end of every Round thereafter. This will also occur if a negative Ward is cast on Ravik.
spellNotes: Deals 153 Ice Damage for 1 Pip, 245 Ice Damage for 2 Pips, or 340 Ice Damage for 3 Pips and +35% Ice Trap on target.
strategy: Hit with Fire or Myth (+35% each) and avoid Ice (80% resist).
He opens with a -5% Doom and Gloom on Round 1 and every fifth Round; it barely dents healing, so no special counter is needed.
He summons a Cursed Mouse Knight from Round 3, faster after Round 10 or once his first minion dies; casting a negative ward on him also triggers a summon, so avoid trapping him and race him down.
His natural attack applies an Ice Trap on you, which does not affect your Fire or Myth setup; just keep a heal ready.
Stack Fire or Myth blades and Feint, then finish before the board fills with mice.
cheatNotes: He casts a weak -5% Doom and Gloom on Round 1 and every fifth Round; it barely affects healing, so no counter needed.
He summons Cursed Mouse Knights from Round 3, faster after Round 10 or once one dies; a negative ward on him also triggers a summon, so avoid trapping him and burst him instead.

name: Renegade Djinni
cheats: "Mekalekah-Minion!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Renegade Djinni will cast a Balance spell to summon a Renegade Puppet Minion.
"Mekalekah-Shield!" - At the beginning of the second Round, he will use a Shield Minion on his Renegade Puppet.

"Mekalekah-Me!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, he will cast a Polymorph spell on his Renegade Puppet, turning it into a Colossus, and increasing its health to 2,875, but it loses its other stats.
spellNotes: This Boss can cast a Myth version of Balanceblade (+30%).
It has been observed this this Boss casts a Mend Minion that restores 770 health.
strategy: Myth boss (6000). Hit with Storm (+15%); avoid Myth and Balance (25% resist).
He summons a Renegade Puppet round 1 and shields it round 2. On round 4 he Polymorphs the puppet into a Colossus with 2875 health, so kill the puppet before round 4 or it becomes a real tank.
AoE both, or drop the puppet early, then stack blades and land a Storm hit on the Djinni.
cheatNotes: He summons a Renegade Puppet on round 1. Plan to clear it early.
Round 2 he shields the puppet, so hit it before the shield lands or pierce through.
On round 4 the puppet becomes a 2875-health Colossus. Kill it before round 4 to avoid a second tanky body.

name: Renegade Druid
locations: Wizard City: The Catacombs - Crannoc Dorcha
cheats: "An ambush? I'm unafraid, latecomer!" If a Wizard is late joining the duel, he will cast Impede on the late Wizard.
"I unleash my spite upon you!" In the beginning of the first Round, casts a Moon Spell to summon the following Minions:
Renegade Druid (Moon)
Renegade Druid (Star)
Renegade Druid (Shadow)
"My envy burns, let it burn you!" In the beginning of the second Round, casts a version of Shadow Sentinel with 100% intercept.
"My rage is free - it will destroy you!" At the end of every fourth Round, Renegade Druid (Star) will cast an AoE version of one of the following Auras:
Brace
Quicken
Vengeance
"My spite will fuel my powers!" Every ? Rounds, Renegade Druid (Shadow) will cast an AoE version of Donate Shadow, giving a Shadow Pip to all his allies.
"Brace yourself, Scion!" Every ? Rounds, Renegade Druid (Moon) will attack each Wizard with one of the following spells:
Shift Grendel
Shift Dread Paladin
Shift Piscean
Shift Ogre
Shift GreenOak
Shift Sugar Glider
Shift Thornpaw

"Did you really think that would work?" If a Feint is placed on Renegade Druid, he will remove all Wards from himself. Casting Mass Feint results in removal of all Wards from Druid Minions as well. If a Feint with Indemnity is placed on Renegade Druid, any Traps or conversions will be removed and only the Feint will remain.
If any of the Minions are defeated before the Renegade Druid, he will cast Rebirth and revive any defeated Minions at the end of the Round.
spellNotes: Casts an Aegis-protected Stormspear (+10%) and Stormblade (+30%).
strategy: Run this as 1 hitter plus 3 support. Fire (Rain of Fire) or Ice (Snow Angel) hitters are best, needing only about 9 buffs; Death, Life, and Myth work with 10-11 buffs.

The whole fight hinges on the Round 2 Shadow Sentinel with 100% intercept: every AoE you cast on the minions is intercepted onto the Druid, so you kill him without hitting him directly. Do not clear the Sentinel.
Spend Rounds 1-3 stacking roughly 9-11 buffs on the hitter (school blade, Sharpened Blade, Balanceblade/Dark Pact, Elemental or Spirit blade, and a damage aura).
Keep a Storm Dispel on the Druid at all times so he cannot remove your blades, hold 45%+ global resist on the hitter and 60%+ on support, and drop Virulent Plague if a Death wizard is present.
Do NOT Feint him: a Feint makes him strip all his own Wards, and an Indemnity Feint deletes your other traps. Buff with blades instead.
Never kill a minion before the boss or he casts Rebirth and revives them. Round 4 the hitter casts the enchanted AoE (Rain of Fire / Snow Angel) on the minions; intercept damage kills the Druid.
Round 5, mop up the low-health minions with a cheap Tempest treasure card. Ideal clear is 4-6 rounds.
cheatNotes: Join the duel on time; any wizard who enters late gets hit with Impede (an accuracy/pip dispel).
At the start of Round 1 he casts a Moon spell to summon three copies of himself: a Moon, Star, and Shadow minion, each with its own cheats.
On Round 2 he casts a Shadow Sentinel with 100% intercept, so all your minion-targeted AoE damage is redirected onto the Druid himself. This is the exploit the whole fight is built on.
Every fourth round the Star minion drops an AoE aura on his own team (Brace for defense, Quicken for speed, or Vengeance for crit).
The Shadow minion periodically Donates a Shadow Pip to the entire enemy team, speeding up their shadow attacks.
The Moon minion periodically attacks every wizard with a random Shift polymorph spell (Grendel, Dread Paladin, Piscean, and others).

Placing a Feint makes him remove all Wards from himself; a Mass Feint also strips his minions' Wards; a Feint carrying Indemnity deletes any of your other traps or conversions and leaves only the Feint. Avoid Feinting him.
If any minion dies before the Druid, he casts Rebirth at end of round and revives every defeated minion, so kill him first through the intercept.

guide.title: Renegade Druid Strategy Guide and Speed Run

name: Rententen
locations: Novus: La Ville Rose - Sournois Pas
cheats: "Bad timing" - When damaged, Rententen will cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% of his total Health.
This still happens even if Rententen is defeated by the Damage.
"Take your Shot" - At the start of any Round that Rententen casts Snowball Barrage (150), he will gain an Aura.
Attacking Rententen during this turn will not result in him healing himself.
"Missed your chance" - At the end of the Round Rententen used his Aura cheat, he will cast a 0 Damage Star version of Supernova on himself to remove this Aura.
"You can't spy on a legendary spy" - The turn after all of Rententen's Minions are defeated, or they're all beguiled, Rententen will give all Wizards a Legendary Spy card.
[Legendary Spy]
strategy: Rank 22 Death boss (14595) in La Ville Rose. Run a team of four with flexible roles; the fight is about timing damage into a single open window, not chipping.
Round 1: everyone AoEs the minions down — two Life minions (4360) and one Myth minion (3270). Killing them fast cuts incoming damage and triggers the spy handout.
Rounds 2-3: stack blades, traps, and a Feint on Rententen while one wizard casts the Legendary Spy card he gives every wizard once the minions are dead. Use it as intended.
Never hit him on a normal round — any damage triggers a Moon Blessing that heals him to full, even a lethal hit. It is completely wasted.

His only opening is the round he casts Snowball Barrage and gains the 'Take your shot' aura: attacks that turn do NOT heal him. The next round he removes the aura ('Missed your chance') and healing resumes.

Attack with Life (his only boost at +5%; he resists most schools 45%). Funnel a fully-stacked hit onto one wizard and unload it during the Snowball Barrage window — the fight can end as early as round four.
cheatNotes: Any damage to him triggers a Moon Blessing that heals him to full — never hit him on a normal round; it's wasted.
Even a hit that would kill him triggers the full heal — you cannot burst him down on a normal turn.
On any round he casts Snowball Barrage he gains an Aura — this is your window, and attacks this turn don't heal him, so unload your fully-stacked hit here.
Defeat (or beguile) all his minions and he hands every wizard a Legendary Spy card the next turn — use it as intended.

name: Reverend Mother Meowhaim
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness is a transgression!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Reverend Mother Meowhaim will place a protected Spirit Armor (500) on herself.
[Manipulation] "Ancestral powers - activate!" - At the beginning of every third Round, she will cheat-cast Beguile on a random Wizard.
[Feint] "I foresaw such tactics" - If a Wizard places a Feint on her, she will cast Swap Ward to swap the traps.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
Casting a Feint during the Round that she uses Beguile will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Hit with Death - she takes 15% more from Death and resists Life 30%.
She Beguiles a random wizard every 3rd round; solo this can turn your hit around, so plan your attack off those rounds.
If you Feint her she Swap Wards it away. Use Mass Feint instead (does not trigger), protect a single Feint with Indemnity, or place the Feint on the same round she Beguiles (also safe).
She has strong outgoing healing - pack Doom and Gloom or an Infection so she cannot heal back your progress.
Do not join late - a latecomer just gives her a protected 500 Spirit Armor to chew through.
cheatNotes: A wizard joining late gives her a protected Spirit Armor (500 absorb). Enter on time.
Every 3rd round she Beguiles a random wizard. Plan hits around those rounds.
Feinting her makes her Swap Ward the trap off herself. Use Mass Feint, Indemnity, or Feint on her Beguile round.
Indemnity on the Feint prevents the Swap Ward. Recommended for a single Feint.
Mass Feint does not trigger the Swap Ward - a safe way to trap her.
Feinting on the same round she casts Beguile also avoids the Swap Ward.

name: Reversed Chariot Card
locations: Darkmoor: Outsiders Camp - Zone of Fortune

stats.resist: 27% to all schools57% to all schools

cheats: [Protected Trap] "Embrace Your Truth, Use It." - The Reversed Chariot Card will cast a 100% Damage Trap on each Wizard, then give each Wizard a copy of Cleanse Ward (Reversed Chariot Card).
[Cleanse Ward (Reversed Chariot Card)]
This spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat Reversed Chariot Card.
strategy: Cleanse your trap first. It puts a protected +100% damage trap on each wizard, so incoming hits on you are doubled until you cast the Cleanse Ward it hands you.
Attack with Ice (it takes +30% Ice); never Fire (57% resist) and expect 27% resist on the other schools.
Blade, Feint, and hit as normal, just keep your personal +100% trap cleansed so its return hits do not spike.
At 14,485 Health with no minions and one simple cheat, this is soloable with a bladed Ice hitter.
cheatNotes: It puts a protected +100% damage trap on each wizard and hands each of you a Cleanse Ward to remove it. The trap doubles damage you take, so cleanse it before it hits you.
You are not required to cast the Cleanse Ward to win, but doing so prevents doubled incoming damage.

name: Reversed Strength Card
locations: Darkmoor: Outsiders Camp - Zone of Fortune

stats.resist: 27% to all schools57% to all schools

cheats: [Protected Damage over Time] "Embrace Your Truth, Use It." - At the beginning of the First Round, Reversed Strength Card will cast a Protected Damage over Time spell that deals 206,000 Damage over 100 Rounds to each Wizard, then give each Wizard a copy of Triage (Reversed Strength Card).
[Triage (Reversed Strength Card)]
This spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat Reversed Strength Card.
strategy: Attack with Ice; it takes +30% and the card resists Fire 57%, everything else 27%. It is not stunable.
Round 1 it lays a protected DoT on each wizard (206,000 over 100 rounds, roughly 2,000 per round) and hands each of you a special Triage to remove it.
Cast that provided Triage early to clear the DoT, since a normal Triage cannot remove a protected DoT.
After the DoT is handled it is a straight race: stack Ice blades and end its 14,485 health with one big hit before the DoT wears you down.
If you can burst it fast, you can skip the Triage and simply out-damage it, but clearing the DoT is the safer line.
cheatNotes: Round 1 it casts a protected DoT on each wizard (206,000 over 100 rounds, about 2,000 per round) and hands each of you a special Triage to remove it. Cast that Triage early or the DoT grinds you down.
You do not have to cast the provided Triage to win. If you out-race the DoT you can ignore it, but casting it is the safe play.

name: Ricky
cheats: "Cheater!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, then Ricky will interrupt-cast Healing Current on himself three times in a row, healing for 100, 400, and 1,000 from each respective cast.
"Nice try!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, then Ricky will interrupt-cast Steal Charm on the Wizard that received the Blade.
If a triple Blade Spell is cast, then only the last part of the Blade will be removed (Fire for Elemental Blades and Life for Spirit Blades).
If an all allies Blade Spell is cast, then Ricky will only interrupt-cast Steal Charm on the caster.
Supercharge does not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Hit with Myth (+15%); he lightly resists Storm (25%).
He Steal Charms any blade you cast (off the receiver) — use Supercharge (which doesn't trigger it) or triple blades where only the last part is taken.
Alternatively blade and hit the same round so there's nothing to steal.
He's stunable and beguilable — a stun buys a safe setup round.
Blade up and burst his 5,600 health with a Myth hit.
cheatNotes: Late wizards let him Healing Current himself three times (100, 400, 1,000) — join on time.
Cast a blade and he Steal Charms it off whoever received it — use Supercharge or hit the same round.
A triple blade only loses its last part (Fire or Life).
An all-allies blade only gets stolen from the caster.
Supercharge doesn't trigger the steal at all — safe to use.

name: Ridenhoer Delish
cheats: [Round] At the beginning of Round 1, Ridenhoer Delish will interrupt-cast Dr. Von's Monster.
[Healing] If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will interrupt-cast Link at the target that was healed.
Pet heals will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: He takes +35% from Fire and Myth and resists Ice 80%. Hit with Fire or Myth. He is stunnable.
Expect an interrupt Dr. Von's Monster on Round 1; shield or heal up before it lands.
Avoid normal heals: a healing spell makes him Link the healed target (a DoT). Use pet heals instead, which do NOT trigger the Link.
Blade up, stun if handy, and hit with Fire or Myth.
cheatNotes: He interrupts with Dr. Von's Monster on Round 1. Pre-shield or be ready to heal it off.
A healing spell makes him Link the healed wizard (a DoT). Heal with your pet instead, which does not trigger it.
Pet heals are safe; they never trigger the Link.

name: Rogue Engineer
cheats: "Hands up! Hand's down!" - At the beginning of the first Round, two copies of Card Limit (14 Pip) will be added to the Wizard's hand, which cannot be discarded.
[Card Limit (14 Pip)]
"For the Minimalism!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round and every third Round after, a Card Limit (14 Pip) Spell will be added to the Wizard's hand, which cannot be discarded.
If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, four copies of Card Limit (14 Pip) will be added to the Wizard's hand.
"Raise the shields!" - At the end of every Round, even though it is not "cast", Rogue Engineer will use a version of Legion Shield on her team if she has no Shields.
This can be avoided with Melt.
strategy: Round 1 she jams two undiscardable Card Limit (14-pip) cards into your hand, and adds more on round 4 and every 3rd round after. Your hand clogs fast, so win quickly.
She Legion-Shields her whole side at the end of any round she has no shields. Bring Melt to strip them, or pierce through.
Hit with Myth (+5% incoming); avoid Storm (70% resist), so prism if you are a Storm wizard.
Set up with a couple blades and Feint, then burst the 8740 health before the Card Limits choke your hand.
AoE catches the Rampaging Potbelly alongside her.
cheatNotes: Round 1 she stuffs two undiscardable 14-pip Card Limit cards into your hand, shrinking your usable draws. Plan to end the fight fast.
On round 4 and every 3rd round after she adds another Card Limit card. The longer you take, the more clogged your hand gets.
Joining late gives you four Card Limit cards instead of two. Arrive on time.
At the end of any round she has no shields, she Legion-Shields her whole team. Bring Melt or pierce.
Melt prevents or removes that Legion Shield.

name: Rotten Rob
locations: Wizard City: Triton Avenue - Rotten Rob's Hideout
cheats: During the first Round, Rotten Rob will use his turn to cast Scald.
Quench can be used to prevent this spell.

[Damage over Time] "Oh, he's doing that Fire school thing, I think Storms can counter it!" - At the end of every Round, Rotten Rob will cast a version of Efreet (Tier 2b) that deals 100 Fire Damage and deals 450 Fire Damage over 6 Rounds if target has a Damage over Time effect.
Quench can be used to prevent this cheat.
If Rotten Rob's initial Scald fizzles, or is prevented with Quench, he will continue to cast Efreet without any Damage over Time effect.
If the Wizard is second to cast, Triage can be used to remove the Damage over Time and prevent additional Damage over Time effects when Efreet is cast.
[Healing] If Rotten Rob is defeated while his Efreet cheat is still active, then he will revive with full Health.

[Spell] *"Hang on! Try this on him!" - At the beginning of the 4th Round, or after Rotten Rob has revived and then randomly at the end of subsequent Rounds, Daisy Willowmancer will give all Wizards the following Spell:
[Stormzilla (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
[Storm] "Wowzer, Storm douses Fire, huh?" - If a Wizard casts Stormzilla (Tier 2b), Rotten Rob will remove all Damage over Time effects on all Wizards and stop his Efreet cheat.

"How'd you do that?" - If a Wizard uses another Storm Spell that removes Damage over Time effects, such as Lightning Bats (Tier 2b), Rotten Rob will remove all Damage over Time effects from all Wizards and stop his Efreet cheat.
strategy: Hit with Storm or Life (+30%), avoid Ice and Myth (34% resist).
He revives at full health if you kill him while his Efreet DoT cheat is active, so you must shut the cheat off before the killing blow.

Stop the cheat by clearing DoTs: cast the Stormzilla (Tier 2b) card Daisy hands you on Round 4 (or another Storm DoT-remover like Lightning Bats), which makes Rob drop all DoTs and end his Efreet cheat.
Early, Quench his Round 1 Scald (or Triage if you cast second) so no DoT sits on you feeding his Efreet.
Once the Efreet cheat is stopped, blade up and finish him. At only 3,845 health one Storm hit does it.
cheatNotes: He casts Scald on Round 1, putting a Fire DoT on you.
Quench prevents that Scald, cast it Round 1 to stay DoT-free.
At the end of every round he casts Efreet, adding 450 Fire over 6 rounds if you already carry a DoT, this is the cheat that revives him.
Quench also prevents the Efreet cheat.
If his Scald is Quenched or fizzles, he still casts Efreet but without the extra DoT.
If you cast second, Triage removes your DoT and blocks the extra DoT from his Efreet.
Kill him while his Efreet cheat is active and he revives at full health, shut the cheat off first.
On Round 4 (or after he revives) Daisy Willowmancer gives all wizards a Stormzilla (Tier 2b) card, save it to disable the cheat.
Casting that Stormzilla makes Rob remove all DoTs and stop his Efreet cheat, do this before you kill him.
Any Storm spell that removes DoTs (like Lightning Bats) also stops the Efreet cheat.

name: Sand Squid Tentacle
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea - The Poseidon Adventure
cheats: [Minion] "My tendrils are endless!" - If at least one Sand Squid Tentacle Boss is still present, any Tentacle Minions defeated this round will respawn in two rounds.
If a Boss has been defeated when this cheat activates, a Minion will spawn in its place.
If one Boss tentacle and two Tentacle Minions are defeated, then this cheat no longer activates and no more Tentacles will respawn.
[Healing] Whenever a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, each enemy present will cheat-cast Aftershock.
Pet may-cast heals do not activate this cheat.
strategy: Multiple Sand Squid Tentacles for a team. Minions respawn two rounds after death while a boss tentacle stands, so coordinate to defeat one boss tentacle and two minion tentacles to shut off respawns.
Avoid healing spells. Every heal makes each enemy cast Aftershock (an AoE that hits and strips blades). Use pet may-cast heals (which do not trigger it) or shields.
Hit with Storm or Death (+35%, resists Myth 80%); AoE to pressure all tentacles.
Funnel blades to the hitter and burst, timing your kills to hit the respawn-off condition.
cheatNotes: Tentacle minions respawn two rounds after dying while any boss tentacle lives, so killing minions alone will not clear the board.
If a boss tentacle dies as this triggers, a minion spawns in its place.
Defeating one boss tentacle plus two minion tentacles stops all respawns, so aim for that combination.
Every heal makes all enemies cast Aftershock (an AoE that strips blades), so avoid healing spells here.
Pet may-cast heals do not trigger the Aftershock, so those are safe.

name: Sand Squid Tentacle (Boss, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea (Challenge) - The Poseidon Adventure (Challenge)

stats.resist: 8% to all schools28% to all schools48% to all schools

cheats: [Shield] "Here, take this too!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Shield Spell, a Boss will cast Myth Trap (+35%) on the caster.
All Allies Shield Spells such as Legion Shield will still only result in a Myth Trap on the caster.
[Curse] "Here, take this too!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Charm Spell, a Boss will cast Tower Shield on the recipient of the Charm.
All Enemies harmful Charm Spells such as Plague will cause the Boss to cast Legion Shield instead.

[Minion] "My tendrils are endless!" - At the beginning of the Round after a Sand Squid Tentacle Minion is defeated, any Bosses still present will say this as a warning. Any Minions defeated will be resummoned during the next Round.
strategy: Solo-friendly (5,325 health). Do not cast shields: each one gives you a +35% Myth Trap so the boss hits you harder.
Do not cast weaknesses or harmful charms on the tentacles; they Tower Shield the target, or Legion Shield if it is an all-enemies charm.
Hit with Fire or Life (only 8% resist); avoid Ice and Death (48%).
Killed tentacle minions resummon the next round, so do not chase them. AoE the boss and minion together, or blade a single hit onto the boss and race it down.
Blade up (charms are safe), skip shields and weaknesses, then unload.
cheatNotes: Every shield you cast puts a +35% Myth Trap on you, so the boss hits you harder. Skip shields.
Even an all-allies shield only yields one Myth Trap, on the caster.
Casting a weakness or harmful charm on a tentacle makes it Tower Shield the target instead. Do not debuff with charms.
An all-enemies weakness like Plague makes it cast Legion Shield on the whole group.
Kill a tentacle minion and it returns the next round, so race the boss down rather than chasing minions.

name: Satharilith

stats.resist: 5% to all schools70% to all schools

cheats: "More? You too will perish!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Satharilith will cast Mana Burn (Shadow) on the Wizard.
At the start of the first Round, the Valencian Royal Guard closest to Satharilith will cast a version of Shadow Sentinel which intercepts 100% of Damage.
When the Minion who cast the Shadow Sentinel is defeated, the next Valencian Royal Guard will recast the Shadow Sentinel.
This cheat stops when all three Valencian Royal Guard Minions have been defeated.

"I twist your powers into nothing!" - When Feint is cast on the Boss or the Minions, Satharilith will cast an AoE Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all Traps from the target. Mass Feint will also trigger this cheat.

"How simple, to turn your powers against you" - When one of the Valencian Royal Guards is defeated, the Boss will use its Natural Attack on the Wizard opposite the defeated Minion, and summon one of the following Minions with the same school as that Wizard. If there is not a Wizard in the opposite location, it will target the first target with a Natural Attack. Aspect Minions will not recast the 100% intercept Shadow Sentinel.
Balance Aspect
Death Aspect
Fire Aspect
Ice Aspect
Life Aspect
Myth Aspect
Storm Aspect

"Your mind is mine to toy with!" - If there are any missing Valencian Royal Guard minions at the start of the battle, Satharilith will summon a random Aspect Minion for each. The defeat of these Aspect Minions will trigger the above cheat.
strategy: Run a 4-wizard team: a Fire hitter with 120+ damage and 20+ pierce, plus three supports (Death and Balance are best for extra blades), all in high-resist gear. Give one support Pigsie for emergency healing.
All four wizards must enter the fight on time. Anyone who joins late eats a Shadow Mana Burn, so load in together before starting.
Do not use Feint or any traps. Feint (and Mass Feint) makes Satharilith cast an AoE Cleanse Ward that wipes all traps off the target, so setup is done entirely with blades.

Stack about 14 blades on the Fire hitter over 3-4 rounds: supports pile on Elemental Blade, Balanceblade, Dark Pact, and Sharpened versions while the hitter self-blades Fire Blade twice and adds Infallible.

Kill with Rain of Fire, not Meteor Strike. The lead Royal Guard casts a Shadow Sentinel that intercepts 100% of damage, and Rain of Fire is the only known hit whose damage-over-time still lands through an active Sentinel. Keep Meteor Strike as a backup finisher once the Sentinels are gone.
Expect the fight to run 6-7 rounds because of the guards and Aspect summons. Carry spare blades on supports to rebuild if the boss strips buffs, and restart if a stun or death breaks the setup.
cheatNotes: Anyone loading into the fight after it starts gets hit with a Shadow Mana Burn that drains their pips. Get all four wizards in before the first round.
The Royal Guard nearest Satharilith immediately puts up a Shadow Sentinel that soaks 100% of all incoming damage, which is why normal hits do nothing until it is gone.
Kill the guard holding the Sentinel and the next guard just re-raises it, so a plain hit stays blocked no matter how many blades you stack.
Once all three Royal Guards are dead, no new Sentinel goes up and your hits finally connect for full damage.
Casting any Feint or Mass Feint makes Satharilith cast an AoE Cleanse Ward that removes every trap on the target, so traps are wasted here. Set up with blades only.

Killing a Royal Guard makes the boss hit the wizard opposite the dead minion and summon an Aspect Minion matching that wizard's school. For example, defeating the guard across from your Storm support spawns a Storm Aspect. These Aspects do not re-raise the 100% Sentinel.
If any Royal Guard is already missing when the fight begins, Satharilith replaces each one with a random Aspect Minion, and killing those Aspects still triggers the opposite-wizard attack and summon.

name: Senior Cabal Soldier
locations: Novus: La Ville Rose - Matheus' House
cheats: "All those Mega-Snacks were worth it!" - Senior Cabal Soldier's Pet will cast the following Spells:
Brace
Dragonlance
strategy: Balance boss; hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+5% each) and avoid the schools in his 45% resist.
His only cheats are pet-cast: it casts Brace (a shield) and Dragonlance on his side, so expect modest self-protection rather than punishing counters.
With no reactive punish cheats, play the standard loop: stack blades and Feint over a few Rounds.
Clear the Cabal Soldier (Fire) minion or split your hit, then finish with one big Myth, Life, or Death hit.
cheatNotes: His pet casts support spells for him; expect the two below rather than reactive punishes.
The pet Braces (shields) him; strip it or just hit through it.
The pet can cast Dragonlance to buff his side; nothing you must react to, just push your setup.

name: Shadow Self Magician
cheats: [Aura] "I am your mirror. Speak your words, feel your heart." - At the beginning of the First Round, Shadow Self Magician will cast an Invulnerability Aura with no round limit.
This Aura can be replaced with Negative Auras such as Attenuate without penalty, but the Invulnerability effect will not be removed.

[Damage over Time][Heal over Time] "You must acknowledge painful truths and comfortable lies." - At the beginning of the Second Round, Shadow Self Magician will apply as many Damage over Time effects dealing 1035 damage over 10 Rounds to itself as there are Wizards in the duel, and will apply a Heal over Time effect healing 555 health over 10 Rounds to each Wizard.

Starting at the end of the Fourth Round and every other Round thereafter, Shadow Self Magician will add one Damage over Time effect to itself or one Heal over Time effect to a random Wizard and restore its Invulnerability Aura.
[Charm][Ward] "Yes. See the obvious. Embrace the hurt. Ignore the lies." - Triggers when a Wizard casts a Charm or Ward.
If a Wizard casts a Positive Charm or Ward, Shadow Self Magician will shift one Healing Over Time effect from the Wizard who cast the spell onto itself.
Positive Charms or Wards that apply multiple hanging effects such as Elemental Blade and Elemental Shield will cause multiple Heal over Time effects to be shifted.
If a Wizard casts a Negative Charm or Ward, Shadow Self Magician will shift one Damage over Time effect from itself to the Wizard that cast the spell.
Negative Charms or Wards that apply multiple hanging effects such as Elemental Trap and Fuel will cause multiple Damage over Time effects to be shifted.
Feint, which places a Negative Ward on both the caster and Shadow Self Magician, will cause Shadow Self Magician to shift one Damage over Time effect, and one Heal over Time effect.

[Manipulation] "Are you seeing the truth beyond the comfortable lie?" - When all Healing over Time effects are applied to Shadow Self Magician and all Damage over Time effects are applied to the Wizard(s), Shadow Self Magician's Invulnerability Aura will be removed.
If a Negative Aura has been used to replace Shadow Self Magician's Invulnerability Aura, it will be removed.

[Afterlife] "You deny pain, but its shadow lingers." - If Shadow Self Magician is defeated by a Wizard who does not have a Damage over Time effect shifted to them, it will revive after being defeated.
strategy: Shadow puzzle boss (17295) that starts Invulnerable; you cannot damage it until you solve its aura. Once vulnerable, hit with any school through its 35% resist.
Round 2 it loads Damage-over-Times on itself (one per wizard) and Heal-over-Times on you. To drop its Invulnerability you must move ALL those DoTs onto the wizards and ALL the HoTs onto it.

Cast negative charms and wards (Feint, traps, Weakness) to shift a DoT from it onto yourself; cast positive charms and wards (blades, shields) to shift a HoT from you onto it. Feint shifts one of each.
Once the aura drops, hit it, but the killer MUST have a DoT shifted onto them or it revives. Make sure your hitter is carrying one of its DoTs on the kill.
It re-adds a DoT/HoT and restores Invulnerability every other round from round 4, so keep shifting and finish before it rebuilds.
cheatNotes: Round 1 it becomes Invulnerable with no time limit; no damage lands until you solve the aura. Do not waste hits into it.
You can overwrite the aura with a negative one, but the Invulnerability effect stays. Removing it requires the DoT/HoT swap, not an aura strip.
Round 2 it puts a DoT on itself per wizard and a HoT on each wizard. Your goal is to reverse both: DoTs onto you, HoTs onto it.
From the end of round 4, every other round it adds a DoT/HoT and restores Invulnerability. Finish before it rebuilds the shield.
Casting Charms or Wards is how you move the effects, as detailed below.
A positive Charm or Ward (blade, shield) shifts one Heal-over-Time from you onto it. Feed it all the HoTs.
Multi-effect buffs like Elemental Blade shift several HoTs at once, which is efficient for loading heals onto it.
A negative Charm or Ward (trap, Weakness) shifts one Damage-over-Time from it onto you. Pull all the DoTs onto the team.
Multi-effect debuffs like Elemental Trap shift several DoTs at once. Use them to speed up the swap.
Feint moves one DoT and one HoT in a single cast, the most efficient tool for the swap.
When every HoT is on it and every DoT is on the wizards, its Invulnerability drops. That is your window to attack.
If the wizard who lands the kill has no DoT shifted onto them, it revives. Make sure your hitter is carrying one of its DoTs on the kill shot.

name: Shadow Trickster
locations: Arcanum: The Arcanum - Shadow Training Room
cheats: [Damage] After using his Natural Attack, the Shadow Trickster will interrupt-cast Steal Pips.
spellNotes: Always uses his first turn to cast Dark Pact (+30%).
strategy: Expect pip theft after his attack. Every time he uses his natural attack he interrupt-casts Steal Pips, so do not sit on a large pip pool he can raid; spend or hit before he swings.
Bank your setup before his attack turns. Blade and trap, then hit promptly rather than saving pips across his natural-attack rounds.
He opens with Dark Pact (+30%) and a protected Shadowblade, so his first hits scale up; shield or race him before that damage lands.
He resists 35% to all schools but has only 15% boost and low pierce (5%), so any school works; stack blades to overcome the flat resist.
He is stunnable and beguilable, so a stun or beguile can skip an attack (and its Steal Pips) and give you a free setup round.
Build a quick stack and burst his 7,840 Health in one hit before Steal Pips can drain your momentum.
cheatNotes: After each natural attack he interrupt-casts Steal Pips, so avoid holding a big pip pool going into his attack turns; spend or hit first.

name: Shadow Trickster (Rank 22)
cheats: [Charm][Curse][Ward][Jinx] "Act, wizard, I always act!" - At the end of the Round after a Wizard casts a Charm or Ward, Shadow Trickster will remove one Charm or Ward and heal itself for 10% of its maximum Health.
The type of effect removed corresponds to the most recently cast effect.
This cheat can trigger multiple times in one Round.
Auras will not trigger this cheat.
Aegis or Indemnity does not prevent the removal.
Casting an attack before the corresponding effect is removed will prevent this cheat.
strategy: After you cast a Charm or Ward, at the end of that Round it removes one matching effect and heals 10% of its max Health, and it can do this several times in one Round.
The counter is timing: cast your attack the same Round, before the effect would be removed, and the cheat does not fire (Auras never trigger it, and Aegis or Indemnity do not stop it).
So set up and hit in a single Round rather than blading over several turns; do not leave charms or wards sitting on the board into the end of the Round.
It resists every school 42% with no weakness, so bring pierce or a prism and a strong enchanted hit.
It is stunable and beguilable, so a stun can buy you a clean setup-and-hit Round.
cheatNotes: At the end of the Round after you cast a Charm or Ward, it removes one Charm or Ward and heals 10% of its max Health.
The effect it removes matches the most recently cast one.
This can trigger several times in a single Round.
Auras do not trigger this cheat.
Aegis and Indemnity do not prevent the removal.
Casting your attack before the effect is removed prevents the cheat entirely.

name: Shadow of the Land
cheats: [Damage] At the beginning of every fifth round, Shadow of the Land will interrupt with Power Nova dealing damage based on how many Laughing Calaca minions are in the duel.
"My Shadow brings ruin to all!" - No Minions: Deals 400 Balance damage.
