"This Undead doubles your doom!" - 1 Minion: Deals 800 Balance damage.
"Every soul feeds the power of my Shadow!" - 2 Minions: Deals 1,600 Balance damage.
"Undead darkness fuels my Shadow!" - 3 Minions: Deals 3,200 Balance damage.
Unbalance on the boss can dispel this cheat.
[Minion] "Rise from the Dead!" - He will randomly summon another minion after any of them are defeated.
spellNotes: The Shadow of the Land uses a version of Shadow Sentinel with +15 Backlash.
strategy: Hit Myth, Life, or Death, each +30%, and never Balance, which he resists 80%.
Keep the Laughing Calaca minion count low before every fifth Round. His Power Nova scales from 400 with no minions up to 3,200 with three.
Better yet, keep an Unbalance on the boss to dispel the Power Nova cheat outright.
He resummons a minion after each one dies, so do not over-invest in the adds. Focus setup on the boss.
Stack blades and Feint and drop one Myth, Life, or Death hit, clearing stray minions with an AoE.
cheatNotes: Every fifth Round he interrupts with Power Nova, and its damage scales with how many Laughing Calaca minions are alive.
With no minions up it deals only 400 Balance damage.
One minion up: 800 Balance damage.
Two minions up: 1,600 Balance damage.
Three minions up: 3,200 Balance damage. Keep the count low before Round 5 and 10.
An Unbalance on the boss dispels the Power Nova cheat.
He randomly resummons a minion whenever one is defeated, so they keep coming.

name: Shadowbones
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the Second Round, Shadowbones will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard.
[Manipulation] "Shadow-shadow! I love shadow!" - He will randomly cast Dark Convert once in the fight, which converts three Pips into one Shadow Pip for himself.
[Feint] "Why don't we trade!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Shadowbones, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to exchange it with the Wizard.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
[Heal] "Let's dampen the heals!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%).
Pet Heals will trigger this cheat.
[Aura] "Oh, how cute!" - If any type of Fairy Spell is used (by friend or foe), he will cast Magnify on himself.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Tower Shield (-55%), Wraithblade, and Sharp Wraithblade.
Deals 82.5 Shadow Damage per Pip to all enemies, rounded down
strategy: Hit Fire or Life. He takes +10% from both and resists 40% Ice and Death, so those are your damage schools. Join by round 2 to avoid Mana Burn.
Set up Feints, but do it safely. A normal Feint on him gets swapped back onto you. Use Mass Feint (+75%), the Balance version of Feint, or cover a normal Feint with Indemnity.
Do not heal into him unprotected. Any heal - including pet heals - makes him cast Doom and Gloom (-75% healing).
Avoid Fairy spells from either side; a Fairy makes him Magnify himself for more damage.
He is not stunable and can Dark Convert three pips into a Shadow pip once, so do not stall. Stack blades, land a Mass Feint, then finish with one big Fire or Life hit through his 12,800 health.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins after round 2 gets hit with Mana Burn, draining pips.
Once per fight he randomly casts Dark Convert, turning three of his pips into a single Shadow pip.
Placing a normal Feint on him makes him Swap Ward it back onto you.
Mass Feint (+75%) is exempt - it does not get swapped back, so use it for setup.
The Balance version of Feint is also exempt from the swap.
Indemnity also protects a normal Feint from being swapped.
Casting any healing spell makes him cast Doom and Gloom (-75% healing).
Pet heals also trigger that Doom and Gloom.
Any Fairy spell used by either side makes him Magnify himself.

name: Shadowwock
cheats: "It's after brillig!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Shadowwock will cast Mana Burn at the Wizard, dealing 3,000 Balance damage.
"Snicker-Snack" - Beginning on Round 2, he will interrupt to remove all Traps from himself at the start of every second Round (Round 4, Round 6, etc.).
Indemnity can be used to protect traps from this cheat.

"You make me frumious!" - At the beginning of every three Rounds, he will cast a Shadow version of Meteor Strike that randomly deals 1650 - 3,300 Shadow damage, and places a +200% universal Trap on himself.
He will not always cast the trap if a Wizard is defeated with the Meteor Strike.
Using Weakness-type spells can help reduce the amount of damage dealt by his Meteor Strike.
"How mimsy!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on Shadowwock, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to swap the Traps with the Wizard.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) doest not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Whenever a Fizzle occurs, Shadowwock will cast a Shadow version of Empower, similar to Dark Surge to give himself 2 Power Pips.
After casting his Natural Attack, Shadowwock will cast a protected Black Mantle (Shadow) on the Wizard.
spellNotes: Deals 110 Shadow Damage per Pip, despite the Spell card's 3-Pip maximum description.
strategy: Attack with Ice (+10%); he resists Fire 40%.

Exploit his self-trap: every 3 rounds he casts a Shadow Meteor Strike (1,650-3,300) and drops a +200% universal Trap on himself. That trap is yours to use, so line up blades and hit into it right after for massive damage.
His Meteor Strike hurts, so soften it with Weakness-type spells and keep shields up on the 3-round timer.
He strips all traps off himself every 2nd round (round 4, 6, and so on), so protect your own traps with Indemnity or rely on the +200% self-trap instead.
Do not Feint him normally: he Swap Wards the trap onto you (Melt or Indemnity avoids it), though Mass Feint and Balance Feint do not trigger it.
Keep your accuracy up since every fizzle gives him 2 power pips. Set up blades, wait for his +200% self-trap, and burst.
cheatNotes: Late arrivals eat a 3,000 Balance Mana Burn, so join on time.
Starting round 2, every second round (4, 6, and so on) he strips all traps off himself, so protect key traps with Indemnity.
Indemnity protects your traps from that removal.
Every 3 rounds he casts a Shadow Meteor Strike (1,650-3,300) and puts a +200% universal Trap on himself. Hit into that self-trap for huge damage.
He may skip the self-trap if the Meteor Strike defeats a wizard.
Weakness-type spells reduce his Meteor Strike damage, so pack one.
Feinting him makes him Swap Ward the trap onto you. Use Melt or Indemnity, or skip Feint.
Melt dispels the swapped trap.
Indemnity avoids the swap entirely.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger the swap, a safe way to trap him.
The Balance version of Feint also does not trigger it.
Every fizzle hands him 2 power pips via an Empower, so keep your accuracy high.
After his Natural Attack he puts a protected Black Mantle on you (fizzle chance), so cleanse it or lean on accuracy.

name: Shaka Zebu
cheats: "Nature Spirit protect me!" - If Shaka Zebu is not at full Health, a Satyr will heal him by interrupt at the beginning of the Round.
Placing an Entangle on Shaka Zebu will dispel the Satyr for the next Round.
This cheat will stop if Shaka or all of his Minions are defeated.
"Where did everyone go?" - If all of his Minions are defeated, Shaka will say this line. The Meteor Strike cheat will follow a few rounds later.
"Feel Shaka Zebu's Might!" - Every 2-3 rounds, Shaka will interrupt-cast a 600-750 Damage version of Meteor Strike. This cast can be prevented with Quench.
This cheat will also begin if all of his Minions are Beguiled.
spellNotes: Deals 275 Balance Damage for 3 Pips maximum, despite the Spell card's description.
strategy: He heals via an interrupt Satyr whenever he's below full health, so either Entangle him to shut off the heal for the next round, then hit, or one-shot him from full so the Satyr never fires.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (he takes +25% from all three); never hit into his 80% Balance resist without a prism.
Bring Quench to stop his Meteor Strike (600-750 every 2-3 rounds).
Stack blades and a Feint, then burst his 5,015 health in one big hit.
cheatNotes: While he's below full health a Satyr heals him by interrupt — Entangle him or one-shot him from full so the heal can't keep up.
Casting Entangle on Shaka dispels the Satyr for the next round — your window to hit safely.
Every 2-3 rounds he interrupt-casts Meteor Strike for 600-750 — Quench prevents it.

name: Shane von Shane
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Upper Halls
cheats: "I can see through you!" - If a Feint is placed on Shane von Shane, he will cast a Dark Pact (+30%).
Mass Feint does not trigger this cheat.
"I will not bow down to you!" - If a negative Charm, such as Weakness or Bad Juju, is placed on him, he will cast Sacrifice (750) on himself.
Multi-target negative Charms such as Plague do not trigger cheat.
"More life for me!" - If a Wizard uses a Healing Spell, he will cast an enhanced version of Vampire at the Wizard, which deals 1,050 Health Steal Damage and returns half to the caster.
Pet 'May Cast' Heals will trigger this cheat.
This cheat will not activate while beguiled.
Death Interrupt Cycle:

"We feast tonight!" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Shane von Shane will cast an enhanced version of Vampire at a random Wizard, which deals 1,050 Health Steal Damage and returns half to the caster.
"Hiss!" - This cycle can be reset by attacking Shane von Shane.
Steal spells, such as Ghoul or Lord of Night, will not reset this cycle.
Mana Burn and Supernova will not reset this cycle.
Damage over Time attacks, such as Fire Elf or Link, will reset this cycle.
"The monster begins to rise!" - At the beginning of the third Round in this cycle, he will cast Dr. Von's Monster (900) on a random Wizard.
This cycle can be reset by attacking Shane von Shane, following the same rules as above.
"It's a graveyard smash!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round in this cycle, he will cast Monster Mash (1,170), which Beguiles the Wizard.
strategy: Team fight (19k, Darkmoor). Hit with Life (+20%); he resists Death 50%.

Attack him regularly to reset his Death Interrupt Cycle — it escalates from a 1,050 Vampire (round 2) to Dr. Von's Monster (round 3) to a Beguiling Monster Mash (round 4). A normal attack or DoT resets it; steals, Mana Burn, and Supernova do NOT.
Use Mass Feint, not single Feint — a single Feint makes him Dark Pact himself +30%.
Use multi-target curses (Plague) rather than single negative charms, which make him Sacrifice.

Avoid healing when possible — a heal triggers a 1,050 Vampire on the healer; if you must heal, shield first (note his 40% pierce). Blade the hitter, keep him attacked each round, and burst him and the Darkmoor minions down.
cheatNotes: Put a single Feint on him and he Dark Pacts himself +30% — use Mass Feint instead.
Mass Feint doesn't trigger it.
A single negative charm (Weakness, Bad Juju) makes him Sacrifice for 750 — use multi-target curses.
Multi-target curses like Plague don't trigger it.
Heal and he hits the healer with a 1,050 Vampire — shield or avoid heals.
Pet may-cast heals trigger the Vampire too.
Round 2 of his cycle he Vampires a random wizard for 1,050 — attack him to reset the cycle.
Attacking him resets the cycle.
Steal spells (Ghoul, Lord of Night) won't reset it.
Mana Burn and Supernova won't reset it.
DoT attacks (Fire Elf, Link) DO reset it — handy.
Round 3 of the cycle he casts Dr. Von's Monster (900) on a wizard.
Attacking him resets this too.
Round 4 of the cycle he casts Monster Mash (1,170) and Beguiles the wizard — keep him attacked so it never reaches this.

name: Shane von Shane (Star)
cheats: [Charm] "I revel in my power!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Supercharge (+12%) on himself.
[Curse] "I will not bow down to you!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm on Shane, he will cheat-cast Sacrifice (Death) on himself.
Secondary Spell effects will trigger this cheat.
Harmful Charms that target all enemies will not trigger this cheat.
[Feint] "I can see through you" - If a Wizard casts a Feint on Shane, he will cheat-cast Dark Pact (+30%) on himself.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.

[Healing] "More life for me!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Vampire, dealing 1,050 Death Drain Damage on a random Wizard and heals the caster for half the Damage dealt.
Beguiled players will not trigger this cheat.
[Damage] "Bah, you are nothing!" - Whenever Shane is Damaged, he will cheat-cast a version of Healing Current on himself that restores 100, 400 or 1,000 Health.
Dissipate can be used to Dispel this cheat.
Damage over Time effects will trigger this cheat.
Supernova will not trigger this cheat.
Interrupt Cycles - This fight revolves around 2 independent cheat cycles: a "Storm Interrupt Cycle" and a "Death Interrupt Cycle."
"Prepare for the thunder!" - At the end of the first Round in this cycle, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Stormblade on himself.
"And the lightning!" - At the end of the second Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Stormspear on himself.
"The storm grows!" - At the end of the third Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Galvanic Field.
"And shatters you!" - At the end of the fourth Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast his Natural Attack on a random Wizard.
[Damage] "Hiss!" - Whenever Shane von Shane is Damaged by a Wizard, the cycle resets to Round one on the following turn. Otherwise, nothing happens during the first Round of this cheat cycle.

"We feast tonight!" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Vampire, dealing 1,050 Death Drain Damage on a random Wizard and heals the caster for half the Damage dealt.
"The monster begins to rise!" - At the beginning of the third Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast a Dr. Von's Monster (900) on a random Wizard.
"It's a graveyard smash!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, Shane von Shane will cheat-cast Monster Mash (1,170) on a random Wizard, beguiling them.
This cheat will continue to occur every Round until Shane von Shane is hit again.
Supernova and Mana Burn will not reset this cycle.
Wizards that are beguiled will not trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: This Boss lacks Mastery of the School of Storm.
strategy: Darkmoor Graveyard boss (21,200). He takes +20% from Myth and Life and resists Storm and Death 50%. Hitters use Myth or Life.

He heals himself (Healing Current) whenever he is damaged, and DoTs trigger it repeatedly. Dissipate dispels this heal-on-damage; Supernova is the exception that does NOT trigger it. Avoid DoTs and set up for one big hit.

He runs two independent cheat cycles (a Storm one and a Death one). Damaging him resets the Death cycle to Round 1, so hitting him each round keeps the Vampire, Dr. Von's, and Monster Mash escalation from building.
Do not heal: a heal triggers an enhanced Vampire (1,050 drain). Beguiled wizards do not trigger it, but plan healing around his cheats.
Do not single-Feint him (he gets Dark Pact +30%); use Mass Feint (+75%), which does not trigger it. Helpful charms give him Supercharge, so blade in the same round you hit.

Roles: supports Mass Feint and blade the hitter, the hitter lands a big Myth or Life single hit (no DoTs), and the team keeps chipping him to reset the Death cycle. The healer times heals carefully given the Vampire punish.
cheatNotes: A helpful charm makes him Supercharge himself (+12%). Blade in the same round you hit so he gains less.
A harmful single-target charm on him makes him Sacrifice himself, minor self-harm, but do not rely on it.
Secondary-effect debuffs trigger that Sacrifice too.
All-enemies harmful charms do NOT trigger it, so multi-target debuffs are safe.
A Feint on him gives him Dark Pact (+30% damage). Use Mass Feint instead.
Mass Feint (+75%) is safe; it does not trigger Dark Pact.
A heal makes him cast an enhanced Vampire (1,050 drain) on a random wizard. Heal sparingly and expect the hit.
Beguiled wizards do not trigger the heal-Vampire, a niche safety valve.
Whenever he is damaged he heals himself (Healing Current, 100-1,000). Dissipate dispels this; plan for one big hit rather than many small ones.
Dissipate removes his heal-on-damage. Worth casting before your burst.
DoTs trigger the self-heal every tick. Do not use damage-over-time here.
Supernova does NOT trigger the self-heal, a clean way to damage him.
Storm cycle round 1: he Stormblades himself. His Storm hits ramp over the cycle.
Round 2: he adds Stormspear. Expect a bigger Storm hit coming.
Round 3: he casts Galvanic Field. Plan around the trap.
Round 4 of the Storm cycle: his Natural Attack hits a random wizard. Shield or heal for it.
Damaging him resets the Death cheat cycle to Round 1. Chip him every round to keep the Vampire and Monster Mash escalation from building.
Death cycle round 2: enhanced Vampire (1,050) on a random wizard if you have not re-hit him.
Round 3: Dr. Von's Monster (900) on a random wizard.
Round 4: Monster Mash (1,170) beguiles a random wizard. Keep hitting him to reset before this.
If you stop hitting him, the Death cycle cheats repeat every round until you hit him again. Keep the pressure on.
Supernova and Mana Burn do NOT reset the Death cycle, so they will not stop the escalation; hit him for real to reset it.
Beguiled wizards cannot reset the cycle by hitting, so a beguiled hitter is a liability.

name: Shark Principal
locations: Lemuria: Night Forest - Second Story Tomb
cheats: [Late] "Look at you, flailing around!" - If a Wizard is late, Shark Principal will cheat-cast his Storm Shark which devours the Wizard for three turns.
[Manipulation] "Mine!" - At the end of every Round, Shark Principal uses Steal Pip.
[Shadow][Spell] "Nom-Nom" - Boss will cast a Shadow version of Storm Shark which devours the Wizard.
[Feint] "Safe At Last" - If Feint is cast on Shark Principal, Shark Student will cast a Shadow Spell moving the Feint onto himself.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.

[Round] "Combat is Hungry Work!" - Every 5 turns, Shark Principal casts a Shadow version of Storm Shark, which first removes all Charms, Wards and Auras then devours a Wizard, dealing no immediate Damage.
While Wizards are devoured, they gain no Pips, lose no Pips, and do not get to see their cards. They do not benefit from group heals, or suffer damage from group attacks.
Devoured Wizards are skipped. Shark Principal still steals a Pip every turn, but Wizards do not lose a Pip when devoured.
At the conclusion of the third Round of being devoured, Shark Principal casts a Shadow version of Tempest, dealing 3,000 Damage.
[Afterlife] "Dinner... is cancelled" - After the Boss is defeated, a Tempest (Shadow) will be cast on the Wizards.
[Afterlife] After any combatant in the battle ring is defeated, a Tempest (Shadow) will be cast on the Wizards, using none of his pips.
This cheat may trigger multiple times.
This cheat will cause damage even to Devoured Wizards.
strategy: Hit with Myth or any non-Storm school; he resists Storm 73% and takes only +5% Myth.
He Steals a Pip at the end of every Round, so your pip economy is slower. Bring blades and Feint you can afford and hit inside four rounds.
Every 5th turn he strips all Charms, Wards, and Auras from a Wizard and devours them (a 3-round Shadow Tempest for 3,000). Land your kill before that strip.
If you Feint him, protect it with Indemnity or use the Balance Feint; otherwise the Shark Student moves your Feint onto himself.
Expect Shadow Tempests after any combatant dies, including at his own death. Shield Storm or top off before and after the kill.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets you devoured by a Storm Shark for three turns.
He Steals a Pip from a Wizard at the end of every Round. Expect slower setup.
He can cast a Shadow Storm Shark to devour a Wizard.
Feint on him is moved onto the Shark Student instead.
Indemnity protects your Feint from being moved.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger the move.
Every 5th turn he strips all Charms, Wards, and Auras off a Wizard, then devours them.
A devoured Wizard gains and loses no Pips and cannot see their cards.
A devoured Wizard's turn is skipped, and they do not lose a Pip to his steal while eaten.
After three rounds devoured, the Shark hits that Wizard with a 3,000 Shadow Tempest.
When he dies, a Shadow Tempest hits all Wizards. Shield before the kill.
Any combatant dying triggers a free Shadow Tempest on all Wizards.
This can happen several times.
These Tempests hit even devoured Wizards.

name: Simon the Sayer
locations: Wizard City: Sunken City - Simon's Workshop
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the second Round, then Simon will interrupt-cast an enhanced version of Mana Burn that deals 400 Balance Damage on the Wizard.
[Round] "Simon says no attacks!" - For the first three Rounds, using a Damage Spell on Simon or one of his Minions will result in Simon retaliating against the caster.

[Damage] "Simon didn't say!" - If a Damage Spell is cast on Simon or one of his Minions while Simon says "no attacks", then Simon will interrupt-cast a version of Insane Bolt that always hits the Wizard who cast the Damage Spell.
Steal Spells do not trigger this cheat.
[Round] "Simon says you can attack now!" - On Rounds 4 through 6, using a Damage Spell will not trigger the "Simon didn't say!" cheat.
[Round] On Round 7, Simon will once again use the "Simon says no attacks!" cheat, and this will last for three more Rounds.
The cycle of being unable and then able to attack for three Rounds at a time will repeat indefinitely for the duration of the duel.
[Round] "I have my friends too! See?" - At the beginning of the second Round, Simon will interrupt-cast a Spell that will either summon a Heartburner Golem or Virulent Gas Golem Minion.
If any of Simon's Minions are defeated before him, then Simon will summon another Golem two Rounds after they are defeated.
If multiple Minions are defeated before Simon, then Simon will summon one new Golem every two Rounds.
[Blade] "Simon says... none of that!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, then Simon will interrupt-cast Disarm on the caster.
[Ward] "My Pet knows tricks too!" - If a Wizard casts a Prism or positive Ward, then Simon's Polterpooch will interrupt-cast Feint on the caster.
Stun Block will trigger this cheat.
Daniel
[Round] On Round 3, and randomly after that, Daniel will interrupt-cast Curse on Simon after any other cheats that Simon uses at the beginning of that Round.
[Round] After Round 3, Daniel will rarely interrupt-cast a Death version of Fairy that restores 300 Health on all Wizards and also applies Guidance.
This Spell can be affected by negative Healing Charms and Life Dispels that have been placed on Simon.
"I got this." or "Easy as pie." - Daniel will always use this line before casting Fairy, although he uses it for Curse as well.
strategy: Obey Simon Says. During 'Simon says no attacks!' rounds (1 to 3, then 7 to 9, and so on) any damage on Simon or his golems hits the caster with a 1,000 Moon Insane Bolt. Only attack in the 'you can attack now' windows (rounds 4 to 6, etc.).

Set up during the no-attack rounds, but not with blades or wards. A Blade triggers Disarm on you, and a Prism or positive Ward (Stun Block counts) triggers his pet's Feint. Stack traps and Feint on Simon instead; steal spells are safe anytime.
Let Daniel help: he throws +30% universal traps onto Simon and, after round 3, an AoE Fairy that heals the whole team, so lean on that trap stack and free healing.
Do not clear the golems early. The guide warns the Heartburner and Virulent Gas Golems get re-summoned one at a time every two rounds, so killing them just resets the timer.
Hit with Storm (+30% incoming) during the round 4 to 6 window, using an AoE like Storm Lord to catch Simon and both golems at once.
Funnel your traps and Feint into one big AoE to burst Simon's 12,960 health inside the attack window, before the next 'no attacks' phase locks you out again.
cheatNotes: Joining after round 2 brings a 400 Balance Mana Burn on the latecomer.
For the first three rounds ('Simon says no attacks!') any damage spell on him or his golems gets punished.
Attacking during a no-attacks phase makes him Insane Bolt the caster. Wait for the attack window.
Steal spells do not count as attacks and are safe during no-attacks.
On rounds 4 to 6 ('you can attack now') damage is safe. This is your burst window.
Round 7 re-enters no-attacks for three more rounds.
The attack and no-attack cycle repeats all fight, so plan every hit around it.
Round 2 he summons a Golem (Heartburner or Virulent Gas).
Kill a minion and he resummons another two rounds later.
Kill several and he replaces one every two rounds, so clearing minions is temporary.
Casting a Blade gets the caster Disarmed. Use traps, not blades.
Casting a Prism or positive Ward makes his pet Feint the caster.
Stun Block counts as a ward and triggers that pet Feint too.

After round 3 Daniel rarely casts a Death Fairy that heals all wizards 300 and gives Guidance, a free heal for your side.
That Fairy heal is weakened by negative healing charms or Life dispels placed on Simon.

name: Sir Blackwater
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Castle Darkmoor (Dungeon)
cheats: "I disarm you Wizard!" - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm or Global spell, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast Disarm on the Wizard who cast the Spell.
"Weapon Cast" and Pet "Maycast" that grant Blades will trigger this cheat.
Armor Piercing Charms will not trigger this cheat. (Example: Stormspear)
Aegis can be used to avoid this cheat.
"I reach inside myself to harm you!" - If a Wizard places any type of Weakness Spell on Sir Blackwater, he will interrupt-cast a Dark Pact (+30%) on himself.
AOE Weakness Spells, such as Virulent Plague, will not trigger this cheat.
Interrupt Cycles: All cycles are 5 Rounds each, and repeat until Sir Blackwater is defeated.
"I make this sacrifice!" - At the end of every Round in this cycle, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast Sacrifice (Death) on himself.

"I call upon the spirits of the deep!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast Age of Reckoning, Virulence, a Deathblade (+45%), and Call of Khrulhu. This Round will be the end of the Death cycle and the beginning of the Life cycle.
"Darkmoor armors us!" - At the end of every Round in this cycle, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast a Spirit Armor (500) on himself.

"I am steadfast in my resolve!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast Sanctuary, Cycle of Life, a Guiding Light, and Rebirth (730, 90%). This Round will be the end of the Life cycle and the beginning of the Storm cycle.
"I lay on hands." - At the end of every Round in this cycle, Sir Blackwater will interrupt-cast Healing Current on himself.

"The power of the Storm will destroy you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, he will interrupt-cast Astraphobia, Galvanic Field, a Stormblade, and Glowbug Squall. This Round will be the end of the Storm cycle and the beginning of the Death cycle.
strategy: Dungeon team fight (18,000 health). Hit with Myth (+30%) or Fire/Ice (+15%), avoid Storm (30% resist).
Any blade or global makes him Disarm the caster. Protect blades with Aegis, use armor-piercing charms (Stormspear) which do not trigger it, and funnel Aegis-blades to one hitter.
Use AoE weaknesses (Virulent Plague) rather than a single Weakness. A single Weakness on him triggers a +30% Dark Pact self-buff, while AoE weakness does not.
He self-heals every round through his cycles (Sacrifice, Spirit Armor, Healing Current), so bring Infection or Doom and Gloom to cut his healing or your damage stalls.
He runs 5-round Death, Life, and Storm cycles, each ending in a big cast on Round 5. Burst him inside a cycle before the Round-5 heals and spells while the healer covers the AoE.
cheatNotes: Cast a positive charm or global and he interrupt-Disarms the caster, protect blades with Aegis or use armor-piercing charms.
Weapon and pet maycast blades also trigger the Disarm.
Armor-piercing charms like Stormspear do NOT trigger the Disarm, safe to cast.
Aegis protects a charm from the Disarm.
Place a single Weakness on him and he Dark-Pacts himself for +30% damage, use AoE weakness instead.
AoE weaknesses like Virulent Plague do not trigger the Dark Pact.
During his Death cycle he Sacrifices to heal himself every round, bring Infection to cut it.
Death cycle round 5: Age of Reckoning, Virulence, a +45% Deathblade, and Call of Khrulhu, a big damage round, shield up.
During his Life cycle he adds a 500 Spirit Armor every round.
Life cycle round 5: Sanctuary, Cycle of Life, Guiding Light, and Rebirth, heavy self-healing, so burst before it.
During his Storm cycle he Healing-Currents himself every round.
Storm cycle round 5: Astraphobia, Galvanic Field, a Stormblade, and Glowbug Squall, shield for Storm.
