cheats: Sister Sanderson
[Steal] "Absumo Mortalis!" - At the end of the second Round, and every two Rounds thereafter, Sister Sanderson will cast Gravestorm (Tier 5a) on the marked Wizard.
If Sister Sanderson is at full Health, she will also cast a -100% incoming Damage Aura on herself.
Sister Morbidmary

[Random] "Sisters, this one is the weakest, take them!" - At the end of the first Round, and every two Rounds thereafter, Sister Morbidmary will randomly choose one of the following conditions, and place a Mark on that Wizard:
The Wizard with the lowest Pips - Sister Morbidmary will cast a version of Mana Burn that removes all of the Wizard's Pips
The Wizard with the lowest percentage of Health - Sister Morbidmary will cast a version of Leprechaun that deals 20% of the Wizard's max Health
The Wizard with the lowest value of Health - Sister Morbidmary will cast a version of Hungry Caterpillar that only deals damage
Occasionally this cheat will happen multiple times in a row.
Sister Scara
[Threat] "Dear child... over here" - At the end of the first Round, and every two Rounds thereafter, Sister Scara will cast Mega Taunt on a random sister.
strategy: Attack with Life (she takes +30% Life); never Death (79% resist) and expect 49% resist on the other schools.
Chip Sanderson off full Health before your real hit. While at full Health she keeps a -100% incoming damage aura up, so a small ding removes it and lets your bladed hit land.
Deal with Sister Scara early. She Mega Taunts a random sister each cycle, which can pull your hits onto the wrong target.
Keep pips and Health even across the team. Sister Morbidmary marks a wizard each cycle for Mana Burn (lowest pips), Leprechaun (lowest % Health), or Hungry Caterpillar (lowest Health).
Clear the minions or AoE all three, funnel blades to a hitter, then hit Sanderson once she is off full Health.
At 22,950 Health with two minions, this is a team fight with a hitter, supports, and a healer managing the marks.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 2 and every two Rounds after, Sanderson casts Gravestorm on the marked wizard.
While she is at full Health she also keeps a -100% incoming damage aura up, so she takes nothing; chip her below full before your real hit.
At the end of Round 1 and every two Rounds after, Morbidmary marks a wizard by one of three conditions (next lines).
If she picks lowest pips, she Mana Burns that wizard, stripping all their pips.
If she picks lowest Health percentage, she Leprechauns them for 20% of their max Health.
If she picks lowest Health value, she hits them with a damage-only Hungry Caterpillar.
This marking can occasionally fire several times in a row, so expect back-to-back hits.
At the end of Round 1 and every two Rounds after, Scara Mega Taunts a random sister, which can pull your hits onto the wrong target; kill Scara early.

name: Sisyphus Stone Thrower (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Garden of Hesperides - Temple of Sisyphus (Challenge)

stats.resist: 13% to all schools33% to all schools53% to all schools

cheats: [Global] "This isn't a sprint. It's a test of endurance!" - At the beginning of the first Round, and repeating each time he casts his Natural Attack, Sisyphus will interrupt-cast a version of Equalize that sets the Damage cap to one of the following values based on the number of times Sisyphus casts his Natural Attack:
Beginning of 1st Round: 500
One Natural Attack: 1,000
Two Natural Attacks: 3,000
Three Natural Attacks and onward: 9,999
The Equalize will be recast if replaced, and the Damage cap will not increase.

[Damage] "Boulder you than me!" - At the end of every third Round, occurring one Round earlier per Wizard present if there are 2 or more Wizards present, Sisyphus will cast his Natural Attack on a random Wizard, dealing 5% max Health Damage. If the Wizard has a helpful Charm, this Damage increases by 5%, and by 15% for each additional Charm after the first.
[Spell] "Who will cast the first stone?" - After casting his Natural Attack, Sisyphus will give a random Wizard one Rock Put:
[Rock Put]
[Curse][Jinx] "Hya! Pushed away!" - If a harmful Charm or Ward is placed on Sisyphus, he will push the effects to the caster.
Infections will not trigger this cheat.
Harmful Aura effects that decrease outgoing Damage, such as Starfall, will trigger this cheat.
Secondary effects from Spells will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Attack with Fire or Life; he resists Ice and Death 53% and takes those two least-resisted (13%). He is not stunable.

An Equalize caps your damage, and it only rises as HE casts his Natural Attack: 500 at round 1, 1,000 after his first, 3,000 after his second, 9,999 after his third. Do not waste a big nuke while the cap is low.
Play the long game. Build blades across the early rounds and let his Natural Attacks raise the cap to 9,999, then unload once the cap is high.

Keep your helpful charm count low until you hit. His Natural Attack deals 5% max health, +5% if you hold a charm and +15% per extra charm, so a fat blade stack sitting on you makes his hits hurt more.
Do not place harmful Charms or Wards on Sisyphus, including outgoing-damage auras like Starfall. He pushes them back onto the caster. Infections are the exception and are safe.
cheatNotes: An Equalize caps your damage. It starts low and rises only as he casts his Natural Attack, so do not waste a big nuke while the cap is small. The Equalize recasts if you replace it and the cap will not rise from replacing.
At the start of round 1 the damage cap is 500.
After his first Natural Attack the cap rises to 1,000.
After his second Natural Attack, 3,000.
After his third Natural Attack and onward, 9,999. This is your window to unload.
Replacing the Equalize just makes him recast it, and it will not raise the cap. Let his Natural Attacks raise it.
At the end of every third round (sooner with more wizards) he Natural-Attacks for 5% max health, +5% if you hold a helpful charm and +15% per extra charm. Keep your charm count low until you hit.
After each Natural Attack he hands a random wizard a Rock Put card.
Any harmful Charm or Ward placed on Sisyphus is pushed back onto the caster. Do not try to weaken or trap him.
Infections are the exception and do not get pushed back.
Outgoing-damage auras like Starfall count as harmful and do get pushed back.
Secondary debuff effects riding on a spell also trigger the push-back.

name: Sisyphus Stone Thrower (Standard)
locations: Wizard City: Garden of Hesperides - Temple of Sisyphus (Standard)
cheats: [Global] "This isn't a sprint. It's a test of endurance!" - At the beginning of the first Round, and repeating each time he casts his Natural Attack, Sisyphus will interrupt-cast a version of Equalize that sets the Damage cap to one of the following values based on the number of times Sisyphus casts his Natural Attack:
Beginning of 1st Round: 500
One Natural Attack: 1,000
Two Natural Attacks: 3,000
Three Natural Attacks and onward: 9,999
The Equalize will be recast if replaced, and the Damage cap will not increase.

[Damage] "Boulder you than me!" - At the end of every third Round, occurring one Round earlier per Wizard present if there are 2 or more Wizards present, Sisyphus will cast his Natural Attack on a random Wizard, dealing 5% max Health Damage. If the Wizard has a Charm, this Damage increases by 5%, and by 15% for each additional Charm after the first.
[Spell] "Who will cast the first stone?" - After casting his Natural Attack, Sisyphus will give the targeted Wizard one Rock Put:
[Rock Put]
[Curse][Jinx] "Hya! Pushed Away!" - If a harmful Charm or Ward is placed on Sisyphus, he will push the effects to the caster.
Infections will not trigger this cheat.
Dispels will not trigger this cheat.
Harmful Aura effects that decrease outgoing Damage, such as Starfall, will trigger this cheat.
Secondary effects from Spells will trigger this cheat, but the effect will be pushed to a random Wizard.
Indemnity and Unbalance can be used to prevent this cheat.
strategy: Solo-friendly (16,100 health, no minions). Know the damage cap: it is 500 until he throws his natural attack, then 1,000, 3,000, and finally 9,999 after his third, so early nukes are wasted and this is a setup-heavy grind.
Keep charms off yourself. His round-3 natural attack deals 5% max health plus 5-15% more per charm you carry.
Do not Feint or trap him raw: harmful charms and wards are pushed back to you. Protect a Feint with Indemnity or an Unbalance so it sticks instead.
Attack with Storm (+30%); he resists Myth 47% and everything else 17%. Stun him when available to skip a natural-attack turn.
Build blades on your hitter and unload once the cap has climbed to 9,999.
cheatNotes: He interrupt-casts Equalize to cap all damage, starting at 500 and rising each time he casts his natural attack.
The Equalize is recast if replaced, and the cap does not increase for it.
At end of every third round (sooner with more wizards) he hits a random wizard for 5% max health, plus more per charm you carry.
After that natural attack he gives the target a Rock Put card.
Any harmful charm or ward placed on him is pushed back to the caster.
Infections do not trigger this push-back.
Dispels do not trigger this push-back.
Harmful auras that cut outgoing damage, like Starfall, do trigger it.
Secondary effects from spells trigger it too, but get pushed to a random wizard.
Indemnity and Unbalance can be used to prevent this cheat, so a protected Feint sticks.

name: Skeletaur
cheats: [Late] "Latecomers shall be DESTROYED!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Skeletaur will cast his Natural Attack on the Wizard. This will also trigger the "Feel the might of Skeletaur" cheat, which casts Stun (Shadow) on the Wizard.
[Healing] "Skeletaur is the last to fall!" - If Skeletaur is defeated before or during the same Round as his Minions, he'll cast a Moon version of Blessing, restoring 100% Health.
[Feint] "Such an obvious feint!" - If a Wizard casts a Feint on Skeletaur, he will convert half the percentage value of the Trap. (Example: +70% becomes +35% Trap)
Indemnity can not be used to avoid this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint will not trigger this cheat.
[Manipulation] "Obey the will of Skeletaur!" - After casting his Natural Attack, he'll cast a 2 Round Confuse on the Wizard.
[Manipulation] "Feel the might of Skeletaur!" - After casting his Natural Attack, he'll cast Stun (Shadow) on the Wizard which Stuns for 2 Rounds.
[Manipulation] "Skeletaur is your king now!" - At the beginning of every 4th and 5th Round, Skeletaur will cheat-cast Beguile on a random Wizard.
[Minion] If Skeletaur has one Minion and it's defeated, Skeletaur will immediately cast a Shadow Spell to spawn in an Armor Totem, Hex Totem or Taunting Totem.
If Skeletaur has more than one Minion defeated having no Minions, he will cast the summoning Spell multiple times, but the Minion doesn't spawn from this.
strategy: Kill order is the fight. If Skeletaur dies before or in the same round as his minions, he revives to 100% via a Moon Blessing, so his Death Lemurs must die first, then Skeletaur on a later round.

Do not rely on Feint. He converts any Feint on him to half value (70% becomes 35%) and Indemnity does not stop it, so use the Balance version of Feint, which does not trigger the cheat, or lean on blades.

Bring stun blocks and expect confusion. His natural attack is followed by a 2-round Confuse and a 2-round Shadow Stun, and he Beguiles a random wizard at the start of Rounds 4 and 5, so protect your hitter.
Manage the totems. Killing his last minion makes him summon an Armor, Hex, or Taunting Totem, so plan to clear minions in a way that still leaves you a clean window on the boss afterward.
He is Shadow with 68% Balance resist and no boost school, so avoid Balance and stack blades on any other school; join on time since late wizards eat his attack plus a Stun.
Clear the Lemurs first, deal with the summoned totem, then stack blades (Balance Feint if used) and burst Skeletaur down a round after the minions are gone so the revive cannot trigger.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard is hit by Skeletaur's natural attack, which also triggers his Shadow Stun on that wizard; be present at the start.
If Skeletaur dies before or in the same round as his minions, he casts a Moon Blessing and revives to 100%. Kill the minions first, then him a round later.
Feint on him is halved (70% becomes a 35% Trap), so its value is cut.
Indemnity does not protect your Feint from the halving cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger the halving, so use it if you want full-value trap pressure.
After his natural attack he casts a 2-round Confuse on the target wizard.
After his natural attack he also casts a 2-round Shadow Stun, so carry stun blocks for your hitter.
At the start of Rounds 4 and 5 he Beguiles a random wizard, so watch for forced attacks on your own team.
Killing his last remaining minion makes him summon an Armor, Hex, or Taunting Totem in its place.
If he has multiple minions defeated and ends with none, he casts the summon spell repeatedly but no extra minion actually spawns.

name: Skurkis Screaming Moon (Xibalba)
cheats: "A Necromancer is never alone!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Skurkis will summon a Shambling Zombie Minion that enters the duel at the beginning of the second Round and becomes active in the third Round.
"Feed me my minion!" - The Shambling Zombie Minion will cast a 0-pip Sacrifice on Skurkis for 3,000 healing every 2-3 Rounds.
"Time to make some new friends!" - If the Shambling Zombie Minion dies, Skurkis will summon another one.
strategy: Hit with Ice or Life (+35% each) and avoid Death (80% resist).
He is stunable and beguilable; use stuns to interrupt his setup.
He summons a Shambling Zombie on Round 1 that becomes active Round 3; that zombie Sacrifices itself on him for 3,000 healing every 2-3 Rounds, and he resummons it when it dies.
Kill the zombie to stop the heal, but expect a replacement; time your burst so the zombie cannot Sacrifice-heal him back up.
Stack Ice or Life blades and Feint, then land one big hit through his healing window.
cheatNotes: He summons a Shambling Zombie on Round 1 that wakes on Round 3; it is his healing engine, so plan to remove it.
The zombie Sacrifices itself on Skurkis for 3,000 healing every 2-3 Rounds; kill it or burst him before it heals him.
When the zombie dies he summons another, so time your kill on Skurkis around the heal rather than grinding zombies.

name: Snow Queen
locations: Lemuria: Night Forest - First Story Tomb
cheats: "Do You Want to Battle a Snowman?" - If a Wizard is late to combat, another Awful Snowman will be summoned.
"You are my Chosen One!" - The Snow Queen will cast a +15% Damage, +15% Armor Piercing Icewind Aura lasting 1 Round on a random Wizard. The Wizard will be considered "chosen" for one round.
"No Feinting Spells Here! - If a Wizard casts a Feint, a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2) will be cast on her twice, making Indemnity ineffective in protecting the removal.
"I am Insulting You!" - The Awful Snowman Minions will cast a version of Mega Taunt that lasts 5 Rounds.

"I Didn't Choose You!" - If a Wizard without a mark attacks her, she will cast a version of Celestial Intervention that heals for 500, removes 6 Shields and heal for 180 per Shield, and some unknown effect after.
"You Hit Me - I'll Let it Slide!" - If the marked Wizard attacks the Snow Queen, she will provide the Wizard with a +100% Aegis-Protected Balanceblade.
"Leave my Subjects Alone!" - If an attacking Wizard uses an All Enemies Spell, the Snow Queen will cast Tower Shield (Moon) on herself.
"Focus on Me and Me Alone!" - If a Wizard uses a single target attack on an Awful Snowman, the Snow Queen will cast Tower Shield (Moon) on herself.
Dark Pact will also trigger this cheat
"Let it Snow!" - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect on her, she will use Cooldown.
"Bad Choice, Chosen One!" - If the chosen Wizard does anything other than hit, or a non-Chosen Wizard does anything, the Snow Queen will cast Tower Shield (Moon) on herself.
Death school drain Spells will not count as a hit.
There's no penalty for passing.
Pet and Weapon May Cast Spells can also trigger the cheat.

"Let the Storm Rage On" - The Snow Queen will cast a version of Blizzard that targets all allies, (the Boss's Team) removing all positive Wards, or targets all enemies (the Wizard's Team) removing all positive Charms.
Aegis will prevent the removal from this Spell.
strategy: Moon boss (15,350). She resists almost everything - Storm/Myth/Balance 60%, Ice/Death 73% - and boosts only to Fire and Life (+5%). The guide's recommended plan for most teams is hit-spamming, not a big alpha strike.
Pack ONLY single-hit spells and target the boss. AoE hits, Feints (double Cleanse Ward), DoTs (Cooled down), and any non-hit action all make her Tower Shield herself.
Rounds 1-5 the Awful Snowman Taunts, forcing your hits onto it. There is no penalty for passing, so simply pass and wait the Taunt out rather than triggering shields.

Each round she marks one random wizard as the Chosen One with a 1-round Icewind aura (+15% universal damage and pierce). Only that wizard should act, and they must ATTACK - a non-Chosen wizard hitting her triggers Celestial Intervention (she heals and strips shields).

From round 6 on, single-target the Snow Queen. When the Chosen wizard hits her she hands back a +100% Aegis-protected Balanceblade; bank those free blades. The guide killed her in about 5 hits letting the blade cheat stack the damage.

Keep a key charm under Aegis so her Blizzard cannot strip it. After she dies, AoE the remaining Snowmen. Alternative for a loaded team: an alpha strike with pierce blades, auras, bubbles, Shrike and ~150% pierce - but it is riskier since most non-single-hit setup draws the triple -100% Tower Shields.
cheatNotes: A late joiner makes her summon another Awful Snowman. Be in on round 1.
Each round she marks a random wizard as Chosen with a 1-round Icewind aura. That wizard is the only one who should act this round.
Feint her and she Cleanse-Wards twice, beating Indemnity. Do not Feint; use blades instead.
Her Snowmen can Mega Taunt for 5 rounds, forcing your hits onto them. Clear taunts or wait them out.
If a wizard who is not Chosen attacks her, she Celestial Interventions, healing and stripping shields. Only the Chosen wizard should hit.
When the Chosen wizard attacks her, she gives that wizard a +100% Aegis-protected Balanceblade. Bank these free blades toward your kill.
An all-enemies attack makes her Tower Shield herself. Use single-target hits.
Single-target on a Snowman also gives her a Tower Shield. Focus her, not the snowmen, unless taunted.
Dark Pact also triggers the Tower Shield. Avoid it here.
DoTs on her get Cooled down. Skip damage-over-time.
If the Chosen wizard does anything but hit, or any non-Chosen wizard casts, she Tower Shields. Off-turn wizards should pass.
Death drain spells do not count as a hit for the Chosen rule, so they still trigger a Tower Shield. Use direct attacks.
There is no penalty for passing, so non-Chosen wizards should simply pass rather than risk a shield.
Pet and wand May Cast procs also count as a non-Chosen action and shield her. Be mindful of proc pets.
Her Blizzard strips all positive charms from your team (or wards from hers). Aegis protects your key charms from it.
Aegis stops the Blizzard from taking a protected charm. Shield your main blade before she casts it.

name: Snowbeard
cheats: [Blade] Every fifth Round, he will use the Lifeblade Spell, then the Round after use the Lifeblade Treasure Card.
strategy: Every fifth Round he casts Lifeblade, then follows with a Lifeblade treasure card the next Round, so he only meaningfully buffs on a slow cycle.
This gives you long, safe setup windows; stack your blades and Feints between his blade Rounds.
He resists Life 80% and takes 35% more from Fire and Death, so hit with Fire or Death, not Life.
Build up and land one big hit; he has no reactive cheats to punish your setup.
cheatNotes: Every fifth Round he casts Lifeblade, then follows with a Lifeblade treasure card the next Round.

name: Sofia DarkSide
cheats: [Minion] "Join me, valiant servant!" - At the end of the first Round, Sofia will summon Arkyn MoonBlade. He becomes active on the third Round.
[Minion][Afterlife] "Rise, Arkyn Moonblade. Be my sword!" - If Arkyn MoonBlade is defeated, Sofia will resummon him after two Rounds.

[Minion] "Bring your sword to my cause!" - At the end of each Round, Arkyn MoonBlade will summon a Dread Paladin until all duel slots are filled up. If Sofia Darkside is defeated before the second Dread Paladin joins combat, a third Dread Paladin will be spawned in.
strategy: Hit Fire or Death, each +35%, and never Life, which she resists 80%; prism if you are Life.
Race Sofia down before her adds pile up. Arkyn, from Round 1 and active on Round 3, spawns a Dread Paladin every Round until slots fill.
Do not bother permanently killing Arkyn. She resummons him in two Rounds, so blade past him onto the boss.
Stack setup fast and land one big Fire or Death hit on Sofia. Kill her cleanly rather than slow-playing, since an early kill mid-summon just spawns another Paladin.
cheatNotes: She summons Arkyn MoonBlade at the end of Round 1, and he starts acting on Round 3.
Kill Arkyn and she resummons him two Rounds later, so he is a recurring problem, not a one-time kill.
Arkyn spawns Dread Paladins each Round until the board is full. Killing Sofia before the second Dread Paladin joins spawns a third instead, so focus her down decisively.

name: Sothmekhet
cheats: "A Charm! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Blade will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat, but Aura Spells will not.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A ward! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Trap (including Feint) or a Shield will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
The text for this cheat only appears after Sothmekhet casts the Hex.
strategy: Attack with Fire (+35%); he resists Ice 35%.
Every blade, trap, or shield you cast makes him Hex you (+35% trap boosting his next hit), and Globals trigger it too. Buff through Auras and pet spells instead, which do not trigger the cheat.
Because setup gets punished, keep it lean: a couple of blades (accept the Hexes) and shield his boosted hits, or Cleanse the Hex before he attacks.
He is only 4,540 health, so a fast Fire burst ends it before the Hexes stack.
Stack minimal setup, shield, and finish.
cheatNotes: Casting a blade makes him Hex you (+35% trap boosting his next hit). Shield or cleanse it, or use Auras which do not trigger it.
Globals trigger the Hex too, but Auras do not, so buff with Auras when you can.
Pet spells do not trigger it either.
Casting a trap or shield also triggers the +35% Hex on the caster, so every ward-type setup gets punished.

name: Spellhammer Sorcerer
cheats: When Sylster Glowstorm has been defeated, the Spellhammer Sorcerer will take over casting the Doom and Gloom cycle, starting with the 3 Round white Doom and Gloom.

If a Wizard casts a single-target attack without activating a Trap [Damage Spell] [Ward Spell] (white Doom and Gloom) orBlade [Damage Spell] [Charm Spell] (black Doom and Gloom), then the enemy that was attacked will cheat-cast the following spell:
All Enemy [All Enemies Spell] attack spells will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that deal damage to one target initially, then damage all enemies will trigger this cheat (e.g.Sun Serpent).
strategy: This is a Waterworks fight, so coordinate as a team and expect the Doom & Gloom cycle (healing cut); once Sylster Glowstorm falls, this Sorcerer takes over casting it.
Use all-enemies AoE attacks — a single-target hit that doesn't activate a Trap (white Doom & Gloom) or Blade (black) makes the target counter-cast.
Attack with Ice or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Death resist without a prism.
Funnel blades to one hitter, run an AoE to clear the room, and pack Infections or pet heals to work around Doom & Gloom.
Note that spells hitting one target then all (Sun Serpent) count as single-target and DO trigger the counter.
cheatNotes: Once Sylster Glowstorm is defeated, this Sorcerer takes over the Doom & Gloom cycle (starting with the 3-round white Doom & Gloom) — plan healing around the cut.
A single-target attack that doesn't activate a Trap (white phase) or Blade (black phase) makes the target counter-cast — use AoE, or make sure a trap/blade pops with the hit.
All-enemies attack spells don't trigger the counter — the safe way to deal damage here.
Spells that hit one target then all (Sun Serpent) DO trigger it — treat them as single-target for this cheat.

name: Sphinx
locations: Empyrea: Northeast Aero Plains - Sphinx Cavern
cheats: "For those of you arriving late, TAKE THIS!" - If a Wizard is late, he will interrupt-cast Desert Hound (Sun).

Every second Round, the Sphinx will ask a riddle. The answer to the riddle will be a type of Spell. Casting a Spell of the correct type to answer the riddle will not cause any response from the Sphinx. However, giving the incorrect answer will cause the Sphinx to cheat cast a Spell in response. Once all riddles have been asked, they will then be asked in random order. Players must wait until the next riddle before casting a different type of Spell.
Pet "May Cast" talents can activate this cheat.
Passing will not activate this cheat.
The correct answer is Blade.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast Orthrus (Sun).
The correct answer is "hurt me", given by casting a Damage Spell.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast Power Nova (Shadow).
The correct answer is Trap.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast a +200% Shadow version of Feint with no self Trap.
The correct answer is Weakness.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast an Indemnity-protected Weakness (Shadow).
The correct answer is Heal.
"*Buzzer* Awwwwwww!" - If answered incorrectly, Boss will cast a version of Blessing that heals 10% of his total Health.
strategy: Every second round the Sphinx asks a riddle whose answer is a spell TYPE — cast that type to pass safely. Pack a Blade, a damage spell, a Trap, a Weakness, and a Heal so you always have the right answer.
Correct answers draw no response; wrong ones punish you (Orthrus, Power Nova, a +200% Feint on you, a protected Weakness, or he heals 10% of his health).
Wait for each new riddle before casting a different spell type — casting off-cue can answer wrong.
Passing is always safe and doesn't trigger any cheat, so pass if you lack the right card.
Use the Blade/Trap/Weakness riddles to build your setup, then land a big hit when a Damage riddle comes due (no resist, so any school works).
cheatNotes: Late wizards eat a Desert Hound — join on time.
Every 2nd round he asks a riddle answered by a spell type; answer right and nothing happens, answer wrong and he punishes you. Wait for each riddle before casting.
Pet may-cast talents can accidentally answer a riddle — watch your pet.
Passing never triggers the cheat, so pass if you lack the right card.
This riddle's answer is a Blade.
Answer it wrong and he casts Orthrus.
This riddle's answer is a Damage spell ('hurt me').
Wrong answer here brings a Shadow Power Nova.
This riddle's answer is a Trap.
Wrong answer gives you a +200% Shadow Feint (no self-trap for him).
This riddle's answer is a Weakness.
Wrong answer puts an Indemnity-protected Weakness on you.
This riddle's answer is a Heal.
Wrong answer heals him 10% of his total health.

name: Spirit of Darkmoor
locations: Wizard City: Castle Darkmoor - Upper Halls
cheats: "Burn, Wizard!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global Spell, she will cheat-cast an enhanced version of Scald, which deals 999 Fire Damage over 3 rounds to all Wizards.
Pet spells will trigger this cheat.
"I shatter your chains!" - If a Wizard places a Trap on the Spirit of Darkmoor, she will cheat-cast a version of Assail, which deals 850 Myth Damage and places two +35% Myth Traps on the Wizard.
Multi-target Spells, such as Windstorm, will not trigger this cheat.

The depths of Darkmoor strikes at you! - If a Wizard defeats a Minion, she will cheat-cast a Sun version of Eternal Leviathan (777), which deals 777 Life Damage to the Wizard and restores 777 Health to all allies (reviving the defeated Minions).
Interrupt Cycles All interrupt cycles are five Rounds each, and repeat until the duel is finished.
"This place defeats us all!" - At the end of every Round in this cycle, the Spirit of Darkmoor will cast Pierce on a random Wizard.

"The stories of Lore will defeat you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, she will cast a Mythspear (+12%), a Mythblade (+40%), Mystic Colossus, and Saga of Heroes. This Round will be the end of the Myth cycle and the beginning of the Life cycle.
"Darkmoor holds my spirit." - At the end of every Round in this cycle, the Spirit of Darkmoor will cast version of Minor Blessing on herself for 750 healing.

"The world hunger consumes you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, she will cast a +12% Lifespear, a Lifeblade, Namaste, and Hungry Caterpillar on the highest threat Wizard. This Round will be the end of the Life cycle and the beginning of the Fire cycle.
"We are connected." - At the end of every Round in this cycle, the Spirit of Darkmoor will cast Backdraft worth the amount of Pips she has on a random Wizard.

"Your passion will consume you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this cycle, she will cast a +12% Firespear, a Fireblade (+40%), Combustion, and Fire from Above to the highest-threat Wizard. This Round will be the end of the Fire cycle and the beginning of the Myth cycle.
spellNotes: Orthrus (Sun) deals 700 Sun Damage to all enemies.
strategy: Classic Darkmoor team fight (14,700, Sun). She takes +30% from Ice and Death. Hitters use Ice or Death.

Do NOT blade or cast globals: either makes her Scald the whole team (999 Fire over 3 rounds), and pet spells trigger it too. Feed the hitter with enchants and auras, or blade only in the same round as the hit.
Do NOT single-trap her; she counters with Assail (850 Myth plus two +35% Myth traps on you). Multi-target traps like Windstorm do NOT trigger it.
Do NOT kill her Darkmoor Gargoyle early: defeating a minion triggers Eternal Leviathan (777) and revives the minion. Save the minion for your final AoE.

She heals 750 each round (Minor Blessing) and runs 5-round school cycles that dump a full spear, blade, and hit package on the highest-threat wizard every fifth round. Use Doom and Gloom or Infection to cut her healing and manage threat.
Supports blade the hitter same-round or via enchants, the healer covers the Scald and cycle hits, and the hitter lands one big Ice or Death AoE to take her and the revived Gargoyle together.
cheatNotes: Casting a blade or a global makes her Scald the whole team for 999 Fire over 3 rounds. Blade only in the same round you hit, or use enchants instead of blades.
Pet-cast spells also trigger the Scald. Consider a pet that will not cast during setup.
A single-target trap on her triggers Assail: 850 Myth and two +35% Myth traps back on you. Trap with multi-target spells instead.
Multi-target traps like Windstorm do NOT trigger the Assail. Use those if you must trap her.
Killing a minion makes her cast Eternal Leviathan (777) and revive the minion. Do not pick off the Gargoyle early; take it with your final AoE.
Each cycle round she Pierces a random wizard. Expect steady pierce damage.
On the fifth round of the Myth cycle she loads Mythspear, Mythblade, Mystic Colossus, and Saga of Heroes. Heal the highest-threat wizard through it.
She heals 750 every round with Minor Blessing. Use Doom and Gloom or Infection to cut it.
On the fifth round of the Life cycle she dumps Lifespear, Lifeblade, Namaste, and Hungry Caterpillar on the highest-threat wizard. Manage threat and pre-shield.
Each round of the Fire cycle she Backdrafts a random wizard for her pip count. Expect the trap and heal through the Fire follow-up.
On the fifth round of the Fire cycle she loads Firespear, Fireblade, Combustion, and Fire from Above on the highest-threat wizard. Shield and heal that wizard.

name: Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 1)
locations: Wizard City: Crab Alley - Crab Alley Gold Key Room
cheats: Steal Pips! - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Feckless Soul will cheat-cast a Spell that steals one Pip.
strategy: Very low health (1,200) fight with three weak minions. A quick AoE clears the room.
Join on time; the only cheat triggers on latecomers.
Blade once and AoE everything down.
He is stunable if you ever need to skip a turn.
cheatNotes: Only matters if you join late: a Feckless Soul steals one pip from the latecomer. Arrive on time and it never fires.

name: Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 2)
locations: Wizard City: Crab Alley - Crab Alley Gold Key Room
cheats: "Get ready for the powerfulest spell EVER!" -At the start of the duel, Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 2) will gain a -99% Tower Shield.
If a single target Damage Spell breaks the Shield, another Shield will be placed on him.
All Enemies Damage Spells will not cause this cheat to trigger.
He will not react if the -99% Shield is removed, if he has another Shield on.
Melt will dispel this cheat.
"What the what? Like, where'd it go?" - If a Wizard uses Pierce or Steal Ward, he will say this line:
"I cannot be defeated! I know your schools!" - The Round after the cheat above triggers, he will place an Aegis protected -100% Shield of the Wizard's School, not including Balance.
"Not this time!" - If a Wizard tries to remove the matching School Shield, he will recast another one.
"You can't charm your way out of this!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, he will use Disarm (Sun) on the receiver.
"I won't fall into your traps!" - If a Wizard casts a trap on him, he will use Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) and remove one Trap from himself.
"What? Why didn't that work?!" - He says this if a Wizard casts a multi-trap spell on him, such as Elemental Trap, or an Indemnity protected Trap.
"Muahahaha! Two rounds 'till you go DOWN!" - Every 5 Rounds, he will place a Shadow Deferred Overtime dealing 100,000 Shadow Damage after two Rounds on the Wizard.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+25%), avoid Myth (55% resist). He is low health (4,500) but layered in shields, so race him.
He opens with a -99% Tower Shield and re-shields when single-target hits break it. Attack with all-enemy AoE spells, which do not trigger the re-shield, or Melt to dispel.
If you Pierce or Steal his shield, next round he puts up an Aegis-protected -100% shield of your school, so use off-school AoE or Melt rather than piercing.
Do not blade (he Disarms the receiver) and do not single-trap him (he Cleanse-Wards one trap off), use multi-trap spells or Indemnity traps.
Every 5 rounds he plants a 100,000 Shadow bomb that detonates two rounds later, which is lethal, so burst him dead before Round 7 with AoE Storm damage.
cheatNotes: He starts with a -99% Tower Shield, a single-target hit that breaks it just makes him re-shield.
Single-target damage that breaks the shield triggers a fresh shield, do not chip him single-target.
All-enemy AoE damage does NOT trigger the re-shield, this is how you hit him.
He will not re-shield if you remove the -99% shield while he already has another shield up.
Melt dispels the shield cheat outright.
Pierce or Steal Ward on him sets up the next cheat.
The round after you Pierce or Steal, he puts up an Aegis-protected -100% shield of your school, so avoid piercing and use off-school AoE.
Try to remove that school shield and he recasts it, leave it and hit off-school.
Cast a blade and he Disarms it off the receiver, skip blades.
Single-trap him and he Cleanse-Wards one trap off, use multi-trap spells or Indemnity.
Multi-trap spells (Elemental Trap) and Indemnity traps beat his single-trap removal.
Every 5 rounds he plants a 100,000 Shadow bomb that detonates two rounds later, kill him before it lands (before Round 7).

name: Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 3)
locations: Wizard City: Crab Alley - Crab Alley Gold Key Room
cheats: "I... am... INVINCIBLE!!!" - At the start of the Round, a -300% version of Brace, lasting 99 Rounds, will be placed on Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 3).
"YIKES! What was that?!" - Boss will react if Supernova is cast on his Aura but will not recast it.
"You can't charm your way out of this!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, he will use Disarm (Sun) on the receiver.
"I won't fall into your traps!" - If a Wizard casts a Trap on Spirit of Ignorance, he will use Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -1) and remove one Trap from himself.

"What? Why didn't that work?!" - He says this if a Wizard casts a multi-Blade or multi-Trap Spell on him, such as Elemental Blade or Elemental Trap, or an Aegis protected Blade or Indemnity protected Trap.
"I cannot be defeated! I know your schools!" - The Round after his Aura is removed, Boss will use a -100% protected Shield of the Wizard's School, not including Balance.
Each time a Damage Spell is used on the Boss, he'll recast all Shields based on the Wizard's School.
"No you don't!" - If a Wizard uses Steal Ward on his Shield, he'll recast all Shields based on the Wizard's School.
"Muahahaha! Two rounds 'till you go DOWN!" - Every 5 Rounds, he will place a Shadow Deferred Overtime dealing 100,000 Shadow Damage after two Rounds on the Wizard.
strategy: He starts invincible under a -300% Brace aura, so cast Supernova to strip it; he will not recast it.
Set up with Aegis-protected blades and Indemnity-protected traps (or multi-blade and multi-trap spells); plain blades get Disarmed and plain traps get Cleanse Warded.

After the aura is gone he shields against your exact school every time you damage him (and recasts on Steal Ward), but he cannot shield Balance. Hit with Balance damage, or prism your hits, to sidestep the shields.
Every 5 rounds he plants a Shadow deferred hit for 100,000 damage that lands two rounds later, so keep the fight short and kill before it resolves.
Blade up, Supernova the aura, and burst with Balance or prismed damage.
cheatNotes: He is invincible under this -300% aura until you Supernova it off.
Supernova removes the aura and he will not recast it, so that is your opening.
Plain blades get Disarmed off the recipient, so use Aegis-protected or multi-blade spells.
Plain traps get Cleanse Warded, so use Indemnity-protected or multi-trap spells.
Aegis blades, Indemnity traps, and multi-blade or trap spells slip past his Disarm and Cleanse Ward.
After the aura drops he shields against your exact school, so hit with Balance (which he cannot shield) or prism your damage.
Every damaging hit makes him recast the school shields, so Balance damage avoids the loop.
Stealing his shield just makes him recast it in your school, so use Balance instead.
Every 5 rounds he sets a 100,000 Shadow deferred hit that lands two rounds later, so kill him before it resolves.

name: Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 4)
locations: Wizard City: Crab Alley - Crab Alley Gold Key Room
cheats: "I... am... INVINCIBLE!!!" - At the start of the Round, a -300% version of Brace, lasting 99 Rounds, will be placed on Spirit of Ignorance (Tier 4).
The Aura will be recast if it runs out.
"YIKES! What was that?!" - Spirit of Ignorance will say this if his Aura is removed, but will not recast it.
Negative auras will trigger this cheat however the boss will retain his invulnerability.
"You can't charm your way out of this!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade, he'll use Enfeeble (Shadow) on the target of the Blade.
Multi-target Blade Spells, like Bladestorm, will trigger this cheat for the caster only, and not the other Wizards.
Global spells like Darkwind will target the user with this cheat.
Blades that are created as a secondary effect of a spell (such as Catch of the Day (Tier 2b)) will not trigger this cheat.
"Feint? There's no time to pass out. I'm calling backup!" - If a Wizard casts a Feint on Spirit of Ignorance, he'll use a version of Sacrifice with +500% Armor Piercing on himself.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
"I won't fall into your traps!" - If a Wizard casts a single target Trap on Spirit of Ignorance, he'll use Cleanse Ward (Star, -All) on himself.
"What? Why didn't that work?!" - Boss will react if a Wizard has a protected Charm that didn't get removed by Enfeeble, or if a protected Trap didn't get removed by Cleanse Ward.
Using AoE Trap Spells will cause the Spirit of Ignorance to remove all Traps from the caster, instead of himself.
"I cannot be defeated! I know your schools!" - The Round after his Aura is removed, Boss will use a -100% protected Shield of the Wizard's School, not including Balance.
Each time after a Damage spell is used on the Boss, he'll recast all Shields based on the Wizard's School.
"No you don't!" - If a Wizard uses Steal Ward on his Shield, he'll recast all Shields based on the Wizard's School.
"Muahahaha! Two rounds 'till you go DOWN!" - Every 5 Rounds, he will place a Shadow Deferred Overtime dealing 100,000 Shadow Damage after two Rounds on the Wizard.
strategy: The guide calls for a 4-wizard team and says a Balance wizard is required. Wizard 1 (Balance) is the dispeller/debuffer; wizards 2-4 are damage dealers.

First priority: strip his invulnerability aura. At the start he plants a -300% incoming damage aura for 99 rounds, and Supernova is the essential counter — nothing else removes it. Round 1 the Balance wizard casts Supernova while the others open with AoE attacks.
Round 2: the Balance wizard casts Judgement while the others cast Frenzy to stack damage. Round 3: everyone casts their available hitting spells to burst him down.

Do not rely on prisms. The guide notes prisms do nothing against his shields, and after the aura breaks he re-casts a school-specific Aegis shield every time he is hit. Pierce or off-school pressure through it instead.
Avoid Feint: he has a Sacrifice counter-cheat that bypasses protective braces, so leaning on Feint backfires.
Race the clock. Every 5 rounds he plants a Shadow deferred effect dealing 100,000 damage two rounds later, which you cannot tank, so kill him or remove it before it detonates.
cheatNotes: This aura makes him immune to all damage. You must remove it before anything you cast matters.
If you let the aura expire on its own it comes right back, so remove it manually instead of waiting it out.
Once you strip the aura yourself he cannot put it back. A proper aura-removal ends his invulnerability for the rest of the fight.
Simply applying your own negative aura triggers his reaction line but leaves his invulnerability intact. You need a spell that actually strips HIS aura.
Every Blade you cast puts an Enfeeble on whoever you bladed. Blade under Aegis or you just lose the charm.
Bladestorm and other mass blades only get the caster Enfeebled, not the whole team.
A global blade like Darkwind draws the Enfeeble onto you specifically.
Blades that come as a side effect of another spell (like Catch of the Day) slip past this cheat.
Feinting him makes him Sacrifice with +500% pierce, wiping your trap plan. Protect the Feint with Indemnity or skip it.
Mass Feint does not set off the Sacrifice, so it is a safer way to land a trap on him.
A single-target Trap makes him Cleanse it off. Protect it with Indemnity.
This is the line you want to hear. It means your Aegis- or Indemnity-protected effect survived his removal.
AoE Traps backfire: he strips every trap off you instead of himself.
The round after you drop his aura he starts shielding your exact school at -100%. Bring a prism or off-school hit.
He re-shields your school every time you damage him, so plan one big hit through the shield rather than many small ones.
Stealing his shield just makes him recast it; pierce or prism around it instead.
Every 5 rounds he plants a 100,000-damage Shadow bomb that goes off two rounds later. It is unsurvivable, so win or remove it before it lands.

name: Spriggan Gisela
locations: Khrysalis: Radiance Reborn - First Chamber of the Mind
cheats: [Damage] "I sense in you... a weakness" - At the start of every fifth Round she casts a 0-Pip Meteor Strike that deals 215-245 Fire Damage.
strategy: Hit Death. She takes +40% Death and resists 70% Life, so Death is your clean damage.
She only has 1,920 health, so this is a race - kill her before her round-5 Meteor Strike.
Her sole cheat fires every fifth round, so a fast blade and one Death hit ends it inside the first cycle.
No traps, charms, or heals are punished here, so set up normally with a blade or two and swing.
If the fight drags past round 5, expect a free 215-245 Fire Meteor on the team each fifth round - just heal through it and finish.
cheatNotes: At the start of every fifth round she casts a 0-pip Meteor Strike for 215-245 Fire damage to the team. With only 1,920 health, kill her well before round 5.

name: Stallion Quartermane (Challenge)
cheats: [Late] Late Cheats - When a Wizard joins after the 2nd Round, the following cheats will occur:
"I find your etiquette lacking" - Solomon Crane casts Bad Juju on the Wizard.
"More of you? No problem!" - Buck Gordon casts Smoke Mantle on the Wizard.
"You're never too late" - The Shadoe casts Shadow Spell to give the late Wizard the following Spells:
[Card Limit (0 Pip)][Card Limit (7 Pip)][Card Limit (2 Shadow Pip)]
"Late to the party, ain't ya?" - Dog Tracy casts a Moon version of Insane Bolt, dealing 1,500 Moon Damage.
"Ha-ha! The more the merrier!" - Stallion Quartermane casts Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"More foes? HA! I am ready for you!" - Mandar casts a Myth Spell to Confuse the Wizard for 2 Rounds.
"Ha! I saw your ambush coming!" - Duck Savage casts Stun on the Wizard.
"Tsk, tsk. You're late!" - The Bantam casts Infection and Angel casts Entangle on the Wizard.
[Balance] Stallion Quartermane
[Spell] "Good show!" - Stallion will say this when casting his Natural Attack.
[Health] "Ah-ah-ah, Never say die!" - If any Boss is low on health, there's a chance he'll cast a Life version of Blessing that restores 10% of the target's max Health.
[Pip] "I claim these pips for the queen!" - Stallion Quartermane will use Steal Pip whenever a Wizard has a bunch of Pips.
Stallion can use a version of Steal Pip that steals 1 or 2 Power Pips.
[Myth] Mandar the Barbarian
[Aura] "Flee! Flee from the savage sword of Mandar!" - At the end of Round 2, Mandar will cast Frenzy (+40%).

[Aura] "By Grom, you'll pay for that!" - If Mandar's Aura is replaced by Stallion Quartermane's Quicken, Mandar will replace it with Frenzy (5 Pip) that applies a +40% outgoing Damage and Quicken Aura for 4 Rounds.
[Damage] "Is that the best you can do, Dog?" - At the beginning of every 7th Round, Mandar will cast a version of Earthquake that deals 145 Damage and removes 1 positive Charm and Ward.
[Heal] "Grom, give me strength!" - Mandar will cast a version of Blessing that restores 5% of his max HP, (652 Health).
[Ice] Duck Savage
[Damage] "Feel my Fists of Bronze!" - Duck Savage will say this when using the 5-Pip version of her Natural Attack and will apply a +75% protected Damage Trap on the target.
[Protected Absorb] "I am the duck of Bronze!" - At the beginning of the first Round, Duck Savage will interrupt-cast a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb version of Frozen Armor on herself.
[Manipulation] No, I don't think so" - If a Wizard casts Steal Ward on her 5,000 Damage Absorb, she'll cast Steal Ward (Star, -All) to steal all positive Wards from the caster.

[Heal over Time] "Don't worry. I'm a doctor" - If a Heal over Time effect is cast by the opponents, such as Buck Gordon casting Power Link, Duck Savage will extend the Heal over Time effect by 1 Round.
[Damage over Time] "I can formulate an antidote!" - If a Damage over Time effect is cast she will interrupt-cast either Cooldown, or a single-target version of Cooldown, on the receivers.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
[Aura] "My stamina has no limits!" - When Duck Savage's 5,000 Damage Absorb is gone, she'll interrupt cast a 99 Round -50% version of Fortify on herself.
This Aura will be recast if it is removed.
[Life] The Bantam
[Pacify] "I need a minute. Talk amongst yourselves." - At the beginning of the first Round, The Bantam will cast a version of Pacify that lasts 3 Rounds.

[Blade] "I'll be taking that, thanks!" - Whenever a Blade is cast on another Wizard or summoned Minion, The Bantam will interrupt-cast a Life Spell on the receiver, replacing it with a Balance Damage Blade equal to half the percent of the original Blade.
For multi-Blade Spells, such as Elemental and Spirit Blade, only the first Blade is removed.
For Blade Spells that target all allies, such as Bladestorm, only the caster's Blade is removed.
[Round] "Get ready, Angel" - The Bantam will shout this line at the end of the Round in which he'll cast Mass Sacred Charge next Round.
[All Friends][Damage] "Angel to the rescue!" - The Bantam will cast Mass Sacred Charge (840).
[Death] Solomon Crane

[Harmful Aura] "Curses be upon thee!" - At the beginning of Round 1 and every 11th Round following, Solomon Crane casts a Damage Aura on all Wizards, starting at -10% Damage and decreasing by 10% each time it is cast, up to a maximum of -50%.

[Aura] "Steel your resolve" - At the end of Round 6 and every 12th Round following, Solomon Crane casts a Resist Aura on his Team, starting at -10% Damage and decreasing by 10% each time it is cast, up to a maximum of -50%.
[Shield] "May your wards crumble upon you" - If a Shield is cast, Solomon Crane will cast a Shadow Spell to convert one Ward to half of the original percent as a Tower Shield.
Aegis is ineffective in preventing this cheat.
For Shields Spells targeting all allies, such as Legion Shield, he will convert all Shields for half the percent.
Absorb Wards trigger this cheat, but the Spell doesn't affect them.
[Trap] "A crude tactic" - If a Wizard casts a Trap, he'll convert the Trap into half the percent as a universal Trap.
Traps placed on all enemies will also be converted.
[Shield][Damage over Time] "Your end is nigh!" - If a Wizard casts a Shield and has a Damage over Time, Solomon Crane will cast Detonate on them.
[Fire] Buck Gordon
[Blade] "All right!" - Whenever a Blade is cast on Buck Gordon, he will convert it to a Fire Blade with double the percent.

This cheat sometimes activates when Stallion Quartermane casts Bladestorm, but it doesn't do anything as Buck Gordon didn't get the Blade. However, this cheat will activate the second Blade from Solomon's Dark Pact.
"You're going down!" - Warning call for the Rain of Fire cheat.
"Oh, come on!" - If Buck Gordon takes Damage, he'll refrain from casting Rain of Fire.
"Here it comes..." - Buck Gordon will cheat-cast Rain of Fire.
[Shadow] The Shadoe
[Round] Round 1 Cheats: - Beginning of Round 1 The Shadoe will cast the following cheats.
[Damage] The Shadoe will cast a version of Dark Fiend from The Shadoe.
[Spell] "Mind Talisman... Activate?" - The Shadoe will cast a Shadow spell giving wizards Card Limit (0 Pip) and Card Limit (2 Shadow Pip).
"I'll shoulder some of the burden" - The Shadoe will cast a version of Shadow Sentinel which intercepts 25% of Damage.
[Global] "Synthesizer's on the blink!" - The Shadoe will cast a Balance Equalize Global which sets the Damage limit to 9,999.
"That solves nothing" - If a Global is cast, The Shadoe will cast a Balance version of Equalize which limits the Damage cap to 9,999.
[Manipulation] "I believe in you!" - Randomly The Shadoe will cast Donate Shadow on a random ally of either team.
[Trap] "The Shadoe says 'No.'" - Shadoe will cast a Shadow Spell to steal a Trap from an ally to herself.
If Traps are placed on multiple enemies at once, she'll only move the Trap from the first recipient.
[Damage] "I can shoulder no more!" - The Shadoe will occasionally cast the Damage attack from Shadow Seraph.
"Hahahahahahahaha!" - After this cheat, she will cast a version of Dark Feind from The Shadoe.
[Storm] Dog Tracy
[Heal] "Hey, don't quit your day job" - Dog Tracy will cast a version of Healing Current (Shadow) that affects all allies.
[Pacify] "You're now in witness protection" - Dog Tracy will cast a version of Mega Soothe on an ally that lasts 3 Rounds.
[Damage] "That's our prime suspect. Get 'em!" - Dog Tracy will say this when casting his Natural Attack. The Natural Attack will taunt himself after dealing Damage.
strategy: Big team finale (~14,000 boss plus seven elite minions): a full team of 4 with a dedicated hitter, supports, and a healer/utility. Enter on time; joining after Round 2 unleashes a punishing barrage from every enemy.
Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (a small boost, and off his 73% Balance resist). Expect Solomon's stacking Damage Auras and Duck Savage's Fortify to cut your numbers, so overstack blades.

Self-blade your hitter only: The Bantam replaces blades cast on others with weak Balance blades, and blading Buck Gordon doubles it onto him. Avoid Shields and Traps near Solomon Crane (he converts them against you) and skip DoTs (Duck Cooldowns them, Solomon Detonates them).
Duck Savage opens with a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb. Break it with Shatter or pierce, never Steal Ward, which makes her steal all your wards back.

Prioritize the support minions that snowball the fight (Solomon's auras, The Bantam's Mass Sacred Charge, Duck Savage), heal through Mandar's Frenzy and the group hits, then funnel everything into one hit on Stallion, never leaving bosses lingering low so Stallion cannot Blessing them back up.
cheatNotes: Joining after Round 2 triggers a barrage of punishing cheats from the whole team. Enter on time.
Late: Solomon Crane hits the latecomer with Bad Juju.
Late: Buck Gordon casts Smoke Mantle on the latecomer.
Late: The Shadoe stuffs the latecomer's deck with Card Limit spells.
Late: Dog Tracy hits for 1,500 Moon damage with Insane Bolt.
Late: Stallion Mana Burns the latecomer's pips.
Late: Mandar confuses the latecomer for 2 Rounds.
Late: Duck Savage stuns the latecomer.
Late: The Bantam Infects and Angel Entangles the latecomer.
When any boss is low on health, Stallion may Blessing-heal it 10% max health. Burst bosses down fast.
Stallion Steals Pips from Wizards who bank a lot. Don't hoard pips.
His Steal Pip can take 1 or 2 Power Pips.
At the end of Round 2, Mandar gives himself Frenzy (+40% damage).
If Stallion's Quicken overwrites Mandar's Aura, Mandar recasts a stronger Frenzy with Quicken.
Every 7th Round Mandar casts Earthquake for 145 and strips 1 positive Charm and Ward from you.
Mandar can Blessing-heal himself about 652 health.
Duck Savage's 5-Pip Natural Attack leaves a +75% protected Damage Trap on her target.
On Round 1 Duck Savage shields herself with a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb.
Steal Ward on her Absorb makes her steal all your positive Wards. Shatter or pierce it instead.
She extends any enemy Heal-over-Time, such as Buck's Power Link, by a Round.
Any DoT you cast makes her Cooldown it off the target.
Pet-cast DoTs also trigger her Cooldown.
Once her 5,000 Absorb is gone she gives herself a 99-Round -50% Fortify.
She recasts the Fortify if you remove it.
On Round 1 The Bantam Pacifies for 3 Rounds, blocking attacks.
A Blade on another Wizard or minion is replaced by a weaker Balance Blade (half the percent). Self-blade the hitter.
Multi-blade spells lose only the first Blade.
All-ally Blade spells like Bladestorm lose only the caster's Blade.
The Bantam warns a Round before casting Mass Sacred Charge.
The Bantam hits all Wizards with Mass Sacred Charge (840).
On Round 1 and every 11th Round, Solomon lays a stacking Damage-reduction Aura on all Wizards, up to -50%.
At the end of Round 6 and every 12th Round, Solomon gives his team a stacking Resist Aura, up to -50%.
Casting a Shield lets Solomon convert one of your Wards into a weaker Tower Shield.
Aegis does not stop this conversion.
Legion Shield and other all-ally Shields get all converted.
Absorb Wards trigger it but are not affected.
Casting a Trap lets Solomon convert it into a weaker universal Trap.
All-enemy Traps are converted too.
If you cast a Shield while holding a DoT, Solomon Detonates the DoT on you.
A Blade on Buck Gordon becomes a Fireblade at double the percent, buffing him. Don't blade him.
Stallion's Bladestorm can trigger it harmlessly, but Solomon's Dark Pact second blade does buff Buck.
'You're going down!' warns of Buck's Rain of Fire.
If Buck takes damage he skips the Rain of Fire. Pressure him to cancel it.
'Here it comes...' means Buck cheat-casts Rain of Fire.
Round 1: The Shadoe casts Dark Fiend for damage.
Round 1: The Shadoe adds Card Limit spells to your deck.
Round 1: The Shadoe casts Shadow Sentinel, intercepting 25% of damage.
The Shadoe sets a 9,999 damage cap with an Equalize global.
Casting your own Global makes her re-cast Equalize to keep the 9,999 cap.
She randomly Donates Shadow pips to an ally of either team.
She steals a Trap off an ally onto herself.
For multi-enemy Traps she only moves the first one.
She occasionally attacks with Shadow Seraph's hit.
After that she casts another Dark Fiend.
Dog Tracy heals all his allies with a Shadow Healing Current.
Dog Tracy Pacifies an ally with Mega Soothe for 3 Rounds.
Dog Tracy's Natural Attack taunts onto himself after it hits.

name: Stallion Quartermane (Standard)
cheats: Stallion Quartermane
"Good show!" - Stallion will say this when casting his Natural Attack.
Duck Savage
"Feel my Fists of Bronze!" - Duck Savage will say this when casting her Natural Attack.
Dog Tracy

strategy: Balance boss with a crowd of minions but no punishing reactive cheats; hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+6% each).
