name: Stay-Puffed Marshfellow
locations: Karamelle: Nibbleheim Mines - Skeleton Key - Marshmellow Critter
cheats: Stay-Puffed Marshfellow
"Fun for everyone!" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast S'more Machine (Tier 3a) on the Wizard.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.

"Hot Potato!" - At the beginning of the first Round and every four Rounds, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Shadow DoT (Damage-over-Time) Spell on a random Wizard. This DoT has about 100% extra pierce dealing 2,000 Damage after 4 Rounds. (Deals 5,265 Damage with the Damage Outboost).

The Wizard who receives the DoT will be given a Hot Potato: Enemy Spell and a Hot Potato: Friend Spell. These Spells are 0-Pip versions of Shift and allow the Wizard to shift a DoT to an enemy or an ally, depending on the Spell used.

"That not how you play!" - If a Wizard uses Triage, Mass Triage, or Shift to remove the Hot Potato DoT, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Shadow Spell to instantly Expel both the caster of the Triage and the Wizard that had their Hot Potato DoT removed by the Triage.
Hamadryad will not trigger this cheat.
"Pass the potato!" - If a Wizard uses Hot Potato: Enemy to pass the Hot Potato to Stay-Puffed Marshfellow, it will shift the Hot Potato to a random Marshfellow Minion in the battle.
This cheat only seems to activate if the Wizard uses the Hot Potato: Enemy the Round after the Hot Potato cheat.
If there are no Marshfellow Minions present in battle, this cheat will not activate.

"That not how you play!" - Stay-Puffed Marshfellow has three cheats that trigger when a Wizard uses Blades or Global Spells. They will cycle in the order they are listed below. After the third cheat, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will start from the first cheat again.
The first time a Blade or Global Spell is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast Mana Burn (Shadow) on the receiver of the Blade.
The second time a Blade or Global Spell is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast Bad Juju (Shadow) on the receiver of the Blade.
The third time a Blade or Global Spell is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast Disarm (Shadow), which removes two Blades from the receiver.
Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will only target the caster of Bladestorm with these cheats.
"That not how you play!" - When a Feint is cast, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Spell to remove all Traps from all his allies.
Indemnity can be used to prevent a Trap’s removal.
Mass Feint can trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.

"Go BOOM!" - After a Marshfellow is defeated by the Shadow Damage overtime that deals 5,265 Damage, Stay-Puffed Marshfellow will cast a Shadow Spell to heal 100% max Health to all allies reviving the minion.
Marshfellow Minions
"Potato to you!" - At the end of a Round, if a Marshfellow Minion has the Hot Potato, the Marshfellow Minion will use a Fire School Spell to shift it onto a random Wizard.
strategy: Karamelle Skeleton Key boss (51200) with three minions (Death, Balance, Myth, ~5500 each). They do NOT respawn, so kill them fast to cut his Fire pressure (Raging Bull, Fire Elf, S'More Machine). Full team fight. Hit with Ice or Balance (+5%); avoid Fire and Shadow (70% resist).

Round 1 and every 4th round he plants a high-pierce Shadow Hot Potato DoT (~5265 with his boost) on a wizard, plus Hot Potato: Enemy/Friend cards. NEVER Triage or Shift it off — Triage, Mass Triage, or a normal Shift Expels both the caster and the DoT-holder. You will die. Pass it only with the Hot Potato cards.

Pass it to a Marshfellow minion (Hot Potato: Enemy) the round after it is cast, but if a potato-holding minion dies to the DoT he heals the whole enemy side to full and revives it. Time the pass so the minion survives, and keep at least one minion alive as a target.
Lead with an Indemnity-enchanted Feint. Feint or Mass Feint makes him strip every trap from his allies unless Indemnity protects it; the Balance version of Feint is also safe.

Blades and Globals are punished in a cycle on the Bladestorm caster only — first a Mana Burn, then Bad Juju, then a Disarm that removes two blades. Use one designated blader and blade-and-hit in the same round to limit losses.
Funnel blades to one hitter and burst the boss with Ice or Balance while managing the potato each cycle.
cheatNotes: A late joiner gets S'more Machine'd (Quench can dispel it). Be on time.
Round 1 and every 4th round he plants a high-pierce Shadow DoT (~5265) on a wizard. Manage it with the Hot Potato cards, not Triage.
The hit wizard gets Hot Potato: Enemy and Friend cards, 0-pip Shifts to pass the DoT to an enemy or ally. This is the intended way to move it.
Using Triage, Mass Triage, or normal Shift to remove the potato Expels both the caster and the DoT-holder from the fight. Never Triage it.
Hot Potato: Enemy passes the DoT to a random Marshfellow minion the round after the cheat. Use it to offload the DoT onto a minion.
If no Marshfellow minions are alive, the pass-to-enemy will not work. Keep at least one minion around as a potato target.
Blades and Globals trigger a 3-step punish cycle below, only on the Bladestorm caster. Designate one blader.
First blade or global: Mana Burn on the receiver. Keep pips in mind.
Second: Bad Juju (weakness) on the receiver.
Third: Disarm, removing two blades. Blade and hit in the same round to limit losses.
The cheats only target whoever cast the team blade, so route blades through one wizard and hit with another.
Feint makes him strip all traps from his allies. Indemnity protects a trap from this.
Indemnity keeps a Feint or trap safe from that removal. Protect your key trap.
Mass Feint triggers the trap wipe too. Use Indemnity or single Feints.
The Balance version of Feint does NOT trigger the trap removal, so it is the safe way to Feint here.
If a Marshfellow minion dies to the ~5265 Hot Potato DoT, he heals the whole enemy side to full and revives it. Do not let a potato-holding minion die to the DoT.
A minion holding the potato shifts it back onto a random wizard at end of round. Be ready to receive and re-pass it.

name: Stheno (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Tartarus (Challenge) - Temple of Longing (Challenge)
cheats: [Blade][Aura] "Winter always wins!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm or helpful Aura, Stheno will cast Ice Elemental on the recipient.
[Harmful Aura] "Winter withstands!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Aura on Stheno, she will cast Tower Shield on herself.
strategy: Do not cast helpful Charms or Auras. Each one makes Stheno hit that recipient with Ice Elemental, so set up with Traps and Feints instead of blades where you can.
Do not put harmful Auras on her; she answers with a Tower Shield on herself.
She resists Ice 80% and takes bonus Fire, so hit with Fire and pierce any Tower Shields.
Low 7,200 health: trap up and burst her and the Bronze Hoplite together.
cheatNotes: Casting a helpful Charm or helpful Aura makes her hit that recipient with Ice Elemental.
Casting a harmful Aura on her makes her Tower Shield herself.

name: Stokes

stats.resist: 43% to all schools73% to all schools

cheats: [Round] At the beginning of the first Round, and every other Round thereafter, Stokes will summon an additional Minion at random:
[Ice] "Aye, Ice Magic, that's the missing ingredient!" Stokes will cast an Ice Ninja Pigs Spell that deals 500 Ice Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons an Ice Ninja Pig Minion.
[Life] "Maybe the Curse just needs more Life!" Stokes will cast a Life Cyclops Spell that deals 500 Life Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Life Cyclops Minion.
[Storm] "Now feel the Scholomance's Storm!" Stokes will cast a Storm Medusa Spell that deals 500 Storm Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Storm Medusa minion.
[Fire] "Hmm, have a little Fire!" Stokes will cast a Fire Bloodbat Spell that deals 500 Fire Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Fire Bloodbat Minion.
[Death] "Let's see the Scholomance powered by Death!" Stokes will cast a Death Troll that deals 500 Death Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Death Troll Minion.
[Balance] "I'll mix the Curse up with Balance!" Stokes will cast a Balance Minotaur Spell that deals 500 Balance Damage and, if there is an open slot, summons a Balance Minotaur Minion.
The final Minion summoned can take the place of Stokes if he is defeated on the same Round.
strategy: He summons a random-school minion (each also hits for 500) every other round whenever there is an open slot, so the board keeps refilling. Bring AoE to clear minions and boss together.
Hit with Storm - he takes 30% more from Storm and resists Myth 73%.
Do not kill him on a round he just summoned a minion into an open slot - that fresh minion can take his place. Clear the minions, then finish him, ideally with one AoE that wipes the whole board.
Funnel blades to one hitter and keep the team shielded against the assorted 500-damage minion hits.
Keeping slots filled with weakened minions between AoEs limits how often he can summon a fresh one.
cheatNotes: Round 1 and every other round he summons a random-school minion into any open slot; each summon also deals 500 damage.
"Ice" summon: casts Ice Ninja Pigs for 500 Ice and, if a slot is open, summons an Ice Ninja Pig.
"Life" summon: Life Cyclops for 500 Life, plus a Life Cyclops minion if there is room.
"Storm" summon: Storm Medusa for 500 Storm, plus a Storm Medusa if there is room.
"Fire" summon: Fire Bloodbat for 500 Fire, plus a Fire Bloodbat if there is room.
"Death" summon: Death Troll for 500 Death, plus a Death Troll if there is room.
"Balance" summon: Balance Minotaur for 500 Balance, plus a Balance Minotaur if there is room.
The last minion summoned can replace Stokes if he is killed the same round. Wipe the board with AoE or do not kill him right after a summon.

name: Stone of Echoes
locations: Azteca: Zultun Dock - Abandoned House (Zultun Dock)

stats.resist: 13% to all schools43% to all schools

cheats: [Curse] "The end must be echoed or changed!" - At the beginning of every Round, Stone of Echoes will interrupt-cast to give all Wizards a -15% Charm and a DOT that does 150 Balance Damage over 3 Rounds.
[Healing] If Stone of Echoes is defeated while his Weakness cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "...Or the end comes to pass!" - At the end of the 3rd Round, and after Stone of Echoes revives, he will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Chimera (Tier 2b)]
[Balance] "The end is delayed..." - If Chimera (Tier 2b) is used, Stone of Echoes will remove all negative Charms and DOTs.
strategy: Know the revive: it weakens the whole team every Round, and if you kill it while that weakness is on you it revives at full Health.

Use the Chimera it gives you. At the end of Round 3 (and after any revive) it hands each wizard a Chimera card that clears all your negative charms and DOTs; cast it right before the kill to disable the revive.
Attack with any school except Balance. It resists Balance 43% but only 13% elsewhere, so hit into anything else.
At 7,585 Health, Chimera off the weakness, blade once, and finish it in the same window.
Heal or shield through the per-Round -15% charm and 150 Balance DOT while you set up.
cheatNotes: Every Round it interrupt-casts a -15% weakness and a 150 Balance damage-over-time on all wizards.
If you defeat it while that weakness is still on you it revives at full Health, and it reapplies the weakness every Round.
At the end of Round 3 (and after any revive) it gives each wizard a Chimera card.
Casting that Chimera removes all your negative charms and DOTs, clearing the weakness so the revive cheat cannot trigger; do this right before the kill.

name: Sudri
cheats: "Wrong brother! I'm it!" - Casting Spells at a Boss without the Stun Shield will trigger Sudri casting Hydra dealing 190 Fire, Ice & Storm Damage.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Ice Damage.
strategy: Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (+25%); he resists Balance 80%.
Target the correct brother, the one wearing the Stun Shield. Hitting a boss that lacks the Stun Shield triggers Sudri's Hydra (190 Fire, Ice, and Storm).
Watch which target has the Stun Shield each round and funnel your attacks there.
Stack blades and a Feint, then hit the shielded target to avoid the Hydra counter.
With only 3,520 health, a focused boosted hit ends it quickly.
cheatNotes: Attacking a boss that does not have the Stun Shield makes Sudri cast Hydra (190 each of Fire, Ice, Storm). Always target the brother wearing the Stun Shield.

name: Sugarr Eater of Souls
cheats: "More souls for Sugarr" - If a Wizard is late to combat, Sugarr Eater of Souls will cast a Shadow version of S'more Machine which deals 1,020 Shadow Damage, removes all Pips and places an Impede on the Wizard.

"Sugarr still has some bite left!" - If a Wizard defeats Sugarr Eater of Souls while a Sugar Cube minion is still alive, he'll be revived with 75% of his total health, and cast a Shadow S'more Machine that removes 100% of the Wizard's Health defeating the Wizard who defeated the boss.

"Beware the aftertaste!" - If a Wizard defeats a Sugar Cube, the defeated Sugar Cube will cast the 1,020 Damage Shadow S'more Machine on the Wizard that defeated them, removing their pips, and placing an Impede on them.

"Sugarr Rush!" - If Sugarr Eater of Souls takes damage, the Boss will cast a version of Sugar Rush that gives all allies 5 Pips, first giving 2 Power Pips then 1 Pip without an Impede aura, then places Quicken on all allies.
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Death Damage.
strategy: Kill every Sugar Cube before you kill Sugarr — defeating Sugarr while a Cube is alive revives him at 75% health and instantly defeats whoever killed him.
Expect a counter when you kill a Sugar Cube: it hits the killer with a 1,020 Shadow S'more Machine, strips pips, and Impedes — recover pips and health before going for Sugarr.
Minimize chip hits on Sugarr: any damage to him triggers Sugarr Rush, handing his side 5 pips and Quicken.
Attack with Life (his only boost at +5%; he resists Death 73%), stacking blades and a Feint to burst him once the Cubes are gone.
Enter on time — late wizards eat a 1,020 S'more that strips pips and Impedes.
cheatNotes: Join late and he hits you with a 1,020 Shadow S'more Machine that removes your pips and Impedes you — enter on time.
Defeating Sugarr while any Sugar Cube is still alive revives him at 75% health and instantly defeats whoever killed him — clear all Cubes first.
Killing a Sugar Cube makes it counter-hit the killer for 1,020, strip their pips, and Impede them — expect to recover after clearing each Cube.
Any damage to Sugarr triggers Sugarr Rush, handing his side 5 pips and Quicken — don't poke him; burst him once the Cubes are down.

name: Sylster Glowstorm
cheats: Interrupt Cycle: All cycles last four Rounds, except for the first Trap cycle, which lasts three Rounds. These cycles start on the first Round of the fight and repeat until all enemies are defeated.

"Traps go undetected in the light!" - Sylster Glowstorm will cheat-cast a light Doom and Gloom, during which Wizards may cast Traps and Prisms. Sylster Glowstorm will remove positive Charms by cheat-casting a version of Cleanse Charm that removes three positive Charms from the caster.
Dissipate can be used to dispel this cheat.
Negative Charm Spells, such as Weakness, Smoke Screen, and Dispels, trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells do not trigger this cheat.
Shadow Creature Blades trigger this cheat.
Aegis prevents one Charm spell from being removed.

At the beginning of the first Round of this cycle starting on Round 8, Sylster Glowstorm cheat-casts a version of Cleanse Charm that removes three positive Charms on all Wizards and a version of Cleanse Ward that removes three Traps on himself and all Minions.

"The blade goes unseen in the dark!" - Sylster Glowstorm will cheat-cast a dark Doom and Gloom, during which Wizards may cast Blades and other positive Charms. Sylster Glowstorm will remove Traps and Prisms by cheat-casting Cleanse Ward that removes one Trap or Prism.
Vaporize can be used to dispel this cheat.
For Elemental Trap and Spirit Trap, only the last Trap (Storm, Life) is removed.
Multi-target Trap Spells, such as Windstorm and Mass Prisms, do not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity prevents one Trap spell from being removed.
At the beginning of the first Round of this cycle, Sylster Glowstorm cheat-casts a version of Cleanse Ward that removes three Traps on himself and all Minions.

"No Traps?!" / "No Blades?!" - If a Wizard casts a single-target attack Spell without activating a Trap (during a light cycle) or Blade (during a dark cycle), then the enemy that was attacked will cheat-cast the following Spell:
Sylster Glowstorm casts Ra, dealing 1,460-1,540 Balance Damage.
Unbalance can dispel this cheat.
Spellhammer Sorcerer casts Scarecrow, dealing 600 Death Damage.
Water-Wing casts Rebirth (no Absorbs), healing 750 Health. With Doom and Gloom active, this cheat will only heal 375 Health.
All Enemy attack Spells do not trigger this cheat.
Single-target Spells that also attack all enemies such as Sun Serpent trigger this cheat.
If a Wizard casts any Global Spell, Sylster Glowstorm will replace the Global with the current Doom and Gloom.
Strangle can be used to dispel either Doom and Gloom, although Charms or Traps will still be removed if casted during the wrong Global, no matter what Global is currently active.
Minion Boss Cheats: - Cheats will carry over to the next in Boss in charge as follows.

"Not da' Boss!" - If Sylster Glowstorm has been defeated, the Spellhammer Sorcerer replaces Sylster's role in casting the light and dark Doom and Gloom cycles, starting with the initial three Round light Doom and Gloom.

"Where did everyone go?" - If Sylster Glowstorm and the Spellhammer Sorcerer have been defeated, the Water-Wing replaces the role in casting the light and dark Doom and Gloom cycles, starting with the initial three Round light Doom and Gloom.
strategy: Waterworks finale team fight. Two alternating globals: light Doom and Gloom lets you cast Traps/Prisms (but strips positive charms), dark Doom and Gloom lets you cast Blades (but strips traps). They swap every ~4 rounds.
Match your setup to the global: lay Feints and traps during the light cycle, blades during the dark cycle. Protect one charm with Aegis and one trap with Indemnity across the swap.

When you attack, always activate a Trap (during light) or a Blade (during dark) — a single-target hit without one makes the enemy you hit cheat-cast (Sylster's 1,460-1,540 Ra, Scarecrow, or a Rebirth heal).

Multi-target traps (Windstorm, Mass Prisms) don't get stripped, and hits that also AoE (like Sun Serpent) still count — use these to play around the cycle. Never cast a Global, he replaces it with the current Doom and Gloom.
Funnel to one hitter, set up a trap-and-blade layered hit for a cycle where both are allowed, and burst. If Sylster dies the cheats pass to the Spellhammer Sorcerer, then the Water-Wing.
cheatNotes: During the light Doom and Gloom you may cast Traps and Prisms, but he Cleanse Charms three positive charms off the caster — save blades for the dark cycle.
Dissipate dispels this light cheat.
Negative charms (Weakness, Smoke Screen, Dispels) trigger the charm removal.
Pet spells don't trigger it.
Shadow Creature blades trigger it.
Aegis protects one charm from removal.
On the light cycle starting round 8 he strips three positive charms off all wizards and three traps off himself and his minions.
During the dark Doom and Gloom you may cast Blades and positive charms, but he Cleanse Wards one trap or prism off — save traps for the light cycle.
Vaporize dispels this dark cheat.
Elemental/Spirit Traps only lose their last part (Storm, Life).
Multi-target trap spells (Windstorm, Mass Prisms) don't get stripped — use them.
Indemnity protects one trap from removal.
On the first round of the dark cycle he strips three traps off himself and his minions.
Attack single-target without activating a trap (light) or blade (dark) and the enemy you hit cheat-casts a punish spell.
Sylster's punish is Ra for 1,460-1,540 Balance.
Unbalance dispels that Ra cheat.
The Spellhammer Sorcerer's punish is Scarecrow for 600 Death.
The Water-Wing's punish is a Rebirth healing 750 (375 under Doom and Gloom).
Enemy attack spells don't trigger the punish.
Single-target hits that also AoE (Sun Serpent) still count as answering the cycle.
Cast any Global and he replaces it with the current Doom and Gloom — don't bother.
Strangle can dispel either Doom and Gloom, but charms/traps still get removed if cast during the wrong global.
If Sylster dies, the Spellhammer Sorcerer takes over the cycle, restarting with the light Doom and Gloom.
If Sylster and the Spellhammer both die, the Water-Wing takes over the cycle.

name: Taffy
cheats: Taffy

[Late] "The more the merrier!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Taffy will place an Aegis version of Tower Shield (-55%) on Ricky and Tiki, and an Aegis version of Frozen Armor (185) on himself.
[Trap] "One dirty trick deserves another!" - If a Wizard places a Trap on any of the brothers, he will cast an Ice version of Cleanse Ward to remove 1 Trap from the target.
Indemnity can be used to prevent the removal of a particular Trap.
Spells that leave Traps behind, such as Fire from Above, will trigger this cheat.
Using Mass Feint (+75%) will cause Taffy to remove the Trap placed on the caster. This will not remove any of the Traps on the Bosses.
Spells like Windstorm will not trigger this cheat.
Ricky
[Late] "Cheater!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Ricky will triple-cast Healing Current on himself, giving 100, 400, then 1,000 health.
[Blade] "Nice try!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, then Ricky will cast Steal Charm on the Wizard that received the Blade.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.
Aegis can be used to prevent removal.
If a tri-Blade Spell is used, then only the last part of the Blade will be removed (Fire for Elemental Blades and Life for Spirit Blades).
If a multi-target Blade Spell is used (e.g. Bladestorm), only the Wizard who cast the Spell will be affected.
Steal Charm can be used to steal back the Blade without a penalty.
Supercharge does not trigger this cheat.
Tiki
[Late] "You should be ashamed!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Tiki will cast Guiding Light on himself and Ricky and an Aegis-Protected Spirit Armor on Taffy.
[Damage over Time] "Let's spice things up!" - If a DoT is cast on anyone in the duel, he will remove it.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Enter on time. A late join buffs all three brothers with Aegis shields, heals, and Guiding Lights.
Attack with Fire; Taffy takes +10% and resists Ice 35%. He and his brothers are stunable and beguilable, so stuns can buy tempo.
Do not lean on traps. Taffy Cleanse-Wards a trap off whichever brother you trapped, so Indemnity a key trap if you must use one.
Do not rely on single blades. Ricky Steal-Charms the blade off whoever you bladed, so Aegis the blade, Steal Charm it back, or use Supercharge, which does not trigger it.
Do not use DoTs at all. Tiki removes any DoT in the duel, pet DoTs included. Stack Aegis-protected blades, then AoE all three together since they buff each other.
cheatNotes: Joining late puts an Aegis Tower Shield (-55%) on Ricky and Tiki and an Aegis Frozen Armor (185) on Taffy. Enter at the start.
Placing a Trap on any brother makes Taffy Cleanse-Ward one trap off that target.
Indemnity on a trap prevents that removal.
Spells that leave traps behind, like Fire from Above, also trigger this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) makes Taffy remove only the trap on the caster, not the traps on the bosses.
Spells like Windstorm do not trigger this cheat.
Joining late makes Ricky triple-cast Healing Current on himself for 100, then 400, then 1,000 health.
Casting a Blade makes Ricky Steal-Charm it off the wizard who received it. Aegis, Steal Charm it back, or use Supercharge instead.
Quench can dispel this Steal Charm cheat.
Aegis on the blade prevents the steal.
On a tri-blade, only the last part is stolen (Fire on Elemental blades, Life on Spirit blades).
On a multi-target blade like Bladestorm, only the casting wizard is affected.
You can Steal Charm the blade back without penalty.
Supercharge does not trigger the Steal Charm cheat.
Joining late makes Tiki cast Guiding Light on himself and Ricky and an Aegis Spirit Armor on Taffy.
Any DoT cast on anyone in the duel is removed by Tiki. Do not use damage-over-time.
Pet spells trigger Tiki's DoT removal too.

name: Tajniak
locations: Polaris: River of Frozen Tears - Sunless Shrine
cheats: The goal of this battle is to defeat the untruthful bear before the honest bear. Jawniak is the liar.
This Boss will shout statements about Jawniak's attacks every few Rounds.
Jawniak will shout statements about Tajniak's attacks every few Rounds.
Tajniak
Tajniak will always cast Cloaked versions of positive Charms.
"Why don't we trade!" - If a Feint is placed on Tajniak, he will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward that switches the Traps between himself and the caster.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Mass Feint (+75%) will not trigger this cheat.
Jawniak
Jawniak will always cast Cloaked versions of negative Charms.
If Tajniak is defeated first, Jawniak will cast Poison on the Wizard in the first position, and then detonate it immediately for ~33,000 Death Damage.
Every Round, he will cast Poison on another Wizard, going in order of battle position.
Fleeing or porting will not stop this cheat.
spellNotes: This Creature's Brilliant Light (Cloaked) is a single target Spell.
strategy: Two-bear puzzle: defeat the LIAR, Jawniak, before the honest bear Tajniak. If Tajniak dies first, Jawniak wipes the team with a ~33,000 Death Poison detonation.
Focus all damage on Jawniak. Tajniak takes +15% from Death (resists Life 50%), so Death hits work, but keep Tajniak alive until Jawniak is dead.
Jawniak cloaks negative charms and Tajniak cloaks positive charms; the bears shout hints about each other's attacks.
Do not single-Feint Tajniak; he Swap Wards it onto you (Melt dispels, Mass Feint +75% does not trigger it).
Blade up, funnel damage into Jawniak, kill him first, then clean up Tajniak.
cheatNotes: The whole fight: kill the liar Jawniak before the honest bear Tajniak. Reverse that order and Jawniak wipes you with a ~33,000 Poison.
This boss shouts hints about Jawniak's attacks. Read them to track the puzzle.
Jawniak shouts hints about Tajniak's attacks in turn.
Tajniak always cloaks his positive charms, so you will not see his buffs coming. Expect hidden charms.
A Feint on Tajniak gets Swap Warded onto you. Use Mass Feint (+75%) instead, which does not trigger it.
Melt dispels this swap cheat if you want to Feint normally.
Mass Feint (+75%) is safe against the swap.
Jawniak always cloaks his negative charms. Expect hidden weaknesses and traps from him.
If Tajniak dies first, Jawniak Poisons the front wizard and detonates it for ~33,000 Death, a wipe. Always kill Jawniak first.
After that he Poisons the next wizard each round down the line. There is no recovering once Tajniak is dead.
Fleeing or porting does not stop the Poison chain. Get the kill order right the first time.

name: Takanobu the Masterless (Tier 1)
locations: MooShu: Cave of Solitude - Cave of Solitude Gold Key Room
cheats: Takanobu the Masterless
"No! I disarm you." - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, Takanobu the Masterless will cast Disarm on the Wizard who received the Blade.
If a triple Blade is cast (e.g. Elemental Blade or Spirit Blade), he will only remove the last Blade (Fire or Life).
If a multi-target Blade is cast (e.g. Bladestorm), he will only remove the Blade from the Wizard that cast the Spell.
"My katana dances around your shield." - If a Wizard casts Storm Shield, Volcanic Shield, or Glacial Shield, he will cheat-cast Storm Prism on the Wizard who received the Shield.
"KIAI!" - If Takanobu has at least 5 Pips, and an odd number of Pips, at the end of a Round, he will cheat-cast a 50% Supercharge on himself.
"This wind is an omen!" - If Takanobu has 6 or more Pips at the beginning of the Round, he will cheat-cast Darkwind.
Bladeless Ronin
"We must stay centered!" - If a Wizard attacks any enemy with a Balance Spell, the Bladeless Ronin in the first position (Dagger) will cheat-cast Unbalance on the Wizard.
If an All Enemies attack is used, the Bladeless Ronin will cast Unbalance for each enemy that was hit.
Attacking with Chimera results in the Bladeless Ronin casting three separate Spirit Defuses.
Attacking with Hydra results in the Bladeless Ronin casting three separate Elemental Defuses.
Attacks which deal no direct damage, but apply aura effects, such as Scald or Heck Hound will not activate any cheats by the Bladeless Ronin.
"Guard against the elements!" - If a Wizard attacks any enemy with a Fire, Ice, or Storm Spell, the Bladeless Ronin in the second position (Key) will cheat-cast Elemental Defuse on the Wizard.
If an All Enemies attack is used, the Bladeless Ronin will cast Elemental Defuse for each enemy that was hit.
Triggering a Prism that converts damage to Life, Death, or Myth, will result in the Bladeless Ronin casting Spirit Defuse instead of Elemental Defuse.
"Spirits, guard us!" - If a Wizard attacks any enemy with a Life, Death, or Myth Spell, the Bladeless Ronin in the third position (Gem) will cheat-cast Spirit Defuse on the Wizard.

locations: MooShu: Cave of Solitude - Cave of Solitude Gold Key Room
cheats: Takanobu the Masterless [Spiral]
[Blade] "No! I disarm you." - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, then Takanobu the Masterless will interrupt-cast Disarm on the recipient of the Blade.

[Ward] "My katana dances around your shield." - If a Wizard casts any Storm Shield Spell, then Takanobu will interrupt-cast Storm Prism on the recipient of the Shield.
[All Enemies (Squared)] "Control your Energy!" - If a Wizard casts an All Enemies Spell, then Takanobu will interrupt-cast a version of Tempest that always deals 560 Storm Damage.

[Pip] "KIAI!" - At the end of any Round, if Takanobu has at least 5 Pips, then he will interrupt-cast Supercharge on himself.
Bladeless Ronin
[Dagger][Balance][Dispel] "We must stay centered!" - If any enemy receives Balance Damage, then this Bladeless Ronin will interrupt-cast an all-enemies version of Unbalance.

[Key][Elemental][Dispel] "Guard against the elements!" - If any enemy receives Fire, Ice, or Storm Damage, then this Bladeless Ronin will interrupt-cast an all-enemies version of Quench, Melt, or Dissipate based on the School of the Damage that was received.

[Gem][Spirit][Dispel] "Spirits, guard us!" - If any enemy receives Myth, Life, or Death Damage, then this Bladeless Ronin will interrupt-cast an all-enemies version of Vaporize, Entangle, or Strangle based on the School of the Damage that was received.
Damage over Time effects do not trigger the Bladeless Ronins' cheats.
If a Spell deals Damage more than once, then the Bladeless Ronin will cast an all-enemies Dispel matching the School of the Damage that were received in the order that they were received.
Casting a Spell that hits multiple enemies will result in the respective Bladeless Ronin repeating their cheat per enemy hit.
Casting Spells such as Hydra on any enemy will result in the [Key] Bladeless Ronin casting Quench, Melt, and Dissipate, in that order.

Casting spells such as Scales of Destiny on any enemy will result in the [Gem] Bladeless Ronin casting Entangle, the [Key] Bladeless Ronin casting Melt, and the [Dagger] Bladeless Ronin casting Unbalance, in that order.
Dealing Damage through a Prism will result in the Bladeless Ronin casting a Dispel that matches the School of the Damage after the Prism is applied.
strategy: The fight is easy once you respect the cheats: Takanobu Disarms any blade you cast, so buff with traps instead of blades.
Stack Feints on him, then have your hitter cash them in; the guide says this can be done in 1-2 turns by spamming Feints for the hitter.
Prefer a single-hit, single-target attack for the kill. Any all-enemies spell triggers a guaranteed 560 Storm Tempest on your team, and multi-hit spells trigger one dispel per hit.
Do not cast Storm shields (he answers with a Storm Prism on you) and mind his pips: at 6+ pips he opens with Darkwind, and at 5+ pips he ends the round with Supercharge, so kill fast.

Watch the Bladeless Ronin dispels: any Balance damage triggers Unbalance, any Fire/Ice/Storm damage triggers the matching Quench/Melt/Dissipate, and any Myth/Life/Death damage triggers Vaporize/Entangle/Strangle on your whole team.
A Damage-over-Time hit sidesteps all of it: DoT damage does not trigger the Ronins' dispel cheats, making a DoT hitter the cleanest kill. Hit with Myth (+40%) around his 70% Storm resist.

Damage-over-Time effects do not trigger the Ronins' dispel cheats, so a DoT hitter avoids all of them.
A spell that deals damage more than once triggers one dispel per hit, applied in the order the hits landed, so avoid multi-hit spells.

Casting Hydra pulls all three elemental dispels from the Key Ronin: Quench, then Melt, then Dissipate.
Casting Scales of Destiny triggers Entangle from the Gem Ronin, Melt from the Key Ronin, and Unbalance from the Dagger Ronin, in that order.

strategy: Understand the swap. Cast a helpful Charm, helpful Ward, or HoT and the Tree swaps all effects of that type with the receiver; cast a harmful Charm, harmful Ward, or DoT and it swaps that type with the caster.

Turn the swap into an exploit. Load your hitter with blades, then have that same hitter be the one who triggers a helpful-charm swap so the Tree hands over its own buffs, or Feint it and let the harmful-Charm swap dump your Weakness onto it while pulling its blades to you.
Keep effect types clean per round. Because it swaps by matching type, apply only one category at a time and place the trigger on the wizard you want to end up buffed.
Beware secondary effects and AoE. Multi-target spells like Bladestorm swap with the caster, and riders like Fire Dragon's DoT trigger the swap too, so account for hidden effects before you commit.
This is a team fight: 30,060 Health, 148% boost, 69% Myth resist and 49% across most schools, taking least resist to Death (29%). Bring a Death hitter or prisms and one dedicated finisher.
Clear the Wandering Weed it summons on Round 1, set the swap up so your hitter holds the blades, then AoE the Tree and Weed down together in one burst.

"Virtue dies when truth is realized" - At the end of the first Round, and any Round where a Wizard triggered the above cheat, Taking Tree will cheat-cast one of the following Spells:

strategy: Hit with Death. The Tree takes +30% from Death, resists Life 67%, and resists everything else 37%. Funnel all setup to one hitter.

Understand the swap. Any helpful Charm, Ward, or heal-over-time a Wizard casts gets swapped onto the Tree, and any harmful Charm, Ward, or DoT (traps, Feint, weakness) swaps back onto the caster. Unprotected blades are stolen and unprotected Feints land on your own hitter.
Protect the key setup. Aegis the hitter's main blade and Indemnity the Feint so they survive the one swap. Supports should only feed the hitter through protected or secondary-effect charms.
Avoid DoT hitters and unprotected traps. The DoT tick (even the one from Fire Dragon) swaps to the caster, so stick to direct-damage hits.

Minimize how often you trigger the swap. Each triggered round the Tree buffs itself with a Dark Pact (which also hands it a +35% to +45% Damage Charm) or a 600 absorb. Stack protected blades plus Feint over a couple rounds, then land one Death nuke from the hitter.
cheatNotes: Cast an unprotected blade, shield, or heal-over-time on your hitter and the Tree steals it onto itself. Protect it with Aegis or it is gone.
Traps, Feint, weakness, and DoTs you put on the Tree bounce back onto whoever cast them. Protect with Indemnity or don't cast them raw.
Bladestorm and other mass effects only swap with the caster, sparing the rest of the team.

Side effects like the DoT on Fire Dragon also trigger the swap, so avoid DoT-based hitters here.
Aegis on your blade and Indemnity on your Feint let them survive the swap one time, which is enough to set up the hit.
At the end of Round 1, and every round you trigger the swap, the Tree buffs or heals itself. Minimize swaps to keep it from stacking.

It can also shield itself with a 600 absorb, so your finishing hit needs to clear that buffer.
