These are low-health enemies, but killing one out of order makes the others interrupt-cast attacks every Round (Sandstorm, Big Bad Hound). Don't pick them off one at a time.
Build a quick blade and finish everything with one AoE so nothing survives to retaliate.
cheatNotes: Once Lacombe dies, Berkshire Sandstorms your team every Round. Kill them together, not one at a time.
Beguiling Berkshire or Lacombe also triggers the Sandstorm cheat.
Once Berkshire dies, Tamworth casts Big Bad Hound every Round. AoE everything at once.

name: Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: [Damage] "Here's how the story goes!" - Every Round after when Lacombe is defeated, Berkshire interrupt-casts Sandstorm.
Cheat will trigger when either Berkshire or Lacombe is Beguiled.
[Damage] "Shake it down, shake it down now!" - When Berkshire is defeated, Boss interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every Round.
strategy: Kill everything in one AoE. Defeating Lacombe makes Berkshire Sandstorm the team every round, and defeating Berkshire makes Tamworth cast Big Bad Hound every round.
Do not Beguile the minions either; beguiling Berkshire or Lacombe triggers the Sandstorm cheat.
Stack a blade or trap and drop one AoE so the boss and both houses fall together.
Balance resists itself 50%, so hit off-school.
cheatNotes: Once Lacombe is defeated, Berkshire interrupt-casts Sandstorm on the team every round.
Beguiling Berkshire or Lacombe triggers that Sandstorm cheat too.
Once Berkshire is defeated, Tamworth interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every round.

name: Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 3)
cheats: [Damage] "Here's how the story goes!" - When Lacombe StrawHouse (Tier 3) is defeated, every Round after that Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 3) interrupt-casts Sandstorm.
[Damage] "Shake it down, shake it down now!" - When Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 3) is defeated, Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 3) interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every Round.
Using Beguile on Lacombe StrawHouse (Tier 3) will trigger the first cheat, and using it on Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 3) will trigger both cheats.
strategy: Killing the StrawHouse makes the StickHouse Sandstorm you every round; killing the StickHouse makes Tamworth Big Bad Hound you every round. The longer minions stay dead while the boss lives, the more AoE you eat.
Best line: AoE all three houses down together so no interrupt cheat ever starts. Stack blades/traps and hit with one AoE.
If you cannot one-shot the board, kill the minions last (or the same turn as Tamworth) to minimize the interrupt rounds.
Hit with Myth, Life, or Death - they take 13% more and Tamworth resists Balance 70%.
cheatNotes: Once the StrawHouse is defeated, the StickHouse interrupt-casts Sandstorm every round until it dies. Do not leave it alive alone.
Once the StickHouse is defeated, Tamworth interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every round. Finish him fast after this point.
Beguiling the StrawHouse triggers the first cheat; Beguiling the StickHouse triggers both. Avoid Beguile on the minions.

name: Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 4)
cheats: [Damage] "Here's how the story goes!" - At random times, Berkshire casts a 0-pip version of Sandstorm every round after 5 rounds.
[Damage] "Shake it down, shake it down now!" - When both minions are dead, he casts Big Bad Hound every round.
strategy: Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (he takes +25% each); never Balance (80% resist).
Do not leave him alone at high Health. The moment both minions die he casts Big Bad Hound every Round, so AoE all three down together or be ready to finish him immediately.
Close before the fight drags. Starting after Round 5, Berkshire randomly adds a free 0-pip Sandstorm each Round.
Blade and AoE the whole board so the minions and boss fall in the same window.
At 7,800 Health with two minions, this is soloable with a strong AoE hitter.
cheatNotes: Starting after Round 5, Berkshire randomly adds a free 0-pip Sandstorm each Round, so close the fight before it drags on.
The moment both minions are dead, Tamworth casts Big Bad Hound every Round, so AoE all three down together or be ready to finish him immediately.

name: Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Spiral Cup Gauntlet - Spiral Cup Gauntlet (Tier 5)
cheats: [Damage] "Here's how the story goes!" - When Lacombe StrawHouse (Tier 5) is defeated, every Round after Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 5) interrupt-casts Sandstorm.
[Damage] "Shake it down, shake it down now!" - When Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 5) is defeated, Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 5) interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every Round.

When Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 5) is Beguiled and his turn comes before Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 5), he will cast his Sandstorm cheat first following with the Big Bad Hound cheat from Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 5). Otherwise, Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 5) will cast his cheat before him.
When Lacombe StrawHouse (Tier 5) is Beguiled, only the Sandstorm cheat is triggered.

Berkshire StickHouse (Tier 5) and Tamworth BrickHouse (Tier 5) have simple checks to see if a Creature is on their side. When Beguiled, their cheats are triggered because they assume the Creature is defeated. In the case of the double cheat, Berkshire is not with Lacombe, and Tamworth isn't with Berkshire, thus double cheating.
strategy: Attack with Myth, Life, or Death; Tamworth resists Balance 80% and takes +30% from those three. He is not stunable.
Do not kill the minions one at a time. Killing Lacombe (straw) escalates to Sandstorm every round, and then killing Berkshire (stick) escalates to Big Bad Hound every round.
Stack blades and clear all three houses together with one big AoE rather than picking them off in order.
Do not beguile the pigs. Their side-check reads a beguiled ally as gone and triggers the cheats early, sometimes double-cheating in one round.
Set up over a few rounds, then finish Lacombe, Berkshire, and Tamworth on the same turn with a single Myth, Life, or Death AoE.
cheatNotes: Once Lacombe (straw) is dead, Berkshire (stick) interrupt-casts Sandstorm every round. Do not kill the straw house early on its own.
Once Berkshire (stick) is dead, Tamworth (brick) interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every round. Killing the minions in order just escalates the punishment; clear all three together with an AoE.
If Berkshire is beguiled and his turn comes before Tamworth, both the Sandstorm and Big Bad Hound cheats fire in one round.
If Lacombe is beguiled, only the Sandstorm cheat triggers.
Their cheats use a simple check for whether an ally is still present, so beguiling a pig reads as it being gone and triggers the cheat. Avoid beguiling them.

name: Tantrum (Death-Life, Rank 22)
cheats: "Snicker" - Occasionally, Tantrum (Death-Life, Rank 22) will cast a Death Equalize Global which sets the Damage cap to 500.
spellNotes: Luminous Weaver (Tier 3b) is the Item Card version and does not have a casting animation.
strategy: He is stunable and beguilable, so a stun buys safe setup rounds.
His only cheat occasionally sets a Death Equalize capping your damage at 500, so a single giant hit can get wasted. If the cap is up, plan several 500 hits; if it is not, burst normally.
Watch the Global before committing your big hit. If the 500 cap is active, replace it with your own Global or grind him down in 500-damage chunks.
No resist is listed, so attack with your strongest school; Life boosts against his Death side.
Stack blades and a Feint, then hit when the cap is down.
cheatNotes: He occasionally drops a Death Equalize capping all damage at 500. Check the Global before your kill shot; if it is up, overwrite it or hit in 500-damage chunks.

name: Taweret
cheats: "I AM THE SHIELD OF VENGEANCE!" - Taweret will say this at the beginning of combat.
"I WILL PROTECT THE PEOPLE!" - At the end of the first Round and at the end of every fourth Round, Taweret will cast a version of Taunt.
Cheat Cycle: This cycle begins on Round 2 and repeats every 3 Rounds.
At the beginning of the first Round of the cycle, Taweret will cheat-cast a -25% version of Tower Shield on herself.
At the beginning of the second Round of the cycle, Taweret will cheat-cast Legion Shield.
"YOU LIE TRAMPLED AT MY MIGHTY FEET!" - At the beginning of the third Round of the cycle, Taweret will cast one of the following attacks:
Earth Walker
Gnomes!
Forest Lord
Wings of Fate
"HA! WELL DONE!" - Taweret will say this if attacked during this Round. If defeated, she will not respawn.
"You shouldn't skip the story." - If Taweret is defeated on a Round other than the third Round of her cheat cycle, she will cast Blessing, restoring herself to 133 Health.
If defeated by a Damage over Time effect, she will heal an additional 133 Health for each Round remaining.

"MY PROTECTION SHALL NOT BE BREACHED!" - If any of Taweret's Minions are defeated before her, in response to multi-target attacks or effects, she will cast her Natural Attack on a random Wizard, dealing 1500 Life Damage. She will not do this if you defeat the minion on the 3rd turn of her cycle.
"I HEAR ALL!" - If she is attacked on the first or second round of the cycle, or a Wizard steals one of Taweret's Charms or Wards, she will cast her Natural Attack on the offending Wizard.
strategy: Moonseum boss (21,145 health), so run it as a team: hitter, blading supports, and a healer.

She can only be defeated on the third round of her cheat cycle — her 'TRAMPLED' attack round (round 4, then 7, 10). Kill her on any other round and she Blessings back to 133 health (more if killed by a DoT).
Hold all damage until that attack round: hitting her on the first or second round of the cycle triggers her Natural Attack on the attacker, and stealing her charms/wards does too.
Use rounds 1-3 to stack blades on the hitter and Feint her; bring Pierce or Shatter, since she stacks a -25% Tower and a Legion Shield during the cycle.
On the attack round, unload one huge Death hit (she takes +30%; she resists Life 71%) to kill her outright — attacking that round is safe and she won't respawn.
Be careful clearing her minions: killing them before her with a multi-target hit makes her Natural Attack a random wizard for 1,500 Life.
cheatNotes: She Taunts at the end of round 1 and every 4th round, forcing targeting onto her — plan around the taunt window.
First round of each cycle she puts up a -25% Tower Shield on herself.
Second round of the cycle she adds a Legion Shield — by her attack round she's shielded, so bring Pierce or Shatter for the kill hit.
Third round of the cycle she casts a big attack (Earth Walker, Gnomes, Forest Lord, or Wings of Fate) — this is the ONLY round you can defeat her, and attacking her here is safe.
Defeating her on this attack round sticks — she won't respawn.
Defeat her on any round other than her attack round and she Blessings back to 133 health — time your kill to the attack round only.
Killing her with a DoT is worse: she heals an extra 133 per remaining DoT round — use direct damage for the kill.
Killing her minions before her with a multi-target hit makes her Natural Attack a random wizard for 1,500 Life (unless you kill the minion on her attack round) — be careful clearing them.
Attacking her on the first or second round of the cycle, or stealing her charms/wards, triggers her Natural Attack on the offender — only commit on the attack round.

name: Teles (Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Atlantea (Challenge) - Temple of Sea Songs (Challenge)
cheats: [Jinx] "Fire burns Myth." - Whenever a harmful Ward is placed on Teles, Teles will cast Brimstone Revenant on the caster.
Spells that place harmful Wards on all enemies will trigger this cheat.
[Ward] "You cannot shelter from the flame!" - Whenever a helpful Ward is cast, Teles will cast Helephant on the recipient.
Spells that place Shields on all allies will trigger this cheat, and Teles will cast Helephant on the caster.
strategy: Solo-friendly (6,025 health). Never hit Fire; he resists it 80%. Hit with Ice, his boost school.
Do not trap him: any harmful ward triggers Brimstone Revenant on you, and all-enemies traps trigger it too.
Do not shield: any helpful ward makes him Helephant the recipient, and all-allies shields hit the caster.
Clear or AoE the Siren minion alongside him.
Stack Ice blades (charms are safe), skip traps and shields, then land one big Ice hit.
cheatNotes: Putting a trap or weakness on Teles gets you hit with Brimstone Revenant. Blade instead of trap.
An all-enemies trap, like a group Feint, still triggers it.
Casting any shield makes Teles hit the shielded wizard with Helephant.
An all-allies shield triggers it too, hitting the caster.

name: Termonarch
locations: MooShu: Cave of Solitude - Termite Lair
cheats: [Jinx] "SCREE!" - At the beginning of every Round, Termonarch will cast a version of Keeper of the Flame that gives a +15% chromatic Trap on the Wizard with the highest Health. This increases by 5% each Round, up to a maximum of 40%.
[Healing] If Termonarch is defeated while their Keeper of the Flame cheat is still active, they will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "Oh, oh, I have something for this. Here!" - At the end of the fourth Round and whenever Termonarch revives, The Chosen One will give Wizards the following Spell:
[Centaur (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]

[Life] "Scree?" - If a Wizard casts a Life Damage Spell that removes Traps from Wizards on any opponent, such as Leprechaun (Tier 2b), they will remove all Traps on Wizards and end their Keeper of the Flame cheat.
strategy: Clear its trap cheat before killing it. Termonarch stacks a chromatic Keeper of the Flame trap on your highest-Health wizard every round, and if it dies while that cheat is active it revives at full Health.
Use the intended counter: a Life damage spell that removes traps from wizards (like Leprechaun Tier 2b) strips all those traps and ends the Keeper of the Flame cheat, disabling the revive.
Line the kill up right after you clear. Cast the trap-removing Life spell, then finish Termonarch the same window so the cheat cannot re-arm before the kill.
It is Myth and takes boosted Fire and Life, so a Fire or Life hitter fits; the Leprechaun-style Life counter doubles as your setup clear.
It has 6 starting pips and only 4,040 Health, so it can hit early but dies fast once the revive is disabled.
Take the free Centaur card it hands out at the end of Round 4 (and on each revive) if useful, then burst it down with the Keeper of the Flame cheat cleared.
cheatNotes: Every round it casts a chromatic trap (Keeper of the Flame) on your highest-Health wizard, starting +15% and rising 5% per round to a 40% cap.
If Termonarch dies while the Keeper of the Flame cheat is still active, it revives with full Health, so clear the trap cheat before the kill.
At the end of Round 4 and whenever it revives, it gives all wizards a free Centaur (Tier 2b) card.
Casting a Life damage spell that removes traps from wizards, such as Leprechaun (Tier 2b), strips all those traps and ends the Keeper of the Flame cheat, disabling the revive.

name: The Abyss
locations: Novus: La Ville Rose - Hall of Purposeful Abstraction
cheats: "You are tardy!" - If a Wizard joins after the battle has started, The Abyss casts a protected -75% version of Mass Infection.
"The Abyss fears no attack!" - At the start of the first Round:
The Abyss casts Doom and Gloom (-75%)
The Abyss casts a Shadow version of Guardian Spirit. (Unknown Effect)
"The Abyss has a secret for you..." - At the beginning of every 2nd Round, The Abyss will Beguile a Wizard for 2 Rounds:
Round 2: Beguiles Slot 1 [Sun]
Round 4: Beguiles Slot 2 [Eye]
Round 6: Beguiles Slot 3 [Star]
Round 8: Beguiles Slot 4 [Moon]
This cycle repeats, so Sun will be beguiled again on Round 10, Eye on Round 12, Star on Round 14, and Moon on Round 16, and so on.
If a slot that would be targeted on a specific Round is empty, then The Abyss will skip this cheat.
"The Abyss will defend itself" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, The Abyss will use a Shadow Spell that applies a -80% Healing Shield on self
"The Abyss cannot allow you to heal!" - At the beginning of the 4th Round, and every 2nd Round following, The Abyss will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%).
"The Abyss cannot tolerate these Heals..." - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, The Abyss may cast one of these Spells:
Dragonlance
Power Current
Power Stunner
Ominous Scarecrow (Death)
Applies a -20% incoming Healing Aura for 6 Rounds that increases Healing to Damage by 20%.
Aura resembles Virulence in appearance.
strategy: Anti-heal boss: Doom and Gloom (-75%) is up from round 1, your incoming healing sits at -200%, and healing triggers punishment spells — survive on shields and absorbs, not heals, and kill fast.
He's immune to Globals, so don't waste time on global prisms or auras — buff with blades and traps instead.
Every 2nd round he Beguiles a wizard by slot in order (slot 1 round 2, slot 2 round 4, and so on); keep the beguiled wizard from wasting your setup, and note empty slots are skipped.
He shields himself -80% against healing every 3rd round and recasts Doom and Gloom from round 4 on — again, don't build around heals.
Blade and Feint him, then land a big hit before beguiles and the anti-heal grind you down.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get a protected -75% Mass Infection — join on time.
Round 1 he casts Doom and Gloom (-75%), crushing all healing.
Round 1 he also casts a Shadow Guardian Spirit (unknown effect).
Every 2nd round he Beguiles a wizard for two rounds, going slot by slot.
Round 2 he beguiles slot 1.
Round 4 he beguiles slot 2.
Round 6 he beguiles slot 3.
Round 8 he beguiles slot 4, then the cycle repeats.
If the targeted slot is empty he skips the beguile.
Every 3rd round he puts a -80% healing shield on himself.
From round 4 on he recasts Doom and Gloom (-75%) every 2nd round — don't rely on heals.
Cast a heal and he may punish you with one of the spells below.
He also applies a -20% incoming healing aura for 6 rounds — stack shields instead of healing.

name: The Bantam
cheats: "Tsk, tsk, You're late!" - If a Wizard enters combat late, he will cast Infection, and Angel casts Entangle on the Wizard.

"I need a minute. Talk amongst yourselves." - At the beginning of the first round, and intermittently thereafter, The Bantam will use his Natural Attack on a random Wizard. After casting his Natural Attack, Boss will be Pacified for 3 Rounds, and immune to being targeted.
"I'll be taking that, thanks!" - If a Blade is cast on another Wizard, The Bantam will exchange the given blade for a Balanceblade of half the original value.
For Elemental Blade and Spirit Blade, only the first blade of the three (Fire, Life) is exchanged.
"Get ready, Angel" - The Bantam will shout this line at the end of the round in which he'll cast Mass Sacred Charge next Round.
"Angel to the rescue!" - The Bantam will cast Mass Sacred Charge (840).
spellNotes: Casts a Mass Sacred Charge (840) with an Aegis-Protected Absorb.
Angel casts Empower (Life) giving a Power Pip to all allies.
strategy: He takes +5% from Death and resists Life 73%. Hit with Death or a prism.
He periodically uses his Natural Attack, then becomes Pacified and untargetable for 3 rounds. You cannot damage him during that window, so bank blades and hit the moment he is targetable again.
Blades cast on ANOTHER wizard get downgraded to a half-value Balanceblade, so blade the hitter directly (or keep blades on yourself as the solo hitter) rather than passing them.
Watch for his 'Get ready, Angel' shout; the next round the Angel casts Mass Sacred Charge (840 absorb). His Lifeblade and the Angel's Absorb are Aegis-protected.
Blade up during his untargetable window, then unload a Death hit as soon as he can be targeted.
cheatNotes: Late arrival gets Infection plus an Entangle from the Angel. Zone in on time.
He periodically Natural Attacks, then goes Pacified and untargetable for 3 rounds. Bank your setup and hit the instant he is targetable again.
A blade cast on another wizard becomes a half-value Balanceblade. Blade the hitter directly; do not pass blades.
For Elemental and Spirit blades, only the first of the three gets downgraded, a partial workaround.
When he shouts 'Get ready, Angel,' the Angel casts Mass Sacred Charge next round. Expect an 840 team absorb to appear.
That Mass Sacred Charge (840) lands as a team shield. Hit hard enough to punch through it, or strip it first.

name: The Bard (Death)
locations: Krokotopia: Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater,Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater: Act II
cheats: [Random] "My high charms work... they now are in my power." - The Bard will cheat-cast Ship of Fools (Tier 5b).
[Round] Cycle:
"Your revels now are ended!" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, The Bard will cheat-cast Plague.
"This thing of darkness, I acknowledge mine." - At the beginning of every 6th Round, The Bard will cheat-cast Virulence (6 Rounds).
strategy: He stacks damage-reduction on you with Plague every third Round and a 6-Round Virulence every sixth Round, so pack Cleanse Charm or plan your big hit for a Round when the weaknesses are lightest.
Cast your finisher the Round after a cleanse, or use pierce and heavy blades to punch through the stacked weaknesses.
He resists Death 73% and other schools 43% but takes 30% more from Life, so a Life hitter is ideal.
Watch for random Ship of Fools; keep your setup compact and hit before the weakness layers pile up.
cheatNotes: He cheat-casts Ship of Fools on you at random.
At the start of every third Round he cheat-casts Plague, cutting your damage.
At the start of every sixth Round he cheat-casts a 6-Round Virulence, stacking more damage reduction.

name: The Bard (Fire)
locations: Krokotopia: Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater,Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater: Act II
cheats: "March on, let us to pell-mell!" - At the beginning of every third Round, The Bard will cast Bladestorm (+40%).
"My kingdom for a horse!" - The Bard will cast a -65% Tower Shield on himself.
This cheat will trigger multiple times if the enemy takes Damage.
strategy: Hit with Ice (+30% incoming); avoid Fire (57% resist).
He Tower-Shields himself (-65%) every time he takes damage, so chip damage is wasted. Use pierce or Shatter, or set up and land one big hit.
He self-casts a +40% Bladestorm every 3rd round, ramping his damage. Remove the blades or race him.
Stack traps and Feint (he has no ward-stealing cheats) and burst through the 14,460 health in one hit.
cheatNotes: Every 3rd round he self-buffs with a +40% Bladestorm. Strip it or out-pace it.
He puts a -65% Tower Shield on himself, blunting your hit. Pierce or Shatter it.
He re-applies that Tower Shield every time he is damaged, so many small hits get shielded away. Commit to one big attack instead.

name: The Bard (Life)
locations: Krokotopia: Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater,Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater: Act II

stats.resist: 39% to all schools69% to all schools

cheats: [Damage] "Beware the ides... of Bard!" - Beginning on Round 3, each time someone takes Damage, The Bard will cast one of the following:
Mass Spirit Armor
A 2-Pip version of Spirit Armor that applies a 300 Damage Absorb Ward
Spirit Armor
Spirit Armor (500)
A 4-Pip version of Spirit Armor that applies a 600 Damage Absorb Ward
[Round] Cycle:
"Mischief, thou art afoot!" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, The Bard will cheat-cast Gnomes!.
"Wizards die many times..." - At the beginning of every 5th Round, The Bard will cheat-cast Regenerate.
strategy: Team fight at 18,075 Health. Hit Death, +30%, and never Life, which he resists 69%.
Any damage from Round 3 on makes the Bard slap a Spirit Armor absorb (up to 600) on his side. Do not chip. Set up fully and land one hit big enough to blow through the absorb and kill.
Avoid damage-over-time and multi-Round chipping. Each tick just triggers another absorb.
Bring an infection or Doom and Gloom for his Round-5 Regenerate, and expect Gnomes! every third Round.
Funnel blades and Feint to one hitter for a single overwhelming Death hit.
cheatNotes: From Round 3 on, every time anyone takes damage the Bard casts a Spirit Armor absorb on his side, so chip damage just builds him shields.
His biggest version is a 600-damage absorb ward, so hit through it in one big blow rather than tapping.
Every third Round he cheat-casts Gnomes! at the team.
Every fifth Round he Regenerates, so bring an infection or Doom and Gloom to cut the heal.

name: The Bard (Storm)
locations: Krokotopia: Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater,Marketplace of Ideas - Rogue Theater: Act II
cheats: "The gentler gamester is the soonest loser." - The Bard will cast one of the following:
Storm Fuel
Storm Trap (+35%)
[Round] Cycle:
"Once more unto the breach!" - At the beginning of every 3rd Round, The Bard will cast Windstorm.
"Let life be short, else shame will be too long." - At the end of the 12th Round, and every other Round following, The Bard will cast Insane Bolt.
strategy: Hit Myth. He takes +20% Myth and resists 54% Storm and 24% elsewhere, so Myth is your damage school.
Race the clock. From the end of round 12 he casts Insane Bolt every other round, so aim to close the fight before then.
Play around his 3-round Windstorm: every third round he casts it to boost his side's Storm damage. Do not let setup drag through several Windstorms.
Expect him to self-buff with Storm Fuel or a +35% Storm Trap, so shield or heal for a bigger natural attack.
Clear the Malcontent minion, stack Myth blades and Feints on your hitter, and land one big Myth hit inside the first ten rounds.
cheatNotes: He self-buffs with one of two spells.
Every third round he casts Windstorm, boosting his side's Storm damage.
From the end of round 12 and every other round after, he casts Insane Bolt. Close the fight before then.

name: The Bat
cheats: "You need to hit me, Wizard. Hit me!" - He will cheat cast Equalize on Round 1 which limits the Damage cap to one. If the Global is replaced, he will say the line again, adding back his Global.
When hit with a Shadow or Shadow Creature Spell, he will say the following lines while interrupt-casting Donate Shadow (0 Shadow Pip) on himself. Casting Dark Shepherd does not activate his cheat.
First hit. "Good! Ergh... Again!"
Second hit. "That's it! Essence... building..."
Third hit. "Good! Ergh... Again!"
Fourth hit. "One more hit, Wizard! Strike now!"
Fifth hit. "That's it! Enough Wizard - Enough."
He will automatically surrender the duel after he obtains his fifth Shadow Pip.
strategy: You cannot out-damage him. His Round 1 Equalize caps all damage at 1, so the win condition is to hit him with five Shadow (or Shadow-creature) spells.
Pack Shadow attack spells. Each Shadow hit makes him Donate Shadow to himself, gaining a shadow pip. Note Dark Shepherd does NOT count.
Land five qualifying Shadow hits, after his fifth shadow pip he automatically surrenders the duel.
Do not bother replacing his Equalize global, he just re-casts it.
Since damage is capped at 1, ignore blades and burst, just chain your five Shadow spells as fast as your pips allow.
cheatNotes: His Round 1 Equalize caps all damage at 1, so you cannot kill him normally, and he re-casts it if you replace the global.
Every Shadow or Shadow-creature spell you hit him with gives him a shadow pip. Dark Shepherd does not count.
At his fifth shadow pip he surrenders the duel, that is the win. Just land five Shadow hits.

name: The Bundozer
locations: Karamelle: Nibbleheim Mines - Mining Tunnel A
cheats: "Oh, It does not like the surprise crowds!"'- If a Wizard joins the battle after two Rounds (or flees and returns), the Bundozer will cast its Natural Attack on the Wizard, followed by a Sun version of Mass Supernova that removes 33%, 66%, or 99% max Health from all Wizards.
"No, don't do that! It eats those!"'- If a Wizard casts a positive Charm or a Global Spell, The Bundozer will cast Disarm (Sun) to remove one Charm.
Single-target Blades cast on another Wizard, only result in the receiver being disarmed, while multi-target Blades result in the caster being disarmed.

If this cheat is triggered, The Bundozer will use an invisible attack which has a chance to instantly defeat the Wizard (may be triggered when The Bundozer has a charm that is transferred to the Wizard - needs further confirmation).
"Oh, it is gurgling in preparation. Watch out!"- At the end of Round three, and every third Round after, Hans will say this as a warning for the next Round.
"Yes, do that!" - Dealing Damage to The Bundozer the Round after the warning is given will cause Hans to say this line, and prevent The Bundozer's Natural Attack cheat from occurring.
"That will not work!"- Otherwise, at the end of the Round, The Bundozer will cast its Natural Attack and then will cast a Sun version of Mass Supernova.
The first time this cheat triggers, the Sun version of Mass Supernova will deal 33% of every Wizard's Health.
The second time this cheat triggers, the Sun version of Mass Supernova will deal 66% of every Wizard's Health.
The third time this cheat triggers, the Sun version of Mass Supernova will deal 99% of every Wizard's Health.
Even when casting its Natural Attack without a cheat, the Bundozer casts a Sun version of Mass Supernova that deals 33% of all Wizards' Health.
Harmful Charms and Helpful Wards will not reduce the amount of Health taken.
strategy: Do not cast positive charms or globals. Each one makes Bundozer Disarm you, and that can trigger an invisible attack with a chance to instantly defeat you. Set up with traps like Feint instead of blades.

Heed the warning ('gurgling in preparation') at the end of Round 3 and every third round after: you must deal damage to Bundozer the following round, or it hits with its Natural Attack plus a Mass Supernova removing 33%, then 66%, then 99% of everyone's health. Keep a hit ready each cycle.
Shields and weaknesses will not reduce the Supernova percentage, so triggering the 'Yes, do that!' line by hitting it in time is the only defense.
Hit with Fire, Storm, or Death (+5%, resists Ice, Myth, and Life 70%); trap-stack and burst.
Everyone join within two rounds; latecomers eat the natural attack plus Mass Supernova.
cheatNotes: Joining late (or fleeing and returning) triggers its Natural Attack plus a Mass Supernova removing up to 99% of everyone's health, so join within two rounds and do not flee.
Any positive charm or global makes it Disarm you, so set up with traps, not blades.
Single blades on another wizard disarm the recipient; multi-target blades disarm the caster.
That disarm can come with an invisible attack that may instantly defeat a wizard, so avoid charms entirely.
This warning at end of Round 3 and every third round means you must damage Bundozer next round.
Damaging it the round after the warning cancels the Natural Attack and Mass Supernova, so always keep a hit ready.
If you do not hit it in time, it Natural Attacks and Mass Supernovas the team.
First trigger removes 33% of everyone's health.
Second trigger removes 66%.
Third trigger removes 99%, so you cannot survive stalling; hit every cycle.
Even on a normal turn its Natural Attack comes with a 33% Mass Supernova, so stay healed.
Shields and weaknesses do not reduce the percentage-based Supernova, so hitting it on time is the only defense.

name: The Devourer
locations: Wizard City: The Catacombs - Edge of the Spiral
cheats: If a Wizard joins the battle late, The Devourer will "devour" the Wizard.
At the start of the first Round, he casts a Sun Spell to summon any of the following Minions:
Acrid Bile - Fire
Pestilient Bile - Ice
Seething Bile - Storm
Noxious Bile - Myth
Poison Bile - Life
Toxic Bile - Death
Hazardous Bile - Balance
After the first Round, and again after the fifth, he will cast a Natural Attack that "devours" a random Wizard. This cycle will stop after the second Wizard is "devoured".
The Natural Attack removes all unprotected Wards, Charms, and Auras from the Wizard.

After being devoured, the Wizard will lose their combat interface and will be forced to pass for the duration of the idle timer. The Wizard is not able to be targeted and gains no Pips during this state.
During the third Round after his turn, he will cast an AoE Shadow Natural Attack for 1,500 Shadow Damage, and return the devoured Wizard to combat.
Any Spells or cheats cast by him after three Rounds will trigger this cheat.
He will then summon one of the above Minions.
If a Wizard has a Blade present for three Rounds (including the Round it was cast), The Devourer will cast an AoE version of Steal Charm (Shadow) that steals one positive Charm.
For example, on Round 1, a Wizard places a Stormblade on himself. At the beginning of Round 3, The Devourer will cast his Spell to steal one Charm from everyone.
"<SHRIEK!>" - If a Feint is placed on him, The Devourer will cast Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2) on himself which removes the last two Traps placed.
This can be avoided by using Indemnity.
At the end of every fourth Round, The Devourer will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward, which will remove all Traps from himself.
For each Trap he removes, he will add a protected Balanceblade (+100%) to his Minions or himself.
Indemnity-Protected Traps will result in two Blades being added.
spellNotes: Casts a Chimera (Shadow) dealing 375 Myth, Life, and Death Damage to all enemies.
strategy: Run this as a 4-wizard team: two supports (a Death wizard is a must, for Bad Juju to cut his 200% outgoing damage) and two hitters, one to clear the Bile minions and one to burst the Devourer.
He resists 70% to almost every school and only takes a small boost from Life and Balance, so build your hits around Life/Balance or prism into them.

Protect every blade: if a blade sits on you for three rounds he casts an AoE Steal Charm that takes a charm from all wizards. Use Aegis, Elemental/Spirit Blades, or Precision and hit before that timer.

Keep only one Feint at a time and shield it with Indemnity cast right before the hitter attacks, or his SHRIEK cheat cleanses your traps and each removed trap becomes a protected +100% Balanceblade for him.
Kill the Bile minions fast, prioritizing the Myth Bile (Noxious) for its Earthquake, so the boss is left standing alone for the burst.

Plan around the devour: after Round 1 and Round 5 he devours a random wizard, then spits them back for heavy Shadow AoE (up to 4,500) three rounds later. Arrange things so your main hitter is not the one locked out during the burst window.
Go first in turn order so your protected blades and Indemnity resolve before his cheats do.
cheatNotes: Join before the fight begins. If you enter late he instantly devours you, removing you from combat entirely.
He opens Round 1 by summoning one Bile minion; each Bile is tied to a school (Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, or Balance).
After Round 1 and again after Round 5, a Natural Attack devours a random wizard; the cycle stops once two wizards have been devoured.
That devour attack strips all unprotected wards, charms, and auras from its target, so Aegis-protect key blades before it can land.
A devoured wizard is fully locked out: no interface, forced to pass, cannot be targeted, and gains no pips until returned.
Three rounds after devouring, he hits the whole team for 1,500 Shadow and returns the devoured wizard to the fight.
Any spell or cheat he casts past that three-round mark triggers the spit-back-and-damage, so expect the AoE roughly on schedule.
Along with returning the wizard, he summons another Bile minion.
Leave a blade on yourself for three full rounds and he casts an AoE Steal Charm that takes one positive charm from everyone. Hit within two rounds of blading, or protect the blade.
Example: a Stormblade cast on Round 1 gets stolen from the whole team at the start of Round 3.
SHRIEK: placing a Feint on him triggers a Shadow Cleanse Ward (-2) that removes your last two traps.
Protect Feints with Indemnity to stop the SHRIEK cleanse from removing them.
At the end of every fourth round he Cleanse Wards himself, wiping all traps, so land your hit before that window.
Every trap he removes this way becomes a protected +100% Balanceblade on him or his minions, so don't over-stack traps you can't cash in first.
An Indemnity-protected trap he removes gives him two Balanceblades instead of one.

name: The Emperor Card
locations: Darkmoor: Outsiders Camp - Zone of Fortune

stats.resist: 33% to all schools63% to all schools

cheats: [Protected Ward] "Embrace Your Truth, Use It." - At the beginning of the First Round, the Emperor Card will cast three Protected -100% Universal Damage Wards, and one Protected 5,000 Damage Absorb Ward on itself, then give each Wizard a copy of Shatter (The Emperor Card).
[Shatter (The Emperor Card)]
This spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat The Emperor Card.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+30%); it resists Myth 63%.
On Round 1 it protects itself with three -100% Universal Shields plus a 5,000 Damage Absorb, and hands each Wizard a Shatter (The Emperor Card).

Break through the wall: cast the granted Shatter to strip the shields, or throw cheap hits to burn the -100% shields and the Absorb before your real attack. The Shatter is optional but makes it far faster.
Once the protected wards and Absorb are cleared, stack blades and land one big Storm hit.
cheatNotes: Round 1 it self-casts three protected -100% Universal Shields and a protected 5,000 Absorb, then gives everyone a Shatter card to break them.
You can win without casting the granted Shatter, but it clears the shield wall much faster.

name: The Gray Man (Challenge)
cheats: [Manipulation] "You cannot kill the undying" - At the beginning of the first round, The Gray Man will receive a Guardian Spirit effect

[Round] "Magic is mine to wield, your power strengthens me!" - At the end of each round, if there is at least one wizard with a Positive Charm, The Gray Man will target a random wizard and receive a number of Power Pips depending on the amount of Positive Charms.
1 charm = 1 Power Pip
2 charm = 2 Power Pip
3 charms = 3 Power Pips
4+ charms = 4 Power Pips
[Health] "Pain is my strength" - At the beginning of each round, if The Gray Man is not at full health, he will cast a special version of Bladestorm that increases in value the lower his health is.
The minimum blade value is 20% and the maximum blade value is 100%. If the blade is 100%, it will also be Protected.
Further testing is required to determine blade values between the minimum and maximum and their corresponding HP thresholds.
strategy: He starts with a Guardian Spirit, so he revives once at full health when first defeated. Plan for two kills, or remove the Guardian Spirit before finishing him.
Positive Charms feed him: at the end of each round he pulls Power Pips based on how many positive Charms are on the team (up to 4), so keep blades off until the round you actually hit.
As his health drops he self-Bladestorms for more (up to a protected +100% at low health), so do not slow-chip him; burst him down in one big hit rather than trading rounds.
Team fight (15,625 health plus two Demimortal minions): supports Feint and blade the hitter in the same round, hitter clears boss and minions with one AoE. He takes +Life and resists Death.
cheatNotes: At round 1 he gains a Guardian Spirit, so his first defeat revives him at full health.
At the end of each round, if any wizard has a positive Charm, he takes Power Pips based on the charm count.
At the start of each round while below full health, he self-casts a Bladestorm that scales up as his health drops.
The blade ranges from +20% at high health to a protected +100% at low health.

name: The Gray Man (Standard)

stats.resist: 49% to all schools79% to all schools

cheats: [Manipulation] "You cannot kill the undying" - At the beginning of the first Round, The Gray Man will cast a Death version of Guardian Spirit that revives himself with 15% Health.

[Round] "Magic is mine to wield, your power strengthens me!" - At the end of each Round, if there is at least one wizard with a helpful Charm, The Gray Man will target a random Wizard and receive a number of Power Pips depending on the amount of helpful Charms.
1 Charm = 1 Power Pip
2 Charms = 2 Power Pip
3 Charms = 3 Power Pips
4+ Charms = 4 Power Pips
[Health] "Pain is my strength" - At the beginning of each Round, if The Gray Man is not at full Health, he will cast a special version of Bladestorm that increases in value the lower his Health is.
The minimum Blade value is +20% and the maximum Blade value is +100%. If the Blade is +100%, it will also be protected.
Further testing is required to determine Blade values between the minimum and maximum and their corresponding Health thresholds.
strategy: He drains your charms: at the end of each round every helpful charm your team holds feeds him up to 4 power pips. Do not sit on blades - blade the same round you hit, and lean on traps/Feint for setup.
The lower his health, the bigger the Bladestorm he self-casts each round (up to a protected +100%). Do not leave him lingering at low health; burst him from healthy to dead in one big window.
Round 1 he casts Guardian Spirit, so his first death revives him at 15% health. Plan a follow-up hit to finish the revive.
Hit with Life - he takes 30% more from Life and resists Death 79%. Deal with the Demimortal Gargoyle and Vampire minions too.
As a team: keep charms off the board until the hit turn, supports use Feint/traps, then two hits - one to consume the revive, one to finish.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he casts Guardian Spirit on himself, so his first death revives him at 15% health. Have a second hit ready.
At the end of every round, if any wizard holds a helpful charm, he steals power pips from a random wizard - more charms, more pips.
1 helpful charm on the team = he gains 1 power pip.
2 charms = 2 power pips.
3 charms = 3 power pips.
4 or more charms = 4 power pips. Keep charms off the board except the turn you hit.
Every round he is below full health he self-casts a Bladestorm that grows as his health drops. Do not leave him at low HP.
The self-blade ranges from +20% up to +100%, and at +100% it is protected. Burst him down fast so he never reaches the big blades.

name: The Headmaster
cheats: "Tardy, Tardy!" - If a Wizard is late, The Headmaster will cast a Star version of Insane Bolt taking 75% of the target's max Health.

Interrupt Cycles The Headmaster uses two different cheat cycles: a "Primary Interrupt Cycle" and a "Secondary Interrupt Cycle." The primary cycle occurs only once, and the secondary cycle can occur at any point after the first ends.
Primary Interrupt Cycle:
During this cycle, the Headmaster will not attack the Wizard team.
At the start of the duel, the following cheats occur:
Boss will interrupt-cast a Star Spell giving him -999% Shields for each of the seven Schools of Magic.
If a Wizard joins the duel after the health bars show after someone has joined, this cheat triggers twice on the first Round.
Casting a Damage spell on his Shields will result in him casting a single -999% Shield for the resulting School Shield that was broken.
Pierce and Homework can be used to remove the Shields.
The single Balance Shield has the appearance of a Tower Shield.
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard casts Shatter, Boss will recast a Star Spell giving him -999% Shields for each of the seven Schools of Magic.
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard casts Steal Ward, Boss will cast Steal Ward (Star, -1) to steal it back.
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard casts Double Steal Ward, Boss will cast a Star version of Double Steal Ward to steal them back.
If a Wizard casts Empower, Boss will cast a Star Spell giving him -999% Death Shield.
An invisible Polymorph will be cast on all Wizards giving them different Spells for the cheats that follow.
All Wizards' Health will be set at a fraction of 10,000, proportionate to the Health they had entering the duel.
Wizards will not be able to use their own Spells until the end of this cycle.
At the end of this cycle, the invisible Polymorph will end, and each Wizard's Health will be set back to their own, proportionate to the Health they had out of 10,000.
This cycle only occurs once at the beginning of the duel.
Treasure Cards that have been drawn during the Polymorph Stages cannot be discarded.
"Roll Call! How Many Students in Attendance?" - Each Wizard must select the total number of Wizards in the duel.
Polymorph Spells given during this stage include. Cards are in a randomized order.
Attendance (1), Attendance (2), Attendance (3) & Attendance (4)
Draw will give Fake Attendance
"You Disappoint Me" - If a Wizard passes, Boss will cast a Star version of Crow removing 10% max Health.
"Wrong!" - If a Wizard chooses incorrectly, Boss will cast a Star version of Crow removing 10% max Health.
"No Cheating!" - If a Wizard casts Fake Attendance, Boss casts a Star version of Immolate removing 35% max Health.
"Hand In Your Homework!" - This turn can be used for Shield breaking.
Polymorph Spells given during this stage include.
Homework (Balance), Homework (Death), Homework (Fire), Homework (Ice), Homework (Life), Homework (Myth) & Homework (Storm)
Discarding and using Draw spell will give Copy.
"Gold Star" - Boss will say this if a Wizard casts any Spell at him.
"No Cheating!" - If a Wizard uses Copy, Boss casts a Star version of Frostbite removing 15% max Health.
"You Disappoint Me" - If the second Wizard doesn't turn in Homework he'll cast a Star version of Insane Bolt removing 20% max Health on the Wizard. This includes if they casted Copy.
"The Lecture is About to Begin" - Each Wizard may select the buff/debuff combination they wish to use on themselves.
Listen, Participate, Take Notes, Snack Break & Ask Questions
Draw will give Doze Off.
Casting Snack Break will cause the Wizard to fail the next Pop Quiz.
A Pet-cast Conviction before this turn may help the Wizard avoid the Stun.
"Hey, Wake Up!" - If a Wizard passes, Boss will cast a Star version of Cyclops removing 30% max Health.
"Cheaters never prosper!"' - If the Wizard casts Doze Off, Boss will cast a Star version of Leprechaun removing 25% max Health, and a Star version of Cyclops removing 30% Health from the Wizard.
If two Wizards both cast Doze Off, this cheat doesn't trigger.
"Pop Quiz! HA HA HA!" - Boss will cast a School Aura on each Wizard and ask which of the School Spells is opposite.
"Name the opposing school"
"Death!!!" "Fire!!!" "Ice!!!" "Life!!!" "Myth!!!" "Storm!!!" - are the Aura Effects that appear when the Aura is cast on a Wizard.
Death School, Fire School, Ice School, Life School, Myth School & Storm School
Draw will give Cheat
"Let's See How You Did..." - Boss will cast a Star version of Supernova removing 40% max Health on the Wizards who fail to answer correctly.
"Correct! Surprising..." - If a Wizard answers correctly, the Aura will be dispelled without Damage.
"Hmm...Correct!"' - Boss will use a version of Supernova removing the Aura without Damage.
"Cheaters never prosper!"' - If a Wizard casts Cheat, Boss will cast Impede on the Wizard.
Secondary Interrupt Cycle: "Hand In Your Homework!"
Occasionally, Boss will interrupt with two-Round Homework cycles from the Second Polymorph Stage.
During this cycle, the Headmaster has normal cards, and will attack the Wizard team.
Boss will cast an invisible Polymorph that ends at the end of the second Round.
These turns can be used for further Shield breaking.
Shadow Creature and Shadow Self spells will be removed when the Polymorph is placed on the Wizards, causing the Backlash to deal 4,000 Damage.
spellNotes: Casts an AoE version of Hex.
Deals 420 Star Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Never join late. Latecomers are hit by a Star Insane Bolt for 75% of max health. Enter together before the health bars appear so the shield and bolt cheats do not double up.

Survive the one-time Primary quiz cycle. He polymorphs the whole team, scales your health to a share of 10,000, and locks you out of your own spells - you can only play the quiz cards he hands you. He does not attack during this cycle as long as you answer correctly.

Roll Call (attendance): every wizard plays the Attendance card equal to the party size (all four play Attendance (4) in a full team). A wrong count or a pass is a Star Crow for 10% max health; playing Fake Attendance is a Star Immolate for 35%.

Homework: play your own school's Homework card to turn it in - this both answers the round and breaks his -999% shields. Using Copy costs 15% max health, and the second-position wizard skipping homework costs 20%.

Lecture: pick a study buff like Participate or Take Notes. Avoid Snack Break (auto-fails your next quiz) and Doze Off (Leprechaun + Cyclops for 55% total). A pet-cast Conviction beforehand helps dodge the stun.

Pop Quiz: he puts a school Aura on you and you play the OPPOSING school card (Death aura -> Life, Fire -> Ice, Storm -> Myth, and so on). A right answer removes the aura free; a wrong one is a Star Supernova for 40% max health.

Break his shields and burst. He self-shields -999% for all seven schools, so clear them with Pierce and the Homework turns. Once the cycle ends you get your spells back - hit for at least 10,000 damage together. He resists Ice/Storm/Death 70% and heals at 45%, so attack with Fire/Myth/Life or a huge Balance hit (75% boost).

Watch the Secondary Homework cycle: mid-fight he can interrupt for two rounds with normal cards and attacks. Use those turns to break more shields, but strip any Shadow Creature/Shadow Self first - the polymorph removes them for 4,000 backlash.
cheatNotes: Any late wizard is interrupt-hit by a Star Insane Bolt for 75% of current max health, so everyone must be in before combat starts.
He runs a one-time Primary quiz cycle at the start (no attacks) and can later drop shorter Secondary Homework cycles where he does attack.
During the primary cycle he will not attack the team at all, so the only danger is answering the quizzes wrong.
He self-casts -999% shields for every one of the seven schools, so ordinary attacks do nothing until you strip them.
If someone joins after the health bars appear, the seven-school shield cheat fires twice on the first round.
Hitting a shield with a damage spell just makes him re-shield that one broken school, so do not try to burn shields off with attacks.
Use Pierce and the Homework turns, not damage, to actually remove his shields.
His single Balance shield shows up looking like a Tower Shield.
Casting Shatter makes him recast all seven -999% shields, so leave Shatter out of the deck.
Casting Steal Ward fails - he immediately casts a Star Steal Ward to take the shield back.
Casting Double Steal Ward is also countered by a Star version that steals both back.
Casting Empower gives him an extra -999% Death shield, so avoid Empower.
He casts an invisible polymorph on the whole team, replacing your deck with quiz cards.
Your health is temporarily scaled to a fraction of 10,000 during the cycle, proportional to what you entered with.
You cannot cast your own spells until the primary cycle ends - only the quiz cards work.
When the cycle ends the polymorph drops and your real health is restored proportionally.
This full primary cycle happens only once, at the very start of the duel.
Any Treasure Cards you draw while polymorphed are stuck and cannot be discarded.
For Roll Call, each wizard selects the total number of wizards in the duel by playing the matching Attendance card.
Using Draw during Roll Call hands you Fake Attendance - do not play it.
Passing during Roll Call costs 10% max health to a Star Crow.
Choosing the wrong attendance count also costs 10% max health to a Star Crow.
Playing Fake Attendance is punished with a Star Immolate for 35% max health.
The Homework turn is where you turn in homework and break his shields.
Using Draw during Homework gives you a Copy card, which is itself punished.
He says 'Gold Star' as flavor whenever you cast a spell directly at him.
Using the Copy card costs 15% max health to a Star Frostbite.
The second-position wizard loses 20% max health to a Star Insane Bolt if they fail to turn in homework, including if they cast Copy.
On the Lecture turn each wizard picks a self buff/debuff study card.
Using Draw during the Lecture gives you Doze Off - do not play it.
Snack Break makes you automatically fail the next Pop Quiz, so skip it.
A pet-cast Conviction placed before this turn can block the lecture stun.
Passing the Lecture costs 30% max health to a Star Cyclops.
Casting Doze Off is punished twice - a Star Leprechaun (25%) plus a Star Cyclops (30%) for 55% total.
If two wizards both cast Doze Off, the punishment does not trigger.
For the Pop Quiz he puts a school Aura on you and asks for its opposite - answer with the opposing school card.
Using Draw during the Pop Quiz gives you a Cheat card, which he punishes.
A wrong quiz answer costs 40% max health to a Star Supernova.
A correct answer dispels the aura with no damage.
He removes the aura harmlessly with a Supernova when you answer correctly.
Playing the Cheat card gets you an Impede debuff.
Later in the fight he can interrupt with short two-round Homework cycles reusing the second polymorph stage.
These secondary cycles run the Homework stage for two rounds.
Unlike the primary cycle, he keeps his normal deck and attacks the team during the secondary cycle.
The invisible polymorph in this cycle ends at the close of the second round.
Use these secondary-cycle turns to break more of his shields.
Shadow Creature and Shadow Self charms are stripped when the polymorph lands, causing 4,000 backlash damage, so do not hold them into this cycle.

name: The Hermit Card
locations: Darkmoor: Outsiders Camp - Zone of Fortune

stats.resist: 33% to all schools63% to all schools

cheats: [Protected Curse] "Embrace Your Truth, Use It." - The Hermit Card will cast a number of Protected -100% Damage Negative Charms on each Wizard equal to the number of wizards in the battle, then give each Wizard a copy of Purge (The Hermit Card).
[Purge (The Hermit Card)]
This spell is not required to be cast in order to defeat The Hermit Card.
strategy: Purge the weaknesses first. It stacks protected -100% damage weaknesses on each wizard (one per wizard present), which zero out your hits until you cast the Purge it hands you.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (it takes +30% each); never Balance (63% resist) and expect 33% resist on the other schools.
Purge off the weaknesses, then blade and hit as normal.
At 16,820 Health with no minions and one simple cheat, this is soloable once the weaknesses are cleared.
cheatNotes: It puts protected -100% damage weaknesses on each wizard, as many as there are wizards, and hands each of you a Purge to remove them. Your hits do nothing until you Purge them off.
You are not required to cast the Purge to win, but you must clear the weaknesses to deal damage.

name: The Hierophant
locations: Darkmoor: Black Lagoon - Scholomari Shrine Window
cheats: [Charm] "No child dares disobey the Scholomari." - Whenever The Hierophant casts a helpful Charm or Global, her Minions will apply a +35% Damage Charm of the same School on The Hierophant.
[Damage] "My turn is when I say it is." - The Hierophant will cast Chain Lightning on a random Wizard.

[Spell] "Wizard, take this!" "I don't think so!" "Now we're taking you to school!" "No more! No more of you!" - Velma von Venkman will say one of these lines, and give a copy of one of her Spells to a random Wizard.
[Chain Lightning (Battle Card)][Velma's Blade][Velma's Shield][Velma's Trap][Velma's Weakness]
spellNotes: Casts a version of Iceblade (+40%) that applies two Blades instead of one.
strategy: This is a 20,220-health Darkmoor boss with minions, so run it as a team: a hitter, supports who blade and Feint, and a healer. She is Ice and takes boosted Fire damage, so hit with Fire.
Pressure her minions (Velma). Whenever the Hierophant casts a helpful Charm or Global, her minions pile a +35% same-school damage charm on her, so she snowballs blades fast if you leave them up.
Expect chip damage: she Chain-Lightnings a random wizard and Velma hands out copies of her spells.
She casts a +40% Iceblade that applies two blades at once, so her damage builds quickly; shield the team and keep the fight moving.
Stack blades and Feints, then land one big Fire AoE that clears Velma and the Hierophant together.
cheatNotes: Whenever the Hierophant casts a helpful Charm or Global, her minions pile a +35% same-school damage charm on her. Pressure the minions or she snowballs blades fast.
She casts Chain Lightning on a random wizard for chip damage.
Her minion Velma hands a random wizard a copy of one of her spells.

name: The Hierophant of Destruction (Challenge)
locations: Darkmoor: Black Lagoon - Bog of Vengeance (Challenge)
cheats: [Round] "No child dares disobey the Scholomari" - Hierophant of Destruction will say this at the beginning of the first round and after she casts a Blade, Trap, Aura, or Global with no effect.
[Random] "My turn is when I say it is." - Randomly, Hierophant of Destruction will interrupt and cheat-cast Chain Lightning.
[Round] Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle begins at the end of the first round and repeats from the beginning starting on the next turn after the cycle ends.
"You will cast what I tell you!" - Hierophant of Destruction will interrupt and force a random wizard to cast one of the following spells:
[Elemental] [Weakness] A special 15% version of Elemental Weakness
[Deferred Damage] A triple Bomb DoT that deals 50 Fire damage after 5 rounds.
[Heal over Time] A triple HoT that heals 50 health over 5 rounds.
[Spirit][Blade] A special 15% version of Spirit Blade

"The willful student hurts themselves" - At the end of the round two rounds after forcing a wizard to cast a spell (e.g. round 3 for the first cycle), Hierophant of Destruction casts a special Ice (if the hanging effect was blades or weaknesses) or Storm (if the hanging effect was DoTs or HoTs) damage spell that clears the hanging effects that wizards were forced to cast and deals 10% of the wizard's max health per hanging effect cleared.
strategy: Team fight (20,220 health). Hit with Fire (she resists Ice and takes boosted Fire); use a Fire prism if you are an Ice wizard.
She forces random wizards to cast hanging effects (a weakness, a Spirit Blade, a Bomb DoT, or a HoT), then two rounds later hits them for 10% max health per forced effect still on them.
Clear the forced hanging effects before that willful-student hit lands: cleanse the weakness or blade, or triage the DoT or HoT.
Expect random interrupt Chain Lightnings, so keep resist up all fight.
Blade a single Fire hitter and burst between the forced-cast cycles.
cheatNotes: Flavor line she says at the start and after casting an empty blade, trap, aura, or global.
She randomly interrupts with a free Chain Lightning, so keep resist up.
The forced-cast cycle starts at the end of round 1 and repeats after it finishes.
She interrupts and forces a random wizard to cast one of the four hanging effects listed below.
Two rounds after forcing you, she hits for 10% max health per forced hanging effect still on you (an Ice hit for blades or weaknesses, a Storm hit for DoTs or HoTs). Clear those effects first.

name: The Hierophant of Destruction (Standard)
locations: Darkmoor: Black Lagoon - Bog of Vengeance (Standard)
cheats: "No child dares disobey the Scholomari" - The Hierophant of Destruction will say this at the beginning of the first Round with no effect. Whenever The Hierophant of Destruction casts a buff, her Minions will cheat-cast similar buffs of the same type.
[Global] = Stormblade
[Aura] = Magnify, Sleet Storm, or Empowerment
[Trap] = Ice Trap or Storm Trap
[Blade] = Balanceblade, Iceblade (+45%), or Stormblade
[Random] "My turn is when I say it is." - Randomly, The Hierophant of Destruction will interrupt and cheat-cast Chain Lightning.
[Round] Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle begins on the first Round and repeats from the beginning starting on the next Round after the cycle ends.
"You will cast what I tell you!" - The Hierophant of Destruction will force a random Wizard to cast one of the following spells:
[Elemental][Weakness] A Balance Spell that is a -15% version of Elemental Weakness
[Deferred Damage] A Fire Spell that applies three Damage over Time effects that each deal 50 Fire Damage after 5 Rounds
[Heal over Time] A Life Spell that applies three Heal over Time effects that each restore 50 Health over 5 Rounds
[Spirit][Blade] A Balance Spell that is a +15% version of Spirit Blade

[Round] "The willful student hurts themselves" - At the end of the Round two Rounds after forcing a Wizard to cast a Spell (e.g. Round 3 for the first cycle), Hierophant of Destruction casts an Ice Spell (if the hanging effect was Blades or Weaknesses) or a Storm Spell (if the hanging effect was Damage over Times or Heal over Times) that removes the hanging effects that Wizards were forced to cast and deals 10% of the Wizard's max Health per hanging effect cleared.
strategy: Expect her minions to mirror her. Whenever she buffs herself, her minions cheat-cast the same type (Global to Stormblade, aura, trap, or blade), so kill or blade past the shared buffs on one big hit.
Bring Fire. She is Ice and takes 30% boosted Fire while resisting Ice 75%, Storm 63%, and most schools 45%, so a Fire hitter or Fire prisms is the clear pick.

Play around the forced-cast cycle. She forces a random wizard to cast a hanging effect (Elemental Weakness, deferred Fire DoTs, Life HoTs, or Spirit Blade), then two rounds later removes those effects and deals 10% of that wizard's max Health per effect cleared.
Do not pile extra hanging effects on the forced wizard. The punishment scales per hanging effect cleared, so minimize what stacks on them before the removal round.
Watch her random Chain Lightning interrupts and the minion buff mirror when timing your setup; shield the forced wizard ahead of the removal round.
This is a team fight: 20,220 Health. Funnel blades to one Fire hitter, have supports Feint her, keep a healer on the forced-cast target, and burst her down in one window.
cheatNotes: Whenever she casts a buff, her minions cheat-cast a matching buff of the same type, so her side stacks buffs quickly.
A Global she casts is mirrored by the minions as Stormblade.
An aura she casts is mirrored as Magnify, Sleet Storm, or Empowerment.
A trap she casts is mirrored as Ice Trap or Storm Trap.
A blade she casts is mirrored as Balanceblade, Iceblade (+45%), or Stormblade.
She randomly interrupts to cheat-cast Chain Lightning, so expect surprise Storm damage.
She forces a random wizard to cast one of four hanging effects, setting up the delayed punishment below.
The forced effect may be a -15% Elemental Weakness.
The forced effect may be three Fire DoTs that each deal 50 after 5 rounds.
The forced effect may be three Life HoTs that each restore 50 over 5 rounds.
The forced effect may be a +15% Spirit Blade.
Two rounds after forcing the cast, she removes those hanging effects and deals 10% of the forced wizard's max Health per effect cleared, so keep extra effects off that wizard and shield them.

name: The Mad Chronomonger
cheats: "Start the clock!" - Start of the cheat cycle.
"Reset the clock" - The Mad Chronomonger will reset the cheat cycle when hit.

"Tick, Tock" - Every Round The Mad Chronomonger is not hit, a Mass Feint (+70%/+30%) is placed on all Wizards. Hits must come from Spells cast each Round, subsequent ticks of a Damage Over Time won't count.
"The clock punches you" - The Round after the Tick Tock cheat, The Mad Chronomonger will use a version of Mockenspiel that hits all Wizards.
strategy: Balance boss (13135). Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+5%); it resists 45% across most schools, so over-stack blades and traps.

The core rule: land a real hit on him EVERY round. Any round he is not hit, he Mass Feints your whole team (+70%/+30% traps on you) and then Mockenspiels all wizards the next round, turning his own attack into big damage through those traps.
DoT ticks do not count as a hit; you need a fresh damaging spell each round. Keep a cheap attack ready every turn to reset his clock while you build.
Stack blades and traps between forced hits, then land your big Myth, Life, or Death finisher.
cheatNotes: Hitting him resets his cheat cycle. Land a real damaging spell every round to keep it reset.
Any round you do not hit him, he Mass Feints your whole team (+70%/+30%), turning his next attack into a heavy hit on you. Never skip a hitting turn, and DoT ticks do not count.
The round after that Mass Feint, he Mockenspiels all wizards through those traps for big damage. Keep the clock reset with a hit every round.

name: The Malughast (Malughast Nest)
cheats: "Roar!" - At the beginning of the battle, The Malughast will cast a Shadow Spell that gives each Wizard three copies of Malughast's Feint.
[Malughast's Feint]
If a Wizard joins the battle late, they will receive the three cards on the next round.
"Rooaarr!!" - If a Feint is cast, The Malughast will cast a Shadow version of Cleanse Ward (-2) on himself twice, making Indemnity ineffective in protecting the removal.
Mass Feint will trigger this cheat.
Malughast's Feint will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: He hands each wizard three Malughast's Feint cards at the start; these are your setup tool, and the only Feints that do not trigger his cleanse.
Never use a regular Feint or Mass Feint on him: either makes him Cleanse Ward himself twice, which bypasses Indemnity and strips your trap. Use the provided Malughast's Feint instead.
He resists Balance 68% and has no boost school, so attack with your strongest non-Balance school.
Stack blades plus the Malughast's Feint, then land one big hit.
Watch his +120% damage and protected Curse, and shield his hits while you set up.
cheatNotes: He gives each wizard three Malughast's Feint cards at the start; use these to set up, since they do not trigger his cleanse.
Late arrivals get the three cards on the following round.
A regular Feint on him makes him Cleanse Ward twice, stripping the trap and beating Indemnity. Only use his Malughast's Feint.
Mass Feint triggers the double-cleanse too, so avoid it.
Malughast's Feint is the exception that will not trigger the cleanse, so it is your safe setup.

name: The Malughast (Solomon Crane's House)
locations: Lemuria: Night Forest - Solomon Crane's House
cheats: [Round] "Roar!" - At the beginning of the battle, The Malughast will cast a Shadow Spell that gives each Wizard three copies of Malughast's Feint.
[Malughast's Feint]

strategy: Use the three Malughast's Feints he gives you rather than your own Feints; a normal Feint just makes him strip it with a double Cleanse Ward that Indemnity cannot protect.

Stack Malughast's Feints on him, blade your hitter, and land one big hit into the piled traps.
He resists Balance 68% and has strong outgoing and incoming healing, so cut his healing where you can and pack Cleanse or Triage if his Curse and Sacrifice pressure you.
cheatNotes: At the start he gives every wizard three copies of 'Malughast's Feint'; these are the traps you actually want to use.

name: The Monster (Challenge)
locations: Marleybone: Barkingham Palace - Ransacked Throne Room (Challenge)
cheats: [Minion] "I just... I just... want my father back." - At the beginning of the first Round, The Monster will make himself untargetable.
If a Wizard targeted The Monster with a Spell this Round, they will be forced to Pass. Spells that do not target The Monster will still cast.
If no Notes, or Note 1, have been selected, The Monster will summon a Memory of Katzenstein (Balance, Challenge).
The Monster will become targetable again starting on the third Round.

[Afterlife] "Father's memory drives me. I will not fall!" - If The Monster is defeated before all of his Minions have been defeated, he will cast a version of Heartbeat that restores 25% of his max Health (1,012) to himself.
[Death][Charm] "Your pitchforks can't hurt me." - If a Wizard casts a helpful Charm, The Monster will cast Banshee (Tier 5c) on the recipient of the Charm.
[Storm][Heal over Time] "There is no healing in this life." - If a Wizard casts a Heal over Time effect, The Monster will cast Triton (Tier 5b) on the recipient of the Heal over Time effect.
[Storm][Damage over Time] "Torches won't burn me." - If a Wizard casts a Damage over Time effect, the recipient of the Damage over Time effect will cast Stormzilla (Tier 5b) on the caster.
[Monstrology Rank 1][Balance] If Note 1 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Balance, Challenge) on the first Round.
[Trap] "Father kept all things in Balance... as they should be!" - While the Balance Minion is present, whenever a Wizard casts a Trap, The Monster will cast Trap Dilution on all enemies.
[Monstrology Rank 2][Death] If Note 2 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Death, Challenge) on the first Round.

[Afterlife] "Father's loss will be felt by all!" - While the Death Minion is present, whenever a Minion is defeated, The Monster will cast a Spell that deals 25% max Health Damage to the Wizard who defeated the Minions for each Minion defeated.
This cheat will still trigger if the Death Minion was one of the Minions that was defeated.
[Monstrology Rank 3][Fire] If Note 3 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Fire, Challenge) at the end of the second Round.
[Global] "Father taught me the power of rage!" - At the end of the third Round, The Monster will cast a Fire Spell that applies a +50% outgoing Damage Global.
This cheat will not re-trigger if the Global is replaced.
This cheat will still trigger even if the Fire Minion is defeated on the third Round.
[Monstrology Rank 4][Ice] If Note 4 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Ice, Challenge) at the end of the second Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Threat] "Father wanted to shield me from the world." - At the beginning of the Round after the Ice Minion becomes active, and every other Round thereafter, the Ice Minion will cast Mega Taunt.
[Monstrology Rank 5][Life] If Note 5 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Life, Challenge) at the end of the third Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Monstrology Rank 6][Myth] If Note 6 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Myth, Challenge) at the end of the third Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Trap] "Father's glory lives through me!" - While the Myth Minion is present, after The Monster damages a Wizard, he will cast an all enemies version of Hex.
[Monstrology Rank 7][Storm] If Note 7 is selected, the following cheats will occur:
The Monster will summon the Memory of Katzenstein (Storm, Challenge) at the end of the third Round, or at the end of the Round after the previous Minions have been defeated.
[Damage] "Father used lightning to give me life. I have another use." - While the Storm Minion is present, randomly at the end of the Round The Monster will cast Wild Bolt on a random Wizard.
strategy: Do not target The Monster on Rounds 1 and 2. He is untargetable, and aiming at him forces you to Pass. Set up blades and traps instead; he is targetable from Round 3.
Kill his summoned Memory of Katzenstein minions before him. Killing the boss with minions up revives him for 25% Health (1,012).
Avoid charms, heals-over-time, and damage-over-time on your side. Each triggers a punish: Banshee, Triton, or a reflected Stormzilla. Use plain blades and a direct hit.

Your chosen Monstrology Note sets which minion and cheats appear, such as the Death Note punishing minion kills or the Storm Note adding random Wild Bolts. Pick the Note whose cheat you can play around.
Hit Ice or Myth, +15% each. At 4,050 Health one stacked hit finishes him once the minions are clear.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he makes himself untargetable.
Any spell aimed at him that Round forces you to Pass; spells not aimed at him still go off. Do not target him Round 1.
If you picked no Monstrology Note or Note 1, he summons the Balance Katzenstein minion.
He becomes targetable again from Round 3.
Killing him before all his minions are dead revives him for 25% Health (1,012). Clear minions first.
Any helpful charm you cast makes him Banshee the Wizard who received it.
Any heal-over-time you cast makes him Triton the Wizard who received it.
Any damage-over-time you cast makes its recipient Stormzilla the caster, so damage-over-time backfires.
Note 1 summons the Balance minion on Round 1.
With the Balance minion up, every trap you cast triggers Trap Dilution on all enemies, weakening your traps. Avoid traps during this Note.
Note 2 summons the Death minion on Round 1.
With the Death minion up, defeating any minion makes him deal 25% max Health to the killer per minion defeated.
This still fires even if the Death minion itself was among those killed.
Note 3 summons the Fire minion at the end of Round 2.
With Note 3, at the end of Round 3 he casts a +50% outgoing damage global for his side.
Note 4 summons the Ice minion at the end of Round 2, or the Round after previous minions die.
The Ice minion Mega Taunts every other Round starting the Round after it is active, forcing your hits onto it.
Note 5 summons the Life minion at the end of Round 3, or the Round after previous minions die.
Note 6 summons the Myth minion at the end of Round 3, or the Round after previous minions die.
With the Myth minion up, after he damages a Wizard he casts an all-enemies Hex on his side.
Note 7 summons the Storm minion at the end of Round 3, or the Round after previous minions die.
With the Storm minion up, he randomly casts Wild Bolt on a random Wizard at the end of the Round.

name: The Nullity
cheats: Buffs:
During this fight a Wizard can cast Shadow Surge and Retribution during this battle.

Additionally, Wizards can use all previously available inspired casts spells including: Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle, and Monumental (All Allies) which gives all Wizards one Monumental card.
Inspire Cantrips may also be cast into this fight by anyone who hasn't joined after a player initiates the fight.
The Nullity:
At the beginning of the first Round, The Nullity will cast a Shadow Spell that forces all Wizards to pass.
At the end of every Round, The Nullity will cast Power Current on itself.

The Nullity will announce a School at the beginning of the battle and will choose another School every three Rounds. The new School will be announced after the fourth turn begins. These Schools conform to Roshambo rules and govern what Charms, Wards, Heal- or Damage over Time Spells may be used. At the end of the third Round, The Nullity will cast an all enemies Power Nova version of the Scion Spells that benefits based on the chosen School.
Schools are announced in text as follows:
"The Nullity is Fired Up" - Fire Phase: [Fire]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Fire Elf on a wizard.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Scald on all wizards.
The Scion is powered up if two or more Damage over Time effects are on a Wizard.
"The Nullity Keeps Its Cool" - Ice Phase: [Ice]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Tower Shield on itself.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Shield Wall on itself.
The Scion is powered up if four or more positive Wards are on the Nullity.
"The Nullity is Electrifying" - Storm Phase: [Storm]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Star Blade on itself.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Guiding Light (x3) on itself.
The Scion is powered up if four or more positive Charms are on the Nullity.
"The Nullity Ascends to Legend" - Myth Phase: [Myth]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Mythic Fuel on a wizard.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Mass Myth Trap on all wizards.
The Scion is powered up if four or more negative Wards are on a Wizard.
"The Nullity Feels Alive" - Life Phase: [Life]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Sprite on itself.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Sprite Swarm on all allies.
The Scion is powered up if two or more Heal over Time effects are on the Nullity.
"The Nullity Foresees your Doom" - Death Phase: [Death]
At the end of the first turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Threefold Fever on a wizard.
At the end of the second turn of this cycle, The Nullity will cast Mass Infection on all wizards.
The Scion is powered up if four or more negative Charms are on a Wizard.

The Nullity is joined in battle by an elite Minion from a random School, both of which also follow Roshambo rules to counter Wizard Spells. Fire counters positive and negative wards. Ice counters positive and negative Charms. Storm counters Damage over Time and Heal over Time effects. Myth counters negative Charms and positive Wards. Death counters positive Charms and Heal over Time effects. Life counters negative Wards and Damage over Time effects. Balance counters when over nine Pips.
spellNotes: Casts a 6 Pip version of Blizzard that deals 555 Ice Damage and removes 3 positive Charms from all enemies.
Casts a 6 Pip version of Ratatoskr's Spin that deals 555 Life Damage, and removes 3 negative Wards from all allies.
strategy: Voracious Void raid boss (40,000 health, 100% base resist), so coordinate a full team around its Roshambo school phases.
Track the school it announces each cycle (it switches every 3 rounds) — it dictates which charms, wards, DoTs, and HoTs are usable or countered, so adapt your spell types to the current phase.

Starve its Scion Power Nova at the end of round 3 of each cycle: each phase has a power-up condition you must NOT feed (e.g. don't stack 2+ DoTs on a wizard in Fire phase, don't put 4+ wards on it in Ice phase, don't leave 4+ charms on it in Storm phase).

Lean on the inspired casts unlocked here (Shadow Surge, Retribution, Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle, Monumental), since your normal schools are fully resisted.
It self-buffs Power Current every round, so keep the fight moving; handle its elite minion per that minion's school Roshambo counter.
Round 1 everyone is forced to pass, so plan your opening from round 2.
cheatNotes: You can cast Shadow Surge and Retribution in this fight — build around them.
Inspired casts (Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle, Monumental) are available — your main damage since normal schools are fully resisted.
Round 1 it forces every wizard to pass — you can't act, so plan your opening from round 2.
It stacks Power Current on itself every round, so its damage climbs — keep the fight moving.

It announces a school and switches every 3 rounds under Roshambo rules, then at the end of round 3 of each cycle casts a Scion Power Nova whose strength depends on the phase condition — track the school and adapt.
