In Fire phase, don't let 2+ DoTs sit on any wizard — that powers up its Scion Nova. Clear damage-over-time.
In Ice phase, don't put 4+ positive wards on the Nullity — that powers up its Scion. Avoid warding it.
In Storm phase, don't let 4+ positive charms sit on the Nullity — steal or avoid feeding it charms.
In Myth phase, keep negative wards on wizards under 4 — 4+ powers its Scion. Cleanse them.
In Life phase, don't let 2+ heal-over-time effects sit on the Nullity — deny it HoTs.
In Death phase, keep negative charms on wizards under 4 — 4+ powers its Scion. Cleanse them.

Its elite minion follows Roshambo too, countering specific spell types by its school (Fire counters wards, Ice counters charms, Storm counters DoT/HoT, Myth counters negative charms and positive wards, Death counters positive charms and HoT, Life counters negative wards and DoT, Balance counters when you hold over 9 pips) — mind the minion's school before you cast.

name: The Prophet
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Tomb of Night
cheats: The Prophet

"No Sanctuary in the Night." - At the beginning of the first Round and if a Wizard joins the duel late, The Prophet casts a negative version of Acrimony on all Wizards that turns all incoming Healing into Damage for 99 Rounds. The Aura is applied twice, but the effects do not stack.

If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell on the first Round, one of the negative Auras will be replaced, and the Wizard will receive the effects of the remaining one, as well the one they cast, at the same time. This will change the 99 Round Aura timer to 4 Rounds to match the Wizard's Aura Spell, and all Aura effects will end once the timer expires.
Casting any Aura on any other turn will cause The Prophet to cast a no-Damage version of Supernova, removing the Wizard's Aura, and then will recast this cheat on all wizards.
The Aura does not convert healing received from drain spells
Shadow Guardian
At the beginning of the first Round, both Shadow Guardians will cast a permanent version of Shadow Sentinel which provides Intercept 90%, +80 Critical Block, and -30% incoming Damage.
strategy: Team fight (~15,000 health, two Shadow Guardians): hit with Life (+30%); the Prophet resists Death 71%.
Do not heal with normal spells. His Acrimony turns all incoming healing into damage for the fight. Recover with Death drain spells instead, which are not converted.
Kill the Shadow Guardians first. Their permanent Shadow Sentinel intercepts 90% of your damage, so hits on the Prophet are mostly wasted until the Guardians are gone.
Be careful with Auras. Casting one after Round 1 makes him Supernova it off and reapply Acrimony to everyone. Only overwrite it on Round 1 if you plan for it.
With the Guardians down, stack blades and Feint, then land one big Life hit.
cheatNotes: On Round 1, and on late joins, he casts Acrimony on all Wizards, turning your healing into damage for the fight. Heal with Death drains instead.
Casting an Aura on Round 1 replaces one Acrimony and shortens the timer to 4 Rounds, matching your Aura.
Casting an Aura on any later turn makes him Supernova it off and re-apply Acrimony to everyone.
Healing received from drain spells is not converted, so drains still heal you.
Both Shadow Guardians cast a permanent Shadow Sentinel (intercept 90%, +80 block, -30% incoming). Kill them before hitting the Prophet.

name: The Prophet (Chamber of Phoebe)
locations: Krokotopia: Tomb of the Forgotten (Krokotopia) - Chamber of Phoebe
cheats: The Prophet

"No Sanctuary in the Night." - At the beginning of the first Round and if a Wizard joins the duel late, The Prophet casts a negative version of Acrimony on all Wizards that turns all incoming Healing into Damage for 99 Rounds. The Aura is applied twice, but the effects do not stack.

If a Wizard casts an Aura Spell on the first Round, one of the negative Auras will be replaced, and the Wizard will receive the effects of the remaining one, as well the one they cast, at the same time.
Casting any Aura on any other turn will cause The Prophet to cast a no-Damage version of Supernova, removing the Wizard's Aura, and then will recast this cheat on all wizards.
Enki the Heretic
"To face the wolf, bare the claws." - At the beginning of every second Round, Enki the Heretic will cheat-cast a +25% Fireblade or +25% Deathblade on each Wizard.
"The Light burns!" - At the beginning of the every fifth Round, Enki will cheat-cast Incindiate.
"The Light blesses you." - If a Wizard casts a positive Charm, Enki will cheat-cast an all enemies version of Sprite, restoring 500 Health over 4 Rounds on each Wizard.
"Feel the bite of Raven!" - After Enki attacks, or if a Wizard joins the duel late, Enki will cheat-cast Scald.
strategy: His Acrimony turns all incoming healing into damage for the whole team for 99 rounds, so do not heal; win by bursting, not sustaining.
Cast an Aura on round 1 to overwrite one of the two Acrimony auras. Only round-1 auras help; any later Aura triggers a Supernova that strips it and re-applies Acrimony.
Do not cast positive Charms. Enki answers with a Sprite heal on your team, which becomes damage under Acrimony. Use Enki's free Fireblades and Deathblades (every 2nd round) as your buffs instead.
Watch Enki's timers (Incindiate every 5th round, Scald after he attacks) and burst the group down; the Prophet takes +Life and resists Death 71%.
Team: there is no healer role here, so run extra shields and absorbs behind a big hitter who AoEs the boss and minions together.
cheatNotes: At round 1 (or on a late join) he casts a negative Acrimony on all wizards that turns incoming healing into damage for 99 rounds; it is applied twice but does not stack.
Casting an Aura on round 1 overwrites one of the two Acrimony auras, and you get your own aura plus the remaining one.
Casting an Aura on any later turn makes him Supernova it away and re-apply the Acrimony to everyone.
Every 2nd round Enki gives each wizard a +25% Fireblade or +25% Deathblade.
Every 5th round Enki casts Incindiate.
If a wizard casts a positive Charm, Enki casts an all-enemy Sprite that heals your team 500 over 4 rounds, which becomes damage under Acrimony.
After Enki attacks, or on a late join, he casts Scald on the wizards.

name: The Quake Charmer
cheats: "Better quake than never!" - The Quake Charmer will cast his Shadow Natural Attack on a Wizard who joins the battle after the first Round.
This cheat is followed up by the "Boom Chick-a-Boom" cheat.
"Let's shake things up!" - At the end of the first Round and once again at the end of the fifth Round, The Quake Charmer will cast an Earthquake.

"Makin' with the quakin'" - At the end of the third Round and every three Rounds after that, The Quake Charmer will cast a Balance Spell that deals 1,000 Balance Damage after three rounds. No damage is taken during the first two rounds and activates on the third round.
"What's shakin'?" - Casts an Earthquake dealing 145 Myth Damage removing one positive Charm and one positive Ward.

"BOOM-CHICKA-BOOM!" - The Quake Charmer will say this after casting his Natural Attacks, but only his Shadow Natural Attack will trigger an Immolate spell that removes one positive Charm and one positive Ward.
spellNotes: Casts a +30% version of Mass Hex.
strategy: Hit with Storm, Life, or Death (+5%); avoid Myth (65%) and Balance (70%).
His Earthquake at end of round 1 (and round 5) strips one positive charm and one ward from everyone — don't stack blades before round 1's quake; build after it.
Set up rounds 2-4 and land your hit before the round-5 Earthquake wipes your blades again.
Watch the delayed Balance bomb from round 3: 1,000 damage that lands three rounds later — shield for it.
Blade after the quake and burst him with a Storm/Life/Death hit.
cheatNotes: Late wizards eat his Shadow natural attack — join on time.
End of round 1 and round 5 he Earthquakes, removing a positive charm and ward from everyone — build blades after each quake.
Starting round 3 he casts a delayed Balance spell that hits for 1,000 three rounds later — shield for it.
His Earthquake deals 145 Myth and strips one charm and one ward.
His Shadow natural attack triggers an Immolate that strips a charm and a ward.

name: The Rat
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - Wizards who join the duel after the battle has started, will be punished with a +60% Fire, Ice, and Global Trap, as well as a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that transfers all negative hanging effects on The Rat to the late Wizard.
"Fool me once?" - If a Feint is placed on The Rat, he will shout this as a warning.

"Can't get fooled again!" - If a Feint has already been placed on The Rat, any subsequent attempts to Feint him will trigger a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that removes all negative hanging effects on him, and places them onto the Wizard who cast the Feint.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint will not be removed, but will instead summon a Titan Experiment 07 minion as per The Rat's "The Spiral is a disease, we have the cure!" cheat.
Minions
As long as there are Minions occupying all three enemy spots, the Rat will be unable to summon additional Minions in response to a trigger cast until a spot opens up.

"The Spiral is a disease, we have the cure!" - If a Wizard casts a Trap spell, The Rat will cast a Death Spell to summon a Titan Experiment 07 Minion with 1 Health 103% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 3% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
This can be dispelled with Strangle, preventing the Minion from being summoned.

On its first turn, the Minion will cast a Plague (-25%), then polymorph to have 10 Health and 105% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 5% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its second turn, the Minion will cast a Mass Infection, and polymorph to have 100 Health and 107% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 7% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
On its third turn, the Minion will cast a multi-target all enemies Bad Juju (2 Pip), then self-destruct.
Mass Feint will trigger these cheats.
Mockenspiel will trigger these cheats.
Spells that place negative charms will also trigger these cheats.

"You call that healing? Behold my creation!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, The Rat will summon a Titan Experiment 34 Minion with 1 Health and 103% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 3% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its first turn, the Minion will cast Guiding Light and Sanctuary, then polymorph to have 10 Health and 105% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 5% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its second turn, the Minion will either cast Regenerate (225+1,100), if The Rat has been injured, or pass if he is uninjured. It will then polymorph to have 100 Health 107% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 7% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
On its third turn, the Minion will either cast Rebirth (730, 100%) (if The Rat has been injured), or Spinysaur on a random Wizard (if The Rat is uninjured), then self-destruct.
"Say bye-bye to my friend, and hello to your doom!" - If the Minion used Rebirth, The Rat will also cast a Doom and Gloom (-75%).
When a defeated Minion is affected by Rebirth, it will be revived for an additional Round. The revived Minion will always cast the same spell it used previous to its defeat.
Pet Spells will trigger this cheat.
Spells such as Link and Power Link will not trigger this cheat.

"You have blades, but I have Borealis!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global spell, The Rat will cast a Storm Spell to summon a Titan Experiment 291 Minion with 1 Health and 103% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 3% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
This can be dispelled with Dissipate, preventing the Minion from being summoned.

On its first turn, the Minion will cast Windstorm (+25%), and polymorph to have 10 Health and 105% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 5% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.

On its second turn, the Minion will cast Wild Bolt (15-150-1,150) on a random Wizard, and polymorph to have 100 Health and 107% Resist to Fire, Ice, Storm, Myth, Life, Death, and Balance, and 7% Resist to Shadow and Astral school Damage Spells.
On its third turn, the Minion will cast a multi-target all enemies Insane Bolt (1,125), then self-destruct.
"Borealis is hard to pierce, eh?" - The Rat will shout this whenever a Titan Experiment 291 self-destructs.
Titan Timer

"Down comes the Sky Anchor, and the Spiral with it!" - At the beginning of Rounds 2, 10, and 20, the Titan will attack the Sky Anchor, causing 1,043 Shadow Damage to all Wizards. The Damage will increase for Rounds 10 and 20.
"My Titan will see you disintegrated!" - At the beginning of Rounds 5, 15, and 25, the Titan will deal 1,043 Shadow Damage to all Wizards. The Damage will increase for Rounds 15 and 25.

"And alas, the Anchor falls, the Spiral unravels..." - At the start of Round 30 (and every Round thereafter), the Titan will deal 1,000,000 Shadow Damage with a higher Armor Piercing to all Wizards, thus ending the fight.
spellNotes: Uses Brainfreeze that also stuns all enemies.
strategy: Field a team of 4: a hitter with high damage and pierce, a 'Minion Killa' with 21%+ pierce and a 4-pip AoE, and supports with trained elemental/spirit blades. The Rat resists 15% to most schools but NOT Fire or Ice, so a Fire or Ice hitter is ideal.

Win by Round 4. The Titan clock deals ~1,043 shadow AoE on Rounds 2 and 5 (escalating on 10/15/20/25) and wipes the team with 1,000,000 damage at Round 30. Minion summons also take three turns to ramp, so a fast kill outruns everything.

Do not trigger summons carelessly. Traps summon a Death minion (Titan 07), heals summon a healer minion (Titan 34), and blades or globals summon a Storm minion (Titan 291). Front-load blades on the hitter and save traps/Feint for the burst round.

R1: everyone blades the hitter (unless the dispeller must cast Melt). R2: the Minion Killa fires an Unstoppable-enchanted 4-pip AoE to clear minions while others keep blading; trap the Rat if you have it.

R3: everyone blades the hitter, aiming for 10-11 total buffs (trained/sharpened/item school blades plus tri-blades). R4: apply Feint and any last trap, the healer casts Rebirth or Unicorn if needed, then the hitter fires the kill shot.
Feint carefully. The first Feint is only a warning, but a second single-target Feint bounces all debuffs back onto the caster. Use Mass Feint instead - it never triggers the bounce.
Cast Melt to remove the Rat's 7-pip group Frostbite that stuns the whole team. Have the hitter port home and Dungeon Recall to enter last in turn order.
cheatNotes: Wizards who join after the battle starts are hit with +60% Fire/Ice/Global traps and a Shadow Dimension Shift that dumps all of the Rat's debuffs onto them, so never join late.
The first Feint placed on him is free - he only shouts a warning and lets it stick.
A second single-target Feint triggers a Shadow Dimension Shift that strips every debuff off him and slaps it onto the wizard who cast it, so only Feint him once with single-target Feint.
Mass Feint does not trigger the debuff-bounce, making it the safe way to stack Feints on him.
The Balance version of Feint is not removed but instead summons a Titan Experiment 07 minion via his trap-summon cheat.
While all three enemy slots are full he cannot summon new minions, so leaving a low-threat minion alive can actually block worse summons until a slot opens.
Casting any trap makes him summon a Titan Experiment 07 (1 HP, ~103% resist to normal schools), a Death minion that ramps over three turns.
You can dispel the pending Titan 07 with Strangle to stop the summon entirely.
On its first turn the 07 casts Plague (-25%) and hardens to 10 HP / 105% resist.
On its second turn it casts Mass Infection and hardens to 100 HP / 107% resist, so it is much tougher if left alive two rounds.
On its third turn it casts a multi-target Bad Juju (2 pip) on the whole team and then self-destructs.
Mass Feint also triggers the Titan 07 summon, so weigh its use against spawning a minion.
Mockenspiel likewise triggers the Titan 07 summon.
Any spell that places a negative charm triggers the Titan 07 summon, not just traps.
Casting any heal makes him summon a Titan Experiment 34, a Life/healer minion that will keep him alive if left up.
On its first turn the 34 casts Guiding Light and Sanctuary, then hardens to 10 HP / 105% resist.
On its second turn it casts Regenerate (225+1,100) if the Rat is hurt, or passes if he is full, then hardens to 100 HP / 107% resist.
On its third turn it casts Rebirth (730) if the Rat is hurt, or Spinysaur on a random wizard if he is full, then self-destructs.
If the 34 used Rebirth, the Rat follows with Doom and Gloom (-75%), gutting your own healing for a while.
Rebirth on a defeated minion revives it for one more round, and it repeats the exact spell it cast before dying.
Pet-cast heal spells also trigger the Titan 34 summon, so watch may-cast heal pets.
Link and Power Link do not count as heals and will not trigger the Titan 34 summon.
Casting any blade or global makes him summon a Titan Experiment 291, a Storm minion that throws heavy bolts.
You can dispel the pending Titan 291 with Dissipate to stop the summon.
On its first turn the 291 casts Windstorm (+25%) and hardens to 10 HP / 105% resist.
On its second turn it casts Wild Bolt (up to 1,150) on a random wizard and hardens to 100 HP / 107% resist.
On its third turn it casts a multi-target Insane Bolt (1,125) on the whole team and self-destructs.
He shouts a flavor line whenever a Titan 291 self-destructs, no mechanical effect.
On Rounds 2, 10, and 20 the Titan hits the Sky Anchor for ~1,043 shadow damage to all wizards, escalating on the later rounds.
On Rounds 5, 15, and 25 the Titan deals ~1,043 shadow damage to all wizards, escalating on the later rounds.
At the start of Round 30 (and every round after) the Titan deals 1,000,000 shadow damage with high pierce, wiping the team, so you must win well before Round 30.

name: The Rat (Challenge)
cheats: [Aura] "You are no match for my ambitions" - At the beginning of the first Round, the Rat will cast an Invulnerability Aura on himself.
[Shadow][Damage] "My titan will see you disintegrated!" - Whenever a Minion dies, the Titan will deal 450 Shadow Damage to all Wizards.

[Shadow][Damage] "Down comes the Sky Anchor, and the Spiral with it!" - Whenever the Rat has 7 Shadow Pips, he will use his turn to cast a Shadow Spell that makes the Titan deal ??? Shadow Damage to all Wizards.
[Aura] "Enough! This ends now." - At the end of the Round that all Minions have been defeated, the Rat will cast a Frenzy to replace his Aura.
[Death]
"The Spiral is a disease, we have the cure!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Trap Spell, the Titan Experiment 07 will give a Shadow Pip to The Rat.
[Storm]
"Blades, shields, neither will save you." - Whenever a Wizard casts a Blade or Shield Spell, the Titan Experiment 291 will give a Shadow Pip to The Rat.
[Life]
"Your power is nothing before the Borealis!" - Whenever a Wizard casts a Global Spell, the Titan Experiment 34 will give a Shadow Pip to The Rat.
strategy: Round 1 he is under an Invulnerability aura - you cannot meaningfully damage him until it is gone, so spend the opening clearing minions.

The three Titan Experiments feed him Shadow Pips based on your casts: Traps (Death Titan 07), Blades/Shields (Storm Titan 291), and Globals (Life Titan 34). At 7 shadow pips he fires a large Titan AoE at the team. Minimize those cast types and kill the Titan that punishes your setup first.
Each minion death also triggers a 450 Shadow AoE on all wizards - heal and shield through the clear.
Once all minions are dead he swaps his aura for Frenzy, spiking his damage, so be set up to burst him fast after the board is clear.
Team: pick a hitter whose blades are minimal, clear Titans while managing pip-feeding, then unload before his Frenzy window hurts.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he puts an Invulnerability aura on himself - damage barely lands until it is replaced. Clear minions first.
Every time a minion dies, the Titan hits all wizards for 450 Shadow. Shield the team as you clear.
When the Rat reaches 7 Shadow Pips he spends his turn on a large Titan AoE against the whole team. Do not let his pips reach 7.
Once all minions are defeated he replaces his aura with Frenzy, spiking his damage. Be ready to burst him.
Casting a Trap makes Titan Experiment 07 give the Rat a Shadow Pip. Limit traps.
Casting a Blade or Shield makes Titan Experiment 291 give the Rat a Shadow Pip. Limit blades and shields.
Casting a Global makes Titan Experiment 34 give the Rat a Shadow Pip. Avoid globals.

name: The Rat (Husk)
cheats: Tardiness Cheats:
The Rat

"Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins a Round late, The Rat will cast a +60% Fire, Ice, and Universal Trap, and a Shadow-version of Dimension Shift transfering all negative hanging effects on The Rat to the late Wizard.
The Scorpion
"Oh Yes! You will fall too!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, The Scorpion will cast Bad Juju (Shadow) on the Wizard.
Round 1 Cheats:
Starting Round 1, The Scorpion casts a version of Donate Shadow that gives himself 2 Shadow pips.
"Thank you, dear brother" - The Rat then will cast a Shadow spell that steals one Shadow pip from The Scorpion.
"I'll take that, weakling" - The Scorpion will retaliate back casting Steal Pips at The Rat, stealing 2 Pips.
The Rat:
"Fool me once?" - If a Feint is placed on The Rat, he will shout this as a warning.

"Can't get fooled again!" - If a Feint has already been placed on The Rat, any subsequent attempts to Feint him will trigger a Shadow version of Dimension Shift that removes all negative hanging effects on him, and places them onto the Wizard who cast the Feint.
Mass Feint does not trigger this cheat.
The Balance version of Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Pet Spells do not trigger this cheat.
The Scorpion:
"I invoke the power of Shadow!" - At the end of every three rounds, he will cheat-cast Poison (Shadow) to all Wizards.
"Now... your doom!" - At the end of every Round, he will cheat-cast a Shadow version of Detonate to all Wizards, as long as one Wizard has a DoT.
The HoT portion of healing spells, such as from Spritely, can prevent a DoT from detonating when the HoT is positioned before the DoT. The HoT is removed by the Detonate.
"The Rat warned me of that!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on The Scorpion, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all unprotected traps from himself.
"I planned this special for you!" - If a Wizard uses a healing spell, he will cast his Natural Attack at the Wizard.
Pet heals will not trigger this cheat with the exception of Fairy Friend and Happy Accident.
The HoT portion of healing spells, such as from Spritely, will not trigger this cheat.
Retaliation Cheats:
If either The Rat or The Scorpion casts a spell that upsets the other, they'll retaliate and cast the following cheats on each other.
"Fool! Stay out of my way!" - If The Scorpion casts Enfeeble (2 Pip) or Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 1,120), The Rat will cast Scorching Scimitars at The Scorpion.

"You DOLT! I have had enough of you!" - Sometimes after The Scorpion's Iron Sultan (Shadow) cheat aimed at The Rat, he will retaliate by casting Grand Blue Phoenix at The Scorpion, and casting Impede at The Scorpion.
"No! That spell was mine!" - If The Rat casts Steal Charm or Steal Ward, The Scorpion will cast Iron Sultan (Shadow) at The Rat.
"Enough! I'm telling father!" - Sometimes after The Rat's Scorching Scimitars cheat, The Scorpion will cast Impede and Supernova (Shadow) at The Rat.
spellNotes: Uses Brainfreeze that also stuns all enemies.
strategy: Two-boss fight (The Rat and The Scorpion), 28,000 health, full team. Both resist little (15%), so hit with your strongest school.
Feint carefully. The first Feint on The Rat is a free warning, but a second regular Feint bounces all his negatives back onto the caster. Use Mass Feint or Balance Feint, which never trigger it.
Do not Feint The Scorpion either; he Cleanse-Wards all unprotected traps off himself in response.
Avoid DoTs. The Scorpion Detonates them on the whole team every round if anyone carries one.
Do not self-heal. The Scorpion natural-attacks any wizard who heals (a HoT-first heal avoids it). Join on time to skip the tardiness traps and Bad Juju.
Funnel to one hitter and burst each brother; the two constantly retaliate on each other, which chips them for you.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets you +60% Fire/Ice/Universal traps and a Dimension Shift dumping The Rat's negatives onto you.
Joining late also gets you Bad Juju from The Scorpion.
Round 1 The Scorpion gives himself 2 Shadow pips.
The Rat then steals one Shadow pip from The Scorpion, part of their infighting.
The Scorpion retaliates, stealing 2 pips back from The Rat.
The first Feint on The Rat is safe; he only warns you.
A second regular Feint on The Rat bounces all his negatives onto the caster via Dimension Shift. Do not stack regular Feints on him.
Mass Feint does not trigger that bounce, so it is safe.
Balance Feint does not trigger it either.
Pet-cast Feints do not trigger it.
Every 3 rounds The Scorpion Poisons all wizards (Shadow DoT).
Every round he Detonates DoTs on the whole team if anyone has one. Avoid carrying DoTs.
Placing a HoT before a DoT lets the HoT absorb the Detonate instead of the DoT, a way to shield your DoTs.
Feinting The Scorpion makes him Cleanse-Ward all unprotected traps off himself.
Healing makes The Scorpion natural-attack the healer.
Pet heals do not trigger it, except Fairy Friend and Happy Accident.
The HoT portion of a heal does not trigger the natural attack.
If The Scorpion casts Enfeeble or Glowbug Squall, The Rat hits him with Scorching Scimitars, free chip on The Scorpion.
Sometimes after The Scorpion's Iron Sultan, The Rat retaliates with Grand Blue Phoenix and Impede on him.
If The Rat casts Steal Charm or Steal Ward, The Scorpion hits The Rat with Iron Sultan.
Sometimes after The Rat's Scimitars, The Scorpion answers with Impede and Supernova on The Rat.

name: The Red Herring
cheats: The inspired cast spells obtained from the 4 bosses surrounding Mr. Ghastly are also sent to The Red Herring's fight.

"Only one school can hurt me. But which one? And why?" - At the beginning of the first Round, The Red Herring will cast a Quasi-Invulnerability Aura, making him immune to six of the seven main Schools. The School he is vulnerable to is random.

At the end of each of the first 6 turns after he casts his Quasi-Invulnerability Aura, The Red Herring will shout out one of the following phrases, each phrase informing Wizards of which Schools of Damage he is immune to. The phrase he does not shout out is the School he is vulnerable to:
[Fire] “I’m steaming mad right now!” - The Red Herring is immune to Fire Damage.
[Ice] “Play it cool. You’ll figure it out.” - The Red Herring is immune to Ice Damage.
[Storm] “Even the fish of the seas fear the tempest.” - The Red Herring is immune to Storm Damage.
[Myth] “I sure hope you don’t CONJURE the solution…” - The Red Herring is immune to Myth Damage.
[Life] “What is the secret of Life?” - The Red Herring is immune to Life Damage.
[Death] “I have a sudden fear of my own mortality!” - The Red Herring is immune to Death Damage.
[Balance] “I hate sand. Too dry." - The Red Herring is immune to Balance Damage.

"Wrong! Oh, were you misled by a... Red Herring?" - If The Red Herring is hit by Damage from a School he is immune to, he will say this and cheat cast a Shadow-Enhanced AOE based on the School he was hit with on all Wizards.

"Really? Of course I thought of that. Reset!" - If a Dispel is cast on The Red Herring or The Red Herring's Quasi-Invulnerability Aura is removed, The Red Herring will remove all negative Charms from himself, cast a new Quasi-Invulnerability Aura, and cheat-cast a version of Glowbug Squall that deals Damage equivalent to 50% of all Wizards' maximum Health.

"You are late. How embarrassing. " - If a Wizard joins after the first turn, The Red Herring will cheat cast a version of Storm Shark that deals Damage equivalent to 25% of that Wizard's maximum Health.
spellNotes: This boss casts a version of Tempest that costs 4 Pips.
This boss casts a version of Storm Lord that doesn't Stun.
strategy: Solve the puzzle before you attack. He opens with a Quasi-Invulnerability Aura making him immune to six of the seven schools; only one random school can hurt him.
Listen to his first six shouts. Each names a school he is immune to, so the single school he never names is the one you can damage.
Bring prisms so your hitter can convert to whichever school turns out to be the weak one, and set up blades while you wait out the shouts.
Never hit with an immune school. It triggers a Shadow-enhanced AoE on the whole team based on that school.
Never Dispel him or remove his aura. It resets the puzzle, gives him a fresh aura, and fires a Glowbug Squall for 50% of everyone's max Health.

At 24,140 Health, run this Raid as a team with a flexible prism hitter. Note the four inspired-cast spells from the surrounding bosses carry into this fight, and enter on time to avoid the late-join Storm Shark.
cheatNotes: The inspired-cast spells you earned from the four bosses around Mr. Ghastly carry into this fight, so bring them.
Round 1 he casts a Quasi-Invulnerability Aura making him immune to six of the seven schools; the one vulnerable school is random each time.
Over his first six turns he names schools he is immune to, so the single school he never names is the one that can damage him; listen before you commit a hit.
'I am steaming mad right now' means he is immune to Fire this fight.
'Play it cool' means he is immune to Ice.
'Even the fish of the seas fear the tempest' means he is immune to Storm.
'I sure hope you do not CONJURE the solution' means he is immune to Myth.
'What is the secret of Life' means he is immune to Life.
'I have a sudden fear of my own mortality' means he is immune to Death.
'I hate sand. Too dry' means he is immune to Balance.
Hit him with a school he is immune to and he retaliates with a Shadow-enhanced AoE on the whole team based on that school, so only swing once you know the weak school.
Dispelling him or removing his aura resets the puzzle, gives him a new aura, and fires a Glowbug Squall for 50% of everyone's max Health, so never Dispel him.
A wizard who joins after the first turn eats a Storm Shark for 25% of their max Health, so enter on time.

name: The Scorpion
cheats: "Oh, yes...you will fall, too." - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, starting on the third Round The Scorpion will cheat-cast Bad Juju (Shadow) on the Wizard.
"I invoke the power of Shadow!" - At the end of every third Round, he will cheat-cast Poison (Shadow) to all Wizards.
"Now... your doom!" - At the end of every Round, he will cheat-cast a Shadow version of Detonate to all Wizards, as long as one Wizard has a DoT.
The HoT portion of Healing Spells, such as from Spritely, can prevent a DoT from detonating when the HoT is positioned before the DoT. The HoT does not get removed by the Detonate.
"The Rat warned me of that!" - If a Wizard places a Feint on The Scorpion, he will cast Cleanse Ward (Shadow, -All) to remove all unprotected Traps from himself.
Mass Feint does not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
"I planned this special for you!" - If a Wizard uses a Healing Spell, he will cast his Natural Attack (Shadow 75+1430) at the Wizard.
The HoT portion of Healing Spells, such as from Spritely, will not trigger this cheat.
Maycast Pet Heals only seem to occasionally trigger this cheat.
spellNotes: Casts protected Tower Shield (Shadow), Shadowblade, and Shadow Trap.
Deals 75 Shadow Damage and 110 Shadow damage per pip minus one pip over 3 Rounds, despite the Spell's text.
strategy: Hit with your strongest school (he resists 30% to all). Bring a HoT like Spritely, it is central here.

He Poisons the whole team every third round, then Detonates any DoT for heavy Shadow damage at the end of every round. Keep a HoT active positioned before the DoT to stop the detonation (the HoT is not consumed).
Do not cast direct heals, a heal makes him Natural-Attack you for 75+1430 Shadow. Use the HoT portion of heals (Spritely) instead, which does not trigger it.
Use Mass Feint or an Indemnity-protected Feint, a plain Feint makes him Cleanse-Ward all your traps off.
Stack blades and a protected Feint, keep a HoT up against the Poison, then land one big hit.
cheatNotes: Join late and from Round 3 he hits you with Bad Juju.
He Poisons the whole team every third round, a DoT you will want to manage before his Detonate.
At the end of every round he Detonates any DoT on the team for big Shadow damage, keep DoTs off yourself.
A HoT placed before the DoT stops that DoT from detonating, and the HoT is not removed, run Spritely to neutralize his Poison.
Plain Feint makes him Cleanse-Ward all unprotected traps off himself.
Mass Feint does not trigger the trap cleanse, use it instead.
Indemnity also protects your Feint from the cleanse.
Cast a direct heal and he Natural-Attacks you for 75+1430 Shadow, avoid flat heals.
The HoT portion of a heal does not trigger that attack, so heal with HoTs.
Pet maycast heals occasionally trigger the attack, watch your pet.

name: The Shedder
locations: Wizard City: The Drains - Shedder's Lair
cheats: ”Your tardiness is disgusting!” - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, the Shedder will cast his Natural Attack on the late Wizard, then cast Stun (Shadow) on the Wizard, which lasts for two Rounds.
When the Shedder is hit with a Damage Spell, he will take 0 Damage due to his immunity. Then, he will damage himself for 1 Damage.
Multi-hitting Spells, such as Minotaur and Hydra, will trigger this cheat multiple times.
Damage over Time will not trigger this cheat.
This cheat also affects the Boss when he is Polymorphed as well removing 6% of his total Health (This may be a Bug).

When the Shedder is reduced to 1 Health, he Polymorphs into an enlarged version of himself, giving him 56,000 Health as well as removing his Damage immunity and the rest of his stats. He will also clear all Charms from himself.
Using Multi-hitting Spells such as Hydra can be used to skip the Polymorph stage and defeat the Shedder if cast when his Health is low enough.
After casting his Natural Attack, the Boss will cast Stun (Shadow) on the target that lasts for two Rounds.
On the 1st Round and every 6 Rounds after they've been defeated, The Shedder summons any combination of the following three Minions, with the following cheats:
Hired Hoof (Balance) - If a Wizard casts an Aura spell, Hired Hoof (Balance) will cast Supernova (535, 75%).
Pet May Casts will trigger this cheat.
Hired Hoof (Death) - If a Healing spell is cast, Hired Hoof (Death) will cast Infection on the caster.
Hired Hoof (Fire) - If a Wizard casts a Blade spell, Hired Hoof (Fire) will cast Steal Charm.
Hired Hoof (Ice) - If a Wizard casts a Ward spell, Hired Hoof (Ice) will cast Steal Ward. If Legion Shield or Rebirth is cast, it will Steal Ward from the caster.
Hired Hoof (Life) - If a Wizard casts a Damage Over Time (DOT) spell, Hired Hoof (Life) will cast Mass Triage.
Hired Hoof (Myth) - If a Wizard casts a Trap spell, Hired Hoof (Myth) will cast Cleanse Ward.
Hired Hoof (Storm) - If a Wizard casts a Weakness spell, Hired Hoof (Storm) will cast Cleanse Charm.
His Natural Attack? can stun for 2 rounds. (needs confirmation)
spellNotes: Deals 105 Shadow Damage per Pip despite the Spell description
strategy: He is immune to damage: every hit deals 0 but makes him self-damage 1. Chip him down with cheap multi-hit spells (Hydra's three hits do 3).
At 1 health he polymorphs into a 56,000-health form with no immunity, or use a multi-hitting spell like Hydra at low health to skip the polymorph and finish him outright.

The Hired Hoof minions each punish a specific action: auras (Supernova), heals (Infection), blades (Steal Charm), wards (Steal Ward), DoTs (Mass Triage), traps (Cleanse Ward), weakness (Cleanse Charm). Keep your setup narrow.
Once in the 56,000 form (or skipping it), blade up and hit for real damage.
Everyone join Round 1; latecomers get natural-attacked and stunned two rounds.
cheatNotes: Late joiners get natural-attacked and Stunned for two rounds, so join on Round 1.
He is immune to damage: every hit deals 0 but makes him self-damage 1, so use cheap multi-hit spells to chip him.
Multi-hit spells like Hydra trigger the self-damage once per hit, so they whittle him faster.
Damage-over-time does not trigger the self-damage, so DoTs will not chip him.
At 1 health he becomes a 56,000-health form that loses the damage immunity, so plan for a real damage race or skip it.
Casting a multi-hit spell like Hydra at low health can skip the polymorph and defeat him outright.
His natural attack is followed by a two-round Shadow Stun.
He summons Hired Hoof minions on Round 1 and every 6 rounds; each punishes a different action.
Casting an aura makes the Balance Hoof Supernova you, so skip auras.
Pet may-casts also trigger the aura Supernova.
Healing makes the Death Hoof Infect the caster.
Blading makes the Fire Hoof Steal Charm.
Warding makes the Ice Hoof Steal Ward (from the caster on Legion Shield or Rebirth).
DoTs make the Life Hoof Mass Triage them off.
Trapping makes the Myth Hoof Cleanse Ward.
Weakness makes the Storm Hoof Cleanse Charm it off.

name: The Terror
cheats: [Late] "*ROOOAAARRR*" - If a Wizard joins the duel after the first Round, The Terror will cast Mana Burn (Sun) on the Wizard that pierces all resistance.
[Round] "*ROOOAAARRR*" - At the end of every 5th Round, The Terror will cast a Sun Spell that applies a protected +100% Fire, Ice, Storm, or Universal Blade.
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Hex.
strategy: Enter on time. A late join eats a Sun Mana Burn that pierces all your resistance and drains pips.
Attack with Myth, Life, or Death (+5%); he resists Fire, Ice, Storm, and Balance 50%. He is stunable, so stun to deny a setup turn.
Beat him inside five rounds. Every fifth round he blades himself +100% (Fire, Ice, Storm, or Universal), so kill him before the blades stack or Steal and Shatter them off.
Expect a Mass Hex weakness on your team, so cleanse it or plan around the reduced damage.
Stack blades and land one big off-Fire/Ice/Storm hit before his fifth-round blade lands.
cheatNotes: Joining after round 1 gets you a Sun Mana Burn that pierces all resistance and drains pips. Enter at the start.
At the end of every fifth round he applies a protected +100% Fire, Ice, Storm, or Universal blade on himself. Kill him before round 5, or Steal/Shatter the blade off.

name: The Woman's Right Hand (Challenge)
cheats: [Myth][Curse] - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Charm, The Woman's Right Hand will cast Myth Trap on the caster.
[Myth][Ward] - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, The Woman's Right Hand will cast Myth Trap on the recipient of the Ward.
[Trap] "I'll... handle them" - Whenever a Wizard doesn't have a Trap on them, The Woman's Fist will cast a version of Hex on all enemies.

[Trap][Damage] "Right into my traps!" - At the beginning of a Round where a Wizard has three or more Traps, The Woman's Right Hand will cast a version of Vermin Virtuoso that deals 943 Myth Damage and doesn't apply a Global.
spellNotes: The Woman's Right Hand will always cast Time of Legend on the first Round.
strategy: Solo-friendly (3,240 health). Do not cast weaknesses (you get a Myth Trap) or shields (the recipient gets a Myth Trap).
She keeps a trap on you: any round you have none, she Hexes all wizards.
Never let your trap count reach three. At 3+ traps she fires Vermin Virtuoso for 943 Myth, so keep it at one or two.
She opens with Time of Legend on round 1. Blade up and burst her down fast given her low health.
Skip weaknesses and shields, hit with a strong non-Myth school, and finish before traps stack.
cheatNotes: Casting a weakness or harmful charm gets you a Myth Trap. Skip weaknesses.
Casting a shield or helpful ward gets the recipient a Myth Trap. Skip shields.
Any round you have no trap on you, she Hexes all wizards to keep a trap on you.
The moment you carry three or more traps she fires Vermin Virtuoso for 943 Myth. Keep your trap count under three.

name: The Writer
locations: Lemuria: Night Forest - Third Story Tomb
cheats: "How can you follow the plot if you're late?" - The Writer will cast a Star Spell to confuse the late Wizard for three Rounds.
"Look upon my works and despair" - The Writer will cast a Star Spell to summon three Minions at random.
Blank Pages, which target the Wizard's team.
Unhelpful Blank Pages, which target The Writer's team.
The Writer will say this line to re-summon Minions if a Minion is defeated.
"At least my work will live on!" - The Writer will cast a -200% Brace which states Invulnerability on each of his minions.
If a Minion is defeated and revived on the same turn, it will lose its Invulnerability aura.
"No, this isn't ready for feedback!" - If The Writer is defeated, on the next round, the Minions will each use their turn to cast Furnace (6 Rounds), replacing their Brace Auras.
If a Minion fails to cast Furnace (6 Rounds), it will continue to re-cast it the following rounds until it succeeds.
Cheat Cycle:
"Listen to my story. What do you feel?" - At the end of every turn, The Writer will cast a Shadow Spell that applies two of the following random AoE affects, one to each team:
+100% Damage Charm
+100% Accuracy Charm
4 Pip Gain
(Boss' Allies Only) Restore 10% of target's Health
Due to this being a percentage heal, all charms, wards, and stats do not affect this.
-100% Damage Charm
-100% Accuracy Charm
4 Pip Loss
(Wizards Only) Remove 10% of target's Health
Due to this being percentage damage, all charms, wards, and stats do not affect this.
"This script makes no sense" - The Writer will Confuse one of his Minions for two Rounds.
"Time for a rewrite" - The Writer will Sacrifice one of his Minions and heal himself for 2,000 Health.
"Ugh, Fans!" - The Writer will cast Beguile on a Wizard.
spellNotes: Casts a protected and unprotected Mythblade.
strategy: Hit with Storm. He takes +5% from Storm and resists Myth 73%. His minions are wrapped in a -200% Brace ("Invulnerability") and can't be killed normally, so don't waste hits on them.
Expect three random minions on turn one; they get re-summoned when killed. Ignore them and tunnel the boss.

Ride the random cheat cycle. Every turn he throws two random AoE effects, one to each team. You might get +100% damage, +100% accuracy, or 4 pips, or eat -100% damage, -100% accuracy, 4-pip loss, or a flat 10% max-health hit. Keep blades stacked so a +100% damage round becomes your kill window, and re-blade if a -100% charm lands.
Plan for disruption. He Beguiles a random Wizard and can strip your setup. Protect the hitter's key blade with Aegis and keep a support able to hit if the main hitter gets Beguiled.

For the 62,125 health, run hitter, supports, and healer. Supports Feint and blade the hitter; the healer covers the 10% max-health chip and the 2,000 self-heals he gets from sacrificing his own minions. Burst him with one big Storm hit on a favorable cycle round.
cheatNotes: Latecomers get Confused for three rounds. Arrive on time.
He summons three random minions on turn one.
Killed minions come right back, so don't bother clearing them.
His minions are invulnerable and your damage does nothing to them. Focus the Writer only.
A minion killed and revived on the same turn briefly loses its invulnerability, but this isn't worth chasing.
When you kill the Writer his minions cast Furnace, so be ready to finish them or leave before it matters.
Every turn both teams get a random AoE effect. Some help you, some hurt, so build a buffer so a bad roll doesn't sink you.
A +100% Damage roll is your cue to hit. Stack blades so you can cash in immediately.
His side can heal 10% of health ignoring your infections, so expect the minions and boss to top up.
A -100% Damage roll can land on your team. Re-blade before hitting or your nuke does nothing.
A -100% Accuracy roll will fizzle your hit, so hold the finisher a round if it lands.
You can take a flat 10% max-health hit that no ward stops. Keep the team topped up.
He sacrifices his own minion to heal 2,000, so expect his health to tick back up if the fight drags.
He can Beguile a Wizard, turning them against the team. Protect the hitter's blades with Aegis and keep a backup hitter ready.

name: Thoth
locations: Krokotopia: Grand Exposition - The Great Lie-Brary
cheats: [Round] "Let me tell you a tale of one city..." - Thoth says this at the beginning of the first Round.

[Round] Story Cycle: Thoth will say a line that references one of the gods, and give Wizards two Aura Spells of different Schools. Casting the Aura that matches the School of the god mentioned will progress the story. At the end of the second Round, he will cheat-cast a Spell of the god's School.
[Aura] After a Wizard casts an Aura, one of the following will occur:

"Someone has been paying attention." - If a Wizard casts the correct Aura, they will take 0 Damage from the cheat-casted Spell. Then, Thoth will cast a version of Sacrifice, dealing 25% max Health Damage to himself.
"Hmm, lost my place." - If a Wizard fails to cast the correct Aura, Thoth will follow up with his Natural Attack, dealing 700 and 1300 Myth Damage.

[Spell] "Ra came down to smite the Moon Children..." - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. The next Round, he will cast a version of Ra, dealing 0 Balance Damage and applying a -45% Weakness blade.
[Next Verse (Balance)][Next Verse (Fire)]
Correct Answer: Balance
[Spell] "Shu's wrath shook the earth itself..." - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. Two Rounds later, he will cast an Ice version of Earthquake (525) that does not remove weaknesses.
[Next Verse (Ice)][Next Verse (Myth)]
Correct Answer: Ice

[Spell] "And thus did my tales rebirth history into mythology!" - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. Two Rounds later, he will cast a Myth version of Rebirth that restores 0 Health and applies a 600 Absorb Ward.
[Next Verse (Myth)][Next Verse (Life)]
Correct Answer: Myth
[Spell] "Heqet called her Sirens from the deep!" - Thoth will give all Wizards the following Spells. Two Rounds later, he will cast a version of Sirens, dealing 685 Storm Damage.
[Next Verse (Storm)][Next Verse (Ice)]
Correct Answer: Storm
It is not actually necessary to progress through Thoth's story to defeat him.
strategy: You do not have to play his story. Progressing Thoth's tale is optional, so the simplest line is to ignore the aura puzzle and just stack and burst him down.

If you do engage the story, cast the aura matching the mentioned god to nullify his cheat spell. A correct aura makes you take 0 damage from the incoming hit and forces Thoth to Sacrifice 25% of his own Health.
Use the correct answers: Balance for the Ra verse, Ice for the Shu verse, Myth for the rebirth verse, and Storm for the Heqet verse. Wrong auras let his natural attack (700 then 1300 Myth) through.
He is Myth with 69% Myth resist and takes 30% boosted Storm, so a Storm hitter or Storm prisms works best; his own cheat spells often deal 0 damage when answered correctly.
Since correct answers make him Sacrifice 25% of his Health repeatedly, you can let the puzzle chip him while you build a stack, then finish with one Storm hit.
Set up blades and Feint over a few rounds, answer his verses to avoid his Myth natural attack, and burst his 13,410 Health when your stack is ready.
cheatNotes: Each cycle he references a god and hands you two auras of different schools; casting the aura matching that god's school progresses the story and sets up the next step.
Casting the correct aura makes you take 0 damage from his cheat spell and forces Thoth to Sacrifice 25% of his own max Health.
Casting the wrong aura lets his natural attack through for 700 then 1300 Myth damage.
For the Ra verse he later casts Ra for 0 Balance damage plus a -45% Weakness blade.
The correct aura for the Ra verse is Balance.
For the Shu verse he later casts an Ice Earthquake (525) that does not remove weaknesses.
The correct aura for the Shu verse is Ice.
For the rebirth verse he later casts a Myth Rebirth restoring 0 Health with a 600 Absorb ward.
The correct aura for the rebirth verse is Myth.
For the Heqet verse he later casts Sirens for 685 Storm damage.
The correct aura for the Heqet verse is Storm.
You do not actually need to progress his story to win, so ignoring the puzzle and bursting him down is a valid line.

name: Thrym Winterhowl
cheats: [Charm] "If I be Storm, then may I be unrelenting!" - At the beginning of every Round, Thrym Winterhowl will interrupt-cast a version of Catch of the Day (Tier 2b) that gives him 2 +15% chromatic Blades on the Wizard in the first position. This increases by 5% each Round, up to a maximum of 40%.
[Healing] If Thrym Winterhowl is defeated while his Catch of the Day cheat is still active, he will revive with full Health.
[Spell] "Use this... stop my onslaught... for the sake of us both" - At the end of the 3rd Round, and after Thrym revives, he will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Ice Wyvern (Tier 2b)]
[Ice] "Sweet Ice, soothe mine fury" - If Ice Wyvern (Tier 2b) is used, Thrym Winterhowl will remove all Charms from himself and stop his Catch of the Day cheat.

"Nullified my new powers be" - Other Ice Damage Spells that remove Charms, such as Frost Giant (Tier 2b), will trigger Thrym Winterhowl to remove all Charms from himself and stop his Catch of the Day cheat.
strategy: He self-buffs every Round with Catch of the Day, gaining two chromatic blades that grow +5% each Round up to +40%; do not let him ramp for long.
Critically, if you defeat him while his Catch of the Day cheat is still active, he revives at full health, so you cannot simply burst him down.

At the end of Round 3 (and after any revive) he gives every wizard an Ice Wyvern card; cast that Ice Wyvern (or another Ice charm-remover like Frost Giant) to strip his blades and permanently stop the cheat.
Once his cheat is disabled with the Ice Wyvern he no longer revives; then finish him normally.
So: survive to Round 3, use the given Ice Wyvern to shut off his cheat, then kill him.
cheatNotes: Every Round he gains two chromatic blades that grow to +40%; his damage ramps fast, so shut the cheat down rather than trading hits.
If you kill him while Catch of the Day is still active, he revives at full health; you must disable the cheat first.
At the end of Round 3 and after any revive he hands each wizard an Ice Wyvern; this card is your key to stopping his cheat.
Casting the given Ice Wyvern strips his blades and permanently ends the Catch of the Day cheat, so he stops reviving; then kill him normally.
Other Ice charm-removers like Frost Giant also strip his blades and stop the cheat.

name: Thunderfin
cheats: "Preparing a big Spell?" - If a Wizard casts a Spell that uses more than 3 Pips, then Thunderfin will interrupt-cast a Fire version of Power Nova.
Item Cards that are given by Pets will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Storm boss (7790). Hit with Myth (+35%); avoid Storm (35% resist).
Keep every spell at 3 pips or fewer. Casting anything using more than 3 pips makes him interrupt with a Fire Power Nova on the team.
Stack cheap blades and traps, then hit with a low-pip (3 or under) Myth spell you have buffed up. Pet-given item cards do not trigger the nova, so a low-pip item hit is safe too.
cheatNotes: Any spell that uses more than 3 pips triggers a Fire Power Nova on the team. Keep your casts at 3 pips or under and rely on blades and traps to power a small hit.
Pet-given item cards do not trigger the Power Nova, so a low-pip item attack is a safe option.

name: Thundering Elf
cheats: At the end of every Round, Thundering Elf will cast Power Current on itself.
strategy: This is a 17,000-health raid boss that heals itself with Power Current at the end of every round, so bring Doom and Gloom and infections to cap its healing or your damage gets undone each round.
Attack with Myth (Storm's opposite) or your strongest school; no resist is listed.
Run a hitter with blade supports and a healer, funneling blades and bursting hard enough each cycle to outpace the self-heal.
Keep an infection or Doom refreshed before the hitter goes so the round's damage sticks.
Coordinate a big hit to push past the healing rather than trading chip damage.
cheatNotes: It casts Power Current on itself every round to heal, so keep Doom and Gloom or an infection up so your damage is not healed back.

name: Tiddalik
cheats: "The Tiddalik consumes. It's ever so sad.":
At the beginning of Round 1, and 3 Rounds after his Shield is removed, Tiddalik casts a Tower Shield (-90%) on himself.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward can not be used to remove the Shield. It will trigger the "That's not funny!" cheat.
If the Shield is broken by one of the above Spells or an All Enemies Spell, Tiddalik will recast it once.
If the Shield is broken by a single-target Damage Spell, Tiddalik will recast it twice.
Occasionally, if a Wizard has a Blade or Shield, Tiddalik will cast Steal Charm or Steal Ward, respectively.
"That's not funny!" - If a single-target Spell is cast on Tiddalik while he has a Shield, he will cast a Black Mantle on the caster.
"Hahahahahahahahah!" - If a Wizard fizzles, Tiddalik will cast a version of Colossafrog that deals no Damage, and then pushes all positive Charms and Wards to a random Wizard.
strategy: The whole fight is his -90% Tower Shield. He casts it on himself on Round 1 and re-casts it 3 rounds after it comes off, so you never hit into it directly.
Do not use Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward to remove the shield — those trigger his "That's not funny!" cheat instead of working.

Exploit the fizzle cheat: when any wizard fizzles, he pushes all positive charms and wards to a random wizard — including his own Tower Shield. Deliberately fizzle a low-accuracy spell to strip the shield off him for free.

If you'd rather break it with damage, use an All-Enemies (AoE) spell — he only re-casts the shield once. A single-target damage spell makes him re-cast it twice, so never chip at him with single hits.

Stack your blades while the shield is up, then break it (fizzle-strip or AoE) and hit the same or next round before the 3-round timer restores it. Hit with Storm — he takes +30% from it and resists nearly everything else; Myth is worst (71% resist).
Keep charms and shields off yourself when you can — he occasionally Steal Charms a blade or Steal Wards a shield right off you.
cheatNotes: He shields himself for -90% almost the whole fight, so raw hits do almost nothing until the shield is gone. The 3-round re-cast timer is your attack window.
The obvious shield-strippers backfire here. Casting Pierce, Shatter, or a Steal Ward on his shield sets off the "That's not funny!" punishment instead of removing it.
Breaking the shield with an AoE (or one of the forbidden strippers) is the cheaper option — he only puts it back once.
Breaking the shield with a single-target damage spell is the worst option — he immediately re-shields twice, doubling your problem.
If you walk in with a blade or shield already up, he may just steal it. Set up the turn you plan to hit rather than banking buffs early.
While he's shielded, any single-target spell on him earns the caster a Black Mantle (big accuracy debuff). Use All-Enemies spells, or expect to fizzle next turn.
This is the key cheat: a fizzle makes him shove every positive charm and ward — his Tower Shield included — onto a random wizard. Fizzling on purpose is the cleanest way to take his shield off him.

name: Tiki
cheats: Tiki
[Late] "You should be ashamed!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Tiki will cast Guiding Light on his teammate(s) and an Aegis version of Spirit Armor on a random ally.
[Damage over Time] "Let's spice things up!" - If a DoT is cast on anyone in the duel, he will cast Lifeblade, remove the DoT, and heal with Sprite Swarm (2 Pip).
Pet spells will trigger this cheat.
Ricky
[Late] "Cheater!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, then Ricky will triple-cast Healing Current on himself, healing 100, 400, then 1,000 Health.
[Blade] "Nice try!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade spell, then Ricky will cast Steal Charm on the Wizard that received the Blade.
Quench can be used to dispel this cheat.
Aegis can be used to prevent removal.
If a tri-Blade spell is used, then only the last part of the Blade will be removed (Fire for Elemental Blades and Life for Spirit Blades).
If a multi-target blade spell is used (e.g. Bladestorm), only the Wizard who cast the Spell will be affected.
Steal Charm can be used to steal back the Blade without a penalty.
Supercharge does not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Two bosses: Tiki (Life) heals and counters your damage-over-time, while Ricky steals your blades.

Cast a Blade and Ricky steals it off whoever received it; protect key blades with Aegis, or use Steal Charm to take it back without penalty. Supercharge does not trigger the steal, and on a tri-blade only the last part is taken.
Avoid DoTs, including pet spells: any damage-over-time makes Tiki Lifeblade, remove the effect, and heal with Sprite Swarm, undoing your work.
Join on time; late joins give Tiki free Guiding Light and Aegis Spirit Armor and let Ricky triple-heal up to 1,500.
Tiki resists Life 30% and takes 15% more from Death, so hit with Death, blade with Aegis-protected charms, and burst them down.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard lets Tiki cast Guiding Light on his team and an Aegis Spirit Armor on a random ally.
If any damage-over-time is cast, Tiki casts Lifeblade, removes the effect, and heals with a 2-pip Sprite Swarm.
Even pet spells trigger this damage-over-time cheat.
A late-joining wizard lets Ricky triple-cast Healing Current on himself, healing 100, 400, then 1,000.
Cast a Blade and Ricky casts Steal Charm on whoever received it.
Quench can dispel this steal cheat.
Aegis can prevent the blade from being removed.
On a tri-blade spell only the last part is stolen (Fire for Elemental, Life for Spirit).
On a multi-target blade like Bladestorm, only the casting wizard is affected.
Steal Charm can be used to take the blade back without penalty.
Supercharge does not trigger this steal cheat.

name: Tim-tim Snakeeye
cheats: Tim-tim Snakeeye will interrupt-cast a Tower Shield (-90%) at the start of the duel.
Tim-tim replaces this Shield by interrupt each time it is removed by Damage.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward can be used to safely remove the Tower Shield, and Tim-tim will not replace the Shield until he is damaged.
Tim-tim will not replace the Shield until after the end of a multi-hit attack, like Hydra.

"Feel better? Now I'll show you my stuff!" - If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will use Pierce and remove his Tower Shield. This indicates to hit the boss during the next Round, else he'll recast Tower Shield after that Round.
Pet Spells, Pet Talent Cards (e.g. Heart o'Pixie), and subsequent Rounds of Heal over Time Spells (e.g. Sprite) will not affect this cheat.
Although Pierce is not cast, it can be Dispelled.
strategy: He hides behind a -90% Tower Shield that he refreshes whenever damage removes it, so don't try to punch through it with raw hits.
Exploit his heal cheat: cast a Healing Spell and he strips his own Tower Shield (via Pierce), then hit him hard the very next round before he re-shields.
Alternatively Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward removes the Tower safely — he won't replace it until he's actually damaged.
Attack with Myth or Life (he takes +35% from both); never hit into his 80% Storm resist without a prism.
Stack blades, then land one clean single hit in the open window (avoid multi-hit spells that let him re-shield mid-attack).
cheatNotes: He opens with a -90% Tower Shield by interrupt — you can't meaningfully damage him through it.
He refreshes the Tower every time a hit breaks it, so remove it another way.
Pierce, Shatter, and Steal Ward remove the Tower safely — he won't re-shield until he's actually damaged.
Cast a Healing Spell and he strips his own Tower Shield — hit him the next round before he re-shields, the cleanest opening.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 1)
cheats: "You're late! Or are you early?" - Casts a Stun (Shadow) on any Wizard who joins the duel after the first Round.
Every two Rounds, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) will cast a Shadow version of Empower to give 1 Power Pip to all allies, and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the duel.
If a Feint is placed on Time Butterfly (Tier 1), then it will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast it.
If Time Butterfly (Tier 1) is defeated before or on the same turn that all of the Minions are defeated, then it will cast a Sun version of Blessing to restore 100% Health on all allies.
This Spell will revive any Minions defeated during the Round.
Minion Summon Cycle:
At the start of the battle, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) will cast a Shadow Spell to summon Young Morganthe (Tier 1). Occasionally, three of the Minions will be summoned.
On the following Round after Young Morganthe (Tier 1) has been defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) casts a Shadow Spell to summon Young Morganthe (Tier 1) and Roberto (Tier 1).

On the following Round after Young Morganthe (Tier 1) and Roberto (Tier 1) have been defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 1) casts a Shadow Spell to summon Young Morganthe (Tier 1), Roberto (Tier 1) and Broodmother (Tier 1).

On the first Round, three Young Morganthe (Tier 1) will be summoned instead of just one. In this case, the Stage 2 summon will be omitted, and the Round after the three Minions are defeated, the Stage 3 summon will occur.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+20%); it resists Myth 50%.

Kill order matters: if the Butterfly dies before or the same round as its minions, it Blessings the whole side back to full and revives them. Clear all minions first, then kill the Butterfly a round later.
Don't single-Feint it — it Swap Wards your traps onto you; use Mass Feint or Indemnity.
It's very low health (1,915), so hold your hit on it until the minions are already down.
AoE the minions, then finish the Butterfly cleanly on a later round.
cheatNotes: Late wizards get Stunned — join on time.
Every two rounds it Empowers its side (+1 power pip) and secretly switches turn order.
Single Feint on it makes it Swap Ward your traps onto you — use Mass Feint or Indemnity.
If it dies before or the same round as its minions, it Blessings everyone back to full health — kill it last.
That Blessing also revives any minions defeated during the round.
It opens by summoning Young Morganthe, sometimes three minions at once.
After Young Morganthe dies it summons Young Morganthe and Roberto.
After those die it summons Young Morganthe, Roberto, and Broodmother.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 2)
cheats: Time Butterfly (Tier 2) will cast a Shadow version of Empower on itself to gain 1 Power Pip and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the duel.
If a Feint is placed on Time Butterfly, he will cast Swap Ward to switch the Traps so that the higher percentage is on the Wizard.
Minions:

Time Butterfly (Tier 2) will summon Minions starting with Young Morganthe (Tier 2) on the first turn. If Young Morganthe (Tier 2) is defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 2), both Young Morganthe (Tier 2) and Roberto (Tier 2) will be summoned on the following turn. If Young Morganthe (Tier 2) and Roberto (Tier 2) are defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 2) will resummon both of them, and also Broodmother (Tier 2) on the following turn.

On the first round, three Young Morganthe (Tier 1) will be summoned instead of just one. In this case, the Stage 2 summon will be omitted and the round after the three minions are defeated, the Stage 3 summon will occur.
"You're Late, or Are You Early?" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Time Butterfly (Tier 2) will use Stun that will last two turns.
"Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 2) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects four Power Pips or more.
Young Morganthe:
"Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 2) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects more than 7 Pips.
"No More of That!" - If a Ward is used, she'll use Power Nova, followed by Earthquake dealing 400 Myth damage to all enemies, and 100 Fire Damage to self.
Broodmother:
"Give me your health, drone!" - Broodmother (Tier 2) will cast Cheat to steal 15% max Health from Time Butterfly and restores 15% of its max Health
Sometimes after this cheat, a Dragonblade is cast on a random ally, and Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) (-55%) to all allies. "Need to verify which Round this trigger happens."
strategy: He takes +20% from Storm and resists Myth 60%. Hit with Storm.
Do not hoard pips: Young Morganthe Mana Burns any wizard with 4+ power pips (or more than 7 pips). Spend pips as you go rather than banking a huge attack.
Do not Feint him; he Swap Wards it back onto you.
Do not use wards: a shield makes Young Morganthe cast Power Nova then Earthquake (400 Myth AoE). Rely on hitting fast instead of defending.
He summons escalating minions (Young Morganthe, then Roberto, then Broodmother). Broodmother drains health from Time Butterfly to itself. AoE the board with Storm and keep pip counts low.
cheatNotes: He Empowers himself and hidden-casts a turn-order switch on the opening. Do not assume you move first.
A Feint on him gets Swap Warded onto you. Skip Feint here.
He summons in stages: Young Morganthe first, then Roberto, then Broodmother. Expect the board to escalate.
On Round 1 he may summon three Young Morganthes at once, skipping the stage-2 summon. Be ready for a crowded board early.
Late arrival gets a 2-turn Stun. Zone in on time.
Young Morganthe Mana Burns any wizard sitting on 4+ power pips. Do not bank pips; spend them.
She also Mana Burns anyone holding more than 7 pips. Keep your pip count low and hit steadily.
Using a ward makes her Power Nova then Earthquake for 400 Myth to all. Avoid shields; race the board down instead.
Broodmother drains 15% of Time Butterfly's max health to itself. Kill Time Butterfly before the Broodmother stage if you can.
She sometimes follows with a Dragonblade on an ally and a team Tower Shield. Expect the minions to buff up.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 3)
cheats: Every two Rounds, Time Butterfly (Tier 3) will cast a Shadow version of Empower to give 1 Power Pip to all allies, and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the duel.
Minions:

Time Butterfly (Tier 3) will summon Minions starting with Young Morganthe (Tier 3) on the first turn. If Young Morganthe (Tier 3) is defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 3), both Young Morganthe (Tier 3) and Roberto (Tier 3) will be summoned on the following turn. If Young Morganthe (Tier 3) and Roberto (Tier 3) are defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 3) will resummon both of them, and also Broodmother (Tier 3) on the following turn.
"You're Late, or Are You Early?" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Time Butterfly (Tier 3) will use Stun that will last two turns.
"Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 3) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects four Power Pips or more.
Young Morganthe:
"Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 3) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects more than 7 Pips.
"No More of That!" - If a Ward is used, she'll use Power Nova, followed by Earthquake dealing 400 Myth damage to all enemies, and 100 Fire Damage to self.
Broodmother:
"Give me your health, drone!" - Every 3 Rounds Broodmother (Tier 3) will cast Cheat to steal 15% max Health from Time Butterfly and restores 15% of its max Health
Sometimes after this cheat, a Dragonblade is cast on a random ally, and Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) (-55%) to all allies.
strategy: Do not stockpile pips. Young Morganthe Mana Burns any wizard holding 4+ power pips (or 7+ total). Spend pips as you go and hit promptly.
Do not cast Wards or shields. A ward makes Young Morganthe Power Nova and Earthquake (400 Myth) that also strips your hanging effects. Set up with traps on the boss and hit the same round.
Hit with Storm (+20% incoming); avoid Myth (60% resist).
Once Broodmother is up, she heals the Butterfly 15% every 3 rounds. Focus the Butterfly (only 5570 health) down before that matters.
Join on time to avoid the 2-turn stun.
cheatNotes: Every 2 rounds it gives its whole side a power pip and secretly flips who goes first, so expect turn order to swap.
It summons Young Morganthe first, then escalates to Roberto and Broodmother as you kill minions, so clearing minions summons tougher ones.
Joining late gets you stunned for two turns.
Young Morganthe Mana Burns any wizard sitting on 4+ power pips. Do not hoard pips.
She also Mana Burns a wizard holding more than 7 pips total. Spend before you overflow.
Casting a Ward makes her Power Nova then Earthquake (400 Myth), wiping your hanging effects. Avoid shields and time traps with your hit.
Once up, Broodmother heals the Butterfly 15% of its max health every 3 rounds. Burst the boss before this stacks.
She sometimes follows that with a Dragonblade on an ally and a -55% Mass Tower Shield on her side.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 4)
cheats: [Late]"You're Late, or Are You Early?" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will use Stun (Shadow) that will last two turns.
[Manipulation] Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will cast a Mass Shadow version of Empower on itself and its allies to gain 1 Power Pip and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the battle.
[Feint] If a Feint is placed on Time Butterfly (Tier 4), then it will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast it.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
[Minion] Minions:

Time Butterfly (Tier 4) will summon Minions starting with Young Morganthe (Tier 4) on the first turn. If Young Morganthe (Tier 4) is defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 4), both Young Morganthe (Tier 4) and Roberto (Tier 4) will be summoned on the following turn. If Young Morganthe (Tier 4) and Roberto (Tier 4) are defeated, Time Butterfly (Tier 4) will resummon both of them, and also Broodmother (Tier 4) on the following turn.
[Death] Young Morganthe:
[Pip] "Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 3) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects more than 7 Pips.
[Ward] "No More of That!" - If a Ward is used, she'll use Power Nova, followed by Earthquake dealing 400 Myth damage to all enemies, and 100 Fire Damage to self.
[Fire] Broodmother:
[Steal] "Give me your health, drone!" - Every 3 Rounds Broodmother (Tier 3) will cast Cheat to steal 15% max Health from Time Butterfly and restores 15% of its max Health
Sometimes after this cheat, a Dragonblade is cast on a random ally, and Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) (-55%) to all allies.
strategy: Hit Storm, +10%, and avoid Myth, which it resists 65%; prism if you are Myth.
Use Mass Feint, not a single Feint. A lone Feint gets Swap Warded back onto you, or protect it with Indemnity or dispel with Melt.
Keep your pip count at or below 7. Young Morganthe Mana Burns anyone who banks more, and do not cast shields while she is up, since they trigger her Earthquake.
Do not chase the summons. Killing them just brings more back, so funnel setup onto the Butterfly and burst it.
Do not slow-play. Broodmother heals the boss 15% every three Rounds.
cheatNotes: A late Wizard gets a two-turn Shadow Stun. Come in on Round 1.
It casts a Mass Shadow Empower for a Power Pip and hidden-switches first turn, so expect turn order to flip.
Placing a single Feint on it makes it Swap Ward the traps back onto the caster, so your Feint lands on you instead.
Melt can dispel that Swap Ward cheat.
Mass Feint does not trigger the swap, so use Mass Feint here.
Indemnity on your Feint also prevents the swap.
It summons Young Morganthe first; kill her and both she and Roberto return, then killing both brings all three back plus Broodmother. Do not chase the minions; focus the Butterfly.
Young Morganthe Mana Burns any Wizard who banks more than 7 pips. Hit before you overstock.
Any ward you cast makes her Power Nova, then Earthquake for 400 Myth to all enemies. Avoid shielding while she is up.
Every three Rounds Broodmother steals 15% Health from the Butterfly to heal itself.
That cheat is sometimes followed by a Dragonblade on an ally and a Mass -55% Tower Shield on their side.

name: Time Butterfly (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Doomsday Krok Gauntlet - Doomsday Krok Gauntlet (Tier 5)
cheats: "You're Late, or Are You Early?" - If a Wizard joins the battle late, Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will use Stun (Shadow) that will last two turns.
Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will cast a Mass Shadow version of Empower on itself and its allies to gain 1 Power Pip and hidden-casts a Spell that switches the first turn of the battle.
If a Feint is placed on Time Butterfly (Tier 5), then it will cast an Ice version of Swap Ward to switch the Traps with the Wizard who cast it.
Melt can be used to dispel this cheat.
Mass Feint will not trigger this cheat.
Indemnity can be used to prevent this cheat.
Minions:

Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will summon Minions cast as Shadow, starting with Young Morganthe (Tier 5) on the first turn. Occasionally, there will be three Minions summoned on the first turn rather than just one, in which case all three Minions will be resummoned once.

If Time Butterfly (Tier 5) is defeated before or on the same turn that all of the Minions are defeated, then it will revive itself and all of the Minions by casting a Mass Sun version of Blessing that restores 100% Health.

If all Minions in battle are defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 5), then Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will re-summon the defeated Minions along with the next Minion with a Shadow Spell on the following turn. The Minions are summoned in order:
Example: (Morganthe, Morganthe & Roberto, Morganthe, Roberto, and Broodmother)
Young Morganthe (Tier 5)
Roberto (Tier 5)
Broodmother (Tier 5)
When all three Minions have been defeated before Time Butterfly (Tier 5), then Time Butterfly (Tier 5) will stop respawning itself and its allies.
If Time Butterfly (Tier 5) is defeated on any turn it summons Minions, then it will not revive itself. It is unclear if this is a bug or not.
Young Morganthe:
"Time for a New Strategy" - Young Morganthe (Tier 5) will cast Mana Burn on a Wizard who collects more than 7 Pips.
"No More of That!" - If a Ward is used, she'll use Power Nova, followed by Earthquake dealing 400 Myth damage to all enemies, and 100 Fire Damage to self.
Broodmother:

"Give me your health, drone!" - Broodmother (Tier 5) will cast Cheat to steal 15% max Health from Time Butterfly and restore 15% of its max Health, then a Dragonblade to a random ally, and Mass Tower Shield (Shadow) (-55%) to all allies. "Need to verify which Round this trigger happens."
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Myth Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+5%), avoid Myth (70% resist). This is a revive puzzle, so kill order is everything.
You must defeat all three minions (Young Morganthe, Roberto, Broodmother) before the Time Butterfly. If the Butterfly dies while any minion lives, it revives itself and all minions to full health.
Kill the minions first and save the Butterfly for last, it stops respawning only once all three minions are already dead.
Keep your pips under 7 (Young Morganthe Mana Burns anyone who hoards 7+) and do not cast wards (she answers with Power Nova and a 400-Myth Earthquake).
Use Mass Feint or an Indemnity/Melt-protected Feint (plain Feint gets Swap-Warded onto you), kill Broodmother early since she heals the Butterfly, then burst the Butterfly last.
cheatNotes: Join late and it Stuns you for two turns.
It Mass-Empowers itself and its minions for a power pip and secretly swaps who goes first, expect turn order to flip.
Plain Feint gets Swap-Warded onto you, the traps move to the caster.
Melt dispels the Swap Ward cheat.
Mass Feint does not trigger the swap, use it to trap safely.
Indemnity also protects your Feint from the swap.
It summons Shadow minions starting with Young Morganthe, sometimes three at once (resummoned once).
Kill the Butterfly while any minion lives and it revives itself and all minions to full health, minions must die first.
If all minions die before the Butterfly, it re-summons the fallen ones plus the next in order.
Once all three minions are dead before the Butterfly, it stops respawning, then finish the Butterfly.
If the Butterfly dies on a turn it summons minions, it will not revive (possibly a bug).
Young Morganthe Mana Burns any wizard holding more than 7 pips, stay under 7.
Cast a ward and she answers with Power Nova and a 400-Myth Earthquake on the team, skip shields.
Broodmother steals 15% health to heal the Butterfly, gives an ally a Dragonblade, and Mass Tower Shields the minions, kill her early to stop the healing.

name: Titan's Trident (Challenge)
cheats: On the first Round, Titan's Trident will interrupt-cast an Equalize Global which sets the Damage cap to 949,999. The Trident will recast the Global when it is replaced.
Joining the battle late (after Round 1) will cause the Storm Titan to interrupt-cast Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 2,250) on the Wizard, and summons a random Refraction of the following.
Deadly Ice Refraction
Mythic Fire Refraction
Stormy Life Refraction
The Storm Titan will pull up the Trident and slam it down randomly, removing all unprotected Wards and Charms from itself.

The Storm Titan will randomly laugh at the end of a Round, and at the end of the following Round he will shoot lasers from his eyes, dealing 1,375 Shadow Damage to all and removing a Shadow Pip from all Wizards.

The Storm Titan will pull up the Trident at the end of the Round that it gets its fifth Shadow Pip, which will render the Trident untargetable. While in this state, the Boss can only pass or cast Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 2,250). At the end of the third Round after it was pulled up, the Storm Titan will swing the Trident, dealing 5,500 Shadow Damage to all Wizards, and return it to the battle.
While the Trident is gone from the battle, the Storm Titan can still laugh, and subsequently use his laser eye attack the following Round.

Mellori - "Aww, you gotta be kidding me! The Paradox Chain just keeps charging him back up! Look out!" - After taking 20,000 Damage, the Storm Titan will emerge at the end of the Round and completely restore the Trident's health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
When the Trident is healed, it summons Refractions depending on the number of Wizards present. (Confirmation needed)
Mellori - "The Paradox Chain just keeps recharging him. This is hopeless!"
