Bat - "No it's not! I know what we have to do now! Wizard, just keep attacking!" - After taking another 20,000 Damage, the Storm Titan will again completely restore the Trident's health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
The Trident's healing will summon more Refractions.
After the Trident takes 20,000 Damage for a third time, a cutscene will occur where Mellori breaks the Paradox Chain, destroying the Trident, and defeating the Boss.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Stormspear and Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).

Glowbug Squall (Shadow, 2,250) is not a Glowbug Squall, but rather the Trident's special move. When cast, the Storm Titan appears behind a Wizard and squeezes them, dealing massive Damage. Despite what the Spell text indicates, this is a single-target Spell.
strategy: Ignore the million health. You win by dealing 20,000 damage to the Trident three separate times; each time the Storm Titan heals it via the Paradox Chain, but the third 20,000 burst triggers the cutscene kill. Bring a full team.
Hit with Ice or Death (+25%; he is immune to Fire and Life, resists Storm, Myth, Balance, and Shadow 50%). Build big blade-stacked bursts of 20,000+.
Manage the laser cycle: when the Titan laughs, next round he lasers 1375 Shadow to all and strips a shadow pip, so heal to full before it.

When he reaches his fifth shadow pip he pulls the Trident up (untargetable) and swings for 5500 Shadow to all three rounds later, so heal through and wait for the Trident to return before your next burst.
Everyone join Round 1 (late joiners eat Glowbug Squall and spawn a Refraction); the hitter funnels blades and lands the three 20k bursts.
cheatNotes: An Equalize global caps damage at 949,999 and is recast if removed, which is irrelevant since you win via 20,000-damage bursts, not raw health.
Late joiners eat a 2250 Glowbug Squall and spawn a Refraction, so join on Round 1.
He randomly slams the Trident, stripping all unprotected wards and charms off himself, so protect your Feints.
When he laughs, the next round he lasers all wizards for 1375 Shadow and strips a shadow pip, so heal to full first.
At his fifth shadow pip the Trident goes untargetable and swings for 5500 Shadow to all three rounds later, so heal through and wait for it to return before bursting.
He can still laser while the Trident is gone, so keep healing during that window.
Dealing 20,000 damage makes him heal the Trident but counts as your first of three needed bursts.
Healing the Trident summons more Refractions, so keep AoE for adds.
Your second 20,000-damage burst heals him again but is the second of three, so keep attacking.
The third 20,000-damage burst triggers the cutscene that destroys the Trident and wins, so aim for three big bursts.

name: Titan's Trident (Rematch, Challenge)
cheats: [Storm][Aura] - At the beginning of the first Round, the Titan's Trident will cast a Storm's Protection Aura on itself that greatly reduces all incoming Damage.
[Death][Ice]
"The storm stirs with Death and Ice magic." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Death or Ice Damage, it will say this.
"Death and Ice fuse into a roil." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Death or Ice Damage for a second time, it will say this.
"A refraction emerges from the storm." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Death or Ice Damage for a third time, it will summon a Deadly Ice Refraction (Rematch, Challenge).
At the beginning of the first Round that the Deadly Ice Refraction is in combat, it will cast Mass Weakness.
At the beginning of the third Round that the Deadly Ice Refraction is in combat, it will cast a Shield on itself that limits all incoming Damage to 50.

When the Deadly Ice Refraction is defeated, the Titan's Trident will replace its Aura with a Death's Refraction Aura on itself and cast a version of Scion of Ice on itself that deals 24,999 Damage. It will then re-cast its Storm's Protection Aura on itself.
[Fire][Myth]
"The storm stirs with Fire and Myth magic." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Fire or Myth Damage, it will say this.
"Fire and Myth fuse into a roil." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Fire or Myth Damage for a second time, it will say this.
"A refraction emerges from the storm." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Fire or Myth Damage for a third time, it will summon a Mythic Fire Refraction (Rematch, Challenge).
At the beginning of the first Round that the Mythic Fire Refraction is in combat, it will cast Mass Fire Trap.
At the beginning of the second Round that the Mythic Fire Refraction is in combat, it will cast Fire Dragon.

When the Mythic Fire Refraction is defeated, the Titan's Trident will replace its Aura with a Myth's Refraction Aura on itself and cast a version of Scion of Fire on itself that deals 24,999 Damage. It will then re-cast its Storm's Protection Aura on itself.
[Storm][Life]
"The storm stirs with Storm and Life magic." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Storm or Life Damage, it will say this.
"Storm and Life fuse into a roil." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Storm or Life Damage for a second time, it will say this.
"A refraction emerges from the storm." - When the Titan's Trident is damaged by Storm or Life Damage for a third time, it will summon a Stormy Life Refraction (Rematch, Challenge).
After the Stormy Life Refraction casts a Spell, it will cast Balanceblade (+35%) on itself.

When the Stormy Life Refraction is defeated, the Titan's Trident will replace its Aura with a Life's Refraction Aura on itself and cast a version of Scion of Storm on itself that deals 24,999 Damage. It will then re-cast its Storm's Protection Aura on itself.
[Aura] "The storm breaks." - When all three Scion Spells have been cast, the Titan's Trident will remove its Storm's Protection Aura and become vulnerable to Damage.
At the beginning of the Round after all Minions have been defeated, the Titan's Trident will cast Shadow Trap on all enemies, or Spirit Armor (500) on itself.
spellNotes: Casts a protected version of Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow).
strategy: This is a 99,999-health raid boss protected by a damage-reduction aura - bring a full team. You cannot simply burst it; you win by triggering and killing its Refractions.

The Titan's Storm's Protection Aura caps your damage from round 1. Instead of hitting through it, use paired-school hits to spawn Refractions: three Death or Ice hits summon the Deadly Ice Refraction, three Fire or Myth hits summon the Mythic Fire Refraction, three Storm or Life hits summon the Stormy Life Refraction.
Kill each Refraction. Every Refraction death forces the Titan to Scion itself for 24,999 - three kills is roughly its full 99,999 bar.

Manage the Refraction cheats as they come: Deadly Ice opens with Mass Weakness then a 50-damage-cap shield, Mythic Fire opens with Mass Fire Trap then Fire Dragon, and Stormy Life self-blades after every spell. Have your healer clear weaknesses and cover the Fire Dragon.

After all three Scions have self-cast, the Storm's Protection drops and the Titan finally takes full damage. Blade up during the refraction phase so you can finish it in the vulnerable window before it re-shields.
cheatNotes: On round 1 the Titan raises a Storm's Protection Aura that heavily reduces all incoming damage.
Hitting it with Death or Ice damage once triggers this warning line - the first of three needed to spawn its Refraction.
A second Death or Ice hit triggers the second warning line.
A third Death or Ice hit summons the Deadly Ice Refraction.
That Refraction opens with Mass Weakness on your team.
On its third combat round it shields itself, capping all incoming damage at 50.
Killing it makes the Titan self-cast Scion of Ice for 24,999, swap to a Death's Refraction Aura, then re-raise Storm's Protection.
Hitting it with Fire or Myth damage once triggers this warning line.
A second Fire or Myth hit triggers the second warning line.
A third Fire or Myth hit summons the Mythic Fire Refraction.
That Refraction opens with Mass Fire Trap.
On its second combat round it casts Fire Dragon.
Killing it makes the Titan self-cast Scion of Fire for 24,999, swap to a Myth's Refraction Aura, then re-raise Storm's Protection.
Hitting it with Storm or Life damage once triggers this warning line.
A second Storm or Life hit triggers the second warning line.
A third Storm or Life hit summons the Stormy Life Refraction.
After it casts any spell it self-blades with a +35% Balanceblade.
Killing it makes the Titan self-cast Scion of Storm for 24,999, swap to a Life's Refraction Aura, then re-raise Storm's Protection.
Once all three Scions have been cast, the Storm's Protection drops and the Titan finally takes full damage - burst it now.
The round after all minions are gone, the Titan casts Shadow Trap on your team or Spirit Armor (500) on itself.

name: Titan's Trident (Standard)
cheats: On the first Round, Titan's Trident will cast an Equalize Global which sets the Damage cap to 7,999, and will recast it when replaced.
Unbalance can dispel this cheat.
If a Wizard uses a Nightbringer Enchanted Attack and changes the Global the next Round, the Global will not be changed back until after the next hit.

Mellori - "Aww, you gotta be kidding me! The Paradox Chain just keeps charging him back up! Look out!" - After taking more than 7,750 Damage, the Storm Titan will emerge at the end of the Round and completely restore the Trident's Health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
Mellori - "The Paradox Chain just keeps recharging him. This is hopeless!"

Bat - "No it's not! I know what we have to do now! Wizard, just keep attacking!" - After taking another 7,750 Damage, the Storm Titan will again completely restore the Trident's Health by grasping the Paradox Chain.
After the Trident takes 7,750 Damage for a third time, a cutscene will occur where Mellori breaks the Paradox Chain, destroying the Trident, and defeating the Boss.
spellNotes: Casts a protected Stormspear and Reaperblade (Storm-Shadow)
strategy: Bring two hitters plus two support. The primary hitter uses a big Damage-over-Time spell (Fire Heckhound, Ice Snow Angel, Death Skeletal Dragon, Life Spinysaur, Myth King Artorius, or a Storm Thunderstorm item card); the secondary hitter is the minion killer.

The Trident fully heals each time it takes 7,750 damage and you must trigger that heal three separate times before the third crossing destroys it, so a single burst is useless. A large DoT crosses one threshold per tick and defeats it over three rounds.

The Round 1 Equalize Global caps damage at 7,999, so you cannot out-burst it. Either live with the cap or dispel it with Unbalance; a Nightbringer-enchant trick also lets one hit ignore the cap before it resets.

Spend Rounds 1-3 stacking 13+ buffs on the primary hitter (tri-blades regular/sharp/TC/item, school blade, Dark Pact or Balanceblade, Elemental/Spirit blade, a damage aura), aiming for 40,000+ total DoT damage.
Round 4 the primary hitter lands the DoT; its ticks trigger the three Paradox Chain restores over the following rounds and the third defeats the Trident.
Myth minions spawn after the first DoT tick with shields and Earthquakes. The secondary hitter clears them (a Storm wizard needs only two blades for Epic Sirens or Glowbug Squall).

Hitters hold 45%+ global resist, support 60%+; recast Brace/Fortify each round before the trident swings, use Death's Indemnity Juju since it is the only buff that survives the trident attack, and Storm hitters must Prism through the 70% Storm resist.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 it casts an Equalize Global that caps all damage at 7,999 per hit, and recasts it if you replace it, so you cannot one-shot the boss.
You can remove that damage-cap Global by dispelling it with Unbalance.
If you use a Nightbringer-enchanted attack and then change the Global the following round, it will not revert until after your next hit lands, letting you sneak a hit under a friendlier Global.

After it takes more than 7,750 damage, the Storm Titan surfaces at end of round and fully heals the Trident by grasping the Paradox Chain, so you must cross that threshold three times rather than burst it once.
After another 7,750 damage the Titan fully heals the Trident a second time; a DoT ticking each round naturally supplies these repeated thresholds.
The third time the Trident takes 7,750 damage, a cutscene plays where Mellori breaks the Paradox Chain and the boss is defeated.

guide.title: Empyrea Storm Titan Strategy Guide and Speed Run

name: Tree Root (Death)
cheats: "Chop Away A Little Each Time!" - When a Tree Root is defeated, one of the surviving Bosses will spawn an Elite Minion matching its school listed below in three Rounds:
Tree Root (Death Elite)
Tree Root (Balance Elite)
Tree Root (Myth Elite)
Tree Root (Life Elite)
strategy: Four Tree Roots (Death, Life, Myth, Balance); hit with Ice or Life (+35%) and avoid Death (80% resist on the Death root).
Killing any Tree Root makes a surviving one spawn an Elite Minion of the dead root's school three Rounds later, so every kill creates a delayed reinforcement.
Use AoE to damage all four together rather than picking them off one at a time, aiming to end the fight before the Elite reinforcements arrive.
Stack universal blades and a Feint, then deliver one big AoE (Ice or Life) to drop multiple roots at once.
If you must go one at a time, plan for the Elite that spawns three Rounds after each kill.
cheatNotes: Killing any Tree Root makes a survivor spawn an Elite Minion of that school three Rounds later; every pick-off adds a reinforcement, so favor AoE and finish fast.

name: Treemugger
cheats: "More Wizards sprouting? Such a tree thing to do" If a Wizard joins the battle after the first Round, Treemugger casts Stun (Shadow) on the late Wizard.

"I will break this, with my rage!" - At the start of the duel, Treemugger will use a Shadow Spell to Remove all Pips from all Wizards, impose a 99-Round version of Impede, and use Blinding Light (85%).
"One-two PUNCH!" - At the end of every Odd Round, Treemugger will say this.
"Three-four PUNCH!" - At the end of every Even Round, Treemugger will say this.
In both these stages he will remove all existing Pips and use individual Donate Power Spells to sequentially increase the number of Pips based on the number of Rounds which have elapsed.
For example, on the first Round, each Wizard is given one white Pip; second Round, two white Pips, etc., until each Wizard is given seven Power Pips.
During the stage when Power Pips are given, even though all Pips get removed during the animation, the single pips return back to the Wizard when the next Round starts.

"Make like a tree and fall down!" - If a Wizard doesn't have an Impede active on them and any Wizard in the fight casts any spell, Treemugger will cast the 99-Round Impede on all Wizards without an Impede on them.
Replacing the Aura will allow the Wizard to regain Pips, until any Wizard casts another spell, repeating this cheat.
strategy: Life boss (11650). Hit with Death (+5%); never Life (70% resist).

Round 1 he wipes your pips, applies a 99-round Impede aura, and Blinding Lights you. He then donates escalating pips each round (one more per round, up to 7 power pips), so you build toward a big hit even though Impede blocks normal gain.
Ride the donated pips: by the later rounds you will have 7 power pips. Stack a blade or two and unload a Death hit when your pip count peaks.
Do not waste turns clearing the Impede aura; casting a spell just makes him re-apply it. Bring accuracy for the Blinding Light so your hit does not fizzle.
cheatNotes: Late joiners get Stunned. Be in on round 1.
Round 1 he strips your pips, applies a 99-round Impede (chokes pip gain), and Blinding Lights you. Bring accuracy and plan around donated pips.
On odd rounds he resets and donates pips based on the round number, so your pips grow each cycle toward 7 power pips.
Same on even rounds; he keeps donating an increasing number of pips. Save them for one big hit.
He removes then re-donates pips sequentially each round, so you gain more the longer it goes. Time your hit for the peak.
If anyone lacks the Impede aura and any wizard casts, he re-applies Impede to all. Do not bother stripping it.
Replacing the aura lets you regain pips briefly, until any wizard casts and he re-Impedes. Not worth chasing; just use donated pips.

name: Trengil Hoarder
cheats: [Late] "*Eyes Glower*" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, he will cast Steal Pips (Moon) stealing 1 Pip from the Wizard.

[Blade][Shield] "*Eyes Widen*" / "*Eyes Shine*" - At the end of every Round if a positive Charm or Ward is cast, the Trengil Hoarder will cast an AoE Steal Charm (Shadow) that steals one positive Charm (including Pierce Charms), or an AoE Steal Ward (Shadow) that steals one Shield from all Wizards.
Traps and Stun Block will trigger the AoE Steal Ward (Shadow).
Using Steal Charm and Steal Ward on Trengil Hoarder won't trigger this cheat.
Aegis can be used to prevent the steal.
[Shadow][Manipulation] After casting his natural attack, Trengil Hoarder will use Steal Charm (Shadow) and Steal Ward (Shadow) on it's target.
[Shadow][Manipulation] "*Eyes Sparkle*" - If the Trengil Hoarder casts Bladestorm or Legion Shield, he will cast Steal Charm (Shadow) or Steal Ward (Shadow) from his Minion.
strategy: Hit with Fire (+15%); he is Ice and resists Ice 30%.
At the end of every Round, if any Charm or Ward was cast, he steals one from all Wizards (Traps and Stun Blocks count as Wards). Don't leave buffs sitting out.
Protect your key blade with Aegis, or blade and hit in the same round before the end-of-Round steal.
His Natural Attack also steals a Charm and Ward from its target, so re-buff only right before you swing.
Stack an Aegis-protected Fireblade, then finish him in one hit.
cheatNotes: Joining late costs you 1 Pip to a Moon Steal Pips.
At the end of each Round, if any Charm or Ward was cast, he steals one from every Wizard. Blade and hit the same round or use Aegis.
Traps and Stun Blocks count as Wards and trigger the AoE Steal Ward.
Casting Steal Charm or Steal Ward on him does not trigger the cheat.
Aegis protects your charm or ward from the steal.
His Natural Attack also steals a Charm and a Ward from its target.
When he casts Bladestorm or Legion Shield, he pulls a Charm or Ward steal from his minion.

name: Trickster Jack
cheats: [Pet][Shield] "Protect Me!" His Pet Wartle will cast a Spirit Shield Item Card.
strategy: His pet Wartle throws up a Spirit Shield that blocks Myth, Life, and Death, so hit him with an Elemental school (Fire, Ice, or Storm) or break the shield first.
He resists Balance 30% and takes a little extra Myth, Life, and Death, but the Spirit Shield counters those, so Elemental hits are cleaner.
Clear the Shadow Shrike and Shadow Sentinel with your setup, then AoE all three; the boss has 12,640 health.
cheatNotes: His pet Wartle casts a Spirit Shield item card on him, blocking Myth, Life, and Death hits.

name: Tse-Tse Snaketail (Challenge)
cheats: "I am the master of disguise!" - At the end of the 1st Round, Tse-Tse Snaketail will Polymorph into one of the following listed below.
"Curses! Let's try another guise" - After Tse-Tse Snaketail is defeated the first time, he will cast a version of Blessing that restores 100% of his health. This cheat only activates once.
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tim-tim Snakeeye (Challenge) - Elephant Polymorph Transformation
After Polymorphing, Tim-tim Snakeeye will cast an Aura that limits incoming damage on himself to a max of 100 for 99 Rounds.

"An elephant never forgets, do you?" - At the beginning of the 2nd Round, and every 2 Rounds after, Tim-tim Snakeeye will cast 3 different Blades on himself. This indicates the order of which schools attacks he will need to be hit by. The 3 Blades will always be one of the following possibilities:
[Death] [Life] [Myth]
[Fire] [Ice] [Storm]
[Balance] [Myth] [Ice]
The progress made into finishing the 3 hits will not be reset when Tim-tim Snakeeye recasts the Blades every 2 Rounds.
"You Remembered!" - Tim-tim Snakeeye will say this after each successful hit in the code.
"Your memory is perfect!" - After getting the code correct, Tim-tim Snakeeye will replace his damage limit aura with Frenzy.
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tom-tom Snakeskin (Challenge) - Rhino Polymorph Transformation

"This is gonna hurt!" - At the beginning of the first Round of this Polymorph, Tom-tom Snakeskin will cast a +15% per Pip version of Supercharge, a +10% per Pip version of Supercharge and a +35% Stormblade on himself.
"Charge!" - At the end of the first Round, Tom-tom Snakeskin will cast a non-dispellable version of Storm Owl (Tier 5a).
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tik-tik Snaketongue (Challenge) - Lion Polymorph Transformation
"Just a little catnap..." - If Tik-tik Snaketongue receives any damage during this Polymorph, he will cast a version of Cat Nap that restores 100% max health. This cheat only activates once.
[Enemy][Polymorph] Tse-Tse Snaketail (Challenge) - Spider Polymorph Transformation (3 [Round])
At the end of each Round, Tse-Tse Snaketail will cast a unique +30% Shadowblade that stacks with all of his other Shadowblades.

"The Shadow Queen sends her regards!" - At the end of the third Round of this Polymorph, Tse-Tse Snaketail will cast Dark Fiend and a version of Sacrifice on himself that deals 100% damage and restores no health, defeating him.
strategy: He Polymorphs at the end of round 1 into one of several animal forms, and after his first defeat he Blessings back to full health once - plan to kill him twice.

Elephant (Tim-tim): his incoming damage is capped at 100 until you hit him with the three schools he blades himself with. Bring off-school hits/treasure cards to match (Death/Life/Myth, Fire/Ice/Storm, or Balance/Myth/Ice). Progress carries between recasts; finish the code and his cap becomes a Frenzy.
Rhino (Tom-tom): he Superchargers and Stormblades himself then casts a non-dispellable Storm Owl - stack Storm shields and burst him before it lands.
Lion (Tik-tik): the first time he is damaged he Cat Naps to full health (once) - bait it with a small hit, then set up your real burst.
Spider (Tse-Tse): he stacks +30% Shadowblades each round and Sacrifices himself on round 3 - you can just survive and let him kill himself.
Otherwise hit with Myth (he takes more from Myth, resists Storm 80%) and deal with the Starburst Spider and Goliath Bruiser minions.
cheatNotes: End of round 1 he Polymorphs into one of the animal forms below. Identify which and adapt.
The first time you defeat him he casts Blessing back to full health (once). Expect to kill him twice.
As the Elephant his incoming damage is capped at 100 for the fight until you solve his code.
Round 2 and every 2 rounds he blades himself with 3 schools - the schools you must hit him with to break the cap.
Possible code: Death, Life, Myth.
Possible code: Fire, Ice, Storm.
Possible code: Balance, Myth, Ice.
Your progress through the code is not reset when he recasts the blades. Keep going.
Finishing the code replaces his damage-limit aura with Frenzy - now hit him hard.
As the Rhino he opens with two Supercharges and a +35% Stormblade on himself.
End of round 1 as the Rhino he casts a non-dispellable Storm Owl. Storm-shield the team.
As the Lion, the first damage he takes triggers a Cat Nap to full health (once). Bait it with a small hit.
As the Spider he stacks a +30% Shadowblade every round.
At the end of his 3rd round as the Spider he casts Dark Fiend and Sacrifices himself, defeating himself - you can let him do it.

name: Tse-Tse Snaketail (Standard)
cheats: [Polymorph] "I am the master of disguise!" - At the end of the 1st round, Snaketail will Polymorph into one of the following listed below:
[Polymorph]Tim-tim Snakeeye (Standard) - Elephant Polymorph Transformation
After Polymorphing, he will cast an Aura that limits incoming damage on himself to a max of 100 for 99 Rounds.

This aura will be cast again if a wizard attempts to replace it with a negative aura. However, if he removes it by using another aura such as Galvanic Field, the effects of the aura will be removed and will not be recast.

"An elephant never forgets, do you?" - At the beginning of the 2nd round, and every 2 rounds after, Snakeeye will cast 3 Blades of different Schools on himself. This indicates the order of which schools attacks he will need to be hit by. The 3 Blades will always be one of the following possibilities:
[Death] [Life] [Myth]
[Fire] [Ice] [Storm]
[Balance] [Myth] [Ice]
The progress made into finishing the 3 hits will not be reset when Snakeeye recasts the Blades every 2 rounds.
"You Remembered!" - Snakeeye will say this after each successful hit in the code.
"Your memory is perfect!" - After getting the code correct, Snakeeye will replace his damage limit aura with a Frenzy.
[Polymorph] Tom-tom Snakeskin (Standard) - Rhino Polymorph Transformation
"This is gonna hurt!" - At the beginning of the round after Snaketail revived, he will cast a +15% version of Supercharge, a Supercharge and +35% Stormblade on himself.
"Charge!" - At the end of the round where the above cheat is used, Snaketail will cast No Dispel version of Storm Owl (Tier 5a)
[Polymorph] Tik-tik Snaketongue (Standard) - Lion Polymorph Transformation
"Just a little catnap..." - Casts a version of Cat Nap that restores 100% max health. Activates when he is attacked, once per round. This cheat can revive the boss.
[Polymorph] Tse-Tse Snaketail (Standard) - Spider Polymorph Transformation
This Polymorph is immune to damage
This Polymorph only lasts 3 rounds, and each round Snaketail will cast a Shadowblade which stacks.

"The Shadow Queen sends her regards!" - At the end of the third round after the Spider Polymorph was activated, Snaketail will cast Dark Fiend and will use a 100% Max Health version of Sacrifice on himself.
strategy: Identify his form at the end of Round 1. He polymorphs into one of four random forms, each with its own gimmick, so adapt to whichever appears.

Elephant (Snakeeye): he caps incoming damage at 100 and shows three school blades as a code. Hit him with those schools in the shown order to break the cap, since progress carries between his re-casts, then burst once it swaps to a Frenzy.
Lion (Snaketongue): he Cat Naps to full Health once per Round whenever attacked and can even revive, so kill him in a single big hit rather than chipping.
Spider (Snaketail): he is immune to damage for three Rounds while stacking Shadowblades, then Dark Fiends and Sacrifices to full Health. Shield up and wait it out instead of wasting hits.
Rhino (Snakeskin): after reviving he Supercharges and Stormblades, then fires a no-Dispel Storm Owl, so shield for the incoming Storm hit.
Attack with Myth (his boosted school); never Storm (80% resist). He is stunable, so use stuns to skip dangerous turns.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1 he polymorphs into one of four random forms, each with its own gimmick (below).
In Elephant form he caps all incoming damage at 100 with a 99-Round aura, so normal hits barely scratch him.
Do not overwrite that aura with a negative aura, since he just recasts it; you can remove it with a non-negative aura like Galvanic Field, or by completing the blade code.
In Elephant form he shows three different-school blades each cycle, so hit him with those schools in that order to break the damage cap.
One possible code is Death, then Life, then Myth.
Another is Fire, then Ice, then Storm.
Another is Balance, then Myth, then Ice.
Your progress through the three hits carries over when he re-shows the blades every two Rounds, so you do not restart.
He says 'You Remembered!' after each correct hit in the sequence, so use it to track progress.
After the full code he replaces the damage-cap aura with a Frenzy and now takes normal damage, so burst him then.
In Rhino form, the Round after he revives he stacks a +15% Supercharge, a Supercharge, and a +35% Stormblade.
Then at the end of that Round he fires a no-Dispel Storm Owl, so shield for the incoming Storm hit.
In Lion form he Cat Naps to full Health once per Round whenever attacked, and it can even revive him, so kill him in one big hit rather than chipping.
In Spider form he is immune to all damage, so do not waste hits.
The Spider form lasts three Rounds, stacking a Shadowblade each Round.
At the end of the third Spider Round he casts Dark Fiend and Sacrifices himself to full Health, so shield through it and wait for the form to end.

name: Tumbler
cheats: "Delay .. recorded!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Tumbler will cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
"New Target Acquired!" - If a Wizard returns to the duel after fleeing, Tumbler will cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.

"Offense Mode Engage" - At the start of the Round, Tumbler gets a +100% Berserk Aura for 98 Rounds, increased by an additional +100% for each Wizard beyond the first in the duel, up to a maximum of +400%.

"Defense Mode Engage" - At the beginning of the fourth Round, it changes to a -100% Brace Aura on self for 98 Rounds, increased by an additional -5% for each Wizard beyond the first in the duel, up to a maximum of -115%.
The Auras cycle every three Rounds after the fourth Round.
"Anomaly detected...corrected" If a Wizard removes Tumbler's Aura, Tumbler will recast it.
strategy: Team plan: stack blades on one hitter, cast double Feints, and burst Tumbler in the first two or three rounds before Defense Mode engages on Round 4. The guide killed him in two rounds this way.

Read the aura cycle. Offense Mode is a +100% Berserk aura (scaling to +400% with four wizards), so he hits hard. Defense Mode from Round 4 is a -100% to -115% Brace on himself, so he takes almost no damage. Land your kill shot during an Offense window when his Brace is down.
Solo: play defensively through the first offense phase, build blades and pips, and unleash your big hit during a later Offense window (around Round 7) once Brace has cycled off.
Do not waste turns stripping his auras or protected charms. Removing an aura just makes him recast it, his Bladestorm is Aegis-protected, and his Feint is Indemnity-protected.
Never join late or return after fleeing - Tumbler casts Mana Burn (one player lost 12,285 damage even with 38% resist).

Pierce his resist. He resists Ice/Myth/Death 70% but boosts Fire/Storm/Life 5%. Hit with a boosted school and add Infallible; comments note Shrike plus Infallible works great, though raw Shadow spells like Shrike can trigger aura recasts.
cheatNotes: Joining the duel late triggers Mana Burn on you, draining pips and dealing heavy damage, so enter on time.
Fleeing and returning to the duel also triggers Mana Burn, so avoid fleeing once the fight is underway.
At the start he gains a +100% Berserk aura (up to +400% with four wizards) that spikes his outgoing damage, so expect big hits during offense rounds.
On Round 4 he swaps to a -100% Brace aura (up to -115% with four wizards) that reduces his incoming damage to almost nothing, so do not waste your kill shot while it is up.
The auras cycle every three rounds after Round 4, so plan your burst for the offense windows when Brace is off.
If you remove his aura he simply recasts it, so dispelling it just wastes a turn.

name: Tymen WhiteFlame
cheats: At the beginning of every third Round, the Wizard with the most Pips will be hit with Mana Burn.
Unbalance cast on Tymen will dispel this cheat.
If a Wizard has already queued a Spell to be cast before being hit by the Mana Burn, then the Spell will finish, regardless of how many Pips are available at the time it is cast.
If a Wizard casts a Healing Spell, he will Stun the Wizard for two Rounds.
Pet Heals and Item Cards received from Pets will not trigger this cheat.
Guardian Spirit will trigger this cheat.
strategy: Tymen is an Ice boss with 80% Ice resist that takes 35% extra from Fire and Myth, so hit with Fire or Myth. Ice wizards must pack an Ice Prism to get through his resist.
Bring Unbalancer. Casting Unbalance on Tymen dispels his Mana Burn cheat outright, which is the single most useful tool for the fight.

Do not cast healing spells. Any heal spell stuns the caster for two rounds. Instead wear high-resist gear and use a healing pet; pet heals and pet item cards do not trigger the stun. Note Guardian Spirit does trigger it.
Carry stun protection anyway: Stun Shield, Conviction, or Stun Resist, and consider Vaporize as an alternative that avoids the stun trigger.

Bring ward-stripping. Tymen stacks Fortify, Volcanic Shield, and Legion Shield (-35%), so pack Shatter, Pierce, or Steal Ward. Fire, Ice, and Storm wizards especially get their Feints blocked by Volcanic Shield.

Beat the Mana Burn timer: every third round the wizard with the most pips gets Mana Burned. If you queue your spell before the Mana Burn lands, it still fires regardless of remaining pips, so time big hits for just before that window and plan overkill damage.
cheatNotes: Every third round Tymen Mana Burns whichever wizard is holding the most pips, draining their pips. Spread pips or dump them before rounds 3, 6, and 9.
Casting Unbalance on Tymen removes the Mana Burn cheat entirely, so bring an Unbalancer.
If you have already queued a spell before the Mana Burn hits, that spell still goes off even if you no longer have the pips for it. Queue your hit ahead of the Mana Burn round.
If any wizard casts a healing spell, Tymen stuns that wizard for two rounds. Avoid heal spells and rely on resist gear plus a healing pet.
Pet-cast heals and item cards received from your pet do not trigger the stun, so pet healing is safe.
Guardian Spirit counts as a healing spell and will trigger the two-round stun, so leave it out of your deck.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 1)
cheats: The boss cheat casts a Humongofrog spell when the Grendel Ransacker (Tier 1) is defeated at the beginning of the next round.
The boss cheat casts a Blinding Light after the Grendel Cracknail (Tier 1) or Grendel Scavenger (Tier 1) is defeated at the beginning of the next round.
Boss will also casts these cheats if he does not begin a battle with these minions.
All cheat-spells will be casted at the same time if all minions are defeated on one round.
If Boss starts out with no minions, all cheat-spells will be casted at the same time on first Round.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker Minion casts a Tower Shield (Sun -55%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +30%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger Minion casts a Sprite (50+250), starting on Round 3.
strategy: Low-health tier 1 fight (2,000); he's stunnable, so a stun buys a setup round.

Killing the Grendel Ransacker makes him cast Humongofrog (AoE) next round; killing the Cracknail or Scavenger makes him Blinding Light — expect these when you clear minions and shield/heal through the frog.
His minions buff and heal him every 3 rounds (Tower Shield, Dragonblade, Sprite), so clear them before they stack value.
Blade up and AoE the whole board; if all minions die together he fires all the punish spells at once.
Finish him with a big hit once the adds are handled.
cheatNotes: Kill the Grendel Ransacker and he Humongofrogs the team next round.
Kill the Cracknail or Scavenger and he Blinding Lights the team next round.
He casts these even if he doesn't start with those minions.
Kill all minions in one round and he fires all the punish spells together.
If he starts with no minions he casts all the punishes on round 1.
The Ransacker Tower Shields him (Sun -55%) from round 1.
The Cracknail Dragonblades him (Sun +30%) from round 2.
The Scavenger Sprites him (heal 50+250) from round 3.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 2)
cheats: This Boss has Resistance to the Myth and Death Schools and has Boost to the Storm and Life Schools.
This Boss will cheat cast Spells dependant on if a Minion dies, and which Minion dies.
Once his Grendel Ransacker (Tier 2) or Grendel Scavenger (Tier 2) Minion is defeated, he will cheat cast Blinding Light the next Round.
Once his Grendel Cracknail (Tier 2) Minion is defeated, he will cheat cast an enhanced version of Humongofrog the next Round.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker Minion casts a Tower Shield (Sun -55%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +35%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger Minion casts a Sprite (75+270), starting on Round 3.
strategy: He resists Myth 50% and Death 25%, and takes +20% from Storm and Life. Hit with Storm or Life.
His Grendel minions buff and heal him: Ransacker Tower Shields him, Cracknail Dragonblades him, Scavenger Sprites him, every 3 rounds. Expect him to be shielded and bladed.

Killing minions triggers counters: defeating the Ransacker or Scavenger gives him Blinding Light next round (accuracy debuff on you); defeating the Cracknail gives him an enhanced Humongofrog (AoE). Bring accuracy so Blinding Light does not fizzle your hit.
The cleanest line is a Storm or Life AoE that drops the minions and Tyrfing together, rather than picking them off one at a time and eating each counter.
Blade up and land one big AoE.
cheatNotes: He cheat-casts based on which minion dies, so kill order changes what he throws back. Prefer an AoE that clears everything at once.
Killing the Ransacker or Scavenger gives him Blinding Light next round, lowering your accuracy. Bring accuracy or hit before it lands.
Killing the Cracknail gives him an enhanced Humongofrog AoE next round. Be ready to heal it.
Every 3 rounds his minions buff him. Expect stacked shields and blades on Tyrfing.
The Ransacker Tower Shields him (-55%) from Round 1. Punch through or strip it.
The Cracknail Dragonblades him (+35%) from Round 2, raising his hits.
The Scavenger Sprites him for healing from Round 3. Pressure him faster than the heals.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 3)
cheats: Upon killing each of his Minions a spell triggers.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker Minion casts a Tower Shield (Sun -65%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail Minion casts a Dragonblade (Sun +40%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger Minion casts a Sprite (100+300), starting on Round 3.
strategy: His three Grendels support him every 3 rounds: the Ransacker Tower-Shields him, the Cracknail Dragonblades him, the Scavenger heals him with Sprite. Kill the minions to cut off his shields, blades, and heals.
Hit with Storm or Life (+25% incoming); avoid Myth (60% resist).
AoE works well here to hit Tyrfing and all three Grendels at once. He is stunable, so a stun can buy a turn.
Blade, Feint, and burst the 8000 health once the support minions are cleared.
cheatNotes: Killing each of his minions triggers a spell, so expect a reaction when a Grendel dies.
His minions buff him every 3 rounds, so clear them to stop the support.
The Grendel Ransacker gives him a -65% Tower Shield from round 1. Pierce it or kill the Ransacker.
The Grendel Cracknail gives him a +40% Dragonblade from round 2. Strip it or kill the Cracknail.
The Grendel Scavenger heals him with Sprite (100+300) from round 3. Kill it to stop the healing.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 4)
cheats: Upon Defeating each of Tyrfing's Minions, an interrupt Spell will trigger.
Upon Defeating the Grendel Ransacker (Tier 4), Tyrfing will cast 325 + 351 version of Deer Knight.
Upon Defeating the Grendel Cracknail (Tier 4), Tyrfing will cast a 300 + 300 version of Deer Knight.
Upon Defeating the Grendel Scavenger (Tier 4), Tyrfing will cast Blinding Light.
Defeating all three of Tyrfing's Minions in one Round will trigger all three of these Spells to interrupt in succession.
Every 3 Rounds his Minions cast the following Spells on him.
His Grendel Ransacker (Tier 4) casts a Tower Shield (Sun -70%), starting on Round 1.
His Grendel Cracknail (Tier 4) casts a Dragonblade (Sun +45%), starting on Round 2.
His Grendel Scavenger (Tier 4) casts a Regenerate (60+1,130), starting on Round 3.
strategy: Hit with Storm or Life (+30%), avoid Myth (70% resist). He is stunable and beguilable, so use that to skip his turns.
His three Grendels buff him every 3 rounds (Ransacker: -70% Tower Shield, Cracknail: +45% Dragonblade, Scavenger: Regenerate). Kill them to stop the ramp, but each death triggers a punish.
Killing the Ransacker gives you a Deer Knight DoT, the Cracknail another Deer Knight, and the Scavenger a Blinding Light. Killing all three in one round fires all three punishes back-to-back.
Prefer clearing minions on separate rounds so you eat the punishes one at a time, and Triage the Deer Knight DoTs.
With minions handled, blade up and land one big Storm or Life hit, and stun Tyrfing to buy setup rounds.
cheatNotes: Defeating any of his Grendel minions triggers an interrupt spell on you.
Kill the Grendel Ransacker and he hits you with a 325+351 Deer Knight DoT.
Kill the Grendel Cracknail and he hits you with a 300+300 Deer Knight DoT.
Kill the Grendel Scavenger and he casts Blinding Light (accuracy down) on you.
Killing all three in one round fires all three punishes in succession, spread the kills out to eat them one at a time.
Every 3 rounds his minions buff him, so clear them to stop his ramp.
The Ransacker gives him a -70% Tower Shield starting Round 1, pierce or strip it before hitting.
The Cracknail gives him a +45% Dragonblade starting Round 2.
The Scavenger heals him with Regenerate (60+1,130) starting Round 3, kill it to cut the healing.

name: Tyrfing (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 5)
cheats: Defeating the Grendel Ransacker (Tier 5) will trigger a Sun version of Scarecrow, which deals 650 Death Drain Damage to all enemies and heals the caster for half the Damage dealt.
Defeating the Grendel Scavenger (Tier 5) will trigger a Blinding Light.
Defeating the Grendel Cracknail (Tier 5) will trigger a 375 + 399 version of Deer Knight.
If all three Minions are defeated at the same time, all three Spells will occur in the same turn.
Every three Rounds, his Minions cast the following on him:
Grendel Ransacker (Tier 5) casts a Tower Shield (Sun -90%), starting on Round 1.
Grendel Cracknail (Tier 5) casts a Dragonblade (Sun +50%) Treasure Card, starting on Round 2.
Grendel Scavenger (Tier 5) casts a Regenerate (75+1,250), starting on Round 3.
strategy: His three Grendel minions buff him every three rounds: Ransacker gives a -90% Tower Shield (Round 1), Cracknail a +50% Dragonblade (Round 2), Scavenger a Regenerate heal (Round 3). Kill the minions to stop the buffs, but note each death triggers a punish.

Defeating the Ransacker fires a 650 Death Scarecrow on the team, the Scavenger fires Blinding Light, and the Cracknail fires a Deer Knight. If you kill all three at once with AoE, all three hit the same turn, so heal for it.
Hit Tyrfing with Storm or Life (+35%, resists Myth 80%); strip or hit through the Tower Shield.
Funnel blades to the hitter and burst Tyrfing after thinning the minions.
cheatNotes: Killing the Ransacker fires a 650 Death Scarecrow on the whole team that heals the caster, so heal after.
Killing the Scavenger fires a Blinding Light accuracy debuff.
Killing the Cracknail fires a Deer Knight on the team.
AoE that kills all three minions at once triggers all three punishes the same turn, so be topped up before that.
The Ransacker shields Tyrfing with a -90% Tower Shield every three rounds from Round 1, so strip it or hit through it.
The Cracknail blades Tyrfing with a +50% Dragonblade every three rounds from Round 2.
The Scavenger heals Tyrfing with Regenerate every three rounds from Round 3, so kill it to stop the sustain.

name: Unformed Beast
cheats: [Spell] "Try This" - The Unformed Beast will give the Wizard and itself one of the following Spells that matches the Wizard's school, and the School Pips required to cast.
[Scales of Destiny][Gravestorm][Glimpse of Infinity][Deathly Depths][Starspawn][Improbable Gaze][Tree of Strife]
[Ward] "Arooooo!!!" - At the end of every two Rounds, the Unformed Beast casts one of the following spells:
Balanceblade, Death Shield, Fire Shield, Snow Shield, Myth Shield, Life Shield, Storm Shield.
strategy: Expect no school weakness. The Beast resists every school 36% evenly and has no boosted school, so lean on your strongest hitter and armor pierce rather than a matchup.

Every two rounds it puts up a random single-school shield (or a Balanceblade on itself). Carry Armor Piercing, a shatter or steal ward, or a hit of a different school than the shield so your damage isn't eaten.

The "Try This" cheat hands both you and the Beast a powerful spell matching your school (a rank-heavy card like Gravestorm or Tree of Strife). It can hit you back hard with its copy, so keep your health up and consider firing the free spell yourself.
Blade up, pierce through or around its next shield, then hit. With only 13,265 health and no healing cheat, it drops to one well-set-up nuke.
cheatNotes: It gifts you (and itself) a strong spell in your own school. You can use the free spell, but expect the Beast to fire its copy back at you.
Every two rounds it shields itself or blades up. Bring pierce or a ward-remover so your hit isn't blocked.

name: Unfun Guy
cheats: The Wizard can cast Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, and War Oracle as inspired Casts during this battle.
At the end of every Round, Unfun Guy will cast Power Current on itself.
Unfun Guy can cast Sprite, and a version of Donate Power that gives him 4 Power Pips.
"I counter your Charms!" - If a Wizard uses a Blade, Unfun Guy will cast Eye of Vigilance.
"I counter your Damage Over Time!" - If a Wizard uses a Damage over Time, Unfun Guy will cast Jinn's Fortune (currently bugged).
strategy: Raid boss; you can use the Inspired Casts offered this battle (Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle) as free hits, so work them into your plan.
He resists Globals 70%, so Doom and Gloom and similar barely stick; do not rely on them.
Do not cast blades. A blade makes him cast Eye of Vigilance.
Do not cast damage-over-time; a DoT makes him cast Jinn's Fortune (currently bugged, so low risk, but avoid feeding it).
He self-feeds pips with Power Current every Round and Donate Power (+4), so he ramps; end the fight before his damage spikes.
Set up without blades or DoTs, lean on the Inspired Casts, and burst him down.
cheatNotes: You have access to powerful Inspired Cast spells this fight; use them as free damage in your rotation.
He Power-Currents himself for pips every Round, so he ramps quickly; do not let the fight drag.
He can heal with Sprite and hand himself 4 power pips with Donate Power; expect steady pip gain.
Casting a blade makes him cast Eye of Vigilance; keep blade use minimal.
A damage-over-time makes him cast Jinn's Fortune, currently bugged; low risk but avoid feeding it.

name: Union Jack Golem (Challenge)
cheats: [Fire][Jinx] "Initiate: Trap trigger" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Ward, the Union Jack Golem will cast a Fire Spell that deals 120 Fire Damage after 4 Rounds to the Wizard.

[Fire][Ward] "Initiate: Counter protocol" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, the Noxious Gas Golem will cast Fire Elf (Tier 2c) on itself, and a Fire Spell that deals 120 Fire Damage after 4 Rounds to the Wizard.
[Round][Trap] "Initiate: Trap protocol" - At the beginning of the first Round and every other Round thereafter, the Union Jack Golem will cast Mass Fire Trap.
"Initiate: Reversal protocol" - ???
"Initiate: Defensive countermeasures protocol" - ???
strategy: This golem has only 5,000 health, so blade up and burst it down with your strongest attack school; it is not stunable.
Do not cast wards. A harmful Ward (trap) triggers a delayed 120 Fire hit on you, and a helpful Ward (shield) triggers a Fire Elf plus the same delayed Fire hit.
Do not shield-turtle. Blade your hitter and end the fight fast instead.
It lays Mass Fire Trap on round 1 and every other round, boosting the fire coming at you, so kill it before that stacks.
cheatNotes: Casting a harmful Ward (a trap) lands a delayed 120 Fire hit on you 4 rounds later. Skip trap setup.
Casting a helpful Ward (a shield) makes the Noxious Gas Golem Fire Elf itself and drops the same delayed 120 Fire hit on you. Blade and hit instead of shielding.
Round 1 and every other round it casts Mass Fire Trap, boosting the fire damage coming at you. Kill it quickly.

name: Valencian Naval Fleet (Challenge)

stats.resist: 0% to all schools32% to all schools

cheats: Valencian Naval Fleet
Marleybonian Naval Fleet
Monquistan Naval Fleet
"Myth conquers Death." - ???
Polarian Naval Fleet
strategy: Novus fleet challenge (13215) with three co-fleets (Marleybonian, Monquistan, Polarian). Team fight, so funnel blades to a hitter.
It resists every school 32% except Moon (0% resist), so Moon-school or Astral hits punch through cleanly; otherwise over-stack blades and traps to beat the resist.
Cheat details are largely unconfirmed, so play safe: assume punish cheats on heals and blades, and stack setup in single rounds, hitting the same turn you buff.
Clear or AoE the fleet minions with your finisher so they die alongside the flagship.

name: Valencian Naval Fleet (Standard)
cheats: After each Boss is defeated, another random Naval Fleet Boss will respawn in its place. This continues until 4 new Fleets have been summoned.
If all four Bosses are defeated in the same round, one will be healed instead of summoning a fourth.
Doom Moon
The Doom Moon will charge it's laser for each Hanging Effect, and will shoot a laser beam on all enemies after a condition is met.
This laser will use the Balanceblade from Marleybonian Naval Fleet's Justiceblade.
The laser will be fired per condition met, and then all hanging effects used to meet the condition will be removed.
Protections can not prevent removal of any hanging effects.
4 Blades on Wizards -> Dog Tracy
4 Negative Charms on Enemies -> Solomon Crane
4 Shields on Wizards -> Duck Savage
4 Traps on Enemies -> Mandar the Barbarian
2 Damage Overtimes on Enemies -> Buck Gordon
2 Heal Overtimes on Wizards -> The Bantam
2 Blades on Wizards and 2 Negative Charms on Enemies -> Stallion Quartermane
2 Shields on Wizards and 2 Traps on Enemies -> Stallion Quartermane
2 Uses of Shadow-Enhanced Spells by Wizards -> The Shadoe
Note: Charge Up cheats have no function. In order to shoot the Doom Moon laser, the Gambit Conditions must be met.
Dog Tracy: - (4 [Blade])
"We're fleas biting at a rhino. Need more punch..." - Charge Up.
"Power rerouted to weapons!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and clears all Damage Blades from all Wizards.
Solomon Crane: - (4 [Curse])
"The machines have identified weak points..." - Charge Up.
"Target these areas for increased effect!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage and clears all negative Charms from all enemies.
Duck Savage: - (4 [Ward])
"Need some help? Your shields need reinforcement..." - Charge Up.
"Let's polarize the hull with... bronze!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and clears all positive Wards from all Wizards.
Mandar the Barbarian: - (4 [Trap])
"Bah, only fools fall into traps..." - Charge Up.
Frenzy may also trigger this cheat.
"By the power of Mandar!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to and clears all Damage Traps from all enemies.
Buck Gordon: - (2 [Damage over Time])
"Sorry I'm late. You'll see why..." - Charge Up.
"Took a detour for some sabotage." - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to and removes all Damage over Time effects from all enemies.
The Bantam: - (2 [Heal over Time])
"Damage reports from all decks..." - Charge Up
"Oh, what's this button do?" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies to and removes all Heal over Time effects from all Wizards.
Heal Overtimes do NOT have to be on the same Wizard for the condition to be met.
Stallion Quartermane: - (2 [Blade] & 2 [Curse])
"Ha-ha! That's how you adventure..." - Charge Up.
"Targeting systems engaged!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and removes all positive Charms from all Wizards and all negative Charms from all enemies.
Stallion Quartermane: - (2 [Ward] & 2 [Trap])
"Targeting systems engaged!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and removes all positive Wards from all Wizards and all negative Wards from all enemies.
The Shadoe: - (2 [Shadow School])
"I sense fear on those ships. Keep going..." - Charge Up.
"The Shadoe Bestows!" - Deals 6,000 Moon Damage to all enemies and removes all Shadow Pips from all Wizards.
strategy: Novus raid: four Naval Fleet bosses fought in sequence (a defeated one respawns until four new fleets have come), so pace for a long fight with hitter, supports, and healer.

The Doom Moon is the real danger: it fires a 6,000 Moon AoE whenever a gambit condition is met (4 blades on wizards, 4 shields, 4 traps, 4 negative charms, 2 DoTs, 2 HoTs, or 2 shadow-enhanced casts, plus combos), then wipes all effects that met it. Protections cannot stop the removal.
Cap your stacks: keep fewer than 4 blades across the team, fewer than 4 traps or shields, and avoid piling DoTs or HoTs or double shadow hits. Set up just under the thresholds, then hit.
Coordinate the hitter to burst each fleet with a controlled amount of setup rather than a big blade stack that trips the laser.
Do not defeat all four bosses in the same round; one gets healed instead of the run ending, so stagger the kills.
Healer covers the 6,000 Moon laser hits; attack with Myth or your strongest school since the bosses are Storm.
cheatNotes: After each boss is defeated another random Naval Fleet respawns until four new fleets have come, so expect a drawn-out fight.
If all four are defeated in the same round, one is healed instead of the run ending. Stagger your kills.
The Doom Moon charges its laser for each hanging effect and fires once a gambit condition is met.
The laser fires per condition met, then removes every hanging effect that met the condition.
Protections cannot prevent that removal, so Aegis and Indemnity will not save your buffs here.
4 blades on wizards triggers Dog Tracy, a 6,000 Moon AoE that clears all your blades.
4 negative charms on enemies triggers Solomon Crane, so cap weaknesses on his side.
4 shields on wizards triggers Duck Savage, so keep team shields under four.
4 traps on enemies triggers Mandar the Barbarian, so keep traps under four.
2 DoTs on enemies triggers Buck Gordon, so do not double up DoTs.
2 HoTs on wizards triggers The Bantam, so limit heal-over-times.
2 blades on wizards plus 2 negative charms on enemies triggers Stallion Quartermane.
2 shields on wizards plus 2 traps on enemies also triggers Stallion Quartermane.
2 shadow-enhanced casts triggers The Shadoe, so limit shadow hits.
The Charge Up flavor cheats do nothing; only meeting a gambit condition fires the laser.
Dog Tracy deals 6,000 Moon to all enemies and clears all damage blades from all wizards.
Solomon Crane deals 6,000 Moon and clears all negative charms from all enemies.
Duck Savage deals 6,000 Moon to all enemies and clears all positive wards from all wizards.
Frenzy may also trigger the trap gambit.
Mandar deals 6,000 Moon and clears all damage traps from all enemies.
Buck Gordon deals 6,000 Moon and removes all damage-over-time effects from all enemies.
The Bantam deals 6,000 Moon to all enemies and removes all heal-over-time effects from all wizards.
The heal-over-times do not have to be on the same wizard to meet the condition.
This Stallion Quartermane deals 6,000 Moon and removes all positive charms from wizards and all negative charms from enemies.
This Stallion Quartermane deals 6,000 Moon and removes all positive wards from wizards and all negative wards from enemies.
The Shadoe deals 6,000 Moon to all enemies and removes all Shadow Pips from all wizards.

name: Vanitus
cheats: "Help! Save Me!" - If a Wizard is late to join the duel, Vanitus casts Beguile (Star) that lasts three Rounds on the Wizard, and casts a Star Spell to Summon one additional Justicon (Storm). Cannot summon more than three Minions.
"Servants!" - Starting on Round 2, he casts a Star Spell which summons Justicons.
"Justicons! Defend me!" - If attacked, he will casts a Star version of Steal Health without the Heal, to remove all Health on one of his summoned Minions.
"I Surrender!" - Starting on Round 2, if the Boss is attacked without any Minions present, he will interrupt-cast a Spell that defeats himself.
spellNotes: Deals 420 Star Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Don't try to out-damage his 11,340 health — exploit his surrender cheat instead. When you attack Vanitus while he has no minions present (round 2+), he defeats himself.
Each time you attack him he sacrifices one of his own Justicon minions to absorb the hit, so keep hitting to burn through his minions.
Once he's minionless, land one more hit to trigger the self-defeat.
He resummons Justicons every round from round 2, so hit fast and repeatedly (single hits or AoE both chew through minions) to out-pace the summons.
Enter on time — late wizards get Beguiled for three rounds and he summons an extra Justicon.
cheatNotes: Join late and he Beguiles you for three rounds and summons an extra Justicon — enter on time.
From round 2 he keeps summoning Justicon minions — you'll need to out-pace them to strip him down to zero minions.
When you attack him he sacrifices one of his own Justicons to absorb the hit, so every hit removes a minion — keep swinging to clear them all.
Once he has no minions and you attack him (round 2+), he interrupt-casts a spell that defeats himself — that's the win condition, not his health bar.

name: Verboten Mimic
locations: Mirage: Aggrobah - Vault of the Verboten
cheats: "A surprise for you too!" - If a Wizard is late, he will cheat-cast Steal Pips (Moon) to steal 1 Pip from the Wizard.
"A treasure of tricks!" & "I can change forms all day long!" & "Perhaps this form's too easy?" - Every three Rounds, the Verboten Mimic will Polymorph into one of the forms listed below.
"You can't trick a Mimic!" - He will remove all Traps from himself the first time trapped.
"Oh! I've got an idea!" - He will cast Mystic Colossus.
During this Cycle, the Boss will pass since all these Spells are interrupt.
Round 1 - He will cast a triple Tower Shield on himself, Legion Shield, and Brace.
Round 2 - He will cheat-cast Wings of Fate.
Round 3 - He will cast Blinding Freeze.
"Only attack if you have been inked!" - At the beginning of each Round, he will "Ink" the Wizard in the first spot (Sun spot), which applies a Smoke Mantle.
"Still fighting? Have some more ink!" - After losing 25% of his health, he will begin to ink the Wizard in the second spot (eye).
"If you can still see, don't look at me!" - If a non-inked Wizard uses a single target attack Spell on him, he will cast one of the following Spells.
Poison - 800 + 2,000 Death Damage over 3 Rounds
Power Link - 400 + 1,500 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds & 200 + 800 Heal over Time on itself.
He will remove traps which are used on him.
Indemnity can be used to to avoid this cheat.
If a multi-trap Spell is used, such as Elemental Trap, he will only remove the last trap applied (Storm/Life Trap).
"Protector!" - At the beginning of the first Round, she will summon a Stellar Protector.
She will cast Smoke Screen (-50%) on her first turn and every round when a Wizard successfully casts a Spell.
"The cards align! Now's your chance!" When both Blades and Traps are present in the battle, the cheat will end until they are no longer present.
"Your body and soul are mine once more." - At the end of the first Round, he will summon a Hagakure Blade and a Seijun Killer.
"Again face the poetry of war!" - On any given turn, he will cast a Death Damage over Time Spell that deals 4,000 Death Damage over 10 Rounds.
This can be countered with Triage or Mass Triage.
"The way of the Samoorai is found in death!" - Casts a Detonate (Death) on the target.
"I retain my resolve!" - If a Wizard casts any Ward, he will cast Shatter.
spellNotes: See the respective pages for the spell casts and stats of each of this Boss's Polymorph Forms.
strategy: A team fight against a 32,000-health boss best kept short. Supports stack blades onto one hitter and the team ends it fast, because the Mimic Polymorphs into a new form (Cyrus Drake, Ghost Dog, Luska, Mithraya, or Prince Gobblestone) every three rounds, and a long fight cycles you through every form's cheats.

Do not rely on traps. He removes traps placed on him, and with a multi-trap spell like Elemental Trap he strips only the last trap applied. Indemnity can protect a trap, but blade stacking is the safer damage source since Feints do not stack well here.
Bring a Shatter (trained or TC) for the Prince Gobblestone form, which stacks a triple Tower Shield, Legion Shield, and Brace over three rounds. Shatter blows through that wall.
Equip your best accuracy gear before entering, especially to avoid fizzles against the Luska and Mithraya forms.

Watch the ink cheat: he Smoke Mantles the wizard in the first (Sun) spot every round, and after he drops below 75% health he also inks the second (eye) spot. If a NON-inked wizard single-target attacks him, he retaliates with an 800+2,000 Death Poison or a 400+1,500 Fire Power Link that also heals him, so let an inked wizard throw the hit.
Enter on round 1 to avoid the Steal Pips latecomer cheat, then build blades and burst him down in one big hit before the polymorph cycle grinds you out.
cheatNotes: A latecomer gets a Moon Steal Pips that takes one of their pips. Enter on round 1.
Every three rounds he Polymorphs into one of five boss forms, each with its own cheats, so drag the fight out and you face all of them. Kill him quickly.
The first time you trap him he removes every trap on himself, which is why blades are the better setup here.
As part of the polymorph/cheat cycle he can cast Mystic Colossus, a large Myth hit. Keep some resist or a shield ready.
During this interrupt cycle he simply passes his own turn, so these are extra cheat casts rather than his normal attack.
In the round-1 phase of the cycle he shields up with a triple Tower Shield, Legion Shield, and Brace. Bring Shatter or pierce to get through.
In the round-2 phase he cheat-casts Wings of Fate to heal himself, so out-damage the heal.
In the round-3 phase he casts Blinding Freeze, an Ice attack. Have Ice resist available.
Each round he 'inks' the wizard in the first (Sun) spot with a Smoke Mantle. That inked wizard is the one cleared to attack him.
Once he drops below 75% health he also inks the wizard in the second (eye) spot, giving you a second wizard allowed to attack.
If a wizard who has NOT been inked lands a single-target attack on him, he punishes them with one of the DoTs below. Only let inked wizards hit.
That punishment can be an 800 + 2,000 Death Poison over 3 rounds. Triage it off or avoid triggering it.
The punishment can instead be a 400 + 1,500 Fire Power Link over 3 rounds that also heals him 200 + 800, so it hurts you and mends him.
He removes traps used on him, reinforcing that traps are wasted here.
Indemnity on a trap stops him from removing it, if you insist on trapping.
With a multi-trap spell like Elemental Trap he only strips the last trap applied (the Storm/Life trap), leaving the earlier ones.
In one form she opens by summoning a Stellar Protector minion. Consider clearing it or hitting through it.
That form casts Smoke Screen (-50% accuracy) on turn one and again whenever a wizard lands a spell, so bring accuracy gear to fight the fizzle debuff.
In another form, once both Blades and Traps are on the board the cheat pauses until they are gone, so managing your charms and traps can stall it.
One form summons a Hagakure Blade and a Seijun Killer at the end of round 1. Be ready to handle two minions.
That form can cast a Death DoT dealing 4,000 Death damage over 10 rounds on any turn.
Remove that 4,000 Death DoT with Triage or Mass Triage before it stacks up.
It can also Detonate that Death DoT for a burst of the remaining damage, so clear the DoT before it detonates.
In one form, if any wizard casts a helpful Ward he Shatters it. Avoid shielding in that form.

name: Vestri
cheats: "Wrong brother! I'm it!" - Casting Spells at a Boss without the Stun Shield will trigger Vestri casting Rebirth for 750 Healing, without the Absorb.
strategy: He takes +35% from Fire and Death and resists Life 80%. Hit with Fire or Death.

His cheat: casting spells at a boss that does not have the Stun Shield makes Vestri Rebirth (750 heal). This is a multi-boss puzzle where you must target the correct, Stun-Shielded boss to avoid feeding heals.
Blade up and land a Fire or Death hit on the correct target.
cheatNotes: Attacking a boss that lacks the Stun Shield makes Vestri Rebirth for 750 healing. Target the Stun-Shielded boss so you do not feed the heal.

name: Vigilant Adapa
cheats: [Late] "Dishonorable late entry!" - If a Wizard joins the duel after it has already begun, Vigilant Adapa will cast Luminous Weaver on the Wizard.
[Global] "The Light subverts Shadow!" - At the beginning of Round 1, he will cast Power Play (Sun) for -100% Shadow Pip chance.
"The Light persists!" - If a Wizard casts a Global Spell, Adapa will recast the Power Play.
[Friend][Shadow Pip] "I take your Shadow!" - If a Wizard gains a Shadow Pip, he will cast Constrain on the Wizard, removing the Shadow Pip.
[Shadow Pip] "And turn it to Light!" - If Adapa has a Shadow Pip at the end of a Round, he will cast a Sun version of Dark Empower, converting it to a Power Pip and a regular Pip.
spellNotes: Deals 120 Sun Damage per Pip, despite the Spell card's 3-Pip maximum description.
strategy: Solo-friendly (8,400 health). He shuts off shadow pips: a round-1 Sun Power Play sets your shadow-pip chance to -100%, and he Constrains any you gain. Plan a normal-pip kill, not a shadow hit.
Do not cast your own globals; he recasts his Power Play each time you do.
Hit with Storm, Ice, or Death (+15%); he resists Fire, Myth, and Life 30%.
Enter on time: late entry gets you Luminous Weaver.
Blade up with regular pips and land one strong hit.
cheatNotes: Wizards who join late get Luminous Weaver cast on them.
Round 1 he casts a Sun Power Play setting your shadow-pip chance to -100%, so you will not earn shadow pips. Plan a normal-pip kill.
Casting your own global makes him recast that Power Play.
If you gain a shadow pip, he Constrains it away immediately.
Any shadow pip he holds at end of round he converts into a power pip plus a regular pip.

name: Vigilant Sargun (Rank 17)
cheats: [Late] "You're Late!"' - If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Vigilant Sargun will interrupt-cast Queen Calypso (Shadow) on the late Wizard.
[Round] "Let's equalize the situation" - At the beginning of the first Round, Vigilant Sargun will interrupt-cast Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 7,999.
[Global] "Not going to happen!" - If a Wizard attempts to replace the Global, Vigilant Sargun will recast it.
[Dispel] "You dare silence me?" - If a Wizard casts a Dispel on Vigilant Sargun, he will cast Sun version of Cleanse Charm on himself.
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Hex.
Deals 480 Fire Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
Deals 460 Myth Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
Deals 420 Life Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: Respect the damage cap. On Round 1 he Equalizes the fight, setting the damage cap to 7,999, so a single overkill nuke is wasted; plan a hit that lands near but not far over the cap.
Do not bother swapping the Global. If you replace it he just recasts it, so leave it and build your setup instead.
Skip Dispels on him. A Dispel makes him Cleanse Charm it off himself, so it accomplishes nothing; use blades and traps instead.
He is Sun-flagged but resists Fire, Myth, and Life at 70% and takes 5% boosted Ice, Storm, and Death, so hit with Ice, Storm, or Death.
Come in on time. Joining after Round 2 draws an interrupt Queen Calypso (Shadow) on the late wizard, and he has strong outgoing and incoming heals (45%/50%), so pack an Infection or Doom.
Watch his protected Balanceblade and Mass Hex; stack blades and Feint, then hit for close to the 7,999 cap and clear the Trained Death Ant with AoE.
cheatNotes: Joining the battle after Round 2 makes him interrupt-cast Queen Calypso (Shadow) on the late wizard, so be present early.
On Round 1 he interrupt-casts Equalize, capping all damage at 7,999; oversized nukes are wasted, so size your hit to the cap.
If you try to replace the Global spell, he simply recasts it, so leave it alone.
If you cast a Dispel on him, he Cleanse-Charms it off himself, making the Dispel useless.

name: Vigilant Sargun (Rank 18)
cheats: [Late] "You're Late!" - If a Wizard joins the battle after the second Round, Vigilant Sargun will interrupt-cast Queen Calypso (Shadow) on the late Wizard.
[Round] "Let's equalize the situation" - At the beginning of the first Round, Vigilant Sargun will interrupt-cast Equalize, which sets the Damage cap to 7,999.
[Global] "Not going to happen!" - If a Wizard attempts to replace the Global, Vigilant Sargun will recast it.
[Dispel] "You dare silence me?" - If a Wizard casts a Dispel on Vigilant Sargun, he will cast Sun version of Cleanse Charm on himself.
spellNotes: Casts a Mass version of Hex.
Deals 480 Fire Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
Deals 460 Myth Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
Deals 420 Life Damage for 4 Pips maximum.
strategy: At Round 1 he casts Equalize, capping all damage at 7,999, so you cannot one-shot him; plan for at least two hits.
He recasts his Global if you try to replace it and Cleanse-Charms himself if you Dispel him, so do not waste turns fighting his Global or dispelling.
Join before the second Round; a late wizard gets hit with Shadow Queen Calypso.
He is stunable and beguilable, resists Fire, Myth, and Life 70%, and takes 5% more from Ice, Storm, and Death, so bring Ice, Storm, or Death and use a stun to buy a setup Round.
Because of the 7,999 damage cap, build two solid hits rather than one giant one, and heal through his Fire, Myth, and Life natural attacks.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins after the second Round is interrupt-cast with Shadow Queen Calypso.
At Round 1 he interrupt-casts Equalize, capping all damage at 7,999.
If you try to replace his Global, he recasts it.
If you Dispel him, he casts a Sun Cleanse Charm on himself to clear it.

name: Vinnie Vicegrip

stats.resist: 8% to all schools43% to all schools

cheats: [Ward] "Ain't nothing piercing my shell" - At the start of every Round, Vinnie Vicegrip will interrupt-cast Tower Shield on himself.
[Ward] "Boom! Shell still goin' strong" - If the Tower Shield is removed by an attack, Vinnie Vicegrip will interrupt-cast Tower Shield on himself.
[Healing] If Vinnie Vicegrip is defeated while his Tower Shield cheat is still active, then he will revive with full health.
[Spell] "You can't beat me! It'd take an elephant to crack my shell" - At the start of the fourth Round, or after reviving himself, Vinnie Vicegrip will give each Wizard the following Spell:
[Helephant (Tier 2b) (Battle Card)]
[Fire] "Hey, where'd that elephant come from?" - If a Wizard casts the given Helephant on Vinnie Vicegrip, then he will remove all Wards from himself, ending the Tower Shield cheats.

"Ah, my shell don't feel right no more!" - If a Wizard casts a Fire Spell that removes helpful Wards other than Helephant on Vinnie Vicegrip, such as Fire Elf (Tier 2b), Vinnie Vicegrip will remove all Wards from himself and end his Tower Shield cheats.
strategy: Hit Fire or Myth, each +35%, and avoid Storm and Death, which he resists 43%.
He self-shields every Round and revives to full if killed while the shield cheat is on, so do not just race him.
On Round 4 he hands everyone a Helephant card. Cast it on him, or use a Fire ward-remover like Fire Elf, to strip his shields and permanently end the cheat.
With the cheat off, stack blades and drop one Fire or Myth hit to finish him.
cheatNotes: He Tower Shields himself every Round, so a normal hit gets partly eaten.
Remove the shield with an attack and he immediately re-shields, so you cannot just chip through.
Killing him while the shield cheat is active revives him full. Turn the cheat off first.
On Round 4, or after reviving, he gives every Wizard a Helephant card.
Casting that given Helephant on him strips all his wards and permanently ends the Tower Shield cheat. This is the key.
Any Fire ward-removal spell like Fire Elf also strips his wards and ends the shield cheat.

name: Vizier Rafaj
locations: Mirage: Alkali Barrows - Tomb of the Forgotten (Mirage)
cheats: [Weakness] "Feel the rot!" - At the end of every Round, he will place a Weakness (-30%) on a random Wizard.
[Late] "You're late to the party!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Vizier Rafaj will cast Mana Burn on the Wizard.
[Trap] "Oldest trick on the Tablet!" - If a Wizard places a Trap on Vizier Rafaj or his Minion, he will place a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard.
[Blade][Global] "Oldest trick on the Tablet!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade or Global spell, he will cast Balanceblade (+30%) on himself.
strategy: Hit Life, Myth, or Death - he takes a small boost to all three and resists 30% Balance, so avoid Balance. Join on time to dodge Mana Burn.
Expect a -30% Weakness on a random wizard at the end of every round. Carry Cleanse Charm or just blade through it before your hit.
Do not trap him or his minion. A trap returns a +35% Hex onto you, raising your incoming damage.
Blades and globals feed him a +30% Balanceblade each time, so keep setup lean and blade on the same turn you hit.
With only 8,400 health and no punish on Feints from himself, set up a Feint plus a quick blade, clear the weakness, and finish with one Life, Myth, or Death hit.
cheatNotes: At the end of every round he places a -30% Weakness on a random wizard. Cleanse it or blade through it.
A wizard who joins after the fight starts gets hit with Mana Burn, draining pips.
Placing a trap on him or his minion returns a +35% Hex onto you, increasing your incoming damage.
Casting any blade or global makes him self-cast a +30% Balanceblade.

name: Voice of Death
cheats: "I speak into existence MORE PIPS!" - Voice of Death casts a Quicken on itself and all Wizards, giving max Power Pips when anyone is lacking an Aura.
"I speak your PIPS INTO PAIN!" - At the end of every Round, if a Wizard has Pips remaining, Voice of Death will cast Mana Burn on all Wizards.
"That shall become... PIPS!" - If a Wizard casts an X-Pip Charm or Ward, Voice of Death will remove the hanging effect and convert it into Pips (needs clarification: how many pips?)
strategy: Raid boss, 56,500 health, team roles. The core rule: never end a round with pips. He Mana Burns the whole team at the end of any round a wizard still holds pips, so spend everything every turn.
Keep an Aura on everyone. When anyone lacks an aura he Quickens the team to max power pips, which then feeds his Mana Burn.
Do not cast X-pip charms or wards; he converts them straight into pips, which then get burned.
Structure setup so each wizard fully spends its pips each round: cheap blades and Feints and timed hits, no banking pips for a big turn.
Funnel to the hitter and burst in windows where pips are spent cleanly.
cheatNotes: When anyone lacks an Aura he Quickens the team to max power pips. Keep auras up to avoid the pip flood.
At the end of any round a wizard still has pips, he Mana Burns the whole team. Spend all your pips every single round.
Casting an X-pip charm or ward makes him remove it and turn it into pips. Avoid X-pip spells here.

name: Volkir Rimeaxe
locations: Grizzleheim: Savarstaad Pass - Volkir Rimeaxe's Crag
cheats: "A Charm! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Blade will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
Global Spells will trigger this cheat, but Aura Spells will not.
Pet Spells will not trigger this cheat.
"A ward! Let's even the playing field!" - Casting a Trap (including Feint) or a Shield will cause him to interrupt-cast a Hex (+35%) on the Wizard who cast it.
The text for this cheat only appears after Volkir casts the Hex.
strategy: Hit with Storm (+35%), avoid Myth (35% resist).

Blades, globals, traps (including Feint), and shields all make him Hex (+35%) the caster, so every normal buff puts a damage-trap on you. Buff with Auras and pet-cast spells instead, which do not trigger the Hex.
At only 3,590 health you do not need much setup, an Aura plus one Storm hit ends it.
If you must blade, accept that each Hex is only on you, and hit before it matters or cleanse it.
Open with an aura, then land a single big Storm attack.
cheatNotes: Cast a blade and he interrupt-Hexes (+35%) you, buff with auras or pets instead.
Globals also trigger the Hex, but Auras do NOT, use auras to buff safely.
Pet-cast spells do not trigger the Hex either.
Cast a trap (Feint included) or a shield and he Hexes you the same way.
The cheat text only shows up after he casts the Hex, so expect it whenever you buff or trap.

name: Wally
cheats: "Where oh where is Wally?" - At the beginning of Round 1, Wally will summon two Wally (Fake)s and one Wally (Real). Then, he will cast a -300% Invulnerability Brace Aura on himself. Summoned Minions have their Health concealed.
"You didn't find Wally!" - If a Wally (Fake) takes Damage, Wally will cast Colossafrog.

"You found me!" - If Wally (Real) takes Damage, Wally will cast a Star version of Supernova, removing his Aura and dealing no Damage to himself, and then cast a Shadow Spell to expel all Minions one by one.
All Enemy Spells will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Wally is invulnerable under a -300% aura and summons two fake Wallys and one real Wally with hidden health on Round 1.

Hit the summons with single-target spells to find the real Wally. Damaging a fake makes Wally cast Colossafrog on you, but damaging the real one makes him Supernova off his own aura and expel the minions.
Do not AoE to find him; all-enemy spells will not trigger the reveal. Poke each summon single-target.
Once the aura is gone and minions expelled, blade up and hit Wally with Life (+30%, resists Death 67%).
Burst him down in the vulnerable window.
cheatNotes: He is invulnerable under a -300% aura and hides among two fake Wallys and one real one, so you must find the real Wally to break it.
Damaging a fake Wally gets you Colossafrog, so expect a Death hit each time you guess wrong.
Damaging the real Wally makes him Supernova off his own aura and expel the minions, which is how you make him vulnerable.
AoE spells will not trigger the reveal, so poke each summon with single-target hits to find the real one.

name: Warden Vissarovich
locations: Polaris: The Basstille - Cell Block A
cheats: [Late] "Tardiness Will Not Be Tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Warden Vissarovich will cheat-cast Frostbite on the Wizard.
[Absorb] "Time for me to suit up!" - Warden Vissarovich will cast Frozen Armor on himself on the first Round.
[Round] Cycle:
[Blade] "Cold discipline always does the trick." - He will cast Iceblade on himself.
[Trap]"Welcome to your new home... the Basstille!" - He will cast Hailstorm.
[Damage] "This is what you get for challenging authority!" - He will cast Blizzard.
[Aura] "I've been trained to deal with such rabble." - He will cast Amplify.
[Damage] "Smash one and the rest will scatter!" - He will cast Woolly Mammoth (600-700) if he has enough pips, otherwise he will pass.
