[Heal over Time][Blade] "I didn't become warden by being weak!" - His Pet will cast Availing Hands (85+870) and he will cast Balanceblade (+30%).
[Round] "A wise warden bides his time!" - He uses his turn to pass.
[Damage]"How about a taste of polarian justice!" - He will cast Judgement (125).
[Shield] "Tower defense is no game." - He will cast Tower Shield on himself.
[Blade] "Cold discipline always does the trick." - The cycle repeats but gives him two Iceblades every repeated cycle.
strategy: Hit with Fire (+15%); he resists Ice 50%.
He opens with Frozen Armor. Break the Absorb with Shatter or pierce before your real hit.

He runs a fixed cycle, stacking Iceblades on himself (two per cycle after the first) and an Amplify Aura, then hitting with Hailstorm, Blizzard, Woolly Mammoth (600-700), or Judgement (125). Shield Ice and heal through the big hits.
His pet heals him with Availing Hands, so pressure him faster than he and the pet can recover, and consider an infection to cut the heal.
Set up blades behind the broken Absorb and finish with one Fire hit before his Iceblades and Tower Shield stack up.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets you Frostbitten.
On Round 1 he shields himself with a Frozen Armor Absorb. Shatter or pierce it.
Cycle: he Iceblades himself, two per cycle after the first loop.
Cycle: he casts Hailstorm.
Cycle: he casts Blizzard. Shield Ice.
Cycle: he casts Amplify to boost his damage.
Cycle: he casts Woolly Mammoth (600-700) if he has the pips, else passes.
Cycle: his pet casts Availing Hands (85+870 heal) and he casts Balanceblade.
Cycle: he passes a turn.
Cycle: he casts Judgement (125).
Cycle: he Tower Shields himself.
The cycle then repeats, giving him two Iceblades each loop.

name: Water-Wing
cheats: "Where did Everyone go?" - Water-Wing will take over casting the Doom and Gloom cycle, after the other two Bosses have been defeated, starting with the three Round white Doom and Gloom.

"No Traps?!" / "No Blades?!" - If a Wizard casts a single-target attack Spell without activating a Trap Damage Ward (during a light cycle) or Blade Damage Charm (during a dark cycle), then the enemy that was attacked will cheat-cast the following Spell:
The Water-Wing will cast a 750 Healing version of Rebirth (without the Absorbs).
(Spell heals for 375 with Doom and Gloom)
Sylster Glowstorm will cast an enhanced version of Ra, which deals 1,630 Balance Damage.
The Spellhammer Sorcerer will cast Scarecrow.
"All Enemy" Attack Spells will not trigger this cheat.
Spells that deal Damage to one target initially, then Damage "all enemies" will trigger this cheat (e.g.Sun Serpent).
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Fire Damage.
strategy: Hit with Ice or Storm. Water-Wing takes +35% from both and resists Fire 80%. Never attack into its Fire resist.

Respect the light/dark Doom and Gloom cycle. On a single-target attack you must consume a Trap during the light (white) cycle, or a Blade during the dark cycle. Hit "naked" and the boss you struck counters: Water-Wing self-heals 750 (Rebirth), Sylster Glowstorm fires a 1,630 Ra, or the Spellhammer Sorcerer casts Scarecrow.

Easiest workaround: use all-enemy (AoE) attacks. "All Enemy" spells don't trigger the cheat at all. Note that a single-then-AoE spell like Sun Serpent still counts as single-target and does trigger it.
So the clean line is to keep a Blade up (dark cycle) or a Trap up (light cycle) matched to the current phase, then land a single-target Ice or Storm hit that consumes it, or just AoE the trio down.
Water-Wing only takes over the Doom and Gloom cycle after the other two bosses are down, so kill order still ends on it. Keep the cycle rule in mind the whole fight.
cheatNotes: Once Sylster and the Spellhammer Sorcerer are down, Water-Wing runs the Doom and Gloom cycle itself, opening with the three-round white (light) phase.
Attack single-target without consuming a Trap in the light cycle or a Blade in the dark cycle and the boss you hit punishes you. Always have the matching charm or trap up, or use AoE.
Water-Wing's counter is a 750 heal (375 under Doom and Gloom), so a naked hit just heals it.
Sylster's counter is a 1,630 Balance Ra to the face, so never hit him without a blade or trap active.
The Spellhammer Sorcerer counters with Scarecrow, draining your team.
AoE (all-enemy) attacks are exempt and never trigger the counter, so they're the safe way to hit.
Spells that hit one target then splash to all, like Sun Serpent, still count as single-target and DO trigger the counter.

name: Were-Bear Brute (Challenge)

stats.resist: 26% to all schools46% to all schools66% to all schools

cheats: [Afterlife] "*growl* I'll get you yet." - When Mysterious Werewolf is defeated, he will revive himself for 100% of his max health and will exit combat at the end of the turn.
Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle repeats every 4 rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is still alive.

[Heal] "You will not fall!" - At the beginning of the first round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a special version of Pigsie that heals all allies and applies 3 heal over times for 4 rounds.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - At the beginning of the fourth round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a special version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% damage charms to all allies.
strategy: Kill the Mysterious Werewolf first. While it lives it heals the whole enemy team (Pigsie plus HoTs) and blades them (two +30% each) on a 4-round cycle.
Expect the Werewolf to survive one kill: it revives once at full health, then leaves combat, ending those buffs. Burn it down twice or overkill it so it is gone before you commit to the boss.
Storm boss with a huge 164% damage boost; his hits hurt, so keep shields up. Use your strongest boosted school (his lowest resist is Fire and Life at 66%).
Team: the hitter focuses the Werewolf out first, then the group AoEs the Brute and Bauk Scavenger. Blade the hitter the same round to beat the heal cycle.
cheatNotes: When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated it revives once at full health, then exits combat at end of turn.
The buff cycle repeats every 4 rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is alive.
At the start of the cycle's first round the Werewolf casts a Pigsie that heals all allies and gives them three HoTs for 4 rounds.
At the start of the cycle's fourth round the Werewolf casts a Bladestorm giving all allies two +30% damage charms.

name: Were-Bear Brute (Standard)
cheats: [Afterlife] "*Growl* I'll get you yet." - When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated, he will revive himself for 100% of his max Health and will exit combat at the end of the turn.
Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle repeats every 4 rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is still alive.

[Heal] "You will not fall!" - At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a version of Pigsie that heals all allies and applies 3 Heal over Time effects for 4 rounds.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - At the beginning of the fourth Round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% Damage Charms on all allies.
strategy: Hit with Myth - he takes a huge 66% more from Myth and resists Storm 66%. Myth wizards or a Myth prism shred him.
A Mysterious Werewolf backs him up, healing the team with Pigsie (round 1 of its cycle) and blading them with Bladestorm (round 4). Kill the Werewolf to stop the heals and blades.
The Werewolf revives once at full health when killed, then leaves combat at end of that turn - so kill it, let it revive and exit, then focus the boss uninterrupted.
Stack blades/Feint on your hitter and land a big Myth hit; time it so the Werewolf's fresh HoTs and blades do not undo your burst.
cheatNotes: When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated it revives at 100% health, then leaves combat at end of turn. Kill it once and let it exit.
The Werewolf's support cycle repeats every 4 rounds until it is gone.
Cycle round 1: the Werewolf casts Pigsie, healing its team and adding 3 HoTs for 4 rounds. Kill it to stop the healing.
Cycle round 4: the Werewolf Bladestorms two +30% charms onto its team. Another reason to remove it early.

name: Werewolf Tracker (Challenge)

stats.resist: 25% to all schools45% to all schools65% to all schools

cheats: [Afterlife] "*growl* I'll get you yet." - When Mysterious Werewolf is defeated, he will revive himself for 100% of his max health and will exit combat at the end of the turn.
Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle repeats every 4 rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is still alive.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - At the beginning of the first round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a special version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% damage charms to all allies.
[Trap] "We hunt as a pack!" - At the beginning of the second round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a Death spell that applies three +35% universal traps on a wizard.
strategy: Attack with Storm or Life, his lowest resists at 25%; avoid Ice and Myth (65% resist) and expect 45% on most other schools.
Expect the Mysterious Werewolf to revive once. When killed it comes back at full Health and leaves at the end of that turn, so it dies for good on the second kill and its cycle stops.
Clear the traps it stacks on you. Its cycle round two puts three +35% universal traps on one wizard, boosting the damage that wizard takes, so remove them before the boss hits.
Focus the Tracker while the Werewolf feeds its cycle (two +30% blades on round one, the traps on round two, repeating every four Rounds).
At 15,270 Health with two minions, run it as a team: funnel blades to a hitter, Feint the Tracker, and burst.
cheatNotes: When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated it revives at 100% Health and then leaves combat at the end of that turn, so it dies for good on the second kill.
The cheat cycle repeats every four Rounds for as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is alive.
Cycle Round 1: the Werewolf casts a special Bladestorm giving its whole side two +30% damage charms.
Cycle Round 2: it stacks three +35% universal traps on one wizard, boosting the damage that wizard takes, so clear them before the boss's hit.

name: Werewolf Tracker (Standard)

stats.resist: 29% to all schools59% to all schools

cheats: [Afterlife] "*Growl* I'll get you yet." - When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated, he will revive himself for 100% of his max Health and will exit combat at the end of the turn.
Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle repeats every 4 Rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is still alive.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - At the beginning of the first Round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% Damage Charms on all allies.
[Trap] "We hunt as a pack!" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a Death spell that applies three +35% Damage Wards on all Wizards.
strategy: Attack with Ice; the Tracker takes +30% from it and resists Fire 59%, everything else 29%. He is not stunable.
Do not try to keep the Mysterious Werewolf dead. When defeated it revives at full health and leaves at end of turn, so focus the Tracker instead.
While the Werewolf lives, a 4-round cycle buffs the enemy team, so expect their hits to escalate.
Watch the three +35% damage wards the cycle stacks on all wizards in its second round; those make the enemies' next hits land much harder, so clear them or shield up.
Stack blades and finish the Tracker with one big Ice hit before the buff cycle turns their damage on you.
cheatNotes: When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated it revives at full health and then leaves at end of turn. You cannot keep it dead, so focus the Tracker.
The Werewolf's buff cycle repeats every 4 rounds as long as it is alive.
Cycle round 1: a Bladestorm puts two +30% damage charms on the whole enemy team.
Cycle round 2: a Death spell stacks three +35% damage wards on all wizards, so the enemies' next hits land much harder. Clear them or shield up.

name: Winged Sorrow
locations: Darkmoor: Black Lagoon - Lair of the Winged Sorrow

stats.resist: 42% to all schools72% to all schools

cheats: [Spell] "Music is truth, truth music." - On turn 1, give all wizards a 1-pip Star spell called "Exit Combat" that allows them to leave combat without penalty.
[Global] "Perish in the storm!" - After a wizard sets the brazier to shadow fire, Winged Sorrow will cast a global spell resembling Darkwind and become untargetable.
[Heal] "The thunder is harsh and dangerous!" - If a wizard uses a healing spell while this global is active, Winged Sorrow will heal back to full health.

While this global is active, at the end of each turn, Winged Sorrow will be struck by lightning and gain full power pips. Despite having the visual appearance of a wand hit, this effect deals no damage.
[Global] "Flourish in the sunshine!" - After a wizard sets the brazier to regular fire, Winged Sorrow will cast a global spell resembling Power Play and become targetable.
Beginning on the second round after this global becomes active, each wizard will take damage equal to 25% of their health at the end of each round.

[Round] "The clear day is calm and enchanting" - Activates the turn after the brazier is switched to regular fire and clears all hanging effects from everyone in combat. If a wizard casts any hanging effect or aura while this global is active, this cheat will activate and will immediately clear the effect.
While this global is active, Winged Sorrow will not cast any attack spells, only buffs.
Randomly applies a -50% chromatic Weakness to wizards after they take damage at the end of the round. It is unknown what, if anything, causes this weakness to be cast.
strategy: Team fight (55,037 health). Assign a hitter using Life or a Life prism (he takes +30% Life and resists Death 72%, everything else 42%) and a support to manage the brazier.
Turn 1 you each get an Exit Combat card; ignore it unless you need to bail.
Keep the brazier on regular fire to make him targetable. On shadow fire he is untargetable, and healing anyone heals him to full, so do not heal while shadow fire is up.
While regular fire is up, all hanging effects are cleared every round, so blade and hit in the same round rather than pre-stacking blades that will be wiped.
Expect 25% max-health damage per round on regular fire plus random -50% chromatic Weaknesses; keep the healer topping the team and cleanse weaknesses off the hitter before the big hit.
Funnel same-round blades to the hitter and unload while the boss is targetable.
cheatNotes: Turn 1 everyone gets a 1-pip Exit Combat card to leave the fight penalty-free.
Setting the brazier to shadow fire makes him cast a Darkwind-like global and go untargetable.
While that shadow-fire global is up, any heal you cast heals Winged Sorrow to full. Do not heal.
On shadow fire he is struck by lightning each turn and refills his power pips, with no damage to you.
Setting the brazier to regular fire makes him cast a Power Play-like global and become targetable again.
Two rounds into the regular-fire global, every wizard loses 25% of their health at the end of each round.
The turn after you switch to regular fire, all hanging effects are wiped, and any blade, trap, or aura you cast while it is up is cleared instantly. Set up and hit in one round.
While that global is up he only buffs himself and casts no attacks.
He randomly slaps a -50% chromatic Weakness on wizards after they take end-of-round damage. Cleanse it off your hitter.

name: Wolf-Man Infiltrator (Challenge)

stats.resist: 31% to all schools51% to all schools71% to all schools

cheats: [Afterlife] "*growl* I'll get you yet." - When Mysterious Werewolf is defeated, he will revive himself for 100% of his max health and will exit combat at the end of the turn.
Cheat Cycle
This cheat cycle repeats every 4 rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is still alive.
[Blade] "Sharpen your claws!" - At the beginning of the first round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a special version of Bladestorm that applies two +30% damage charms to all allies.
[Shield] "Brace yourself!" - At the beginning of the second round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a -40% version of Legion Shield.
strategy: Deal with the Mysterious Werewolf first. It revives to 100% when killed and then leaves combat at the end of that turn, so a kill removes it permanently; its cheat cycle only runs while it is alive.

Race the 4-round buff cycle. While the Werewolf lives, it Bladestorms two +30% charms onto all allies on the first cycle round and Legion-Shields the enemy team on the second, so time your burst before those stack up.
Kill the Werewolf to shut off the blades and shields, then focus the Infiltrator with your funneled stack.
It is Myth with 71% Ice/Death resist and 51% across most schools, taking least resist to Fire and Life (31%), so bring a Fire or Life hitter or prisms.
This is a Challenge team fight: 14,825 Health plus two elite minions. Funnel blades to one hitter, have supports Feint the boss and clear the Bauk Scavenger, keep a healer active.
Set up over a couple of rounds, remove the Werewolf to stop its buff cycle, then AoE the Infiltrator and remaining minion down together.
cheatNotes: When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated it revives to full Health and then exits combat at the end of the turn, so killing it removes it for good and stops its cheat cycle.
On the first round of its cycle the Werewolf casts a special Bladestorm giving two +30% damage charms to all its allies, so the enemy side ramps damage fast.
On the second round of its cycle the Werewolf casts a -40% Legion Shield on the enemy team, adding defense you must hit through.

This cheat cycle repeats every 4 Rounds as long as the Mysterious Werewolf is still alive.

[Shield] "Brace yourself!" - At the beginning of the second Round in this cycle, Mysterious Werewolf will cast a Balance version of Legion Shield that applies a -40% Damage Ward on all allies.
strategy: His minion, the Mysterious Werewolf, revives once at full Health and then leaves combat, so expect to kill it twice before it is gone for good.
While the Werewolf lives it runs a 4-Round cycle: a double +30% Bladestorm on Round 1 and a -40% Balance Legion Shield on all allies on Round 2, so both bosses get buffed and shielded.
Break the Legion Shield or pierce through it before your real hit, and try to time your burst after the shield Round.
The boss is Myth and resists Myth 69% and other schools 39% but takes 30% more from Storm, so bring Storm.
Funnel blades to one hitter, deal with the reviving Werewolf, then burst the Infiltrator through the shield cycle.
cheatNotes: When the Mysterious Werewolf is defeated, it revives at 100% max Health and leaves combat at the end of the turn.
This minion cheat cycle repeats every 4 Rounds as long as the Werewolf is alive.
On the first Round of the cycle the Werewolf casts a Bladestorm giving two +30% damage charms to all allies.

name: Wretched Elexorien (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 1)
cheats: Starts the first Round with Weakness (-30%) to All Friends, and repeats every three Rounds.
Boss does not cast Weakness, though it is only applied when Boss is alive.
strategy: Low-health Life boss; hit with Death (+30%) and avoid Life (60% resist).
He is stunable and beguilable; a stun buys you a free setup Round.
He applies a -30% Weakness to all his allies on Round 1 and every third Round (only while he is alive), which slightly weakens the enemy side, not you.
At 1,560 health a single Death blade and one hit end him; clear the Brume Goblin with AoE if needed.
cheatNotes: Every third Round (from Round 1) a -30% Weakness lands on his own side while he is alive; it works against him, not you.

name: Wretched Elexorien (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 2)
cheats: Elexorien will place a Weakness (-30%, 0 Pip) on all opponents at the start of the first Round, and will repeat this at the start of every third Round.
strategy: Life boss (3660) that is stunable and beguilable. Hit with Death (+30%); never Life (60% resist).
He drops a -30% Weakness on everyone at the start and again every 3rd round. Carry a Cleanse Charm or over-blade so the weakness does not gut your hit.
Clear the Brume Goblin or AoE both, stack blades, and land a Death hit. He is low health.
cheatNotes: He puts a -30% Weakness on all wizards on round 1 and every 3rd round after. Cleanse it before your big hit, or stack extra blades to cover the loss.

name: Wretched Elexorien (Tier 3)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 3)
cheats: [Weakness] Starts the first Round with Weakness to All Wizards, and repeats every three Rounds.
Boss does not cast Weakness, though it is only applied when Boss is alive.
strategy: Hit Fire or Death, each +30%, and never Life, which he resists 80%; prism if you are Life.
Clear the all-Wizard Weakness, refreshed every third Round, with a Cleanse Charm the Round before you hit so your damage is not reduced.
He is stunable, so a stun buys a clean setup or hit Round.
Kill the Brume Goblin with your AoE, or blade up and drop one Fire or Death hit on the boss.
cheatNotes: Every Wizard starts with a Weakness, refreshed every third Round, cutting your damage. Cleanse it before you hit.
The Weakness only applies while the boss is alive, and he does not cast it himself, so a Cleanse Charm right before your hit lands clean.

name: Wretched Elexorien (Tier 4)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 4)
cheats: [Weakness] Elexorien will place a Weakness on all opponents at start of Round 1, and will repeat this at the start of every third Round.
strategy: Hit Fire or Death. He takes +35% from both and resists 80% Life, so never attack into Life. He is stunable if you need a setup round.
Plan around his Weakness cycle: he weakens the whole team at the start of round 1 and again every third round.
Carry Cleanse Charm to strip the Weakness before you swing, or time your big hit on the rounds between the weakness casts.
Nothing here punishes blades, traps, or Feints, so set up normally - stack Fire or Death blades and a Feint on your hitter.
Clear the Brume Goblin minion, cleanse the team Weakness, then land one big Fire or Death hit through his 10,200 health.
cheatNotes: He places a Weakness on the whole team at the start of round 1 and repeats it every third round. Cleanse Charm it or hit on the rounds between so your damage lands full.

name: Wretched Elexorien (Tier 5)
locations: Housing Instance: Winterbane Hall - Winterbane Hall (Tier 5)
cheats: Every three Rounds, Wretched Elexorian (Tier 5) will interrupt-cast Weakness Treasure Cards on all Wizards.
Every few Rounds, he will interrupt-cast a "Dispel All" Spell on one random Wizard. Need confirmation for the "Spell cast" and when it is triggered (number of Rounds?).
spellNotes: Deals an unknown quantity of Life Damage.
strategy: Attack with Death or Fire (+35%); he resists Life 80%. He is stunable and beguilable, so stuns buy setup time.
Every 3 rounds he Weaknesses the whole team, so pack Cleanse Charm, or blade over the weakness before the hitter goes.
He also Dispels a random wizard every few rounds, so do not over-rely on a single aura or charm the hitter needs; set up the round you hit.
Clear the Brume Goblin minion with an AoE or focus the boss.
Funnel blades to the hitter, Cleanse weaknesses off them, then burst with a boosted Death or Fire hit.
cheatNotes: Every 3 rounds he throws Weakness cards on all wizards. Cleanse them off the hitter or blade over them before attacking.
Every few rounds he Dispels a random wizard, so set up on the same round you hit rather than banking buffs.

name: Xibalba Elemental
cheats: "What is death can never die!" - At the beginning of Round 1, Xibalba Elemental casts an Equalize Global, setting the Damage cap to 1.
After the first cheat, Xibalba Elemental casts one of the following Invunerability Auras:
[Balance] - "Xibalba brings balance!" - Immune to all schools except Balance for 99 Rounds.
[Fire] - "Xibalba burns!" - Immune to all schools except Storm and Life for 99 Rounds.
[Ice] - "Xibalba freezes!" - Immune to all schools except Fire and Myth for 99 Rounds.
[Storm] - "Xibalba crackles!" - Immune to all schools except Ice and Death for 99 Rounds.
[Death] - "Xibalba is death!" - Immune to all schools except Ice and Myth for 99 Rounds.
[Life] - "Xibalba is life!" - Immune to all schools except Storm and Death for 99 Rounds.
[Myth] - "Xibalba is legend!" - Immune to all schools except Fire and Life for 99 Rounds.
"No!" - If a Wizard uses Supernova, it will deal 1 Damage. Xibalba Elemental will then use Blessing on himself, healing for 100% of his max Health.
Because this cheat deals Damage, the next cheat will be activated.
"Aaaahhhhhh!" - If Xibalba Elemental takes any Damage, he will use a no-Damage version of Supernova on himself, then use Exit Combat on the caster, and change to a new Immunity Aura.
"Death comes for you all!" - At the end of every Round, Xibalba Elemental casts a version of Deer Knight that deals 20% max Health Damage on all Wizards.
"I feel... MORTALITY." - After 7 Aura cycles, Xibalba Elemental will cast Doom and Gloom, removing the Damage cap.
(Needs confirmation on Doom and Gloom version).
strategy: Crying Sky raid boss: it caps all damage at 1 with Equalize and shields itself with an Invulnerability Aura, so you cannot burst its 100 health early.
Each aura makes it immune to all schools except two announced ones, and any damage it takes Exit-Combats the caster and swaps the aura — so expect to cycle through it, not tank it down.
Survive its aura cycles: it Deer Knights the whole team for 20% max health at the end of every round, so keep healed and manage the chip.
After 7 aura cycles it casts Doom & Gloom and removes the damage cap — that's your window to finish its 100 health with the currently allowed school.
Don't Supernova it (that only deals 1 and heals it to full); wait out the cycles, then hit with an allowed school.
cheatNotes: Round 1 it casts an Equalize Global setting the damage cap to 1 — no real damage lands until this is gone.
It then puts up an Invulnerability Aura making it immune to all schools except a specific one or two (announced each time).
Using Supernova only deals 1 damage and lets it Blessing itself back to full health — don't Supernova it.
Any damage it takes makes it Supernova itself, Exit-Combat the caster, and swap to a new immunity aura — so you can't just grind it down.
At the end of every round it Deer Knights the whole team for 20% of max health — keep healed through the cycles.
After 7 aura cycles it casts Doom & Gloom and removes the damage cap — that's your window to finish its 100 health with the allowed school.

name: Yevgeny NightCreeper
cheats: [Blade] "I counter your Blade!" - If a Wizard casts a Blade Spell, Yevgeny will cheat-cast Freeze on the Wizard.
[Global] Global Spells will trigger this cheat.
Conviction can be used to try countering this cheat.
[Ward] "You give away your hand!" - If a Wizard casts a helpful Ward Spell (Shields, Absorbs, etc.), he will cheat-cast Pierce on the target of the Ward Spell.
Using a spell that gives the caster a ward, such as Sabertooth, will cause Yevgeny to use this cheat on himself.
"Leave this place!" - The Minions will also cheat-cast Steal Ward, in order to remove any remaining Wards on the Wizard.
[Curse] "I vex you!" - If a Wizard casts a harmful Charm, Yevgeny will cheat-cast Bad Juju on the Wizard.
Secondary Spell effects will trigger this cheat (e.g. Efreet's negative Charm).
Harmful Charms that target all enemies (e.g. Plague) will not trigger this cheat.
Interrupt Cycles
"I wrap you in armor!" - At the beginning of every Round in this sub-cycle, Yevgeny will cast Frozen Armor (185) on himself or one of his Minions.

"Let the cold wind destroy you!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this sub-cycle (and every fifteen Rounds after), he will cast Katabatic Wind, Mass Ice Trap, Iceblade (+45%), and Abominable Weaver. This Spell will target the Wizard with the lowest current Health.
"I empower you!" - At the beginning of every Round in this sub-cycle, Yevgeny will cast Buff Minion on himself or one of his Minions.
"Crush my enemies!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this sub-cycle (and every fifteen Rounds after), he will cast Saga of Heroes, Mass Myth Trap, a Mythblade (+40%), and Mystic Colossus.
"I decimate you!" - At the beginning of every Round in this sub-cycle, Yevgeny will cast a Feint (+75%) on a random Wizard.

"Klaatu Barada Nikto!" - At the beginning of the fifth Round in this sub-cycle (and every fifteen Rounds after), he will will cast Doom and Gloom (-75%), a Deathblade (+45%), Mass Death Trap, and Call of Khrulhu.
"Fear the howl of the moon!" - (Round 3) At the end of the third Round in this cycle, Yevgeny will cast Shift Chaney (3 Pip), dealing 800 Life Damage.
"Fear the bite of Dragonspyre!" - (Round 6) At the end of the sixth Round, he will cast a Fire version of Shift Akhtang, which deals 1,000 Fire Damage.
"Fear the Blackwater!" - (Round 9) At the end of the ninth Round, he will cast a Moon version of Shift Blackwater, which deals 1,200 Moon Damage.
strategy: Team fight (22k, Darkmoor Graveyard). Hit with Life (+30%) or Fire/Storm (+15%); avoid Ice (60%) and Myth/Death (30%).
Setup is punished three ways: any blade Freezes (stuns) the caster, any shield/absorb gets Pierced, and any single harmful charm gets Bad Juju'd. Bring Conviction to shrug off the Freeze/stun.
Use multi-target curses — Mass Feint and Plague don't trigger the Bad Juju, unlike single Weakness/Feint.
Wards are pointless here (they get Pierced) and he has 55% pierce himself, so lean on resist and heals over shields.
Every 5th round he unloads a big combo (Katabatic Wind / Saga + Mystic Colossus / Doom and Gloom + Call of Khrulhu) at the lowest-health wizard — top that wizard first.
Layer multi-target traps, blade the hitter through a Conviction window, and burst; watch the round 3/6/9 Shift hits (800 Life, 1,000 Fire, 1,200 Moon).
cheatNotes: Cast a blade and he Freezes (stuns) the caster — use Conviction to ignore it.
Global spells trigger the Freeze too.
Conviction can counter the Freeze/stun.
Cast a helpful ward (shield/absorb) and he Pierces the target — don't rely on shields.
Spells that give the caster a ward (like Sabertooth) make him Pierce himself.
His minions also Steal Ward any remaining wards off you.
Cast a single harmful charm and he Bad Jujus you.
Secondary curse effects (like Efreet's) trigger the Bad Juju too.
Multi-target curses like Plague do NOT trigger it — use those.
Each round in this sub-cycle he Frozen Armors himself or a minion.
Round 5 of it (and every 15th) he casts Katabatic Wind, Mass Ice Trap, Iceblade, and Abominable Weaver at the lowest-health wizard — top that wizard first.
Each round in another sub-cycle he Buff Minions himself or a minion.
Round 5 of it he casts Saga of Heroes, Mass Myth Trap, Mythblade, and Mystic Colossus.
Each round in another sub-cycle he Feints (+75%) a random wizard.
Round 5 of it he casts Doom and Gloom (-75%), Deathblade, Mass Death Trap, and Call of Khrulhu.
End of round 3 he casts Shift Chaney for 800 Life.
End of round 6 he casts Shift Akhtang for 1,000 Fire.
End of round 9 he casts Shift Blackwater for 1,200 Moon.

name: Ygor
cheats: "Tardiness will not be tolerated!" - If a Wizard joins after the duel has started, Ygor will cast a -100% Virulent Plague that also applies a -100% Black Mantle, and a -100% Infection on the Wizard. Summoned Minions don't trigger this cheat.
(Fanmade Spell to represent this cheat - Impair)
"Please don't hurt me!" - In the first Round, Ygor will cast Breeze Shield which applies a -70% Life and Ice Shield.
"Why are we fighting?" - Every six Rounds, Ygor will cast another Breeze Shield.
"It hurts! Book, please help." - Ygor's Grimoire Pet will cheat-cast Power Link when Ygor or his Minions are attacked.
spellNotes: Ultra Blaze and Ultra Torment are 3 Pip maximum.
His Pet, Ygor's Grimoire, will cast Poison, Deer Knight, Rain of Fire, and Burning Rampage.
strategy: Death boss (19,760) with no listed boost. Avoid Life and Ice attacks; his Breeze Shield gives him -70% to Life and Ice, refreshed every six rounds. Hit with Fire, Storm, Myth, or a prism.
His Grimoire pet casts Power Link to heal him whenever he or his minions are attacked, so expect some healback. Use Doom and Gloom or Infection to cut it and burst hard.
Join on time; a late wizard eats a -100% Virulent Plague, -100% Black Mantle, and -100% Infection, crippling accuracy and damage.
Blade up, avoid Life and Ice, and land a big off-school hit; watch for his pet's own Deer Knight and Burning Rampage casts.
cheatNotes: Late arrival gets hit with -100% Virulent Plague, Black Mantle, and Infection, a crippling triple debuff. Zone in on time.
Round 1 he casts Breeze Shield, giving himself -70% to Life and Ice. Do not attack with Life or Ice; use another school.
He refreshes that Breeze Shield every six rounds, so the Life and Ice resistance keeps coming back. Stick to your off-school hitter.
His Grimoire pet Power Links to heal him whenever he or his minions are hit. Use Infection or Doom and Gloom and burst through the healback.

name: Yogash
cheats: "Too small! Try again!" - Spells that cost less than 3 Pips will result in Yogash interrupt-casting a Tower Shield on himself.
Damage Spells costing less than 3 Pips, result in Yogash interrupt-casting a version of Satyr on himself that restores 5,000 Health, then casts a Tower Shield on himself.
X Pip spells only trigger this cheat if the Spell costs less than 3 Pips.
Global Spells costing less than 3 Pips, result in Yogash interrupt-casting Tower Shield on himself and a -5% version of Doom and Gloom.
If a Wizard casts a Healing or Manipulation Spell, then Yogash will interrupt-cast Wraith on the caster.
Steal Spells result in Yogash interrupt-casting a Tower Shield on himself. (Example:Ghoul)
Polymorph Spells will cause Yogash to interrupt-cast Orthrus on the caster.
Taco Toss and Stinky Salute will result in Yogash casting Satyr on himself, and Wraith at the Wizard.
Unknown Cheat Trigger:
If a Wizard casts a Steal Spell, then Yogash will interrupt-cast a -90% version of Tower Shield on himself. (Ghoul does not trigger this cheat) Needs confirmation if this cheat still triggers.
Manipulation Spells that steal hanging effects do not trigger this cheat.
(Example:Steal Charm, Steal Ward, Snow Drift)
Cooldown does not trigger this cheat.
X Pip Minion Spells Spells do not trigger this cheat.
Global Spells that cost more than 2 Pips do not trigger this cheat.
Aura Spells, Shadow Self Spells, Shadow Creature Spells, and Pet May Cast Spells will not trigger this cheat.
Charms and Wards that are cast by active Shadow Creatures will not trigger this cheat.
strategy: Never hit him with a damage spell under 3 pips; a cheap attack heals him 5,000 with Satyr. Only attack with 3+ pip spells.
Any spell under 3 pips (blades, cheap globals, cheap Feints) makes him Tower Shield himself. Expect shields and bring pierce or Shatter, or keep setup minimal.
Avoid Healing and Manipulation spells; they trigger a Wraith on the caster. Steal spells and cheap globals also just shield him.
Auras and Shadow spells do not trigger the cheat, so they are safe ways to buff.
Hit with Myth (+35% incoming); avoid Storm (35% resist). Land one big enchanted 3+ pip hit through his Tower Shield to drop the 6585 health.
cheatNotes: Any spell under 3 pips makes him Tower Shield himself, so cheap blades and Feints will shield him.
A damage spell under 3 pips is worse: he Satyrs himself for 5,000 health, then shields. Never poke him with cheap attacks.
X-pip spells only trigger this if they actually cost under 3 pips, so pump them to 3+.
A global under 3 pips makes him Tower Shield and drop a -5% Doom and Gloom, cutting your healing.
Casting a Heal or Manipulation spell triggers a Wraith on the caster. Avoid healing here.
Steal spells also make him Tower Shield himself (e.g. Ghoul).
Polymorph spells trigger an Orthrus on the caster.
Taco Toss and Stinky Salute make him Satyr himself and Wraith you.
A steal spell may also trigger a -90% Tower Shield on himself (Ghoul excepted), unconfirmed.
Manipulation spells that steal hanging effects do not trigger the cheat.
Cooldown does not trigger the cheat.
X-pip minion spells do not trigger it.
Globals costing 3+ pips are safe.
Auras, Shadow Self, Shadow Creature, and pet may-cast spells do not trigger it, safe ways to set up.
Charms and wards cast by your active Shadow Creatures do not trigger it either.

name: Yoshinobu (Tier 1)
locations: Housing Instance: Midnight Sun Pagoda - Midnight Sun Pagoda (Tier 1)
cheats: At the start of the first Round these Bosses will cast the following spells using their turn not using Pips.
Yoshinobu (Tier 1) will use Mass Infection.
Hisamoto (Tier 1) will use Legion Shield and every three Rounds after.
"Chain Reaction!" - If a Wizard attacks Yoshinobu (Tier 1) with a Spell that costs 6 Pips or more (e.g. Colossus), then the following cheats will occur by interrupt.
Yoshinobu (Tier 1) casts a -60% Smoke Screen.
Hisamoto (Tier 1) casts Unicorn that restores 350 Health to all Samoorai.
Kensaburo (Tier 1) casts Meteor Strike dealing 355-395 Fire Damage.
"The Chain Continues!" - If a Wizard attacks Yoshinobu (Tier 1) with a Spell that costs 6 Pips or more after Hisamoto (Tier 1) is defeated, then the following cheats will occur by interrupt.
If Kensaburo (Tier 1) is not defeated:
Yoshinobu (Tier 1) casts Blizzard dealing 250-290 Ice Damage
Kensaburo (Tier 1) casts a Minor Blessing restoring 100 Health for each Samoorai casting one at a time.
Yoshinobu (Tier 1) will cast a Balance spell to place a +25% Hex and -30% Weakness on all Wizards, one cast at a time.
If Kensaburo (Tier 1) is defeated:
Yoshinobu (Tier 1) casts Blizzard dealing 250-290 Ice Damage, and Sandstorm dealing 255-295 Balance Damage.
strategy: Storm boss with only 695 health, bring cheap, low-pip attacks and AoE.

Never attack Yoshinobu with a 6-pip-or-higher spell (Colossus and the like). It triggers a Chain Reaction: Smoke Screen on you, a 350-heal to all Samoorai, and a Meteor Strike. Keep attacks under 6 pips.
Use a low-pip AoE to hit all the Samoorai at once so nothing survives to heal or retaliate.
Expect Yoshinobu's opening Mass Infection (cuts your healing) and Hisamoto's Legion Shield every 3 rounds, chip through with cheap hits.
If you break the chain rules after Hisamoto is down you will eat Blizzards and Hex/Weakness, so just clear the board fast with sub-6-pip AoE.
cheatNotes: Yoshinobu opens with Mass Infection, cutting your healing.
Hisamoto casts Legion Shield on Round 1 and every 3 rounds after, group shields to work through.
Attack Yoshinobu with a 6+ pip spell and you trigger the Chain Reaction below, keep attacks under 6 pips.
Chain Reaction: Yoshinobu casts a -60% Smoke Screen on you.
Chain Reaction: Hisamoto casts Unicorn, healing all Samoorai 350, why you avoid big single hits.
Chain Reaction: Kensaburo casts Meteor Strike for 355-395 Fire.
After Hisamoto is dead, a 6+ pip attack on Yoshinobu triggers a different chain.
Yoshinobu casts Blizzard for 250-290 Ice.
Kensaburo heals each Samoorai 100 with Minor Blessing.
Yoshinobu puts a +25% Hex and -30% Weakness on all wizards.
Yoshinobu casts Blizzard (250-290 Ice) and Sandstorm (255-295 Balance).

name: Yoshinobu (Tier 2)
locations: Housing Instance: Midnight Sun Pagoda - Midnight Sun Pagoda (Tier 2)
cheats: At the start of the first Round these Bosses will cast the following spells using their turn not using Pips.
Yoshinobu (Tier 2) will use Mass Infection.
Hisamoto (Tier 2) will use Legion Shield and every three Rounds after.
"Chain Reaction!" - If a Wizard attacks Yoshinobu (Tier 2) with a Spell that costs 6 Pips or more, such as Colossus, the following cheats will occur by interrupt.
Yoshinobu (Tier 2) casts a -60% Smoke Screen.
Hisamoto (Tier 2) casts Unicorn that restores 450 Health.
"The Chain Continues!" - If Yoshinobu (Tier 2) is damaged by a Spell that costs 6 Pips or more after Hisamoto (Tier 2) is defeated, the following cheats will occur by interrupt.
If Kensaburo (Tier 2) is not defeated:
Yoshinobu (Tier 2) casts Blizzard dealing 300-350 Ice Damage.
Kensaburo (Tier 2) casts a Minor Blessing restoring 125 Health for each Samoorai casting one at a time.
If Kensaburo (Tier 2) is defeated:
Yoshinobu (Tier 2) casts Blizzard dealing 300-350 Ice Damage and Sandstorm dealing 275-300 Balance Damage.
strategy: Attack with spells costing 5 pips or fewer. Hitting Yoshinobu with a 6+ pip spell triggers the Chain Reaction (a Smoke Screen on you plus a 450 Unicorn heal for them).
Expect Mass Infection (Round 1) cutting your healing and Hisamoto's Legion Shield every three rounds; blade over the infection or Cleanse it.
Hit with Myth (Storm boss resists Storm); at 1560 health small blade-stacked hits finish it.
After Hisamoto dies, 6+ pip hits still chain (Blizzard, and Kensaburo heals), so keep hits under 6 pips until you can burst everything down.
cheatNotes: Yoshinobu opens with Mass Infection, cutting your healing, so cleanse or blade over it.
Hisamoto Legion Shields the group on Round 1 and every three rounds, so hit through the shields.
Attacking Yoshinobu with a 6+ pip spell triggers the Chain Reaction, so keep hits at 5 pips or fewer.
The chain hits you with a -60% Smoke Screen.
And heals them 450 with Unicorn, so big single hits backfire here.
After Hisamoto dies, 6+ pip hits on Yoshinobu still chain, so stay under 6 pips until you can burst.
The chain includes a 300-350 Ice Blizzard on you.
And Kensaburo heals with Minor Blessing if still alive.
With Kensaburo dead the chain adds a Sandstorm on top of the Blizzard.

name: Yoshinobu (Tier 3)
cheats: [Round] During the first Round, the following Bosses will cast the following Spells using their turn but not using Pips:
[Curse] Yoshinobu will cast Mass Infection.
[Shield] Hisamoto will cast Legion Shield
Hisamoto will use his turn to cast Legion Shield every three Rounds after the first Round.
[Damage] "Chain Reaction!" - If a Wizard damages Yoshinobu with a Spell that costs at least 7 Pips, then the following cheats will occur:
[Curse] Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a -60% version of Smoke Screen.
[Healing] Hisamoto interrupt-casts a version of Rebirth that restores 650 Health to and applies a 250 Damage Absorption Ward on allies.
[Stun] Kensaburo interrupt-casts a version of Frost Giant that deals 500 Ice Damage to and Stuns all enemies.
[Damage] "The Chain Continues!" - If a Wizard damages Yoshinobu with a Spell that costs at least 7 Pips after Hisamoto is defeated, then the following cheats will occur:
If Kensaburo is not defeated:
[Damage] Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a version of Earthquake that deals 350 Myth Damage.
[Healing] Kensaburo individually interrupt-casts a version of Minor Blessing that restores 150 Health on each Samoorai.
If Kensaburo is defeated:
[Damage] Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a version of Earthquake that deals 350 Myth Damage, and a version of Scarecrow that deals 425 Death Drain Damage.
strategy: Hit with Myth (+50%); Yoshinobu resists Storm 80%.

Never damage Yoshinobu with a spell costing 7 or more Pips. The Chain Reaction answers with a -60% Smoke Screen, a 650 Rebirth heal plus a 250 Absorb on the enemies, and a 500 Ice Frost Giant that stuns your whole team. Use cheap sub-7-pip hits.
Hisamoto Legion Shields the enemies on Round 1 and every 3rd Round after. Break or pierce those shields, or hit between them.
At only 1,505 health, chip Yoshinobu and the minions down with low-pip Myth spells rather than one big AoE.
If you must go through Hisamoto and Kensaburo, expect the follow-up Earthquake and Scarecrow chains on 7+ pip hits. Keep spells cheap throughout.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 the bosses take free scripted turns, spending no pips.
Round 1: Yoshinobu casts Mass Infection, cutting your healing.
Round 1: Hisamoto casts Legion Shield on the enemies.
Hisamoto re-casts Legion Shield every 3rd Round after. Pierce or break through it.
Hitting Yoshinobu with a 7+ pip spell triggers the Chain Reaction below. Keep hits cheap.
Chain: Yoshinobu casts a -60% Smoke Screen on you.
Chain: Hisamoto Rebirths the enemies for 650 and adds a 250 Absorb.
Chain: Kensaburo hits all enemies for 500 Ice and Stuns them.
After Hisamoto is dead, a 7+ pip hit on Yoshinobu triggers a new chain.
Chain (Kensaburo alive): Yoshinobu Earthquakes for 350 Myth.
Chain (Kensaburo alive): Kensaburo heals each Samoorai 150.
Chain (Kensaburo dead): Yoshinobu Earthquakes for 350 Myth and Scarecrows for 425 Death drain.

name: Yoshinobu (Tier 4)
cheats: During the first Round, the following Bosses will cast the following Spells using their turn but not using Pips:
Yoshinobu will cast Mass Infection.
Hisamoto will cast Legion Shield
Hisamoto will use his turn to cast Legion Shield every three Rounds after the first Round.
"Chain Reaction!" - If a Wizard damages Yoshinobu with a Spell that costs at least 8 Pips, then the following cheats will occur:
Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a -60% version of Smoke Screen.
Hisamoto interrupt-casts a version of Rebirth that restores 750 Health to and applies a 250 Damage Absorption Ward on allies.
Kensaburo interrupt-casts a version of Power Nova that deals 550 Balance Damage to and applies a -50% Weakness on all enemies.
"The Chain Continues!" - If a Wizard damages Yoshinobu with a Spell that costs at least 8 Pips after Hisamoto is defeated, then the following cheats will occur:
If Kensaburo is not defeated:
Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a version of Storm Lord that deals 690 Storm Damage, but does not Stun.
Kensaburo individually interrupt-casts a version of Minor Blessing that restores 175 Health on each Samoorai.
If Kensaburo is defeated:
Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a version of Storm Lord that deals 690 Storm Damage but does not Stun, and a version of Ra that deals 565-645 Balance Damage but does not apply a Weakness.
Storm Lord reacts to Empowerment but not Adapt, while Ra reacts to both Empowerment and Adapt.
strategy: Attack with Myth or Life; Yoshinobu takes +30% from both. Avoid Storm hits (40% resist) and do not plan around stuns, he is not stunable. There are three minions: Hisamoto, Kensaburo, and Takazane.

Keep every hit under 8 Pips, or clear the minions first. Any single spell of 8+ Pips that damages Yoshinobu triggers the Chain Reaction: a -60% Smoke Screen on you, a 750-Health Rebirth heal from Hisamoto, and Kensaburo's Power Nova for 550 Balance plus a -50% Weakness on your whole team.

Plan for the free Round 1 casts. Yoshinobu opens with Mass Infection (cut your healing) and Hisamoto lays a Legion Shield absorb; Hisamoto recasts Legion Shield every three Rounds, so time your damage right after a recast or chip through the absorb.
Follow the kill order. Defeat Hisamoto first to stop the Legion Shield recasts and the Rebirth heal, then Kensaburo to stop Power Nova and the 175-Health Minor Blessing heals on the minions.

With Hisamoto and Kensaburo gone, an 8+ Pip hit only draws a non-stunning Storm Lord (690) plus Ra (565-645); no more enemy heals. Stack blades and traps, then finish Yoshinobu with one large Myth or Life hit and tank the counter.
cheatNotes: The bosses each take a free Round 1 setup cast that costs them no Pips, so expect their opening spells to land before you can respond.
You start the fight under a Mass Infection that weakens your heals; cleanse it before you rely on any healing spell.
Hisamoto's free Legion Shield puts a shared damage-absorb on the enemy side, so your first hits are partly soaked.
The Legion Shield comes back every third Round after the first. If you break or waste it, wait or expect a fresh absorb on Rounds 4, 7, and so on.
This is the core rule: any single spell of 8 or more Pips that damages Yoshinobu sets off the full chain below. Hit for 7 Pips or less, or kill the minions first.
The chain drops a -60% Smoke Screen on you, so your next spells may fizzle. Carry accuracy or a Sniper enchant if you must hit big.
Hisamoto heals the enemy side for 750 and adds a 250 absorb ward, which can undo a chunk of your damage; killing Hisamoto removes this from the chain.
Power Nova hits your whole team for 550 Balance and stacks a -50% Weakness, cutting your next attack; killing Kensaburo removes it.
After Hisamoto is dead, an 8+ Pip hit triggers a different chain, shown below.
While Kensaburo is still alive, the post-Hisamoto chain includes both of the following.
You eat a 690 Storm Lord, but note it does not Stun, so you can act normally next Round.
Kensaburo heals each remaining Samoorai for 175, slowly undoing minion damage; defeat him to end it.
Once Kensaburo is also dead, the 8+ Pip chain switches to the following pair.
With both Hisamoto and Kensaburo down, an 8+ Pip hit only costs you a 690 Storm Lord and a 565-645 Ra, both non-stunning and with no Weakness; this is safe to tank on your finishing turn.
These interrupt hits scale with certain buffs: Storm Lord answers Empowerment, and Ra answers both Empowerment and Adapt, so avoid feeding them those before you take the hit.

name: Yoshinobu (Tier 5)
cheats: [Round] During the first Round, the following Bosses will cast the following Spells using their turn but not using Pips:
[Curse] Yoshinobu will cast Mass Infection.
[Shield] Hisamoto will cast Legion Shield
Hisamoto will use his turn to cast Legion Shield every three Rounds after the first Round.
[Damage] "Chain Reaction!" - If a Wizard damages Yoshinobu with a Spell that costs at least 8 Pips, then the following cheats will occur:
[Curse] Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a -60% version of Smoke Screen.
[Healing] Hisamoto interrupt-casts a version of Rebirth that restores 850 Health to and applies a 350 Damage Absorption Ward on allies.
[Damage] Kensaburo interrupt-casts a version of Sirens that deals 100 Storm Damage to, removes 2 helpful Charms from, and places a +50% universal Trap on all enemies.
The universal Trap has the appearance of a harmful Accuracy Charm.
[Damage] "The Chain Continues!" - If a Wizard damages Yoshinobu with a Spell that costs at least 8 Pips after Hisamoto is defeated, then the following cheats will occur:
If Kensaburo is not defeated:
[Damage] Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a version of Snow Angel that deals 100 Ice Damage and 660 Ice Damage over 3 Rounds.
[Healing] Kensaburo individually interrupt-casts a version of Minor Blessing that restores 200 Health on each Samoorai.
If Kensaburo is defeated:

[Damage] Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a version of Snow Angel that deals 100 Ice Damage and 660 Ice Damage over 3 Rounds, and a version of Rain of Fire that deals 300 Fire Damage and 800 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds.
strategy: Expect the round-1 openers: Yoshinobu lays Mass Infection (weakens your healing) and Hisamoto casts Legion Shield, repeating every 3 rounds. Pack pierce or shatter for the shields and avoid leaning on heals.

His punish triggers on any hit costing 8+ pips, so keep your damage spells under 8 pips or expect the counter chain (a -60% Smoke Screen, a team heal and absorb from Hisamoto, and a Sirens hit that strips 2 charms and traps you).
Work through the samoorai in order: with Hisamoto up the 8+ pip chain heals and absorbs; after Hisamoto falls the chain shifts to Snow Angel damage plus Kensaburo heals.
Kill Kensaburo before pushing Yoshinobu, since once Kensaburo is down the 8+ pip chain adds a Rain of Fire on top of Snow Angel.
He resists Storm 40% and takes +40% from Myth and Life; build blades on one hitter and finish with sub-8-pip hits so you never feed the chain.
cheatNotes: During round 1 the bosses take free opening turns without spending pips, listed below.
Yoshinobu opens with Mass Infection on the team.
Hisamoto opens with Legion Shield.
Hisamoto re-casts Legion Shield every 3 rounds after round 1.
Damaging Yoshinobu with a spell costing 8+ pips triggers the chain of counters below.
Yoshinobu interrupt-casts a -60% Smoke Screen on the team.
Hisamoto interrupt-casts a Rebirth healing allies 850 and giving them a 350 absorb.
Kensaburo interrupt-casts Sirens, hitting all wizards 100 Storm, removing 2 helpful charms, and adding a +50% universal trap.
That trap is disguised as a harmful Accuracy Charm.
After Hisamoto is defeated, an 8+ pip hit on Yoshinobu triggers the second chain below.
While Kensaburo is alive, Yoshinobu casts Snow Angel (100 Ice plus 660 Ice over 3 rounds).
While Kensaburo is alive, he also heals each samoorai 200 with Minor Blessing.
Once Kensaburo is defeated, Yoshinobu casts Snow Angel plus a Rain of Fire (300 Fire plus 800 Fire over 3 rounds).

name: Young Morganthe
locations: Avalon: Ghost Avalon - Merle Ambrose's Tower
cheats: "No more of that!" - If a Wizard casts a Ward Spell (Shields or Traps), she will randomly cheat-cast Power Nova and Earthquake dealing 400 Myth Damage to all enemies and 100 Fire Damage to herself.
The Earthquake portion of this cheat will not remove any of her Charms or protective Wards.
She is more likely to use this cheat if the Ward is cast after she uses an Attack, including her Mana Burn.
Pet casts do not activate this cheat.
Multi-target Trap Spells, such as Mass Feint (+75%), will trigger this cheat.
Unbalance can be used to Dispel the Power Nova portion of this cheat.
"Time for a new strategy!" - If a Wizard acquires 8 or more Pips, she will cheat-cast Mana Burn on the Wizard at the beginning of the Round.
Unbalance can be used to Dispel this cheat.
If the Wizard has already queued a Spell to be cast before being hit by the Mana Burn, then the Spell will finish, regardless of how many Pips are available at the time it is cast.
strategy: Hit with Ice or Life (+35% each) and avoid Death (80% resist).
Do not cast Wards (Shields or Traps). A ward makes her randomly cheat Power Nova and Earthquake, dealing 400 Myth to all enemies and stripping your blades; Mass Feint triggers this too.
You can Unbalance to Dispel the Power Nova portion if you must place a ward.
Do not bank pips: reaching 8 or more pips makes her Mana Burn you at the start of the Round; Unbalance also dispels this, and a spell already queued still fires.
Since Earthquake strips blades, set up quickly and hit before she can respond; keep your pip count under 8 until you are ready to cast.
Stack Ice or Life blades in one burst, then fire before hitting the 8-pip line.
cheatNotes: Casting any Shield or Trap makes her randomly cheat Power Nova and Earthquake, hitting all enemies for 400 Myth and stripping your blades; avoid wards and set up with blades quickly.
The Earthquake part does not touch her own charms or wards, only yours.
She is more likely to use this after she has attacked, including after her Mana Burn.
Pet-cast wards do not trigger this cheat.
Mass Feint counts as a ward and triggers the Power Nova and Earthquake; avoid it.
Unbalance can Dispel the Power Nova portion if you need to place a ward.
Reaching 8 or more pips makes her Mana Burn you at the start of the Round; hit before you bank that many pips.
Unbalance also Dispels the Mana Burn cheat.
A spell you already queued still fires even if the Mana Burn drains your pips, so commit your hit before she can burn you.

name: Yowck
locations: Empyrea: Aeriel Shores - Beastman Fight Club
cheats: "You're Missing The Party!" - Yowck will use Mana Burn (Star) if a Wizard joins the duel late, followed by a Star version of Donate Power giving 7 Power Pips to his entire team.

"See How You Like It!" - Yowck will use a Star version of Shift, if Traps are put on Adrok, putting the Trap back on a random Wizard. Yowck will also cast this if any ally of his casts a Trap moving only the negative wards a Wizard has cast on the target.
Prisms will activate this cheat only at the end of the Round.
Indemnity can be used to avoid this cheat.
After casting his Natural Attack, Yowck will place an Elemental Shield (-55%) or Spirit Shield (-55%) on a random ally.
"Haha! Nice try" - If a Wizard fizzles, he will cast Black Mantle (-55%, 0 Pip) on the one who fizzled.
If a Wizard places a single-target Ward on an ally (such as Tower Shield and Feint) Yowck will do one of the following:
"First one is on the house!" - Boss will allow the first cast.
"Second one has a cover charge!" - Further casts in the same round will trigger Yowck to use Pierce (Star) on the recipient of the Ward and Freeze on the caster.
This cycle resets every round.
If a Wizard uses a mass Ward on their allies (such as Legion Shield and Rebirth)
"Group Concert? I'll Allow it This Time!" - Boss will allow the first cast.
"This party is out of control!" - Further casts in the same round will trigger Yowck to use Pierce (Star, All Enemies) and Blinding Freeze on all Wizards.
Adrok
"Leave Adrok Alone!" - If a negative charm is cast on Yowck, Adrok will cast Cleanse Charm (Star), to remove it.
Mykedee
"Time for one last Beatdown" - Mykedee will cast Guardian Spirit (20%) on Beets.
strategy: Ice boss (23370) in the Beastman trio (with Adrok and Mykedee), with 100% incoming healing so never heal him and expect drains to rebound. Hit with Fire (+10%); avoid Ice/Balance (75%) and Storm/Shadow (40%).

Traps are risky: traps on Adrok (or ally-cast negative wards) get Shifted back onto a random wizard. Indemnity protects them, and prisms only trigger the shift at end of round, so plan accordingly. Trap Yowck directly.

Warding allies is gated: the first single-target Ward each round is free, but a second makes him Pierce the recipient and Freeze the caster. Mass Wards work the same, one free, the second Pierces all and Blinding Freezes the team. Limit to one ward per round.
Negative charms on Yowck get Cleansed by Adrok, so debuffs on him are wasted. Expect a -55% shield on an ally after his Natural Attack and Black Mantle on anyone who fizzles, so bring accuracy.
Funnel blades to the hitter (Indemnity-protect key traps), manage one ward per round, and burst with Fire.
cheatNotes: A late joiner gets Mana Burned and his whole team gets 7 Power Pips. Everyone in on round 1.
Traps placed on Adrok (or ally-cast negative wards) get Shifted onto a random wizard. Trap Yowck directly and Indemnity-protect key traps.
Prisms trigger this shift only at the end of the round. Account for the delay when using them.
Indemnity keeps your trap safe from the shift. Protect the trap that matters.
After his Natural Attack he puts a -55% Elemental or Spirit Shield on an ally. Over-stack blades or pierce through it on your hit.
If a wizard fizzles he Black Mantles them (-55% accuracy), snowballing more fizzles. Carry accuracy so you do not start the spiral.
Single-target Wards on allies are gated: one free per round, the second is punished as below.
The first single-target ward each round is allowed. Cast only one.
A second single-target ward in the same round makes him Pierce the recipient and Freeze the caster. Keep it to one ward per round.
The ward limit resets each round, so spread your shielding across turns.
Mass Wards follow the same one-free rule below.
The first mass ward each round is allowed.
A second mass ward makes him Pierce all wizards and Blinding Freeze the team. One mass ward per round, max.
Negative charms on Yowck get Cleansed off by Adrok. Do not waste turns debuffing him.

name: Zebra Spectral Guardian
cheats: This Boss will cast a 0-Pip Blizzard (reported to do Colossal Blizzard damage) if a player is marked and does not cast a Charm Spell within 2 Rounds of being marked.
strategy: Attack with Fire or Myth (+30%); it resists Ice 40%.
Watch for the mark: a marked wizard must cast a Charm (any blade) within 2 rounds or take a 0-pip Colossal Blizzard. Always keep a blade in hand to dump when marked, which doubles as setup.
Since the required Charm is just a blade, stack blades naturally to satisfy the mark while building damage.
It is only 4,225 health, so a couple of blades into a boosted Fire or Myth hit ends it.
Feint and finish.
cheatNotes: When you are marked, cast any Charm (a blade works) within 2 rounds or eat a huge 0-pip Blizzard. Keep a blade ready, which also builds your setup.

name: Zeus Sky Father (Mount Olympus)
locations: Wizard City: Mount Olympus - Throne Room (Mount Olympus)
cheats: "YOU HESITATE TOO MUCH!" - If a Wizard casts a Spell that costs 6 or more Pips, Zeus will cheat-cast a Minotaur that deals 75 Myth Damage, plus 525 Myth Damage.
Shadow Spells will trigger an additional Minotaur.
spellNotes: Casts a 0-Pip version of his Natural Attack that deals any Damage between the range of 1-3 Pips.
strategy: Don't cast spells costing 6+ pips — Zeus counters each one with a Minotaur (75 + 525 Myth) on you.
Keep your attacks at 5 pips or fewer and rely on blades for damage, and avoid Shadow-enhanced spells (they trigger an extra Minotaur).
Attack with Storm (he takes +50%); he resists Myth 80%.
He is stunable, so stun him to skip a turn if you need breathing room.
Stack a couple of blades and traps, then finish his 4,000 health with a cheap (5-pip-or-less) Storm hit.
cheatNotes: Any spell costing 6+ pips makes him cheat-cast a Minotaur (75 + 525 Myth) on you — keep attacks at 5 pips or fewer and rely on blades.
Shadow-enhanced spells trigger an extra Minotaur on top — skip shadow spells here.

name: Zeus Sky Father (Mount Olympus, Challenge)
locations: Wizard City: Mount Olympus (Challenge) - Throne Room (Mount Olympus, Challenge)
cheats: [Curse] "Did you think you could silence an Immortal?" - Whenever a Wizard casts a harmful Charm on Zeus, Zeus will cast Athena Battle Sight on the caster.
[Ward] "Your punishment shall be swift" - Whenever a Wizard casts a helpful Ward, Zeus will cast Minotaur on the recipient that deals 75 Myth Damage, plus 525 Myth Damage.

strategy: Keep every attack under 5 pips - a 5-pip-or-greater spell triggers a natural attack on the caster. Blade up and hit with small, hard spells.
Do not shield or cast helpful wards - each one triggers a Minotaur hit (up to ~600 Myth) on whoever you warded.
Do not cast weaknesses or other harmful charms on Zeus - he answers with Athena Battle Sight on the caster.
Hit with Storm - he takes 50% more from Storm and resists Myth 80%. With only 2,750 health, a couple of blades plus a sub-5-pip Storm hit ends it.
Deal with the Minotaur Warrior minion, but keep to blades and small hits throughout.
cheatNotes: Casting a harmful charm (weakness) on Zeus makes him put Athena Battle Sight on the caster. Skip weaknesses.
Casting a helpful Ward (any shield) makes him hit the warded wizard with Minotaur for roughly 600 Myth. Do not shield.
Using a 5-pip-or-greater spell makes him natural-attack the caster. Keep your spells under 5 pips.

name: Zeus Sky Father (Zeus Exalted Challenge)
cheats: [Myth] Zeus Sky Father
"Did you think you could silence an Immortal?" - If a Dispel is placed on any enemy, Zeus Sky Father will remove it by cheat-casting Cleanse Charm.
"You deserve an immortal boon." - If an All Enemies Spell is used, Zeus Sky Father will cast Healing Current (325) on the Wizard.

[Fire] Ares Savage Spear
"Your underhanded tactics are unbefitting of a true warrior!" - If a Trap is placed on an enemy, Ares will cheat-cast an Efreet (1,000) on the Wizard who cast the Spell.
If Apollo Bright One and Zeus Sky Father have been defeated, this cheat can be prevented by using Quench.
Spells that target all enemies, such as Windstorm do not trigger this cheat.
"Such prowess, but I think you can do better!" - If a Damage Spell is used, Ares Savage Spear will give the Wizard a Blade by casting Dark Pact (+30%).
"We battle on! For Zeus!" - If Zeus Sky Father has been defeated, Ares Savage Spear will use his Natural Attack at the start of each Round.

"Observe the might of a true weapon!" If a Blade Spell is used, Poseidon Earth Shaker will cast Shift Atlantean on the Wizard who received the Blade(s), causing an Enfeeble and 360 Storm Damage.
Aegis enchantment can be used to prevent any positive Charms from being removed.
Bladestorm will only affect the caster.
"The Seas are puddles without stars" - If an Aura or Shadow Spell is used, Poseidon Earth Shaker will place a Tower Shield on the caster.
"The Sea mourns for mighty Zeus" - If Zeus Sky Father is defeated, Poseidon Earth Shaker will cast his Tempest Natural Attack every three Rounds.
[Death] Hades the Unseen
"The selfish belong in Tartarus!" - If a Healing Spell is used on themselves, Hades the Unseen will cast Shift Wraith, dealing 335 Death Damage and adds a -90% Infection to the Wizard.
This cheat will not trigger if a Wizard uses a single-target heal on another player.
AoE heals will trigger this cheat.
Pet heals will trigger this cheat.
"Your donation to Tartarus is greatly appreciated." - If a Wizard or a Boss is defeated, Hades the Unseen will cast Donate Power (+3 Pips) to whoever cast the defeating Spell.
"Zeus sends his regards from Tartarus" - If Zeus Sky Father is defeated, Hades the Unseen will cast his Natural Attack on a random Wizard every two Rounds.
Pairing Interrupt Cycles:
The following is a list of cheats that involve two Bosses that use two spells by interrupt.
All interrupt cycles in this battle last over the course of 14 Rounds and repeat until the battle is finished.
Zeus & Ares [Myth] [Fire]
"Let our army shake the Earth" - Every tenth Round, Zeus will use his Earthquake Natural Attack
Ares Savage Spear will follow up to use his Natural Attack.
Zeus & Hades [Myth] [Death]

"Storms above and Death below!" - At the beginning of every fifth Round, Zeus Sky Father will cast his Earthquake Natural Attack which deals 630 Myth Damage to all Wizards and removes Charms and Wards
This cheat is followed by his Lernaean Hydra Natural Attack.
Zeus & Poseidon [Myth] [Storm]

"Angry Skies make vengeful Seas!" - At the beginning of every fifth Round, Zeus Sky Father will cast his Earthquake Natural Attack which deals 630 Myth Damage to all Wizards and removes Charms and Wards.
Poseidon may then follow this cheat with his Tempest Natural Attack, dealing 960 Storm Damage and stealing one Pip from each Wizard.
Poseidon & Apollo [Storm] [Life]
"The Seas boil beneath the angry Sun!" - Poseidon Earth Shaker will use his Snow Shark Natural Attack dealing 650 Ice Damage to all Wizards.
This cheat follows with Apollo Bright One's Natural Attack in the same turn.
Poseidon & Hades [Storm] [Death]
"Tsunami from the Underworld!" - Every fifth Round, Poseidon Earth Shaker will use his Snow Shark Natural Attack.
Hades will follow up with his Lycian Chimera Natural Attack, which deals 590 Death Damage to all Wizards and adds a -45% version of Virulent Plague.
"Let the Underworld Seas turn to rage!" - Every tenth Round, Poseidon Earth Shaker will use his Snow Shark Natural Attack dealing 650 Ice Damage to all Wizards.
Hades the Unseen will follow up with Lernaean Hydra Natural Attack, dealing 795 Storm Damage and adding a Black Mantle to all Wizards.
During the first Round, Hades will cast his 4 Damage over Time Natural Attack before Poseidon.

"Tartarus is the true victor in war!" - At the beginning of every first Round, Hades the Unseen will use his Natural Attack on a random Wizard that deals 4 Death Damage, then applies 4 DoT that deals 1,175 Death Damage four Rounds later, 1 Death Damage three Rounds later, 2 Death Damage two Rounds later, and 3 Death Damage for one Round with 100% Armor Piercing.
Ares Savage Spear will then follow this cheat with their Natural Attack.
Ares & Apollo [Fire] [Life]
"Wither under the Sun's rage!" - Every first Round, Ares Savage Spear will cast a Spell similar to Wrath of Ares dealing 705 Fire Damage to all Wizards and Detonate one DoT from each.
This cheat follows Apollo Bright One's Natural Attack which deals 30 + 780 Fire Damage over 3 Rounds to all Wizards, and 525 Healing to all Bosses over 3 Rounds.
spellNotes: Zeus uses an enhanced version of Earthquake as his Natural Attack.

strategy: Five-boss team challenge (Zeus, Apollo, Ares, Poseidon, Hades). Kill Apollo Bright One first — if any other boss dies before him, he revives all fallen bosses with Eternal Leviathan.
Hit Zeus with Storm or Death (+35%); avoid Myth (80%). Adapt your school for each of the other bosses.

Mind the setup punishes: a Trap triggers Ares' Efreet (1,000) on the caster, a Blade triggers Poseidon's Shift Atlantean (Enfeeble + 360), an Aura/Shadow spell triggers Poseidon's Tower Shield, a self-heal triggers Hades' Shift Wraith + Infection, and a single-target spell can trigger Zeus' stunning Shift Minotaur. Use Aegis on blades and favor multi-target spells.

Exploit the gifts: a Damage spell makes Ares hand you a +30% Dark Pact blade, an All-Enemies spell makes Zeus heal you, an AoE heal makes Apollo give you Guiding Light, and any kill gives +3 pips from Hades — so AoE-based play buffs and sustains your team.

Once Zeus dies, every other boss starts firing natural attacks on a timer and the pairing cycles (Earthquake combos every 5th/10th round that strip charms/wards and hit all wizards) ramp up — so burst the remaining bosses fast after Apollo and Zeus are down.

Heal yourself and Hades Shift Wraiths you (335 Death + -90% Infection) — heal others via AoE instead.

name: Aphrodite II
strategy: Fight the three tendrils (Life ~20,270 HP, Death ~16,095, Myth ~12,465) as a team; most runs spawn the Life and Death variants.
Hit any tendril every single round. If no tendril is struck, Aphrodite pollinates the whole team with a Shadow DoT (~400 up front, then ~300/round for three rounds).
Assign roles: 1-2 Feint specialists trapping one tendril, wand-spam supports keeping a tendril hit each round, and a hammer in the fourth slot stacking the feints for the kill.
Avoid DoT spells: Life answers with Mass Triage, Myth with Shift, and Death with Deer Knight (which removes your over-time attacks). Never DoT the Death tendril.
Don't blade or drop a damage bubble opposite a Myth tendril, or the tendril across from you fires two cheats (one immediately, one at end of turn).
Round flow: wand a single untrapped tendril while trappers layer Feint on it; once it has 2-3 Feints the hammer casts a big AoE, aiming to drop multiple tendrils in the same round.
Alternative: run an all-AoE team (Meteor, Sandstorm) with an active aura and a strong healer to grind every tendril down at once.
Don't cast Deer Knight yourself, since its over-time damage just gets removed.

name: Roberto
locations: Wizard City: Olde Town - Roberto's Sanctum
strategy: This is a trivial solo fight. Roberto is a Rank 2 Fire boss with only 350 health and no minions or recorded cheats; the guide notes even a level 5 wizard can beat him without much trouble.
He does -20% outgoing damage, so his own hits are weak. Just blade once and hit.
Open with a single blade (any school blade you carry), then land any 2-3 pip attack to close it out in a round or two.
He is stunable and beguilable if you want extra safety, but it is rarely needed at this health total. No prisms or setup are required.

name: Sapoti
locations: Krokotopia: The Oasis - Sapoti's Treasure Chamber
strategy: Straightforward fight: the guide notes Sapoti has no cheats, so just run the standard setup-and-hit loop.
Sapoti is a Life boss, so it resists Life and takes boosted damage from Death; hit with Death or your strongest off-school attack.
Take out the Rank 3 Storm minion (~525 HP) quickly with a wand hit or small spell so it can't chip you during setup.
Stack a couple of blades, then finish with one AoE to clear the minion and the 3,040-HP boss together.
Sapoti is stunnable and beguilable if you want extra control, but the fight is short enough to simply rush it.
